<root main_tree_to_execute="MainTree">
  <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
    <PipelineSequence name="NavigateWithReplanning">
      <RateController hz="1.0">
        <ComputePathToPose goal="{goal}" path="{path}" planner_id="GridBased"/>
      </RateController>
      <FollowPath path="{path}"  controller_id="FollowPath"/>
    </PipelineSequence>
  </BehaviorTree>
</root>
mrq: The mission describes a navigation task that computes a global path at 1 Hz and advances to path following while allowing continuous replanning. First, the robot will execute the following actions (RateController, FollowPath) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute the following actions (ComputePathToPose) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails while an action is running or has succeeded, it will go to the next action. It will run the custom action RateController at 1.0 Hz to throttle the ROS action ComputePathToPose. It will run the ROS action ComputePathToPose using the provided goal and will store the resulting path at the controlled rate. Next, it will run the ROS action FollowPath using the stored path with the specified controller while path computation continues at the controlled rate.

BT:
<root main_tree_to_execute="MainTree">
  <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
    <PipelineSequence name="NavigateWithReplanning">
      <DistanceController distance="1.0">
        <ComputePathToPose goal="{goal}" path="{path}" planner_id="GridBased"/>
      </DistanceController>
      <FollowPath path="{path}"  controller_id="FollowPath"/>
    </PipelineSequence>
  </BehaviorTree>
</root>
mrq: The mission describes a navigation task that continuously recomputes a global path every meter while advancing along that path. First, the robot will execute the following actions (DistanceController, FollowPath) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. While an action is running or has succeeded, it will go to the next action. It will execute delayed ComputePathToPose under a distance-based trigger by running the custom action DistanceController that wraps the ROS action ComputePathToPose and starts a new plan whenever the traveled distance reaches 1.0 meters. It will run the ROS action ComputePathToPose with the provided goal and will store the resulting path each time the distance condition is met. It will run the ROS action FollowPath using the most recently stored path with the specified controller while planning continues in parallel due to the pipeline advancement semantics.

    <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
       <Sequence name="root_sequence">
           <Go_point   name="Go_point" goal="{destination}"/>
           <Go_object   name="Go_object" target="{object}"/>
       </Sequence>
    </BehaviorTree>
</root>
mrq: The mission describes a navigation-and-acquisition task that first goes to a specified point and then goes to a specified object in strict order. First, The robot will execute the following actions (Go_point, Go_object) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action Go_point with the provided destination goal. It will run the custom action Go_object with the provided object target only after the point navigation succeeds.

    <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
      <SequenceStar name="root_sequence">
        <Go_point   name="Go_point" goal="{destination}"/>
        <Sequence name="root_sequence">
          <Turn   name="Turn"/>
          <Go_object   name="Go_object" target="{object}"/>
        </Sequence>
      </SequenceStar>
    </BehaviorTree>
</root>
mrq: The mission describes a two-stage task that reaches a point and then turns and approaches a target object in order, with memory of completed stages across retries. First, the robot will execute the following actions (Go_point, Sequence) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will run the custom action Go_point using the provided destination as its goal. Next, it will execute the following actions (Turn, Go_object) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action Turn to achieve the required orientation. It will run the custom action Go_object using the provided object as its target after the turn succeeds.

<root main_tree_to_execute="MainTree">
  <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
    <Sequence name="root">
      <RateController hz="1.0">
        <Fallback>
          <GoalReached/>
          <ComputePathToPose goal="${goal}"/>
        </Fallback>
      </RateController>
      <FollowPath path="${path}"/>
    </Sequence>
  </BehaviorTree>
</root>
mrq:
The mission describes a navigation task that computes a path to a provided goal at a fixed rate and then follows the latest computed path in strict order.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (RateController, FollowPath) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will go to the next action while the first action is running or has succeeded. It will run the ROS action FollowPath using the path available on the blackboard after the rate-controlled planning completes.

Next, it will execute the following actions (Fallback) in order while being invoked by the rate controller at 1.0 Hz. It will execute the following actions (GoalReached, ComputePathToPose) consecutively. If any action (GoalReached) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will run the ROS action ComputePathToPose with the goal from the blackboard when the goal has not been reached. It will make the fallback outcome available to the parent at the configured rate.

mrq:
The mission describes a mobile manipulation task that navigates to a room, finds a bottle, and grasps it with recovery when needed.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (FB2, S2) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute the following actions (S7, FollowHuman) consecutively. If any action (S7) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will execute the following actions (Room_known, FB10) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute the following actions (Robot_in_room, Go_to_room) consecutively. If any action (Robot_in_room) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will read the condition Room_known and verify that it is true before attempting to enter the room. It will read the condition Robot_in_room and verify whether it is true before deciding to navigate. It will run the custom action Go_to_room if it is not already in the room. It will run the custom action FollowHuman if room access via S7 does not succeed.

Next, it will execute the following actions (FB3, FB4) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute the following actions (Bottle_found, Find_bottle) consecutively. If any action (Bottle_found) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will execute the following actions (Bottle_grasped, FB5) consecutively. If any action (Bottle_grasped) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will run the custom action Find_bottle if the bottle is not yet found. It will execute the following actions (S3, FB6) consecutively. If any action (S3) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will execute the following actions (FB7, S4) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute the following actions (S6, Compute_inv_pose) consecutively. If any action (S6) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will execute the following actions (Inv_pose_computed, Inv_pose_valid) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will read the condition Inv_pose_computed and verify that it is true before using the inverse pose. It will read the condition Inv_pose_valid and verify that it is true before moving to the inverse pose. It will run the custom action Compute_inv_pose if the inverse pose is not computed or not valid. It will execute the following actions (FB8, S5) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute the following actions (Robot_at_inv_pose, S8) consecutively. If any action (Robot_at_inv_pose) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will execute the following actions (Keep_arms_for_grasp, Go_to_inv_pose) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action Keep_arms_for_grasp to prepare the manipulators for grasping. It will run the custom action Go_to_inv_pose to reach the inverse pose for grasping. It will execute the following actions (FB9, Grasp_bottle) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute the following actions (Bottle_located, Locate_bottle) consecutively. If any action (Bottle_located) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will run the custom action Locate_bottle if the bottle is not yet localized. It will run the custom action Grasp_bottle to close the gripper on the bottle. It will execute the following actions (Set_inv_pose_not_valid, Keep_running) consecutively. If any action (Set_inv_pose_not_valid) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will run the custom action Set_inv_pose_not_valid to invalidate the current inverse pose when grasping fails. It will run the custom action Keep_running to continue attempts after invalidating the pose.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (S1, Ask_for_help) consecutively. If any action (S1) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will run the custom action Ask_for_help if all prior attempts in S1 fail.

mrq:
The mission describes a household drink-serving task that decides how to reach a room and then fetches a bottle and a glass to pour a drink, with a contingency to ask for help if all actions fail.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (FB2, S2) consecutively. If any action (FB2) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will then execute the following actions (S2) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails.

Next, it will execute the following actions (S6, Follow_human) consecutively. If any action (S6) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will execute the following actions (Room_known, Go_to_room) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule. It will run the custom action Go_to_room after the condition succeeds. If the sequence S6 fails, it will run the custom action Follow_human as the fallback option.

Next, it will execute the following actions (Find_bottle, S3) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action Find_bottle to locate the bottle. It will execute the following actions (Fetch_bottle, S4) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action Fetch_bottle to pick up the bottle. It will execute the following actions (Find_glass, S5) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action Find_glass to locate the glass. It will execute the following actions (Fetch_glass, Pour_drink) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action Fetch_glass to pick up the glass. It will run the custom action Pour_drink to pour the drink into the glass.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (FB1, Ask_for_help) consecutively. If any action (FB1) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will run the custom action Ask_for_help after the previous subtree fails.

<root main_tree_to_execute = "MainTree" >
    <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
            <ReactiveSequence name="root">
                    <AIP_isHolding      goal="0; 0; 1.3; 0.3; 0; 0; 0; 0; 1; 1;"/> <!-- State value; state index; x_obj_loc; y_obj_loc; object index -->
                    <AIP_isAt           goal="0; 2; 0.3; -1.3; 0.0; 0.0; 0.0; -0.707; 0.707"/> <!-- State value; state index; x, y, z, quaternion xyzw -->
                    <AIP_isPlacedAt     goal="0; 4; 0.3; -1.3; 0; 0; 0; -0.707; 0.707; 1;"/> <!-- State value; state index; x_obj_loc; y_obj_loc; object index -->
            </ReactiveSequence>
    </BehaviorTree>
</root>
mrq: The mission describes a reactive pick-and-place verification task that checks holding state, checks position, and checks object placement in sequence with continuous re-evaluation. First, the robot will execute the following actions (AIP_isHolding, AIP_isAt, AIP_isPlacedAt) in sequence. It will stop execution if any action fails. While an action is still running, it will re-evaluate previous actions. It will run the custom action AIP_isHolding to verify that it is not holding any object and to register the target object location at coordinates x=0.0 and y=1.3 with object index 1. It will run the custom action AIP_isAt to verify that it is at pose index 2 located at x=0.3 and y=-1.3 with the specified orientation quaternion x=0.0, y=0.0, z=-0.707, w=0.707. It will run the custom action AIP_isPlacedAt to verify that the object with index 1 is placed at coordinates x=0.3 and y=-1.3 with the specified orientation quaternion x=0.0, y=0.0, z=-0.707, w=0.707.

BT:
<root main_tree_to_execute="BehaviorTree">
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="BehaviorTree">
        <Repeat num_cycles="-1">
            <Sequence name="Pursue Goals">
                <Fallback name="No Item to pick">
                    <Inverter name="Not">
                        <Condition ID="SeeItem" name="See an Item"/>
                    </Inverter>
                    <Action ID="Pick" name="Pick it up"/>
                </Fallback>
                <Fallback name="No Item in gripper">
                    <Inverter name="Not">
                        <Condition ID="HoldingItem" name="Holding an Item"/>
                    </Inverter>
                    <Action ID="Place" name="Place it"/>
                </Fallback>
            </Sequence>
        </Repeat>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="Init">
        <Action ID="Print" message="Initialize"/>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <TreeNodesModel>
        <Action ID="AlwaysRunning"/>
        <Action ID="GetItemPose"/>
        <Condition ID="HoldingItem"/>
        <SubTree ID="Init"/>
        <Action ID="Pick"/>
        <Action ID="Place"/>
        <Action ID="Print">
            <input_port name="message"/>
        </Action>
        <Condition ID="SeeItem"/>
        <Action ID="Trigger">
            <input_port name="service_name"/>
        </Action>
    </TreeNodesModel>
    <!-- ////////// -->
</root>
mrq:
The mission describes a continuous pick-and-place cycle that repeats forever to pursue available items and manage placement based on perceived state.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (Pursue Goals) for a set number of times or infinitely. It will execute the following actions (No Item to pick, No Item in gripper) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute the following actions (Not, Pick) consecutively. If any action (Not) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution will fail. It will execute inverted Find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule. If Find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule succeeds, it will reverse it to fail. Else if Find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule fails, it will reverse it to succeed. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for the SeeItem condition. It will execute the custom action Pick and make it succeed for the Fallback when the Inverter fails. It will execute the following actions (Not, Place) consecutively. If any action (Not) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution will fail. It will execute inverted Find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule. If Find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule succeeds, it will reverse it to fail. Else if Find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule fails, it will reverse it to succeed. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for the HoldingItem condition. It will execute the custom action Place and make it succeed for the Fallback when the Inverter fails.

mrq:
The mission describes a task that will execute two initial actions and then attempt a prioritized set of fallback actions within a larger ordered sequence. First, the robot will execute the following actions (go1, go2, Fallback) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action go1 as the first step in the ordered sequence. It will run the custom action go2 as the second step only after the first step succeeds. It will then execute the Fallback subtree as the third step of the ordered sequence.

Next, it will execute the following actions (go3, go4, go5, Sequence) consecutively. If any action (go3, go4, go5, Sequence) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution will fail. It will attempt the custom action go3 as the first choice in the fallback. It will attempt the custom action go4 as the second choice if the first choice fails. It will attempt the custom action go5 as the third choice if the second choice fails. It will attempt the Sequence subtree as the final choice if all previous choices fail.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (go6, go7) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action go6 as the first step of this inner ordered sequence. It will run the custom action go7 only after the previous step succeeds.

mrq: The mission describes a placing task that retrieves an object identifier, determines a placing pose, moves to that pose, and then places the object in strict order. First, the robot will execute the following actions (GetPickObject, GetPlacingPose, Move, PlaceSimple) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action GetPickObject using the provided object_id to identify the object to place. It will run the custom action GetPlacingPose using the identified object and will produce a target pose stored as pose. It will run the custom action Move to drive the arm or base toward the stored pose after the placing pose is available. It will run the custom action PlaceSimple to release the object at the stored pose after the movement completes successfully.

<root main_tree_to_execute="MainTree">
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="DoorClosed">
        <Sequence name="door_closed_sequence">
            <Inverter>
                <Condition ID="IsDoorOpen"/>
            </Inverter>
            <RetryUntilSuccesful num_attempts="4">
                <Action ID="OpenDoor"/>
            </RetryUntilSuccesful>
            <Action ID="PassThroughDoor"/>
            <Action ID="CloseDoor"/>
        </Sequence>
    </BehaviorTree>

    <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
        <Fallback name="root_Fallback">
            <Sequence name="door_open_sequence">
                <Condition ID="IsDoorOpen"/>
                <Action ID="PassThroughDoor"/>
            </Sequence>
            <SubTree ID="DoorClosed"/>
            <Action ID="PassThroughWindow"/>
        </Fallback>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <TreeNodesModel>
        <Action ID="CloseDoor"/>
        <SubTree ID="DoorClosed"/>
        <Condition ID="IsDoorOpen"/>
        <Action ID="OpenDoor"/>
        <Action ID="PassThroughDoor"/>
        <Action ID="PassThroughWindow"/>
    </TreeNodesModel>
    <!-- ////////// -->
</root>
mrq: The mission describes a door traversal task that prioritizes passing through an open door, attempts to open a closed door if needed, and finally uses a window if door options fail. First, the robot will execute the following actions (IsDoorOpen, PassThroughDoor) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for IsDoorOpen. It will run the custom action PassThroughDoor only after the condition IsDoorOpen returns success. Next, it will execute the following actions (door_open_sequence, DoorClosed, PassThroughWindow) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will run the door_open_sequence first and will succeed immediately if the door is already open and traversal through the door completes. It will run the DoorClosed subtree next if the door_open_sequence fails due to the door being closed or traversal failing. It will run the custom action PassThroughWindow if both prior actions fail. Finally, it will execute the following actions (Inverter(IsDoorOpen), RetryUntilSuccess(OpenDoor), PassThroughDoor, CloseDoor) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute inverted IsDoorOpen and will reverse success to failure and failure to success to require that the door is closed before proceeding. It will keep trying to execute the custom action OpenDoor until it succeeds and will then continue. It will run the custom action PassThroughDoor after the door has been opened. It will run the custom action CloseDoor to finish the traversal through a previously closed door.

<!--
  This Behavior Tree replans the global path periodically at 1 Hz and it also has
  recovery actions.
-->
<root main_tree_to_execute="MainTree">
  <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
    <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="6" name="NavigateRecovery">
      <PipelineSequence name="NavigateWithReplanning">
        <RateController hz="1.0">
          <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="1" name="ComputePathToPose">
            <ComputePathToPose goal="{goal}" path="{path}" planner_id="GridBased"/>
            <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearGlobalCostmap-Context" service_name="global_costmap/clear_entirely_global_costmap"/>
          </RecoveryNode>
        </RateController>
        <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="1" name="FollowPath">
          <FollowPath path="{path}" controller_id="FollowPath"/>
          <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearLocalCostmap-Context" service_name="local_costmap/clear_entirely_local_costmap"/>
        </RecoveryNode>
      </PipelineSequence>
      <ReactiveFallback name="RecoveryFallback">
        <GoalUpdated/>
        <SequenceStar name="RecoveryActions">
          <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearLocalCostmap-Subtree" service_name="local_costmap/clear_entirely_local_costmap"/>
          <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearGlobalCostmap-Subtree" service_name="global_costmap/clear_entirely_global_costmap"/>
          <Spin spin_dist="1.57"/>
          <Wait wait_duration="5"/>
        </SequenceStar>
      </ReactiveFallback>
    </RecoveryNode>
  </BehaviorTree>
</root>
mrq:
The mission describes a navigation task that computes a global path at 1 Hz, follows the path, and performs layered recoveries when needed.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (RateController, RecoveryNode) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. While an action is running or has succeeded, it will go to the next action. It will execute delayed the ROS action ComputePathToPose with a 1.0 Hz rate controller. If execution succeeds, it will start the delay timer for one second and will resume execution to enforce periodic replanning. It will execute the main custom action ComputePathToPose. If it fails, it will execute recovery action the custom action ClearEntireCostmap-Context. After recovery, it will retry main action the custom action ComputePathToPose once. If the recovery action fails and retries are exhausted, it will stop execution of this replanning step.

Next, it will execute the following actions (RecoveryNode) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. While an action is running or has succeeded, it will go to the next action. It will execute the main ROS action FollowPath using the stored path with the specified controller. If it fails, it will execute recovery action the custom action ClearLocalCostmap-Context. After recovery, it will retry main action the ROS action FollowPath once. If the recovery action fails and retries are exhausted, it will stop execution of the following step.

Finally, it will execute main the previous PipelineSequence as the primary action. If it fails, it will execute recovery action the following actions (ReactiveFallback) to restore navigation. After recovery, it will retry main action the previous PipelineSequence until six retries are exhausted. If the recovery action fails and retries are exhausted, it will stop execution.

Finally, it will execute the following actions GoalUpdated, SequenceStar in order. If GoalUpdated succeeds, it will stop execution. Else it will keep executing and re-evaluating previous actions whenever an action is running. It will execute the following actions (ClearLocalCostmap-Subtree, ClearGlobalCostmap-Subtree, Spin, Wait) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will run the custom action ClearLocalCostmap-Subtree to clear the local costmap. It will run the custom action ClearGlobalCostmap-Subtree to clear the global costmap. It will run the custom action Spin with a rotation distance of 1.57 radians. It will run the custom action Wait for a duration of five seconds.

    <!--  ===================================  -->

mrq:
The mission describes a work lifecycle where the robot will fetch a job, attempt specialized or standard job routines, and manage auxiliary behaviors such as combat, hunger, thirst, sleep, training, and uniform handling.

First, it will execute the following actions (GetJob, FallbackStar) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will run the custom action GetJob to retrieve a job assignment. It will execute the following actions (JobButcherAnimal, JobDyeAnimal, JobLeadAnimalToPasture, JobHarvestAnimal, JobTameAnimal, JobHaulMultiple, JobStandard) consecutively. If any action succeeds, it will stop execution. Else it will keep executing and re-evaluating previous actions whenever an action is running.

Next, it will execute a navigation and combat routine while remembering completed steps. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for IsInCombat. It will execute the custom action AbortJob to cancel the current job before engaging. It will execute GetTarget and Move in sequence and will then check adjacency and attack within a forced success wrapper. It will execute AttackTarget only after TargetAdjacent succeeds. It will execute AttackTarget and make sure it always succeeds to allow progress even if attacks fail. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for IsInCombat to confirm combat status remains valid.

Next, it will ensure a uniform is obtained and equipped while remembering completed steps. It will execute the custom action CheckUniform to verify current uniform status. It will execute the following actions (Move, UniformCleanUp) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will execute the custom action EquipUniform to put on the uniform after preparation succeeds.

Next, it will satisfy hunger by acquiring food, reaching a dining area opportunistically, and eating while confirming fullness. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for IsHungry. It will execute the following actions (FindFood, Move, PickUpItem, ForceSuccess, Eat) in sequence while remembering completed steps. It will execute FindDining and Move in sequence and make sure it always succeeds to avoid blocking if dining cannot be found. It will execute the following actions (IsFull) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails.

Next, it will execute a butcher-animal job only if the job type matches and will otherwise abort. It will read blackboard value and compare it against a set of cases. If a case matches the blackboard value, it will execute corresponding action gating the subtree. Else if no case matches, it will execute default action gating failure. It will execute the following actions (InitAnimalJob, Move, GrabAnimal, GetItemDropPosition, Move, FinalMoveAnimal, ButcherAnimal, FinishJob) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will execute the custom action AbortJob if the main job flow fails.

Next, it will execute a dye-animal job only if the job type matches and will otherwise abort. It will read blackboard value and compare it against a set of cases. If a case matches the blackboard value, it will execute corresponding action gating the subtree. Else if no case matches, it will execute default action gating failure. It will execute the following actions (InitJob, ClaimItems, MoveAllItemsToJob, InitAnimalJob, Move, GrabAnimal, GetItemDropPosition, Move, FinalMoveAnimal, DyeAnimal, FinishJob) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will execute the custom action AbortJob if the main job flow fails.

Next, it will execute a harvest-animal job only if the job type matches and will otherwise abort. It will read blackboard value and compare it against a set of cases. If a case matches the blackboard value, it will execute corresponding action gating the subtree. Else if no case matches, it will execute default action gating failure. It will execute the following actions (InitAnimalJob, Move, GrabAnimal, HarvestAnimal, ReleaseAnimal, FinishJob) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will execute the custom action AbortJob if the main job flow fails.

Next, it will execute a haul-multiple-items job only if the job type matches and will otherwise abort. It will read blackboard value and compare it against a set of cases. If a case matches the blackboard value, it will execute corresponding action gating the subtree. Else if no case matches, it will execute default action gating failure. It will execute the following actions (InitJob, FindTool, Move, EquipTool, Inverter, AllPickedUp, GetItemDropPosition, Move, DropAllItems, FinishJob) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will execute inverted a loop where it moves and picks up items until a running status returns, thereby continuing until all items are gathered. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for AllPickedUp before proceeding to drop-off. It will execute the custom action AbortJob if the main job flow fails.

Next, it will execute a lead-animal-to-pasture job only if the job type matches and will otherwise abort. It will read blackboard value and compare it against a set of cases. If a case matches the blackboard value, it will execute corresponding action gating the subtree. Else if no case matches, it will execute default action gating failure. It will execute the following actions (InitAnimalJob, Move, GrabAnimal, GetItemDropPosition, Move, FinalMoveAnimal, ReleaseAnimal, FinishJob) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will execute the custom action AbortJob if the main job flow fails.

Next, it will execute standard jobs or abort if they fail. It will execute the following actions (InitJob, ClaimItems, FindTool, Move, EquipTool, MoveAllItemsToJob, GetWorkPosition, Move, Work, FinishJob) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will execute the following actions (AbortJob) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails.

Next, it will execute a tame-animal job only if the job type matches and will otherwise abort. It will read blackboard value and compare it against a set of cases. If a case matches the blackboard value, it will execute corresponding action gating the subtree. Else if no case matches, it will execute default action gating failure. It will execute the following actions (InitAnimalJob, Move, TameAnimal, GrabAnimal, GetItemDropPosition, Move, FinalMoveAnimal, ReleaseAnimal, FinishJob) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will execute the custom action AbortJob if the main job flow fails.

Next, it will move all required items to a job location by iterating until all items are placed. It will execute delayed checks by inverting a loop that keeps running until the placement is complete. It will execute inverted a sequence that tests if not all items are in place, then performs a pickup and drop pipeline, and then returns a running status to continue looping. It will execute the following actions (Move, PickUpItem, GetItemDropPosition, Move, DropItem) in sequence while remembering completed steps. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for AllItemsInPlaceForJob to confirm completion.

Next, it will attempt the older item-moving strategy with retries and enforced failure on abort. It will execute the following actions (AllItemsInPlaceForJob) consecutively and stop when one succeeds. If none succeed, it will start all over again until an action succeeds. It will keep trying to execute a sequence up to a specified number of attempts until it succeeds. It will execute the following actions (Move, PickUpItem, Fallback, Fallback, DropItem) in sequence while remembering completed steps. It will execute the following actions (GetItemDropPosition, DropItem) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will execute the following actions (Move, DropItem) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will execute inverted the custom action AbortJob and make sure it always fails.

Next, it will satisfy sleepiness by finding a bed, moving toward it under a permissive wrapper, and sleeping. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for IsSleepy. It will execute FindBed and Move in sequence and make sure it always succeeds to avoid blocking if a bed is unavailable. It will execute the custom action Sleep after preparation completes.

Next, it will satisfy thirst by acquiring a drink, reaching a dining area opportunistically, and drinking while confirming fullness. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for IsThirsty. It will execute the following actions (FindDrink, Move, PickUpItem, ForceSuccess, Drink) in sequence while remembering completed steps. It will execute FindDining and Move in sequence and make sure it always succeeds to avoid blocking if dining cannot be found. It will execute the following actions (IsDrinkFull) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails.

Finally, the robot will handle training as instructor and trainee in separate remembered sequences. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for IsTrainer. It will execute the following actions (FindTrainerPosition, Move, SuperviseTraining) in sequence and will remember progress across retries. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for IsTrainingTime. It will execute the following actions (FindTrainingGround, Move, Train) in sequence and will remember progress across retries.

The mission describes a patrol-and-inspect task that sets two goals, navigates between them, and captures snapshots in a fixed repetition.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (SetBlackboard, SetBlackboard, Sequence) for three cycles in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute SetBlackboard to store the first goal under the key Goal_a with the provided coordinates. It will execute SetBlackboard to store the second goal under the key Goal_b with the provided coordinates. It will execute the following actions (SubTree MoveRobot targeting Goal_a, SnapshotClient, SubTree MoveRobot targeting Goal_b, SnapshotClient) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the SubTree MoveRobot with the target set to Goal_a. It will run the custom action SnapshotClient to capture a snapshot after arriving at Goal_a. It will run the SubTree MoveRobot with the target set to Goal_b. It will run the custom action SnapshotClient to capture a snapshot after arriving at Goal_b. It will execute the Repeat for a set number of times defined as three cycles.

Next, it will execute the following actions (Nav2Client) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the ROS action Nav2Client with the goal provided by the target input to move to the specified pose.

It will, while an action is running or has succeeded, go to the next action. It will run the ROS action ComputePathToPose at 1.0 Hz under a rate controller and will produce a path to the provided goal using the GridBased planner. It will run the ROS action FollowPath with the most recently computed path using the specified controller while path computation continues at the fixed rate.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (SetBlackboard, SetBlackboard, SetBlackboard, Sequence) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will write the waypoint value 0.4;0.4;0.0;1.0 to the blackboard key Goal_a. It will write the waypoint value 0.4;-0.4;0.0;1.0 to the blackboard key Goal_b. It will write the waypoint value -0.4;-0.4;0.0;1.0 to the blackboard key Goal_c. It will then execute the following actions (SubTree: MoveRobot with target Goal_a, SubTree: MoveRobot with target Goal_b, SubTree: MoveRobot with target Goal_c) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. Next, it will execute the following actions (Sequence) for a set number of times or infinitely. It will execute Sequence for exactly 100 cycles.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (Nav2Client) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the ROS action Nav2Client with the goal read from the subtree input port named target and will treat this as a single-step sequence for MoveRobot.

mrq: The mission describes a navigation task that plans a global path at fixed distance intervals and advances along the path while allowing downstream actions to proceed when upstream actions are running or have succeeded. First, the robot will execute the following actions (DistanceController, FollowPath) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will, while an action is running or has succeeded, go to the next action. It will run the custom action DistanceController with a 1.0 meter threshold that wraps the ROS action ComputePathToPose and will trigger a new global plan each time it moves another 1.0 meter toward the goal. It will run the ROS action ComputePathToPose with the provided goal and will store the resulting path in the blackboard variable path whenever the distance threshold is met. It will run the ROS action FollowPath using the most recently stored path with the specified controller and will continue advancing even while ComputePathToPose is running due to the pipeline semantics.

<root main_tree_to_execute="MainTree">

  <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
    <Sequence name="main_sequence">
        <NavigateVehicle/>
        <Sequence name="main_manipulator_sequence">
            <Fallback name="find_object">
                <Sequence name="search_frame1">
                    <Sequence name="search1">
                        <PlanManipulatorPath plan_to_frame="search1"/>
                        <MoveManipulator path="{manipulator_path}"/>
                    </Sequence>
                    <ObjectSearch/>
                </Sequence>
                <Sequence name="search_frame2">
                    <Sequence name="search2">
                        <PlanManipulatorPath plan_to_frame="search2"/>
                        <MoveManipulator path="{manipulator_path}"/>
                    </Sequence>
                    <ObjectSearch/>
                </Sequence>
                <Sequence name="search_frame3">
                    <Sequence name="search3">
                        <PlanManipulatorPath plan_to_frame="search3"/>
                        <MoveManipulator path="{manipulator_path}"/>
                    </Sequence>
                    <ObjectSearch/>
                </Sequence>
            </Fallback>
            <Sequence name="handle_object">
                <Fallback name="handle_not_able_to_close">
                    <Sequence name="pick_object">
                        <Sequence name="main_move_to_object">
                            <PlanManipulatorPath plan_to_frame="object" object_pose="{pose}"/>
                            <MoveManipulator path="{manipulator_path}"/>
                        </Sequence>
                        <MoveGripper action="close"/>
                    </Sequence>
                    <ForceFailure name="force_failure1">
                        <Sequence name="movetodrive">
                            <PlanManipulatorPath plan_to_frame="driveposition"/>
                            <MoveManipulator path="{manipulator_path}"/>
                        </Sequence>
                    </ForceFailure>
                </Fallback>
                <Sequence name="leave_object">
                    <Fallback name="find_empty_frame">
                        <Fallback name="check_empty_frame">
                            <EmptyFrame check_frame="carryarea1" empty_frame="{empty_frame}"/>
                            <EmptyFrame check_frame="carryarea2"/>
                            <EmptyFrame check_frame="carryarea3"/>
                        </Fallback>
                        <ForceFailure name="force_failure2">
                            <MoveGripper action="open"/>
                        </ForceFailure>
                    </Fallback>
                    <Sequence name="emptyframe">
                        <PlanManipulatorPath plan_to_frame="{empty_frame}"/>
                        <MoveManipulator path="{manipulator_path}"/>
                    </Sequence>
                    <MoveGripper action="open"/>
                    <Sequence name="drive">
                        <PlanManipulatorPath plan_to_frame="driveposition"/>
                        <MoveManipulator path="{manipulator_path}"/>
                    </Sequence>
                </Sequence>
            </Sequence>
        </Sequence>
    </Sequence>
  </BehaviorTree> 
</root>
mrq:
The mission describes a mobile manipulation task that navigates to a work area, searches for an object across multiple frames, grasps the object, finds an empty placement frame, places the object, and returns the manipulator to a driving position.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (NavigateVehicle, main_manipulator_sequence) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action NavigateVehicle to reach the designated work area before starting manipulation.

Next, it will execute the following actions (find_object, handle_object) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will search for the object first and will handle the object only after the search succeeds.

Next, it will execute the following actions (search_frame1, search_frame2, search_frame3) consecutively. If any action (a search frame) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will complete one search frame successfully to finish the object search.

Next, it will execute the following actions (search1, ObjectSearch) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute the following actions (PlanManipulatorPath, MoveManipulator) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action PlanManipulatorPath with the target frame set to search1 and will store the resulting manipulator path. It will run the custom action MoveManipulator using the stored manipulator path after the plan succeeds. It will run the custom action ObjectSearch to detect the target object in the search1 frame.

Next, it will execute the following actions (search2, ObjectSearch) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute the following actions (PlanManipulatorPath, MoveManipulator) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action PlanManipulatorPath with the target frame set to search2 and will store the resulting manipulator path. It will run the custom action MoveManipulator using the stored manipulator path after the plan succeeds. It will run the custom action ObjectSearch to detect the target object in the search2 frame.

Next, it will execute the following actions (search3, ObjectSearch) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute the following actions (PlanManipulatorPath, MoveManipulator) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action PlanManipulatorPath with the target frame set to search3 and will store the resulting manipulator path. It will run the custom action MoveManipulator using the stored manipulator path after the plan succeeds. It will run the custom action ObjectSearch to detect the target object in the search3 frame.

Next, it will execute the following actions (handle_not_able_to_close, leave_object) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will attempt to grasp the object first and will proceed to place and stow only after the grasp phase succeeds.

Next, it will execute the following actions (pick_object, force_failure1) consecutively. If any action (pick_object) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will attempt to pick the object and will use the next action only if the pick fails.

Next, it will execute the following actions (main_move_to_object, MoveGripper) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute the following actions (PlanManipulatorPath, MoveManipulator) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action PlanManipulatorPath with the target frame set to object and with the object_pose provided, and will store the resulting manipulator path. It will run the custom action MoveManipulator using the stored manipulator path after the plan succeeds. It will run the custom action MoveGripper with the action set to close to grasp the object.

Next, it will execute the custom action Sequence named movetodrive and make sure it always fails. It will execute the following actions (PlanManipulatorPath, MoveManipulator) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action PlanManipulatorPath with the target frame set to driveposition and will store the resulting manipulator path. It will run the custom action MoveManipulator using the stored manipulator path after the plan succeeds.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (find_empty_frame, emptyframe, MoveGripper, drive) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will first locate an empty placement frame, then move to that frame, then open the gripper to release, and then stow to the driving position.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (check_empty_frame, force_failure2) consecutively. If any action (check_empty_frame) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will attempt to find an empty frame and will use the next action only if the checks fail.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (EmptyFrame, EmptyFrame, EmptyFrame) consecutively. If any action (an EmptyFrame check) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will run the custom action EmptyFrame with the check_frame set to carryarea1 and will store the resulting empty_frame. It will run the custom action EmptyFrame with the check_frame set to carryarea2. It will run the custom action EmptyFrame with the check_frame set to carryarea3.

Finally, it will execute the custom action MoveGripper with the action set to open and make sure it always fails. It will force failure to trigger fallback selection of a previously found empty frame.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (PlanManipulatorPath, MoveManipulator) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action PlanManipulatorPath with the target frame set to the stored empty_frame and will store the resulting manipulator path. It will run the custom action MoveManipulator using the stored manipulator path after the plan succeeds.

Finally, it will run the custom action MoveGripper with the action set to open to release the object.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (PlanManipulatorPath, MoveManipulator) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action PlanManipulatorPath with the target frame set to driveposition and will store the resulting manipulator path. It will run the custom action MoveManipulator using the stored manipulator path after the plan succeeds.

The mission describes a pursuit-and-engagement task that first seeks a target, then moves toward it, and finally attacks when adjacent, with a fallback to random movement if the main pursuit fails. First, the robot will execute the following actions (GetTarget, Move, Sequence) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will run the custom action GetTarget to select a target to pursue. It will run the custom action Move to advance toward the selected target after the target selection succeeds. It will execute the following actions (TargetAdjacent, AttackTarget) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for TargetAdjacent. It will run the custom action AttackTarget if the adjacency check succeeds.

Next, it will execute the following actions (SequenceStar, RandomMove) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will treat the previous sequence with memory as a single action that must succeed to complete the fallback. It will run the custom action RandomMove if the sequence with memory does not succeed.

BT:
<root main_tree_to_execute="MainTree">
	<BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
		<Repeat num_cycles="10">
			<Sequence>
				<Delay delay_msec="1000">
					<PoseDetectionControlAction enable="true" />
				</Delay>
				<TrackAction mode="Track" rate="3" detect_voice="0" turn_base="0" />

				<!-- Spin a bit to get localized in the case the initial pose was just set -->
				<RobotSpin angle="-180" velocity="0.4" />
				<RobotSpin angle="180" velocity="0.4" />

				<RobotFindInitAction game_file="robot_find.json" object_type="shapes" random_order="0" needs_init="{needs_init}" robot_pose="{robot_pose}" />

				<!--
				<ForceSuccess>
					<Sequence>
						<BlackboardCheckInt value_A="{needs_init}" value_B="0" return_on_mismatch="FAILURE">
							<Sequence>
								<TextToSpeechActionClient msg="Do you want to setup the game?" />
								<SpeechToTextActionClient text="{said}" />
								<TextCompareAction text="{said}" ck_for=".*(yes|yeah|okay|ok|sure|yep).*" />
								<SetBlackboard output_key="needs_init" value="1" />
							</Sequence>
  					</BlackboardCheckInt>
					</Sequence>
				</ForceSuccess>
        -->

				<Fallback>
					<BlackboardCheckInt value_A="{needs_init}" value_B="1" return_on_mismatch="FAILURE">
						<Sequence>
							<TrackAction mode="Track" rate="3" detect_voice="0" turn_base="1" />
							<TextToSpeechActionClient msg="Game setup. I will ask you to stand at each of the locations.  Put both hands on your hips when you arrive at each location to continue." />
							<ForceSuccess>
								<Sequence>
									<TextToSpeechActionClient msg="Please send in the middle of the game. After the robot aligns to you, put both hands on your hips." />
									<RetryUntilSuccesful num_attempts="20">
										<Delay delay_msec="2000">
											<HumanPoseDetect expected_pose_left="OnHip" expected_pose_right="OnHip" pose_lr_check="both" detected_person="{detected_person}" pose_left_speech="{pose_left_speech}" pose_right_speech="{pose_right_speech}" />
										</Delay>
									</RetryUntilSuccesful>
									<TrackAction mode="Track" rate="3" detect_voice="0" turn_base="0" />
								</Sequence>
							</ForceSuccess>

							<ForceSuccess>
								<KeepRunningUntilFailure>
									<Sequence>
										<RobotFindNextStepAction item_name="{item_name}" />
										<TextToSpeechActionClient msg="Please stand on " msg2="{item_name}" />
										<RetryUntilSuccesful num_attempts="20">
											<Delay delay_msec="2000">
												<Sequence>
													<HumanPoseDetect expected_pose_left="OnHip" expected_pose_right="OnHip" pose_lr_check="both" detected_person="{detected_person}" pose_left_speech="{pose_left_speech}" pose_right_speech="{pose_right_speech}" />
													<ObjectLocationStatusAction x="{x}" y="{y}" />
													<RobotFindSetPositionAction x="{x}" y="{y}" />
												</Sequence>
											</Delay>
										</RetryUntilSuccesful>
									</Sequence>
								</KeepRunningUntilFailure>
							</ForceSuccess>
							<TextToSpeechActionClient msg="Finished setting up" />
							<TrackAction mode="Track" rate="3" detect_voice="0" turn_base="0" />
						</Sequence>
					</BlackboardCheckInt>

					<Sequence>
						<Nav2Client goal="{robot_pose}" />
						<TextToSpeechActionClient msg="Let's play the game!" />

						<TrackAction mode="Track" rate="3" detect_voice="0" turn_base="0" />
						<ForceSuccess>
							<KeepRunningUntilFailure>
								<Sequence>
									<RobotFindNextStepAction item_name="{item_name}" />
									<TextToSpeechActionClient msg="Please stand on " msg2="{item_name}" />
									<RetryUntilSuccesful num_attempts="200">
										<Delay delay_msec="1000">
											<Sequence>
												<ObjectLocationStatusAction x="{x}" y="{y}" />
												<RobotFindCheckStepAction x="{x}" y="{y}" />
												<TextToSpeechActionClient msg="Good job" />
											</Sequence>
										</Delay>
									</RetryUntilSuccesful>
								</Sequence>
							</KeepRunningUntilFailure>
						</ForceSuccess>
						<TextToSpeechActionClient msg="Finished playing the game" />
						<TrackAction mode="Track" rate="3" detect_voice="0" turn_base="0" />
					</Sequence>
				</Fallback>
			</Sequence>
		</Repeat>
	</BehaviorTree>
</root>
mrq:
The mission describes an interactive “robot find” game that repeats a guided setup and play routine up to ten times, beginning with perception enablement, localization spins, initialization, conditional setup, and gameplay.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (PoseDetectionControlAction, TrackAction, RobotSpin, RobotSpin, RobotFindInitAction) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute delayed PoseDetectionControlAction and will enable pose detection after a one-second delay. It will run the ROS action TrackAction in Track mode at rate 3 without voice detection and without turning the base. It will run the ROS action RobotSpin to rotate negative 180 degrees at velocity 0.4. It will run the ROS action RobotSpin to rotate positive 180 degrees at velocity 0.4. It will run the custom action RobotFindInitAction to load the game file with object type shapes in fixed order and will store whether initialization is needed and the robot pose.

Next, it will execute the following actions (BlackboardCheckInt, Sequence) consecutively. If any action fails, move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule that needs_init equals 1. It will execute the following actions (TrackAction, TextToSpeechActionClient, ForceSuccess, ForceSuccess, TextToSpeechActionClient, TrackAction) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the ROS action TrackAction in Track mode at rate 3 without voice detection and with base turning enabled. It will run the ROS action TextToSpeechActionClient to announce the game setup instructions. It will execute TextToSpeechActionClient, RetryUntilSuccesful, and TrackAction and make sure it always succeeds. It will run the ROS action TextToSpeechActionClient to ask the participant to stand in the middle and place both hands on hips after alignment. It will keep trying to execute a delayed HumanPoseDetect until it succeeds. It will execute delayed HumanPoseDetect and will start a two-second delay before checking for both hands on hips and storing detected person and pose speech, and it will keep retrying up to the provided limit until success. It will run the ROS action TrackAction in Track mode at rate 3 without voice detection and without turning the base. It will execute KeepRunningUntilFailure of a Sequence and make sure it always succeeds. It will execute RobotFindNextStepAction, TextToSpeechActionClient, and RetryUntilSuccesful in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. While an action is running or has succeeded, it will restart the sequence item due to KeepRunningUntilFailure only when a failure occurs. It will run the custom action RobotFindNextStepAction to fetch the next item name. It will run the ROS action TextToSpeechActionClient to ask the participant to stand on the named item. It will keep trying to execute a delayed Sequence until it succeeds. It will execute delayed Sequence and will start a two-second delay before running HumanPoseDetect, ObjectLocationStatusAction, and RobotFindSetPositionAction. It will run the ROS action HumanPoseDetect to confirm both hands on hips and will store detected person and pose speech. It will run the custom action ObjectLocationStatusAction to fetch coordinates x and y. It will run the custom action RobotFindSetPositionAction to record the position for the named item. It will run the ROS action TextToSpeechActionClient to announce that setup is finished. It will run the ROS action TrackAction in Track mode at rate 3 without voice detection and without turning the base.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (Sequence) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the ROS action Nav2Client to navigate to the stored robot pose. It will run the ROS action TextToSpeechActionClient to announce the start of the game. It will run the ROS action TrackAction in Track mode at rate 3 without voice detection and without turning the base. It will execute KeepRunningUntilFailure of a Sequence and make sure it always succeeds. It will execute RobotFindNextStepAction, TextToSpeechActionClient, and RetryUntilSuccesful in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action RobotFindNextStepAction to fetch the next item name for gameplay. It will run the ROS action TextToSpeechActionClient to ask the participant to stand on the named item. It will keep trying to execute a delayed Sequence until it succeeds. It will execute delayed Sequence and will start a one-second delay before running ObjectLocationStatusAction, RobotFindCheckStepAction, and TextToSpeechActionClient. It will run the custom action ObjectLocationStatusAction to fetch coordinates x and y for the current check. It will run the custom action RobotFindCheckStepAction to verify the participant stands on the correct location. It will run the ROS action TextToSpeechActionClient to give positive feedback. It will run the ROS action TextToSpeechActionClient to announce the end of the game. It will run the ROS action TrackAction in Track mode at rate 3 without voice detection and without turning the base.

<?xml version="1.0"?>
<root main_tree_to_execute="MainTree">
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
        <Repeat num_cycles="99">
            <Fallback name="get_answer">
                <Sequence>
                    <Delay delay_msec="3000">
                        <Action ID="TextToSpeechActionClient" msg="What's your name?"/>
                    </Delay>
                    <Action ID="SpeechToTextActionClient" text="{said}"/>
                    <Action ID="TextToSpeechActionClient" msg="You said"/>
                    <Action ID="TextToSpeechActionClient" msg="{said}"/>
                </Sequence>
                <Action ID="TextToSpeechActionClient" msg="I'm sorry, but I didn't hear a response"/>
            </Fallback>
        </Repeat>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <TreeNodesModel>
        <Action ID="SpeechToTextActionClient">
            <output_port name="text" type="std::string"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="TextToSpeechActionClient">
            <input_port name="msg" type="std::string"/>
        </Action>
    </TreeNodesModel>
    <!-- ////////// -->
</root>
mrq:
The mission describes an interaction loop that asks for a user’s name, listens for a reply, and speaks back the heard content or a fallback message.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (Fallback) consecutively. It will execute the following actions (Sequence, TextToSpeechActionClient) consecutively. If any action (Sequence) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution will fail.

Next, it will execute the following actions (Delay, SpeechToTextActionClient, TextToSpeechActionClient, TextToSpeechActionClient) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute delayed TextToSpeechActionClient and make sure the wait occurs before proceeding. It will run the ROS action TextToSpeechActionClient with the message "What's your name?" and start the delay timer for 3000 milliseconds before continuing. It will run the ROS action SpeechToTextActionClient and store the recognized speech in the blackboard entry named said. It will run the ROS action TextToSpeechActionClient with the message "You said". It will run the ROS action TextToSpeechActionClient with the message sourced from the blackboard entry named said.

Finally, it will execute the ROS action TextToSpeechActionClient and speak "I'm sorry, but I didn't hear a response". It will execute the following actions (Sequence, TextToSpeechActionClient) consecutively. If any action (TextToSpeechActionClient) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution will fail.

Finally, it will execute Repeat for a set number of times or infinitely. It will execute the Fallback subtree for 99 cycles in total.

BT:
<root main_tree_to_execute = "MainTree">
    <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
        <Sequence>
        <Repeat num_cycles="1">
            <Fallback name="get_answer">
              <Sequence>
                 <TextToSpeechActionClient msg="Hi there" />
                 <Delay delay_msec="500">
                   <SmileAction level="3" duration_ms="2000"/>
                 </Delay>
                 <Delay delay_msec="2000">
                   <TextToSpeechActionClient msg="What's your name?" />
                 </Delay>
                 <SpeechToTextActionClient text="{said}" />
                 <TextToSpeechActionClient msg="You said" />
                 <TextToSpeechActionClient msg="{said}" />
              </Sequence>
              <ForceFailure>
                <HeadTiltAction angle="45" dwell_ms="1500" />
              </ForceFailure>
              <TextToSpeechActionClient msg="I'm sorry, but I didn't you" />
            </Fallback>
        </Repeat>
        <TextToSpeechActionClient msg="Goodbye" />
        </Sequence>
    </BehaviorTree>
</root>
mrq:
The mission describes a social interaction task that greets a person, attempts to capture their spoken name, provides a spoken acknowledgment, and then ends the interaction.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (Repeat, TextToSpeechActionClient) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute Repeat for a set number of times or infinitely. It will execute the ROS action TextToSpeechActionClient with the message Goodbye after the repeated interaction finishes.

Next, it will execute the following actions (Fallback) consecutively. If any action (Sequence) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails.

Then, it will execute the following actions (TextToSpeechActionClient, Delay, Delay, SpeechToTextActionClient, TextToSpeechActionClient, TextToSpeechActionClient) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the ROS action TextToSpeechActionClient with the message Hi there. It will execute delayed the custom action SmileAction and will start a delay timer of 500 milliseconds after the smile execution succeeds and will resume execution after the delay period is exhausted. It will execute delayed the ROS action TextToSpeechActionClient with the message What's your name? and will start a delay timer of 2000 milliseconds after the speech execution succeeds and will resume execution after the delay period is exhausted. It will run the ROS action SpeechToTextActionClient and will store the recognized text in the blackboard entry said. It will run the ROS action TextToSpeechActionClient with the message You said. It will run the ROS action TextToSpeechActionClient with the message from the blackboard entry said.

Next, it will execute the custom action HeadTiltAction and make sure it always succeeds. It will tilt its head to angle 45 and will hold the pose for 1500 milliseconds.

Finally, it will run the ROS action TextToSpeechActionClient with the message I'm sorry, but I didn't you.

<root main_tree_to_execute="MainTree">
	<BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
		<Sequence>
			<Delay delay_msec="4000">
        <TrackAction mode="Scan" rate="3" />
			</Delay>
      <Delay delay_msec="10000">
        <TrackAction mode="Off" rate="3" />
      </Delay>
      <Delay delay_msec="4000">
        <TrackAction mode="Track" rate="3" />
      </Delay>
		</Sequence>

	</BehaviorTree>
</root>
mrq: The mission describes a sensing task that performs three timed tracking modes in strict order. First, the robot will execute the following actions (Delay, Delay, Delay) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute delayed the custom action TrackAction in Scan mode at rate 3. If execution succeeds, it will start a delay timer of 4000 milliseconds and will resume execution after the delay period is exhausted. Next, it will execute delayed the custom action TrackAction in Off mode at rate 3. If execution succeeds, it will start a delay timer of 10000 milliseconds and will resume execution after the delay period is exhausted. Finally, it will execute delayed the custom action TrackAction in Track mode at rate 3. If execution succeeds, it will start a delay timer of 4000 milliseconds and will resume execution after the delay period is exhausted.

mrq: The mission describes a pick-and-place acquisition task that checks battery, prepares the gripper, approaches an object, and grasps it in strict order. First, the robot will execute the following actions (CheckBattery, OpenGripper, ApproachObject, CloseGripper) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action CheckBattery to verify that the power level meets the required threshold before any manipulation begins. It will run the custom action OpenGripper to ensure the end-effector is open and ready to grasp after the battery check succeeds. It will run the custom action ApproachObject to move to the target object’s grasp pose while maintaining approach alignment after the gripper is open. It will run the custom action CloseGripper to secure the object firmly after the approach completes successfully.

BT:
<root main_tree_to_execute="MainTree">
  <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
    <Sequence name="root">
      <FallbackStar name="AutoLocalization">
        <initialPoseReceived/>
        <SequenceStar name="doSelfLocalization">
          <RetryUntilSuccesful num_attempts="5" name="retry_client_request">
            <globalLocalizationServiceRequest/>
         </RetryUntilSuccesful>
         <RetryUntilSuccesful num_attempts="10" name="retry_localization">
            <Sequence>
              <Fallback>
                <IsLocalized/>
                <SequenceStar>
                 <Spin/>
                <BackUp/>
                 <Spin/>
              </SequenceStar>
             </Fallback>
             <IsLocalized/>
            </Sequence> 
          </RetryUntilSuccesful>
        </SequenceStar>
      </FallbackStar>
      <FallbackStar name="check_motion">
        <Inverter name="is_not_stuck">
          <IsStuck/>
        </Inverter>
        <SequenceStar name="stuck_recovery">
          <Stop/>
          <BackUp/>
          <Spin/>
        </SequenceStar>
      </FallbackStar>
      <SequenceStar name="navigate">
        <ComputePathToPose endpoints="${endpoints}" path="${path}"/>
        <ParallelNode threshold="1">
          <FollowPath path="${path}"/>
          <Sequence>
            <RateController hz="2">
              <ComputePathToPose endpoints="${endpoints}" path="${path}"/>
            </RateController>
            <UpdatePath/>
          </Sequence>
        </ParallelNode>
      </SequenceStar>
    </Sequence>
  </BehaviorTree>
</root>
mrq:
The mission describes a mobile robot that will establish its initial pose, ensure it is not stuck, and perform dynamic path planning and navigation under recovery-aware control.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (initialPoseReceived, doSelfLocalization) consecutively. If any action (initialPoseReceived) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, it will start all over again until an action succeeds. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for the condition that an initial pose has been received. It will execute the following actions (retry_client_request, retry_localization) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will keep trying to execute the custom action globalLocalizationServiceRequest until it succeeds. It will keep trying to execute the following actions (unnamed Sequence) until they succeed. It will execute the following actions (unnamed Fallback, IsLocalized) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute the following actions (IsLocalized, unnamed SequenceStar) consecutively. If any action (IsLocalized) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will execute the following actions (Spin, BackUp, Spin) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for the condition that localization has succeeded.

Next, it will execute the following actions (is_not_stuck, stuck_recovery) consecutively. If any action (is_not_stuck) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, it will start all over again until an action succeeds. It will execute inverted IsStuck. If IsStuck succeeds, it will reverse it to fail. Else if IsStuck fails, it will reverse it to succeed. It will execute the following actions (Stop, BackUp, Spin) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (ComputePathToPose, unnamed ParallelNode) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will run the ROS action ComputePathToPose with the provided endpoints and will store the resulting path. It will execute the following actions (FollowPath, unnamed Sequence) simultaneously. If either a success threshold is reached or all actions succeed, the execution was successful. Else if any action fails, the execution fails. It will run the ROS action FollowPath using the stored path. It will execute the following actions (RateController, ComputePathToPose, UpdatePath) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. While an action is running or has succeeded, it will go to the next action. It will execute the custom action RateController. It will run the ROS action ComputePathToPose with the provided endpoints and will store the updated path. It will execute the custom action UpdatePath to apply the updated path.

<root main_tree_to_execute="MainTree">
  <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
    <ReactiveFallback name="FollowWaypoints">
      <AllGoalsAchieved goal_achieved="{goal_achieved}"/>
      <KeepRunningUntilFailure>
        <ReactiveSequence>
          <GetNextGoal goals="{goals}" goal="{goal}" goal_achieved="{goal_achieved}"/>
          <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="1">
            <NavigateToPose goal="{goal}"/>
            <Wait wait_duration="5"/>
          </RecoveryNode>
          <SetBlackboard output_key="goal_achieved" value="true"/>
        </ReactiveSequence>
      </KeepRunningUntilFailure>
    </ReactiveFallback>
  </BehaviorTree>
</root>
mrq:
The mission describes a waypoint-following task that repeatedly attempts to navigate to each goal, waits once upon failure, and marks progress until all goals are achieved.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (AllGoalsAchieved, KeepRunningUntilFailure) in order. It will execute the following actions AllGoalsAchieved, KeepRunningUntilFailure in order. If AllGoalsAchieved succeeds, it will stop execution. Else it will keep executing and re-evaluating previous actions whenever an action is running.

Next, it will execute GetNextGoal, RecoveryNode, SetBlackboard in sequence. If any action fails, it will stop execution. While an action is still running, it will re-evaluate previous actions. It will run the custom action GetNextGoal to read from goals, to output a single goal, and to update goal_achieved. It will execute the main action NavigateToPose with the provided goal. If it fails, it will execute the recovery action Wait with a wait_duration of 5. After recovery, it will retry the main action NavigateToPose once. If recovery fails and retries are exhausted, it will stop execution. It will run the custom action SetBlackboard and will set goal_achieved to true after the navigation step completes.

Finally, it will execute GetNextGoal, RecoveryNode, SetBlackboard. If GetNextGoal and RecoveryNode succeed, it will restart them. Else if either fails, it will stop looping and continue execution.
