<?xml version="1.0"?>
<root main_tree_to_execute="BehaviorTree">
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="BehaviorTree">
        <Fallback>
            <ReactiveSequence>
                <Condition ID="PullOver_Request"/>
                <Condition ID="PullOver_Ready"/>
                <Action ID="PullOver_Plan" output="{output}"/>
            </ReactiveSequence>
            <ReactiveSequence>
                <Condition ID="PullOut_Request"/>
                <Condition ID="PullOut_Ready"/>
                <Action ID="PullOut_Plan" output="{output}"/>
            </ReactiveSequence>
            <ReactiveSequence>
                <Condition ID="LaneChange_Request"/>
                <Fallback>
                    <Condition ID="LaneChange_Ready"/>
                    <Condition ID="ForceLaneChange_ForceApproval"/>
                </Fallback>
                <Action ID="LaneChange_Plan" output="{output}"/>
            </ReactiveSequence>
            <Action ID="LaneFollowing_Plan" output="{output}"/>
        </Fallback>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="a">
        <ReactiveFallback>
            <Condition ID="LaneChange_CheckApproval"/>
            <KeepRunningUntilFailure>
                <Action ID="LaneChange_PlanCandidate" output="{output}"/>
            </KeepRunningUntilFailure>
        </ReactiveFallback>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="b">
        <ReactiveFallback>
            <Condition ID="Avoidance_CheckApproval"/>
            <KeepRunningUntilFailure>
                <Action ID="Avoidance_PlanCandidate" output="{output}"/>
            </KeepRunningUntilFailure>
        </ReactiveFallback>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <TreeNodesModel>
        <Condition ID="Avoidance_CheckApproval"/>
        <Action ID="Avoidance_Plan">
            <output_port name="output" type="behavior_path_planner::BehaviorModuleOutput">desc</output_port>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="Avoidance_PlanCandidate">
            <output_port name="output" type="behavior_path_planner::BehaviorModuleOutput">desc</output_port>
        </Action>
        <Condition ID="Avoidance_Ready"/>
        <Condition ID="Avoidance_Request"/>
        <Condition ID="ExternalApproval"/>
        <Condition ID="ForceApproval"/>
        <Condition ID="ForceLaneChange_ForceApproval"/>
        <Condition ID="LaneChange_CheckApproval"/>
        <Action ID="LaneChange_Plan">
            <output_port name="output" type="behavior_path_planner::BehaviorModuleOutput">desc</output_port>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="LaneChange_PlanCandidate">
            <output_port name="output" type="behavior_path_planner::BehaviorModuleOutput">desc</output_port>
        </Action>
        <Condition ID="LaneChange_Ready"/>
        <Condition ID="LaneChange_Request"/>
        <Action ID="LaneFollowing_Plan">
            <output_port name="output" type="boost::optional<autoware_planning_msgs::PathWithLaneId_<std::allocator<void> > >">desc</output_port>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="LaneFollowing_PlanCandidate">
            <output_port name="output_candidate" type="boost::optional<autoware_planning_msgs::PathWithLaneId_<std::allocator<void> > >">desc</output_port>
        </Action>
        <Condition ID="LaneFollowing_Ready"/>
        <Condition ID="LaneFollowing_Request"/>
        <Action ID="PullOut_Plan">
            <output_port name="output"/>
        </Action>
        <Condition ID="PullOut_Ready"/>
        <Condition ID="PullOut_Request"/>
        <Action ID="PullOver_Plan">
            <output_port name="output" type="behavior_path_planner::BehaviorModuleOutput">desc</output_port>
        </Action>
        <Condition ID="PullOver_Ready"/>
        <Condition ID="PullOver_Request"/>
        <Condition ID="SideShift_CheckApproval"/>
        <Action ID="SideShift_Plan">
            <output_port name="output" type="behavior_path_planner::BehaviorModuleOutput">desc</output_port>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="SideShift_PlanCandidate">
            <output_port name="output" type="behavior_path_planner::BehaviorModuleOutput">desc</output_port>
        </Action>
        <Condition ID="SideShift_Ready"/>
        <Condition ID="SideShift_Request"/>
        <SubTree ID="SubTree"/>
        <SubTree ID="a"/>
        <SubTree ID="b"/>
    </TreeNodesModel>
    <!-- ////////// -->
</root>
mrq:
The mission describes a driving behavior that prioritizes pull over, pull out, lane change with approval or forced approval, and lane following planning using a fallback structure. First, the robot will execute the following actions (PullOver_Request check, PullOver_Ready check, PullOver_Plan) in sequence. It will execute the following actions (PullOver_Request check, PullOver_Ready check, PullOver_Plan) in order while re-evaluating earlier checks as later actions run. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for PullOver_Request. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for PullOver_Ready. It will run the custom action PullOver_Plan and will write its output to output when both conditions succeed. Next, it will execute the following actions (PullOut_Request check, PullOut_Ready check, PullOut_Plan) in sequence. It will execute the following actions (PullOut_Request check, PullOut_Ready check, PullOut_Plan) in order while re-evaluating earlier checks as later actions run. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for PullOut_Request. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for PullOut_Ready. It will run the custom action PullOut_Plan and will write its output to output when both conditions succeed. Next, it will execute the following actions (LaneChange_Request check, LaneChange approval check, LaneChange_Plan) in sequence. It will execute the following actions (LaneChange_Request check, LaneChange approval check, LaneChange_Plan) in order while re-evaluating earlier checks as later actions run. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for LaneChange_Request. It will execute the following actions (LaneChange_Ready check, ForceLaneChange_ForceApproval check) consecutively. If any action (LaneChange_Ready check) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will run the custom action LaneChange_Plan and will write its output to output when either readiness or forced approval succeeds. Finally, it will run the custom action LaneFollowing_Plan and will write its output to output if all prior actions fail.

The mission describes a lane change candidate planning behavior that persistently generates candidates until approval arrives. First, it will execute the following actions (LaneChange_CheckApproval check, LaneChange_PlanCandidate loop) in order. If LaneChange_CheckApproval check succeeds, it will stop execution. Else it will keep executing and re-evaluating previous actions whenever an action is running. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for LaneChange_CheckApproval. It will execute LaneChange_PlanCandidate. If LaneChange_PlanCandidate succeeds, it will restart it. Else if LaneChange_PlanCandidate fails, it will stop looping and continue execution. It will run the custom action LaneChange_PlanCandidate and will write its output to output while approval remains absent.

The mission describes an avoidance candidate planning behavior that persistently generates candidates until approval arrives. First, it will execute the following actions (Avoidance_CheckApproval check, Avoidance_PlanCandidate loop) in order. If Avoidance_CheckApproval check succeeds, it will stop execution. Else it will keep executing and re-evaluating previous actions whenever an action is running. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for Avoidance_CheckApproval. It will execute Avoidance_PlanCandidate. If Avoidance_PlanCandidate succeeds, it will restart it. Else if Avoidance_PlanCandidate fails, it will stop looping and continue execution. It will run the custom action Avoidance_PlanCandidate and will write its output to output while approval remains absent.

mrq:
The mission describes a patrol and fallback navigation task that will attempt a reactive multi-goal route up to three times and will then try a single fallback goal if the patrol does not succeed.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (Repeat, Sequence) consecutively. If any action (Repeat) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution will fail. It will execute Repeat for a set number of times or infinitely.

Next, it will execute the following actions (ReactiveSequence) for a set number of times or infinitely. It will execute the following actions [CheckBattery, Sequence] in sequence. If [CheckBattery] fails, it will stop execution. While [Sequence] is still running, it will re-evaluate previous actions.

Next, it will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule. It will then execute the following actions (MoveBase, MoveBase, MoveBase, MoveBase) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. While an action is running or has succeeded, it will go to the next action. It will run the ROS action MoveBase to the goal -3.1;-0.2;0.0 when the battery check succeeds. It will run the ROS action MoveBase to the goal 0.7;1.1;0.0 after the first MoveBase starts or succeeds. It will run the ROS action MoveBase to the goal -3;-2;0.0 after the second MoveBase starts or succeeds. It will run the ROS action MoveBase to the goal 1.0;-2;0.0 after the third MoveBase starts or succeeds.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (Sequence) consecutively. If any action (Sequence) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, it will start all over again until an action succeeds. It will execute the following actions (MoveBase) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the ROS action MoveBase to the goal 1.0;-1;0.0 as the fallback navigation attempt.

        <RateController hz="1.0">

        <FollowPath path="${path}"/>

      <SequenceStar name="RecoveryActions">
        <ClearEntireCostmap service_name="/local_costmap/clear_entirely_local_costmap"/>
        <ClearEntireCostmap service_name="/global_costmap/clear_entirely_global_costmap"/>
      </SequenceStar>
    </RecoveryNode>
  </BehaviorTree>
</root>

          <Wait wait_duration="5"/>
        </SequenceStar>
      </ReactiveFallback>
    </RecoveryNode>
  </BehaviorTree>
</root> -->
mrq:
The mission describes a navigation task that computes a path to a goal at 1 Hz, follows that path, and invokes recovery when needed.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (NavigateWithReplanning) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute delayed actions by rate and will enforce a 1 Hz cycle before each planning attempt. It will execute the following actions (GoalReached, ComputePathToPose, ClearEntireCostmap) consecutively. If any action (GoalReached) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will run the ROS action GoalReached to check if the goal condition is already satisfied. It will run the ROS action ComputePathToPose with the provided goal and will store the resulting path if the goal is not yet reached. It will run the ROS action ClearEntireCostmap on the global costmap if path computation does not succeed and no prior action has succeeded. It will run the ROS action FollowPath using the stored path after the rate-controlled fallback finishes successfully.

Next, it will execute main actions and recovery actions with bounded retries. It will execute main NavigateWithReplanning. If it fails, it will execute recovery action (RecoveryActions). After recovery, it will retry main action (NavigateWithReplanning). If recovery action (RecoveryActions) fails and retries are exhausted, it will stop execution.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (RecoveryActions) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will run the ROS action ClearEntireCostmap on the local costmap to remove local obstacles. It will run the ROS action ClearEntireCostmap on the global costmap to remove global obstacles.

The mission describes a simple interaction that checks a streamed number and announces a message based on its value.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (GetFloat64, Fallback) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action GetFloat64 to read a floating-point value from the topic /numbers and will store the value as number. It will then execute the following actions (Sequence, DebugAction) consecutively. If any action in the first branch fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails.

Next, it will execute the following actions (CompareDouble, DebugAction) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule. It will check whether number is greater than or equal to 5 is true. It will run the custom action DebugAction to log the message "Number is bigger or equal to 5" after the comparison succeeds.

Finally, it will run the custom action DebugAction to log the message "Less than 5". It will run this action only if the earlier comparison fails within the preceding fallback branch.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (CheckBattery, OpenGripper, ApproachObject, CloseGripper) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action CheckBattery before any other action. It will run the custom action OpenGripper after the battery check succeeds. <?xml version="1.0"?>
<root main_tree_to_execute="BehaviorTree">
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="BehaviorTree">
        <Repeat num_cycles="{num_cycles}">
            <Sequence>
                <Fallback>
                    <Condition ID="LTLPreCheck" ltl_state_current="{ltl_state_current}" ltl_state_desired_sequence="{ltl_state_desired_sequence}" ltl_state_executed_sequence="{ltl_state_executed_sequence}"/>
                    <ForceFailure>
                        <Action ID="ReplanningRequestLevel2" ltl_state_current="{ltl_state_current}" ltl_state_executed_sequence="{ltl_state_executed_sequence}" replanning_request="{replanning_request}"/>
                    </ForceFailure>
                </Fallback>
                <ReactiveSequence>
                    <Fallback>
                        <Sequence>
                            <RetryUntilSuccesful num_attempts="3">
                                <Fallback>
                                    <Condition ID="LocomotionStatusCheck" locomotion_status="{locomotion_status}"/>
                                    <Sequence>
                                        <Action ID="RecoveryStand" locomotion_status="{locomotion_status}"/>
                                        <Delay delay_msec="2000">
                                            <Action ID="LocomotionStart" locomotion_status="{locomotion_status}"/>
                                        </Delay>
                                    </Sequence>
                                </Fallback>
                            </RetryUntilSuccesful>
                            <Action ID="FakeDetectionLevel1" replanning_fake_input="{replanning_fake_input}"/>
                        </Sequence>
                        <ForceFailure>
                            <Action ID="ReplanningRequestLevel1" ltl_state_current="{ltl_state_current}" ltl_state_executed_sequence="{ltl_state_executed_sequence}" replanning_request="{replanning_request}"/>
                        </ForceFailure>
                    </Fallback>
                    <Fallback>
                        <Condition ID="ReactiveLTLStateCheck" ltl_state_current="{ltl_state_current}" ltl_state_desired_sequence="{ltl_state_desired_sequence}"/>
                        <ForceFailure>
                            <Action ID="ReplanningRequestLevel2" ltl_state_current="{ltl_state_current}" ltl_state_executed_sequence="{ltl_state_executed_sequence}" replanning_request="{replanning_request}"/>
                        </ForceFailure>
                    </Fallback>
                    <Fallback>
                        <Action ID="FakeDetectionLevel3" replanning_fake_input="{replanning_fake_input}"/>
                        <ForceFailure>
                            <Action ID="ReplanningRequestLevel3" ltl_state_current="{ltl_state_current}" ltl_state_executed_sequence="{ltl_state_executed_sequence}" replanning_request="{replanning_request}"/>
                        </ForceFailure>
                    </Fallback>
                    <Fallback>
                        <Switch6 case_1="move" case_2="stay" case_3="pick" case_4="drop" case_5="start_training" case_6="terminate_training" variable="{bt_action_type}">
                            <Action ID="MoveAction" bt_action_type="{bt_action_type}" current_action="{current_action}" goal_sent="{goal_sent}" move_base_finished="{move_base_finished}" move_base_idle="{move_base_idle}"/>
                            <Action ID="StayAction" bt_action_type="{bt_action_type}" current_action="{current_action}" ltl_state_current="{ltl_state_current}"/>
                            <Action ID="PickAction" bt_action_type="{bt_action_type}" current_action="{current_action}" ltl_state_current="{ltl_state_current}"/>
                            <Action ID="DropAction" bt_action_type="{bt_action_type}" current_action="{current_action}" ltl_state_current="{ltl_state_current}"/>
                            <Action ID="GuideAction" bt_action_type="{bt_action_type}" current_action="{current_action}" ltl_state_current="{ltl_state_current}"/>
                            <Action ID="BackNormalAction" bt_action_type="{bt_action_type}" current_action="{current_action}" ltl_state_current="{ltl_state_current}"/>
                            <AlwaysFailure/>
                        </Switch6>
                        <ForceFailure>
                            <Action ID="ReplanningRequestLevel3" ltl_state_current="{ltl_state_current}" ltl_state_executed_sequence="{ltl_state_executed_sequence}" replanning_request="{replanning_request}"/>
                        </ForceFailure>
                    </Fallback>
                </ReactiveSequence>
                <Action ID="UpdateLTL" action_sequence="{action_sequence}" action_sequence_executed="{action_sequence_executed}" bt_action_type="{bt_action_type}" current_action="{current_action}" ltl_state_desired_sequence="{ltl_state_desired_sequence}"/>
            </Sequence>
        </Repeat>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <TreeNodesModel>
        <Action ID="BackNormalAction">
            <input_port name="bt_action_type"/>
            <input_port name="current_action"/>
            <inout_port name="ltl_state_current"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="DropAction">
            <input_port name="bt_action_type"/>
            <input_port name="current_action"/>
            <inout_port name="ltl_state_current"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="FakeDetectionLevel1">
            <input_port name="replanning_fake_input"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="FakeDetectionLevel2">
            <input_port name="replanning_fake_input"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="FakeDetectionLevel3">
            <input_port name="replanning_fake_input"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="GuideAction">
            <input_port name="bt_action_type"/>
            <input_port name="current_action"/>
            <inout_port name="ltl_state_current"/>
        </Action>
        <Condition ID="LTLPreCheck">
            <input_port name="ltl_state_current"/>
            <input_port name="ltl_state_desired_sequence"/>
            <inout_port name="ltl_state_executed_sequence"/>
        </Condition>
        <Action ID="LocomotionStart">
            <input_port name="locomotion_status"/>
        </Action>
        <Condition ID="LocomotionStatusCheck">
            <input_port name="locomotion_status"/>
        </Condition>
        <Action ID="MoveAction">
            <input_port name="bt_action_type"/>
            <input_port name="current_action"/>
            <output_port name="goal_sent"/>
            <input_port name="move_base_finished"/>
            <input_port name="move_base_idle"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="PickAction">
            <input_port name="bt_action_type"/>
            <input_port name="current_action"/>
            <inout_port name="ltl_state_current"/>
        </Action>
        <Condition ID="ReactiveLTLStateCheck">
            <input_port name="ltl_state_current"/>
            <input_port name="ltl_state_desired_sequence"/>
        </Condition>
        <Action ID="RecoveryStand">
            <input_port name="locomotion_status"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="ReplanningRequestLevel1">
            <input_port name="ltl_state_current"/>
            <inout_port name="ltl_state_executed_sequence"/>
            <inout_port name="replanning_request"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="ReplanningRequestLevel2">
            <input_port name="ltl_state_current"/>
            <inout_port name="ltl_state_executed_sequence"/>
            <inout_port name="replanning_request"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="ReplanningRequestLevel3">
            <input_port name="ltl_state_current"/>
            <inout_port name="ltl_state_executed_sequence"/>
            <inout_port name="replanning_request"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="StayAction">
            <input_port name="bt_action_type"/>
            <input_port name="current_action"/>
            <inout_port name="ltl_state_current"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="SynchronizedTransitionAction">
            <input_port name="bt_action_type"/>
            <input_port name="current_action"/>
            <inout_port name="ltl_state_current"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="UpdateLTL">
            <inout_port name="action_sequence"/>
            <inout_port name="action_sequence_executed"/>
            <output_port name="bt_action_type"/>
            <output_port name="current_action"/>
            <inout_port name="ltl_state_desired_sequence"/>
        </Action>
    </TreeNodesModel>
    <!-- ////////// -->
</root>
mrq:
The mission describes a repeated task that verifies linear temporal logic prerequisites, establishes locomotion and reactive checks, dispatches task-specific actions, and updates the logic state across iterations.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (Fallback, ReactiveSequence, UpdateLTL) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute the following actions (LTLPreCheck, ForceFailure(ReplanningRequestLevel2)) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for the condition LTLPreCheck. It will execute ReplanningRequestLevel2 and make sure it always fails.

Next, it will execute the following actions [Fallback, Fallback, Fallback, Fallback] in sequence. If any action fails, it will stop execution. While an action is still running, it will re-evaluate previous actions. It will execute the following actions (Sequence, ForceFailure(ReplanningRequestLevel1)) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, it will start all over again until an action succeeds. It will execute the following actions (RetryUntilSuccessful(Fallback), FakeDetectionLevel1) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will keep trying to execute the following actions (LocomotionStatusCheck OR RecoveryStand→Delay(LocomotionStart)) until it succeeds. It will execute the following actions (LocomotionStatusCheck, Sequence(RecoveryStand, Delay(LocomotionStart))) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, it will start all over again until an action succeeds. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for the condition LocomotionStatusCheck. It will execute the following actions (RecoveryStand, Delay(LocomotionStart)) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute delayed LocomotionStart. If execution succeeds, it will start a delay timer of 2000 milliseconds. After the delay period is exhausted, it will resume execution. It will execute ReplanningRequestLevel1 and make sure it always fails. It will execute the following actions (ReactiveLTLStateCheck, ForceFailure(ReplanningRequestLevel2)) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for the condition ReactiveLTLStateCheck. It will execute ReplanningRequestLevel2 and make sure it always fails. It will execute the following actions (FakeDetectionLevel3, ForceFailure(ReplanningRequestLevel3)) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will execute ReplanningRequestLevel3 and make sure it always fails. It will execute the following actions (Switch, ForceFailure(ReplanningRequestLevel3)) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will read blackboard value and compare it against a set of cases. If a case matches the blackboard value, it will execute corresponding MoveAction, StayAction, PickAction, DropAction, GuideAction, or BackNormalAction. Else if no case matches, it will execute default AlwaysFailure. It will execute ReplanningRequestLevel3 and make sure it always fails.

Finally, it will execute the custom action UpdateLTL. It will run UpdateLTL to write the current action and the action type to the blackboard and to synchronize the desired sequence with the executed sequence. It will execute UpdateLTL after the preceding sequence succeeds. It will execute UpdateLTL once per outer iteration. It will execute UpdateLTL for each cycle governed by the external repeat controller. It will execute UpdateLTL to close the iteration with an updated logic state.

mrq: The mission describes a navigation task that computes a global path at 1 Hz and advances to following the path while replanning continues. First, the robot will execute the following actions (RateController, FollowPath) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will, while an action is running or has succeeded, go to the next action. It will run the custom action RateController at 1.0 Hz to gate the ROS action ComputePathToPose. It will run the ROS action ComputePathToPose with the provided goal and will store the resulting path at each 1.0 Hz tick. It will run the ROS action FollowPath using the most recently stored path with the specified controller while ComputePathToPose continues to update the path at the enforced rate.

        <Sequence>
            <!--room1 = (2,2)-->
            <BaseMovement x="2" y="2"/>
            <!--arm_support_position = (200,200,0,0)-->
            <ArmMovement x="200" y="200" z="0" gripper_position="0"/>

                                    msg="type [y] or [n]"
                                    value="{confirm}"/>

            <StringParameterRequest title="Patient arrived?" 
                                    msg="type [y] or [n]"
                                    value="{confirm}"/>
            <CheckBlackboard input="{confirm}" compare_to="y" />
            <!--arm_home_position = (0,0,0,0)-->
            <ArmMovement x="0" y="0" z="0" gripper_position="0"/>
            <!--waiting_area = (0,0)-->
            <BaseMovement x="0" y="0"/>
        </Sequence>

mrq: The mission describes a guided walking assistance task that moves to a preparation area, positions an arm for support, awaits confirmation to begin, escorts to a destination, confirms arrival, and returns to standby. First, the robot will execute the following actions (BaseMovement, ArmMovement, StringParameterRequest, CheckBlackboard, BaseMovement, StringParameterRequest, CheckBlackboard, ArmMovement, BaseMovement) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action BaseMovement to move its base to coordinates (2, 2). It will run the custom action ArmMovement to set its arm to position (200, 200, 0) with gripper position 0. It will run the custom action StringParameterRequest to ask the operator to start walking to the destination and will store the response in confirm. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule by comparing confirm to y. It will run the custom action BaseMovement to move its base to coordinates (5, 5) after the start confirmation succeeds. It will run the custom action StringParameterRequest to ask whether the patient has arrived and will store the response in confirm. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule by comparing confirm to y. It will run the custom action ArmMovement to set its arm to the home position (0, 0, 0) with gripper position 0 after the arrival confirmation succeeds. It will run the custom action BaseMovement to move its base to coordinates (0, 0) to return to the waiting area.

            <ComputePathToPose goal="{updated_goal}" path="{path}" planner_id="GridBased"/>
          </GoalUpdater>
         <TruncatePath distance="1.0" input_path="{path}" output_path="{truncated_path}"/>
        </Sequence>

mrq:
The mission describes a navigation task that continuously updates a moving goal, recomputes a path, truncates that path to maintain a standoff distance, and follows the truncated path with ongoing advancement.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (RateController, KeepRunningUntilFailure) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. While an action is running or has succeeded, it will go to the next action.

Next, it will execute the custom action RateController at 1.0 Hz to pace downstream updates. It will execute the following actions (Sequence) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. While an action is running or has succeeded, it will go to the next action.

Next, it will execute the following actions (GoalUpdater, TruncatePath) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action GoalUpdater, which will update the input goal and then execute the ROS action ComputePathToPose with the updated goal to produce a path. It will run the custom action TruncatePath to shorten the path to maintain a 1.0 meter distance from the moving goal using the produced path.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (FollowPath) and, if the action succeeds, restart it. Else if the action fails, it will stop looping and continue execution. It will run the ROS action FollowPath using the truncated path with the specified controller.

            <GoWaypoint name="Bottom Right" frame="1" x="0.0" y="4.0" z="-3.0" heading="0.0"/>
            <GoWaypoint name="Bottom Left" frame="1" x="0.0" y="0.0" z="-1.5" heading="0.0"/>
        </SequenceStar>
    </BehaviorTree>
 </root>
mrq:
The mission describes a multistage aerial operation that performs an arm takeoff, flies a square pattern through four waypoints, and completes a landing under a sequence with memory. First, the robot will execute the following actions (ArmTakeoff, Square, Land) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will run the custom action ArmTakeoff to ascend to an altitude of 1.5 meters before any waypoint traversal. It will run the custom action Square only after the takeoff succeeds. It will run the custom action Land only after the square traversal succeeds.

Next, it will execute the following actions (GoWaypoint, GoWaypoint, GoWaypoint, GoWaypoint) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will run the custom action GoWaypoint to reach the waypoint named Top Left in frame 1 at coordinates x 4.0, y 0.0, z -1.5 with heading 0.0. It will run the custom action GoWaypoint to reach the waypoint named Top Right in frame 1 at coordinates x 4.0, y 4.0, z -3.0 with heading 0.0 after the Top Left waypoint succeeds. It will run the custom action GoWaypoint to reach the waypoint named Bottom Right in frame 1 at coordinates x 0.0, y 4.0, z -3.0 with heading 0.0 after the Top Right waypoint succeeds. It will run the custom action GoWaypoint to reach the waypoint named Bottom Left in frame 1 at coordinates x 0.0, y 0.0, z -1.5 with heading 0.0 after the Bottom Right waypoint succeeds.

<?xml version="1.0"?>
<root main_tree_to_execute="BehaviorTree">
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="BehaviorTree">
        <Sequence name="Robot-Aubo Depalletizing Demo">
            <Sequence name="set speed for robots">
                <Action ID="ExecuteGroupSpeed" group_name="arm" name="set robot speed" service_name="robot/execute_group_speed" value="0.7"/>
            </Sequence>
            <Sequence name="add collisions for Robot">
                <Action ID="ExecuteAddPlane" group_name="arm" name="Add groud to scene" plane_name="ground" plane_normal="0 0 1" plane_pose="0 0 0 0 0 0 1" service_name="robot/execute_add_plane"/>
                <Action ID="ExecuteAddBox" auto_subfix="1" box_name="left_wall" box_pose="2.76 1.2 1.35 0 0 0 1" box_size="5.5 0.001 2.7" group_name="arm" is_absolute="1" name="Add left wall" service_name="robot/execute_add_box"/>
                <Action ID="ExecuteAddBox" auto_subfix="1" box_name="right_wall" box_pose="2.76 -1.2 1.35 0 0 0 1" box_size="5.5 0.001 2.7" group_name="arm" is_absolute="1" name="Add right wall" service_name="robot/execute_add_box"/>
                <Action ID="ExecuteAddBox" auto_subfix="1" box_name="top_wall" box_pose="2.5 0 2.7 0 0 0 1" box_size="5 2.4 0.001" group_name="arm" is_absolute="1" name="Add top wall" service_name="robot/execute_add_box"/>
                <Action ID="ExecuteAddBox" auto_subfix="1" box_name="conveyor" box_pose="-1.341 0 0.4 0 0 0 1" box_size="2.56 1.26 0.8" group_name="arm" is_absolute="1" name="Add conveyor" service_name="robot/execute_add_box"/>
                <Action ID="ExecuteAddBox" auto_subfix="0" box_name="boxsur" box_pose="0.881 0 1.35 0 0 0 1" box_size="0.001 2.4 2.7" group_name="arm" is_absolute="1" name="box_surface" service_name="robot/execute_add_box"/>
            </Sequence>
            <Sequence name="initialize robots">
                <Action ID="ExecuteGroupNamedStates" group_name="arm" name="robot initial state" service_name="robot/execute_group_named_states" state_name="left_pre"/>
            </Sequence>
            <Repeat num_cycles="100">
                <Sequence name="Depalletizing Proccess">
                    <Action ID="SenseObjectPose" category="" pose="{box_surface_pose}" service_name="sense_object_pose"/>
                    <Action ID="ExecutePlanning" category="" pick_pose="{pick_pose}" place_pose="{place_pose}" pose="{box_surface_pose}" post_middle_pose="{post_middle_pose}" post_pick_pose="{post_pick_pose}" post_pick_pose_edge="{post_pick_pose_edge}" pre_middle_pose="{pre_middle_pose}" pre_pick_pose="{pre_pick_pose}" service_name="execute_planning"/>
                    <Action ID="ExecuteGroupLinearJointStates" goal="{pre_middle_pose}" group_name="arm" name="pre middle" service_name="robot/execute_group_joint_states" tolerance="0.2"/>
                    <Action ID="ExecuteRemoveObject" is_exact="1" name="remove boxes_surface" obj_name="boxsur" service_name="robot/execute_remove_object"/>
                    <Action ID="ConnectWaypoints" connected_waypoints="{pick_motion}" name="pick motion" service_name="connect_waypoints" waypoint1="{pre_pick_pose}" waypoint2="{pick_pose}" waypoint3="" waypoint4="" waypoint5=""/>
                    <Action ID="ExecuteGroupManyPoses" constraint="" eef_step="0.1" goal_type="0" goals="{pick_motion}" group_name="arm" name="pre pick and pick" service_name="robot/execute_group_many_poses" tolerance="0.1"/>
                    <Delay delay_msec="1000" name="Grasping">
                        <AlwaysSuccess/>
                    </Delay>
                    <Action ID="ConnectWaypoints" connected_waypoints="{pull_out}" name="pull out" service_name="connect_waypoints" waypoint1="{post_pick_pose}" waypoint2="{post_pick_pose_edge}" waypoint3="" waypoint4="" waypoint5=""/>
                    <Action ID="ExecuteGroupManyPoses" constraint="" eef_step="0.1" goal_type="0" goals="{pull_out}" group_name="arm" name="post pick" service_name="robot/execute_group_many_poses" tolerance="0.1"/>
                    <Action ID="ExecuteGroupLinearJointStates" goal="{post_middle_pose}" group_name="arm" name="post middle" service_name="robot/execute_group_joint_states" tolerance="0.1"/>
                    <Action ID="ExecuteGroupLinearJointStates" goal="{place_pose}" group_name="arm" name="place" service_name="robot/execute_group_joint_states" tolerance="0.1"/>
                    <Delay delay_msec="1000" name="Droping">
                        <AlwaysSuccess/>
                    </Delay>
                    <Action ID="ExecuteAddBox" auto_subfix="0" box_name="boxsur" box_pose="0.881 0 1.35 0 0 0 1" box_size="0.001 2.4 2.7" group_name="arm" is_absolute="1" name="box_surface" service_name="robot/execute_add_box"/>
                    <Action ID="ExecuteGroupLinearJointStates" goal="{pre_middle_pose}" group_name="arm" name="back to pre middle" service_name="robot/execute_group_joint_states" tolerance="0.1"/>
                </Sequence>
            </Repeat>
        </Sequence>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <TreeNodesModel>
        <Action ID="ConnectWaypoints">
            <output_port name="connected_waypoints">PoseArray</output_port>
            <input_port default="connect_waypoints" name="service_name"/>
            <input_port name="waypoint1">Pose</input_port>
            <input_port name="waypoint2">Pose</input_port>
            <input_port name="waypoint3">Pose</input_port>
            <input_port name="waypoint4">Pose</input_port>
            <input_port name="waypoint5">Pose</input_port>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="ExecuteAddBox">
            <input_port default="1" name="auto_subfix"/>
            <input_port default="box" name="box_name"/>
            <input_port default="{place_pose}" name="box_pose"/>
            <input_port default="0.2 0.2 0.2" name="box_size"/>
            <input_port default="arm" name="group_name"/>
            <input_port default="1" name="is_absolute"/>
            <input_port default="robot/execute_add_box" name="service_name"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="ExecuteAddPlane">
            <input_port default="arm" name="group_name"/>
            <input_port default="ground" name="plane_name"/>
            <input_port default="0 0 1" name="plane_normal"/>
            <input_port default="0 0 0 0 0 0 1" name="plane_pose">Pose</input_port>
            <input_port default="robot/execute_add_plane" name="service_name"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="ExecuteAttachBox">
            <input_port name="box_name"/>
            <input_port name="box_pose"/>
            <input_port name="box_size"/>
            <input_port default="gripper" name="eef_group_name"/>
            <input_port default="arm" name="group_name"/>
            <input_port default="robot/execute_attach_box" name="service_name"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="ExecuteDetachObject">
            <input_port default="arm" name="group_name"/>
            <input_port name="obj_name"/>
            <input_port default="robot/execute_detach_object" name="service_name"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="ExecuteGroupAngularJointStates">
            <input_port default="0 0 0 0 0 0 0" name="goal"/>
            <input_port default="panda_arm" name="group_name"/>
            <input_port default="robot/execute_group_joint_states" name="service_name"/>
            <input_port default="0.01" name="tolerance"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="ExecuteGroupLinearJointStates">
            <input_port default="0.04 0.04" name="goal"/>
            <input_port default="hand" name="group_name"/>
            <input_port default="robot/execute_group_joint_states" name="service_name"/>
            <input_port default="0.01" name="tolerance"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="ExecuteGroupManyPoses">
            <input_port name="constraint">If 'r', 'p', or 'y' is given, the eef_frame should not rotate about itself along x-, y-, or z-axis. You can combine them like 'rp', 'ry', 'py', and 'rpy'. Default ''</input_port>
            <input_port default="0.1" name="eef_step"/>
            <input_port default="0" name="goal_type">0: Pose wrt the global base frame (robot base); 1: Pose wrt the base frame transferred to current eef base position; 2: pose wrt the eef frame</input_port>
            <input_port default="0 0 0 0 0 0 1" name="goals">Goals for each waypoint to reach, seperate with ; </input_port>
            <input_port default="arm" name="group_name">The group providing the reference frame</input_port>
            <input_port default="robot/execute_group_many_poses" name="service_name"/>
            <input_port default="0.01" name="tolerance"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="ExecuteGroupNamedStates">
            <input_port default="aubo" name="group_name">The group providing the reference frame</input_port>
            <input_port default="aubo/execute_group_named_states" name="service_name"/>
            <input_port default="A" name="state_name">A: Camera to up left; B: Camera to up right; C: Camera to bottom left; D: Camera to bottom right</input_port>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="ExecuteGroupPose">
            <input_port name="constraint">If 'r', 'p', or 'y' is given, the eef_frame should not rotate about itself along x-, y-, or z-axis. You can combine them like 'rp', 'ry', 'py', and 'rpy'. Default ''</input_port>
            <input_port default="0 0 0 0 0 0 1" name="goal"/>
            <input_port default="0" name="goal_type">0: Pose wrt the global base frame (robot base); 1: Pose wrt the base frame transferred to current eef base position; 2: pose wrt the eef frame</input_port>
            <input_port default="arm" name="group_name">The group providing the reference frame</input_port>
            <input_port default="robot/execute_group_pose" name="service_name"/>
            <input_port default="0.01" name="tolerance"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="ExecuteGroupShift">
            <input_port default="z" name="axis">Could be x y z roll pitch yaw</input_port>
            <input_port default="0" name="goal"/>
            <input_port default="arm" name="group_name"/>
            <input_port default="0" name="is_absolute"/>
            <input_port default="robot/execute_group_shift" name="service_name"/>
            <input_port default="0.01" name="tolerance"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="ExecuteGroupSpeed">
            <input_port default="arm" name="group_name">The group providing the reference frame</input_port>
            <input_port default="robot/execute_group_speed" name="service_name"/>
            <input_port default="0.5" name="value">The velocity scaling factor, Allowed values are in (0,1]</input_port>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="ExecutePlanning">
            <input_port name="category">0 for large box, 1 for small box</input_port>
            <output_port default="{pick_pose}" name="pick_pose">Pose</output_port>
            <output_port default="{place_pose}" name="place_pose">Double Array</output_port>
            <input_port default="{box_surface_pose}" name="pose">Pose</input_port>
            <output_port default="{post_middle_pose}" name="post_middle_pose">Double Array</output_port>
            <output_port default="{post_pick_pose}" name="post_pick_pose">Pose</output_port>
            <output_port default="{post_pick_pose_edge}" name="post_pick_pose_edge">Pose</output_port>
            <output_port default="{pre_middle_pose}" name="pre_middle_pose">Double Array</output_port>
            <output_port default="{pre_pick_pose}" name="pre_pick_pose">Pose</output_port>
            <input_port default="execute_planning" name="service_name"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="ExecuteRemoveObject">
            <input_port default="1" name="is_exact"/>
            <input_port name="obj_name"/>
            <input_port default="robot/execute_remove_object" name="service_name"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="ExecuteSuction">
            <input_port default="0" name="enable"/>
            <input_port default="execute_suction" name="service_name"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="FetchDetectedInfo">
            <output_port name="category">String, 0 for large box, 1 for small box</output_port>
            <output_port name="pose">Pose</output_port>
            <input_port default="fetch_detected_info" name="service_name"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="SenseObjectPose">
            <output_port name="category">String, 0 for large box, 1 for small box</output_port>
            <output_port name="pose">Pose</output_port>
            <input_port default="sense_object_pose" name="service_name"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="StoreDetectedInfo">
            <input_port name="category">String, 0 for large box, 1 for small box</input_port>
            <input_port name="pose">Pose</input_port>
            <input_port default="store_detected_info" name="service_name"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="TypeInPose">
            <output_port name="pose_on_blackboard">Pose on blackboard</output_port>
            <input_port default="type_in_pose" name="service_name"/>
            <input_port default="0 0 0 0 0 0 1" name="type_in_pose">Pose to be typed in</input_port>
        </Action>
    </TreeNodesModel>
    <!-- ////////// -->
</root>
mrq:
The mission describes a depalletizing task that configures speed, sets up collision objects, initializes the arm, and then repeats a pick-and-place routine up to one hundred times.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (ExecuteGroupSpeed) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action ExecuteGroupSpeed to set the arm group speed to 0.7.

Next, it will execute the following actions (ExecuteAddPlane, ExecuteAddBox, ExecuteAddBox, ExecuteAddBox, ExecuteAddBox, ExecuteAddBox) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action ExecuteAddPlane to add a ground plane with the specified pose and normal. It will run the custom action ExecuteAddBox to add a thin left wall box at the specified pose and size. It will run the custom action ExecuteAddBox to add a thin right wall box at the specified pose and size. It will run the custom action ExecuteAddBox to add a thin top wall box at the specified pose and size. It will run the custom action ExecuteAddBox to add a conveyor box at the specified pose and size. It will run the custom action ExecuteAddBox to add a box surface barrier named boxsur at the specified pose and size.

Next, it will execute the following actions (ExecuteGroupNamedStates) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action ExecuteGroupNamedStates to move the arm to the named state left_pre.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (Sequence) for a set number of times or infinitely. It will execute the following actions (SenseObjectPose, ExecutePlanning, ExecuteGroupLinearJointStates, ExecuteRemoveObject, ConnectWaypoints, ExecuteGroupManyPoses, Delay, ConnectWaypoints, ExecuteGroupManyPoses, ExecuteGroupLinearJointStates, ExecuteGroupLinearJointStates, Delay, ExecuteAddBox, ExecuteGroupLinearJointStates) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action SenseObjectPose to detect an object and store its pose as box_surface_pose. It will run the custom action ExecutePlanning to compute pre, pick, post, and place poses using the sensed surface pose and store them on the blackboard. It will run the custom action ExecuteGroupLinearJointStates to move to the pre middle joint target using the stored pre_middle_pose. It will run the custom action ExecuteRemoveObject to remove the box surface object named boxsur from the planning scene. It will run the custom action ConnectWaypoints to connect pre_pick_pose and pick_pose into a pick_motion path. It will run the custom action ExecuteGroupManyPoses to execute the pre pick and pick path using the connected pick_motion waypoints. It will execute delayed AlwaysSuccess and make sure it always succeeds. It will run the custom action ConnectWaypoints to connect post_pick_pose and post_pick_pose_edge into a pull_out path. It will run the custom action ExecuteGroupManyPoses to execute the post pick pull_out path using the connected waypoints. It will run the custom action ExecuteGroupLinearJointStates to move to the post middle joint target using the stored post_middle_pose. It will run the custom action ExecuteGroupLinearJointStates to move to the place joint target using the stored place_pose. It will execute delayed AlwaysSuccess and make sure it always succeeds. It will run the custom action ExecuteAddBox to re-add the box surface object named boxsur at the specified pose and size. It will run the custom action ExecuteGroupLinearJointStates to move back to the pre middle joint target using the stored pre_middle_pose.

mrq:
The mission describes a retrieval task that attempts to reach a kitchen, locate a bottle, and fetch it, with a fallback to seeking assistance if the attempt fails. First, the robot will execute the following actions (Go_to_kitchen, Find_bottle, Fetch_bottle) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action Go_to_kitchen before attempting to search. It will run the custom action Find_bottle only after reaching the kitchen. It will run the custom action Fetch_bottle only after the bottle is found.

Next, it will execute the following actions (S1, Ask_for_help) consecutively. If any action (S1) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will run the custom action Ask_for_help if the ordered attempt to go to the kitchen, find the bottle, and fetch it does not succeed.

mrq:
The mission describes a household assistance task that attempts to reach the kitchen, fetch a bottle, and locate the bottle, and otherwise requests help.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (S1, Ask_for_help) consecutively. If any action (S1) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution will fail. It will run the custom action S1 first and will proceed to Ask_for_help only if S1 fails.

Next, it will execute the following actions (Go_to_kitchen, S2) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action Go_to_kitchen before any fetching attempt. It will run the custom action S2 only after Go_to_kitchen succeeds.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (Fetch_bottle, Find_bottle) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action Fetch_bottle as the initial retrieval step. It will run the custom action Find_bottle only after Fetch_bottle succeeds.

<root main_tree_to_execute="MainTree">
  <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
    <Sequence name="root">
      <FallbackStar name="check_motion">
        <Inverter name="is_not_stuck">
          <IsStuck/>
        </Inverter>
        <SequenceStar name="stuck_recovery">
          <Stop/>
          <BackUp/>
          <Spin/>
        </SequenceStar>
      </FallbackStar>
      <SequenceStar name="navigate">
        <ComputePathToPose endpoints="${endpoints}" path="${path}"/>
        <FollowPath path="${path}"/>
      </SequenceStar>
    </Sequence>
  </BehaviorTree>
</root>
mrq:
The mission describes a mobile robot task that first checks for being stuck and recovers if needed, and then performs sequential navigation by computing and following a path.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (FallbackStar) consecutively. It will execute the following actions (Inverter, SequenceStar) consecutively. If any action (Inverter) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, it will start all over again until an action succeeds. It will execute inverted the custom action IsStuck. If the custom action IsStuck succeeds, it will reverse it to fail. Else if the custom action IsStuck fails, it will reverse it to succeed. It will execute the following actions (Stop, BackUp, Spin) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will run the custom action Stop to halt motion. It will run the custom action BackUp to reverse away from the obstacle. It will run the custom action Spin to reorient and clear entanglement.

Next, it will execute the following actions (SequenceStar) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will run the ROS action ComputePathToPose with the provided endpoints and will store the resulting path. It will run the ROS action FollowPath using the stored path after the path computation succeeds.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (FixRudder, DelayFor) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action FixRudder with a target depth of 1.0, a rudder angle of 2.0, and a speed of 3.0 knots. Next, it will execute delayed AlwaysSuccess. If execution succeeds, it will start the delay timer for 1000 milliseconds. After the delay period is exhausted, it will resume execution and ensure the delayed action AlwaysSuccess succeeds.

<root main_tree_to_execute = "LatTree">
    <BehaviorTree ID="LatTree">
        <Fallback>
            <Sequence>
                <!-- lane : left, ego, right  -->
                <!-- distance : m -->
                <CheckLaneFrontClear which_lane="ego" distance="35.2" />
                <ActionKeepLane />
            </Sequence>

            <Sequence>
                <CheckLaneFrontClear which_lane="left" distance="35.2" />
                <CheckLaneBackClear which_lane="left" distance="20.2" />
                <CheckAvailDriveAreaClear which_lane="left" />
                <ActionLeftBypass />
            </Sequence>

            <ActionKeepLane />
        </Fallback>
    </BehaviorTree>
</root>
mrq:
The mission describes lateral driving decisions that prioritize staying in the current lane and perform a left bypass when the left lane is clear.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (CheckLaneFrontClear, ActionKeepLane) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action CheckLaneFrontClear to verify that the front of the ego lane is clear for 35.2 meters. It will run the custom action ActionKeepLane to maintain the current lane after the ego lane front check succeeds.

Next, it will execute the following actions (CheckLaneFrontClear, CheckLaneBackClear, CheckAvailDriveAreaClear, ActionLeftBypass) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action CheckLaneFrontClear to verify that the front of the left lane is clear for 35.2 meters. It will run the custom action CheckLaneBackClear to verify that the back of the left lane is clear for 20.2 meters. It will run the custom action CheckAvailDriveAreaClear to verify that the available drivable area of the left lane is clear. It will run the custom action ActionLeftBypass to perform a left bypass maneuver after all left-lane clearance checks succeed.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (ActionKeepLane) consecutively. If any action (ActionKeepLane) fails, move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will run the custom action ActionKeepLane to maintain the current lane when previous options do not succeed.

<!--
    This Behavior Tree replans the global path periodically at 1 Hz and it also has
    recovery actions.
-->

<root main_tree_to_execute="ElevatorTree">
  <BehaviorTree ID="ElevatorTree">
    <Sequence>
      <RecoveryNode name="ClearCostmapRecorvery">
	<Sequence name="ClearCostmapSequence">
	  <!-- clear costmap intentionally to get a path go through the door -->
	  <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearGlobalCostmap-context" server_timeout="5000"
			      service_name="/local/global_costmap/clear_entirely_global_costmap"/>
	  <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearLocalCostmap-context" server_timeout="5000"
			      service_name="/local/local_costmap/clear_entirely_local_costmap"/>
	  <ComputePathToPose goal="{goal}" path="{path_temp}" planner_id="GridBased"/>
	  <CheckPath  path="{path_temp}" path_topic="/path" plan_topic="/plan" path_out="{path}"
		      average_threshold="0.3"/>
	  <!-- front vibration -->
	  <PublishTopic topic="/cabot/event" value="navigation;event;navigation_start" />
	</Sequence>
	<AlwaysSuccess/>
      </RecoveryNode>
      
      <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="99999999" name="NavigateRecovery">
	<PipelineSequence name="NavigateWithReplanning">
	  <!-- Not using RateController, so that the robot can keep trying to find an updated path -->
	  <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="99999999" name="UpdatePathRecovery">
	    <Sequence>
	      <WaitFloat wait_duration_float="0.5"/>
	      <ComputePathToPose goal="{goal}" path="{path_temp}" planner_id="GridBased"/>
	      <CheckPath  path="{path_temp}" path_topic="/path" plan_topic="/plan" path_out="{path}"
			  average_threshold="0.3"/>
	    </Sequence>
	    <AlwaysSuccess/>
	  </RecoveryNode>
	  <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="5" name="FollowPathRecovery">
	    <FollowPath path="{path}" controller_id="FollowPath" />
	    <AlwaysSuccess/>
	  </RecoveryNode>
	</PipelineSequence>
	<RecoveryNode>
	  <Sequence name="RecoveryActions">
	    <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearGlobalCostmap-context" server_timeout="5000"
				service_name="/local/global_costmap/clear_entirely_global_costmap"/>
	    <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearLocalCostmap-context" server_timeout="5000"
				service_name="/local/local_costmap/clear_entirely_local_costmap"/>
	  </Sequence>
	  <AlwaysSuccess/>
	</RecoveryNode>
      </RecoveryNode>
    </Sequence>
  </BehaviorTree>
</root>
mrq:
The mission describes elevator navigation that clears costmaps, computes and validates paths, and follows paths with continuous replanning and structured recovery.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (ClearEntireCostmap, ClearEntireCostmap, ComputePathToPose, CheckPath, PublishTopic) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute main action ClearCostmapSequence and, if it fails, execute recovery action AlwaysSuccess. It will run the ROS action ClearEntireCostmap on the global costmap using the specified service endpoint. It will run the ROS action ClearEntireCostmap on the local costmap using the specified service endpoint. It will run the ROS action ComputePathToPose with the provided goal and will store the temporary path. It will run the custom action CheckPath to validate the temporary path and will output the validated path. It will run the custom action PublishTopic to announce the navigation start event.

Next, it will execute main action NavigateWithReplanning and, if it fails, execute recovery action RecoveryActions. It will execute the following actions (UpdatePathRecovery, FollowPathRecovery) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. While an action is running or has succeeded, it will go to the next action. It will execute main action consisting of a wait, a path computation, and a path check, and if it fails, execute recovery action AlwaysSuccess, and after recovery, retry the main action until retries are exhausted. It will execute the following actions (WaitFloat, ComputePathToPose, CheckPath) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails within the update step. While an action is running or has succeeded in the update step, it will go to the next action in the pipeline. It will run the custom action WaitFloat to delay for the specified duration before recomputing the path. It will run the ROS action ComputePathToPose to recompute a temporary path toward the goal. It will run the custom action CheckPath to validate the recomputed path and will update the output path. It will execute main action FollowPath and, if it fails, execute recovery action AlwaysSuccess, and after recovery, retry the main action until retries are exhausted. It will run the ROS action FollowPath using the validated path and the specified controller to move toward the goal.

Finally, it will execute main action consisting of two costmap clears, and if it fails, execute recovery action AlwaysSuccess, and after recovery, retry the main action until retries are exhausted. It will execute the following actions (ClearEntireCostmap, ClearEntireCostmap) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the ROS action ClearEntireCostmap on the global costmap using the specified service endpoint during recovery. It will run the ROS action ClearEntireCostmap on the local costmap using the specified service endpoint during recovery.

<root main_tree_to_execute="MainTree">
  <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
    <Sequence name="Sequence">
    	<Print message="=== AddTwoInts demo ==="/>
    	<Print message="a"/>
    	<Print message="{a}"/>
    	<Print message="b"/>
    	<Print message="{b}"/>
    	<AddTwoInts a="{a}" b="{b}" sum="{sum}"/>
    	<Print message="a+b ="/>
    	<Print message="{sum}"/>
    	<AddTwoInts a="{sum}" b="100" sum="{sum}"/>
    	<Print message="+100 ="/>
    	<Print message="{sum}"/>
    	<AddTwoInts a="{sum}" b="{sum}" sum="{sum}"/>
    	<Print message="*2 ="/>
    	<Print message="{sum}"/>
    </Sequence>
  </BehaviorTree>
</root>
mrq: The mission describes a sequential arithmetic demonstration that prints inputs, computes sums, and reports intermediate results in strict order. First, the robot will execute the following actions (Print, Print, Print, Print, Print, AddTwoInts, Print, Print, AddTwoInts, Print, Print, AddTwoInts, Print, Print) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action Print to display the banner "=== AddTwoInts demo ===". It will run the custom action Print to display the label "a". It will run the custom action Print to display the value stored in the blackboard entry a. It will run the custom action Print to display the label "b". It will run the custom action Print to display the value stored in the blackboard entry b. It will run the custom action AddTwoInts using the blackboard entries a and b as inputs and will store the resulting sum in the blackboard entry sum. It will run the custom action Print to display the label "a+b =". It will run the custom action Print to display the current value stored in the blackboard entry sum. It will run the custom action AddTwoInts using the current blackboard entry sum and the literal value 100 as inputs and will overwrite the blackboard entry sum with the result. It will run the custom action Print to display the label "+100 =". It will run the custom action Print to display the updated value stored in the blackboard entry sum. It will run the custom action AddTwoInts using the current blackboard entry sum for both inputs and will overwrite the blackboard entry sum with the doubled result. It will run the custom action Print to display the label "*2 =". It will run the custom action Print to display the final value stored in the blackboard entry sum.

mrq:
The mission describes a material handling task that moves from bin A to bin B and then adjusts the tool orientation for picking.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (MoveToPredefinedPoint, PickingItem) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action MoveToPredefinedPoint first. It will run the custom action PickingItem after the first action succeeds.

Next, it will run the custom action LinearMove as part of MoveToPredefinedPoint. It will execute LinearMove to drive along a straight path to the predefined point.

Finally, it will run the custom action AdjustTCPOrientation as part of PickingItem. It will execute AdjustTCPOrientation to align the tool center point to the required picking orientation.

<root main_tree_to_execute="MainTree">
  <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
    <PipelineSequence name="NavigateWithReplanning">
      <RateController hz="1.0">
        <Sequence>
          <GoalUpdater input_goal="{goal}" output_goal="{updated_goal}">
            <ComputePathToPose goal="{updated_goal}" path="{path}" planner_id="GridBased"/>
          </GoalUpdater>
         <TruncatePath distance="1.0" input_path="{path}" output_path="{truncated_path}"/>
        </Sequence>
      </RateController>
      <KeepRunningUntilFailure>
        <FollowPath path="{truncated_path}" controller_id="FollowPath"/>
      </KeepRunningUntilFailure>
    </PipelineSequence>
  </BehaviorTree>
</root>
mrq:
The mission describes a dynamic navigation task that continuously updates a goal, recomputes a path, truncates the path to maintain a standoff distance, and follows the truncated path with continuous replanning.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (RateController, KeepRunningUntilFailure) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. While an action is running or has succeeded, it will go to the next action. It will run the custom action RateController at 1.0 Hz to regulate how often the following sequence runs. It will then execute the following actions (FollowPath) and, if the action succeeds, restart it. Else if the action fails, it will stop looping and continue execution.

Next, it will execute the following actions (GoalUpdater, TruncatePath) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action GoalUpdater to compute an updated goal from the provided goal. It will then run the ROS action ComputePathToPose with the updated goal and will store the resulting path. It will run the custom action TruncatePath to shorten the stored path to maintain a distance of 1.0 meter from the moving goal.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (FollowPath) and, if the action succeeds, restart it. Else if the action fails, it will stop looping and continue execution. It will run the ROS action FollowPath using the truncated path with the specified controller to keep moving toward the updated goal while respecting the standoff distance.

mrq:
The mission describes a dynamic navigation task that will repeatedly update a moving goal, compute a path, truncate the path to a set distance, and follow the truncated path with continuous advancement and periodic replanning.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (RateController, KeepRunningUntilFailure) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. While an action is running or has succeeded, it will go to the next action.

Next, it will execute the following actions (Sequence) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run at a controlled rate of 1.0 Hz before evaluating its child actions.

Next, it will execute the following actions (GoalUpdater, TruncatePath) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action GoalUpdater to take the current goal and produce an updated_goal suitable for planning. It will run the ROS action ComputePathToPose with the updated_goal and will store the resulting path. It will run the custom action TruncatePath to shorten the stored path to a distance of 1.0 meter and will save it as truncated_path.

Finally, it will execute FollowPath. If FollowPath succeeds, it will restart it. Else if FollowPath fails, it will stop looping and continue execution.

        <ComputePathToPose goal="{goal}" path="{path}" planner_id="GridBased"/>

    </PipelineSequence>

mrq: The mission describes a navigation task that computes a global path at a controlled rate and follows it while allowing progress to continue during path computation. First, the robot will execute the following actions (SpeedController, FollowPath) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute the following actions (ComputePathToPose) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails within the SpeedController. It will run the ROS action ComputePathToPose with the provided goal and will store the resulting path while the SpeedController enforces a planning rate between 0.1 Hz and 1.0 Hz with a 0.3 second filter duration and a speed range between 0.0 m/s and 0.26 m/s. It will run the ROS action FollowPath using the stored path with the specified controller after a plan is available. It will advance to FollowPath while ComputePathToPose is running or has succeeded because the PipelineSequence will execute the following actions (SpeedController, FollowPath) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails, and while an action is running or has succeeded, it will go to the next action.

mrq:
The mission describes a target-seeking and engagement task that chooses between a structured pursuit-and-attack routine and a fallback roaming behavior.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (GetTarget, Move, Sequence(TargetAdjacent, AttackTarget)) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will run the custom action GetTarget to select a target. It will run the custom action Move to advance toward the selected target after the target selection succeeds. It will execute the following actions (TargetAdjacent, AttackTarget) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for TargetAdjacent. It will run the custom action AttackTarget only after the adjacency condition succeeds.

Next, it will execute the following actions (SequenceStar(Main), RandomMove) consecutively. If any action (SequenceStar(Main)) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will run the custom action RandomMove if the Main sequence fails to achieve success.

mrq:
The mission describes a patrol and event-driven navigation task that initializes three goal positions and then reacts to detected animals by navigating to corresponding goals in a continuous loop.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (SetBlackboard, SetBlackboard, SetBlackboard, Repeat) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule when setting Goal_a to the coordinates -0.579;-1.341;0.0;1.0. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule when setting Goal_b to the coordinates 5.214;-1.533;0.0;1.0. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule when setting Goal_c to the coordinates -1.588;1.253;0.0;1.0. It will execute ForceSuccess continuously until it fails or succeeds, else keep executing ForceSuccess forever. It will execute a Fallback that contains three sequences and will execute the following actions (OpenVINOEvent, SubTree) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, it will start all over again until an action succeeds because ForceSuccess will execute the Fallback and make sure it always succeeds. It will execute the OpenVINOEvent that detects a cat and, if it succeeds, it will execute the SubTree MoveRobot with the target Goal_c. It will execute the OpenVINOEvent that detects a dog and, if the previous attempt fails, it will execute the SubTree MoveRobot with the target Goal_a. It will execute the OpenVINOEvent that detects a cow and, if the previous attempts fail, it will execute the SubTree MoveRobot with the target Goal_b.

Next, it will execute the following actions (Nav2Client) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the ROS action Nav2Client with the goal provided by the input parameter target.

<root main_tree_to_execute="MainTree">
  <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
    <Sequence name="root">
      <RateController hz="1.0">
        <Fallback>
          <GoalReached/>
          <ComputePathToPose goal="${goal}"/>
        </Fallback>
      </RateController>
      <FollowPath path="${path}"/>
    </Sequence>
  </BehaviorTree>
</root>
mrq: The mission describes a navigation task that computes paths at a controlled rate and follows the most recent path until the goal is reached. First, the robot will execute the following actions (RateController, Fallback, GoalReached, ComputePathToPose, FollowPath) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute delayed Fallback under a rate of 1.0 Hz by running delayed Fallback and, after each delay period is exhausted, resuming execution. It will execute the following actions (GoalReached, ComputePathToPose) consecutively. If any action (GoalReached) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will run the custom action GoalReached and will succeed only when the goal condition is met. It will run the ROS action ComputePathToPose with the goal provided and will make the fallback succeed when a valid path is computed. Next, it will run the ROS action FollowPath using the path stored on the blackboard after the rate-controlled fallback succeeds.
