<?xml version="1.0"?>
<root main_tree_to_execute="BehaviorTree">
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="BehaviorTree">
        <Sequence>
            <Action ID="ReceiveTargetPose" target_pose="{target_pose}"/>
            <Action ID="LoadConstraints" constrain_a="{constraint_a}" constraint_b="{constraint_b}" constraint_c="{constraint_c}" file=""configuration.yaml""/>
            <SubTree ID="RunPlannerSubtree" constraint_a="constraint_a" constraint_b="constraint_b" constraint_c="constraint_c" path="path" target_pose="target_pose"/>
            <SubTree ID="ExecutePath" path="path"/>
        </Sequence>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="ExecutePath">
        <Fallback>
            <Sequence>
                <Condition ID="OnAir"/>
                <Action ID="Fly" path="{path}"/>
            </Sequence>
            <Sequence>
                <Condition ID="OnGround"/>
                <Action ID="Roll" path="{path}"/>
            </Sequence>
            <Sequence>
                <Condition ID="OnWater"/>
                <Action ID="Swim" path="{path}"/>
            </Sequence>
        </Fallback>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="RunPlannerSubtree">
        <Fallback>
            <Action ID="RunPlannerA" constraint_a="{constraint_a}" constraint_b="{constraint_b}" contratint_c="{constraint_c}" path="{path}" target_pose="{target_pose}"/>
            <Action ID="RunPlannerB" constraint_a="{constraint_a}" constraint_b="{constraint_b}" constraint_c="{constraint_c}" path="{path}" target_pose="{target_pose}"/>
        </Fallback>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <TreeNodesModel>
        <SubTree ID="ExecutePath">
            <input_port name="path"/>
        </SubTree>
        <Action ID="Fly">
            <input_port name="path"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="LoadConstraints">
            <output_port name="constrain_a"/>
            <output_port name="constraint_b"/>
            <output_port name="constraint_c"/>
            <input_port name="file"/>
        </Action>
        <Condition ID="OnAir"/>
        <Condition ID="OnGround"/>
        <Condition ID="OnWater"/>
        <Action ID="ReceiveTargetPose">
            <output_port name="target_pose"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="Roll">
            <input_port name="path"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="RunPlannerA">
            <input_port name="constraint_a"/>
            <input_port name="constraint_b"/>
            <input_port name="contratint_c"/>
            <output_port name="path"/>
            <input_port name="target_pose"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="RunPlannerB">
            <input_port name="constraint_a"/>
            <input_port name="constraint_b"/>
            <input_port name="constraint_c"/>
            <output_port name="path"/>
            <input_port name="target_pose"/>
        </Action>
        <SubTree ID="RunPlannerSubtree">
            <input_port name="constraint_a"/>
            <input_port name="constraint_b"/>
            <input_port name="constraint_c"/>
            <output_port name="path"/>
            <input_port name="target_pose"/>
        </SubTree>
        <Action ID="Swim">
            <input_port name="path"/>
        </Action>
    </TreeNodesModel>
    <!-- ////////// -->
</root>
mrq:
The mission describes a multi-modal navigation task that receives a target, loads constraints, plans a path, and executes that path using a mode selected by environmental conditions. First, the robot will execute the following actions (ReceiveTargetPose, LoadConstraints, RunPlannerSubtree, ExecutePath) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action ReceiveTargetPose to obtain and store the target pose. It will run the custom action LoadConstraints to read configuration.yaml and store constraint_a, constraint_b, and constraint_c. It will run the subtree RunPlannerSubtree to compute a path using the stored target pose and constraints. It will run the subtree ExecutePath to traverse the computed path.

Next, it will execute the following actions (RunPlannerA, RunPlannerB) consecutively. If any action (RunPlannerA) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will run the custom action RunPlannerA with constraint_a, constraint_b, and the provided contratint_c together with the target pose to produce and store the path. It will run the custom action RunPlannerB with constraint_a, constraint_b, and constraint_c together with the target pose to produce and store the path if the first planner fails.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (Sequence(OnAir, Fly), Sequence(OnGround, Roll), Sequence(OnWater, Swim)) consecutively. If any action (Sequence) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will execute the following actions (OnAir, Fly) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails, and while this sequence is selected it will run the custom condition OnAir and then the custom action Fly on the given path. It will execute the following actions (OnGround, Roll) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails, and while this sequence is selected it will run the custom condition OnGround and then the custom action Roll on the given path. It will execute the following actions (OnWater, Swim) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails, and while this sequence is selected it will run the custom condition OnWater and then the custom action Swim on the given path.

<root main_tree_to_execute="MainTree">
  <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
    <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="6" name="NavigateRecovery">
      <PipelineSequence name="NavigateWithReplanning">
        <RateController hz="0.333">
          <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="1" name="ComputePathThroughPoses">
            <ReactiveSequence>
              <RemovePassedGoals input_goals="{goals}" output_goals="{goals}" radius="0.7"/>
              <ComputePathThroughPoses goals="{goals}" path="{path}" planner_id="GridBased"/>
            </ReactiveSequence>
            <ReactiveFallback name="ComputePathThroughPosesRecoveryFallback">
              <GoalUpdated/>
              <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearGlobalCostmap-Context" service_name="global_costmap/clear_entirely_global_costmap"/>
            </ReactiveFallback>
          </RecoveryNode>
        </RateController>
        <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="1" name="FollowPath">
          <FollowPath path="{path}" controller_id="FollowPath"/>
          <ReactiveFallback name="FollowPathRecoveryFallback">
            <GoalUpdated/>
            <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearLocalCostmap-Context" service_name="local_costmap/clear_entirely_local_costmap"/>
          </ReactiveFallback>
        </RecoveryNode>
      </PipelineSequence>
      <ReactiveFallback name="RecoveryFallback">
        <GoalUpdated/>
        <RoundRobin name="RecoveryActions">
          <Sequence name="ClearingActions">
            <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearLocalCostmap-Subtree" service_name="local_costmap/clear_entirely_local_costmap"/>
            <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearGlobalCostmap-Subtree" service_name="global_costmap/clear_entirely_global_costmap"/>
          </Sequence>
          <Spin spin_dist="1.57"/>
          <Wait wait_duration="5"/>
          <BackUp backup_dist="0.15" backup_speed="0.025"/>
        </RoundRobin>
      </ReactiveFallback>
    </RecoveryNode>
  </BehaviorTree>
</root>
mrq:
The mission describes a navigation task that continuously replans a global path through a list of goals and follows the path with layered recoveries. First, the robot will execute the following actions (NavigateWithReplanning, RecoveryFallback) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute the main action NavigateWithReplanning. If the main action fails, it will execute the recovery action RecoveryFallback. After the recovery action, it will retry the main action NavigateWithReplanning. If the recovery action fails and retries are exhausted, it will stop execution.

Next, it will execute the following actions (ComputePathThroughPoses, FollowPath) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. While an action is running or has succeeded, it will go to the next action. It will run the ComputePathThroughPoses subtree at a regulated rate of 0.333 Hz. It will then run the FollowPath subtree as soon as the planning action is running or has already succeeded.

Next, it will execute the main action ComputePathThroughPoses. If it fails, it will execute recovery action ComputePathThroughPosesRecoveryFallback. After recovery, it will retry the main action ComputePathThroughPoses. If recovery action ComputePathThroughPosesRecoveryFallback fails and retries are exhausted, it will stop execution.

Next, it will execute the following actions (RemovePassedGoals, ComputePathThroughPoses) in sequence. If ComputePathThroughPoses fails, it will stop execution. While ComputePathThroughPoses is still running, it will re-evaluate the previous action RemovePassedGoals. It will run the custom action RemovePassedGoals to filter out goals already reached using the provided radius. It will run the ROS action ComputePathThroughPoses to create a path through the remaining goals and store it.

Next, it will execute the following actions (GoalUpdated, ClearEntireCostmap-Context) in order. If GoalUpdated succeeds, it will stop execution. Else it will keep executing and re-evaluating previous actions whenever an action is running. It will run the custom action GoalUpdated to detect a change in the goal list. If the goal did not update, it will run the ROS action ClearEntireCostmap with the global costmap service to clear obstacles.

Next, it will execute the main action FollowPath. If it fails, it will execute recovery action FollowPathRecoveryFallback. After recovery, it will retry the main action FollowPath. If recovery action FollowPathRecoveryFallback fails and retries are exhausted, it will stop execution.

Next, it will run the ROS action FollowPath using the stored path with the specified controller.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (GoalUpdated, RecoveryActions) in order. If GoalUpdated succeeds, it will stop execution. Else it will keep executing and re-evaluating previous actions whenever an action is running. It will run the custom action GoalUpdated to detect if a new goal has been issued. If the goal did not update, it will execute the custom action RecoveryActions to attempt system recovery in a round-robin manner.

<root main_tree_to_execute = "MainTree" >

    <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
        <ReactiveFallback name="root_R_Fallback">
            <ReactiveSequence name="mission_over_R_Sequence">
                <MissionOver/>
                <BackToStation/>
            </ReactiveSequence>
            <ForceRunning>
                <ReactiveFallback name="mission_R_Fallback">
                    <ReactiveSequence name="idle_R_Sequence">
                        <IsBatteryEnough/>
                        <ReactiveFallback>
                            <ForceFailure>
                                <SubTree ID="DropToolTree"/>
                            </ForceFailure>
                            <ReactiveSequence>
                                <Inverter>
                                    <Idle/>
                                </Inverter>
                                <SubTree ID="PerformTaskTree"/>
                            </ReactiveSequence>
                        </ReactiveFallback>
                    </ReactiveSequence>
                    <ReactiveFallback name="wait_R_Fallback">
                        <IsBatteryFull/>
                        <SubTree ID="RechargeTree"/>
                    </ReactiveFallback>
                </ReactiveFallback>
            </ForceRunning>
        </ReactiveFallback>
    </BehaviorTree>

    <BehaviorTree ID="DropToolTree">
        <ReactiveSequence>
            <NeedToDropTheTool/>
            <ReactiveSequence>
                <Fallback>
                    <IsAgentNearStation/>
                    <GoNearStation/>
                </Fallback>
                <DropTool/>
            </ReactiveSequence>
        </ReactiveSequence>
    </BehaviorTree>

    <BehaviorTree ID="PerformTaskTree">
        <ReactiveFallback name="select_task_R_Fallback">
            <ReactiveSequence name="is_monitor_R_Sequence">
                <IsTaskMonitor/>
                <SubTree ID="MonitorTree"/>
            </ReactiveSequence>
            <ReactiveSequence name="is_inspect_R_Sequence">
                <IsTaskInspect/>
                <SubTree ID="InspectTree"/>
            </ReactiveSequence>
            <ReactiveSequence name="is_deliver_tool_R_Sequence">
                <IsTaskDeliverTool/>
                <SubTree ID="DeliverToolTree"/>
            </ReactiveSequence>
            <ReactiveSequence name="is_Recharge_task_R_Sequence">
                <IsTaskRecharge/>
                <SubTree ID="RechargeTree"/>
            </ReactiveSequence>
        </ReactiveFallback>
    </BehaviorTree>

    <BehaviorTree ID="MonitorTree">
        <ReactiveSequence name="is_monitor_R_Sequence">
            <Fallback>
                <IsAgentNearHumanTarget/>
                <GoNearHumanTarget/>
            </Fallback>
            <MonitorHumanTarget/>
        </ReactiveSequence>
    </BehaviorTree>

    <BehaviorTree ID="InspectTree">
        <ReactiveSequence name="is_inspect_R_Sequence">
            <Fallback name="AgentNearWP_R_Fallback">
                <IsAgentNearWP/>
                <GoNearWP/>
            </Fallback>
            <TakeImage/>
        </ReactiveSequence>
    </BehaviorTree>

    <BehaviorTree ID="DeliverToolTree">
        <ReactiveSequence name="is_deliver_tool_R_Sequence">
            <ReactiveFallback name="Tool_R_Fallback">
                <HasAgentTheTool/>
                <ReactiveSequence name="PickTool_R_sequence">
                    <Fallback name="station_fallback">
                        <IsAgentNearStation/>
                        <GoNearStation/>
                    </Fallback>
                    <PickTool/>
                </ReactiveSequence>
            </ReactiveFallback>
            <Fallback>
                <IsAgentNearHumanTarget/>
                <GoNearHumanTarget/>
            </Fallback>
            <DeliverTool/>
        </ReactiveSequence>
    </BehaviorTree>

    <BehaviorTree ID="RechargeTree">
        <ReactiveSequence name="recharge_task_R_Sequence">
            <Fallback>
                <IsAgentNearChargingStation/>
                <GoNearChargingStation/>
            </Fallback>
            <Recharge/>
        </ReactiveSequence>
    </BehaviorTree>

</root>
mrq:
The mission describes a supervisory patrol and service task that will end when the mission is over, otherwise will keep running a mission loop that idles, performs assigned tasks, or recharges as needed.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (MissionOver, BackToStation) in sequence. If MissionOver fails, it will stop execution. While BackToStation is still running, it will re-evaluate MissionOver. It will check if the mission is over. It will run the custom action BackToStation if the mission is over.

Next, it will execute the following actions (IsBatteryEnough, ForceFailure(DropToolTree), Inverter(Idle), PerformTaskTree) in sequence. If any action fails, it will stop execution. While any action is still running, it will re-evaluate previous actions. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for IsBatteryEnough. It will execute the custom action DropToolTree and make sure it always fails. It will execute inverted Idle and will reverse its outcome. It will run the custom action PerformTaskTree after the inverted Idle succeeds.

Next, it will execute the following actions (IsBatteryFull, RechargeTree) in order. If IsBatteryFull succeeds, it will stop execution. Else it will keep executing and re-evaluating IsBatteryFull whenever RechargeTree is running. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for IsBatteryFull. It will run the custom action RechargeTree if the battery is not full.

Next, it will execute the following actions (NeedToDropTheTool, Fallback(IsAgentNearStation, GoNearStation), DropTool) in sequence. If any action fails, it will stop execution. While any action is still running, it will re-evaluate previous actions. It will check if it needs to drop the tool. It will execute the following actions (IsAgentNearStation, GoNearStation) consecutively. If IsAgentNearStation fails, it will move to GoNearStation until one succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will run the custom action DropTool after it is near the station.

Next, it will execute the following actions (ReactiveSequence(IsTaskMonitor, MonitorTree), ReactiveSequence(IsTaskInspect, InspectTree), ReactiveSequence(IsTaskDeliverTool, DeliverToolTree), ReactiveSequence(IsTaskRecharge, RechargeTree)) in order. If any action succeeds, it will stop execution. Else it will keep executing and re-evaluating previous actions whenever a later action is running. It will execute the following actions (IsTaskMonitor, MonitorTree) in sequence. If any action fails, it will stop execution. While any action is still running, it will re-evaluate previous actions. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for IsTaskMonitor. It will run the custom action MonitorTree after the monitor task is selected. It will execute the following actions (IsTaskInspect, InspectTree) in sequence. If any action fails, it will stop execution. While any action is still running, it will re-evaluate previous actions. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for IsTaskInspect. It will run the custom action InspectTree after the inspect task is selected. It will execute the following actions (IsTaskDeliverTool, DeliverToolTree) in sequence. If any action fails, it will stop execution. While any action is still running, it will re-evaluate previous actions. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for IsTaskDeliverTool. It will run the custom action DeliverToolTree after the deliver tool task is selected. It will execute the following actions (IsTaskRecharge, RechargeTree) in sequence. If any action fails, it will stop execution. While any action is still running, it will re-evaluate previous actions. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for IsTaskRecharge. It will run the custom action RechargeTree after the recharge task is selected.

Next, it will execute the following actions (Fallback(IsAgentNearHumanTarget, GoNearHumanTarget), MonitorHumanTarget) in sequence. If any action fails, it will stop execution. While any action is still running, it will re-evaluate previous actions. It will execute the following actions (IsAgentNearHumanTarget, GoNearHumanTarget) consecutively. If IsAgentNearHumanTarget fails, it will move to GoNearHumanTarget until one succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will run the custom action MonitorHumanTarget after it is near the human target.

Next, it will execute the following actions (Fallback(IsAgentNearWP, GoNearWP), TakeImage) in sequence. If any action fails, it will stop execution. While any action is still running, it will re-evaluate previous actions. It will execute the following actions (IsAgentNearWP, GoNearWP) consecutively. If IsAgentNearWP fails, it will move to GoNearWP until one succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will run the custom action TakeImage after it is near the waypoint.

Next, it will execute the following actions (ReactiveFallback(HasAgentTheTool, ReactiveSequence(Fallback(IsAgentNearStation, GoNearStation), PickTool)), Fallback(IsAgentNearHumanTarget, GoNearHumanTarget), DeliverTool) in sequence. If any action fails, it will stop execution. While any action is still running, it will re-evaluate previous actions. It will execute the following actions (HasAgentTheTool, ReactiveSequence(Fallback(IsAgentNearStation, GoNearStation), PickTool)) in order. If any action succeeds, it will stop execution. Else it will keep executing and re-evaluating previous actions whenever a later action is running. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for HasAgentTheTool. It will execute the following actions (Fallback(IsAgentNearStation, GoNearStation), PickTool) in sequence. If any action fails, it will stop execution. While any action is still running, it will re-evaluate previous actions. It will execute the following actions (IsAgentNearStation, GoNearStation) consecutively. If IsAgentNearStation fails, it will move to GoNearStation until one succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will run the custom action PickTool after it is near the station. It will execute the following actions (IsAgentNearHumanTarget, GoNearHumanTarget) consecutively. If IsAgentNearHumanTarget fails, it will move to GoNearHumanTarget until one succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will run the custom action DeliverTool after it is near the human target.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (Fallback(IsAgentNearChargingStation, GoNearChargingStation), Recharge) in sequence. If any action fails, it will stop execution. While any action is still running, it will re-evaluate previous actions. It will execute the following actions (IsAgentNearChargingStation, GoNearChargingStation) consecutively. If IsAgentNearChargingStation fails, it will move to GoNearChargingStation until one succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will run the custom action Recharge after it is near the charging station.

<root main_tree_to_execute="MainTree">
  <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
    <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="6" name="NavigateRecovery">
      <PipelineSequence name="NavigateWithReplanning">
        <RateController hz="1.0">
          <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="1" name="ComputePathToPose">
            <ComputePathToPose goal="{goal}" path="{path}" planner_id="GridBased"/>
            <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearGlobalCostmap-Context" service_name="global_costmap/clear_entirely_global_costmap"/>
          </RecoveryNode>
        </RateController>
        <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="1" name="FollowPath">
          <FollowPath path="{path}" controller_id="FollowPath"/>
          <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearLocalCostmap-Context" service_name="local_costmap/clear_entirely_local_costmap"/>
        </RecoveryNode>
      </PipelineSequence>
      <ReactiveFallback name="RecoveryFallback">
        <GoalUpdated/>
        <SequenceStar name="RecoveryActions">
          <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearLocalCostmap-Subtree" service_name="local_costmap/clear_entirely_local_costmap"/>
          <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearGlobalCostmap-Subtree" service_name="global_costmap/clear_entirely_global_costmap"/>
          <Spin spin_dist="1.57"/>
          <Wait wait_duration="5"/>
        </SequenceStar>
      </ReactiveFallback>
    </RecoveryNode>
  </BehaviorTree>
</root>
mrq:
The mission describes a navigation task that periodically replans a global path at 1 Hz, follows the path with pipelined progression, and applies layered recoveries when actions fail.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (NavigateWithReplanning, RecoveryFallback) using a recovery control that retries the main action up to six times. It will execute main NavigateWithReplanning. If it fails, it will execute recovery action RecoveryFallback. After recovery, it will retry main action NavigateWithReplanning. If the recovery action fails and retries are exhausted, it will stop execution.

Next, it will execute the following actions (RateController, FollowPath) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. While an action is running or has succeeded, it will go to the next action. It will run RateController at 1.0 Hz to pace downstream actions. It will execute main ComputePathToPose once per tick within the rate controller. If it fails, it will execute recovery action ClearEntireCostmap-Context for the global costmap. After recovery, it will retry main action ComputePathToPose once. It will then execute main FollowPath. If it fails, it will execute recovery action ClearLocalCostmap-Context for the local costmap. After recovery, it will retry main action FollowPath once.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (GoalUpdated, RecoveryActions) in order. If GoalUpdated succeeds, it will stop execution. Else it will keep executing and re-evaluating previous actions whenever an action is running. It will execute the following actions (ClearLocalCostmap-Subtree, ClearGlobalCostmap-Subtree, Spin, Wait) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will run the custom action ClearLocalCostmap-Subtree to clear the local costmap. It will run the custom action ClearGlobalCostmap-Subtree to clear the global costmap. It will run the custom action Spin with a spin distance of 1.57. It will run the custom action Wait for a duration of 5 seconds.

mrq: The mission describes a navigation task that continually replans a global path every meter while advancing along that path. First, the robot will execute the following actions (DistanceController, FollowPath) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. While an action is running or has succeeded, it will go to the next action. It will run the custom action DistanceController, which will gate the ROS action ComputePathToPose by distance and will trigger replanning when the traveled distance reaches 1.0 meters. It will run the ROS action ComputePathToPose with the goal and will store the resulting path each time the distance condition is met. Next, it will run the ROS action FollowPath using the most recently stored path with the specified controller after each planning event succeeds.

mrq: The mission describes a reactive goal-selection task that prioritizes three motion-generation goals based on live end-effector thresholds and proceeds with the first goal that becomes viable. First, the robot will execute the following actions (EEYThreshold, SetMGGoal) in sequence. If EEYThreshold is still running, it will re-evaluate the previous action. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for the EEYThreshold using the provided y_threshold and ee_y. It will run the custom action SetMGGoal with mp_param_x set to mp_3_x and mg_trans_x after the EEYThreshold succeeds. Next, it will execute the following actions (EEZThreshold, SetMGGoal) in sequence. If EEZThreshold is still running, it will re-evaluate the previous action. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for the EEZThreshold using the provided z_threshold and ee_z. It will run the custom action SetMGGoal with mp_param_x set to mp_2_x and mg_trans_x after the EEZThreshold succeeds. Finally, it will execute the following actions (SetMGGoal) in order. If any action succeeds, it will stop execution. Else it will keep executing and re-evaluating previous actions whenever an action is running. It will run the custom action SetMGGoal with mp_param_x set to mp_1_x and mg_trans_x as the default goal when the thresholded goals do not succeed.

The mission describes an animal-behavior control that prioritizes mating, birthing, egg laying, production, and sheltering based on conditions and retries until one path succeeds. First, the robot will execute the following actions (Sex, Birth, EggLayer, Producer, ShedTree) consecutively. If any action (Sex, Birth, EggLayer, Producer, ShedTree) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, it will start all over again until an action succeeds. It will execute the following actions (IsMale, IsAdult, TryHaveSex) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for IsMale. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for IsAdult. It will execute the custom action TryHaveSex after both conditions have been remembered as successful. Next, it will execute the following actions (IsFemale, IsReadyToGiveBirth, GiveBirth) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for IsFemale. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for IsReadyToGiveBirth. It will execute the custom action GiveBirth after both conditions have been remembered as successful. Next, it will execute the following actions (IsEggLayer, LayEgg) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for IsEggLayer. It will execute the custom action LayEgg after the condition has been remembered as successful. Next, it will execute the following actions (IsProducer, Produce) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for IsProducer. It will execute the custom action Produce after the condition has been remembered as successful. Finally, it will read blackboard value and compare it against a set of cases. If a case matches the blackboard value, it will execute corresponding the custom action ShedTree. Else if no case matches, it will execute default the custom action ShedTree.

The mission describes a sheltering and pasture-mobility control that manages leaving the shed by day, returning to the shed by night, and grazing by day outside the shed. First, it will execute the following actions (DaytimePastureExit, NighttimeReturn, DaytimeGraze) consecutively. If any action (DaytimePastureExit, NighttimeReturn, DaytimeGraze) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, it will start all over again until an action succeeds. It will execute the following actions (IsOnPasture, IsDay, IsInShed, LeaveShed, FindRandomPastureField, Move) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for IsOnPasture. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for IsDay. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for IsInShed. It will execute the custom action LeaveShed after the shed-presence check has been remembered as successful. It will execute the custom action FindRandomPastureField after leaving the shed. It will execute the custom action Move to reach the selected field. Next, it will execute the following actions (IsOnPasture, IsNight, not IsInShed, FindShed, Move, EnterShed) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for IsOnPasture. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for IsNight. It will execute inverted IsInShed. If IsInShed succeeds, it will reverse it to fail. Else if IsInShed fails, it will reverse it to succeed. It will execute the custom action FindShed after the inverted shed check has been remembered as successful. It will execute the custom action Move to approach the shed. It will execute the custom action EnterShed to complete sheltering. Finally, it will execute the following actions (IsDay, not IsInShed, RandomMove, Graze) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for IsDay. It will execute inverted IsInShed. If IsInShed succeeds, it will reverse it to fail. Else if IsInShed fails, it will reverse it to succeed. It will execute the custom action RandomMove to wander on pasture. It will execute the custom action Graze after moving.

<?xml version="1.0"?>
<root main_tree_to_execute="BehaviorTree">
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="BehaviorTree">
        <Repeat num_cycles="{num_cycles}">
            <Sequence>
                <Fallback>
                    <Condition ID="LTLPreCheck" ltl_state_current="{ltl_state_current}" ltl_state_desired_sequence="{ltl_state_desired_sequence}" ltl_state_executed_sequence="{ltl_state_executed_sequence}"/>
                    <ForceFailure>
                        <Action ID="ReplanningRequestLevel2" ltl_state_current="{ltl_state_current}" ltl_state_executed_sequence="{ltl_state_executed_sequence}" replanning_request="{replanning_request}"/>
                    </ForceFailure>
                </Fallback>
                <ReactiveSequence>
                    <Fallback>
                        <RetryUntilSuccesful num_attempts="3">
                            <Fallback>
                                <Condition ID="LocomotionStatusCheck" locomotion_status="{locomotion_status}"/>
                                <Sequence>
                                    <Action ID="RecoveryStand" locomotion_status="{locomotion_status}"/>
                                    <Delay delay_msec="2000">
                                        <Action ID="LocomotionStart" locomotion_status="{locomotion_status}"/>
                                    </Delay>
                                </Sequence>
                            </Fallback>
                        </RetryUntilSuccesful>
                        <ForceFailure>
                            <Action ID="ReplanningRequestLevel1" ltl_state_current="{ltl_state_current}" ltl_state_executed_sequence="{ltl_state_executed_sequence}" replanning_request="{replanning_request}"/>
                        </ForceFailure>
                    </Fallback>
                    <Fallback>
                        <Condition ID="ReactiveLTLStateCheck" ltl_state_current="{ltl_state_current}" ltl_state_desired_sequence="{ltl_state_desired_sequence}"/>
                        <ForceFailure>
                            <Action ID="ReplanningRequestLevel2" ltl_state_current="{ltl_state_current}" ltl_state_executed_sequence="{ltl_state_executed_sequence}" replanning_request="{replanning_request}"/>
                        </ForceFailure>
                    </Fallback>
                    <Fallback>
                        <Switch6 case_1="move" case_2="stay" case_3="pick" case_4="drop" case_5="start_training" case_6="terminate_training" variable="{bt_action_type}">
                            <Action ID="MoveAction" bt_action_type="{bt_action_type}" current_action="{current_action}" goal_sent="{goal_sent}" move_base_finished="{move_base_finished}" move_base_idle="{move_base_idle}"/>
                            <Action ID="StayAction" bt_action_type="{bt_action_type}" current_action="{current_action}" ltl_state_current="{ltl_state_current}"/>
                            <Action ID="PickAction" bt_action_type="{bt_action_type}" current_action="{current_action}" ltl_state_current="{ltl_state_current}"/>
                            <Action ID="DropAction" bt_action_type="{bt_action_type}" current_action="{current_action}" ltl_state_current="{ltl_state_current}"/>
                            <Action ID="GuideAction" bt_action_type="{bt_action_type}" current_action="{current_action}" ltl_state_current="{ltl_state_current}"/>
                            <Action ID="BackNormalAction" bt_action_type="{bt_action_type}" current_action="{current_action}" ltl_state_current="{ltl_state_current}"/>
                            <AlwaysFailure/>
                        </Switch6>
                        <ForceFailure>
                            <Action ID="ReplanningRequestLevel3" ltl_state_current="{ltl_state_current}" ltl_state_executed_sequence="{ltl_state_executed_sequence}" replanning_request="{replanning_request}"/>
                        </ForceFailure>
                    </Fallback>
                </ReactiveSequence>
                <Action ID="UpdateLTL" action_sequence="{action_sequence}" action_sequence_executed="{action_sequence_executed}" bt_action_type="{bt_action_type}" current_action="{current_action}" ltl_state_desired_sequence="{ltl_state_desired_sequence}"/>
            </Sequence>
        </Repeat>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <TreeNodesModel>
        <Action ID="BackNormalAction">
            <input_port name="bt_action_type"/>
            <input_port name="current_action"/>
            <inout_port name="ltl_state_current"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="DropAction">
            <input_port name="bt_action_type"/>

        <Condition ID="LTLPreCheck">

        </Action>
        <Condition ID="ReactiveLTLStateCheck">
            <input_port name="ltl_state_current"/>
            <input_port name="ltl_state_desired_sequence"/>
        </Condition>
        <Action ID="RecoveryStand">

        </Action>
        <Action ID="ReplanningRequestLevel1">
            <input_port name="ltl_state_current"/>

        <Action ID="ReplanningRequestLevel2">
            <input_port name="ltl_state_current"/>
            <inout_port name="ltl_state_executed_sequence"/>

        </Action>
        <Action ID="ReplanningRequestLevel3">
            <input_port name="ltl_state_current"/>

mrq:
The mission describes a looped task that governs LTL prechecks, locomotion readiness, action execution by action type, replanning requests at three levels, and LTL state updates across repeated cycles.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (Fallback, ReactiveSequence, UpdateLTL) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute the Fallback node that will execute the following actions (LTLPreCheck, ForceFailure) consecutively. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for the condition LTLPreCheck. It will execute ReplanningRequestLevel2 and make sure it always fails if the condition LTLPreCheck fails. It will then execute the ReactiveSequence that will execute the following actions (Fallback, Fallback, Fallback) in sequence. If any action fails, it will stop execution. While an action is still running, it will re-evaluate previous actions. It will execute the final action UpdateLTL after the ReactiveSequence succeeds.

Next, it will execute the following actions (RetryUntilSuccesful, ForceFailure) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will keep trying to execute the following actions (Fallback) until it succeeds. If try succeeds, it will stop retrying and continue. If try fails, it will keep retrying until it succeeds. It will execute the following actions (LocomotionStatusCheck, Sequence) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, it will start all over again until an action succeeds. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for the condition LocomotionStatusCheck. It will execute the following actions (RecoveryStand, Delay) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action RecoveryStand to reach a safe locomotion posture. It will execute delayed LocomotionStart. If execution succeeds, it will start a 2000 ms delay timer. After the delay period is exhausted, it will resume and run the custom action LocomotionStart to enter locomotion mode. It will execute ReplanningRequestLevel1 and make sure it always fails if locomotion readiness cannot be established.

Next, it will execute the following actions (ReactiveLTLStateCheck, ForceFailure) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for the condition ReactiveLTLStateCheck. It will execute ReplanningRequestLevel2 and make sure it always fails if the condition ReactiveLTLStateCheck fails.

Finally, it will read a blackboard value and compare it against a set of cases. If a case matches the blackboard value, it will execute the corresponding action. Else if no case matches, it will execute a default action. It will read bt_action_type and compare it against the set of cases move, stay, pick, drop, start_training, and terminate_training. It will run the custom action MoveAction when bt_action_type equals move. It will run the custom action StayAction when bt_action_type equals stay. It will run the custom action PickAction when bt_action_type equals pick. It will run the custom action DropAction when bt_action_type equals drop. It will run the custom action GuideAction when bt_action_type equals start_training. It will run the custom action BackNormalAction when bt_action_type equals terminate_training. It will execute the default AlwaysFailure when no case matches. It will execute ReplanningRequestLevel3 and make sure it always fails if the switch block does not produce a success.

<root main_tree_to_execute="MainTree">
  <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
    <PipelineSequence name="NavigateWithReplanning">
      <DistanceController distance="1.0">
        <ComputePathToPose goal="{goal}" path="{path}" planner_id="GridBased"/>
      </DistanceController>
      <FollowPath path="{path}"  controller_id="FollowPath"/>
    </PipelineSequence>
  </BehaviorTree>
</root>
mrq: The mission describes a navigation task that continuously replans a global path every 1 meter while advancing along that path. First, the robot will execute the following actions (DistanceController, FollowPath) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will, while an action is running or has succeeded, go to the next action. It will run the custom action DistanceController that wraps the ROS action ComputePathToPose with a distance threshold of 1.0 meter. It will have the DistanceController trigger path recomputation by running the ROS action ComputePathToPose with the provided goal and by updating the stored path whenever the traveled distance reaches 1.0 meter. It will then run the ROS action FollowPath using the most recent stored path with the specified controller while the DistanceController continues to allow progression when running or after success.

<root main_tree_to_execute="MainTree">
  <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
    <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="6" name="NavigateRecovery">
      <PipelineSequence name="NavigateWithReplanning">
        <RateController hz="1.0">
          <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="1" name="ComputePathToPose">
            <ComputePathToPose goal="{goal}" path="{path}" planner_id="GridBased"/>
            <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearGlobalCostmap-Context" service_name="global_costmap/clear_entirely_global_costmap"/>
          </RecoveryNode>
        </RateController>
        <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="1" name="FollowPath">
          <FollowPath path="{path}" controller_id="FollowPath"/>
          <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearLocalCostmap-Context" service_name="local_costmap/clear_entirely_local_costmap"/>
        </RecoveryNode>
      </PipelineSequence>
      <ReactiveFallback name="RecoveryFallback">
        <GoalUpdated/>
        <SequenceStar name="RecoveryActions">
          <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearLocalCostmap-Subtree" service_name="local_costmap/clear_entirely_local_costmap"/>
          <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearGlobalCostmap-Subtree" service_name="global_costmap/clear_entirely_global_costmap"/>
          <Spin spin_dist="1.57"/>
          <Wait wait_duration="5"/>
        </SequenceStar>
      </ReactiveFallback>
    </RecoveryNode>
  </BehaviorTree>
</root>
mrq:
The mission describes a navigation task that periodically replans a global path and follows it while applying structured recovery behaviors when needed.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (RateController, RecoveryNode) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will advance to the next action while an action is running or has succeeded. It will run the custom action RateController at 1.0 Hz to pace subsequent path computation attempts. It will execute main ROS action ComputePathToPose and make sure it always succeeds or is retried once by executing recovery action ClearEntireCostmap-Context and then retrying ComputePathToPose if the initial attempt fails. It will use the goal provided and will store the resulting path when ComputePathToPose succeeds.

Next, it will execute main ROS action FollowPath and make sure it always succeeds or is retried once by executing recovery action ClearEntireCostmap-Context and then retrying FollowPath if the initial attempt fails. It will run the ROS action FollowPath using the stored path with the specified controller after a path is available.

Finally, it will execute main PipelineSequence and, if it fails, execute recovery action ReactiveFallback and then retry the main PipelineSequence until the six retries of the recovery node are exhausted. It will execute the following actions (GoalUpdated, SequenceStar) in order. If GoalUpdated succeeds, it will stop execution. Else it will keep executing and re-evaluating previous actions whenever an action is running. It will execute the following actions (ClearLocalCostmap-Subtree, ClearGlobalCostmap-Subtree, Spin, Wait) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will run the ROS action ClearEntireCostmap for the local costmap. It will run the ROS action ClearEntireCostmap for the global costmap. It will run the ROS action Spin with a spin distance of 1.57. It will run the ROS action Wait for 5 seconds.

mrq:
The mission describes a complete task that pushes a puck, collects a goldonium item, weighs the item, clears an area, and coordinates these tasks in strict order.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (MoveAhead, Turn, MoveAhead, MoveAX_4, MoveAX_3, MoveAhead, MoveAX_3) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action MoveAhead to advance forward by a specified distance. It will run the custom action Turn to rotate left by 90 degrees. It will run the custom action MoveAhead to advance forward by a specified distance. It will run the custom action MoveAX_4 to turn the clamp 90 degrees to the right in joint mode to set its orientation. It will run the custom action MoveAX_3 to move the clamp forward in joint mode to contact the puck. It will run the custom action MoveAhead to push the puck forward by a specified distance while the clamp holds position. It will run the custom action MoveAX_3 to move the clamp backward in joint mode to retract after the push.

Next, it will execute the following actions (MoveAX_1, MoveAhead, MoveAX_1, MoveAX_3, MoveAX_4) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action MoveAX_1 to open the clamp in joint mode to prepare for grasping. It will run the custom action MoveAhead to move forward to the goldonium location by a specified distance. It will run the custom action MoveAX_1 to close the clamp in joint mode to grasp the goldonium securely. It will run the custom action MoveAX_3 to move the clamp backward in joint mode to secure the grasped item. It will run the custom action MoveAX_4 to turn the clamp 90 degrees to the right in joint mode to align for transport.

Next, it will execute the following actions (MoveAhead, Turn, MoveAhead, MoveAhead, MoveAX_2, OpenClamp, MoveAX_2) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action MoveAhead to move backward by a specified distance to create space. It will run the custom action Turn to rotate left by 90 degrees to face the approach direction. It will run the custom action MoveAhead to move forward by a specified distance to reach the waiting point. It will run the custom action MoveAhead to move forward by a specified distance to reach the scale. It will run the custom action MoveAX_2 to move the clamp forward in joint mode to position the item over the scale. It will run the custom action OpenClamp to release the item onto the scale. It will run the custom action MoveAX_2 to move the clamp backward in joint mode to clear the scale.

Finally, it will run the custom action MoveAhead to move backward by a specified distance to start clearing the area. It will run the custom action Turn to rotate left by a specified angle to face the target clearing direction. It will run the custom action MoveAhead to move forward by a specified distance to finish clearing the area.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (SubTree PushPuck, SubTree GetG, SubTree WeightG, SubTree ChasseNeige) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action SubTree PushPuck to complete the puck pushing procedure. It will run the custom action SubTree GetG to complete the goldonium collection procedure. It will run the custom action SubTree WeightG to complete the weighing procedure. It will run the custom action SubTree ChasseNeige to complete the area clearing procedure.

mrq:
The mission describes an access task that attempts to move through a doorway and, if that fails, tries a recovery subtree before attempting an alternative path through a window. First, the robot will execute the following actions (Sequence, SubTree DoorClosed, PassThroughWindow) consecutively. It will execute the following actions (IsDoorOpen, PassThroughDoor) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action IsDoorOpen and will proceed only if the door state check succeeds. It will run the custom action PassThroughDoor after confirming the door is open. It will move to the SubTree DoorClosed if the initial sequence fails to pass through the door. It will execute PassThroughWindow if the SubTree DoorClosed fails to enable passage through the door.

Next, it will execute the following actions (Inverter, RetryUntilSuccessful, PassThroughDoor) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute inverted IsDoorOpen and will reverse success to fail and failure to succeed to confirm the door is closed before attempting to open it. It will keep trying to execute OpenDoor until it succeeds. It will run the custom action PassThroughDoor after the door has been confirmed open by the successful opening attempt.

                    <Action ID="ExecuteGroupLinearJointStates" goal="0.7015 0.4237 1.7017 -0.0747 -0.0040 -0.0806" group_name="arm" name="post middle left" service_name="execute_group_joint_states" tolerance="0.01"/>

                    <Action ID="ExecuteGroupShift" axis="z" goal="0.1" group_name="arm" is_absolute="0" name="lift up" service_name="execute_group_shift" tolerance="0.01"/>

                    <Action ID="ExecuteGroupLinearJointStates" goal="0.7015 -1.8999 -2.0660 -0.7844 0.0080 -0.0615" group_name="arm" name="place left" service_name="execute_group_joint_states" tolerance="0.01"/>
                    <Action ID="ExecuteGroupLinearJointStates" goal="0.7015 -0.4631 -1.6642 -1.0472 0.0081 -0.0615" group_name="arm" name="post middle left" service_name="execute_group_joint_states" tolerance="0.01"/>

        <Action ID="ExecuteGroupAngularJointStates">

        <Action ID="ExecuteGroupLinearJointStates">

            <input_port default="0.01" name="tolerance"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="ExecuteGroupManyPoses">
            <input_port name="constraint">If 'r', 'p', or 'y' is given, the eef_frame should not rotate about itself along x-, y-, or z-axis. You can combine them like 'rp', 'ry', 'py', and 'rpy'. Default ''</input_port>
            <input_port default="0.1" name="eef_step"/>
            <input_port default="0" name="goal_type">0: Pose wrt the global base frame (robot base); 1: Pose wrt the base frame transferred to current eef base position; 2: pose wrt the eef frame</input_port>
            <input_port default="0 0 0 0 0 0 1" name="goals">Goals for each waypoint to reach, seperate with ; </input_port>
            <input_port default="arm" name="group_name">The group providing the reference frame</input_port>
            <input_port default="execute_group_many_poses" name="service_name"/>
            <input_port default="0.01" name="tolerance"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="ExecuteGroupPose">
            <input_port name="constraint">If 'r', 'p', or 'y' is given, the eef_frame should not rotate about itself along x-, y-, or z-axis. You can combine them like 'rp', 'ry', 'py', and 'rpy'. Default ''</input_port>
            <input_port default="0 0 0 0 0 0 1" name="goal"/>
            <input_port default="0" name="goal_type">0: Pose wrt the global base frame (robot base); 1: Pose wrt the base frame transferred to current eef base position; 2: pose wrt the eef frame</input_port>
            <input_port default="arm" name="group_name">The group providing the reference frame</input_port>
            <input_port default="execute_group_pose" name="service_name"/>
            <input_port default="0.01" name="tolerance"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="ExecuteGroupShift">
            <input_port default="z" name="axis">Could be x y z roll pitch yaw</input_port>
            <input_port default="0" name="goal"/>
            <input_port default="arm" name="group_name"/>
            <input_port default="0" name="is_absolute"/>
            <input_port default="execute_group_shift" name="service_name"/>
            <input_port default="0.01" name="tolerance"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="ExecutePlanning">
            <input_port default="0" name="category">0 for large box, 1 for small box</input_port>
            <output_port name="pick_pose">Pose</output_port>
            <output_port name="place_obj_pose">Pose</output_port>
            <output_port name="place_pose">Pose</output_port>
            <input_port name="pose">Pose</input_port>

            <output_port name="pre_place_pose">Pose</output_port>

        <Action ID="ExecuteRemoveObject">

            <input_port name="obj_name"/>
            <input_port default="execute_remove_object" name="service_name"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="ExecuteSuction">

            <input_port default="execute_suction" name="service_name"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="FetchDetectedInfo">
            <output_port name="category">String, 0 for large box, 1 for small box</output_port>
            <output_port name="pose">Pose</output_port>
            <input_port default="fetch_detected_info" name="service_name"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="SenseObjectPose">

            <output_port name="pose">Pose</output_port>
            <input_port default="sense_object_pose" name="service_name"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="StoreDetectedInfo">

            <input_port name="pose">Pose</input_port>
            <input_port default="store_detected_info" name="service_name"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="TypeInPose">
            <output_port name="pose_on_blackboard">Pose on blackboard</output_port>
            <input_port default="type_in_pose" name="service_name"/>
            <input_port default="0 0 0 0 0 0 1" name="type_in_pose">Pose to be typed in</input_port>
        </Action>
    </TreeNodesModel>
    <!-- ////////// -->
</root>
mrq:
The mission describes a manipulation task that prepares a constrained scene, creates a temporary surface barrier, and performs left and right pick-and-place motions in strict order.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (ExecuteAddPlane, ExecuteAddBox, ExecuteAddBox, ExecuteAddBox, ExecuteAddBox) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action ExecuteAddPlane to add a ground plane to the scene for the arm group. It will run the custom action ExecuteAddBox to add a left wall with the specified absolute pose and size. It will run the custom action ExecuteAddBox to add a right wall with the specified absolute pose and size. It will run the custom action ExecuteAddBox to add a top wall with the specified absolute pose and size. It will run the custom action ExecuteAddBox to add a conveyor with the specified absolute pose and size.

Next, it will execute the custom action ExecuteAddBox to add a thin box-shaped surface named boxsur at the specified absolute pose and size for the arm group.

Next, it will execute Execute [Action] for a set number of times or infinitely. It will execute the following actions (ExecuteGroupLinearJointStates, ExecuteRemoveObject, ExecuteGroupManyPoses, ExecuteGroupShift, ExecuteGroupShift, ExecuteGroupLinearJointStates, ExecuteGroupLinearJointStates, ExecuteGroupLinearJointStates, ExecuteAddBox) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action ExecuteGroupLinearJointStates to move the arm to the specified joint configuration named pre middle left. It will run the custom action ExecuteRemoveObject to remove the object named boxsur exactly from the scene. It will run the custom action ExecuteGroupManyPoses to drive the end effector through the given pick pose waypoints without self-rotation constraints and with the specified step and tolerance. It will run the custom action ExecuteGroupShift to lift the end effector by 0.1 along the z axis relative to the current pose. It will run the custom action ExecuteGroupShift to pull the end effector by -0.4 along the x axis relative to the current pose. It will run the custom action ExecuteGroupLinearJointStates to move the arm to the specified joint configuration named post middle left. It will run the custom action ExecuteGroupLinearJointStates to move the arm to the specified joint configuration named place. It will run the custom action ExecuteGroupLinearJointStates to move the arm back to the specified joint configuration named post middle left. It will run the custom action ExecuteAddBox to restore the thin box-shaped surface named boxsur at the specified absolute pose and size for the arm group.

Finally, it will execute Execute [Action] for a set number of times or infinitely. It will execute the following actions (ExecuteGroupLinearJointStates, ExecuteRemoveObject, ExecuteGroupManyPoses, ExecuteGroupShift, ExecuteGroupShift, ExecuteGroupLinearJointStates, ExecuteGroupLinearJointStates, ExecuteGroupLinearJointStates, ExecuteAddBox) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action ExecuteGroupLinearJointStates to move the arm to the specified joint configuration named pre middle right. It will run the custom action ExecuteRemoveObject to remove the object named boxsur exactly from the scene. It will run the custom action ExecuteGroupManyPoses to drive the end effector through the given right-side pick pose waypoints without self-rotation constraints and with the specified step and tolerance. It will run the custom action ExecuteGroupShift to lift the end effector by 0.1 along the z axis relative to the current pose. It will run the custom action ExecuteGroupShift to pull the end effector by -0.4 along the x axis relative to the current pose. It will run the custom action ExecuteGroupLinearJointStates to move the arm to the specified joint configuration named post middle left. It will run the custom action ExecuteGroupLinearJointStates to move the arm to the specified joint configuration named place left. It will run the custom action ExecuteGroupLinearJointStates to move the arm back to the specified joint configuration named post middle left. It will run the custom action ExecuteAddBox to restore the thin box-shaped surface named boxsur at the specified absolute pose and size for the arm group.

mrq: The mission describes a console-driven Fibonacci computation that prints context, reads an order from memory, computes the sequence, and prints the result in strict order. First, the robot will execute the following actions (Print, Print, Print, Fibonacci, Print, Print) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action Print to display the banner message "=== Fibonacci demo ===". It will run the custom action Print to display the label "Order". It will run the custom action Print to display the current value of the blackboard key fibonacci_order. It will run the custom action Fibonacci with the order taken from the blackboard key fibonacci_order and will store the resulting list in the blackboard key fibonacci_sequence. It will run the custom action Print to display the label "Sequence". It will run the custom action Print to display the current value of the blackboard key fibonacci_sequence.

mrq:
The mission describes a single arm-manipulation routine that moves an arm group to a preparation pose, lowers the end-effector, and then executes a square-drawing motion plan in strict order.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (ExecuteGroupAngularJointStates, ExecuteGroupPose, ExecuteGroupPlan) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action ExecuteGroupAngularJointStates to send the arm group to the angular joint state goal "0 -10 10 0 0 0" within a tolerance of 0.01 using the service execute_group_joint_states. It will then run the custom action ExecuteGroupPose to lower the arm by applying the relative pose "0 0 -0.3 0 0 0 1" to the same arm group within a tolerance of 0.01 using the service execute_group_pose. It will then run the custom action ExecuteGroupPlan to execute a relative motion plan named "Draw square" for the arm group with the specified sequence of poses "0 0.3 0 0 0 0 1; 0 0 0.6 0 0 0 1; 0 -0.6 0 0 0 0 1; 0 0 -0.6 0 0 0 1; 0 0.3 0 0 0 0 1; 0 0 0.3 0 0 0 1" under no-collision permissions set to 0 using the service execute_group_plan with the final timestamp set to 30.

                        <Action ID="PassThroughWindow"/>

                            <Action ID="PassThroughWindow"/>

mrq:
The mission describes a traversal and securing task that attempts multiple window-based passages or a combined window-and-door passage before closing a door.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (root_Fallback, CloseDoor) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute the following actions (SeqB, SeqE) consecutively. If any action (SeqB or SeqE) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will execute the following actions (PassThroughWindow, SeqC) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute the following actions (PassThroughWindow, PassThroughWindow, SeqD) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute the following actions (PassThroughWindow, PassThroughWindow, PassThroughWindow, PassThroughDoor) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute the custom action PassThroughWindow as the first window traversal attempt. It will execute the custom action PassThroughWindow as the second window traversal attempt. It will execute the custom action PassThroughWindow as the third window traversal attempt. It will execute the custom action PassThroughDoor as the door traversal that follows the three window attempts. It will execute the following actions (PassThroughWindow, SeqF, PassThroughWindow) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute the following actions (PassThroughWindow, SeqG, PassThroughWindow) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute the following actions (PassThroughWindow) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute the custom action PassThroughWindow as the first window traversal in the fallback branch. It will execute the custom action PassThroughWindow as the middle window traversal in the fallback branch. It will execute the custom action PassThroughWindow as the final window traversal in the fallback branch. It will execute the ROS action CloseDoor after the root_Fallback succeeds.

<?xml version="1.0"?>
<root main_tree_to_execute="MainTree">
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
        <Sequence>
            <Fallback name="root_Fallback">
                <Sequence name="door_open_sequence">
                    <Condition ID="IsDoorOpen"/>
                    <Action ID="PassThroughDoor"/>
                </Sequence>
                <Sequence name="door_closed_sequence">
                    <Inverter>
                        <Condition ID="IsDoorOpen"/>
                    </Inverter>
                    <RetryUntilSuccesful num_attempts="4">
                        <Action ID="OpenDoor"/>
                    </RetryUntilSuccesful>
                    <Action ID="PassThroughDoor"/>
                </Sequence>
                <Action ID="PassThroughWindow"/>
            </Fallback>
            <Action ID="CloseDoor"/>
        </Sequence>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <TreeNodesModel>
        <Action ID="CloseDoor"/>
        <SubTree ID="DoorClosed"/>
        <Condition ID="IsDoorOpen"/>
        <Action ID="OpenDoor"/>
        <Action ID="PassThroughDoor"/>
        <Action ID="PassThroughWindow"/>
    </TreeNodesModel>
    <!-- ////////// -->
</root>
mrq:
The mission describes a door traversal task that attempts to pass through an opening and then closes the door after completion.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (root_Fallback, CloseDoor) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute the following actions (door_open_sequence, door_closed_sequence, PassThroughWindow) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will execute the following actions (IsDoorOpen, PassThroughDoor) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for IsDoorOpen. It will run the custom action PassThroughDoor if the door open check succeeds. It will execute inverted IsDoorOpen. If IsDoorOpen succeeds, it will reverse it to fail. Else if IsDoorOpen fails, it will reverse it to succeed. It will keep trying to execute the custom action OpenDoor until it succeeds. If try succeeds, it will stop retrying and continue. If try fails, it will keep retrying until it succeeds. It will run the custom action PassThroughDoor after the door is opened. It will run the custom action PassThroughWindow if both door-based strategies fail. It will run the custom action CloseDoor after a successful traversal.

mrq: The mission describes a simple task that runs two custom actions in order with a blackboard write between them. First, the robot will execute the following actions (go1, SetBlackboard, go2) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action go1 before any blackboard update occurs. It will read blackboard value and compare it against a set of cases only when required by child nodes; however, here it will instead set a value. It will execute delayed SetBlackboard and make sure to start a delay timer only if a delay is defined; after setting the output_key ${valueA} to 8, it will resume execution. It will run the custom action go2 only after the blackboard has stored the value 8 in ${valueA}.

mrq: The mission describes a single sequential data-processing task that feeds inputs to actions and then writes multiple outputs to blackboard keys in strict order. First, the robot will execute the following actions (inOnlyA, inOnlyB, inOnlyB, inOnlyA, outOnlyA, outOnlyB, outOnlyC, outOnlyD, outOnlyE, outOnlyF) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action inOnlyA with an empty input string. It will run the custom action inOnlyB with the input string ice. It will run the custom action inOnlyB with the default input string foo. It will run the custom action inOnlyA with the input string resolved from the blackboard key foo.baz. It will run the custom action outOnlyA and write its output to an unnamed sink without storing it. It will run the custom action outOnlyB and write its output to the blackboard key baz. It will run the custom action outOnlyC and write its output to the blackboard key baz after resolving ${baz} at runtime. It will run the custom action outOnlyD and write its output to the blackboard key foo.goo. It will run the custom action outOnlyE and write its output to the blackboard key fan. It will run the custom action outOnlyF and write its output to the blackboard key long.untraveled.path.

<root main_tree_to_execute="MainTree">
  <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
    <PipelineSequence name="NavigateWithReplanning">
      <DistanceController distance="1.0">
        <ComputePathToPose goal="{goal}" path="{path}" planner_id="GridBased"/>
      </DistanceController>
      <FollowPath path="{path}"  controller_id="FollowPath"/>
    </PipelineSequence>
  </BehaviorTree>
</root>
mrq: The mission describes a navigation task that continuously computes a global path at 1-meter intervals and advances along that path while allowing downstream execution to proceed when upstream actions are running or have succeeded. First, the robot will execute the following actions (DistanceController, FollowPath) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will, while an action is running or has succeeded, go to the next action. Next, it will execute delayed ComputePathToPose under a 1.0 meter distance trigger inside the custom action DistanceController. It will start the delay-like distance timer after the ComputePathToPose execution succeeds and will resume ComputePathToPose when the traveled distance since the last plan reaches 1.0 meter. Finally, it will run the ROS action FollowPath using the latest stored path with the specified controller as soon as the pipeline advances, and it will keep advancing even while path computation is running or has recently succeeded.

BT:
<root main_tree_to_execute="MainTree">
  <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
    <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="6" name="NavigateRecovery">
      <PipelineSequence name="NavigateWithReplanning">
        <RateController hz="1.0">
          <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="1" name="ComputePathToPose">
            <ComputePathToPose goal="{goal}" path="{path}" planner_id="GridBased"/>
            <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearGlobalCostmap-Context" service_name="global_costmap/clear_entirely_global_costmap"/>
          </RecoveryNode>
        </RateController>
        <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="1" name="FollowPath">
          <FollowPath path="{path}" controller_id="FollowPath"/>
          <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearLocalCostmap-Context" service_name="local_costmap/clear_entirely_local_costmap"/>
        </RecoveryNode>
      </PipelineSequence>
      <ReactiveFallback name="RecoveryFallback">
        <GoalUpdated/>
        <SequenceStar name="RecoveryActions">
          <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearLocalCostmap-Subtree" service_name="local_costmap/clear_entirely_local_costmap"/>
          <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearGlobalCostmap-Subtree" service_name="global_costmap/clear_entirely_global_costmap"/>
          <Spin spin_dist="1.57"/>
          <Wait wait_duration="5"/>
        </SequenceStar>
      </ReactiveFallback>
    </RecoveryNode>
  </BehaviorTree>
</root>
mrq:
The mission describes a navigation task that periodically replans a global path at 1 Hz, follows the path with local control, and applies layered recoveries when failures occur.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (RateController, RecoveryNode, RecoveryNode) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. While an action is running or has succeeded, it will go to the next action. It will run the custom action RateController to gate planning at 1.0 Hz. It will execute main the ROS action ComputePathToPose and, if it fails, execute recovery action the ROS action ClearEntireCostmap and then retry the main action once. It will execute main the ROS action FollowPath and, if it fails, execute recovery action the ROS action ClearEntireCostmap and then retry the main action once.

Next, it will execute main the PipelineSequence subtree and, if it fails, execute recovery action the ReactiveFallback subtree and then retry the main action until six retries are exhausted.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (GoalUpdated, SequenceStar) in order. If any action succeeds, it will stop execution. Else it will keep executing and re-evaluating previous actions whenever an action is running. It will execute the custom action GoalUpdated and, if it succeeds, it will stop execution. It will execute the following actions (ClearEntireCostmap, ClearEntireCostmap, Spin, Wait) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning.

mrq: The mission describes a manipulation and navigation routine that positions the robot at successive waypoints while coordinating head, torso, and dual-arm poses in strict order. First, the robot will execute the following actions (HeadControl, TorsoControl, MoveBase, GetArmJointsForPose, SetArmJointsLeft, SetArmJointsRight, HeadControl, MoveBase, GetArmJointsForPose, SetArmJointsLeft, SetArmJointsRight, HeadControl, MoveBase, GetArmJointsForPose, SetArmJointsLeft, SetArmJointsRight, HeadControl, MoveBase, HeadControl, TorsoControl, TorsoControl, TorsoControl, GetArmJointsForPose, SetArmJointsLeft, SetArmJointsRight) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action HeadControl to set pitch to 0.0 degrees and yaw to 90.0 degrees. It will run the custom action TorsoControl to set a relative height of 0.4. It will run the custom action MoveBase to reach the goal at coordinates 0;-3;0. It will run the custom action GetArmJointsForPose to retrieve joints for the pose UPTRAY and store them in joints_pose. It will run the custom action SetArmJointsLeft using joints_pose. It will run the custom action SetArmJointsRight using joints_pose. It will run the custom action HeadControl to set pitch to -45.0 degrees and yaw to 90.0 degrees. It will run the custom action MoveBase to reach the goal at coordinates 1;-3;0. It will run the custom action GetArmJointsForPose to retrieve joints for the pose EXTENDED and store them in joints_pose. It will run the custom action SetArmJointsLeft using joints_pose. It will run the custom action SetArmJointsRight using joints_pose. It will run the custom action HeadControl to set pitch to 45.0 degrees and yaw to 90.0 degrees. It will run the custom action MoveBase to reach the goal at coordinates 2;-3;0. It will run the custom action GetArmJointsForPose to retrieve joints for the pose GRIPPING and store them in joints_pose. It will run the custom action SetArmJointsLeft using joints_pose. It will run the custom action SetArmJointsRight using joints_pose. It will run the custom action HeadControl to set pitch to 0.0 degrees and yaw to 90.0 degrees. It will run the custom action MoveBase to reach the goal at coordinates 3;-3;0. It will run the custom action HeadControl to set pitch to 0.0 degrees and yaw to 120.0 degrees. It will run the custom action TorsoControl to set a relative height of 1. It will run the custom action TorsoControl to set a relative height of 0. It will run the custom action TorsoControl to set a relative height of 0.4. It will run the custom action GetArmJointsForPose to retrieve joints for the pose REST and store them in joints_pose. It will run the custom action SetArmJointsLeft using joints_pose. It will run the custom action SetArmJointsRight using joints_pose.

<root main_tree_to_execute="MainTree">
  <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
    <PipelineSequence name="NavigateWithReplanning">
      <RateController hz="1.0">
        <Sequence>
          <GoalUpdater input_goal="{goal}" output_goal="{updated_goal}">
            <ComputePathToPose goal="{updated_goal}" path="{path}" planner_id="GridBased"/>
          </GoalUpdater>
         <TruncatePath distance="1.0" input_path="{path}" output_path="{truncated_path}"/>
        </Sequence>
      </RateController>
      <KeepRunningUntilFailure>
        <FollowPath path="{truncated_path}" controller_id="FollowPath"/>
      </KeepRunningUntilFailure>
    </PipelineSequence>
  </BehaviorTree>
</root>
mrq:
The mission describes a dynamic navigation task that continually updates a goal, recomputes a path, truncates that path to maintain a following distance, and advances path execution as planning progresses.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (RateController, KeepRunningUntilFailure) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. While an action is running or has succeeded, it will go to the next action. It will use the custom action RateController to throttle internal updates at 1.0 Hz. It will execute the custom action KeepRunningUntilFailure to repeatedly run path following as long as it does not fail.

Next, it will execute the following actions (Sequence) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action GoalUpdater to refresh the input goal and to produce an updated_goal. It will run the ROS action ComputePathToPose with the updated_goal to produce a path using the GridBased planner. It will run the custom action TruncatePath to create a truncated_path that ends 1.0 meters before the target along the computed path.

Finally, it will execute FollowPath. If FollowPath succeeds, it will restart it. Else if FollowPath fails, it will stop looping and continue execution. It will run the ROS action FollowPath using the truncated_path with the FollowPath controller.

mrq: The mission describes a navigation task that computes a global path under a speed-controlled planner and then follows that path while advancing as soon as planning runs or succeeds. First, the robot will execute the following actions (SpeedController, FollowPath) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute the following actions (ComputePathToPose) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails under a speed controller that limits planning updates between 0.1 Hz and 1.0 Hz with speeds between 0.0 m/s and 0.26 m/s and a filter duration of 0.3 seconds. It will run the ROS action ComputePathToPose with the provided goal and will store the resulting path. It will run the ROS action FollowPath using the stored path with the specified controller while the planning action is running or has succeeded.
