<root main_tree_to_execute="BehaviorTree">
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="BehaviorTree">
        <Fallback>
            <Sequence>
                <SubTree ID="charging_full_st"/>
                <Action ID="DebugAction" string="Vou patrulhar"/>
                <Action ID="GeneralActionlib" action_name="random_movement" constants="0,1,3" variables="" result="result"/>
                <Action goal_pose="{result}" ID="MoveBase" action_name="move_base" frame_id="map"/>
                <Action ID="DebugAction" string="cheguei!"/>
                <Action seconds="5" ID="WaitForXSeconds" result="SUCCESS"/>
            </Sequence>
            <Action ID="CallEmptySrv" service_name="/move_base/clear_costmaps"/>
        </Fallback>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="ChargeST">
        <SequenceStar>
            <Action language="pt_PT" voice="pt-PT-Wavenet-D" ID="Speak" utterance="Tenho fome" action_name="/gcloud_tts"/>
            <Action seconds="2" ID="WaitForXSeconds" name="Wait2Seconds" result="SUCCESS"/>
            <RetryUntilSuccesful num_attempts="3">
                <Action goal_pose="-4.88723403052;-6.0285904746;0.0;0.0;0.0;0.997843662386;0.0503172395835" ID="MoveBase" name="Go to first point" action_name="move_base" frame_id="map"/>
            </RetryUntilSuccesful>
            <Parallel failure_threshold="1" success_threshold="2">
                <Action ID="Charge" action_name="charging_action" action="CHARGE"/>
                <Repeat num_cycles="10">
                    <Action language="pt_PT" voice="pt-PT-Wavenet-D" ID="Speak" utterance="bip bip" action_name="/gcloud_tts"/>
                </Repeat>
            </Parallel>
        </SequenceStar>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="charging_full_st">
        <Sequence>
            <Fallback>
                <Sequence>
                    <Action ID="DebugAction" string="checking battery state"/>
                    <Action percentage="k_bat_perc" ID="CheckBattery" battery_state="k_bat_state" service_name="/vizzy_batteries_state"/>
                    <Condition A="{k_bat_state}" Condition="==" ID="CompareInt" B="3"/>
                    <Action ID="CheckCharging" service_name="battery_charging_state" charging_state="charge_state"/>
                    <Condition A="{charge_state}" Condition="==" ID="CompareInt" B="0"/>
                    <SubTree ID="ChargeST"/>
                </Sequence>
                <Sequence>
                    <Action ID="DebugAction" string="checking if batteries are charged"/>
                    <Action ID="CheckCharging" service_name="battery_charging_state" charging_state="charge_state"/>
                    <Condition A="{charge_state}" Condition="==" ID="CompareInt" B="1"/>
                    <Action percentage="k_bat_perc" ID="CheckBattery" battery_state="k_bat_state" service_name="/vizzy_batteries_state"/>
                    <Condition A="{k_bat_state}" Condition="==" ID="CompareInt" B="0"/>
                    <Action language="pt_PT" voice="pt-PT-Wavenet-D" ID="Speak" utterance="Estou cheio, vou sair da estação" action_name="/gcloud_tts"/>
                    <Action ID="Charge" action_name="charging_action" action="STOP_CHARGE"/>
                </Sequence>
                <Sequence>
                    <Action ID="DebugAction" string="check if failed"/>
                    <Action percentage="k_bat_perc" ID="CheckBattery" battery_state="k_bat_state" service_name="/vizzy_batteries_state"/>
                    <Condition A="{k_bat_state}" Condition="==" ID="CompareInt" B="3"/>
                    <Action ID="CheckCharging" service_name="battery_charging_state" charging_state="charge_state"/>
                    <Condition A="{charge_state}" Condition="==" ID="CompareInt" B="0"/>
                    <Action language="pt_PT" voice="pt-PT-Wavenet-D" ID="Speak" utterance="Ajudem-me, tenho fome e não consigo carregar" action_name="/gcloud_tts"/>
                    <Action ID="CallEmptySrv" service_name="/move_base/clear_costmaps"/>
                    <Action seconds="10" ID="WaitForXSeconds" result="SUCCESS"/>
                </Sequence>
                <Sequence>
                    <Action percentage="k_bat_perc" ID="CheckBattery" battery_state="k_bat_state" service_name="/vizzy_batteries_state"/>
                    <Condition A="{k_bat_state}" Condition="!=" ID="CompareInt" B="3"/>
                </Sequence>
            </Fallback>
            <Sequence>
                <Action ID="CheckCharging" service_name="battery_charging_state" charging_state="charge_state"/>
                <Condition A="{charge_state}" Condition="==" ID="CompareInt" B="0"/>
                <Action ID="DebugAction" string="I can move"/>
            </Sequence>
        </Sequence>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <TreeNodesModel>
        <Action ID="ArmCartesian">
            <input_port name="action_name"/>
            <input_port name="frame_id"/>
            <input_port name="pose"/>
            <input_port name="type"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="ArmRoutines">
            <input_port name="gesture"/>
            <input_port name="topic"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="CallEmptySrv">
            <input_port name="service_name"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="Charge">
            <input_port name="action"/>
            <input_port name="action_name"/>
        </Action>
        <SubTree ID="ChargeST"/>
        <Action ID="CheckBattery">
            <output_port type="int" name="battery_state"/>
            <output_port type="double" name="percentage"/>
            <input_port type="std::string" name="service_name"/>
        </Action>
        <Condition ID="CheckBool">
            <input_port name="variable"/>
        </Condition>
        <Action ID="CheckCharging">
            <output_port type="int" name="charging_state"/>
            <input_port type="std::string" name="service_name"/>
        </Action>
        <Condition ID="CompareDouble">
            <input_port name="A"/>
            <input_port name="B"/>
            <input_port name="Condition"/>
        </Condition>
        <Condition ID="CompareInt">
            <input_port name="A"/>
            <input_port name="B"/>
            <input_port name="Condition"/>
        </Condition>
        <Action ID="DebugAction">
            <input_port type="std::string" name="string"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="Destroy"/>
        <Action ID="DetectBlocks">
            <output_port type="social_cues::BlockList_<std::allocator<void> >" name="block_list"/>
            <input_port type="std::string" name="block_topic"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="DetectFaces">
            <output_port name="faceListOut"/>
            <input_port name="face_topic"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="FaceCounter">
            <input_port name="faceListIn"/>
            <input_port name="number_faces"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="GazeAtTarget">
            <input_port name="action_name"/>
            <input_port name="fixation_pose"/>
            <input_port name="frame_id"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="GeneralActionlib">
            <input_port name="action_name"/>
            <input_port name="constants">A coma separated list of constants.</input_port>
            <output_port name="result">This is a string</output_port>
            <input_port name="variables">A coma separated list of variables.</input_port>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="GetFloat64">
            <output_port type="double" name="number"/>
            <input_port type="std::string" name="topic"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="GetInt16">
            <output_port name="number"/>
            <input_port name="topic"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="GetPoseArray">
            <output_port name="pose_array"/>
            <input_port name="topic"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="GiveFeedback">
            <input_port default="850" name="almost_thr"/>
            <input_port name="correct_blocks"/>
            <input_port name="incorrect_blocks"/>
            <input_port default="0" name="personality"/>
            <input_port name="score"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="MoveBase">
            <input_port name="action_name"/>
            <input_port name="frame_id"/>
            <input_port name="goal_pose"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="MoveTorso">
            <input_port name="angle"/>
            <input_port name="topic"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="ResetVars"/>
        <Action ID="SelectBlock">
            <input_port type="social_cues::BlockList_<std::allocator<void> >" name="block_list"/>
            <output_port type="geometry_msgs::PoseStamped_<std::allocator<void> >" name="block_pose"/>
            <input_port type="std::string" name="position_in_list"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="SelectFieldFromPoseStamped">
            <input_port name="field"/>
            <output_port name="output_val"/>
            <input_port name="pose_stamped"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="SelectPose">
            <input_port name="pose_array"/>
            <input_port name="pose_stamped"/>
            <input_port name="position_in_list"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="SenseScore">
            <output_port name="score"/>
            <input_port name="score_topic"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="Speak">
            <input_port name="action_name"/>
            <input_port name="language"/>
            <input_port name="utterance"/>
            <input_port name="voice"/>
        </Action>
        <SubTree ID="SubTree"/>
        <Action ID="TimerAction">
            <input_port name="s_between_success">Time (s) before this node returns SUCCESS. It will return FAILURE in the meanwhile</input_port>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="WaitForXSeconds">
            <input_port default="SUCCESS" name="result">Result after specified time: SUCCESS or FAILURE</input_port>
            <input_port default="5" name="seconds">Time in seconds</input_port>
        </Action>
        <SubTree ID="charging_full_st"/>
    </TreeNodesModel>
    <!-- ////////// -->
</root>

The mission describes a patrol and charging management task that prioritizes checking charging status before random patrolling and movement.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (charging_full_st, DebugAction, GeneralActionlib, MoveBase, DebugAction, WaitForXSeconds) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. It will run the subtree charging_full_st and will continue only if that subtree fails. It will execute the custom action DebugAction to log the message “Vou patrulhar”. It will run the custom action GeneralActionlib with the action_name random_movement and with constants set to 0,1,3, and it will store the resulting goal in result. It will run the ROS action MoveBase using the stored result as the goal in the map frame. It will execute the custom action DebugAction to log the message “cheguei!”. It will run the custom action WaitForXSeconds for 5 seconds and it will return SUCCESS after the wait completes. If all of these actions fail, it will run the custom action CallEmptySrv to clear the costmaps on the /move_base/clear_costmaps service.

Next, it will execute the following actions (Speak, WaitForXSeconds, RetryUntilSuccess, Parallel) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will run the ROS action Speak to say “Tenho fome” using the pt-PT-Wavenet-D voice. It will run the custom action WaitForXSeconds for 2 seconds and it will return SUCCESS after the wait completes. It will keep trying to execute the ROS action MoveBase to the fixed pose until it succeeds, with up to 3 attempts configured for retry. It will execute the following actions (Charge, Repeat) simultaneously. If either a success threshold is reached or all actions succeed, the execution was successful. Else if any action fails, the execution fails. It will run the ROS action Charge with action CHARGE. It will execute the action Speak for a set number of times by repeating it 10 cycles to say “bip bip” using the pt-PT-Wavenet-D voice.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (Fallback, Sequence) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. While an action is running or has succeeded, it will go to the next action. It will execute the following actions (Sequence, Sequence, Sequence, Sequence) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will execute the following actions (DebugAction, CheckBattery, CompareInt, CheckCharging, CompareInt, ChargeST) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute the custom action DebugAction to log the message “checking battery state”. It will run the custom action CheckBattery to read k_bat_state and k_bat_perc. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule by verifying that k_bat_state equals 3. It will run the custom action CheckCharging to read charge_state. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule by verifying that charge_state equals 0. It will run the subtree ChargeST. It will execute the following actions (DebugAction, CheckCharging, CompareInt, CheckBattery, CompareInt, Speak, Charge) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute the custom action DebugAction to log the message “checking if batteries are charged”. It will run the custom action CheckCharging to read charge_state. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule by verifying that charge_state equals 1. It will run the custom action CheckBattery to read k_bat_state and k_bat_perc. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule by verifying that k_bat_state equals 0. It will run the ROS action Speak to say “Estou cheio, vou sair da estação” using the pt-PT-Wavenet-D voice. It will run the ROS action Charge with action STOP_CHARGE. It will execute the following actions (DebugAction, CheckBattery, CompareInt, CheckCharging, CompareInt, Speak, CallEmptySrv, WaitForXSeconds) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute the custom action DebugAction to log the message “check if failed”. It will run the custom action CheckBattery to read k_bat_state and k_bat_perc. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule by verifying that k_bat_state equals 3. It will run the custom action CheckCharging to read charge_state. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule by verifying that charge_state equals 0. It will run the ROS action Speak to say “Ajudem-me, tenho fome e não consigo carregar” using the pt-PT-Wavenet-D voice. It will run the custom action CallEmptySrv to clear the costmaps on the /move_base/clear_costmaps service. It will run the custom action WaitForXSeconds for 10 seconds and it will return SUCCESS after the wait completes. It will execute the following actions (CheckBattery, CompareInt) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action CheckBattery to read k_bat_state and k_bat_perc. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule by verifying that k_bat_state does not equal 3. It will execute the following actions (CheckCharging, CompareInt, DebugAction) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action CheckCharging to read charge_state. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule by verifying that charge_state equals 0. It will execute the custom action DebugAction to log the message “I can move”.

mrq:
The mission describes a parallel personality announcement task that reads a personality value and speaks a matching sentence.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (GetInt16, Fallback) simultaneously. It will execute the ROS action GetInt16 to read the blackboard value from the topic robot_personality and store the result as personality. It will execute the following actions (Sequence, Sequence) consecutively. If any action (Sequence) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution will fail. It will execute the following actions (CompareInt, Speak) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule by verifying that personality equals 0. It will execute the ROS action Speak to utter “Sou um bom robô” in language pt_PT with voice pt-PT-Wavenet-D using action_name /gcloud_tts after the comparison succeeds. It will execute the following actions (CompareInt, Speak) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule by verifying that personality equals 1. It will execute the ROS action Speak to utter “Sou um mau robô” in language pt_PT with voice pt-PT-Wavenet-D using action_name /gcloud_tts after the comparison succeeds. It will execute the following actions (GetInt16, Fallback) simultaneously. If either a success threshold is reached or all actions succeed, the execution will be successful. Else if any action fails, the execution will fail.

<root main_tree_to_execute="MainTree">
  <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
  <PipelineSequence name="NavigationSequence">
    <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="6" name="NavigateRecovery">
      <PipelineSequence name="NavigateWithReplanning">
        <RateController hz="1.0">
          <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="1" name="ComputePathToPose">
            <ComputePathToPose goal="{goal}" path="{path}" planner_id="GridBased"/>
            <RoundRobin name="GlobalPlannerRecoveryActions">
              <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearGlobalCostmap-Context" service_name="global_costmap/clear_entirely_global_costmap"/>
              <Wait wait_duration="2.0"/>
            </RoundRobin>
          </RecoveryNode>
        </RateController>
        <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="1" name="FollowPath">
          <!-- Change below to corresponding planner from nav2_params.yaml-->
          <FollowPath path="{path}" controller_id="FollowPath"/> 
          <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearLocalCostmap-Context" service_name="local_costmap/clear_entirely_local_costmap"/>
        </RecoveryNode>
      </PipelineSequence>
      <ReactiveFallback name="RecoveryFallback">
        <GoalUpdated/>
        <SequenceStar name="RecoveryActions">
          <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearLocalCostmap-Subtree" service_name="local_costmap/clear_entirely_local_costmap"/>
          <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearGlobalCostmap-Subtree" service_name="global_costmap/clear_entirely_global_costmap"/>
          <Spin spin_dist="1.57"/>
          <Wait wait_duration="5"/>
        </SequenceStar>
      </ReactiveFallback>
    </RecoveryNode>
    <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearGlobalCostmap-Finish" service_name="global_costmap/clear_entirely_global_costmap"/>
  </PipelineSequence>
  </BehaviorTree>
</root>
mrq:
The mission describes a navigation task that will compute a global path, follow the path with online progression, and perform layered recoveries while advancing when actions are running or have succeeded.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (NavigateRecovery, ClearEntireCostmap-Finish) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. While an action is running or has succeeded, it will go to the next action. It will execute main NavigateWithReplanning. If it fails, it will execute recovery action RecoveryFallback. After recovery, it will retry main action NavigateWithReplanning up to six times. If recovery action RecoveryFallback fails and retries are exhausted, it will stop execution.

Next, it will execute the following actions (RateController, FollowPath) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. While an action is running or has succeeded, it will go to the next action. It will execute main ComputePathToPose. If it fails, it will execute recovery action GlobalPlannerRecoveryActions. After recovery, it will retry main action ComputePathToPose one time. If recovery action GlobalPlannerRecoveryActions fails and retries are exhausted, it will stop execution. It will execute main FollowPath. If it fails, it will execute recovery action ClearLocalCostmap-Context. After recovery, it will retry main action FollowPath one time. If recovery action ClearLocalCostmap-Context fails and retries are exhausted, it will stop execution.

Next, it will read blackboard value and compare it against a set of cases. If a case matches the blackboard value, it will execute corresponding GoalUpdated. Else if no case matches, it will execute default RecoveryActions. It will execute the following actions [GoalUpdated, RecoveryActions] in order. If GoalUpdated succeeds, it will stop execution. Else it will keep executing and re-evaluating previous actions whenever an action is running.

Next, it will execute the following actions (ClearLocalCostmap-Subtree, ClearGlobalCostmap-Subtree, Spin, Wait) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will run the ROS action ClearEntireCostmap named ClearLocalCostmap-Subtree on the local costmap. It will run the ROS action ClearEntireCostmap named ClearGlobalCostmap-Subtree on the global costmap. It will run the custom action Spin with a rotation distance of 1.57. It will run the custom action Wait with a duration of 5 seconds.

Finally, it will run the ROS action ComputePathToPose with the goal provided and will store the resulting path. It will run the ROS action FollowPath using the stored path with the specified controller after the path computation starts or succeeds. It will run the ROS action ClearEntireCostmap named ClearGlobalCostmap-Finish on the global costmap.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (CheckBattery, OpenGripper, ApproachObject, CloseGripper) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action CheckBattery and will proceed only if the battery check reports success. It will run the custom action OpenGripper to ensure the gripper is open before any motion toward the object. It will run the custom action CloseGripper to grasp the object after the approach action reports success.

mrq:
The mission describes a household assistance task that attempts to find a room or follow a human, then locate and fetch a bottle and a glass, pour a drink, and ask for help if the task cannot proceed.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (find_and_fetch, Ask_for_help) consecutively. If any action (find_and_fetch) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails.

Next, it will execute the following actions (pippo, Find_bottle, Fetch_bottle, Find_glass, Fetch_glass, Pour_drink) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the subtree pippo before attempting object search and manipulation.

Next, it will execute the following actions (pluto, Follow_human) consecutively. If any action (pluto) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails.

Next, it will execute the following actions (Room_known, Go_to_room) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule. It will run the custom action Go_to_room only after the room is known.

Next, it will run the custom action Follow_human.

Next, it will run the custom action Find_bottle.

Next, it will run the custom action Fetch_bottle.

Next, it will run the custom action Find_glass.

Next, it will run the custom action Fetch_glass.

Finally, it will run the custom action Pour_drink.

Finally, it will run the custom action Ask_for_help.

mrq:
The mission describes a supervisory assistance task that continuously checks stop and diagnostic conditions, ensures power and safety, and then executes a user-selected behavior.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (CheckStop, CheckDiagnosticStatus, Fallback, Fallback, Sequence) in sequence. If any action fails, it will stop execution. While any later action is still running, it will re-evaluate the previous actions.

Next, it will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for the condition CheckStop. It will stop the overall sequence if this condition check fails.

Next, it will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for the condition CheckDiagnosticStatus. It will stop the overall sequence if this condition check fails.

Next, it will execute the following actions (CheckBattery, Recharge) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails.

Next, it will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for the condition CheckBattery. It will proceed to the next action only if this battery check succeeds.

Next, it will execute the custom action Recharge. It will proceed only if this recharge action succeeds after the battery check fails.

Next, it will execute the following actions (CheckPatientCondition, Emergency) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails.

Next, it will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for the condition CheckPatientCondition. It will proceed to the next action only if this patient condition check succeeds.

Next, it will execute the custom action Emergency. It will proceed only if this emergency action succeeds after the patient condition check fails.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (StringParameterRequest, BehaviorSwitch) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. While an action is running or has succeeded, it will go to the next action.

Finally, it will run the custom action StringParameterRequest to present selectable options and will store the selected option in the blackboard variable selected_behavior. It will proceed only after the selection succeeds.

Finally, it will read blackboard value and compare it against a set of cases. If a case matches the blackboard value, it will execute the corresponding custom action among DevicePairingWithRequest, BaseMovementWithRequest, ArmMovementWithRequest, BatteryChargingWithRequest, FreeWalking, GuidedWalking, or TransportWithDevice. Else if no case matches, it will execute default custom action AlwaysFailureNode.

The mission describes a strictly ordered task that runs a nested set of actions from go1 through go8 inside a main Sequence.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (go1, Sequence2, go7, go8) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action go1 before any other action. It will then enter the nested subtree Sequence2 only after go1 succeeds. It will run the custom action go7 only after Sequence2 succeeds. It will run the custom action go8 only after go7 succeeds.

Next, it will execute the following actions (go2, Sequence3, go6) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action go2 before any other action in this subtree. It will then enter the nested subtree Sequence3 only after go2 succeeds. It will run the custom action go6 only after Sequence3 succeeds.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (go3, go4, go5) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action go3 before go4. It will run the custom action go4 only after go3 succeeds. It will run the custom action go5 only after go4 succeeds.

mrq: The mission describes a login and session setup task that authenticates, selects a channel and world, and enters the game using an existing or newly created character in strict order. First, the robot will execute the following actions (RetryUntilSuccessful, SelectChannelAndWorld, FallBack) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will keep trying to execute the custom action Login until it succeeds and will stop retrying after success. Next, it will run the custom action SelectChannelAndWorld with the specified channel and world after the login succeeds. Finally, it will execute the following actions (SelectAndLogCharacter, CreateAndLogCharacter) consecutively. If any action (SelectAndLogCharacter) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will run the custom action SelectAndLogCharacter using the specified name to enter the game if the character exists. It will run the custom action CreateAndLogCharacter using the specified name to create and enter the game if the selection fails.

mrq: The mission describes a navigation task that computes a path to a goal and then follows that path while remembering prior successes. First, the robot will execute the following actions (ComputePathToPose, FollowPath) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will run the ROS action ComputePathToPose with the provided goal and will store the resulting path on the blackboard. It will run the ROS action FollowPath using the stored path after the path computation succeeds.

<root main_tree_to_execute="MainTree">
  <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
    <PipelineSequence name="NavigateWithReplanning">
      <RateController hz="1.0">
        <Sequence>
          <GoalUpdater input_goal="{goal}" output_goal="{updated_goal}">
            <ComputePathToPose goal="{updated_goal}" path="{path}" planner_id="GridBased"/>
          </GoalUpdater>
         <TruncatePath distance="1.0" input_path="{path}" output_path="{truncated_path}"/>
        </Sequence>
      </RateController>
      <KeepRunningUntilFailure>
        <FollowPath path="{truncated_path}" controller_id="FollowPath"/>
      </KeepRunningUntilFailure>
    </PipelineSequence>
  </BehaviorTree>
</root>
mrq: The mission describes a dynamic navigation task that updates a moving goal, recomputes a path at a fixed rate, truncates the path to stop at a specified distance, and continuously follows the truncated path until following fails. First, the robot will execute the following actions (RateController, KeepRunningUntilFailure) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. While an action is running or has succeeded, it will go to the next action. Next, it will execute the custom action RateController at 1.0 Hz to pace the following sequence. It will execute the following actions (Sequence) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action GoalUpdater to update the input goal into an updated goal while nesting the ROS action ComputePathToPose to compute a path to the updated goal using the GridBased planner. It will run the custom action TruncatePath to generate a truncated path that ends 1.0 meters before the goal using the computed path as input after the path computation succeeds. Finally, it will execute FollowPath and make sure it always restarts it when it succeeds, and it will stop the loop only if FollowPath fails. It will run the ROS action FollowPath using the truncated path with the FollowPath controller.

mrq: The mission describes a navigation task that continuously computes a global path at 1 Hz and advances to path following while path computation is running or has succeeded. First, the robot will execute the following actions (RateController, FollowPath) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute delayed ComputePathToPose through the ROS action wrapper RateController at 1.0 Hz and will resume the planner after each delay interval. It will run the ROS action ComputePathToPose with the provided goal and will store the resulting path in memory when the computation succeeds or continues running. It will run the ROS action FollowPath using the most recently stored path with the specified controller while the planner is running or has succeeded.

mrq: The mission describes an approach task that computes a path toward a goal, truncates the path to stop at a safe stand-off distance, and advances along the truncated path while continually progressing through planning and control. First, the robot will execute the following actions (RateController, FollowPath) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. While an action is running or has succeeded, it will go to the next action. It will run the custom action RateController at 1.0 Hz to pace an internal planning sequence. It will execute the following actions (ComputePathToPose, TruncatePath) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails within the RateController. It will run the ROS action ComputePathToPose with the provided goal and will store the resulting path. It will run the custom action TruncatePath to shorten the stored path to stop 0.45 meters before the goal and will store the truncated path. Next, it will run the ROS action FollowPath using the stored truncated path with the specified controller after the first planning tick starts running or succeeds.

<?xml version="1.0"?>
<root main_tree_to_execute="BehaviorTree">
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="BehaviorTree">
        <WhileDoElse>
            <AlwaysSuccess name="WhileConditionIsTrue"/>
            <Sequence>
                <Action ID="ClearEntireCostmap" name="AskForNewTaskAtKiosk" service_name="/kiosk"/>
                <Switch6 case_1="Manual " case_2="Normal-Cam" case_3="FLIR-Cam" case_4="Reverse-Mode " case_5="Movement-Detect" case_6="QR Code" variable="{task-id}">
                    <Sequence name="ManualDrive">
                        <AlwaysSuccess/>
                    </Sequence>
                    <Sequence name="Normal-Cam">
                        <Action ID="OpenMVInterface" cam_mode="Setup" cam_port="/dev/ttyACM0"/>
                        <Action ID="PrintToLEDNametag" msg="N-Cam Setup" serial_port="/dev/ttyACM0"/>
                    </Sequence>
                    <Sequence name="FLIR-Cam">
                        <Action ID="OpenMVInterface" cam_mode="Setup" cam_port="/dev/ttyACM1"/>
                        <Action ID="PrintToLEDNametag" msg="Hello world!" serial_port="/dev/ttyACM0"/>
                    </Sequence>
                    <Sequence name="Reverse-Mode">
                        <Action ID="OpenMVInterface" cam_mode="Setup" cam_port="/dev/ttyACM3"/>
                        <Action ID="PrintToLEDNametag" msg="Reverse!" serial_port="/dev/ttyACM0"/>
                    </Sequence>
                    <Sequence name="Movement-Detect">
                        <Action ID="OpenMVInterface" cam_mode="Mov_Detect" cam_port="/dev/ttyACM0"/>
                        <Action ID="PrintToLEDNametag" msg="Move-Detect" serial_port="/dev/ttyACM0"/>
                    </Sequence>
                    <Sequence name="QR-Code">
                        <AlwaysSuccess/>
                        <Action ID="PrintToLEDNametag" msg="QR Codes" serial_port="/dev/ttyACM0"/>
                    </Sequence>
                    <Delay delay_msec="1000" name="DefaultSwitchCase">
                        <AlwaysFailure name="JustFailForNextLoop"/>
                    </Delay>
                </Switch6>
            </Sequence>
        </WhileDoElse>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <TreeNodesModel>
        <Action ID="BackUp">
            <input_port name="backup_dist">Distance to backup</input_port>
            <input_port name="backup_speed">Speed at which to backup</input_port>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="ClearEntireCostmap">
            <input_port name="service_name">Service name</input_port>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="ComputePathThroughPoses">
            <input_port name="goals">Destinations to plan through</input_port>
            <output_port name="path">Path created by ComputePathToPose node</output_port>
            <input_port name="planner_id"/>
            <input_port name="start">Start pose of the path if overriding current robot pose</input_port>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="ComputePathToPose">
            <input_port name="goal">Destination to plan to</input_port>
            <output_port name="path">Path created by ComputePathToPose node</output_port>
            <input_port name="planner_id"/>
            <input_port name="start">Start pose of the path if overriding current robot pose</input_port>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="ControllerSelector">
            <input_port name="default_controller">Default controller of the controller selector</input_port>
            <output_port name="selected_controller">Name of the selected controller received from the topic subcription</output_port>
            <input_port name="topic_name">Name of the topic to receive controller selection commands</input_port>
        </Action>
        <Decorator ID="DistanceController">
            <input_port name="distance">Distance</input_port>
        </Decorator>
        <Condition ID="DistanceTraveled">
            <input_port name="distance">Distance to check if passed</input_port>
            <input_port name="global_frame">reference frame to check in</input_port>
            <input_port name="robot_base_frame">Robot frame to check relative to global_frame</input_port>
        </Condition>
        <Action ID="FollowPath">
            <input_port default="FollowPath" name="controller_id"/>
            <input_port default="GoalChecker" name="goal_checker_id">Goal checker</input_port>
            <input_port name="path">Path to follow</input_port>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="GoalCheckerSelector">
            <input_port name="default_goal_checker">Default goal checker of the controller selector</input_port>
            <output_port name="selected_goal_checker">Name of the selected goal checker received from the topic subcription</output_port>
            <input_port name="topic_name">Name of the topic to receive goal checker selection commands</input_port>
        </Action>
        <Condition ID="GoalReached">
            <input_port name="goal">Destination</input_port>
        </Condition>
        <Condition ID="GoalUpdated"/>
        <Decorator ID="GoalUpdater">
            <input_port name="input_goal">Original goal in</input_port>
            <output_port name="output_goal">Output goal set by subscription</output_port>
        </Decorator>
        <Condition ID="InitialPoseReceived"/>
        <Condition ID="IsBatteryLow">
            <input_port name="battery_topic">Topic for battery info</input_port>
            <input_port name="is_voltage">Bool if check based on voltage or total %</input_port>
            <input_port name="min_battery">Min battery % or voltage before triggering</input_port>
        </Condition>
        <Condition ID="IsStuck"/>
        <Action ID="NavigateThroughPoses">
            <input_port name="goals">Goals</input_port>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="NavigateToPose">
            <input_port name="goal">Goal</input_port>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="OpenMVInterface">
            <input_port default="Setup" name="cam_mode">Function to call from interface</input_port>
            <input_port default="/dev/ttyACM0" name="cam_port">Serial port for camera</input_port>
        </Action>
        <Control ID="PipelineSequence"/>
        <Action ID="PlannerSelector">
            <input_port name="default_planner">Default planner of the planner selector</input_port>
            <output_port name="selected_planner">Name of the selected planner received from the topic subcription</output_port>
            <input_port name="topic_name">Name of the topic to receive planner selection commands</input_port>

mrq:
The mission describes a kiosk-driven task loop that solicits a task selection, dispatches a mode-specific setup, and repeats while a loop condition remains true.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (WhileDoElse) while checking a condition for each action. It will perform the following template: While [Condition], do [Action]. If [!Condition], execute alternative [Action]. It will treat the custom action WhileConditionIsTrue as the condition signal that remains true. It will therefore execute the following actions (Sequence) as long as the condition remains true.

Next, it will execute the following actions (AskForNewTaskAtKiosk, Switch6) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action AskForNewTaskAtKiosk using the service /kiosk to request a new task and to set the blackboard value task-id. It will then read the blackboard value and compare it against a set of cases. If a case matches the blackboard value, it will execute the corresponding action. Else if no case matches, it will execute the default action.

Next, it will handle the case Manual by executing the following actions (ManualDrive) consecutively. If any action (ManualDrive) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will execute the following actions () in sequence within ManualDrive. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning.

Next, it will handle the case Normal-Cam by executing the following actions (OpenMVInterface, PrintToLEDNametag) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action OpenMVInterface in Setup mode on port /dev/ttyACM0. It will run the custom action PrintToLEDNametag with the message N-Cam Setup on port /dev/ttyACM0 after the camera setup succeeds.

Next, it will handle the case FLIR-Cam by executing the following actions (OpenMVInterface, PrintToLEDNametag) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action OpenMVInterface in Setup mode on port /dev/ttyACM1. It will run the custom action PrintToLEDNametag with the message Hello world! on port /dev/ttyACM0 after the camera setup succeeds.

Next, it will handle the case Reverse-Mode by executing the following actions (OpenMVInterface, PrintToLEDNametag) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action OpenMVInterface in Setup mode on port /dev/ttyACM3. It will run the custom action PrintToLEDNametag with the message Reverse! on port /dev/ttyACM0 after the camera setup succeeds.

Next, it will handle the case Movement-Detect by executing the following actions (OpenMVInterface, PrintToLEDNametag) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action OpenMVInterface in Mov_Detect mode on port /dev/ttyACM0. It will run the custom action PrintToLEDNametag with the message Move-Detect on port /dev/ttyACM0 after the camera setup succeeds.

Next, it will handle the case QR Code by executing the following actions () in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will then run the custom action PrintToLEDNametag with the message QR Codes on port /dev/ttyACM0 after the preceding step succeeds.

Finally, if no case matches the blackboard value task-id, it will execute delayed action. If execution succeeds, it will start a delay timer. After the delay period is exhausted, it will resume execution. It will execute the custom action JustFailForNextLoop and make sure it always fails after a 1000 millisecond delay to force the loop to iterate again.

<?xml version="1.0"?>
<root main_tree_to_execute="BehaviorTree">

    <BehaviorTree ID="BehaviorTree">
        <Fallback name="Pick_hold_place">
            <Sequence name="Back home">
                <Action ID="BTIsObjContainer" tagisobjconin="{tagisobjcon}"/>
                <SetBlackboard output_key="target" value="name;home"/>
                <SetBlackboard output_key="gripperout" value="open"/>
                <SubTree GripperOut="gripperout" ID="Execution" Target="target"/>
                <SetBlackboard output_key="tagishold" value="0"/>
                <SetBlackboard output_key="tagisobjcon" value="0"/>
                <SetBlackboard output_key="tagisobjpose" value="0"/>
            </Sequence>
            <Sequence name="Place">
                <Action ID="BTIsHoldObj" tagisholdin="{tagishold}"/>
                <Parallel threshold="3">
                    <SetBlackboard output_key="containerpose" value="pose;-0.3;0.4;0.010;0;1;0;0.0002"/>
                    <SubTree GripperOut="gripperout" ID="Execution" Target="target"/>
                    <SubTree ContainerPose="containerpose" GripperOut="gripperout" ID="Place_subtree" TagIsObjCon="tagisobjcon" Target="target"/>
                </Parallel>
            </Sequence>
            <Sequence name="Pick">

                <Parallel threshold="2">
                    <SubTree GripperOut="gripperout" ID="Execution" Target="target"/>
                    <SubTree GripperOut="gripperout" ID="Pick_subtree" ObsObjPose="observedpose" TagIsHold="tagishold" Target="target"/>
                </Parallel>
            </Sequence>
            <Sequence>
                <Action ID="BTCameraFindTarget" tagisobjposeout="{tagisobjpose}" targetout="{observedpose}"/>
                <SetBlackboard output_key="tagishold" value="0"/>

        </Fallback>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="CameraObj">

            <SetBlackboard output_key="TagIsObjCon" value="0"/>

    <!-- ////////// -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="Execution">

            <Sequence>
                <Action ID="BTCheckGripperCommand" commandin="{GripperOut}" commandout="{grippercom}"/>

    </BehaviorTree>
    <!-- ////////// x 0.3 y 0.4 z 0.012 x' 0.05-->
    <BehaviorTree ID="Pick_subtree">
        <Fallback name="pick_fallback">

                <Sequence name="went up?">
                    <SetBlackboard output_key="height" value="0.03"/>
                    <Action ID="BTCheckCondition" heightin="{height}" targetin="{ObsObjPose}"/>
                </Sequence>
                <SetBlackboard output_key="TagIsHold" value="1"/>
            </Sequence>
            <Sequence name="go up">
                <Sequence name="Went down to pick?">
                    <SetBlackboard name="SetBlackBoard" output_key="height" value="0"/>
                    <Action ID="BTCheckCondition" heightin="{height}" targetin="{ObsObjPose}"/>
                </Sequence>
                <SetBlackboard output_key="height" value="0.03"/>
                <Action ID="BTCloseToTarget" height="{height}" targetin="{ObsObjPose}" targetout="{Target}" targettype="waypoint"/>
                <SetBlackboard output_key="gripperin" value="no"/>
                <Action ID="BTAdvertiseGripperCommand" commandin="{gripperin}" commandout="{GripperOut}" name="keep close"/>
            </Sequence>
            <Sequence name="go_down_close">
                <Sequence name="Went above obj?">
                    <SetBlackboard output_key="height" value="0.05"/>
                    <Action ID="BTCheckCondition" heightin="{height}" targetin="{ObsObjPose}"/>
                </Sequence>
                <SetBlackboard output_key="height" value="0"/>
                <Action ID="BTCloseToTarget" height="{height}" targetin="{ObsObjPose}" targetout="{Target}" targettype="waypoint"/>
                <SetBlackboard output_key="gripperin" value="close"/>
                <Action ID="BTAdvertiseGripperCommand" commandin="{gripperin}" commandout="{GripperOut}" name="close gripper"/>
            </Sequence>
            <Sequence name="go_above">
                <SetBlackboard output_key="height" value="0.05"/>
                <Action ID="BTCloseToTarget" height="{height}" targetin="{ObsObjPose}" targetout="{Target}" targettype="waypoint"/>
                <SetBlackboard output_key="gripperin" value="no"/>
                <Action ID="BTAdvertiseGripperCommand" commandin="{gripperin}" commandout="{GripperOut}" name="open gripper"/>
            </Sequence>
        </Fallback>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="Place_subtree">

            </Sequence>
        </Fallback>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <TreeNodesModel>

        <Action ID="BTAdvertiseGripperCommand">

        </Action>

        <SubTree ID="CameraObj">

            <output_port default="tagisobjcon" name="TagIsObjCon"/>
        </SubTree>

</root>
mrq:
The mission describes a pick-hold-place task that alternates between returning home, placing an object, picking an object, or detecting a target based on success, with supporting navigation and manipulation subtrees.

First, it will execute the following actions (BTIsObjContainer, SetBlackboard[target=name;home], SetBlackboard[gripperout=open], Execution, SetBlackboard[tagishold=0], SetBlackboard[tagisobjcon=0], SetBlackboard[tagisobjpose=0]) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action BTIsObjContainer using the current container flag. It will write the home waypoint into the blackboard key target. It will write the open command into the blackboard key gripperout. The robot will execute the following actions (wait for target, plan path, follow path, check gripper command, move gripper) in sequence and remember which ones have already succeeded. It will reset the hold flag to zero. It will reset the container flag to zero. It will reset the object pose flag to zero.

Next, it will execute the following actions (BTIsHoldObj, Parallel[SetBlackboard[containerpose=pose;-0.3;0.4;0.010;0;1;0;0.0002], Execution, Place_subtree]) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will run the custom action BTIsHoldObj using the current hold flag. It will execute the following actions [SetBlackboard container pose, run Execution, run Place_subtree] simultaneously. If either a success threshold is reached or all actions succeed, the execution was successful. Else if any action fails, the execution fails. It will write the container pose into the blackboard key containerpose. It will execute the following actions (wait for target, plan path, follow path, check gripper command, move gripper) in sequence and remember which ones have already succeeded. It will execute the place strategy that checks placement, moves to container poses, and commands the gripper while falling back to alternative steps if earlier steps fail.

Next, it will execute the following actions (BTIsObjPose, Parallel[Execution, Pick_subtree]) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will run the custom action BTIsObjPose to confirm an observed object pose and to output the same pose to the blackboard. It will execute the following actions [Execution, Pick_subtree] simultaneously. If either a success threshold is reached or all actions succeed, the execution was successful. Else if any action fails, the execution fails. It will execute the following actions (wait for target, plan path, follow path, check gripper command, move gripper) in sequence and remember which ones have already succeeded. It will execute the pick strategy that checks approach heights, moves to object poses, and commands the gripper while falling back to alternative steps if earlier steps fail.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (BTCameraFindTarget, SetBlackboard[tagishold=0], SetBlackboard[tagisobjcon=0]) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action BTCameraFindTarget to detect a target and to set the object pose flag and the observed pose. It will reset the hold flag to zero. It will reset the container flag to zero.

First, it will execute the following actions (SetBlackboard[TagIsHold=0], SetBlackboard[TagIsObjCon=0], BTCameraFindTarget) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will reset the subtree hold flag to zero. It will reset the subtree container flag to zero. It will run the custom action BTCameraFindTarget to detect a target and to set the subtree object pose flag and the observed pose.

Next, it will execute the following actions (BTWaitForTarget, BTPathPlanning, BTFollowPath) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. While an action is running or has succeeded, it will go to the next action. It will run the custom action BTWaitForTarget to read the current target and to output it. It will run the custom action BTPathPlanning to compute a plan for the current target. It will run the custom action BTFollowPath to follow the computed plan. It will execute the following actions (BTCheckGripperCommand, BTGripperMoveSchunk) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. While an action is running or has succeeded, it will go to the next action. It will run the custom action BTCheckGripperCommand to parse the pending gripper command. It will run the custom action BTGripperMoveSchunk to actuate the gripper.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (hold, go up, go_down_close, go_above) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, it will start all over again until an action succeeds. It will execute the following actions (SetBlackboard[height=0.03], BTCheckCondition, SetBlackboard[TagIsHold=1]) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute the following actions (SetBlackboard[height=0], BTCheckCondition, SetBlackboard[height=0.03], BTCloseToTarget, SetBlackboard[gripperin=no], BTAdvertiseGripperCommand) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute the following actions (SetBlackboard[height=0.05], BTCheckCondition, SetBlackboard[height=0], BTCloseToTarget, SetBlackboard[gripperin=close], BTAdvertiseGripperCommand) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute the following actions (SetBlackboard[height=0.05], BTCloseToTarget, SetBlackboard[gripperin=no], BTAdvertiseGripperCommand) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails.

First, it will execute the following actions (Check back, Go up, Go down into container, Go above container) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, it will start all over again until an action succeeds. It will execute the following actions (SetBlackboard[height=0.03], BTCheckCondition, SetBlackboard[TagIsObjCon=1]) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute the following actions (SetBlackboard[height=-0.00], BTCheckCondition, SetBlackboard[height=0.03], BTCloseToTarget, SetBlackboard[gripperin=no], BTAdvertiseGripperCommand) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute the following actions (SetBlackboard[height=0.05], BTCheckCondition, SetBlackboard[height=-0.0], BTCloseToTarget, SetBlackboard[gripperin=open], BTAdvertiseGripperCommand) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute the following actions (SetBlackboard[height=0.05], BTCloseToTarget, SetBlackboard[gripperin=no], BTAdvertiseGripperCommand) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails.

The mission describes a target acquisition and engagement task that first tries a structured approach and then falls back to exploratory movement.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (SequenceWithMemory Main, RandomMove) consecutively. If any action (SequenceWithMemory Main) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will run the custom action SequenceWithMemory Main as the primary strategy. It will run the custom action RandomMove as the fallback strategy if the primary strategy fails.

Next, it will execute the following actions (GetTarget, Move, Sequence) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will run the custom action GetTarget to select a target. It will run the custom action Move to navigate toward the selected target after the target is obtained. It will execute the following actions (TargetAdjacent, AttackTarget) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. While an action is running or has succeeded, it will go to the next action. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule by evaluating the condition TargetAdjacent. It will run the custom action AttackTarget if the adjacency condition succeeds. It will restart the SequenceWithMemory if any action in this subsequence fails.

mrq:
The mission describes a navigation task that computes a path to a goal at a fixed rate and then follows the path in strict order.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (RateController, FollowPath) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action RateController and will advance when the inner action is running or has succeeded. It will run the ROS action FollowPath using the path from the blackboard after the rate-controlled planning succeeds.

Next, it will execute the following actions (GoalReached, ComputePathToPose) consecutively. If any action (GoalReached) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will run the ROS action GoalReached and will succeed when the goal condition is verified.               <ComputePathToPose goal="{updated_goal}" path="{path}" planner_id="GridBased"/>
            </GoalUpdater>
          </Sequence>
        </RateController>
        <KeepRunningUntilFailure>
          <FollowPath path="{path}" controller_id="FollowPath"/>

      </PipelineSequence>
      <ReactiveFallback name="RecoveryFallback">
        <GoalUpdated/>
        <RoundRobin name="RecoveryActions">
          <Sequence name="ClearingActions">

            <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearGlobalCostmap-Subtree" service_name="global_costmap/clear_entirely_global_costmap"/>
            <ComputePathToPose goal="{updated_goal}" path="{path}" planner_id="GridBased"/>
          </Sequence>
          <Wait wait_duration="0.2"/>
        </RoundRobin>
      </ReactiveFallback>
    </RecoveryNode>
  </BehaviorTree>
</root>

mrq:
The mission describes a recovery-enabled navigation task that will continuously replan toward a dynamically updated goal and will follow the latest path while attempting structured recovery when progress fails.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (NavigateWithReplanning) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute the following actions (RateController, KeepRunningUntilFailure) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute the following actions (Sequence) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails while the RateController limits execution to 1.0 Hz. It will run the custom action GoalUpdater to transform the input goal into an updated goal. It will run the ROS action ComputePathToPose using the updated goal to produce a path. It will execute the ROS action FollowPath, and if the action succeeds it will restart it, else if the action fails it will stop looping and continue execution. It will advance to FollowPath while path computation is running or has succeeded.

Next, it will execute main RecoveryNode action. If it fails, it will execute recovery action (RecoveryFallback). After recovery, it will retry the main action (NavigateWithReplanning). If recovery action (RecoveryFallback) fails and retries are exhausted, it will stop execution.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (RecoveryFallback) in order. If any action succeeds, it will stop execution. Else it will keep executing and re-evaluating previous actions whenever an action is running. It will check if a value on the blackboard matches a given rule using the custom action GoalUpdated. It will execute the following actions (RecoveryActions) consecutively. If any action (RecoveryActions) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, it will start all over again until an action succeeds. It will execute the following actions (ClearingActions) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action ClearEntireCostmap for the local costmap. It will run the custom action ClearEntireCostmap for the global costmap. It will run the ROS action ComputePathToPose using the updated goal to produce a path. It will run the custom action Wait for 0.2 seconds as the next recovery attempt.

The mission describes a navigation task that computes a path to a goal, follows the path with continuous replanning, and performs structured recovery when navigation encounters failures.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (RateController, RecoveryNode, RecoveryNode) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. While an action is running or has succeeded, it will go to the next action. It will run the custom action RateController at 1.0 Hz to pace path computation. It will execute main action the ROS action ComputePathToPose and, if it fails, it will execute recovery action the custom action ClearEntireCostmap-Context and then retry the main action until the single retry is exhausted. It will execute main action the ROS action FollowPath and, if it fails, it will execute recovery action the custom action ClearLocalCostmap-Context and then retry the main action until the single retry is exhausted.

Next, it will execute main action the custom action NavigateWithReplanning. If it fails, it will execute recovery action the custom action RecoveryFallback. After recovery, it will retry main action the custom action NavigateWithReplanning. If recovery action the custom action RecoveryFallback fails and retries are exhausted after six attempts, it will stop execution.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (GoalUpdated, RecoveryActions) in order. If an action succeeds, it will stop execution. Else it will keep executing and re-evaluating previous actions whenever an action is running. It will read the goal state with the custom action GoalUpdated and succeed immediately if a new goal is detected. It will execute the following actions (ClearLocalCostmap-Subtree, ClearGlobalCostmap-Subtree, Spin, Wait) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will run the custom action ClearLocalCostmap-Subtree to clear the local costmap. It will run the custom action ClearGlobalCostmap-Subtree to clear the global costmap. It will run the custom action Spin with a spin distance of 1.57 to rotate in place. It will run the custom action Wait for a duration of 5 seconds.

It will execute Repeat for a set number of times or infinitely with three total iterations as the final step of this top-level sequence.

Next, it will execute the following actions (TextToSpeechActionClient, ReactiveSequence) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails during each of the three Repeat iterations. It will run the custom action TextToSpeechActionClient with the message set to “Starting spin cycle” at the start of each iteration. It will then execute the following actions (Inverter, RobotSpin) in sequence and, if any action fails, stop execution while re-evaluating previous actions while an action is still running.

Finally, it will execute inverted ObjectDetectionAction and ensure that a detection of a person causes the inverted status to fail. It will run the custom action ObjectDetectionAction to detect the class person and produce a pose into detected_Pose. It will execute the custom action RobotSpin with an angle of 360 degrees and a velocity of 0.5 while the previous inverted condition remains successful, and it will re-check the inverted condition while the spin is running.

							<Sequence>

								<TextToSpeechActionClient msg="Not Detected" />

						</Sequence>
					</ForceSuccess>
				</Fallback>
			</Repeat>
		</Sequence>
	</BehaviorTree>
</root>

mrq:
The mission describes a linear interaction where the robot sets motion parameters, moves forward, speaks a generated message in European Portuguese, and then waits, all in strict order.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (SetBlackboard, SetBlackboard, GeneralActionlib, Speak, WaitForXSeconds) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will set the blackboard key velocity to the value 0.5. It will set the blackboard key distance to the value 1.0. It will run the custom action GeneralActionlib with the action_name move_forward, the constants list set to 5, and the variables list set to velocity and distance, and it will store the resulting string in the blackboard key result. It will run the custom action Speak with the action_name gcloud_tts using the language code pt_PT and the voice pt-PT-Wavenet-D to utter the text stored in the blackboard key result. It will run the custom action WaitForXSeconds configured to return SUCCESS after 5 seconds.

<root main_tree_to_execute="MainTree">
  <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
    <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="6" name="NavigateRecovery">
      <PipelineSequence name="NavigateWithReplanning">
        <RateController hz="1.0">
          <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="1" name="ComputePathToPose">
            <ComputePathToPose goal="{goal}" path="{path}" planner_id="GridBased"/>
            <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearGlobalCostmap-Context" service_name="global_costmap/clear_entirely_global_costmap"/>
          </RecoveryNode>
        </RateController>
        <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="1" name="FollowPath">
          <FollowPath path="{path}" controller_id="FollowPath"/>
          <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearLocalCostmap-Context" service_name="local_costmap/clear_entirely_local_costmap"/>
        </RecoveryNode>
      </PipelineSequence>
      <ReactiveFallback name="RecoveryFallback">
        <GoalUpdated/>
        <SequenceStar name="RecoveryActions">
          <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearLocalCostmap-Subtree" service_name="local_costmap/clear_entirely_local_costmap"/>
          <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearGlobalCostmap-Subtree" service_name="global_costmap/clear_entirely_global_costmap"/>
          <Spin spin_dist="1.57"/>
          <Wait wait_duration="5"/>
        </SequenceStar>
      </ReactiveFallback>
    </RecoveryNode>
  </BehaviorTree>
</root>
mrq:
The mission describes a navigation task that periodically replans a global path at 1 Hz, follows the path with recovery behaviors, and retries navigation up to a fixed limit.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (NavigateWithReplanning, RecoveryFallback) as a main action with recovery and will retry the main action up to six times. It will execute the main action and, if it fails, it will execute a recovery action and then retry the main action. It will stop execution if the recovery action fails and the allowed retries are exhausted.

Next, it will execute the following actions (Rate-controlled ComputePathToPose with recovery, FollowPath with recovery) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. While an action is running or has succeeded, it will go to the next action. It will thereby allow the path following to advance while the planner is running at the specified rate.

Next, it will execute the rate-controlled planner subtree at 1 Hz. It will therefore trigger the enclosed planner step once per second without exceeding the specified frequency.

Next, it will execute a main action with a single recovery step for computing the global path. It will execute the main action and, if it fails, it will execute the recovery action and then retry the main action once. It will stop execution if the recovery action fails and the single retry is exhausted.

Next, it will run the ROS action ComputePathToPose with the goal provided and will store the resulting path. It will request a global plan from the GridBased planner and will write the plan into the blackboard variable named path.

Next, it will execute the custom action ClearEntireCostmap-Context and make sure it is used as the recovery step for the global planner. It will clear the global costmap via the service named global_costmap/clear_entirely_global_costmap before retrying the planner.

Next, it will execute a main action with a single recovery step for following the path. It will execute the main action and, if it fails, it will execute the recovery action and then retry the main action once. It will stop execution if the recovery action fails and the single retry is exhausted.

Next, it will run the ROS action FollowPath using the stored path with the specified controller after the path is available. It will command the controller named FollowPath to track the path stored in the blackboard variable named path.

Next, it will execute the custom action ClearEntireCostmap-Context and make sure it is used as the recovery step for the local controller. It will clear the local costmap via the service named local_costmap/clear_entirely_local_costmap before retrying the controller.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (GoalUpdated, RecoveryActions) in order. If any action succeeds, it will stop execution. Else it will keep executing and re-evaluating previous actions whenever an action is running. It will thus keep checking for a new goal and otherwise perform structured recovery.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (ClearLocalCostmap-Subtree, ClearGlobalCostmap-Subtree, Spin, Wait) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will clear the local costmap via the service named local_costmap/clear_entirely_local_costmap. It will clear the global costmap via the service named global_costmap/clear_entirely_global_costmap. It will run the ROS action Spin to rotate in place by a distance of 1.57 radians. It will run the ROS action Wait for a duration of 5 seconds.

  <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
    <PipelineSequence name="NavigateWithReplanning">
      <SpeedController min_rate="0.1" max_rate="1.0" min_speed="0.0" max_speed="0.26" filter_duration="0.3">
        <ComputePathToPose goal="{goal}" path="{path}" planner_id="GridBased"/>
      </SpeedController>
      <FollowPath path="{path}"  controller_id="FollowPath"/>
    </PipelineSequence>

mrq: The mission describes a navigation task that computes a global path at a controlled rate and follows that path while advancing as soon as planning begins or succeeds. First, the robot will execute the following actions (SpeedController, FollowPath) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute the following actions (ComputePathToPose) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails while running within a rate limiter that bounds planning updates between 0.1 Hz and 1.0 Hz and caps speed between 0.0 m/s and 0.26 m/s with a 0.3 s filter duration. It will run the ROS action ComputePathToPose with the provided goal and will store the resulting path. It will go to the next action while the planning action is running or after it has succeeded. It will run the ROS action FollowPath using the stored path with the specified controller.

mrq: The mission describes a door traversal task that decides how to pass an obstacle and then secures the door. First, the robot will execute the following actions (door_open_sequence, DoorClosed, PassThroughWindow) in order as a Fallback and will move to the next action until one succeeds or will fail if none succeeds. It will execute the following actions (IsDoorOpen, PassThroughDoor) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for IsDoorOpen. It will run the custom action PassThroughDoor only after the condition IsDoorOpen succeeds. Next, it will execute the following actions (door_closed_sequence) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails as part of the DoorClosed subtree. It will execute inverted IsDoorOpen and will reverse a success to fail or a failure to succeed. It will keep trying to execute OpenDoor until it succeeds and will stop retrying once it succeeds. It will run the custom action PassThroughDoor after the door is confirmed open. Finally, it will run the custom action PassThroughWindow if both the door_open_sequence and the DoorClosed subtree fail. Next, it will run the custom action CloseDoor to secure the door after a successful passage.

The mission describes a repeated inspection task that sets three goals, navigates to each goal in order, and captures a snapshot at each goal for three full cycles.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (SetBlackboard, SetBlackboard, SetBlackboard, Sequence) for three cycles in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute SetBlackboard to store the pose for Goal_a. It will execute SetBlackboard to store the pose for Goal_b. It will execute SetBlackboard to store the pose for Goal_c. It will execute the following actions (SubTree: MoveRobot with target Goal_a, SnapshotClient, SubTree: MoveRobot with target Goal_b, SnapshotClient, SubTree: MoveRobot with target Goal_c, SnapshotClient) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action SnapshotClient after reaching Goal_a. It will run the custom action SnapshotClient after reaching Goal_b. It will run the custom action SnapshotClient after reaching Goal_c.

Next, it will execute the following actions (Nav2Client) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the ROS action Nav2Client using the provided target pose to navigate to that single goal.

mrq:
The mission describes a mobile manipulation task that will locate a room or follow a human, then find a bottle, fetch it, and otherwise request assistance.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (find_and_fetch, Ask_for_help) consecutively. If any action (find_and_fetch) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will run the custom action find_and_fetch as the primary behavior. It will run the custom action Ask_for_help only if the primary behavior fails.

Next, it will execute the following actions (pippo, Find_bottle, Fetch_bottle) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action pippo to secure room access or guidance. It will run the custom action Find_bottle only after pippo succeeds. It will run the custom action Fetch_bottle only after Find_bottle succeeds.

Next, it will execute the following actions (pluto, Follow_human) consecutively. If any action (pluto) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will run the custom action pluto to try a known-room approach first. It will run the custom action Follow_human if the known-room approach does not succeed.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (Room_known, Go_to_room) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for Room_known. It will run the custom action Go_to_room only if the Room_known check succeeds.

        <CheckLaser name="check_laser"/>
        <Sequence>
          <Backward name="backward" duration="3"/>

mrq:
The mission describes a collision-avoidance-and-resume task that first resolves close-obstacle situations and then proceeds forward in strict order. First, the robot will execute the following actions (Fallback, Forward) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the ROS action Forward only after the preceding Fallback completes successfully.

Next, it will execute the following actions (CheckLaser, Sequence) consecutively. If any action (CheckLaser) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution will fail. It will run the custom action CheckLaser and will consider the Fallback successful if the obstacle check passes. It will continue to the next action only if the obstacle check fails.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (Backward, Turn) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action Backward with a duration of 3 seconds to create clearance from the detected obstacle. It will run the custom action Turn with a duration of 4 seconds to reorient away from the obstacle after moving backward succeeds.
