<?xml version="1.0"?>
<root main_tree_to_execute="BehaviorTree">
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="AutoClean">
        <Fallback>
            <Condition ID="IsCleanProcessFinished"/>
            <Fallback>
                <Condition ID="IsThereEnoughWater"/>
                <Sequence>
                    <Timeout msec="120000">
                        <Condition ID="IsWaterTankRemoved"/>
                    </Timeout>
                    <Fallback>
                        <Timeout msec="120000">
                            <Condition ID="IsWaterTankFull"/>
                        </Timeout>
                        <Action ID="FillWaterTank"/>
                    </Fallback>
                    <Timeout msec="120000">
                        <Condition ID="IsWaterTankPlacedInCoffeeMachine"/>
                    </Timeout>
                    <Timeout msec="120000">
                        <Condition ID="IsCleanProcessFinished"/>
                    </Timeout>
                </Sequence>
            </Fallback>
            <Fallback>
                <Inverter>
                    <Condition ID="IsMarroTankFull"/>
                </Inverter>
                <Sequence>
                    <Timeout msec="120000">
                        <Condition ID="IsMarroTankRemoved"/>
                    </Timeout>
                    <Fallback>
                        <Timeout msec="120000">
                            <Condition ID="IsMarroTankEmpty"/>
                        </Timeout>
                        <Action ID="EmptyMarroTank"/>
                    </Fallback>
                    <RetryUntilSuccesful num_attempts="15">
                        <Condition ID="IsMarroTankPlacedInCoffeeMachine"/>
                    </RetryUntilSuccesful>
                    <Timeout msec="120000">
                        <Condition ID="IsCleanProcessFinished"/>
                    </Timeout>
                </Sequence>
            </Fallback>
        </Fallback>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="BehaviorTree">
        <Sequence>
            <SubTree ID="OpenCoffeeMachine" __shared_blackboard="false"/>
            <SubTree ID="AutoClean" __shared_blackboard="false"/>
            <SubTree ID="PutCoffeeCup" __shared_blackboard="false"/>
            <SubTree ID="CoffeeType" __shared_blackboard="false"/>
        </Sequence>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="CoffeeType">
        <Sequence>
            <Fallback>
                <Timeout msec="120000">
                    <Condition ID="IsDesiredCoffeeSelected"/>
                </Timeout>
                <Action ID="PressDesiredCoffee"/>
            </Fallback>
            <Timeout msec="120000">
                <Condition ID="IsCoffeeFinished"/>
            </Timeout>
            <Timeout msec="120000">
                <Condition ID="HasCupOfCoffeeBeenRemoved"/>
            </Timeout>
            <Timeout msec="120000">
                <Fallback>
                    <Condition ID="HasHumanAddedCleaningCup"/>
                    <Sequence>
                        <Action ID="PlaceCleanCup"/>
                        <Action ID="SwitchOffCoffeeMachine"/>
                    </Sequence>
                </Fallback>
            </Timeout>
            <Timeout msec="120000">
                <Fallback name="arallel">
                    <Timeout msec="600000">
                        <Condition ID="HasHumanAddedMilk"/>
                    </Timeout>
                    <Fallback>
                        <Inverter>
                            <Condition ID="IsMilkDesired"/>
                        </Inverter>
                        <Action ID="AddMilkToCoffee"/>
                    </Fallback>
                </Fallback>
            </Timeout>
            <Timeout msec="120000">
                <Fallback>
                    <Timeout msec="600000">
                        <Condition ID="HasHumanAddedSugar"/>
                    </Timeout>
                    <Fallback>
                        <Inverter>
                            <Condition ID="IsSugarDesired"/>
                        </Inverter>
                        <Action ID="AddSugarToCoffee"/>
                    </Fallback>
                </Fallback>
            </Timeout>
        </Sequence>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="OpenCoffeeMachine">
        <Fallback>
            <Condition ID="IsMachineOpen"/>
            <Sequence>
                <RetryUntilSuccesful num_attempts="10">
                    <Condition ID="IsCleanCupReady"/>
                </RetryUntilSuccesful>
                <Fallback>
                    <Timeout msec="60000">
                        <Condition ID="IsMachineOpen"/>
                    </Timeout>
                    <Action ID="SwitchOnCoffeeMachine"/>
                </Fallback>
            </Sequence>
        </Fallback>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="PutCoffeeCup">
        <Fallback>
            <Timeout msec="120000">
                <Condition ID="IsCoffeeCupReady"/>
            </Timeout>
            <Action ID="PlaceCoffeeCup"/>
        </Fallback>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <TreeNodesModel>
        <Action ID="AddMilkToCoffee"/>
        <Action ID="AddSugarToCoffee"/>
        <SubTree ID="AutoClean">
            <input_port default="false" name="__shared_blackboard">If false (default), the Subtree has an isolated blackboard and needs port remapping</input_port>
        </SubTree>
        <SubTree ID="CoffeeType">
            <input_port default="false" name="__shared_blackboard">If false (default), the Subtree has an isolated blackboard and needs port remapping</input_port>
        </SubTree>
        <Action ID="EmptyMarroTank"/>
        <Action ID="FillWaterTank"/>
        <Condition ID="HasCupOfCoffeeBeenRemoved"/>
        <Condition ID="HasHumanAddedCleaningCup"/>
        <Condition ID="HasHumanAddedMilk"/>
        <Condition ID="HasHumanAddedSugar"/>
        <Condition ID="IsCleanCupReady"/>
        <Condition ID="IsCleanProcessFinished"/>
        <Condition ID="IsCoffeeCupReady"/>
        <Condition ID="IsCoffeeFinished"/>
        <Condition ID="IsDesiredCoffeeSelected"/>
        <Condition ID="IsMachineOpen"/>
        <Condition ID="IsMarroTankEmpty"/>
        <Condition ID="IsMarroTankFull"/>
        <Condition ID="IsMarroTankPlacedInCoffeeMachine"/>
        <Condition ID="IsMarroTankRemoved"/>
        <Condition ID="IsMilkDesired"/>
        <Condition ID="IsSugarDesired"/>
        <Condition ID="IsThereEnoughWater"/>
        <Condition ID="IsWaterTankFull"/>
        <Condition ID="IsWaterTankPlacedInCoffeeMachine"/>
        <Condition ID="IsWaterTankRemoved"/>
        <SubTree ID="OpenCoffeeMachine">
            <input_port default="false" name="__shared_blackboard">If false (default), the Subtree has an isolated blackboard and needs port remapping</input_port>
        </SubTree>
        <Action ID="PlaceCleanCup"/>
        <Action ID="PlaceCoffeeCup"/>
        <Action ID="PressDesiredCoffee"/>
        <SubTree ID="PutCoffeeCup">
            <input_port default="false" name="__shared_blackboard">If false (default), the Subtree has an isolated blackboard and needs port remapping</input_port>
        </SubTree>
        <Action ID="SwitchOffCoffeeMachine"/>
        <Action ID="SwitchOnCoffeeMachine"/>
    </TreeNodesModel>
    <!-- ////////// -->
</root>

It will run the custom action OpenCoffeeMachine and will continue only if it succeeds. It will run the custom action AutoClean and will continue only if it succeeds. It will run the custom action PutCoffeeCup and will continue only if it succeeds. It will run the custom action CoffeeType and will complete the mission only if it succeeds.

Next, it will execute the following actions (IsMachineOpen, Sequence) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for IsMachineOpen. It will execute the following actions (RetryUntilSuccesful, Fallback) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. While running RetryUntilSuccesful, it will keep trying to execute IsCleanCupReady until it succeeds. It will execute the following actions (Timeout, SwitchOnCoffeeMachine) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. It will execute child node, but only for a maximum duration for IsMachineOpen with a 60000 ms limit and will interrupt the execution if it exceeds the time. It will run the custom action SwitchOnCoffeeMachine if the timeout-guarded check does not succeed.

Next, it will execute the following actions (IsCleanProcessFinished, Fallback, Fallback) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for IsThereEnoughWater. It will execute the following actions (Timeout, FillWaterTank) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. While executing Timeout around IsWaterTankFull with a 120000 ms limit, it will interrupt the check if it exceeds the time. It will run the custom action FillWaterTank if the tank-full check does not succeed. While executing Timeout around IsWaterTankPlacedInCoffeeMachine with a 120000 ms limit, it will interrupt the check if it exceeds the time. It will execute the following actions (Inverter, Sequence) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. It will execute inverted IsMarroTankFull and will reverse success to failure and reverse failure to success. While executing Timeout around IsMarroTankRemoved with a 120000 ms limit, it will interrupt the check if it exceeds the time. <root main_tree_to_execute="MainTree">
  <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
    <Sequence name="root">
      <RateController hz="1.0">
        <Fallback>
          <GoalReached/>
          <ComputePathToPose goal="${goal}"/>
        </Fallback>
      </RateController>
      <FollowPath path="${path}"/>
    </Sequence>
  </BehaviorTree>
</root>
mrq: The mission describes a navigation task that periodically computes a path to a goal and then follows that path in strict order. First, the robot will execute the following actions (RateController, FollowPath) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute delayed Execute the following actions (GoalReached, ComputePathToPose) consecutively. If any action (GoalReached) fails, move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. If execution succeeds, start delay timer. After delay period is exhausted, resume execution. It will run the ROS action GoalReached to check if the goal condition has been satisfied. It will run the ROS action ComputePathToPose with the goal provided if the goal has not been reached and will publish the resulting path to memory. Next, it will run the ROS action FollowPath using the stored path after the path computation or goal check completes.

            <Sequence name="Pick_Block">
                <SetBlackboard name="IK_Pick_Block_Hover_Position" output_key="pick_hove_position" value="0.7 0.158 0.021"/>
                <SetBlackboard name="IK_Pick_Block_Hover_Orientation" output_key="pick_hove_orientation" value="-0.02496 0.99965 0.00738 0.00486"/>
                <Action ID="MoveToPose" name="Move_To_Pick_Block_Hovre_Pose" position="{pick_hove_position}" orientation="{pick_hove_orientation}"/>
                <Action ID="SetGripper" name="Open_Gripper" command="open"/>
                <SetBlackboard name="IK_Pick_Block_Grip_Position" output_key="pick_gripper_position" value="0.7 0.158 -0.129"/>
                <SetBlackboard name="IK_Pick_Block_Grip_Orientation" output_key="pick_gripper_orientation" value="-0.02496 0.99965 0.00738 0.00486"/>

                <Action ID="SetGripper" name="Close_Gripper" position="70"/>
                <Action ID="MoveToPose" name="Move_To_Gripped_Block_Hover_Pose" position="0.7 0.158 -0.021" orientation="-0.02496 0.99965 0.00738 0.00486"/>
            </Sequence>
            <Sequence name="Place_Block">
                <SetBlackboard name="IK_Place_Block_Hover_Position" output_key="place_hover_position" value="0.75 -0.05 -0.021"/>
                <SetBlackboard name="IK_Place_Block_Hover_Orientation" output_key="place_hover_orientation" value="-0.02496 0.99965 0.00738 0.00486"/>

                <SetBlackboard name="IK_Place_Block_Release_Pose" output_key="place_release_position" value="0.75 -0.05 -0.129"/>
                <SetBlackboard name="IK_Place_Block_Release_Pose" output_key="place_release_orientation" value="-0.02496 0.99965 0.00738 0.00486"/>
                <Action ID="MoveToPose" name="Move_To_Place_Block_Grip_Pose" position="{place_release_position}" orientation="{place_release_orientation}"/>
                <Action ID="SetGripper" name="Open_Gripper" command="open"/>
                <Action ID="MoveToPose" name="Move_To_Relased_Block_Hove_Pose" position="0.75 -0.05 -0.021" orientation="-0.02496 0.99965 0.00738 0.00486"/>
            </Sequence>
        </Sequence>
    </BehaviorTree>

            <input_port default="0 0 0" name="position">IK position</input_port>/>
            <input_port default="0 0 0 1" name="orientation">IK orientation</input_port>/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="SetGripper">

mrq:
The mission describes a pick-and-place task that moves a manipulator to a start pose, picks a block from a known location, and places the block at a target location in strict order.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (MoveJoints, Pick_Block, Place_Block) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action MoveJoints using the provided joint commands to reach the start position before any picking or placing occurs.

Next, it will execute the following actions (IK_Pick_Block_Hover_Position, IK_Pick_Block_Hover_Orientation, Move_To_Pick_Block_Hovre_Pose, Open_Gripper, IK_Pick_Block_Grip_Position, IK_Pick_Block_Grip_Orientation, Move_To_Pick_Block_Grip_Pose, Close_Gripper, Move_To_Gripped_Block_Hover_Pose) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action SetBlackboard to store the pick hover position with the key pick_hove_position. It will run the custom action SetBlackboard to store the pick hover orientation with the key pick_hove_orientation. It will run the custom action MoveToPose using the stored pick hover position and orientation to reach a pre-grasp pose. It will run the custom action SetGripper with the open command to ensure the gripper is open. It will run the custom action SetBlackboard to store the pick grip position with the key pick_gripper_position. It will run the custom action SetBlackboard to store the pick grip orientation with the key pick_gripper_orientation. It will run the custom action MoveToPose using the stored pick grip position and orientation to descend to the grasp pose. It will run the custom action SetGripper with the position value 70 to close the gripper on the block. It will run the custom action MoveToPose using the specified lifted pose to raise the gripped block to the hover height.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (IK_Place_Block_Hover_Position, IK_Place_Block_Hover_Orientation, Move_To_Place_Block_Hove_Pose, IK_Place_Block_Release_Pose, IK_Place_Block_Release_Pose, Move_To_Place_Block_Grip_Pose, Open_Gripper, Move_To_Relased_Block_Hove_Pose) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action SetBlackboard to store the place hover position with the key place_hover_position. It will run the custom action SetBlackboard to store the place hover orientation with the key place_hover_orientation. It will run the custom action MoveToPose using the stored place hover position and orientation to reach a pre-release pose. It will run the custom action SetBlackboard to store the place release position with the key place_release_position. It will run the custom action SetBlackboard to store the place release orientation with the key place_release_orientation. It will run the custom action MoveToPose using the stored place release position and orientation to lower the block to the release pose. It will run the custom action SetGripper with the open command to release the block. It will run the custom action MoveToPose using the specified lifted place hover pose to retreat from the placed block.

<root main_tree_to_execute="BehaviorTree">

                <Action ID="ExecuteAddBox" auto_subfix="1" box_name="right_wall" box_pose="2.76 -1.2 1.35 0 0 0 1" box_size="5.5 0.001 2.7" group_name="arm" is_absolute="1" name="Add right wall" service_name="robot/execute_add_box"/>
                <Action ID="ExecuteAddBox" auto_subfix="1" box_name="top_wall" box_pose="2.5 0 2.7 0 0 0 1" box_size="5 2.4 0.001" group_name="arm" is_absolute="1" name="Add top wall" service_name="robot/execute_add_box"/>
                <Action ID="ExecuteAddBox" auto_subfix="1" box_name="conveyor" box_pose="-1.341 0 0.4 0 0 0 1" box_size="2.56 1.26 0.8" group_name="arm" is_absolute="1" name="Add conveyor" service_name="robot/execute_add_box"/>
                <Action ID="ExecuteAddBox" auto_subfix="0" box_name="boxsur" box_pose="0.881 0 1.35 0 0 0 1" box_size="0.001 2.4 2.7" group_name="arm" is_absolute="1" name="box_surface" service_name="robot/execute_add_box"/>
            </Sequence>

                <Action ID="ExecuteAddBox" auto_subfix="1" box_name="top_wall" box_pose="0 0 1.9 0 0 0 1" box_size="2 2.4 0.001" group_name="aubo" is_absolute="1" name="Add top wall" service_name="aubo/execute_add_box"/>
                <Action ID="ExecuteAddBox" auto_subfix="1" box_name="left_wall" box_pose="0 -1.2 0.55 0 0 0 1" box_size="2 0.001 2.7" group_name="aubo" is_absolute="1" name="Add left wall" service_name="aubo/execute_add_box"/>
                <Action ID="ExecuteAddBox" auto_subfix="1" box_name="right_wall" box_pose="0 1.2 0.55 0 0 0 1" box_size="2 0.001 2.7" group_name="aubo" is_absolute="1" name="Add right wall" service_name="aubo/execute_add_box"/>
                <Action ID="ExecuteAddBox" auto_subfix="1" box_name="Robot_back" box_pose="0.85 0 0.55 0 0 0 1" box_size="0.001 0.15 2.7" group_name="aubo" is_absolute="1" name="Add Robot back" service_name="aubo/execute_add_box"/>

                    <Action ID="ExecuteGroupLinearJointStates" goal="{pre_middle_pose}" group_name="arm" name="pre middle" service_name="robot/execute_group_joint_states" tolerance="0.01"/>
                    <Action ID="ExecuteRemoveObject" is_exact="1" name="remove boxes_surface" obj_name="boxsur" service_name="robot/execute_remove_object"/>
                    <Action ID="ConnectWaypoints" connected_waypoints="{pick_motion}" name="pick motion" service_name="connect_waypoints" waypoint1="{pre_pick_pose}" waypoint2="{pick_pose}" waypoint3="" waypoint4="" waypoint5=""/>
                    <Action ID="ExecuteGroupManyPoses" constraint="" eef_step="0.1" goal_type="0" goals="{pick_motion}" group_name="arm" name="pre pick and pick" service_name="robot/execute_group_many_poses" tolerance="0.01"/>
                    <Action ID="ExecuteSuction" enable="1" service_name="execute_suction"/>
                    <Action ID="ConnectWaypoints" connected_waypoints="{pull_out}" name="pull out" service_name="connect_waypoints" waypoint1="{post_pick_pose}" waypoint2="{post_pick_pose_edge}" waypoint3="" waypoint4="" waypoint5=""/>
                    <Action ID="ExecuteGroupManyPoses" constraint="" eef_step="0.1" goal_type="0" goals="{pull_out}" group_name="arm" name="post pick" service_name="robot/execute_group_many_poses" tolerance="0.01"/>
                    <Action ID="ExecuteGroupLinearJointStates" goal="{post_middle_pose}" group_name="arm" name="post middle" service_name="robot/execute_group_joint_states" tolerance="0.01"/>
                    <Action ID="ExecuteGroupLinearJointStates" goal="{place_pose}" group_name="arm" name="place" service_name="robot/execute_group_joint_states" tolerance="0.01"/>
                    <Action ID="ExecuteSuction" enable="0" service_name="execute_suction"/>
                    <Action ID="ExecuteAddBox" auto_subfix="0" box_name="boxsur" box_pose="0.881 0 1.35 0 0 0 1" box_size="0.001 2.4 2.7" group_name="arm" is_absolute="1" name="box_surface" service_name="robot/execute_add_box"/>
                    <Action ID="ExecuteGroupLinearJointStates" goal="{pre_middle_pose}" group_name="arm" name="back to pre middle" service_name="robot/execute_group_joint_states" tolerance="0.01"/>
                </Sequence>
            </Repeat>
        </Sequence>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <TreeNodesModel>
        <Action ID="ConnectWaypoints">
            <output_port name="connected_waypoints">PoseArray</output_port>
            <input_port default="connect_waypoints" name="service_name"/>
            <input_port name="waypoint1">Pose</input_port>
            <input_port name="waypoint2">Pose</input_port>
            <input_port name="waypoint3">Pose</input_port>
            <input_port name="waypoint4">Pose</input_port>
            <input_port name="waypoint5">Pose</input_port>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="ExecuteAddBox">
            <input_port default="1" name="auto_subfix"/>
            <input_port default="box" name="box_name"/>
            <input_port default="{place_pose}" name="box_pose"/>
            <input_port default="0.2 0.2 0.2" name="box_size"/>
            <input_port default="arm" name="group_name"/>
            <input_port default="1" name="is_absolute"/>
            <input_port default="robot/execute_add_box" name="service_name"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="ExecuteAddPlane">
            <input_port default="arm" name="group_name"/>
            <input_port default="ground" name="plane_name"/>
            <input_port default="0 0 1" name="plane_normal"/>
            <input_port default="0 0 0 0 0 0 1" name="plane_pose">Pose</input_port>
            <input_port default="robot/execute_add_plane" name="service_name"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="ExecuteAttachBox">
            <input_port name="box_name"/>
            <input_port name="box_pose"/>
            <input_port name="box_size"/>
            <input_port default="gripper" name="eef_group_name"/>
            <input_port default="arm" name="group_name"/>
            <input_port default="robot/execute_attach_box" name="service_name"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="ExecuteDetachObject">
            <input_port default="arm" name="group_name"/>
            <input_port name="obj_name"/>
            <input_port default="robot/execute_detach_object" name="service_name"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="ExecuteGroupAngularJointStates">
            <input_port default="0 0 0 0 0 0 0" name="goal"/>
            <input_port default="panda_arm" name="group_name"/>
            <input_port default="robot/execute_group_joint_states" name="service_name"/>
            <input_port default="0.01" name="tolerance"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="ExecuteGroupLinearJointStates">
            <input_port default="0.04 0.04" name="goal"/>
            <input_port default="hand" name="group_name"/>
            <input_port default="robot/execute_group_joint_states" name="service_name"/>
            <input_port default="0.01" name="tolerance"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="ExecuteGroupManyPoses">
            <input_port name="constraint">If 'r', 'p', or 'y' is given, the eef_frame should not rotate about itself along x-, y-, or z-axis. You can combine them like 'rp', 'ry', 'py', and 'rpy'. Default ''</input_port>
            <input_port default="0.1" name="eef_step"/>
            <input_port default="0" name="goal_type">0: Pose wrt the global base frame (robot base); 1: Pose wrt the base frame transferred to current eef base position; 2: pose wrt the eef frame</input_port>
            <input_port default="0 0 0 0 0 0 1" name="goals">Goals for each waypoint to reach, seperate with ; </input_port>
            <input_port default="arm" name="group_name">The group providing the reference frame</input_port>
            <input_port default="robot/execute_group_many_poses" name="service_name"/>
            <input_port default="0.01" name="tolerance"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="ExecuteGroupNamedStates">
            <input_port default="aubo" name="group_name">The group providing the reference frame</input_port>
            <input_port default="aubo/execute_group_named_states" name="service_name"/>
            <input_port default="A" name="state_name">A: Camera to up left; B: Camera to up right; C: Camera to bottom left; D: Camera to bottom right</input_port>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="ExecuteGroupPose">
            <input_port name="constraint">If 'r', 'p', or 'y' is given, the eef_frame should not rotate about itself along x-, y-, or z-axis. You can combine them like 'rp', 'ry', 'py', and 'rpy'. Default ''</input_port>
            <input_port default="0 0 0 0 0 0 1" name="goal"/>
            <input_port default="0" name="goal_type">0: Pose wrt the global base frame (robot base); 1: Pose wrt the base frame transferred to current eef base position; 2: pose wrt the eef frame</input_port>
            <input_port default="arm" name="group_name">The group providing the reference frame</input_port>
            <input_port default="robot/execute_group_pose" name="service_name"/>
            <input_port default="0.01" name="tolerance"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="ExecuteGroupShift">
            <input_port default="z" name="axis">Could be x y z roll pitch yaw</input_port>
            <input_port default="0" name="goal"/>
            <input_port default="arm" name="group_name"/>
            <input_port default="0" name="is_absolute"/>
            <input_port default="robot/execute_group_shift" name="service_name"/>
            <input_port default="0.01" name="tolerance"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="ExecuteGroupSpeed">
            <input_port default="arm" name="group_name">The group providing the reference frame</input_port>
            <input_port default="robot/execute_group_speed" name="service_name"/>
            <input_port default="0.5" name="value">The velocity scaling factor, Allowed values are in (0,1]</input_port>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="ExecutePlanning">
            <input_port name="category">0 for large box, 1 for small box</input_port>
            <output_port default="{pick_pose}" name="pick_pose">Pose</output_port>
            <output_port default="{place_pose}" name="place_pose">Double Array</output_port>
            <input_port default="{box_surface_pose}" name="pose">Pose</input_port>
            <output_port default="{post_middle_pose}" name="post_middle_pose">Double Array</output_port>
            <output_port default="{post_pick_pose}" name="post_pick_pose">Pose</output_port>
            <output_port default="{post_pick_pose_edge}" name="post_pick_pose_edge">Pose</output_port>
            <output_port default="{pre_middle_pose}" name="pre_middle_pose">Double Array</output_port>
            <output_port default="{pre_pick_pose}" name="pre_pick_pose">Pose</output_port>
            <input_port default="execute_planning" name="service_name"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="ExecuteRemoveObject">
            <input_port default="1" name="is_exact"/>
            <input_port name="obj_name"/>
            <input_port default="robot/execute_remove_object" name="service_name"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="ExecuteSuction">
            <input_port default="0" name="enable"/>
            <input_port default="execute_suction" name="service_name"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="FetchDetectedInfo">
            <output_port name="category">String, 0 for large box, 1 for small box</output_port>
            <output_port name="pose">Pose</output_port>
            <input_port default="fetch_detected_info" name="service_name"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="SenseObjectPose">
            <output_port name="category">String, 0 for large box, 1 for small box</output_port>
            <output_port name="pose">Pose</output_port>
            <input_port default="sense_object_pose" name="service_name"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="StoreDetectedInfo">
            <input_port name="category">String, 0 for large box, 1 for small box</input_port>
            <input_port name="pose">Pose</input_port>
            <input_port default="store_detected_info" name="service_name"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="TypeInPose">
            <output_port name="pose_on_blackboard">Pose on blackboard</output_port>
            <input_port default="type_in_pose" name="service_name"/>
            <input_port default="0 0 0 0 0 0 1" name="type_in_pose">Pose to be typed in</input_port>
        </Action>
    </TreeNodesModel>
    <!-- ////////// -->
</root>
mrq:
The mission describes a coordinated depalletizing operation that sets speeds, configures collision objects, initializes two manipulators, and repeatedly executes a pick-and-place cycle under a strict ordered control.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (ExecuteGroupSpeed, ExecuteGroupSpeed) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action ExecuteGroupSpeed for group arm to set the velocity scaling to 1.0. It will run the custom action ExecuteGroupSpeed for group aubo to set the velocity scaling to 0.5.

Next, it will execute the following actions (ExecuteAddPlane, ExecuteAddBox, ExecuteAddBox, ExecuteAddBox, ExecuteAddBox, ExecuteAddBox) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action ExecuteAddPlane for group arm to add a ground plane with the specified normal and pose. It will run the custom action ExecuteAddBox for group arm to add a left wall with the specified pose and size. It will run the custom action ExecuteAddBox for group arm to add a right wall with the specified pose and size. It will run the custom action ExecuteAddBox for group arm to add a top wall with the specified pose and size. It will run the custom action ExecuteAddBox for group arm to add a conveyor with the specified pose and size. It will run the custom action ExecuteAddBox for group arm to add a thin box_surface barrier named boxsur with the specified pose and size.

Next, it will execute the following actions (ExecuteAddBox, ExecuteAddBox, ExecuteAddBox, ExecuteAddBox, ExecuteAddBox) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action ExecuteAddBox for group aubo to add a conveyor with the specified pose and size. It will run the custom action ExecuteAddBox for group aubo to add a top wall with the specified pose and size. It will run the custom action ExecuteAddBox for group aubo to add a left wall with the specified pose and size. It will run the custom action ExecuteAddBox for group aubo to add a right wall with the specified pose and size. It will run the custom action ExecuteAddBox for group aubo to add a rear robot barrier with the specified pose and size.

Next, it will execute the following actions (ExecuteGroupNamedStates, ExecuteGroupNamedStates) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action ExecuteGroupNamedStates for group aubo to reach the named state A. It will run the custom action ExecuteGroupNamedStates for group arm to reach the named state left_pre.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (Sequence) for a set number of times or infinitely. It will execute the sub-sequence exactly 100 times. It will execute the following actions (SenseObjectPose, ExecutePlanning, ExecuteGroupLinearJointStates, ExecuteRemoveObject, ConnectWaypoints, ExecuteGroupManyPoses, ExecuteSuction, ConnectWaypoints, ExecuteGroupManyPoses, ExecuteGroupLinearJointStates, ExecuteGroupLinearJointStates, ExecuteSuction, ExecuteAddBox, ExecuteGroupLinearJointStates) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action SenseObjectPose to estimate the object pose and store it as box_surface_pose. It will run the custom action ExecutePlanning to compute pre-, pick-, post-, and place poses using box_surface_pose and to output pre_middle_pose, pre_pick_pose, pick_pose, post_pick_pose, post_pick_pose_edge, post_middle_pose, and place_pose. It will run the custom action ExecuteGroupLinearJointStates for group arm to move to pre_middle_pose with the given tolerance. It will run the custom action ExecuteRemoveObject for group arm to remove the collision object boxsur exactly. It will run the custom action ConnectWaypoints to connect pre_pick_pose and pick_pose into the waypoint array pick_motion. It will run the custom action ExecuteGroupManyPoses for group arm to follow pick_motion with the specified eef_step, goal_type, and tolerance. It will run the custom action ExecuteSuction to enable the vacuum for grasping. It will run the custom action ConnectWaypoints to connect post_pick_pose and post_pick_pose_edge into the waypoint array pull_out. It will run the custom action ExecuteGroupManyPoses for group arm to execute the pull_out path with the specified eef_step, goal_type, and tolerance. It will run the custom action ExecuteGroupLinearJointStates for group arm to move to post_middle_pose with the given tolerance. It will run the custom action ExecuteGroupLinearJointStates for group arm to move to place_pose with the given tolerance. It will run the custom action ExecuteSuction to disable the vacuum for release. It will run the custom action ExecuteAddBox for group arm to re-add the collision object boxsur with the specified pose and size. It will run the custom action ExecuteGroupLinearJointStates for group arm to return to pre_middle_pose with the given tolerance.

<!--
    This Behavior Tree replans the global path periodically at 1 Hz and it also has
    recovery actions.
-->

<root main_tree_to_execute="QueueTree">
  <BehaviorTree ID="QueueTree">
    <RestoreConfig node_name="/footprint_publisher">
      <RestoreConfig node_name="/controller_server">
	<RestoreConfig node_name="/global_costmap/global_costmap">
	  <Sequence>
	    <!-- set the footprint as smallest -->
	    <ChangeParam node_name="/footprint_publisher"
			 param_name="footprint_mode"
			 param_value="1"  name="ChangeParamFootprintToSmall"/>
	    
	    <ChangeParam node_name="/controller_server"
			 param_name="FollowPath.max_vel_x"
			 param_value="0.5"  name="ChangeParamSpeedDown"/>
	    
	    <ChangeParam node_name="/controller_server"
			 param_name="cabot_goal_checker.xy_goal_tolerance"
			 param_value="0.20"  name="ChangeParamFollowPathXyGoalTolerance"/>
	    <ChangeParam node_name="/controller_server"
			 param_name="cabot_goal_checker.yaw_goal_tolerance"
			 param_value="0.20"  name="ChangeParamFollowPathYawGoalTolerance"/>
	    
	    <ChangeParam node_name="/global_costmap/global_costmap"
			 param_name="navcog_path_layer.walk_weight"
			 param_value="[0.3, 0.0, 0.3] "  name="ChangeParamWalkWeightToCenter"/>
	    <!-- 1: use adjust mode to use actual center of the corridor based on costmap
		 0: use navcog path as the ceter of the corridor -->
	    <ChangeParam node_name="/global_costmap/global_costmap"
			 param_name="navcog_path_layer.path_mode"
			 param_value="0"  name="ChangeParamToAdjust"/>
	    <ChangeParam node_name="/global_costmap/global_costmap"
			 param_name="navcog_path_layer.path_adjusted_center"
			 param_value="0.0"  name="ChangeParamPathAdjustedCenter"/>

	    <ChangeParam node_name="/global_costmap/global_costmap"
			 param_name="inflation_layer.inflation_radius"
			 param_value="0.2"  name="ChangeParamInflationRadius"/>
	    
	    <!-- compute a path -->
	    <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="999999999" name="InitialComputePathRepeat">
	      <Sequence name="ComputePathSequence">
		<!-- clear costmap -->
		<!--
		<ClearCostmapAroundRobot name="ClearCostmapAroundRobot" server_timeout="5000"
					 service_name="global_costmap/clear_around_global_costmap"
					 window_size_x="20" window_size_y="20"/>
		-->
		<ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearGlobalCostmap-context" server_timeout="5000"
				    service_name="global_costmap/clear_entirely_global_costmap"/>
		
		<!-- wait long enough to include neighbor obstacles -->
		<WaitFloat wait_duration_float="0.5"/>
		<ComputePathToPose goal="{goal}" path="{path_temp}" planner_id="GridBased"/>
		<CheckPath  path="{path_temp}" path_topic="/path" plan_topic="/plan" path_out="{path}" />
		<!-- front vibration -->
		<!--
		<PublishTopicInt8 topic="/cabot/notification" value="5" />
		-->
	      </Sequence>
	      <ForceSuccess>
		<WaitFloat wait_duration_float="0.5"/>
	      </ForceSuccess>
	    </RecoveryNode>
	    
	    <!-- main loop -->
	    <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="999999999" name="NavigateRecovery">
	      <!-- navigation -->
	      <PipelineSequence name="NavigateWithReplanning">
		<!-- Check people status frequently -->
		<!-- find people needs to be avoided not in queue -->
		<!--
		<RateController hz="2" name="AvoidPeople">
		  <Fallback name="AvoidPersonFallback">
		    <Sequence name="AvoidPerson">
		      <PeopleExist people_topic="people" people_out="{people}"/>
		      <NeedToAvoid people="{people}" robot_frame="base_footprint" path="{path}" avoid="{people_avoid}"/>
		      <SomeoneNotInQueue queue_topic="queue_people_py/queue" people="{people_avoid}" people_out_not_queue="{people_avoid_not_queue}"/>
		      <IgnorePeople people="{people_avoid_not_queue}" ignore_topic="ignore" wait_duration="0.5"/>
		      <ComputePathToPose goal="{goal}" path="{path_temp}" planner_id="GridBased"/>
		      <CheckPath current="{path}" path="{path_temp}" path_topic="/path" plan_topic="/plan" path_out="{path}" />
		      <Wait wait_duration="5"/>
		    </Sequence>
		    <AlwaysSuccess/>
		  </Fallback>
		</RateController>
		-->
		<RateController hz="2.0" name="UpdatePath">
		  <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="1" name="UpdatePathRecovery">
		    <Sequence>
		      <ClearEntireCostmap name="UpdatePathClearGlobalCostmap-context" server_timeout="5000"
					  service_name="global_costmap/clear_entirely_global_costmap"/>
		      <WaitFloat wait_duration_float="0.5"/>
		      <ComputePathToPose goal="{goal}" path="{path_temp}" planner_id="GridBased"/>
		      <CheckPath  path="{path_temp}" path_topic="/path" plan_topic="/plan" path_out="{path}" />
		    </Sequence>
		    <ForceSuccess>
		      <WaitFloat wait_duration_float="0.5"/>
		    </ForceSuccess>
		  </RecoveryNode>
		</RateController>
		
		<!-- follow path -->
		<RecoveryNode number_of_retries="1" name="FollowPathRecovery">
		  <FollowPath path="{path}" controller_id="FollowPath" />
		  <AlwaysSuccess/>
		</RecoveryNode>
	      </PipelineSequence>
	      
	      <!-- recovery -->
	      <SequenceStar name="RecoveryActions">
		<ClearEntireCostmap name="Recovery-ClearGlobalCostmap-context" server_timeout="5000"
                                    service_name="/global_costmap/clear_entirely_global_costmap"/>
		<ClearEntireCostmap name="Recovery-ClearLocalCostmap-Context" server_timeout="5000"
				    service_name="/local_costmap/clear_entirely_local_costmap"/>
		<Wait wait_duration="1.0"/>
	      </SequenceStar>
	    </RecoveryNode>
	  </Sequence>
	</RestoreConfig>
      </RestoreConfig>
    </RestoreConfig>
  </BehaviorTree>
</root>
mrq:
The mission describes a queued navigation task that configures system parameters, computes an initial path, continually replans at 2 Hz, follows the path, and performs recovery when navigation fails.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (ChangeParamFootprintToSmall, ChangeParamSpeedDown, ChangeParamFollowPathXyGoalTolerance, ChangeParamFollowPathYawGoalTolerance, ChangeParamWalkWeightToCenter, ChangeParamToAdjust, ChangeParamPathAdjustedCenter, ChangeParamInflationRadius, InitialComputePathRepeat, NavigateRecovery) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action ChangeParamFootprintToSmall to set the footprint publisher to the smallest footprint. It will run the custom action ChangeParamSpeedDown to reduce the maximum linear velocity of the FollowPath controller. It will run the custom action ChangeParamFollowPathXyGoalTolerance to set the XY goal tolerance for the goal checker. It will run the custom action ChangeParamFollowPathYawGoalTolerance to set the yaw goal tolerance for the goal checker. It will run the custom action ChangeParamWalkWeightToCenter to set the walk weight parameters of the global costmap path layer. It will run the custom action ChangeParamToAdjust to set the path mode parameter of the global costmap path layer. It will run the custom action ChangeParamPathAdjustedCenter to set the adjusted center parameter of the global costmap path layer. It will run the custom action ChangeParamInflationRadius to set the inflation radius of the global costmap. It will execute main InitialComputePathRepeat. If it fails, it will execute recovery action (a delayed wait) and then retry main InitialComputePathRepeat. If recovery action fails and retries are exhausted, it will stop execution. It will execute main NavigateRecovery. If it fails, it will execute recovery action (RecoveryActions) and then retry main NavigateRecovery. If recovery action fails and retries are exhausted, it will stop execution.

Next, it will execute main ComputePathSequence. If it fails, it will execute recovery action (a delayed wait) and then retry main ComputePathSequence. If recovery action fails and retries are exhausted, it will stop execution. It will execute the following actions (ClearGlobalCostmap-context, WaitFloat, ComputePathToPose, CheckPath) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action ClearGlobalCostmap-context to clear the entire global costmap. It will run the custom action WaitFloat to wait for 0.5 seconds to allow obstacle data to settle. It will run the ROS action ComputePathToPose with the provided goal and will store the temporary path. It will run the custom action CheckPath to validate the temporary path and will store the validated path.

Next, it will execute the following actions (NavigateWithReplanning) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. While an action is running or has succeeded, it will go to the next action. It will execute the following actions (UpdatePath) simultaneously with advancing behavior relative to running status due to the pipeline design. If either a success threshold is reached or all actions succeed, the execution was successful. Else if any action fails, the execution fails. It will execute main UpdatePathRecovery. If it fails, it will execute recovery action (a delayed wait) and then retry main UpdatePathRecovery. If recovery action fails and retries are exhausted, it will stop execution. It will execute the following actions (ClearGlobalCostmap-context, WaitFloat, ComputePathToPose, CheckPath) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action ClearGlobalCostmap-context to clear the entire global costmap at each update cycle. It will run the custom action WaitFloat to wait for 0.5 seconds before replanning. It will run the ROS action ComputePathToPose to compute an updated temporary path. It will run the custom action CheckPath to validate the updated temporary path and will store the resulting path. It will execute main FollowPathRecovery. If it fails, it will execute recovery action (AlwaysSuccess) and then retry main FollowPathRecovery. If recovery action fails and retries are exhausted, it will stop execution. It will run the ROS action FollowPath using the current validated path with the specified controller.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (Recovery-ClearGlobalCostmap-context, Recovery-ClearLocalCostmap-Context, Wait) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will run the custom action Recovery-ClearGlobalCostmap-context to clear the entire global costmap. It will run the custom action Recovery-ClearLocalCostmap-Context to clear the entire local costmap. It will run the custom action Wait to pause for 1.0 seconds before the next retry cycle.

<root main_tree_to_execute="BehaviorTree">
    <!--------------------------------------->
    <BehaviorTree ID="BehaviorTree">
        <Root>
            <Fallback name="FB1">
                <Sequence name="S1">
                    <Fallback name="FB2">
                        <Sequence name="S3">
                            <Condition ID="Room_known"/>
                            <Action ID="Go_to_room"/>
                        </Sequence>
                        <Action ID="Follow_human"/>
                    </Fallback>
                    <Action ID="Find_bottle"/>
                    <Action ID="Fetch_bottle"/>
                </Sequence>
                <Action ID="Ask_for_help"/>
            </Fallback>
        </Root>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!--------------------------------------->
    <TreeNodesModel>
        <Action ID="Ask_for_help"/>
        <Action ID="Fetch_bottle"/>
        <Action ID="Find_bottle"/>
        <Action ID="Follow_human"/>
        <Action ID="Go_to_kitchen"/>
        <Action ID="Go_to_room"/>
        <Condition ID="Room_known"/>
    </TreeNodesModel>
    <!--------------------------------------->
</root>
mrq:
The mission describes a household assistance task that will try a primary strategy to reach a known room, locate a bottle, and fetch it, and will otherwise request help.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (S1, Ask_for_help) consecutively. If any action (S1) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution will fail.

Next, it will execute the following actions (FB2, Find_bottle, Fetch_bottle) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action Find_bottle only after FB2 succeeds. It will run the custom action Fetch_bottle only after Find_bottle succeeds.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (S3, Follow_human) consecutively. If any action (S3) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution will fail.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (Room_known, Go_to_room) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for the condition Room_known. It will run the custom action Go_to_room only after Room_known succeeds.

mrq:
The mission describes a pick-and-place task that searches for a ball, grasps it, finds a bin, approaches the bin, and places the ball, with a help request used only if the main sequence fails.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (Fallback2, Fallback3, Fallback4, Fallback5, Fallback6, Fallback7) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute the following actions (BallFound, FindBall) consecutively. If any action (BallFound) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule. It will run the custom action FindBall if the check fails. It will execute the following actions (BallClose, ApproachBall) consecutively. If any action (BallClose) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule. It will run the custom action ApproachBall if the check fails. It will execute the following actions (BallGrasped, GraspBall) consecutively. If any action (BallGrasped) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule. It will run the custom action GraspBall if the check fails. It will execute the following actions (BinFound, FindBin) consecutively. If any action (BinFound) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule. It will run the custom action FindBin if the check fails. It will execute the following actions (BinClose, ApproachBin) consecutively. If any action (BinClose) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule. It will run the custom action ApproachBin if the check fails. It will execute the following actions (BallPlaced, PlaceBall) consecutively. If any action (BallPlaced) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule. It will run the custom action PlaceBall if the check fails.

Next, it will execute the following actions (Sequence, AskForHelp) consecutively. If any action (Sequence) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will run the custom action AskForHelp only if the preceding sequence fails.

<root main_tree_to_execute="MainTree">
  <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
    <PipelineSequence name="NavigateWithReplanning">
      <SpeedController min_rate="0.1" max_rate="1.0" min_speed="0.0" max_speed="0.26" filter_duration="0.3">
        <ComputePathToPose goal="{goal}" path="{path}" planner_id="GridBased"/>
      </SpeedController>
      <FollowPath path="{path}"  controller_id="FollowPath"/>
    </PipelineSequence>
  </BehaviorTree>
</root>
mrq: The mission describes a navigation task that computes a global path under a speed-controlled planner and advances to following the path while allowing replanning at 1 Hz. First, the robot will execute the following actions (SpeedController, FollowPath) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute the following actions (ComputePathToPose) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails while running the custom action SpeedController to enforce a planning rate between 0.1 Hz and 1.0 Hz with speed limits between 0.0 m/s and 0.26 m/s and a filter duration of 0.3 s. It will run the ROS action ComputePathToPose with the provided goal and will store the resulting path under speed control. It will run the ROS action FollowPath using the stored path and the specified controller while an action is running or has succeeded and will proceed to the next action accordingly.

mrq:
The mission describes an active go-home policy that monitors a go-home condition and triggers a go-home action with continuous re-evaluation.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (Inverter, GoHomeMockNode) in sequence. If any action fails, it will stop execution. While an action is still running, it will re-evaluate previous actions. It will execute inverted the custom action IsGoHomeOkConditionNode. If IsGoHomeOkConditionNode succeeds, it will reverse it to fail. Else if IsGoHomeOkConditionNode fails, it will reverse it to succeed. It will execute the custom action GoHomeMockNode and make the robot go home when the inverted condition allows progression.

<root main_tree_to_execute="BehaviorTree">
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="BehaviorTree">
        <Sequence>
            <Fallback>
                <ReactiveFallback>
                    <Repeat num_cycles="1">
                        <AlwaysFailure/>
                    </Repeat>
                </ReactiveFallback>
            </Fallback>
            <SequenceStar name="DoSequenceStar">
                <RetryUntilSuccesful num_attempts="1">
                    <BlackboardCheckString value_A="" return_on_mismatch="" value_B="">
                        <AlwaysSuccess/>
                    </BlackboardCheckString>
                </RetryUntilSuccesful>
            </SequenceStar>
            <Inverter>
                <ForceFailure>
                    <ForceSuccess>
                        <SetBlackboard value="value" output_key=""/>
                    </ForceSuccess>
                </ForceFailure>
            </Inverter>
            <ReactiveSequence>
                <Action ID="Pippo"/>
            </ReactiveSequence>
        </Sequence>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <TreeNodesModel>
        <Action ID="Pippo"/>
    </TreeNodesModel>
    <!-- ////////// -->
</root>
mrq:
The mission describes a composite control task that executes a top-level ordered sequence containing a fallback guard, a remembered sequence with a guarded blackboard check, a forced-outcome blackboard write wrapped by an inverter, and a reactive sequence that runs a custom action.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (ReactiveFallback) consecutively. If any action (ReactiveFallback) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution will fail. It will execute the following actions [Repeat] in order. If [Repeat] succeeds, it will stop execution. Else it will keep executing and re-evaluating previous actions whenever an action is running. It will execute [Action] for a set number of times or infinitely. It will execute [Action] and make sure it always fails.

Next, it will execute the following actions (Retry) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will execute [Action]. If it fails, it will re-execute it either for a specified number of times or until it succeeds. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule. It will execute [Action] and make sure it always succeeds.

Next, it will execute inverted [Action]. If [Action] succeeds, it will reverse it to fail. Else if [Action] fails, it will reverse it to succeed. It will execute [Action] and make sure it always fails. It will execute [Action] and make sure it always succeeds. It will execute the custom action SetBlackboard and will ensure the blackboard key receives the value value.

Finally, it will execute the following actions [Action] in sequence. If [Action] fails, it will stop execution. While [Action] is still running, it will re-evaluate previous actions. It will execute the custom action Pippo.

mrq: The mission describes a patrol task that repeatedly selects a waypoint and moves to it until the process fails. First, the robot will execute the following actions (Explored, SelectPF, Move, Explored) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action Explored to verify the current exploration status before selecting a waypoint. It will run the custom action SelectPF to produce a waypoint and will store the waypoint in memory as wp. It will run the custom action Move using the stored waypoint wp as the movement goal. It will run the custom action Explored again to verify the exploration status after movement succeeds. Finally, it will execute Move-Select-Explored checks repeatedly by executing the sequence again whenever the sequence succeeds, and it will stop repeating when any action in the sequence fails.

The mission describes a patrolling task that repeatedly navigates through waypoints and recharges if patrolling fails. First, the robot will execute the following actions (Repeat) for a set number of times or infinitely. It will execute the action Fallback for 100 cycles as specified.

Next, it will execute the following actions (patrolling, recharging) consecutively. If any action (patrolling) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails.

Next, it will execute the following actions (NavigateToWp, NavigateToWp, NavigateToWp, NavigateToWp, NavigateToWp, NavigateToWp, NavigateToWp) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action NavigateToWp with the goal wp_1. It will run the custom action NavigateToWp with the goal wp_2 after the previous navigation succeeds. It will run the custom action NavigateToWp with the goal wp_3 after the previous navigation succeeds. It will run the custom action NavigateToWp with the goal wp_4 after the previous navigation succeeds. It will run the custom action NavigateToWp with the goal wp_5 after the previous navigation succeeds. It will run the custom action NavigateToWp with the goal wp_6 after the previous navigation succeeds. It will run the custom action NavigateToWp with the goal wp_7 after the previous navigation succeeds.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (NavigateToWp, Recharge) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action NavigateToWp with the goal recharge_station. It will run the custom action Recharge after the navigation to the recharge station succeeds.

<root main_tree_to_execute="MainTree">
  <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
    <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="10" name="NavigateRecovery">
      <PipelineSequence name="NavigateWithReplanning">
        <RateController hz="1.0">
          <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="1" name="ComputePathToPose">
            <ComputePathToPose goal="{goal}" path="{path}" planner_id="GridBased"/>
            <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearGlobalCostmap-Context" service_name="global_costmap/clear_entirely_global_costmap"/>
          </RecoveryNode>
        </RateController>
        <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="1" name="FollowPath">
          <FollowPath path="{path}" controller_id="FollowPath"/>
          <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearLocalCostmap-Context" service_name="local_costmap/clear_entirely_local_costmap"/>
        </RecoveryNode>
      </PipelineSequence>
      <ReactiveFallback name="RecoveryFallback">
        <GoalUpdated/>
        <SequenceStar name="RecoveryActions">
          <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearLocalCostmap-Subtree" service_name="local_costmap/clear_entirely_local_costmap"/>
          <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearGlobalCostmap-Subtree" service_name="global_costmap/clear_entirely_global_costmap"/>
          <!-- <Spin spin_dist="1.57"/> -->
          <Wait wait_duration="1"/>
        </SequenceStar>
      </ReactiveFallback>
    </RecoveryNode>
  </BehaviorTree>
</root>
mrq:
The mission describes a navigation task that periodically replans a global path at 1 Hz, follows the path, and performs structured recoveries when progress fails.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (RateController, RecoveryNode-ComputePathToPose, RecoveryNode-FollowPath) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute the following actions (RateController) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. While an action is running or has succeeded, it will go to the next action. It will run the custom action RateController at 1.0 Hz to pace subsequent actions. It will execute the following actions (ComputePathToPose, ClearEntireCostmap-Context) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. If the main action fails, it will execute the recovery action and then retry the main action once. It will run the ROS action ComputePathToPose with the provided goal and will store the resulting path. If ComputePathToPose fails, it will run the custom action ClearEntireCostmap-Context to clear the global costmap and will retry ComputePathToPose one time. It will execute the following actions (FollowPath, ClearEntireCostmap-Context) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. If the main action fails, it will execute the recovery action and then retry the main action once. It will run the ROS action FollowPath using the stored path with the specified controller. If FollowPath fails, it will run the custom action ClearEntireCostmap-Context to clear the local costmap and will retry FollowPath one time.

Next, it will execute the main action and use a recovery strategy if needed with up to ten retries. It will execute main NavigateWithReplanning. If it fails, it will execute recovery action RecoveryFallback. After recovery, it will retry main action NavigateWithReplanning. If the recovery action fails and retries are exhausted, it will stop execution.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (GoalUpdated, RecoveryActions) in order. If any action succeeds, it will stop execution. Else it will keep executing and re-evaluating previous actions whenever an action is running. It will run the custom action GoalUpdated to check if a new goal has arrived and will succeed immediately when a new goal is present. It will execute the following actions (ClearLocalCostmap-Subtree, ClearGlobalCostmap-Subtree, Wait) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will run the custom action ClearLocalCostmap-Subtree to clear the local costmap. It will run the custom action ClearGlobalCostmap-Subtree to clear the global costmap. It will run the custom action Wait for 1 second to allow the system to settle before the next attempt.

<root main_tree_to_execute = "MainTree" >

			<Action ID="SaySomething"   name="action_hello" message="Hello"/>
			<Sequence>
				<SubTree ID = "Variant1"/>
				<SubTree ID = "Variant2"/> 
				<AlwaysSuccess/> 
			</Sequence >			
			<Action ID="OpenGripper"    name="open_gripper"/>
			<Action ID="ApproachObject" name="approach_object" velocity="{vp_velocity}"/>

        </Sequence>
     </BehaviorTree>
</root >
mrq:
The mission describes a pick-and-place interaction that greets, configures speed, executes variant behaviors, and performs a grasp sequence in strict order.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (SetBlackboard, SaySomething, Sequence, OpenGripper, ApproachObject, CloseGripper, SaySomething) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action SetBlackboard to store the value 3 under the key vp_velocity. It will run the custom action SaySomething named action_hello to speak the message Hello. It will execute the following actions (SubTree Variant1, SubTree Variant2, AlwaysSuccess) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action OpenGripper named open_gripper to ensure the gripper is open. It will run the custom action ApproachObject named approach_object using the velocity stored in vp_velocity. It will run the custom action CloseGripper named close_gripper to secure the object. It will run the custom action SaySomething named action_bye to speak the message Bye.

Next, it will execute the following actions (SubTree Variant1, SubTree Variant2, AlwaysSuccess) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute SubTree Variant1 and proceed only if it succeeds. It will execute SubTree Variant2 after SubTree Variant1 succeeds. It will execute the custom action AlwaysSuccess and make sure it always succeeds.

mrq: The mission describes a staged navigation routine that will wait between moves and will visit six named goal positions in strict order. First, the robot will execute the following actions (WaitSeconds, UPOGoToGoalByName, WaitSeconds, UPOGoToGoalByName, WaitSeconds, UPOGoToGoalByName, WaitSeconds, UPOGoToGoalByName, WaitSeconds, UPOGoToGoalByName, WaitSeconds, UPOGoToGoalByName, WaitSeconds) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action WaitSeconds for 2 seconds before the first move. It will execute the following actions (UPOGoToGoalByName) and make sure it always succeeds. It will execute the following actions (UPOGoToGoalByName) for a set number of times or infinitely. It will run the custom action UPOGoToGoalByName to go to the named goal stage_1_pos_final_1 up to 10 times. It will run the custom action WaitSeconds for 2 seconds before the second move. It will execute the following actions (UPOGoToGoalByName) and make sure it always succeeds. It will execute the following actions (UPOGoToGoalByName) for a set number of times or infinitely. It will run the custom action UPOGoToGoalByName to go to the named goal stage_1_pos_final_2 up to 10 times. It will run the custom action WaitSeconds for 2 seconds before the third move. It will execute the following actions (UPOGoToGoalByName) and make sure it always succeeds. It will execute the following actions (UPOGoToGoalByName) for a set number of times or infinitely. It will run the custom action UPOGoToGoalByName to go to the named goal stage_1_pos_final_3 up to 10 times. It will run the custom action WaitSeconds for 2 seconds before the fourth move. It will execute the following actions (UPOGoToGoalByName) and make sure it always succeeds. It will execute the following actions (UPOGoToGoalByName) for a set number of times or infinitely. It will run the custom action UPOGoToGoalByName to go to the named goal stage_1_pos_final_4 up to 10 times. It will run the custom action WaitSeconds for 2 seconds before the fifth move. It will execute the following actions (UPOGoToGoalByName) and make sure it always succeeds. It will execute the following actions (UPOGoToGoalByName) for a set number of times or infinitely. It will run the custom action UPOGoToGoalByName to go to the named goal stage_1_pos_final_5 up to 10 times. It will run the custom action WaitSeconds for 2 seconds before the sixth move. It will execute the following actions (UPOGoToGoalByName) and make sure it always succeeds. It will execute the following actions (UPOGoToGoalByName) for a set number of times or infinitely. It will run the custom action UPOGoToGoalByName to go to the named goal stage_1_pos_final_6 up to 10 times. It will run the custom action WaitSeconds for 1 second after reaching the final goal.

<root main_tree_to_execute = "MainTree">
    <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
        <Repeat num_cycles="1">
            <Sequence>
                <Nav2ClearLocalCostMap/>
                <Nav2ClearGlobalCostMap/>
                <SetBlackboard output_key="Goal_a" value="-0.7,-3.8" />
                <Nav2ComputePathClient goal="{Goal_a}" />
            </Sequence>
        </Repeat>
    </BehaviorTree>
</root>
mrq: The mission describes a single-cycle navigation preparation and planning task that clears costmaps, sets a goal, and computes a path in strict order. First, the robot will execute the following actions (Nav2ClearLocalCostMap, Nav2ClearGlobalCostMap, SetBlackboard, Nav2ComputePathClient) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the ROS action Nav2ClearLocalCostMap to remove local obstacles from the local costmap. It will run the ROS action Nav2ClearGlobalCostMap to remove stale obstacles from the global costmap after the local clearing succeeds. It will run the custom action SetBlackboard to store the goal coordinate -0.7,-3.8 under the key Goal_a after both costmaps are cleared. It will run the ROS action Nav2ComputePathClient using the stored Goal_a to compute a path after the goal is set. Next, it will execute the ROS action Repeat for one cycle to execute the prior sequence for exactly one iteration.

mrq: The mission describes a pallet-handling task that loads a pallet, moves to a specified goal, and drops the pallet in strict order. First, The robot will execute the following actions (LoadPallet, Move, DropPallet) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action LoadPallet to place the pallet onto its forks. It will run the custom action Move to drive to the goal specified by the parameter arg2 after the pallet loading succeeds. It will run the custom action DropPallet to place the pallet at the destination after the move action succeeds.

<root main_tree_to_execute = "MainTree">

    <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
        <Sequence name="build a gearbox kit">
            <!--            <SubTree ID="prepare navigation and arm" />-->
            <!--            <SubTree ID="go pick large gear" />-->
            <SubTree ID="go insert large gear" name="" />
            <!--            <SubTree ID="go place machined large gear" />-->
            <!--            <SubTree ID="go pick small gear" />-->
            <!--            <SubTree ID="go place small gear" />-->
            <!--            <SubTree ID="go pick gearbox top" />-->
            <!--            <SubTree ID="go place gearbox top" />-->
            <!--            <SubTree ID="go pick gearbox bottom" />-->
            <!--            <SubTree ID="go place gearbox bottom" />-->

            <!--            <SubTree ID="ask" what="Press Enter to pick 1st screw..."/>-->
            <!--            <SubTree name="go pick first screw" ID="go pick screw" />-->
            <SubTree ID="go pick screw" name="go pick first screw"/>
            <!--            <SubTree ID="ask" what="Press Enter to place 1st screw..."/>-->
            <!--            <SubTree name="go place first screw" ID="go place screw" />-->
            <SubTree ID="go place screw" name="go place first screw"/>
            <!--            <SubTree ID="ask" what="Press Enter to pick 2nd screw..."/>-->
            <!--            <SubTree name="go pick second screw" ID="go pick screw" />-->
            <!--            <SubTree ID="ask" what="Press Enter to place 2nd screw..."/>-->
            <!--            <SubTree name="go place second screw" ID="go place screw" />-->

            <!--            <SubTree ID="go pick caddy" />-->
            <!--            <SubTree ID="go drop off caddy" />-->
        </Sequence>
    </BehaviorTree>

    <BehaviorTree ID="go insert large gear">
        <Fallback name="go insert large gear">
            <Sequence name="go insert large gear">
                <RetryUntilSuccesful num_attempts="3" name="">
                    <Action ID="move to" place="chuck place" name="move to see chuck" />
                </RetryUntilSuccesful>

                <RetryUntilSuccesful num_attempts="3" name="">
                    <Sequence name="">
                        <Sequence name="detect chuck">
                            <Action ID="lift torso" name="" />
                            <Action ID="look at chuck roughly" name="" />
                            <Action ID="detect chuck" output="{object pose}" name="" />
                        </Sequence>
                        <Fallback name="insert large gear">
                            <Sequence name="insert large gear">
                                <Action ID="convert to insertion pose" name="" from="{object pose}" output="{insertion pose}" />
                                <Action ID="scan scene" name="scan scene for collision avoidance" />
                                <Action ID="attach large gear to scene" name="" />
                                <Action ID="look at" name="look at chuck" pose="{object pose}" />
                                <Action ID="reach" name="approach door" pose="{insertion pose}" shift_x="-0.35" shift_y="-0.12" shift_z="0.2" />
                                <Action ID="reach" name="prepare to insert" pose="{insertion pose}" shift_y="-0.17" />
                                <Action ID="trial and error insert" name="" pose="{insertion pose}" />
                                <Action ID="remove large gear from scene" name="" />
                                <Action ID="reach" name="back to prepare to insert" pose="{insertion pose}" shift_y="-0.17" />
                                <Action ID="reach" name="back to approach for door" pose="{insertion pose}" shift_x="-0.35" shift_y="0.12" shift_z="0.2" />
                                <Action ID="retreat arm" name="" />
                            </Sequence>

                            <ForceFailure name="reset arm">
                                <Action ID="retreat arm" name="" />
                            </ForceFailure>
                        </Fallback>
                    </Sequence>
                </RetryUntilSuccesful>
            </Sequence>

            <Action ID="ask" name="" what="unable insert large gear" />
        </Fallback>
    </BehaviorTree>

    <BehaviorTree ID="go pick screw">
        <!--        <Action name="go pick first screw" ID="go pick screw" />-->
        <Fallback name="go pick screw">

            <Sequence name="go pick screw">
                <RetryUntilSuccesful num_attempts="3" name="">
                    <Action ID="move to" place="screw station" name="move to screw station" />
                </RetryUntilSuccesful>

                <RetryUntilSuccesful num_attempts="3" name="">
                    <Sequence name="">
                        <Sequence name="detect screw">
                            <Action ID="lift torso" name="" />
                            <Action ID="look at table" name="" />
                            <Action ID="detect screw" output="{object pose}" name="" />
                        </Sequence>
                        <Fallback name="pick screw">
                            <Sequence name="pick screw">
                                <Action ID="convert to grasp pose" name="" from="{object pose}" output="{grasp pose}" />
                                <Action ID="scan scene" name="scan scene for collision avoidance" />
                                <!--                                <Action ID="attach object" object_name="screw" name="" />-->
                                <Action ID="look at" name="look at screw" pose="{object pose}" />
                                <Action ID="open gripper" name="close griper slightly" position="0.035" />
                                <!--                                <Action ID="reach" name="approach for grasp pose" pose="{grasp pose}" shift_z="0.2" />-->
                                <Action ID="reach" name="approach for grasp pose" pose="{grasp pose}" shift_z="0.1" />
                                <Action ID="allow partial collision" where="{object pose}" width="0.06" padding="0.03" />
                                <Action ID="reach" name="reach for grasp pose" pose="{grasp pose}" shift_z="-0.01" />
                                <Action ID="close gripper" name="grasp screw" />
                                <Condition ID="check grasped" name="check if grasped" />
                                <Action ID="reach" name="back to approach pose" pose="{grasp pose}" shift_z="0.1" />
                                <!--                                <Action ID="reach" name="back to approach pose" pose="{grasp pose}" shift_z="0.2" />-->
                                <Action ID="retreat arm" name="" />
                            </Sequence>

                            <ForceFailure name="reset arm">
                                <Action ID="retreat arm" name="" />
                            </ForceFailure>
                        </Fallback>
                    </Sequence>
                </RetryUntilSuccesful>
            </Sequence>

            <Action ID="ask" name="" what="unable pick screw" />
        </Fallback>
    </BehaviorTree>


    <BehaviorTree ID="go place screw">
        <!--        <Action name="go place first screw" ID="go place screw" />-->
        <Fallback name="go place screw" >

            <Sequence name="go place screw">
                <RetryUntilSuccesful num_attempts="3" name="">
                    <Action ID="move to" place="caddy station" name="move to caddy station" />
                </RetryUntilSuccesful>

                <RetryUntilSuccesful num_attempts="3" name="">
                    <Sequence name="">
                        <Sequence name="detect screw compartment">
                            <Action ID="lift torso" name="" />
                            <Action ID="look at table" name=""/>
                            <Action ID="detect caddy" name="detect screw compartment" part="screw compartment" output="{object pose}" />
                        </Sequence>

                        <Fallback name="place screw into caddy">
                            <Sequence name="place screw into caddy">
                                <Action ID="convert to grasp pose" name="convert to place pose" from="{object pose}" shift_z="0.06" output="{place pose}" />
                                <Action ID="scan scene" name="scan scene for collision avoidance" />
                                <!--                                <Action ID="attach object" object_name="screw" name="" />-->
                                <Action ID="look at" name="look at screw compartment" pose="{object pose}" />
                                <Action ID="reach" name="approach for place pose" pose="{place pose}" shift_z="0.1" />
                                <Action ID="reach" name="reach for place pose" pose="{place pose}" />
                                <Action ID="open gripper" name="release screw" />
                                <Action ID="reach" name="back to approach pose" pose="{place pose}" shift_z="0.1"/>
                                <Action ID="retreat arm" name="" />
                            </Sequence>
                            <ForceFailure name="reset arm">
                                <Action ID="retreat arm" name="" />
                            </ForceFailure>
                        </Fallback>
                    </Sequence>
                </RetryUntilSuccesful>
            </Sequence>

            <Action ID="ask" what="unable to place screw" name="" />
        </Fallback>
    </BehaviorTree>
</root>
mrq:

<root main_tree_to_execute="MainTree">
    <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
        <ReactiveSequence>
            <SetBlackboard output_key="the_answer1" value="hello"/>
            <SetBlackboard output_key="the_answer2" value="hello"/>
            <SetBlackboard output_key="the_answer3" value="hello"/>
            <Fibonacci server_name="/fibonacci" server_timeout="10" cancel_timeout="5" />
            <AlwaysSuccess/>
        </ReactiveSequence>
    </BehaviorTree>
</root>
mrq: The mission describes a reactive workflow that writes three blackboard values, runs a Fibonacci computation, and enforces a successful outcome under continuous re-evaluation. First, the robot will execute the following actions (SetBlackboard, SetBlackboard, SetBlackboard, Fibonacci, AlwaysSuccess) in sequence. If any action fails, it will stop execution. While any action is still running, it will re-evaluate previous actions. It will execute the custom action SetBlackboard to write the key the_answer1 with the value hello. It will execute the custom action SetBlackboard to write the key the_answer2 with the value hello after the first write is confirmed. It will execute the custom action SetBlackboard to write the key the_answer3 with the value hello after the second write is confirmed. It will run the ROS action Fibonacci using the server named /fibonacci with a server timeout of 10 seconds and a cancel timeout of 5 seconds after all three writes succeed. It will execute the custom action AlwaysSuccess and make sure it always succeeds after the Fibonacci action completes or while it is running, with prior steps being re-checked due to the reactive sequence behavior.
