<root main_tree_to_execute="MainTree">
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="DoorClosed">
        <Sequence name="door_closed_sequence">
            <Inverter>
                <Condition ID="IsDoorOpen"/>
            </Inverter>
            <RetryUntilSuccesful num_attempts="4">
                <Action ID="OpenDoor"/>
            </RetryUntilSuccesful>
            <Action ID="PassThroughDoor"/>
        </Sequence>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
        <Sequence>
            <Fallback name="root_Fallback">
                <Sequence name="door_open_sequence">
                    <Condition ID="IsDoorOpen"/>
                    <Action ID="PassThroughDoor"/>
                </Sequence>
                <SubTree ID="DoorClosed"/>
                <Action ID="PassThroughWindow"/>
            </Fallback>
            <Action ID="CloseDoor"/>
        </Sequence>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <TreeNodesModel>
        <Action ID="CloseDoor"/>
        <SubTree ID="DoorClosed"/>
        <Condition ID="IsDoorOpen"/>
        <Action ID="OpenDoor"/>
        <Action ID="PassThroughDoor"/>
        <Action ID="PassThroughWindow"/>
    </TreeNodesModel>
    <!-- ////////// -->
</root>
mrq:
The mission describes a door traversal task that first tries to pass through an open door, then attempts to open a closed door, and finally resorts to a window if needed, before closing the door at the end.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (IsDoorOpen, PassThroughDoor) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for the condition IsDoorOpen. It will run the custom action PassThroughDoor after the door-open condition succeeds.

Next, it will execute the following actions (Inverter(IsDoorOpen), RetryUntilSuccesful(OpenDoor), PassThroughDoor) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute inverted IsDoorOpen. If IsDoorOpen succeeds, it will reverse it to fail. Else if IsDoorOpen fails, it will reverse it to succeed. It will keep trying to execute the custom action OpenDoor until it succeeds. It will run the custom action PassThroughDoor after the door is confirmed open.

Next, it will execute the following actions (door_open_sequence, DoorClosed, PassThroughWindow) consecutively. If any action (door_open_sequence) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will run the custom action PassThroughWindow if both prior options fail.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (root_Fallback, CloseDoor) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action CloseDoor after the selected path through the fallback succeeds.

<root main_tree_to_execute="MainTree">
  <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
    <Sequence name="root">
      <FallbackStar name="check_motion">
        <Inverter name="is_not_stuck">
          <IsStuck/>
        </Inverter>
        <SequenceStar name="stuck_recovery">
          <Stop/>
          <BackUp/>
          <Spin/>
        </SequenceStar>
      </FallbackStar>
      <SequenceStar name="navigate">
        <ComputePathToPose endpoints="${endpoints}" path="${path}"/>
        <ParallelNode threshold="1">
          <FollowPath path="${path}"/>
          <Sequence>
            <RateController hz="2">
              <ComputePathToPose endpoints="${endpoints}" path="${path}"/>
            </RateController>
            <UpdatePath/>
          </Sequence>
        </ParallelNode>
      </SequenceStar>
    </Sequence>
  </BehaviorTree>
</root>
mrq: The mission describes a mobile navigation task that first ensures the robot is not stuck, attempts recovery if needed, and then performs dynamic path planning and execution. First, the robot will execute the following actions (Inverter(IsStuck), SequenceWithMemory(Stop, BackUp, Spin)) consecutively. It will execute inverted IsStuck and, if IsStuck succeeds, reverse it to fail; else if IsStuck fails, reverse it to succeed. It will execute the following actions (Stop, BackUp, Spin) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. Next, it will execute the following actions (ComputePathToPose, Parallel(FollowPath, Sequence(RateController(ComputePathToPose), UpdatePath))) in sequence. It will run the ROS action ComputePathToPose with the provided endpoints and will store the resulting path. It will execute the following actions [FollowPath, Sequence(RateController(ComputePathToPose), UpdatePath)] simultaneously. If either a success threshold is reached or all actions succeed, the execution was successful; else if any action fails, the execution fails. It will run the ROS action FollowPath using the stored path. It will execute the following actions (RateController(ComputePathToPose), UpdatePath) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. While an action is running or has succeeded, it will go to the next action. It will run RateController at 2 Hz to invoke the ROS action ComputePathToPose with the provided endpoints and will update the stored path. It will run the custom action UpdatePath to apply the newly computed path to the ongoing navigation.

  <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
    <PipelineSequence name="NavigateWithReplanning">
      <DistanceController distance="1.0">
        <ComputePathToPose goal="{goal}" path="{path}" planner_id="GridBased"/>
      </DistanceController>
      <FollowPath path="{path}"  controller_id="FollowPath"/>
    </PipelineSequence>
  </BehaviorTree>
</root>
mrq: The mission describes a navigation task that continuously recomputes a global path every meter and advances along that path in order. First, the robot will execute the following actions (DistanceController, FollowPath) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will, while an action is running or has succeeded, go to the next action. It will run the custom action DistanceController that wraps the ROS action ComputePathToPose and will trigger a path recomputation when it has moved 1.0 meters. It will run the ROS action ComputePathToPose with the provided goal and will store the resulting path when the distance condition is met. It will run the ROS action FollowPath with the stored path using the specified controller and will continue advancing even while path recomputation is running.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (Wait, MoveAhead, Turn, MoveAhead, Turn, MoveAhead, Turn, MoveAhead, Turn, MoveAhead, MoveAhead, MoveAhead) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute the custom action Wait with a delay of 5000 milliseconds before any motion begins. It will execute the custom action MoveAhead to advance 100 units forward after the wait completes. It will execute the custom action Turn to rotate 90 degrees clockwise after the initial advance. It will execute the custom action MoveAhead to advance 500 units forward after the first turn. It will execute the custom action Turn to rotate 90 degrees counterclockwise after the 500-unit advance. It will execute the custom action MoveAhead to advance 600 units forward after the second turn. It will execute the custom action Turn to rotate 90 degrees counterclockwise after the 600-unit advance. It will execute the custom action MoveAhead to advance 200 units forward after the third turn. It will execute the custom action Turn to rotate 90 degrees counterclockwise after the 200-unit advance. It will execute the custom action MoveAhead to advance 600 units forward after the fourth turn. It will execute the custom action MoveAhead to translate backward 600 units using a distance of -600 after the preceding forward advance. It will execute the custom action MoveAhead to advance 600 units forward after the backward translation completes.

<!--
    This Behavior Tree replans the global path periodically at 1 Hz and it also has
    recovery actions.
-->

<root main_tree_to_execute="ElevatorTree">
  <BehaviorTree ID="ElevatorTree">
    <RestoreConfig node_name="/footprint_publisher">
      <RestoreConfig node_name="/global_costmap/global_costmap">
	<RestoreConfig node_name="/controller_server">
	  <Sequence>
	    <ChangeParam node_name="/controller_server"
			 param_name="FollowPath.max_vel_x"
			 param_value="0.5"  name="ChangeParamSpeedDown"/>
	    
	    <ChangeParam node_name="/global_costmap/global_costmap"
			 param_name="people_obstacle_layer.people_enabled"
			 param_value="false"  name="DisablePeopleObstacle"/>

	    <SetBlackboard value="true" output_key="first_time" />

	    <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="99999999" name="NavigateRecovery">
	      <PipelineSequence name="NavigateWithReplanning">
		<!-- Not using RateController, so that the robot can keep trying to find the initial/updated path -->
		<RecoveryNode number_of_retries="99999999" name="UpdatePathRecovery">
		  <Sequence>
		    <WaitFloat wait_duration_float="0.5"/>
		    <ComputePathToPose goal="{goal}" path="{path}" planner_id="GridBased" />
		    <BlackboardCheckString value_A="{first_time}" value_B="true">
		      <Sequence>
			<PublishTopic topic="/cabot/event" value="navigation;event;elevator_door_may_be_ready"/>
			<SetBlackboard value="false" output_key="first_time" />
		      </Sequence>
		    </BlackboardCheckString>
		  </Sequence>
		  <AlwaysSuccess/>
		</RecoveryNode>
		<RecoveryNode number_of_retries="1" name="FollowPathRecovery">
		  <FollowPath path="{path}" controller_id="FollowPath" />
		  <AlwaysSuccess/>
		</RecoveryNode>
	      </PipelineSequence>
	      <RecoveryNode>
		<SequenceStar name="RecoveryActions">
		  <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearGlobalCostmap-context" server_timeout="5000"
                                      service_name="/global_costmap/clear_entirely_global_costmap"/>
		  <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearLocalCostmap-Context" server_timeout="5000"
				      service_name="/local_costmap/clear_entirely_local_costmap"/>
		  <Wait wait_duration="1.0"/>		  
		</SequenceStar>
		<AlwaysSuccess/>
	      </RecoveryNode>
	    </RecoveryNode>
	  </Sequence>
	</RestoreConfig>
      </RestoreConfig>
    </RestoreConfig>
  </BehaviorTree>
</root>
mrq:
The mission describes configuring navigation parameters, planning a path with periodic replanning, following the path with recovery behaviors, and restoring system configurations for an elevator navigation scenario.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (ChangeParam, ChangeParam, SetBlackboard, RecoveryNode) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action ChangeParam on /controller_server to set FollowPath.max_vel_x to 0.5. It will run the custom action ChangeParam on /global_costmap/global_costmap to set people_obstacle_layer.people_enabled to false. It will run the custom action SetBlackboard to store the value true under the key first_time. It will execute main PipelineSequence and, if it fails, it will execute recovery action RecoveryNode and then retry the main action until retries are exhausted.

Next, it will execute the following actions (RecoveryNode, AlwaysSuccess) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. While an action is running or has succeeded, it will go to the next action. It will execute main Sequence and, if it fails, it will execute recovery action AlwaysSuccess and then retry the main action until retries are exhausted.

Next, it will execute the following actions (WaitFloat, ComputePathToPose, BlackboardCheckString) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action WaitFloat to wait for 0.5 seconds. It will run the ROS action ComputePathToPose with the goal provided and will store the resulting path. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule using the values first_time and true. It will execute the following actions (PublishTopic, SetBlackboard) in order while checking a condition for each action. If condition ID is true, it will move to the next action. Else if condition ID is false, it will end the sequence.

Next, it will execute delayed AlwaysSuccess and make sure it always succeeds.

Next, it will execute main FollowPath and, if it fails, it will execute recovery action AlwaysSuccess and then retry the main action until retries are exhausted. It will run the ROS action FollowPath using the stored path with the specified controller.

Next, it will execute main SequenceStar and, if it fails, it will execute recovery action AlwaysSuccess and then retry the main action until retries are exhausted.

Next, it will execute the following actions (ClearEntireCostmap, ClearEntireCostmap, Wait) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will run the custom action ClearEntireCostmap to clear the global costmap using the provided service and timeout. It will run the custom action ClearEntireCostmap to clear the local costmap using the provided service and timeout. It will run the custom action Wait to pause for 1.0 seconds.

Finally, it will read blackboard value and compare it against a set of cases. If a case matches the blackboard value, it will execute corresponding PublishTopic. Else if no case matches, it will execute default SetBlackboard. It will run the custom action PublishTopic to send the message navigation;event;elevator_door_may_be_ready on /cabot/event when first_time equals true. It will run the custom action SetBlackboard to store the value false under the key first_time after the publish succeeds.

<root main_tree_to_execute = "MainTree" >
    <!-- This behavior tree lets mobipick fetch the tool, offer it to a person, stow it away if needed,
        and finally drive back home. -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
        <SequenceStar name="mobipick_pick_n_place">
            <InitNode name="Init"/>
            <!-- The get_tool_fallback lets mobipick fetch the tool from the table if it is not holding any objects already. -->
            <Fallback name="get_tool_fallback">
                <HasAttachedObjects name="HasAttachedObjects"/>
                <SequenceStar name="fetch_tool_sequence">
                    <DriveToNode name="DriveTo" goal="pick"/>
                    <MoveItNode name="MoveIt" function="CaptureObject"/>
                    <MoveItNode name="MoveIt" function="PickUpObject"/>
                </SequenceStar>
            </Fallback>
            <DriveToNode name="DriveTo" goal="handover"/>
            <MoveItNode name="MoveIt" function="MoveArmToHandover"/>
            <UserInteractionNode name="UserInteraction"/>
            <!-- The clear_gripper_fallback lets mobipick place any object it is holding onto the table. -->
            <Fallback name="clear_gripper_fallback">
                <Inverter>
                    <HasAttachedObjects name="HasAttachedObjects"/>
                </Inverter>
                <SequenceStar name="stash_tool_sequence">
                    <DriveToNode name="DriveTo" goal="place"/>
                    <MoveItNode name="MoveIt" function="PlaceObject"/>
                </SequenceStar>
            </Fallback>
            <DriveToNode name="DriveTo" goal="home"/>
            <DoneNode name="Done"/>
        </SequenceStar>
    </BehaviorTree>
</root>
mrq:
The mission describes a pick, handover, stow, and return-home task that runs in sequence with memory across all major steps. First, the robot will execute the following actions (InitNode, get_tool_fallback, DriveToNode, MoveItNode, UserInteractionNode, clear_gripper_fallback, DriveToNode, DoneNode) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will run the custom action InitNode to initialize systems and parameters.

Next, it will execute the following actions (HasAttachedObjects, fetch_tool_sequence) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution will fail. It will run the custom action HasAttachedObjects to check whether an object is already attached. It will execute the following actions (DriveToNode, MoveItNode, MoveItNode) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will run the custom action DriveToNode with the goal set to pick. It will run the custom action MoveItNode with the function set to CaptureObject. It will run the custom action MoveItNode with the function set to PickUpObject.

Next, it will execute the custom action DriveToNode with the goal set to handover. It will execute the custom action MoveItNode with the function set to MoveArmToHandover. It will execute the custom action UserInteractionNode to conduct the handover interaction.

Next, it will execute the following actions (Inverter(HasAttachedObjects), stash_tool_sequence) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution will fail. It will execute inverted HasAttachedObjects. If HasAttachedObjects succeeds, it will reverse it to fail. Else if HasAttachedObjects fails, it will reverse it to succeed. It will execute the following actions (DriveToNode, MoveItNode) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will run the custom action DriveToNode with the goal set to place. It will run the custom action MoveItNode with the function set to PlaceObject.

Finally, it will execute the custom action DriveToNode with the goal set to home. It will execute the custom action DoneNode to mark the mission complete.

If any action (ReachRoom child) fails, it will move to the next action until one succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will execute the following actions (ShouldFollow) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule. It will execute the following actions (GoToRoom_Fal) consecutively. If any action (GoToRoom_Fal child) fails, it will move to the next action until one succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule. It will run the custom action GoToRoom if the previous room checks do not succeed. It will run the custom action FollowHuman if the room reaching attempt does not succeed. Next, it will execute the following actions (BottleFound) consecutively. If any action (BottleFound child) fails, it will move to the next action until one succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule. It will run the custom action FindBottle if the bottle is not found. Next, it will execute the following actions (Fetch) consecutively. If any action (Fetch child) fails, it will move to the next action until one succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule. It will execute the following actions (Grasp) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute the following actions (InvPoseCompute) consecutively. If any action (InvPoseCompute child) fails, it will move to the next action until one succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will execute the following actions (SeqInvPose) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule. It will find another value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule. It will run the custom action ComputeInvPose if inverse pose is not both computed and valid. It will execute the following actions (GoInvPose) consecutively. If any action (GoInvPose child) fails, it will move to the next action until one succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule. It will execute the following actions (prepare) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action KeepArmsForGrasp. It will run the custom action GoToInvPose. It will execute the following actions (Locate) consecutively. If any action (Locate child) fails, it will move to the next action until one succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule. It will run the custom action LocateBottle if the bottle is not located. It will run the custom action GraspBottle after successful localization and approach. Finally, it will run the custom action SetInvPoseInvaild if the fetch fallback chooses recovery after grasp. It will run the custom action KeepRunning if additional recovery or continuation is required. Finally, it will execute the following actions (Main) consecutively. If any action (Main child) fails, it will move to the next action until one succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will run the custom action AskForHelp if the autonomous sequence does not succeed.

<root main_tree_to_execute = "ArmMovementWithRequest" >

        <Sequence>
            <FloatParameterRequest title="Bitte geben Sie x, y und z in [mm] an"
                                   value="{x}" key="x" min="-1000" max="1000"/>
            <FloatParameterRequest value="{y}" key="y" min="-1000" max="1000"/>
            <FloatParameterRequest value="{z}" key="z" min="-1000" max="1000"/>

It will run the custom action FloatParameterRequest to request the x coordinate in millimeters and will store the value in the blackboard key x within the range of -1000 to 1000. It will run the custom action FloatParameterRequest to request the y coordinate in millimeters and will store the value in the blackboard key y within the range of -1000 to 1000. It will run the custom action FloatParameterRequest to request the z coordinate in millimeters and will store the value in the blackboard key z within the range of -1000 to 1000. It will run the custom action FloatParameterRequest to request the gripper position from 0 to 1 and will store the value in the blackboard key gripper_position within the range of 0 to 1. mrq:
The mission describes a social navigation task that repeats a structured engagement routine up to one hundred times and logs interaction data.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (Repeat) for a set number of times or infinitely. It will execute Social Interaction Routine for a set number of times or infinitely.

Next, it will execute the following actions (Chrono, SocialActionFallback, NavigateToWp, TurnAgent, WriteCSV) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action Chrono before attempting any social navigation actions. It will execute the following actions (isGoalReached, social_nav2_action) in order. If social_nav2_action succeeds, stop execution. Else keep executing and re-evaluating previous actions whenever an action is running. It will run the custom action isGoalReached to check whether the current social goal has been reached. It will run the ROS action SocialNav2Action with goal set to hri and with agent_id set to agent_3 if the social goal has not been reached. It will run the custom action NavigateToWp with the goal set to home after the social navigation attempt completes. It will run the custom action TurnAgent after reaching home. It will run the custom action WriteCSV to record the interaction data after the agent has turned.

<root main_tree_to_execute="MainTree">
  <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
    <PipelineSequence name="NavigateWithReplanning">
      <RateController hz="2.0">
        <Sequence>
          <GoalUpdater input_goal="{goal}" output_goal="{updated_goal}">
            <ComputePathToPose goal="{updated_goal}" path="{path}" planner_id="GridBased"/>
          </GoalUpdater>
        </Sequence>
      </RateController>
      <ReactiveFallback name="test">
        <GoalUpdated/>
        <FollowPath path="{path}" controller_id="FollowPath"/>
      </ReactiveFallback>
    </PipelineSequence>
  </BehaviorTree>
</root>
mrq:
The mission describes a navigation task that continuously updates a moving goal, recomputes a path at 2 Hz, and follows the updated path with reactive replanning.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (RateController, ReactiveFallback) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. While an action is running or has succeeded, it will go to the next action.

Next, it will execute delayed RateController. If execution succeeds, it will start a 2.0 Hz pacing and will resume execution on each tick. It will execute the following actions (Sequence) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. While an action is running or has succeeded, it will go to the next action.

Next, it will execute the following actions (GoalUpdater) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action GoalUpdater, which will take the current goal and produce an updated_goal for downstream use. It will execute the following actions (ComputePathToPose) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the ROS action ComputePathToPose with the updated_goal and will store the resulting path.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (GoalUpdated, FollowPath) in order. If an action succeeds, it will stop execution. Else it will keep executing and re-evaluating previous actions whenever an action is running. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for GoalUpdated before proceeding. It will run the ROS action FollowPath using the stored path with the specified controller while continuously restarting evaluation from the first action when FollowPath is running.

<root main_tree_to_execute="MainTree">
  <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
    <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="6" name="NavigateRecovery">
      <PipelineSequence name="NavigateWithReplanning">
        <RateController hz="1.0">
          <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="1" name="ComputePathToPose">
            <ComputePathToPose goal="{goal}" path="{path}" planner_id="GridBased"/>
            <ClearEntireCostmap service_name="global_costmap/clear_entirely_global_costmap"/>
          </RecoveryNode>
        </RateController>
        <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="1" name="FollowPath">
          <FollowPath path="{path}" controller_id="FollowPath"/>
          <ClearEntireCostmap service_name="local_costmap/clear_entirely_local_costmap"/>
        </RecoveryNode>
      </PipelineSequence>
      <SequenceStar name="RecoveryActions">
        <ClearEntireCostmap service_name="local_costmap/clear_entirely_local_costmap"/>
        <ClearEntireCostmap service_name="global_costmap/clear_entirely_global_costmap"/>
        <Wait wait_duration="2"/>
      </SequenceStar>
    </RecoveryNode>
  </BehaviorTree>
</root>
mrq:
The mission describes a navigation task that computes a global path at 1 Hz and follows it with layered recovery behaviors.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (NavigateWithReplanning, RecoveryActions) with recovery semantics. It will execute main action NavigateWithReplanning. If it fails, it will execute recovery action RecoveryActions and then retry the main action until six retries are exhausted.

Next, it will execute the following actions (RateController, FollowPath) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. While an action is running or has succeeded, it will go to the next action.

Next, it will execute the action RateController that limits path computation to 1.0 Hz. It will ensure that the nested recovery behavior runs at the specified rate.

Next, it will execute main action ComputePathToPose. If it fails, it will execute recovery action ClearEntireCostmap on the global costmap and then retry the main action until one retry is exhausted.

Next, it will run the ROS action ComputePathToPose with the goal provided and will store the resulting path using the GridBased planner.

Next, it will execute the following actions (FollowPath, ClearEntireCostmap) with recovery semantics. It will execute main action FollowPath. If it fails, it will execute recovery action ClearEntireCostmap on the local costmap and then retry the main action until one retry is exhausted.

Next, it will run the ROS action FollowPath using the stored path with the specified controller after a path is available.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (ClearEntireCostmap, ClearEntireCostmap, Wait) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will execute ClearEntireCostmap on the local costmap. It will execute ClearEntireCostmap on the global costmap. It will run the custom action Wait for 2 seconds.

If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for IsOnPasture. Else if IsInShed fails, it will reverse it to succeed. It will run the custom action FindShed to locate the shed. It will run the custom action Move to approach the shed. It will run the custom action EnterShed to go inside the shed.

mrq:
The mission describes a gnome trader’s autonomous routine that prioritizes physiological needs and job duties using fallback and sequence policies.

First, the Hunger subtree will execute the following actions (SequenceWithMemory of a FallbackStar for sourcing food, Move, PickUpItem, ForceSuccess wrapping a SequenceWithMemory to find and reach dining, Eat) in sequence. It will execute the following actions (IsHungry condition check and FindFood) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails, while a FallbackStar will execute the following actions (that same sequence) consecutively and, if no action succeeds, start all over again until an action succeeds. It will execute the ROS action Move after a food source is identified. It will execute the ROS action PickUpItem to acquire the food item. It will execute the following actions (FindDining, Move) in sequence and make sure it always succeeds. It will execute the ROS action Eat to consume the food.

Next, the Thirst subtree will execute the following actions (SequenceWithMemory of a FallbackStar for sourcing drink, Move, PickUpItem, ForceSuccess wrapping a SequenceWithMemory to find and reach dining, Drink) in sequence. It will execute the following actions (IsThirsty condition check and FindDrink) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails, while a FallbackStar will execute the following actions (that same sequence) consecutively and, if no action succeeds, start all over again until an action succeeds. It will execute the ROS action Move after a drink source is identified. It will execute the ROS action PickUpItem to acquire the drink item. It will execute the following actions (FindDining, Move) in sequence and make sure it always succeeds. It will execute the ROS action Drink to consume the drink.

Next, the Sleepy subtree will execute the following actions (SequenceWithMemory of IsSleepy, ForceSuccess wrapping a SequenceWithMemory to find and reach a bed, Sleep) in sequence. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for the IsSleepy condition. It will execute the following actions (FindBed, Move) in sequence and make sure it always succeeds. It will execute the ROS action Sleep to rest.

Next, the Jobs subtree will execute the following actions (SequenceWithMemory Leave, SequenceWithMemory WorkAtMarketStall) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds, and if no action succeeds, it will start all over again until an action succeeds. It will execute the following actions (IsTimeToLeave, GetExitPosition, Move, LeaveMap) in sequence and will remember which ones have already succeeded. It will execute the following actions (GetMarketStallPosition, Move, Trade) in sequence and will remember which ones have already succeeded.

Finally, the MainTree subtree will execute the following actions (FallbackStar Needs, SubTree Jobs) consecutively and, if no action succeeds, start all over again until an action succeeds. The robot will execute the following actions (SubTree Hunger, SubTree Thirst, SubTree Sleepy) consecutively inside Needs and will stop when one succeeds, else it will retry from the beginning until one succeeds. It will execute the SubTree Jobs if the Needs subtree does not succeed on a given evaluation cycle.

The mission describes a pursuit-and-engagement task that acquires a target, moves toward it, and attacks when adjacent, with a fallback to random movement if the main pursuit fails. First, the robot will execute the following actions (GetTarget, Move, Sequence(TargetAdjacent, AttackTarget)) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will run the custom action GetTarget to select a target to pursue. It will run the custom action Move to advance toward the selected target after the target acquisition succeeds. It will execute the following actions (TargetAdjacent, AttackTarget) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for the condition TargetAdjacent. It will run the custom action AttackTarget after the adjacency condition succeeds.

Next, it will execute the following actions (SequenceStar(Main), RandomMove) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution will fail. It will execute the custom action RandomMove to explore the environment when the main pursuit and engagement sequence fails.

The mission describes a road-driving task that manages waiting, initialization, global following, local maneuvers, stopline handling, and final path setting.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (Condition, Action) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for checkMissionWait. It will run the custom action stop if the mission-wait condition succeeds.

Next, it will execute the following actions (Action, SubTree, Fallback, Action) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action init to initialize its systems. It will read blackboard value and compare it against a set of cases. If a case matches the blackboard value, execute corresponding Action. Else if no case matches, execute default Action for the SubTree treeGenGlobalFollowPath. It will execute the following actions (Sequence, Sequence, Action) consecutively. If any action (Action) fails, move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails for the mission_start. It will run the custom action setPath after the preceding steps succeed.

Next, it will execute the following actions (Condition, Fallback) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for checkFrontObject. It will execute the following actions (Sequence, Fallback) consecutively. If any action (Action) fails, move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will execute the following actions (Sequence) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for checkIsGlobalFollow. It will execute the following actions (Sequence, Sequence, Sequence) consecutively. If any action (Action) fails, move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails for loc_check. It will execute the following actions (Condition, Action) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails for checkNearCorner then acc. It will execute the following actions (Condition, Action) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails for checkNearStopline then acc. It will execute the following actions (Condition, Action) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails for checkProfitKeeplane then acc.

Next, it will execute the following actions (Sequence, Sequence) consecutively. If any action (Action) fails, move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails for the lane_check. It will execute the following actions (Condition, Fallback) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails for checkLaneChange then the nested lane_check. It will execute the following actions (Sequence, Fallback) consecutively. If any action (Action) fails, move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will execute the following actions (Condition, Fallback) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails for checkLeftchageAble then the left-first choice. It will execute the following actions (Sequence, Action) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails for checkCollision then keepLane. It will run the custom action laneChangeLeft if keeping lane is not selected. It will execute the following actions (Sequence, Action) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails for checkCollision then keepLane as the right-first choice. It will run the custom action laneChangeRight if keeping lane is not selected.

Next, it will execute the following actions (Sequence) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails for the Local_path. It will execute the following actions (Condition, Sequence) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails for checkEmptyLocal then Gen_Local. It will execute the following actions (Action, Action, Action) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action stop before generating a local path. It will run the custom action genLocalPath to create a local path. It will run the custom action setStartLocal to mark the start of the local path. It will execute the following actions (Sequence, Sequence, Action) consecutively. If any action (Action) fails, move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails for local_check. It will execute the following actions (Condition, Action) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails for checkFinishLocal then setFinishLocal. It will execute the following actions (Condition, Action) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails for checkCollision then stop. It will run the custom action localPathfollow when neither finishing nor collision conditions trigger.

Next, it will execute the following actions (Condition, Fallback) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for checkNearStopline. It will execute the following actions (Sequence, Action) consecutively. If any action (Action) fails, move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will execute the following actions (Condition, Sequence) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails for checkPassAble then the pass sequence. It will execute the following actions (Sequence, Action) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute the following actions (Condition, Action) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails for checkCollision then stop. It will run the custom action drive after the collision check sequence succeeds. It will run the custom action stop if passing is not allowed.

Finally, it will run the custom action drive when no prior branch in the mission_start succeeds.

The mission describes a global path generation and following task with lane-change logic. First, it will execute the following actions (Condition, Fallback) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for checkGlobalLaneChange. It will execute the following actions (Sequence, Fallback) consecutively. If any action (Action) fails, move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will execute the following actions (Condition, Fallback) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails for checkLetfChange then the left-first choice. It will execute the following actions (Sequence, Action) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails for checkCollision then keepLane. It will run the custom action laneChangeLeft if keeping lane is not selected. It will execute the following actions (Sequence, Action) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails for checkCollision then keepLane as the right-first choice. It will run the custom action laneChangeRight if keeping lane is not selected. Finally, it will run the custom action genGlobalFollowPath if no global lane change path is selected.

<root main_tree_to_execute="MainTree">
  <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
      <Sequence name="root_sequence">
          <InitializeNavigation timeout_s="{timeout_s}"/>
          <!-- Set this value at task_monitor! -->
          <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="100" name="FindElementRecovery">
              <ElementFound/>
              <Sequence name="navigate_and_spin_sequence">
                  <GetNextPose goal_poses="{goal_poses}" new_position_goal="{goal_position}" new_orientation_goal="{goal_orientation}"/>
                  <NavigateToPose position="{goal_position}" orientation="{goal_orientation}"/>
                  <Repeat num_cycles="{spin_cycles}">
                      <Sequence name="spin_and_wait">
                          <Spin spin_dist="{spin_angle}"/>
                          <Wait wait_duration="{wait_time}"/>
                      </Sequence>
                  </Repeat>
              </Sequence> 
            <!-- Insert next sequence once the element is found -->
          </RecoveryNode>
      </Sequence>
  </BehaviorTree>
</root>
mrq:
The mission describes an initialization and search task that prepares navigation, repeatedly attempts to detect a target element, and applies a recovery routine that navigates to successive poses and scans if the element is not detected.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (InitializeNavigation, FindElementRecovery) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action InitializeNavigation with a specified timeout and will proceed only if initialization completes successfully.

Next, it will execute main [Action]. If it fails, execute recovery action (navigate_and_spin_sequence). After recovery, retry main action (ElementFound). If recovery action (navigate_and_spin_sequence) fails and retries are exhausted, stop execution. It will run the custom action ElementFound to check whether the target element is detected and will succeed only if the element is found.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (GetNextPose, NavigateToPose, Repeat) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. While an action is running or has succeeded, go to the next action. It will run the custom action GetNextPose to compute the next goal position and orientation from the provided list of goal poses and will publish a single goal for navigation. It will run the ROS action NavigateToPose using the computed position and orientation and will report success only when the navigation reaches the goal. It will execute the child action continuously until it fails or succeeds and else keep executing the child action forever when repetition has no terminating status. It will execute the following actions (Spin, Wait) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails for each repetition cycle. It will run the custom action Spin by rotating the specified angle to scan the surroundings and will then run the custom action Wait for the specified duration to allow sensing during a pause.

<root main_tree_to_execute="MainTree">
  <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
    <PipelineSequence name="NavigateWithReplanning">
      <RateController hz="1.0">
        <Sequence>
          <GoalUpdater input_goal="{goal}" output_goal="{updated_goal}">
            <ComputePathToPose goal="{updated_goal}" path="{path}" planner_id="GridBased"/>
          </GoalUpdater>
         <TruncatePath distance="1.0" input_path="{path}" output_path="{truncated_path}"/>
        </Sequence>
      </RateController>
      <KeepRunningUntilFailure>
        <FollowPath path="{truncated_path}" controller_id="FollowPath"/>
      </KeepRunningUntilFailure>
    </PipelineSequence>
  </BehaviorTree>
</root>
mrq:
The mission describes a navigation task that continuously updates a goal pose, replans a path at a fixed rate, truncates the path to maintain a following distance, and follows the truncated path while it remains valid.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (RateController, KeepRunningUntilFailure) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. While an action is running or has succeeded, it will go to the next action. It will use the custom action RateController to tick the subsequent planning sequence at 1.0 Hz. It will then execute the custom action KeepRunningUntilFailure that wraps path following, which will keep executing the follow action as long as it does not fail.

Next, it will execute the following actions (GoalUpdater, TruncatePath) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. While an action is running or has succeeded, it will go to the next action. It will run the custom action GoalUpdater to consume the current goal and produce an updated_goal for planning. It will then run the ROS action ComputePathToPose within GoalUpdater to compute a path to the updated_goal and store it in path when planning succeeds. It will run the custom action TruncatePath to shorten path to maintain a 1.0 distance and store the result in truncated_path.

Finally, it will execute FollowPath. It will execute FollowPath and, if FollowPath succeeds, restart it. Else if FollowPath fails, it will stop looping and continue execution. It will run the ROS action FollowPath using truncated_path with the specified controller.

<root main_tree_to_execute = "MainTree" >
    <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
        <Sequence name="root_sequence">
            <!-- Compute the route to desired goal pose. This will be the high-level mission planner which doesn't 
            consider any vehicle constraints or collison information. Later change "trajectory" to "route" -->
            <ComputeRoute goal_pose="{goal_pose}" route="{route}" server_name="/route_planner/plan_route"/>
            <!-- <ComputePlan route="{route}" path="{path}" server_name="/path_planner/plan_path"/> -->
            <ReactiveSequence>
                <RateController hz="10.0">
                    <ComputeTrajectory route="{route}" trajectory="{trajectory}" server_name="/trajectory_planner/plan_trajectory"/>
                </RateController>
                <FollowTrajectory trajectory="{trajectory}" server_name="/path_tracking_controller/follow_trajectory"/>
            </ReactiveSequence>
        </Sequence>
    </BehaviorTree>
</root>
mrq:
The mission describes a route planning and trajectory execution task that first computes a route to a goal pose, then continuously plans trajectories from that route while tracking them.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (ComputeRoute, ReactiveSequence) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the ROS action ComputeRoute with the provided goal pose and will store the resulting route. It will then proceed to run the ReactiveSequence after the route computation succeeds.

Next, it will execute the following actions [RateController, FollowTrajectory] in sequence. If FollowTrajectory fails, it will stop execution. While FollowTrajectory is still running, it will re-evaluate previous actions. It will run the RateController at 10.0 Hz to pace the enclosed action. It will run the ROS action ComputeTrajectory using the stored route to produce a trajectory on each tick of the RateController. It will run the ROS action FollowTrajectory using the most recently computed trajectory while the RateController continues to trigger trajectory updates.

<root main_tree_to_execute="MainTree">
  <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
    <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="6" name="NavigateRecovery">
      <PipelineSequence name="NavigateWithReplanning">
        <RateController hz="1.0">
          <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="1" name="ComputePathToPose">

          <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearLocalCostmap-Context" service_name="local_costmap/clear_entirely_local_costmap"/>
        </RecoveryNode>
      </PipelineSequence>
      <ReactiveFallback name="RecoveryFallback">
        <GoalUpdated/>
        <SequenceStar name="RecoveryActions">
          <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearLocalCostmap-Subtree" service_name="local_costmap/clear_entirely_local_costmap"/>
          <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearGlobalCostmap-Subtree" service_name="global_costmap/clear_entirely_global_costmap"/>
          <Spin spin_dist="1.57"/>

        </SequenceStar>
      </ReactiveFallback>
    </RecoveryNode>

The mission describes a navigation task that periodically replans a global path, follows the path with pipelined execution, and applies layered recovery behaviors when actions fail.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (RateController, RecoveryNode) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. While an action is running or has succeeded, it will go to the next action. It will run the ROS action RateController at 1.0 Hz to regulate downstream execution timing. It will execute main action ComputePathToPose. If it fails, it will execute recovery action ClearEntireCostmap-Context and then retry the main action ComputePathToPose once. It will then execute main action FollowPath. If it fails, it will execute recovery action ClearLocalCostmap-Context and then retry the main action FollowPath once.

Next, it will execute main action NavigateWithReplanning. If it fails, it will execute recovery action RecoveryFallback. After recovery, it will retry main action NavigateWithReplanning. If the recovery action fails and retries are exhausted, it will stop execution.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (GoalUpdated, RecoveryActions) in order. If any action succeeds, it will stop execution. Else it will keep executing and re-evaluating previous actions whenever an action is running. It will check for a new goal with the ROS action GoalUpdated and will succeed immediately if the goal changes. It will execute the following actions (ClearLocalCostmap-Subtree, ClearGlobalCostmap-Subtree, Spin, Wait) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will run the ROS action ClearEntireCostmap for the local costmap. It will run the ROS action ClearEntireCostmap for the global costmap. It will run the ROS action Spin with a spin distance of 1.57. It will run the ROS action Wait for a duration of 5 seconds.

  This Behavior Tree replans the global path periodically at 1 Hz and it also has
  recovery actions.

<root main_tree_to_execute="MainTree">
  <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
    <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="2" name="NavigateRecovery">
      <PipelineSequence name="NavigateWithReplanning">
        <RateController hz="1.0">
          <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="4" name="ComputePathToPose">
            <ComputePathToPose goal="{goal}" path="{path}" planner_id="GridBased"/>
            <ReactiveFallback name="PathToPoseFallback">
              <GoalUpdated/>
              <RoundRobin name="GlobalPlannerRecoveryActions">
                <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearGlobalCostmap-Context" service_name="global_costmap/clear_entirely_global_costmap"/>
                <Wait wait_duration="2"/>
              </RoundRobin>
            </ReactiveFallback>
          </RecoveryNode>
        </RateController>
        <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="4" name="FollowPath">
          <FollowPath path="{path}" controller_id="FollowPath"/>
          <ReactiveFallback name="FollowPathFallback">
            <GoalUpdated/>
            <RoundRobin name="PlannerRecoveryActions">
              <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearLocalCostmap-Context" service_name="local_costmap/clear_entirely_local_costmap"/>
              <Spin spin_dist="1.57"/>
            </RoundRobin>
          </ReactiveFallback>
        </RecoveryNode>
      </PipelineSequence>
      <ReactiveFallback name="RecoveryFallback">
        <GoalUpdated/>
        <Wait wait_duration="5"/>
      </ReactiveFallback>
    </RecoveryNode>
  </BehaviorTree>
</root>
mrq: The mission describes a navigation task that periodically replans a global path and follows it with layered recovery behaviors. First, the robot will execute the following actions (RateController, ComputePathToPose, FollowPath) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. While an action is running or has succeeded, it will go to the next action. It will run the ROS action RateController to tick downstream actions at 1.0 Hz. It will execute main action ComputePathToPose and, if it fails, execute recovery action PathToPoseFallback and then retry ComputePathToPose up to the configured number of retries. It will read blackboard value and compare it against a set of cases within PathToPoseFallback as a ReactiveFallback that will execute the following actions (GoalUpdated, GlobalPlannerRecoveryActions) in order. If GoalUpdated succeeds, it will stop execution. Else it will keep executing and re-evaluating previous actions whenever an action is running. It will run the ROS action GoalUpdated to detect a new goal. It will run the custom action GlobalPlannerRecoveryActions as a round-robin recovery routine that alternates between clearing the global costmap and waiting for 2 seconds. Next, it will execute main action FollowPath and, if it fails, execute recovery action FollowPathFallback and then retry FollowPath up to the configured number of retries. It will execute the following actions (GoalUpdated, PlannerRecoveryActions) in order within FollowPathFallback. If GoalUpdated succeeds, it will stop execution. Else it will keep executing and re-evaluating previous actions whenever an action is running. It will run the ROS action GoalUpdated to detect a new goal. It will run the custom action PlannerRecoveryActions as a round-robin recovery routine that alternates between clearing the local costmap and spinning in place by 1.57 radians. Finally, it will execute main action NavigateWithReplanning and, if it fails, execute recovery action RecoveryFallback and then retry NavigateWithReplanning up to the configured number of retries. It will execute the following actions (GoalUpdated, Wait) in order within RecoveryFallback. If GoalUpdated succeeds, it will stop execution. Else it will keep executing and re-evaluating previous actions whenever an action is running. It will run the ROS action Wait for 5 seconds when no goal update is detected.

<root main_tree_to_execute="MainTree">
    <!--  ===================================  -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="Combat">
        <SequenceStar>
            <Condition ID="IsInCombat"/>
            <Action ID="AbortJob"/>
            <ForceSuccess>
                <SequenceStar>
                    <Action ID="GetTarget"/>
                    <Action ID="Move"/>
                    <Sequence>
                        <Condition ID="TargetAdjacent"/>
                        <Action ID="AttackTarget"/>
                    </Sequence>
                </SequenceStar>
            </ForceSuccess>
            <Condition ID="IsInCombat"/>
        </SequenceStar>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!--  ===================================  -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="GetUniform">
        <SequenceStar name="GetUniform">
            <Action ID="CheckUniform"/>
            <Fallback>
                <Action ID="Move"/>
                <Action ID="UniformCleanUp"/>
            </Fallback>
            <Action ID="EquipUniform"/>
        </SequenceStar>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!--  ===================================  -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="Hunger">
        <SequenceStar>
            <Condition ID="IsHungry"/>
            <Sequence>
                <SequenceStar name="Hunger">
                    <Action ID="FindFood"/>
                    <Action ID="Move"/>
                    <Action ID="PickUpItem"/>
                    <ForceSuccess>
                        <SequenceStar>
                            <Action ID="FindDining"/>
                            <Action ID="Move"/>
                        </SequenceStar>
                    </ForceSuccess>
                    <Action ID="Eat"/>
                </SequenceStar>
                <Condition ID="IsFull"/>
            </Sequence>
        </SequenceStar>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!--  ===================================  -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="JobButcherAnimal">
        <BB_Precondition expected="ButcherAnimal" key="JobType">
            <Fallback>
                <SequenceStar>
                    <Action ID="InitAnimalJob"/>
                    <Action ID="Move"/>
                    <Action ID="GrabAnimal"/>
                    <Action ID="GetItemDropPosition"/>
                    <Action ID="Move"/>
                    <Action ID="FinalMoveAnimal"/>
                    <Action ID="ButcherAnimal"/>
                    <Action ID="FinishJob"/>
                </SequenceStar>
                <Action ID="AbortJob"/>
            </Fallback>
        </BB_Precondition>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!--  ===================================  -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="JobDyeAnimal">
        <BB_Precondition expected="DyeAnimal" key="JobType">
            <Fallback>
                <SequenceStar>
                    <Action ID="InitJob"/>
                    <Action ID="ClaimItems"/>
                    <SubTree ID="MoveAllItemsToJob"/>
                    <Action ID="InitAnimalJob"/>
                    <Action ID="Move"/>
                    <Action ID="GrabAnimal"/>
                    <Action ID="GetItemDropPosition"/>
                    <Action ID="Move"/>
                    <Action ID="FinalMoveAnimal"/>
                    <Action ID="DyeAnimal"/>
                    <Action ID="FinishJob"/>
                </SequenceStar>
                <Action ID="AbortJob"/>
            </Fallback>
        </BB_Precondition>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!--  ===================================  -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="JobHarvestAnimal">
        <BB_Precondition expected="HarvestAnimal" key="JobType">
            <Fallback>
                <SequenceStar>
                    <Action ID="InitAnimalJob"/>
                    <Action ID="Move"/>
                    <Action ID="GrabAnimal"/>
                    <Action ID="HarvestAnimal"/>
                    <Action ID="ReleaseAnimal"/>
                    <Action ID="FinishJob"/>
                </SequenceStar>
                <Action ID="AbortJob"/>
            </Fallback>
        </BB_Precondition>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!--  ===================================  -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="JobHaulMultiple">
        <BB_Precondition expected="HauleMultipleItems" key="JobType">
            <Fallback>
                <SequenceStar>
                    <Action ID="InitJob"/>
                    <Action ID="FindTool"/>
                    <Action ID="Move"/>
                    <Action ID="EquipTool"/>
                    <Inverter>
                        <Sequence>
                            <Inverter>
                                <Condition ID="AllPickedUp"/>
                            </Inverter>
                            <Sequence>
                                <Action ID="Move"/>
                                <Action ID="PickUpItem"/>
                            </Sequence>
                            <Action ID="ReturnAlwaysRunning"/>
                        </Sequence>
                    </Inverter>
                    <Condition ID="AllPickedUp"/>
                    <Action ID="GetItemDropPosition"/>
                    <Action ID="Move"/>
                    <Action ID="DropAllItems"/>
                    <Action ID="FinishJob"/>
                </SequenceStar>
                <Action ID="AbortJob"/>
            </Fallback>
        </BB_Precondition>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!--  ===================================  -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="JobLeadAnimalToPasture">
        <BB_Precondition expected="LeadAnimalToPasture" key="JobType">
            <Fallback>
                <SequenceStar>
                    <Action ID="InitAnimalJob"/>
                    <Action ID="Move"/>
                    <Action ID="GrabAnimal"/>
                    <Action ID="GetItemDropPosition"/>
                    <Action ID="Move"/>
                    <Action ID="FinalMoveAnimal"/>
                    <Action ID="ReleaseAnimal"/>
                    <Action ID="FinishJob"/>
                </SequenceStar>
                <Action ID="AbortJob"/>
            </Fallback>
        </BB_Precondition>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!--  ===================================  -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="JobStandard">
        <Fallback>
            <SequenceStar name="StandardJobs">
                <Action ID="InitJob"/>
                <Action ID="ClaimItems"/>
                <Action ID="FindTool"/>
                <Action ID="Move"/>
                <Action ID="EquipTool"/>
                <SubTree ID="MoveAllItemsToJob"/>
                <Action ID="GetWorkPosition"/>
                <Action ID="Move"/>
                <Action ID="Work"/>
                <Action ID="FinishJob"/>
            </SequenceStar>
            <Action ID="AbortJob"/>
        </Fallback>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!--  ===================================  -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="JobTameAnimal">
        <BB_Precondition expected="TameAnimal" key="JobType">
            <Fallback>
                <SequenceStar>
                    <Action ID="InitAnimalJob"/>
                    <Action ID="Move"/>
                    <Action ID="TameAnimal"/>
                    <Action ID="GrabAnimal"/>
                    <Action ID="GetItemDropPosition"/>
                    <Action ID="Move"/>
                    <Action ID="FinalMoveAnimal"/>
                    <Action ID="ReleaseAnimal"/>
                    <Action ID="FinishJob"/>
                </SequenceStar>
                <Action ID="AbortJob"/>
            </Fallback>
        </BB_Precondition>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!--  ===================================  -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="Jobs">
        <SequenceStar>
            <Action ID="GetJob"/>
            <FallbackStar>
                <SubTree ID="JobButcherAnimal"/>
                <SubTree ID="JobDyeAnimal"/>
                <SubTree ID="JobLeadAnimalToPasture"/>
                <SubTree ID="JobHarvestAnimal"/>
                <SubTree ID="JobTameAnimal"/>
                <SubTree ID="JobHaulMultiple"/>
                <SubTree ID="JobStandard"/>
            </FallbackStar>
        </SequenceStar>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!--  ===================================  -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
        <SubTree ID="Jobs"/>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!--  ===================================  -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="MoveAllItemsToJob">
        <Sequence>
            <Inverter>
                <Sequence>
                    <Inverter>
                        <Condition ID="AllItemsInPlaceForJob"/>
                    </Inverter>
                    <SequenceStar>
                        <Action ID="Move"/>
                        <Action ID="PickUpItem"/>
                        <Action ID="GetItemDropPosition"/>
                        <Action ID="Move"/>
                        <Action ID="DropItem"/>
                    </SequenceStar>
                    <Action ID="ReturnAlwaysRunning"/>
                </Sequence>
            </Inverter>
            <Condition ID="AllItemsInPlaceForJob"/>
        </Sequence>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!--  ===================================  -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="OldMoveAllItemsToJob">
        <FallbackStar>
            <Condition ID="AllItemsInPlaceForJob"/>
            <RetryUntilSuccesful num_attempts="20">
                <Sequence>
                    <SequenceStar>
                        <Action ID="Move"/>
                        <Action ID="PickUpItem"/>
                        <Fallback>
                            <Action ID="GetItemDropPosition"/>
                            <Action ID="DropItem"/>
                        </Fallback>
                        <Fallback>
                            <Action ID="Move"/>
                            <Action ID="DropItem"/>
                        </Fallback>
                        <Action ID="DropItem"/>
                    </SequenceStar>
                    <Condition ID="AllItemsInPlaceForJob"/>
                </Sequence>
            </RetryUntilSuccesful>
            <ForceFailure>
                <Action ID="AbortJob"/>
            </ForceFailure>
        </FallbackStar>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!--  ===================================  -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="Sleepy">
        <SequenceStar name="Sleep">
            <Condition ID="IsSleepy"/>
            <ForceSuccess>
                <SequenceStar>
                    <Action ID="FindBed"/>
                    <Action ID="Move"/>
                </SequenceStar>
            </ForceSuccess>
            <Action ID="Sleep"/>
        </SequenceStar>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!--  ===================================  -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="Thirst">
        <SequenceStar>
            <Condition ID="IsThirsty"/>
            <Sequence>
                <SequenceStar name="Thirst">
                    <Action ID="FindDrink"/>
                    <Action ID="Move"/>
                    <Action ID="PickUpItem"/>
                    <ForceSuccess>
                        <SequenceStar>
                            <Action ID="FindDining"/>
                            <Action ID="Move"/>
                        </SequenceStar>
                    </ForceSuccess>
                    <Action ID="Drink"/>
                </SequenceStar>
                <Condition ID="IsDrinkFull"/>
            </Sequence>
        </SequenceStar>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!--  ===================================  -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="Trainer">
        <SequenceStar name="Trainer">
            <Condition ID="IsTrainer"/>
            <Action ID="FindTrainerPosition"/>
            <Action ID="Move"/>
            <Action ID="SuperviseTraining"/>
        </SequenceStar>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!--  ===================================  -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="Training">
        <SequenceStar name="Training">
            <Condition ID="IsTrainingTime"/>
            <Action ID="FindTrainingGround"/>
            <Action ID="Move"/>
            <Action ID="Train"/>
        </SequenceStar>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!--  ===================================  -->
    <TreeNodesModel>
        <Action ID="AbortJob"/>
        <Condition ID="AllItemsInPlaceForJob"/>
        <Condition ID="AllPickedUp"/>
        <Action ID="AttackTarget"/>
        <Action ID="ButcherAnimal"/>
        <Action ID="CheckUniform"/>
        <Action ID="ClaimItems"/>
        <SubTree ID="Combat"/>
        <Action ID="Drink"/>
        <Action ID="DropAllItems"/>
        <Action ID="DropItem"/>
        <Action ID="DyeAnimal"/>
        <Action ID="Eat"/>
        <Action ID="EquipTool"/>
        <Action ID="EquipUniform"/>
        <Action ID="FinalMoveAnimal"/>
        <Action ID="FindBed"/>
        <Action ID="FindDining"/>
        <Action ID="FindDrink"/>
        <Action ID="FindFood"/>
        <Action ID="FindTool"/>
        <Action ID="FindTrainerPosition"/>
        <Action ID="FindTrainingGround"/>
        <Action ID="FinishJob"/>
        <Action ID="GetItemDropPosition"/>
        <Action ID="GetJob"/>
        <Action ID="GetTarget"/>
        <SubTree ID="GetUniform"/>
        <Action ID="GetWorkPosition"/>
        <Action ID="GrabAnimal"/>
        <Action ID="HarvestAnimal"/>
        <SubTree ID="Hunger"/>
        <Action ID="InitAnimalJob"/>
        <Action ID="InitButcherJob"/>
        <Action ID="InitJob"/>
        <Condition ID="IsButcherJob"/>
        <Condition ID="IsDrinkFull"/>
        <Condition ID="IsFull"/>
        <Condition ID="IsHungry"/>
        <Condition ID="IsInCombat"/>
        <Condition ID="IsSleepy"/>
        <Condition ID="IsThirsty"/>
        <Condition ID="IsTrainer"/>
        <Condition ID="IsTrainingTime"/>
        <Condition ID="IsVeryHungry"/>
        <Condition ID="IsVeryThirsty"/>
        <SubTree ID="JobButcherAnimal"/>
        <SubTree ID="JobDyeAnimal"/>
        <SubTree ID="JobHarvestAnimal"/>
        <SubTree ID="JobHaulMultiple"/>
        <SubTree ID="JobLeadAnimalToPasture"/>
        <SubTree ID="JobStandard"/>
        <SubTree ID="JobTameAnimal"/>
        <SubTree ID="Jobs"/>
        <Action ID="Move"/>
        <SubTree ID="MoveAllItemsToJob"/>
        <SubTree ID="OldMoveAllItemsToJob"/>
        <Action ID="PickUpItem"/>
        <Action ID="RandomMove"/>
        <Action ID="ReleaseAnimal"/>
        <Action ID="ReturnAlwaysRunning"/>
        <Action ID="Sleep"/>
        <SubTree ID="Sleepy"/>

        <Action ID="Train"/>

        <SubTree ID="Training"/>
        <Action ID="UniformCleanUp"/>
        <Action ID="Work"/>
    </TreeNodesModel>

</root>
mrq:
The mission describes a worker robot that selects jobs, executes specialized animal-handling and standard tasks, manages combat, maintains uniform, satisfies hunger and thirst, sleeps, trains, and supervises training through ordered and reactive routines.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (GetJob, FallbackStar over specific job subtrees) in sequence. It will run the custom action GetJob to retrieve a job definition. It will execute the following actions (JobButcherAnimal, JobDyeAnimal, JobLeadAnimalToPasture, JobHarvestAnimal, JobTameAnimal, JobHaulMultiple, JobStandard) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, it will start all over again until an action succeeds.

Next, it will read blackboard value and compare it against a set of cases. If a case matches the blackboard value, it will execute corresponding custom action sequence for JobButcherAnimal. If no case matches, it will execute default custom action AbortJob. It will execute the following actions (InitAnimalJob, Move, GrabAnimal, GetItemDropPosition, Move, FinalMoveAnimal, ButcherAnimal, FinishJob) in sequence. It will start the sequence again from the beginning if any action fails. It will execute the following actions (AbortJob) if the main sequence cannot complete under the job precondition.

Next, it will read blackboard value and compare it against a set of cases. If a case matches the blackboard value, it will execute corresponding custom action sequence for JobDyeAnimal. If no case matches, it will execute default custom action AbortJob. It will execute the following actions (InitJob, ClaimItems, MoveAllItemsToJob, InitAnimalJob, Move, GrabAnimal, GetItemDropPosition, Move, FinalMoveAnimal, DyeAnimal, FinishJob) in sequence. It will start the sequence again from the beginning if any action fails. It will execute the following actions (AbortJob) if the main sequence cannot complete under the job precondition.

Next, it will read blackboard value and compare it against a set of cases. If a case matches the blackboard value, it will execute corresponding custom action sequence for JobHarvestAnimal. If no case matches, it will execute default custom action AbortJob. It will execute the following actions (InitAnimalJob, Move, GrabAnimal, HarvestAnimal, ReleaseAnimal, FinishJob) in sequence. It will start the sequence again from the beginning if any action fails. It will execute the following actions (AbortJob) if the main sequence cannot complete under the job precondition.

Next, it will read blackboard value and compare it against a set of cases. If a case matches the blackboard value, it will execute corresponding custom action sequence for JobHaulMultiple. If no case matches, it will execute default custom action AbortJob. It will execute the following actions (InitJob, FindTool, Move, EquipTool) in sequence and will start again from the beginning if any action fails. It will execute inverted sequence where it will execute inverted [Condition] check AllPickedUp and then execute the following actions (Move, PickUpItem) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails, then run the custom action ReturnAlwaysRunning. It will execute inverted action so that if the inner sequence succeeds, it will reverse it to fail, and if it fails, it will reverse it to succeed. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for AllPickedUp. It will execute the following actions (GetItemDropPosition, Move, DropAllItems, FinishJob) in sequence and will start again from the beginning if any action fails. It will execute the following actions (AbortJob) if the main sequence cannot complete under the job precondition.

Next, it will read blackboard value and compare it against a set of cases. If a case matches the blackboard value, it will execute corresponding custom action sequence for JobLeadAnimalToPasture. If no case matches, it will execute default custom action AbortJob. It will execute the following actions (InitAnimalJob, Move, GrabAnimal, GetItemDropPosition, Move, FinalMoveAnimal, ReleaseAnimal, FinishJob) in sequence. It will start the sequence again from the beginning if any action fails. It will execute the following actions (AbortJob) if the main sequence cannot complete under the job precondition.

Next, it will execute the following actions (InitJob, ClaimItems, FindTool, Move, EquipTool, MoveAllItemsToJob, GetWorkPosition, Move, Work, FinishJob) in sequence. It will start the sequence again from the beginning if any action fails. It will execute the following actions (AbortJob) consecutively if the standard job sequence fails, and if no action succeeds, the execution fails.

Next, it will read blackboard value and compare it against a set of cases. If a case matches the blackboard value, it will execute corresponding custom action sequence for JobTameAnimal. If no case matches, it will execute default custom action AbortJob. It will execute the following actions (InitAnimalJob, Move, TameAnimal, GrabAnimal, GetItemDropPosition, Move, FinalMoveAnimal, ReleaseAnimal, FinishJob) in sequence. It will start the sequence again from the beginning if any action fails. It will execute the following actions (AbortJob) if the main sequence cannot complete under the job precondition.

Next, it will execute the following actions (IsInCombat, AbortJob, ForceSuccess over a pursuit and attack sequence, IsInCombat) in sequence. It will execute the following actions (GetTarget, Move, Sequence of TargetAdjacent then AttackTarget) in sequence and will start again from the beginning if any action fails. It will execute the custom action AbortJob before attempting combat routines. It will execute GetTarget and Move while ensuring success through a forced success decorator. It will execute delayed attack only when TargetAdjacent is true and will stop that inner sequence if any action fails. It will execute the outer sequence in order and will start again from the beginning if any action fails.

Next, it will execute the following actions (CheckUniform, Fallback of Move or UniformCleanUp, EquipUniform) in sequence. It will start the sequence again from the beginning if any action fails. It will execute the following actions (Move, UniformCleanUp) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails.

Next, it will execute the following actions (IsHungry, inner Sequence of eating routine) in order and will start again from the beginning if any action fails. It will execute the following actions (FindFood, Move, PickUpItem, ForceSuccess over dining move, Eat) in sequence and will start again from the beginning if any action fails. It will execute the following actions (FindDining, Move) in sequence and will make sure it always succeeds. It will execute the following actions (IsFull) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails.

Next, it will execute the following actions (MoveAllItemsToJob) in order for item logistics. It will execute inverted sequence where it will execute inverted [Condition] check AllItemsInPlaceForJob and then execute the following actions (Move, PickUpItem, GetItemDropPosition, Move, DropItem) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails, then run the custom action ReturnAlwaysRunning. It will execute inverted action so that if the inner sequence succeeds, it will reverse it to fail, and if it fails, it will reverse it to succeed. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for AllItemsInPlaceForJob. It will execute the following actions (AllItemsInPlaceForJob) in sequence to confirm completion.

Next, it will execute the following actions (IsSleepy, ForceSuccess over finding and moving to bed, Sleep) in sequence. It will start the sequence again from the beginning if any action fails. It will execute the following actions (FindBed, Move) in sequence and will make sure it always succeeds. It will execute the custom action Sleep after reaching or attempting to reach a bed.

Next, it will execute the following actions (IsThirsty, inner Sequence of drinking routine) in order and will start again from the beginning if any action fails. It will execute the following actions (FindDrink, Move, PickUpItem, ForceSuccess over dining move, Drink) in sequence and will start again from the beginning if any action fails. It will execute the following actions (FindDining, Move) in sequence and will make sure it always succeeds. It will execute the following actions (IsDrinkFull) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails.

Next, it will execute the following actions (IsTrainer, FindTrainerPosition, Move, SuperviseTraining) in sequence. It will start the sequence again from the beginning if any action fails.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (IsTrainingTime, FindTrainingGround, Move, Train) in sequence. It will start the sequence again from the beginning if any action fails.

				<Delay delay_msec="3000">
					<AntennaAction rate="0" intensity="10" left_blink_pattern="01" right_blink_pattern="10" />
				</Delay>
				<Delay delay_msec="3000">
					<AntennaAction rate="5" intensity="100" left_blink_pattern="01" right_blink_pattern="10" />
				</Delay>
        		<Delay delay_msec="9000">
          			<AntennaAction rate="10" intensity="100" left_blink_pattern="111100000" right_blink_pattern="00001111" />
        		</Delay>
			</Sequence>
		</KeepRunningUntilFailure>
