mrq:
The mission describes a repetitive signaling task that cycles antenna blink patterns with timed delays in strict order. First, the robot will execute the following actions (Delay, Delay, Delay) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute delayed custom action AntennaAction with rate 0, intensity 10, and left_blink_pattern 01 and right_blink_pattern 10. It will start a 3000 ms delay after the delayed action succeeds and will resume execution when the delay completes. It will execute delayed custom action AntennaAction with rate 5, intensity 100, and left_blink_pattern 01 and right_blink_pattern 10. It will start a 3000 ms delay after the delayed action succeeds and will resume execution when the delay completes. It will execute delayed custom action AntennaAction with rate 10, intensity 100, and left_blink_pattern 111100000 and right_blink_pattern 00001111. It will start a 9000 ms delay after the delayed action succeeds and will resume execution when the delay completes.

Next, it will execute AntennaAction sequencing repeatedly as long as the sequence does not fail. It will execute the sequence again each time the prior execution succeeds. It will stop looping and continue execution only if the sequence fails.

<root main_tree_to_execute = "MainTree">

    <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">

        <Sequence name="main_sequence">
            <SetBlackboard output_key="move_goal" value="1;2;3" />
            <SubTree ID="MoveRobot" target="move_goal" output="move_result" />
            <SaySomething message="{move_result}"/>
        </Sequence>

    </BehaviorTree>

    <BehaviorTree ID="MoveRobot">
        <Fallback name="move_robot_main">
            <SequenceStar>
                <MoveBase       goal="{target}"/>
                <SetBlackboard output_key="output" value="mission accomplished" />
            </SequenceStar>
            <ForceFailure>
                <SetBlackboard output_key="output" value="mission failed" />
            </ForceFailure>
        </Fallback>
    </BehaviorTree>

</root>
mrq:
The mission describes a two-part task that sets a movement goal, attempts a move using a subordinate tree, and reports the move result through speech.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (SetBlackboard, SubTree, SaySomething) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action SetBlackboard to store the value 1;2;3 under the key move_goal. It will run the custom action SubTree with ID MoveRobot using the blackboard key move_goal as the input target and will store its output under the key move_result after the key move_goal is set. It will run the custom action SaySomething using the value stored in move_result as the message after the subordinate tree returns its result.

Next, it will execute the following actions (SequenceStar, ForceFailure) consecutively. If any action (SequenceStar) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will execute the following actions (MoveBase, SetBlackboard) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will run the ROS action MoveBase with the goal provided in the key target. It will run the custom action SetBlackboard to store the value mission accomplished under the key output after MoveBase succeeds. It will execute SetBlackboard and make sure it always fails to store the value mission failed under the key output if the previous sequence does not succeed.

mrq: The mission describes a single navigation and communication task that validates battery status, announces progress, moves to a specified goal, and announces completion in strict order. First, the robot will execute the following actions (BatteryOK, SaySomething, MoveBase, SaySomething) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute the custom action BatteryOK and will proceed only if the battery status check succeeds. It will execute the custom action SaySomething with the message set to “Mission Started!” after the battery check succeeds. It will execute the custom action MoveBase with the goal set to the coordinates “1;2;3” after the start announcement succeeds. It will execute the custom action SaySomething with the message set to “Mission completed!” after the movement to the goal succeeds.

mrq:
The mission describes a repeated navigation workflow that validates a temporal-logic state, moves to a goal, and updates the state each cycle.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (LTLPreCheck, MoveAction, UpdateLTL) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action LTLPreCheck using the current LTL state and the desired LTL state sequence. It will run the custom action MoveAction to navigate to the provided nav_goal while monitoring move_base_idle and move_base_finished. It will run the custom action UpdateLTL to record the executed action_sequence and to update the desired LTL state sequence and the next nav_goal after the move completes.

Next, it will execute UpdateLTL, MoveAction, and LTLPreCheck in the same strict order again only if the previous cycle was restarted or continued due to success or running statuses, which does not apply here due to the basic sequence semantics.

Finally, it will execute the actions for a set number of times or infinitely based on num_cycles. It will execute the sequence continuously until the configured number of repetitions is reached. It will keep executing the sequence forever if num_cycles indicates an infinite loop.

BT:
<?xml version="1.0"?>
<root main_tree_to_execute="BehaviorTree">
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="BehaviorTree">
        <Repeat num_cycles="{num_cycles}">
            <Sequence>
                <Fallback>
                    <Condition ID="LTLPreCheck" ltl_state_current="{ltl_state_current}" ltl_state_desired_sequence="{ltl_state_desired_sequence}" ltl_state_executed_sequence="{ltl_state_executed_sequence}"/>
                    <ForceFailure>
                        <Action ID="ReplanningRequestLevel2" ltl_state_current="{ltl_state_current}" ltl_state_executed_sequence="{ltl_state_executed_sequence}" replanning_request="{replanning_request}"/>
                    </ForceFailure>
                </Fallback>
                <ReactiveSequence>
                    <Fallback>
                        <Action ID="FakeDetectionLevel1" replanning_fake_input="{replanning_fake_input}"/>
                        <ForceFailure>
                            <Action ID="ReplanningRequestLevel1" ltl_state_current="{ltl_state_current}" ltl_state_executed_sequence="{ltl_state_executed_sequence}" replanning_request="{replanning_request}"/>
                        </ForceFailure>
                    </Fallback>
                    <Fallback>
                        <Condition ID="ReactiveLTLStateCheck" ltl_state_current="{ltl_state_current}" ltl_state_desired_sequence="{ltl_state_desired_sequence}"/>
                        <ForceFailure>
                            <Action ID="ReplanningRequestLevel2" ltl_state_current="{ltl_state_current}" ltl_state_executed_sequence="{ltl_state_executed_sequence}" replanning_request="{replanning_request}"/>
                        </ForceFailure>
                    </Fallback>
                    <Fallback>
                        <Action ID="FakeDetectionLevel3" replanning_fake_input="{replanning_fake_input}"/>
                        <ForceFailure>
                            <Action ID="ReplanningRequestLevel3" ltl_state_current="{ltl_state_current}" ltl_state_executed_sequence="{ltl_state_executed_sequence}" replanning_request="{replanning_request}"/>
                        </ForceFailure>
                    </Fallback>
                    <Fallback>
                        <Switch6 case_1="move" case_2="stay" case_3="pick" case_4="drop" case_5="reset" case_6="start_guiding" variable="{bt_action_type}">
                            <Action ID="MoveAction" bt_action_type="{bt_action_type}" current_action="{current_action}" goal_sent="{goal_sent}" move_base_finished="{move_base_finished}" move_base_idle="{move_base_idle}"/>
                            <Action ID="StayAction" bt_action_type="{bt_action_type}" current_action="{current_action}" ltl_state_current="{ltl_state_current}"/>
                            <Action ID="PickAction" bt_action_type="{bt_action_type}" current_action="{current_action}" ltl_state_current="{ltl_state_current}"/>
                            <Action ID="DropActionNew" bt_action_type="{bt_action_type}" current_action="{current_action}" ltl_state_current="{ltl_state_current}"/>
                            <Action ID="ResetAction" bt_action_type="{bt_action_type}" current_action="{current_action}" ltl_state_current="{ltl_state_current}"/>
                            <Action ID="GuideActionNew" bt_action_type="{bt_action_type}" current_action="{current_action}" ltl_state_current="{ltl_state_current}"/>
                            <AlwaysFailure/>
                        </Switch6>
                        <ForceFailure>
                            <Action ID="ReplanningRequestLevel3" ltl_state_current="{ltl_state_current}" ltl_state_executed_sequence="{ltl_state_executed_sequence}" replanning_request="{replanning_request}"/>
                        </ForceFailure>
                    </Fallback>
                </ReactiveSequence>
                <Action ID="UpdateLTL" action_sequence="{action_sequence}" action_sequence_executed="{action_sequence_executed}" bt_action_type="{bt_action_type}" current_action="{current_action}" ltl_state_desired_sequence="{ltl_state_desired_sequence}"/>
            </Sequence>
        </Repeat>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <TreeNodesModel>
        <Action ID="BackNormalAction">
            <input_port name="bt_action_type"/>
            <input_port name="current_action"/>
            <inout_port name="ltl_state_current"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="DropAction">
            <input_port name="bt_action_type"/>
            <input_port name="current_action"/>
            <inout_port name="ltl_state_current"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="DropActionNew">
            <input_port name="bt_action_type"/>
            <input_port name="current_action"/>
            <inout_port name="ltl_state_current"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="FakeDetectionLevel1">
            <input_port name="replanning_fake_input"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="FakeDetectionLevel2">
            <input_port name="replanning_fake_input"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="FakeDetectionLevel3">
            <input_port name="replanning_fake_input"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="GuideActionNew">
            <input_port name="bt_action_type"/>
            <input_port name="current_action"/>
            <inout_port name="ltl_state_current"/>
        </Action>
        <Condition ID="LTLPreCheck">
            <input_port name="ltl_state_current"/>
            <input_port name="ltl_state_desired_sequence"/>
            <inout_port name="ltl_state_executed_sequence"/>
        </Condition>
        <Action ID="LocomotionStart">
            <input_port name="locomotion_status"/>
        </Action>
        <Condition ID="LocomotionStatusCheck">
            <input_port name="locomotion_status"/>
        </Condition>
        <Action ID="MoveAction">
            <input_port name="bt_action_type"/>
            <input_port name="current_action"/>
            <output_port name="goal_sent"/>
            <input_port name="move_base_finished"/>
            <input_port name="move_base_idle"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="PickAction">
            <input_port name="bt_action_type"/>
            <input_port name="current_action"/>
            <inout_port name="ltl_state_current"/>
        </Action>
        <Condition ID="ReactiveLTLStateCheck">
            <input_port name="ltl_state_current"/>
            <input_port name="ltl_state_desired_sequence"/>
        </Condition>
        <Action ID="RecoveryStand">
            <input_port name="locomotion_status"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="ReplanningRequestLevel1">
            <input_port name="ltl_state_current"/>
            <inout_port name="ltl_state_executed_sequence"/>
            <inout_port name="replanning_request"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="ReplanningRequestLevel2">
            <input_port name="ltl_state_current"/>
            <inout_port name="ltl_state_executed_sequence"/>
            <inout_port name="replanning_request"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="ReplanningRequestLevel3">
            <input_port name="ltl_state_current"/>
            <inout_port name="ltl_state_executed_sequence"/>
            <inout_port name="replanning_request"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="ResetAction">
            <input_port name="bt_action_type"/>
            <input_port name="current_action"/>
            <inout_port name="ltl_state_current"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="StayAction">
            <input_port name="bt_action_type"/>
            <input_port name="current_action"/>
            <inout_port name="ltl_state_current"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="SynchronizedTransitionAction">
            <input_port name="bt_action_type"/>
            <input_port name="current_action"/>
            <inout_port name="ltl_state_current"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="UpdateLTL">
            <inout_port name="action_sequence"/>
            <inout_port name="action_sequence_executed"/>
            <output_port name="bt_action_type"/>
            <output_port name="current_action"/>
            <inout_port name="ltl_state_desired_sequence"/>
        </Action>
    </TreeNodesModel>
    <!-- ////////// -->
</root>
mrq:
The mission describes a repeated supervisory task that checks logical task progression, reacts to state and detections, selects and executes an action by type, and then updates the task state for the next cycle.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (Fallback, ReactiveSequence, UpdateLTL) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will begin by executing the following actions (LTLPreCheck, ForceFailure(ReplanningRequestLevel2)) consecutively and will move to the next action until an action succeeds or fail if none succeeds. It will run the custom condition LTLPreCheck to verify that the current logical state matches the desired sequence with respect to the executed sequence. It will execute ReplanningRequestLevel2 and make sure it always fails only if the precheck does not succeed. It will then execute the following actions (Fallback, Fallback, Fallback, Fallback) in sequence while re-evaluating prior actions whenever a later action is still running. It will finally run the custom action UpdateLTL to compute and output the next bt_action_type and current_action and to update the desired sequence after the reactive phase completes.

Next, it will execute the following actions (FakeDetectionLevel1, ForceFailure(ReplanningRequestLevel1)) consecutively and will move to the next action until an action succeeds or fail if none succeeds. It will run the custom action FakeDetectionLevel1 to simulate a level-1 detection and will treat its success as sufficient to stop this fallback. It will execute ReplanningRequestLevel1 and make sure it always fails only if the simulated detection does not succeed.

Next, it will execute the following actions (ReactiveLTLStateCheck, ForceFailure(ReplanningRequestLevel2)) consecutively and will move to the next action until an action succeeds or fail if none succeeds. It will run the custom condition ReactiveLTLStateCheck to verify that the current logical state remains consistent with the desired sequence under reactive evaluation. It will execute ReplanningRequestLevel2 and make sure it always fails only if the reactive logical state check does not succeed.

Next, it will execute the following actions (FakeDetectionLevel3, ForceFailure(ReplanningRequestLevel3)) consecutively and will move to the next action until an action succeeds or fail if none succeeds. It will run the custom action FakeDetectionLevel3 to simulate a level-3 detection and will treat its success as sufficient to stop this fallback. It will execute ReplanningRequestLevel3 and make sure it always fails only if the simulated level-3 detection does not succeed.

Finally, it will read blackboard value and compare it against a set of cases. If a case matches the blackboard value, it will execute corresponding action, else if no case matches, it will execute default action. It will match the variable bt_action_type against the cases move, stay, pick, drop, reset, and start_guiding and then execute the corresponding custom action among MoveAction, StayAction, PickAction, DropActionNew, ResetAction, or GuideActionNew. It will treat any unmatched case as AlwaysFailure to force this switch to fail when no case applies. It will execute the following actions (Switch6, ForceFailure(ReplanningRequestLevel3)) consecutively and will move to the next action until an action succeeds or fail if none succeeds. It will execute ReplanningRequestLevel3 and make sure it always fails only if the switch processing does not succeed.

Finally, it will execute UpdateLTL for as many cycles as specified or infinitely. It will execute UpdateLTL for a set number of times or infinitely. It will repeat the entire sequence body num_cycles times by re-running the child sequence each cycle.

<root main_tree_to_execute = "MainTree">

The mission describes a patrol task that sets three target poses and navigates to them in a fixed order for a specified number of cycles.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (SetBlackboard, SetBlackboard, SetBlackboard, Sequence) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will set the blackboard key Goal_a to the pose -0.579;-1.341;0.0;1.0. It will set the blackboard key Goal_b to the pose 5.214;-1.533;0.0;1.0. It will set the blackboard key Goal_c to the pose -1.588;1.253;0.0;1.0. It will then execute the following actions (SubTree:MoveRobot with target Goal_a, SubTree:MoveRobot with target Goal_b, SubTree:MoveRobot with target Goal_c) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute this set of actions for a set number of times or infinitely. It will execute this set of actions for 3 times.

Next, it will execute the following actions (Nav2Client) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the ROS action Nav2Client with the goal provided by the input port target. It will complete the subtree after the navigation action succeeds.

mrq:
The mission describes a room-entry and object-approach task that first ensures presence in a target room, then aligns to a target transform, and finally approaches a specified object.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (inRoom, ApproachRoom) consecutively. If any action (inRoom) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will run the custom action inRoom with the specified room to check whether it is already inside the target room. It will run the custom action ApproachRoom with the specified room to enter the target room if the room check does not succeed.

Next, it will execute the ROS action setTF with the specified object and will make sure it always succeeds.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (turnToTf, ApproachObject) in sequence. If turnToTf fails, it will stop execution. While turnToTf is still running, it will re-evaluate previous actions. It will run the custom action turnToTf to orient toward the transform of the specified object. It will run the custom action ApproachObject to move closer to the specified object after the orientation action succeeds.

<!--
  This Behavior Tree replans the global path periodically at 1 Hz and it also has
  recovery actions specific to planning / control as well as general system issues.
  This will be continuous if a kinematically valid planner is selected.
-->
<root main_tree_to_execute="MainTree">
  <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
    <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="6" name="NavigateRecovery">
      <PipelineSequence name="NavigateWithReplanning">
        <RateController hz="1.0">
          <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="1" name="ComputePathToPose">
            <ComputePathToPose goal="{goal}" path="{path}" planner_id="GridBased"/>
            <ReactiveFallback name="ComputePathToPoseRecoveryFallback">
              <GoalUpdated/>
              <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearGlobalCostmap-Context" service_name="global_costmap/clear_entirely_global_costmap"/>
            </ReactiveFallback>
          </RecoveryNode>
        </RateController>
        <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="1" name="FollowPath">
          <FollowPath path="{path}" controller_id="FollowPath"/>
          <ReactiveFallback name="FollowPathRecoveryFallback">
            <GoalUpdated/>
            <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearLocalCostmap-Context" service_name="local_costmap/clear_entirely_local_costmap"/>
          </ReactiveFallback>
        </RecoveryNode>
      </PipelineSequence>
      <ReactiveFallback name="RecoveryFallback">
        <GoalUpdated/>
        <RoundRobin name="RecoveryActions">
          <Sequence name="ClearingActions">
            <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearLocalCostmap-Subtree" service_name="local_costmap/clear_entirely_local_costmap"/>
            <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearGlobalCostmap-Subtree" service_name="global_costmap/clear_entirely_global_costmap"/>
          </Sequence>
          <Spin spin_dist="1.57"/>
          <Wait wait_duration="5"/>
          <BackUp backup_dist="0.15" backup_speed="0.025"/>
        </RoundRobin>
      </ReactiveFallback>
    </RecoveryNode>
  </BehaviorTree>
</root>
mrq:
The mission describes a navigation task that periodically replans a global path and follows it with layered recoveries for planning, control, and general navigation issues.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (NavigateWithReplanning, RecoveryFallback) consecutively. If any action (NavigateWithReplanning) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will execute main NavigateWithReplanning. If it fails, it will execute recovery action (RecoveryFallback). After recovery, it will retry main action (NavigateWithReplanning). If recovery action (RecoveryFallback) fails and retries are exhausted, it will stop execution.

Next, it will execute the following actions (RateController, FollowPath) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. While an action is running or has succeeded, it will go to the next action. It will run RateController at 1.0 Hz to pace downstream actions. It will execute main ComputePathToPose. If it fails, it will execute recovery action (ComputePathToPoseRecoveryFallback). After recovery, it will retry main action (ComputePathToPose). If recovery action (ComputePathToPoseRecoveryFallback) fails and retries are exhausted, it will stop execution.

Next, it will execute the following actions (ComputePathToPose, ComputePathToPoseRecoveryFallback) consecutively. If any action (ComputePathToPose) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will run the ROS action ComputePathToPose with the goal provided and will store the resulting path. It will execute the following actions [GoalUpdated, ClearEntireCostmap-Context] in order. If GoalUpdated succeeds, it will stop execution. Else it will keep executing and re-evaluating previous actions whenever an action is running. It will call the ROS action ClearEntireCostmap-Context on the global costmap when the goal has not updated.

Next, it will execute main FollowPath. If it fails, it will execute recovery action (FollowPathRecoveryFallback). After recovery, it will retry main action (FollowPath). If recovery action (FollowPathRecoveryFallback) fails and retries are exhausted, it will stop execution. It will run the ROS action FollowPath using the stored path with the specified controller after the path is available.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (FollowPath, FollowPathRecoveryFallback) consecutively. If any action (FollowPath) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will execute the following actions [GoalUpdated, ClearEntireCostmap-Context] in order. If GoalUpdated succeeds, it will stop execution. Else it will keep executing and re-evaluating previous actions whenever an action is running. It will call the ROS action ClearEntireCostmap-Context on the local costmap when the goal has not updated.

mrq:
The mission describes a service task where a robot will attempt to reach a room, locate a bottle, and fetch it, with contingency behaviors to follow a human or ask for help.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (FB2, S2) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute the following actions (S3, Follow_human) consecutively. If any action (S3) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will execute the following actions (Room_known, Go_to_room) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule. It will run the custom action Go_to_room after the condition succeeds. It will run the custom action Follow_human if going to the room fails. It will execute the following actions (Find_bottle, Fetch_bottle) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action Find_bottle. It will run the custom action Fetch_bottle after the bottle is found.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (S1, Ask_for_help) consecutively. If any action (S1) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will execute the following actions (FB2, S2) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will read blackboard value and compare it against a set of cases only as implied by the conditions already checked. It will execute the following actions (Ask_for_help) and make sure it always follows the fallback rule to attempt it only if previous actions fail. It will run the custom action Ask_for_help if reaching the room or fetching the bottle fails.

mrq:
The mission describes a staged manipulation and navigation task that resets the arm, collects yellow atoms, empties the right barrel, and drives toward a final chaos location in strict order.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (MoveArmFront, TurnArm, PushLeftAtom, PushRightAtom) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action MoveArmFront with position 950 to place the arm back. It will run the custom action TurnArm with position 510 to center the arm. It will run the custom action PushLeftAtom with position 1000 to set the left pusher to its back position. It will run the custom action PushRightAtom with position 580 to set the right pusher to its back position.

Next, it will execute the following actions (MoveAhead, Turn, MoveAhead, Turn, Parallel, SubTree: PickAtomRight, Parallel, SubTree: PickAtomRight, Parallel, SubTree: PickAtomRight, Parallel, SubTree: PickAtomRight, Parallel, SubTree: PickAtomRight, Parallel) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action MoveAhead with distance 632 to approach the yellow atoms. It will run the custom action Turn with angle -90 to face left toward the atoms. It will run the custom action MoveAhead with distance 258 to align longitudinally. It will run the custom action Turn with angle -2 to refine heading. It will execute the following actions (MoveAhead, Sequence) simultaneously. If either a success threshold is reached or all actions succeed, the execution was successful. Else if any action fails, the execution fails. It will run the custom action MoveAhead with distance 100 while concurrently running a sequence that sets the blackboard key barrelGoalPosition to 9535 and then calls the subtree TurnBarrel with that blackboard key. It will execute the subtree PickAtomRight to pick one atom on the right. It will execute the following actions (MoveAhead, Sequence) simultaneously. If either a success threshold is reached or all actions succeed, the execution was successful. Else if any action fails, the execution fails. It will run the custom action MoveAhead with distance 100 while concurrently running a sequence that sets the blackboard key barrelGoalPosition to 19070 and then calls the subtree TurnBarrel with that blackboard key. It will execute the subtree PickAtomRight to pick another atom on the right. It will execute the following actions (MoveAhead, Sequence) simultaneously. If either a success threshold is reached or all actions succeed, the execution was successful. Else if any action fails, the execution fails. It will run the custom action MoveAhead with distance 100 while concurrently running a sequence that sets the blackboard key barrelGoalPosition to 28605 and then calls the subtree TurnBarrel with that blackboard key. It will execute the subtree PickAtomRight again to pick the next atom. It will execute the following actions (MoveAhead, Sequence) simultaneously. If either a success threshold is reached or all actions succeed, the execution was successful. Else if any action fails, the execution fails. It will run the custom action MoveAhead with distance 100 while concurrently running a sequence that sets the blackboard key barrelGoalPosition to 38140 and then calls the subtree TurnBarrel with that blackboard key. It will execute the subtree PickAtomRight again to pick the next atom. It will execute the following actions (MoveAhead, Sequence) simultaneously. If either a success threshold is reached or all actions succeed, the execution was successful. Else if any action fails, the execution fails. It will run the custom action MoveAhead with distance 100 while concurrently running a sequence that sets the blackboard key barrelGoalPosition to 47675 and then calls the subtree TurnBarrel with that blackboard key. It will execute the subtree PickAtomRight again to pick the final atom in this row. It will execute the following actions (MoveAhead, Sequence) simultaneously. If either a success threshold is reached or all actions succeed, the execution was successful. Else if any action fails, the execution fails. It will run the custom action MoveAhead with distance 200 while concurrently running a sequence that sets the blackboard key barrelGoalPosition to 57210 and then calls the subtree TurnBarrel with that blackboard key.

Next, it will execute the following actions (SetBlackboard, SubTree: TurnBarrel, SubTree: PushRight, SetBlackboard, SubTree: TurnBarrel, SubTree: PushRight, SetBlackboard, SubTree: TurnBarrel, SubTree: PushRight, SetBlackboard, SubTree: TurnBarrel, SubTree: PushRight, SetBlackboard, SubTree: TurnBarrel, SubTree: PushRight, SetBlackboard, SubTree: TurnBarrel, SubTree: PushRight) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule by setting barrelGoalPosition to 65460. It will call the subtree TurnBarrel with the key barrelGoalPosition. It will call the subtree PushRight to push at the right station. It will set barrelGoalPosition to 74845 and call the subtree TurnBarrel and then call the subtree PushRight. It will set barrelGoalPosition to 84380 and call the subtree TurnBarrel and then call the subtree PushRight. It will set barrelGoalPosition to 93915 and call the subtree TurnBarrel and then call the subtree PushRight. It will set barrelGoalPosition to 103450 and call the subtree TurnBarrel and then call the subtree PushRight. It will set barrelGoalPosition to 112985* and call the subtree TurnBarrel and then call the subtree PushRight.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (Turn, MoveAhead, Turn, MoveAhead) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action Turn with angle -45 to orient toward the chaos area. It will run the custom action MoveAhead with distance 500 to advance toward the chaos area. It will run the custom action Turn with angle -137 to complete the approach heading. It will run the custom action MoveAhead with distance 1200 to reach the chaos area.

First, it will execute the following actions (MoveArmFront, MoveArmSideJoint, TurnArm, MoveArmSideJoint, Wait, MoveArmSideJoint, TurnArm, MoveArmFront, Wait, MoveArmFront, Wait) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action MoveArmFront with position 950 to place the arm back. It will run the custom action MoveArmSideJoint with position 235 to center the side joint. It will run the custom action TurnArm with position 820 to point the arm to the right. It will run the custom action MoveArmSideJoint with position 1023 to extend toward the right. It will execute delayed Wait. If execution succeeds, start delay timer. After delay period is exhausted, resume execution with a 500 ms pause to allow grasp stabilization. It will run the custom action MoveArmSideJoint with position 235 to retract to center. It will run the custom action TurnArm with position 510 to recenter the arm. It will run the custom action MoveArmFront with position 460 to bring the arm forward for release. It will execute delayed Wait. If execution succeeds, start delay timer. After delay period is exhausted, resume execution with a 1000 ms pause to allow the atom to drop. It will run the custom action MoveArmFront with position 950 to return the arm back. It will execute delayed Wait. If execution succeeds, start delay timer. After delay period is exhausted, resume execution with a 500 ms pause to complete the cycle.

Next, it will execute the following actions (ActivateBarrel, RetryUntilSuccesful: IsBarrelMoveFinished, DeactivateBarrel) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action ActivateBarrel to start turning the barrel. It will keep trying to execute IsBarrelMoveFinished until it succeeds. If try succeeds, stop retrying and continue. If try fails, keep retrying until it succeeds, using the goalPosition provided by the blackboard key barrelGoalPosition. It will run the custom action DeactivateBarrel to stop turning the barrel.

Next, it will execute the following actions (PushLeftAtom, PushLeftAtom) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action PushLeftAtom with position 530 to push the left actuator forward. It will run the custom action PushLeftAtom with position 1000 to retract the left actuator.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (PushRightAtom, PushRightAtom) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action PushRightAtom with position 1000 to push the right actuator forward. It will run the custom action PushRightAtom with position 580 to retract the right actuator.

<root main_tree_to_execute = "MainTree" >

    <BehaviorTree ID="ArmMovementWithRequest">
        <Sequence>
            <FloatParameterRequest title="Bitte geben Sie x, y und z in [mm] an"
                                   value="{x}" key="x" min="-1000" max="1000"/>
            <FloatParameterRequest value="{y}" key="y" min="-1000" max="1000"/>
            <FloatParameterRequest value="{z}" key="z" min="-1000" max="1000"/>
            <FloatParameterRequest msg="Geben Sie die Greiferposition von 0 [open] bis 1 [closed] an"
                                   value="{gripper_position}" key="gripper_position" min="0" max="1"/>
            <ArmMovement x="{x}" y="{y}" z="{z}" gripper_position="{gripper_position}"/>
        </Sequence>
    </BehaviorTree>

    <BehaviorTree ID="BaseMovementWithRequest">
        <Sequence>
            <FloatParameterRequest title="Bitte geben Sie x und y in [m] an"
                                   value="{x}" key="x" min="-10" max="10"/>
            <FloatParameterRequest value="{y}" key="y" min="-10" max="10"/>
            <BaseMovement x="{x}" y="{y}"/>
        </Sequence>
    </BehaviorTree>

    <BehaviorTree ID="BatteryChargingWithRequest">
        <Sequence>
            <FloatParameterRequest title="Bitte geben Sie die Zielladung an von 0 [empty] bis 1 [full]"
                                   value="{goal_percentage}" key="goal_percentage" min="0" max="1"/>
            <BatteryCharging goal_percentage="{goal_percentage}"/>
        </Sequence>
    </BehaviorTree>

    <BehaviorTree ID="DevicePairingWithRequest">
        <Sequence>
            <!--pairing_mode und device_type müssen den Konstanten im .action file entsprechen-->
            <IntParameterRequest title="Bitte geben Sie den Kopplungsmodus und das Gerät an"
                                   options="pair,unpair"
                                   value="{pairing_mode}"/>
            <IntParameterRequest options="wheelchair,rollator,hospitalbed,chargingstation"
                                   value="{device_type}"/>
            <DevicePairing pairing_mode="{pairing_mode}" device_type="{device_type}"/>
        </Sequence>
    </BehaviorTree>

    <BehaviorTree ID="Emergency">
        <Sequence>
            <StringParameterRequest title="Emergency finished?" 
                                    msg="type [y] or [n]"
                                    value="{confirm}"/>
            <CheckBlackboard input="{confirm}" compare_to="y" />
        </Sequence>
    </BehaviorTree>

    <BehaviorTree ID="FreeWalking">
        <Sequence>
            <!--room1 = (2,2)-->
            <BaseMovement x="2" y="2"/>
            <StringParameterRequest title="Start walking to destination?" 
                                    msg="type [y] or [n]"
                                    value="{confirm}"/>
            <CheckBlackboard input="{confirm}" compare_to="y" />
            <!--room2 = (5,5)-->
            <BaseMovement x="5" y="5"/>
            <StringParameterRequest title="Patient arrived?" 
                                    msg="type [y] or [n]"
                                    value="{confirm}"/>
            <CheckBlackboard input="{confirm}" compare_to="y" />
            <!--waiting_area = (0,0)-->
            <BaseMovement x="0" y="0"/>
        </Sequence>
    </BehaviorTree>

    <BehaviorTree ID="GuidedWalking">
        <Sequence>
            <!--room1 = (2,2)-->
            <BaseMovement x="2" y="2"/>
            <!--arm_support_position = (200,200,0,0)-->
            <ArmMovement x="200" y="200" z="0" gripper_position="0"/>
            <StringParameterRequest title="Start walking to destination?" 
                                    msg="type [y] or [n]"
                                    value="{confirm}"/>
            <CheckBlackboard input="{confirm}" compare_to="y" />
            <!--room2 = (5,5)-->
            <BaseMovement x="5" y="5"/>
            <StringParameterRequest title="Patient arrived?" 
                                    msg="type [y] or [n]"
                                    value="{confirm}"/>
            <CheckBlackboard input="{confirm}" compare_to="y" />
            <!--arm_home_position = (0,0,0,0)-->
            <ArmMovement x="0" y="0" z="0" gripper_position="0"/>
            <!--waiting_area = (0,0)-->
            <BaseMovement x="0" y="0"/>
        </Sequence>
    </BehaviorTree>

    <BehaviorTree ID="Recharge">
        <Sequence>
            <!--charging_station = (-5,-5)-->
            <BaseMovement x="-5" y="-5"/>
            <!--mode 1 = pair, type 4 = chargingstation-->
            <DevicePairing pairing_mode="1" device_type="4"/>
            <BatteryCharging goal_percentage="1"/>
            <!--mode 2 = unpair, type 4 = chargingstation-->
            <DevicePairing pairing_mode="2" device_type="4"/>
            <!--waiting_area = (0,0)-->
            <BaseMovement x="0" y="0"/>
        </Sequence>
    </BehaviorTree>

    <BehaviorTree ID="TransportWithDevice">
        <Sequence>
            <!--room1 = (2,2)-->
            <BaseMovement x="2" y="2"/>
            <StringParameterRequest title="Start pairing and move to destination?" 
                                    msg="type [y] or [n]"
                                    value="{confirm}"/>
            <CheckBlackboard input="{confirm}" compare_to="y" />
            <IntParameterRequest options="wheelchair,rollator,hospitalbed,chargingstation"
                                   value="{device_type}"/>
            <!--mode 1 = pair-->
            <DevicePairing pairing_mode="1" device_type="{device_type}"/>
            <!--room2 = (5,5)-->
            <BaseMovement x="5" y="5"/>
            <StringParameterRequest title="Patient arrived?" 
                                    msg="type [y] or [n]"
                                    value="{confirm}"/>
            <CheckBlackboard input="{confirm}" compare_to="y" />
            <!--mode 2 = unpair-->
            <DevicePairing pairing_mode="2" device_type="{device_type}"/>
            <!--waiting_area = (0,0)-->
            <BaseMovement x="0" y="0"/>
        </Sequence>
    </BehaviorTree>

    <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
        <ReactiveSequence>
            <CheckStop/>
            <CheckDiagnosticStatus/>
            <Fallback>
                <CheckBattery/>
                <Recharge/>
            </Fallback>
            <Fallback>
                <CheckPatientCondition/>
                <Emergency/>
            </Fallback>
            <Sequence>
                <StringParameterRequest title="Select a behavior"
                                       options="FreeW,GuidedW,TransportWD"
                                       value="{selected_behavior}"/>
                <BehaviorSwitch variable="{selected_behavior}" case_1="FreeW" case_2="GuidedW" case_3="TransportWD">
                    <FreeWalking/>
                    <GuidedWalking/>
                    <TransportWithDevice/>
                    <AlwaysFailureNode name="default"/>
                </BehaviorSwitch>
            </Sequence>
        </ReactiveSequence>
    </BehaviorTree>

    <TreeNodesModel>
        <SubTree ID="BaseMovementWithRequest"/>
        <SubTree ID="ArmMovementWithRequest"/>
        <SubTree ID="BatteryChargingWithRequest"/>
        <SubTree ID="DevicePairingWithRequest"/>
        <SubTree ID="FreeWalking"/>
        <SubTree ID="GuidedWalking"/>
        <SubTree ID="TransportWithDevice"/>
        <SubTree ID="Emergency"/>
        <SubTree ID="Recharge"/>

        <Condition ID="CheckStop"/>
        <Condition ID="CheckBattery"/>
        <Condition ID="CheckDiagnosticStatus"/>
        <Condition ID="CheckPatientCondition"/>
        <Condition ID="CheckBlackboard"/>
            <input_port name="input" type="std::string"/>
            <input_port name="compare_to" type="std::string"/>

        <Decorator ID="BehaviorSwitch"/>

        <Action ID="DevicePairing">
            <input_port name="pairing_mode" type="int"/>
            <input_port name="device_type" type="int"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="ArmMovement">
            <input_port name="x" type="float"/>
            <input_port name="y" type="float"/>
            <input_port name="z" type="float"/>
            <input_port name="gripper" type="float"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="BatteryCharging">
            <input_port name="goal_percentage" type="float"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="BaseMovement">
            <input_port name="x" type="float"/>
            <input_port name="y" type="float"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="StringParameterRequest">
            <output_port name="value" type="std::string"/>
            <input_port name="title" type="std::string"/>
            <input_port name="msg" type="std::string"/>
            <input_port name="key" type="std::string"/>
            <input_port name="min" type="std::string"/>
            <input_port name="max" type="std::string"/>
            <input_port name="default_value" type="std::string"/>
            <input_port name="options" type="std::string"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="FloatParameterRequest">
            <output_port name="value" type="float"/>
            <input_port name="title" type="std::string"/>
            <input_port name="msg" type="std::string"/>
            <input_port name="key" type="std::string"/>
            <input_port name="min" type="std::string"/>
            <input_port name="max" type="std::string"/>
            <input_port name="default_value" type="std::string"/>
            <input_port name="options" type="std::string"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="IntParameterRequest">
            <output_port name="value" type="int"/>
            <input_port name="title" type="std::string"/>
            <input_port name="msg" type="std::string"/>
            <input_port name="key" type="std::string"/>
            <input_port name="min" type="std::string"/>
            <input_port name="max" type="std::string"/>
            <input_port name="default_value" type="std::string"/>
            <input_port name="options" type="std::string"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="AlwaysFailureNode"/>
    </TreeNodesModel>
 </root>
mrq:
The mission describes a patient-assistive mobile manipulator that checks for stop and diagnostics, manages battery and emergencies, and then executes a selected escort or transport routine with interactive confirmations.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (FloatParameterRequest, FloatParameterRequest, FloatParameterRequest, FloatParameterRequest, ArmMovement) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the ROS action FloatParameterRequest to obtain x in millimeters within the specified bounds and will store it as x. It will run the ROS action FloatParameterRequest to obtain y in millimeters within the specified bounds and will store it as y. It will run the ROS action FloatParameterRequest to obtain z in millimeters within the specified bounds and will store it as z. It will run the ROS action FloatParameterRequest to obtain the gripper position between 0 and 1 and will store it as gripper_position. It will run the custom action ArmMovement using the stored x, y, z, and gripper_position after all parameter requests succeed.

Next, it will execute the following actions (FloatParameterRequest, FloatParameterRequest, BaseMovement) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the ROS action FloatParameterRequest to obtain x in meters within the specified bounds and will store it as x. It will run the ROS action FloatParameterRequest to obtain y in meters within the specified bounds and will store it as y. It will run the custom action BaseMovement using the stored x and y after both parameter requests succeed.

Next, it will execute the following actions (FloatParameterRequest, BatteryCharging) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the ROS action FloatParameterRequest to obtain a target battery fraction between 0 and 1 and will store it as goal_percentage. It will run the custom action BatteryCharging using the stored goal_percentage after the parameter request succeeds.

Next, it will execute the following actions (IntParameterRequest, IntParameterRequest, DevicePairing) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the ROS action IntParameterRequest to obtain a pairing mode selection and will store it as pairing_mode. It will run the ROS action IntParameterRequest to obtain a device type selection and will store it as device_type. It will run the custom action DevicePairing using the stored pairing_mode and device_type after both parameter requests succeed.

Next, it will execute the following actions (StringParameterRequest, CheckBlackboard) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the ROS action StringParameterRequest to obtain confirmation about emergency resolution and will store it as confirm. It will run the custom action CheckBlackboard to compare the stored confirm against the value y and will succeed only if they match.

Next, it will execute the following actions (BaseMovement, StringParameterRequest, CheckBlackboard, BaseMovement, StringParameterRequest, CheckBlackboard, BaseMovement) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action BaseMovement to reach coordinates (2, 2). It will run the ROS action StringParameterRequest to request start approval and will store it as confirm. It will run the custom action CheckBlackboard to compare the stored confirm against the value y and will proceed only if they match. It will run the custom action BaseMovement to reach coordinates (5, 5). It will run the ROS action StringParameterRequest to confirm arrival and will store it as confirm. It will run the custom action CheckBlackboard to compare the stored confirm against the value y and will proceed only if they match. It will run the custom action BaseMovement to return to coordinates (0, 0).

Next, it will execute the following actions (BaseMovement, ArmMovement, StringParameterRequest, CheckBlackboard, BaseMovement, StringParameterRequest, CheckBlackboard, ArmMovement, BaseMovement) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action BaseMovement to reach coordinates (2, 2). It will run the custom action ArmMovement to reach an assistive pose with x 200, y 200, z 0, and gripper position 0. It will run the ROS action StringParameterRequest to request start approval and will store it as confirm. It will run the custom action CheckBlackboard to compare the stored confirm against the value y and will proceed only if they match. It will run the custom action BaseMovement to reach coordinates (5, 5). It will run the ROS action StringParameterRequest to confirm arrival and will store it as confirm. It will run the custom action CheckBlackboard to compare the stored confirm against the value y and will proceed only if they match. It will run the custom action ArmMovement to stow the arm at x 0, y 0, z 0 with gripper position 0. It will run the custom action BaseMovement to return to coordinates (0, 0).

Next, it will execute the following actions (BaseMovement, DevicePairing, BatteryCharging, DevicePairing, BaseMovement) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action BaseMovement to reach coordinates (-5, -5). It will run the custom action DevicePairing with pairing_mode 1 and device_type 4 to pair with the charging station. It will run the custom action BatteryCharging to charge to 1. It will run the custom action DevicePairing with pairing_mode 2 and device_type 4 to unpair from the charging station. It will run the custom action BaseMovement to return to coordinates (0, 0).

Next, it will execute the following actions (BaseMovement, StringParameterRequest, CheckBlackboard, IntParameterRequest, DevicePairing, BaseMovement, StringParameterRequest, CheckBlackboard, DevicePairing, BaseMovement) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action BaseMovement to reach coordinates (2, 2). It will run the ROS action StringParameterRequest to request approval for pairing and transport and will store it as confirm. It will run the custom action CheckBlackboard to compare the stored confirm against the value y and will proceed only if they match. It will run the ROS action IntParameterRequest to select a device type and will store it as device_type. It will run the custom action DevicePairing with pairing_mode 1 using the stored device_type. It will run the custom action BaseMovement to reach coordinates (5, 5). It will run the ROS action StringParameterRequest to confirm arrival and will store it as confirm. It will run the custom action CheckBlackboard to compare the stored confirm against the value y and will proceed only if they match. It will run the custom action DevicePairing with pairing_mode 2 using the stored device_type. It will run the custom action BaseMovement to return to coordinates (0, 0).

Finally, it will execute the following actions [CheckStop, CheckDiagnosticStatus, Fallback, Fallback, Sequence] in sequence. If any action fails, stop execution. While a later action is still running, re-evaluate previous actions. It will execute the following actions (CheckBattery, Recharge) consecutively. If any action (CheckBattery) fails, move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will execute the following actions (CheckPatientCondition, Emergency) consecutively. If any action (CheckPatientCondition) fails, move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will execute the following actions (StringParameterRequest, BehaviorSwitch) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the ROS action StringParameterRequest to select a behavior and will store it as selected_behavior. It will read blackboard value and compare it against a set of cases. If a case matches the blackboard value, execute corresponding Action. Else if no case matches, execute default Action. It will execute FreeWalking when selected_behavior equals FreeW. It will execute GuidedWalking when selected_behavior equals GuidedW. It will execute TransportWithDevice when selected_behavior equals TransportWD. It will execute the custom action AlwaysFailureNode when no case matches.

mrq: The mission describes a goal-processing task that computes a target, reports it, stores an alternate target, and reports the stored value in strict order. First, The robot will execute the following actions (CalculateGoal, PrintTarget, SetBlackboard, PrintTarget) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action CalculateGoal with the provided GoalPosition and will produce an updated goal value. It will run the custom action PrintTarget to output the GoalPosition after the goal calculation succeeds. It will run the custom action SetBlackboard to store the value “-1;3” under the key OtherGoal after the target is printed. It will run the custom action PrintTarget to output the value stored in OtherGoal after the blackboard is updated.

It will run the custom action PlaceSimple using the stored pose to release the object at the target location after the approach succeeds.

mrq:
The mission describes a two-phase task that first collects at two waypoints and then dumps at an available location under a fallback strategy.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (collectTree, dumpTree) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will read the subtree collectTree and then read the subtree dumpTree in strict order.

Next, it will execute the following actions (go1, go2) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action go1 as the first collection step. It will run the custom action go2 as the second collection step after the first collection step succeeds.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (go1, go2) consecutively. If any action (go1) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will run the custom action go1 as the preferred dump step. It will run the custom action go2 as the alternative dump step only if the preferred dump step fails.

<!--
  This Behavior Tree replans the global path periodically at 1 Hz and it also has
  recovery actions.
-->
<root main_tree_to_execute="MainTree">
  <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
    <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="6" name="NavigateRecovery">
      <PipelineSequence name="NavigateWithReplanning">
        <RateController hz="1.0">
          <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="1" name="ComputePathToPose">
            <ComputePathToPose goal="{goal}" path="{path}" planner_id="GridBased"/>
            <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearGlobalCostmap-Context" service_name="global_costmap/clear_entirely_global_costmap"/>
          </RecoveryNode>
        </RateController>
        <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="1" name="FollowPath">
          <FollowPath path="{path}" controller_id="FollowPath"/>
          <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearLocalCostmap-Context" service_name="local_costmap/clear_entirely_local_costmap"/>
        </RecoveryNode>
      </PipelineSequence>
      <ReactiveFallback name="RecoveryFallback">
        <GoalUpdated/>
        <SequenceStar name="RecoveryActions">
          <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearLocalCostmap-Subtree" service_name="local_costmap/clear_entirely_local_costmap"/>
          <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearGlobalCostmap-Subtree" service_name="global_costmap/clear_entirely_global_costmap"/>
          <Spin spin_dist="1.57"/>
          <Wait wait_duration="5"/>
        </SequenceStar>
      </ReactiveFallback>
    </RecoveryNode>
  </BehaviorTree>
</root>
mrq:
The mission describes a navigation task that computes a global path at 1 Hz, follows the path, and performs recoveries when failures occur during planning or control.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (NavigateWithReplanning, RecoveryFallback) using a recovery pattern. It will execute main NavigateWithReplanning. If it fails, it will execute recovery action RecoveryFallback. After the recovery action completes, it will retry the main NavigateWithReplanning. If the recovery action fails and retries are exhausted, it will stop execution.

Next, it will execute the following actions (RateController, FollowPath) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. While an action is running or has succeeded, it will go to the next action. It will run the custom action RateController that enforces a 1.0 Hz cadence on the subsequent planning step. It will execute the following actions (ComputePathToPose) using a recovery pattern. It will execute main ComputePathToPose. If it fails, it will execute recovery action ClearEntireCostmap-Context. After the recovery action completes, it will retry the main ComputePathToPose. If the recovery action fails and retries are exhausted, it will stop execution. It will then execute the following actions (FollowPath, ClearEntireCostmap-Context) using a recovery pattern. It will execute main FollowPath. If it fails, it will execute recovery action ClearEntireCostmap-Context. After the recovery action completes, it will retry the main FollowPath. If the recovery action fails and retries are exhausted, it will stop execution.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (GoalUpdated, RecoveryActions) in order. If any action succeeds, it will stop execution. Else it will keep executing and re-evaluating previous actions whenever an action is running. It will run the custom action GoalUpdated to check if a new goal is available and will succeed immediately when a new goal arrives. It will execute the following actions (ClearLocalCostmap-Subtree, ClearGlobalCostmap-Subtree, Spin, Wait) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will run the custom action ClearLocalCostmap-Subtree to clear the local costmap. It will run the custom action ClearGlobalCostmap-Subtree to clear the global costmap after the local clear succeeds. It will run the custom action Spin with a spin distance of 1.57 after the global clear succeeds. It will run the custom action Wait with a duration of 5 seconds after the spin succeeds.

                                    msg="type [y] or [n]"
                                    value="{confirm}"/>

            <IntParameterRequest options="wheelchair,rollator,hospitalbed,chargingstation"
                                   value="{device_type}"/>
            <!--mode 1 = pair-->

            <!--room2 = (-9,-5)-->
            <BaseMovement x="-9" y="-5"/>
            <StringParameterRequest title="Patient arrived?" 
                                    msg="type [y] or [n]"
                                    value="{confirm}"/>
            <CheckBlackboard input="{confirm}" compare_to="y" />
            <!--mode 2 = unpair-->
            <DevicePairing pairing_mode="2" device_type="{device_type}"/>
            <!--waiting_area = (0,0)-->

mrq: The mission describes a transport assistance task that moves between locations, pairs with a mobility device, escorts it to a destination, confirms arrival, unpairs, and returns to a waiting area in strict order. First, the robot will execute the following actions (BaseMovement, StringParameterRequest, CheckBlackboard, IntParameterRequest, DevicePairing, BaseMovement, StringParameterRequest, CheckBlackboard, DevicePairing, BaseMovement) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action BaseMovement to reach coordinates x=4.5 and y=-7 as the initial location. It will run the custom action StringParameterRequest to prompt the operator with the title “Start pairing and move to destination?” and the message “type [y] or [n]” and will store the response in confirm. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule by comparing confirm to the expected value y. It will run the custom action IntParameterRequest to request a selection from options “wheelchair, rollator, hospitalbed, chargingstation” and will store the result in device_type. It will run the custom action DevicePairing with pairing_mode set to 1 and device_type set to the selected value to initiate pairing. Next, it will run the custom action BaseMovement to reach coordinates x=-9 and y=-5 as the destination location. It will run the custom action StringParameterRequest to prompt the operator with the title “Patient arrived?” and the message “type [y] or [n]” and will store the response in confirm. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule by comparing confirm to the expected value y. Finally, it will run the custom action DevicePairing with pairing_mode set to 2 and device_type set to the previously selected value to initiate unpairing. It will run the custom action BaseMovement to reach coordinates x=0 and y=0 as the waiting area.

mrq: The mission describes a navigation task that continuously recomputes a global path at 1 Hz while advancing to path following. First, the robot will execute the following actions (RateController, FollowPath) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute the following actions (ComputePathToPose) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails while throttled by a 1.0 Hz rate controller. It will run the ROS action ComputePathToPose with the goal provided and will store the resulting path at the enforced rate. It will, while an action is running or has succeeded, go to the next action. It will run the ROS action FollowPath using the most recently stored path with the specified controller.

The mission describes a warehouse manipulation task that verifies required objects, plans or replans actions based on object locations, and executes a move–pick–move–place sequence with recovery, while asking for help if preconditions cannot be met.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (ReactiveSequence, AskForHelpAction) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute the following actions [CheckObjQtySrv, SubTree(Robot_Task_Behaviour)] in sequence. If SubTree(Robot_Task_Behaviour) fails, stop execution. While SubTree(Robot_Task_Behaviour) is still running, re-evaluate previous actions. It will run the custom action CheckObjQtySrv to confirm two WhitePawn and one BlackPawn are detected and will write the result and sku_id to the blackboard. It will run the custom action SubTree(Robot_Task_Behaviour) to continue the task if the quantity check keeps succeeding during reevaluations. If the preceding ReactiveSequence fails to deliver a success, it will execute the custom action AskForHelpAction and will request human help using the provided text and language.

Next, it will execute the following actions [Fallback, Fallback] in sequence. If Fallback fails, stop execution. While Fallback is still running, re-evaluate previous actions. It will execute the following actions (CheckObjLocationSrv, SubTree(Replan_Task)) consecutively. If any action (CheckObjLocationSrv) fails, move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will run the custom action CheckObjLocationSrv to verify the current locations for the sku_ids stored on the blackboard and will store any resolved location names. If CheckObjLocationSrv does not succeed, it will run the custom action SubTree(Replan_Task) to compute alternative locations. It will execute the following actions (TaskCompleteSrv, SubTree(Task_BT)) consecutively. If any action (TaskCompleteSrv) fails, move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will run the custom action TaskCompleteSrv to check whether all sku_ids are already at final location C. If TaskCompleteSrv does not succeed, it will run the custom action SubTree(Task_BT) to perform the required actions.

Next, it will execute the following actions [SubTree(MOVE1), SubTree(PICK2), SubTree(MOVE3), SubTree(PLACE4)] in sequence. If SubTree(MOVE1) fails, stop execution. While SubTree(PLACE4) is still running, re-evaluate previous actions. It will run the custom action SubTree(MOVE1) to reach the first pose. It will run the custom action SubTree(PICK2) to execute the grasp. It will run the custom action SubTree(MOVE3) to reach the second pose. It will run the custom action SubTree(PLACE4) to execute the placement.

Next, it will execute the following actions (ReactiveSequence, GenerateAlarm_and_SetFailure) consecutively. If any action (ReactiveSequence) fails, move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will execute the following actions [Fallback, Fallback] in sequence. If Fallback fails, stop execution. While Fallback is still running, re-evaluate previous actions. It will execute the following actions (MovePointAvailable, FindNewPoint) consecutively. If any action (MovePointAvailable) fails, move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will run the custom action MovePointAvailable to verify a valid navigation goal is available. If MovePointAvailable does not succeed, it will run the custom action FindNewPoint to propose a new navigation goal. It will execute the following actions (RobotAtPoint, MoveBaseModActionClient) consecutively. If any action (RobotAtPoint) fails, move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will run the custom action RobotAtPoint to check if it is already at the target pose. If RobotAtPoint does not succeed, it will run the custom action MoveBaseModActionClient to navigate to x 2.7086 y 5.2084 with the given quaternion and will store the result. If the preceding ReactiveSequence and its fallbacks do not yield success, it will run the custom action GenerateAlarm_and_SetFailure to raise an alarm and propagate failure.

Next, it will execute the following actions (ReactiveSequence, GenerateAlarm_and_SetFailure) consecutively. If any action (ReactiveSequence) fails, move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will execute the following actions [Fallback, Fallback] in sequence. If Fallback fails, stop execution. While Fallback is still running, re-evaluate previous actions. It will execute the following actions (CheckObjPose, ReplanMotion) consecutively. If any action (CheckObjPose) fails, move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will run the custom action CheckObjPose to verify its current pose against the given initial pose and will store the current pose. If CheckObjPose does not succeed, it will run the custom action ReplanMotion to compute a corrected approach. It will execute the following actions (DummyCondition, TrajArmTorsoGripActionClient) consecutively. If any action (DummyCondition) fails, move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will run the custom action DummyCondition to gate the pick action. If DummyCondition does not succeed, it will run the ROS action TrajArmTorsoGripActionClient to execute the provided joint trajectory for the arm, torso, and gripper and will store the result. If the preceding ReactiveSequence and its fallbacks do not yield success, it will run the custom action GenerateAlarm_and_SetFailure to raise an alarm and propagate failure.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (ReactiveSequence, GenerateAlarm_and_SetFailure) consecutively. If any action (ReactiveSequence) fails, move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will execute the following actions [Fallback, Fallback] in sequence. If Fallback fails, stop execution. While Fallback is still running, re-evaluate previous actions. It will execute the following actions (MovePointAvailable, FindNewPoint) consecutively. If any action (MovePointAvailable) fails, move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will run the custom action MovePointAvailable to verify a valid navigation goal is available. If MovePointAvailable does not succeed, it will run the custom action FindNewPoint to propose a new navigation goal. It will execute the following actions (RobotAtPoint, MoveBaseModActionClient) consecutively. If any action (RobotAtPoint) fails, move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will run the custom action RobotAtPoint to check if it is already at the target pose. If RobotAtPoint does not succeed, it will run the custom action MoveBaseModActionClient to navigate to x 4.4370 y 4.0136 with the given quaternion and will store the result. If the preceding ReactiveSequence and its fallbacks do not yield success, it will run the custom action GenerateAlarm_and_SetFailure to raise an alarm and propagate failure.
