<?xml version="1.0"?>
<root main_tree_to_execute="BehaviorTree">
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="BehaviorTree">
        <Fallback name="Priorities">
            <Inverter>
                <Fallback name="Avoid Collisions">
                    <Condition ID="Condition" name="No Obstacle Ahead?"/>
                    <Fallback>
                        <Action ID="Behaviour" name="Replan Around Obstacle"/>
                        <Action ID="Behaviour" name="Emergency Brake"/>
                    </Fallback>
                </Fallback>
            </Inverter>
            <Fallback name="Road Features">
                <SubTree ID="Intersection"/>
                <SubTree ID="Roundabout"/>
                <SubTree ID="Stop"/>
            </Fallback>
            <Fallback name="">
                <Inverter>
                    <Fallback>
                        <Condition ID="Condition" name="Not Slowed by Car in Front?"/>
                        <Fallback>
                            <SubTree ID="Multi-Lane"/>
                            <SubTree ID="Single-Lane"/>
                        </Fallback>
                    </Fallback>
                </Inverter>
                <Sequence>
                    <Condition ID="Condition" name="Right Lane Available?"/>
                    <Condition ID="Condition" name="Not Slowed by Car in Front Right?"/>
                    <Action ID="Behaviour" name="Wait for Right Lane Free"/>
                    <Action ID="Behaviour" name="Switch Lane Right"/>
                </Sequence>
            </Fallback>
            <Action ID="Behaviour" name="Cruise"/>
        </Fallback>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="Intersection">
        <Sequence>
            <Condition ID="Condition" name="Intersection Ahead"/>
            <Sequence>
                <Action ID="Behaviour" name="Approach Intersection"/>
                <Action ID="Behaviour" name="Wait"/>
                <Action ID="Behaviour" name="Enter Intersection"/>
                <Action ID="Behaviour" name="Leave Intersection"/>
            </Sequence>
        </Sequence>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="Multi-Lane">
        <Fallback>
            <Sequence>
                <Condition ID="Condition" name="Multi-Lane?"/>
                <Condition ID="Condition" name="Left Lane available?"/>
                <Action ID="Behaviour" name="Wait for left lane free"/>
                <Action ID="Behaviour" name="switch lane to left"/>
            </Sequence>
        </Fallback>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="Roundabout">
        <Sequence>
            <Condition ID="Condition" name="Roundabout Ahead?"/>
            <Sequence>
                <Action ID="Behaviour" name="Approach Roundabout"/>
                <Action ID="Behaviour" name="Wait"/>
                <Action ID="Behaviour" name="Enter"/>
                <Action ID="Behaviour" name="Leave"/>
            </Sequence>
        </Sequence>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="Single-Lane">
        <Sequence>
            <Condition ID="Condition" name="Single Lane with dotted Line?"/>
            <Action ID="Behaviour" name="Wait Left Lane Free"/>
            <Action ID="Behaviour" name="Switch Lane Left"/>
            <Fallback>
                <Sequence>
                    <Condition ID="Condition" name="Overtaking Possible"/>
                    <Action ID="Behaviour" name="Overtake"/>
                    <Action ID="Behaviour" name="Switch Lane Right"/>
                </Sequence>
                <Action ID="Behaviour" name="Switch Lane Right"/>
            </Fallback>
        </Sequence>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="Stop">
        <Sequence>
            <Condition ID="Condition" name="Stop Ahead?"/>
            <Sequence>
                <Action ID="Behaviour" name="Approach"/>
                <Action ID="Behaviour" name="Wait"/>
                <Action ID="Behaviour" name="Go"/>
            </Sequence>
        </Sequence>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <TreeNodesModel>
        <Action ID="Behaviour"/>
        <Condition ID="Condition"/>
        <SubTree ID="Intersection"/>
        <SubTree ID="Multi-Lane"/>
        <SubTree ID="Roundabout"/>
        <SubTree ID="Single-Lane"/>
        <SubTree ID="Stop"/>
    </TreeNodesModel>
    <!-- ////////// -->
</root>
mrq:
The mission describes an on-road driving task that prioritizes collision avoidance, handles road features, manages car-following and lane changes, and defaults to cruising when no higher-priority behavior applies. First, the robot will execute the following actions (Avoid Collisions, Road Features, car-following and lane-change management, Cruise) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. It will execute inverted Avoid Collisions and make sure it always returns the opposite outcome of its internal evaluation. It will execute the following actions (No Obstacle Ahead?, obstacle handling) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for the condition No Obstacle Ahead?. It will execute the following actions (Replan Around Obstacle, Emergency Brake) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. It will execute the custom action Replan Around Obstacle when obstacle handling is needed. It will execute the custom action Emergency Brake if replanning does not succeed.

Next, it will execute the following actions (Intersection, Roundabout, Stop) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. It will execute the following actions (Intersection Ahead, intersection maneuvers) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. While executing intersection maneuvers, it will execute the following actions (Approach Intersection, Wait, Enter Intersection, Leave Intersection) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute the following actions (Roundabout Ahead?, roundabout maneuvers) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. While executing roundabout maneuvers, it will execute the following actions (Approach Roundabout, Wait, Enter, Leave) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute the following actions (Stop Ahead?, stop maneuvers) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. While executing stop maneuvers, it will execute the following actions (Approach, Wait, Go) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails.

Next, it will execute the following actions (inverted car-following fallback, right-lane change sequence) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. It will execute inverted car-following fallback and make sure it always returns the opposite outcome of its internal evaluation. It will execute the following actions (Not Slowed by Car in Front?, lane-selection fallback) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for the condition Not Slowed by Car in Front?. It will execute the following actions (Multi-Lane, Single-Lane) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. It will execute the following actions (Multi-Lane?, Left Lane available?, Wait for left lane free, switch lane to left) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for the condition Multi-Lane?. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for the condition Left Lane available?. It will execute the custom action Wait for left lane free before the left lane change. It will execute the custom action switch lane to left to complete the left lane change. It will execute the following actions (Single Lane with dotted Line?, Wait Left Lane Free, Switch Lane Left, overtaking fallback) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for the condition Single Lane with dotted Line?. It will execute the custom action Wait Left Lane Free to prepare for an overtake. It will execute the custom action Switch Lane Left to move into the opposing lane. It will execute the following actions (overtake sequence, Switch Lane Right) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. It will execute the following actions (Overtaking Possible, Overtake, Switch Lane Right) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for the condition Overtaking Possible. It will execute the custom action Overtake to pass the slower vehicle. It will execute the custom action Switch Lane Right to return to the original lane. It will execute the following actions (Right Lane Available?, Not Slowed by Car in Front Right?, Wait for Right Lane Free, Switch Lane Right) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for the condition Right Lane Available?. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for the condition Not Slowed by Car in Front Right?. It will execute the custom action Wait for Right Lane Free to prepare for a rightward merge. It will execute the custom action Switch Lane Right to complete the rightward merge.

Finally, it will execute the custom action Cruise when no higher-priority behavior succeeds.

<root main_tree_to_execute = "PegTree" >
    <BehaviorTree ID="PegTree">
    <ReactiveFallback name="root_sequence">
        <ReactiveSequence name="goal_condition">
            <EEPositionDistance name="goal_check"
                threshold="0.05"
                target_x="-0.6"
                target_y="0.0"
                target_z="0.64"
                ee_x="{ee_x}"
                ee_y="{ee_y}"
                ee_z="{ee_z}"/>
            <Parallel name="mg_config_goal_and_check" success_threshold="1" failure_threshold="1">
                <SetMGSin   name="mg_sinus"
                    mp_ampl="0"
                    mp_freq="0"
                    mg_ampl="{mg_ampl}"
                    mg_freq="{mg_freq}"/>
                <SetMGGoal   name="mg_goal1"
                    mp_param_x="-0.6"
                    mp_param_y="0.0"
                    mp_param_z="0.73"
                    mg_trans_x="{mg_trans_x}"
                    mg_trans_y="{mg_trans_y}"
                    mg_trans_z="{mg_trans_z}"/>
                <EEPositionDistance name="goal_check"
                    threshold="0.05"
                    target_x="-0.6"
                    target_y="0.0"
                    target_z="0.64"
                    ee_x="{ee_x}"
                    ee_y="{ee_y}"
                    ee_z="{ee_z}"/>
            </Parallel>
        </ReactiveSequence>
        <ReactiveSequence name="search_motion">
            <EEZThreshold   name="ee_z_threshold" threshold="{z_threshold}" ee_z="{ee_z}" above="false"/>
                <SetMGSin   name="mg_sinus"
                    mp_ampl="{mp_ampl}"
                    mp_freq="{mp_freq}"
                    mg_ampl="{mg_ampl}"
                    mg_freq="{mg_freq}"/>
        </ReactiveSequence>
        <Parallel name="mg_config" success_threshold="1" failure_threshold="1">
            <SetMGSin   name="mg_sinus"
                mp_ampl="0"
                mp_freq="0"
                mg_ampl="{mg_ampl}"
                mg_freq="{mg_freq}"/>
            <SetMGGoal   name="mg_goal1"
                mp_param_x="{mp_1_x}"
                mp_param_y="{mp_1_y}"
                mp_param_z="{mp_1_z}"
                mg_trans_x="{mg_trans_x}"
                mg_trans_y="{mg_trans_y}"
                mg_trans_z="{mg_trans_z}"/>
        </Parallel>
    </ReactiveFallback>
    </BehaviorTree>
</root>

The mission describes a peg-in-hole manipulation task that regulates motion goal configuration, executes a search motion when contact depth is reached, and finalizes goal approach using reactive evaluation.

First, the robot will execute the following actions [goal_condition] in sequence. If [goal_condition] fails, stop execution. While [goal_condition] is still running, re-evaluate previous actions. It will run the custom action EEPositionDistance to check whether the end-effector position is within 0.05 meters of the target at coordinates (-0.6, 0.0, 0.64) using the current end-effector pose. It will execute the following actions [SetMGSin, SetMGGoal, EEPositionDistance] simultaneously. If either a success threshold is reached or all actions succeed, the execution was successful. Else if any action fails, the execution fails. It will run the custom action SetMGSin to set motion generator sinusoidal amplitude to 0 and frequency to 0 with outputs stored to mg_ampl and mg_freq. It will run the custom action SetMGGoal to set the motion generator goal translation to (-0.6, 0.0, 0.73) with outputs stored to mg_trans_x, mg_trans_y, and mg_trans_z. It will run the custom action EEPositionDistance again to verify the end-effector is within 0.05 meters of the same target position using the current end-effector pose.

Next, it will execute the following actions [search_motion] in sequence. If [search_motion] fails, stop execution. While [search_motion] is still running, re-evaluate previous actions. It will run the custom action EEZThreshold to check that the end-effector z value is below the provided z_threshold. It will run the custom action SetMGSin to configure the motion generator with sinusoidal amplitude mp_ampl and frequency mp_freq with outputs stored to mg_ampl and mg_freq.

Finally, it will execute the following actions [SetMGSin, SetMGGoal] simultaneously. If either a success threshold is reached or all actions succeed, the execution was successful. Else if any action fails, the execution fails. It will run the custom action SetMGSin to set motion generator sinusoidal amplitude to 0 and frequency to 0 with outputs stored to mg_ampl and mg_freq. It will run the custom action SetMGGoal to set the motion generator goal translation to (mp_1_x, mp_1_y, mp_1_z) with outputs stored to mg_trans_x, mg_trans_y, and mg_trans_z.

<?xml version="1.0"?>
<root main_tree_to_execute="BehaviorTree">
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="BehaviorTree">
        <Parallel threshold="1">
            <Repeat num_cycles="-1">
                <Action ID="VariantAction" value="{variant}"/>
            </Repeat>
            <Sequence>
                <Action ID="peoplerecognition"/>
                <Action ID="moveRoboterPosition" approachRadius="200" x="5630" y="-12000"/>
                <Switch2 case_1="one" case_2="two" variable="{variant}">
                    <Fallback name="SearchThomas">
                        <Fallback name="Room1">
                            <Sequence name="SearchRoom1">
                                <Action ID="moveRoboterPosition" approachRadius="200" name="Door2" x="7520" y="-12000"/>
                                <Action ID="moveRoboterPosition" approachRadius="200" x="7520" y="-9040"/>
                                <Action ID="getPersonPose" personname="THOMAS" x="{x_}" y="{y_}" z="{z_}"/>
                                <Action ID="moveRoboterPosition" approachRadius="500" name="MoveToPerson" x="{x_}" y="{y_}"/>
                                <Action ID="moveRoboterPosition" approachRadius="200" name="Door2" x="7520" y="-12000"/>
                            </Sequence>
                            <ForceFailure>
                                <Action ID="moveRoboterPosition" approachRadius="200" name="Door2" x="7520" y="-12000"/>
                            </ForceFailure>
                        </Fallback>
                        <Fallback name="Room2">
                            <Sequence name="SearchRoom4">
                                <Action ID="moveRoboterPosition" approachRadius="200" name="Door4" x="14500" y="-12000"/>
                                <Action ID="moveRoboterPosition" approachRadius="200" x="14400" y="-9640"/>
                                <Action ID="getPersonPose" personname="THOMAS" x="{x_}" y="{y_}" z="{z_}"/>
                                <Action ID="moveRoboterPosition" approachRadius="500" name="MoveToPerson" x="{x_}" y="{y_}"/>
                                <Action ID="moveRoboterPosition" approachRadius="200" name="Door4" x="14500" y="-12000"/>
                            </Sequence>
                            <ForceFailure>
                                <Action ID="moveRoboterPosition" approachRadius="200" name="Door4" x="14500" y="-12000"/>
                            </ForceFailure>
                        </Fallback>
                        <Sequence name="Recharge">
                            <Action ID="moveRoboterPosition" approachRadius="200" name="Door8" x="23200" y="-12000"/>
                            <Action ID="moveRoboterPosition" approachRadius="10" name="GotoCharger" x="23200" y="-14800"/>
                        </Sequence>
                    </Fallback>
                </Switch2>
            </Sequence>
        </Parallel>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <TreeNodesModel>
        <Action ID="VariantAction">
            <output_port default="{variant}" name="value"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="activateLocalization"/>
        <Action ID="approachLocation">
            <input_port default="1" name="locationId"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="approachWall"/>
        <Action ID="deactivateLocalization"/>
        <Action ID="getBaseCharging">
            <output_port default="{charging}" name="charging"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="getPersonPose">
            <input_port default="MARIA" name="personname"/>
            <output_port default="{x_}" name="x"/>
            <output_port default="{y_}" name="y"/>
            <output_port default="{z_}" name="z"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="initNavigation"/>
        <Action ID="loadMap"/>
        <Action ID="loadNavigationMap">
            <input_port default="navigation-map" name="mapname"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="moveRoboterBackwardsLocation"/>
        <Action ID="moveRoboterBackwardsRelative"/>
        <Action ID="moveRoboterOrientateRobotAbs"/>
        <Action ID="moveRoboterOrientateRobotRel"/>
        <Action ID="moveRoboterPosition">
            <input_port default="50" name="approachRadius"/>
            <input_port default="0" name="x"/>
            <input_port default="0" name="y"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="peoplerecognition"/>
        <Action ID="setRobotPose">
            <input_port default="0" name="x"/>
            <input_port default="0" name="y"/>
            <input_port default="0" name="yaw"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="startJoystickNavigation"/>
        <Action ID="startReactiveNavigation"/>
        <Action ID="stopJoystickNavigation"/>
        <Action ID="stopReactiveNavigation"/>
        <Action ID="triggerGlobalLocalization"/>
    </TreeNodesModel>
    <!-- ////////// -->
</root>

The mission describes a parallel search-and-approach task that continuously updates a selection variable while it recognizes people, moves to a staging position, and then branches by variant to search for a specific person in designated rooms or recharge.

First, it will execute the following actions (VariantAction) simultaneously with other actions. It will execute VariantAction for a set number of times or infinitely. It will execute VariantAction continuously until it fails or succeeds. Else it will keep executing VariantAction forever.

Next, the robot will execute the following actions (peoplerecognition, moveRoboterPosition, Switch2) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action peoplerecognition. It will run the custom action moveRoboterPosition to the coordinates x=5630 and y=-12000 with an approachRadius of 200. It will read blackboard value and compare it against a set of cases. If a case matches the blackboard value, it will execute corresponding action. Else if no case matches, it will execute default action.

Next, it will read blackboard value and compare it against a set of cases. If a case matches the blackboard value, it will execute corresponding action. Else if no case matches, it will execute default action. It will execute the following actions (SearchThomas) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails.

Next, it will execute the following actions (Room1, Room2, Recharge) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails.

Next, it will execute the following actions (SearchRoom1, ForceFailure) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will execute the following actions (moveRoboterPosition, moveRoboterPosition, getPersonPose, moveRoboterPosition, moveRoboterPosition) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action moveRoboterPosition to the coordinates x=7520 and y=-12000 with an approachRadius of 200. It will run the custom action moveRoboterPosition to the coordinates x=7520 and y=-9040 with an approachRadius of 200. It will run the custom action getPersonPose for the personname THOMAS and will store the resulting x, y, and z. It will run the custom action moveRoboterPosition to the stored coordinates with an approachRadius of 500. It will run the custom action moveRoboterPosition to the coordinates x=7520 and y=-12000 with an approachRadius of 200 after returning from the person location. It will execute moveRoboterPosition and make sure it always fails.

Next, it will execute the following actions (SearchRoom4, ForceFailure) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will execute the following actions (moveRoboterPosition, moveRoboterPosition, getPersonPose, moveRoboterPosition, moveRoboterPosition) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action moveRoboterPosition to the coordinates x=14500 and y=-12000 with an approachRadius of 200. It will run the custom action moveRoboterPosition to the coordinates x=14400 and y=-9640 with an approachRadius of 200. It will run the custom action getPersonPose for the personname THOMAS and will store the resulting x, y, and z. It will run the custom action moveRoboterPosition to the stored coordinates with an approachRadius of 500. It will run the custom action moveRoboterPosition to the coordinates x=14500 and y=-12000 with an approachRadius of 200 after returning from the person location. It will execute moveRoboterPosition and make sure it always fails.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (moveRoboterPosition, moveRoboterPosition) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action moveRoboterPosition to the coordinates x=23200 and y=-12000 with an approachRadius of 200. It will run the custom action moveRoboterPosition to the coordinates x=23200 and y=-14800 with an approachRadius of 10.

<root main_tree_to_execute="MainTree">
  <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
    <PipelineSequence name="NavigateWithReplanning">
      <SpeedController min_rate="0.1" max_rate="1.0" min_speed="0.0" max_speed="0.26" filter_duration="0.3">
        <ComputePathToPose goal="{goal}" path="{path}" planner_id="GridBased"/>
      </SpeedController>
      <FollowPath path="{path}"  controller_id="FollowPath"/>
    </PipelineSequence>
  </BehaviorTree>
</root>
mrq: The mission describes a navigation task that computes a path to a goal at a controlled rate and then follows that path while advancing as soon as actions are running or succeed. First, the robot will execute the following actions (SpeedController, FollowPath) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will, while an action is running or has succeeded, go to the next action. It will run the custom action SpeedController to regulate the execution rate between 0.1 Hz and 1.0 Hz, clamp speed between 0.0 m/s and 0.26 m/s, and apply a 0.3 s filter to its child. It will run the ROS action ComputePathToPose inside the SpeedController using the provided goal and will store the resulting path. <root main_tree_to_execute="MainTree">
  <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
    <SequenceStar name="NavigateWithReplanning">
      <SplitGoal goal="{goal}"
        get_out_teb_need="{getOutTebNeed}" get_out_teb_goal="{getOutTebGoal}"
        nominal_teb_need="{nominalTebNeed}" nominal_teb_goal="{nominalTebGoal}"
        get_in_teb_need="{getInTebNeed}" get_in_teb_goal="{getInTebGoal}"/>

      <Fallback>
        <Inverter>
          <BooleanCondition boolean="{getOutTebNeed}"/>
        </Inverter>

        <PipelineSequence>
          <RateController hz="2.0">
            <ComputePathToPose goal="{getOutTebGoal}" path="{pathGetOut}" planner_id="GridBased"/>
          </RateController>
          <FollowPath path="{pathGetOut}" controller_id="AccurateController"/>
        </PipelineSequence>
      </Fallback>

      <Fallback>
        <Inverter>
          <BooleanCondition boolean="{nominalTebNeed}"/>
        </Inverter>

        <PipelineSequence>
          <RateController hz="2.0">
            <ComputePathToPose goal="{nominalTebGoal}" path="{pathNominal}" planner_id="GridBased"/>
          </RateController>
          <FollowPath path="{pathNominal}" controller_id="NominalController"/>
        </PipelineSequence>
      </Fallback>

      <Fallback>
        <Inverter>
          <BooleanCondition boolean="{getInTebNeed}"/>
        </Inverter>
        <PipelineSequence>
          <RateController hz="2.0">
            <ComputePathToPose goal="{getInTebGoal}" path="{pathGetIn}" planner_id="GridBased"/>
          </RateController>
          <FollowPath path="{pathGetIn}" controller_id="AccurateController"/>
        </PipelineSequence>
      </Fallback>
    </SequenceStar>
  </BehaviorTree>
</root>

The mission describes a navigation task that splits a goal into staged subgoals and conditionally executes path planning and following with periodic replanning. First, the robot will execute the following actions (SplitGoal, Fallback for get-out stage, Fallback for nominal stage, Fallback for get-in stage) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will run the custom action SplitGoal using the provided goal and will write getOutTebNeed, getOutTebGoal, nominalTebNeed, nominalTebGoal, getInTebNeed, and getInTebGoal on the blackboard.

Next, it will execute the following actions (Inverter over BooleanCondition getOutTebNeed, PipelineSequence for get-out stage) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will execute inverted Find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for getOutTebNeed. If the BooleanCondition for getOutTebNeed succeeds, the inverter will reverse it to fail. Else if the BooleanCondition for getOutTebNeed fails, the inverter will reverse it to succeed. It will execute the following actions (RateController over ComputePathToPose, FollowPath) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. While an action is running or has succeeded, it will go to the next action. It will run the ROS action ComputePathToPose at 2.0 Hz using getOutTebGoal and will store the resulting path in pathGetOut. It will run the ROS action FollowPath using pathGetOut with the AccurateController after the planning tick either runs or succeeds.

Next, it will execute the following actions (Inverter over BooleanCondition nominalTebNeed, PipelineSequence for nominal stage) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will execute inverted Find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for nominalTebNeed. If the BooleanCondition for nominalTebNeed succeeds, the inverter will reverse it to fail. Else if the BooleanCondition for nominalTebNeed fails, the inverter will reverse it to succeed. It will execute the following actions (RateController over ComputePathToPose, FollowPath) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. While an action is running or has succeeded, it will go to the next action. It will run the ROS action ComputePathToPose at 2.0 Hz using nominalTebGoal and will store the resulting path in pathNominal. It will run the ROS action FollowPath using pathNominal with the NominalController after the planning tick either runs or succeeds.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (Inverter over BooleanCondition getInTebNeed, PipelineSequence for get-in stage) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will execute inverted Find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for getInTebNeed. If the BooleanCondition for getInTebNeed succeeds, the inverter will reverse it to fail. Else if the BooleanCondition for getInTebNeed fails, the inverter will reverse it to succeed. It will execute the following actions (RateController over ComputePathToPose, FollowPath) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. While an action is running or has succeeded, it will go to the next action. It will run the ROS action ComputePathToPose at 2.0 Hz using getInTebGoal and will store the resulting path in pathGetIn. It will run the ROS action FollowPath using pathGetIn with the AccurateController after the planning tick either runs or succeeds.

The mission describes a repeated engagement task that monitors an object’s tracking status, announces the status, and performs a timed expression in a strict order.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (ForceSuccess wrapper of a detection announcement sequence, a delayed smile sequence, ForceSuccess wrapper of a non-detection announcement sequence, a delayed smile sequence) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute the ForceSuccess wrapper and make sure it always succeeds. It will run the custom action ObjectTrackerStatusAction with a required state of 1 for at least 3 seconds. It will run the custom action TextToSpeechActionClient with the message “Detected” after the tracker reports the required state. It will execute the delayed smile sequence in order and stop this inner sequence if any action fails. It will execute delayed SmileAction. If execution succeeds, it will start a delay timer of 3000 milliseconds. After the delay period is exhausted, it will resume execution and complete the smile sequence. It will execute the ForceSuccess wrapper and make sure it always succeeds. It will run the custom action ObjectTrackerStatusAction with a required state of 0 for at least 4 seconds. It will run the custom action TextToSpeechActionClient with the message “Not Detected” after the tracker reports the required state. It will execute the delayed smile sequence in order and stop this inner sequence if any action fails. It will execute delayed SmileAction. If execution succeeds, it will start a delay timer of 3000 milliseconds. After the delay period is exhausted, it will resume execution and complete the smile sequence.

Next, it will execute the Repeat decorator and execute the prior action block for a set number of times or infinitely. It will execute the full sequence continuously until it fails or succeeds. Else it will keep executing the full sequence forever.

<root main_tree_to_execute="BehaviorTree">
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="BehaviorTree">
        <Repeat num_cycles="{num_cycles}">
            <Sequence>
                <Fallback>
                    <Action ID="LTLPreCheck" ltl_state_current="{ltl_state_current}" ltl_state_desired_sequence="{ltl_state_desired_sequence}" ltl_state_executed_sequence="{ltl_state_executed_sequence}"/>
                    <ForceFailure>
                        <Action ID="ReplanningRequestLevel2" ltl_state_current="{ltl_state_current}" ltl_state_executed_sequence="{ltl_state_executed_sequence}" replanning_request="{replanning_request}"/>
                    </ForceFailure>
                </Fallback>
                <ReactiveSequence>
                    <Fallback>
                        <RetryUntilSuccesful num_attempts="3">
                            <Fallback>
                                <Action ID="LocomotionStatusCheck" locomotion_status="{locomotion_status}"/>
                                <Sequence>
                                    <Action ID="RecoveryStand" locomotion_status="{locomotion_status}"/>
                                    <Delay delay_msec="2000">
                                        <Action ID="LocomotionStart" locomotion_status="{locomotion_status}"/>
                                    </Delay>
                                </Sequence>
                            </Fallback>
                        </RetryUntilSuccesful>
                        <ForceFailure>
                            <Action ID="ReplanningRequestLevel1" ltl_state_current="{ltl_state_current}" ltl_state_executed_sequence="{ltl_state_executed_sequence}" replanning_request="{replanning_request}"/>
                        </ForceFailure>
                    </Fallback>
                    <Fallback>
                        <Switch3 case_1="move" case_2="stay" case_3="synchronized_transition" variable="{bt_action_type}">
                            <Action ID="MoveAction" bt_action_type="{bt_action_type}" current_action="{current_action}" goal_sent="{goal_sent}" move_base_finished="{move_base_finished}" move_base_idle="{move_base_idle}"/>
                            <Action ID="StayAction" bt_action_type="{bt_action_type}" current_action="{current_action}" ltl_state_current="{ltl_state_current}"/>
                            <Action ID="SynchronizedTransitionAction" bt_action_type="{bt_action_type}" current_action="{current_action}" ltl_state_current="{ltl_state_current}"/>
                            <AlwaysFailure/>
                        </Switch3>
                        <ForceFailure>
                            <Action ID="ReplanningRequestLevel3" ltl_state_current="{ltl_state_current}" ltl_state_executed_sequence="{ltl_state_executed_sequence}" replanning_request="{replanning_request}"/>
                        </ForceFailure>
                    </Fallback>
                </ReactiveSequence>
                <Action ID="UpdateLTL" action_sequence="{action_sequence}" action_sequence_executed="{action_sequence_executed}" bt_action_type="{bt_action_type}" current_action="{current_action}" ltl_state_desired_sequence="{ltl_state_desired_sequence}"/>
            </Sequence>
        </Repeat>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <TreeNodesModel>
        <Action ID="LTLPreCheck">
            <input_port name="ltl_state_current"/>
            <input_port name="ltl_state_desired_sequence"/>
            <inout_port name="ltl_state_executed_sequence"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="LocomotionStart">
            <input_port name="locomotion_status"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="LocomotionStatusCheck">
            <input_port name="locomotion_status"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="MoveAction">
            <input_port name="bt_action_type"/>
            <input_port name="current_action"/>
            <output_port name="goal_sent"/>
            <input_port name="move_base_finished"/>
            <input_port name="move_base_idle"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="RecoveryStand">
            <input_port name="locomotion_status"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="ReplanningRequestLevel1">
            <input_port name="ltl_state_current"/>
            <inout_port name="ltl_state_executed_sequence"/>
            <inout_port name="replanning_request"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="ReplanningRequestLevel2">
            <input_port name="ltl_state_current"/>
            <inout_port name="ltl_state_executed_sequence"/>
            <inout_port name="replanning_request"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="ReplanningRequestLevel3">
            <input_port name="ltl_state_current"/>
            <inout_port name="ltl_state_executed_sequence"/>
            <inout_port name="replanning_request"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="StayAction">
            <input_port name="bt_action_type"/>
            <input_port name="current_action"/>
            <inout_port name="ltl_state_current"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="SynchronizedTransitionAction">
            <input_port name="bt_action_type"/>
            <input_port name="current_action"/>
            <inout_port name="ltl_state_current"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="UpdateLTL">
            <inout_port name="action_sequence"/>
            <inout_port name="action_sequence_executed"/>
            <output_port name="bt_action_type"/>
            <output_port name="current_action"/>
            <inout_port name="ltl_state_desired_sequence"/>
        </Action>
    </TreeNodesModel>
    <!-- ////////// -->
</root>

The mission describes a repeated execution cycle that validates LTL prerequisites, ensures locomotion readiness, dispatches an action based on an action type switch, and updates the LTL state.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (LTLPreCheck, ForceFailure(ReplanningRequestLevel2)) consecutively. It will execute the following actions (LTLPreCheck, ForceFailure(ReplanningRequestLevel2)) consecutively. If any action (LTLPreCheck) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will run the custom action LTLPreCheck to verify the current LTL state against the desired sequence and the executed sequence. It will execute ReplanningRequestLevel2 and make sure it always fails, which will force the Fallback to consider alternative actions only if LTLPreCheck fails.

Next, it will execute the following actions [Fallback, Fallback] in sequence. If any action fails, it will stop execution. While any action is still running, it will re-evaluate previous actions. It will execute the following actions (RetryUntilSuccesful(Fallback(LocomotionStatusCheck, Sequence(RecoveryStand, Delay(LocomotionStart)))), ForceFailure(ReplanningRequestLevel1)) consecutively. If any action (RetryUntilSuccesful(Fallback(LocomotionStatusCheck, Sequence(RecoveryStand, Delay(LocomotionStart))))) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will keep trying to execute Fallback(LocomotionStatusCheck, Sequence(RecoveryStand, Delay(LocomotionStart))) until it succeeds. It will execute the following actions (LocomotionStatusCheck, Sequence(RecoveryStand, Delay(LocomotionStart))) consecutively. If any action (LocomotionStatusCheck) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will run the custom action LocomotionStatusCheck to confirm that locomotion is ready. It will execute the following actions (RecoveryStand, Delay(LocomotionStart)) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action RecoveryStand to establish a stable stance. It will execute delayed LocomotionStart. If execution succeeds, it will start a 2000 ms delay timer. After the delay period is exhausted, it will resume execution and complete the start of locomotion. It will execute ReplanningRequestLevel1 and make sure it always fails, which will keep the Fallback from succeeding on the replanning branch.

Next, it will execute the following actions (Switch, ForceFailure(ReplanningRequestLevel3)) consecutively. If any action (Switch) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will read a blackboard value and compare it against a set of cases. If a case matches the blackboard value, it will execute the corresponding action. Else if no case matches, it will execute the default action. It will run the custom action MoveAction when the value is move. It will run the custom action StayAction when the value is stay. It will run the custom action SynchronizedTransitionAction when the value is synchronized_transition. It will execute the default action that always fails if no case matches. It will execute ReplanningRequestLevel3 and make sure it always fails, which will keep the Fallback from succeeding on the replanning branch.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (UpdateLTL) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action UpdateLTL to record the executed action, to update the current action type, and to synchronize the desired LTL sequence with the execution outcome. It will execute UpdateLTL for a set number of times or infinitely.

mrq:
The mission describes a repeated decision-and-execution loop that validates a temporal logic plan, selects and performs an action reactively, and then updates the plan state.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (Fallback, ReactiveSequence, UpdateLTL) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute the following actions (LTLPreCheck, ForceFailure(ReplanningRequestLevel2)) consecutively. If any action (LTLPreCheck) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will execute ReplanningRequestLevel2 and make sure it always fails.

Next, it will execute the following actions (Fallback, Fallback) in sequence. If any action fails, it will stop execution. While an action is still running, it will re-evaluate previous actions. It will execute the following actions (ReactiveLTLStateCheck, ForceFailure(ReplanningRequestLevel2)) consecutively. If any action (ReactiveLTLStateCheck) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will execute ReplanningRequestLevel2 and make sure it always fails. It will execute the following actions (Switch4, ForceFailure(ReplanningRequestLevel3)) consecutively. If any action (Switch4) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will read blackboard value and compare it against a set of cases. If a case matches the blackboard value, it will execute the corresponding action. Else if no case matches, it will execute the default action. It will execute the custom action MoveAction when the case is move. It will execute the custom action StayAction when the case is stay. It will execute the custom action GuideAction when the case is start_training. It will execute the custom action BackNormalAction when the case is terminate_training. It will execute the default action AlwaysFailure when no case matches. It will execute ReplanningRequestLevel3 and make sure it always fails.

Finally, it will execute the custom action UpdateLTL. It will then execute UpdateLTL for a set number of times or infinitely.

<root main_tree_to_execute="MainTree">
  <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
    <PipelineSequence name="NavigateWithReplanning">
      <RateController hz="1.0">
        <Sequence>
          <GoalUpdater input_goal="{goal}" output_goal="{updated_goal}">
            <ComputePathToPose goal="{updated_goal}" path="{path}" planner_id="GridBased"/>
          </GoalUpdater>
         <TruncatePath distance="1.0" input_path="{path}" output_path="{truncated_path}"/>
        </Sequence>
      </RateController>
      <KeepRunningUntilFailure>
        <FollowPath path="{truncated_path}" controller_id="FollowPath"/>
      </KeepRunningUntilFailure>
    </PipelineSequence>
  </BehaviorTree>
</root>
mrq:
The mission describes a navigation task that continuously replans toward a moving goal and follows a truncated path to maintain a fixed standoff distance.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (RateController, KeepRunningUntilFailure) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. While an action is running or has succeeded, it will go to the next action. It will apply the RateController at 1 Hz to its inner sequence before advancing. It will then keep executing the FollowPath action as long as it does not fail.

Next, it will execute the following actions (GoalUpdater, TruncatePath) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action GoalUpdater to refresh the input goal and to provide an updated goal to downstream actions. It will run the ROS action ComputePathToPose within the GoalUpdater using the updated goal and will store the resulting path. It will run the custom action TruncatePath to shorten the computed path to a distance of 1.0 meter and will output the truncated path.

Finally, it will execute FollowPath. If FollowPath succeeds, it will restart it. Else if FollowPath fails, it will stop looping and continue execution. It will run the ROS action FollowPath using the truncated path with the specified controller.

<root main_tree_to_execute="MainTree">
  <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
    <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="6" name="NavigateRecovery">
      <PipelineSequence name="NavigateWithReplanning">
        <RateController hz="0.1">
          <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="1" name="ComputePathToPose">
            <ComputePathToPose goal="{goal}" path="{path}" planner_id="GridBased"/>
            <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearGlobalCostmap-Context" service_name="global_costmap/clear_entirely_global_costmap"/>
          </RecoveryNode>
        </RateController>
        <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="100" name="FollowPath">
          <FollowPath path="{path}" controller_id="FollowPath"/>
          <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearLocalCostmap-Context" service_name="local_costmap/clear_entirely_local_costmap"/>
        </RecoveryNode>
      </PipelineSequence>
      <ReactiveFallback name="RecoveryFallback">
        <GoalUpdated/>
        <SequenceStar name="RecoveryActions">
          <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearLocalCostmap-Subtree" service_name="local_costmap/clear_entirely_local_costmap"/>
          <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearGlobalCostmap-Subtree" service_name="global_costmap/clear_entirely_global_costmap"/>
        </SequenceStar>
      </ReactiveFallback>
    </RecoveryNode>
  </BehaviorTree>
</root>
mrq:
The mission describes a navigation task that computes a global path at a controlled rate and follows it while employing layered recovery behaviors.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (RateController, RecoveryNode, RecoveryNode) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. While an action is running or has succeeded, it will go to the next action. It will execute the ROS action RateController at 0.1 Hz to gate replanning frequency and will let subsequent actions proceed when the controller reports running or success. It will execute main the ROS action ComputePathToPose and, if it fails, will execute recovery action the ROS action ClearEntireCostmap and then retry the main action once. It will execute main the ROS action FollowPath and, if it fails, will execute recovery action the ROS action ClearEntireCostmap and then retry the main action up to 100 times.

Next, it will execute main the PipelineSequence subtree NavigateWithReplanning. If it fails, it will execute recovery action the ReactiveFallback subtree RecoveryFallback. After recovery, it will retry main action the PipelineSequence subtree NavigateWithReplanning. If the recovery action fails and retries are exhausted after six attempts, it will stop execution.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (GoalUpdated, SequenceStar) in order. If GoalUpdated succeeds, it will stop execution. Else it will keep executing and re-evaluating previous actions whenever an action is running. It will execute the following actions (ClearLocalCostmap-Subtree, ClearGlobalCostmap-Subtree) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning.

It will set a blackboard key named Right to the value l;2. It will set a blackboard key named Left to the value j;2. It will execute MoveRobot responses continuously until they fail or succeed and else keep executing them forever. It will execute Execute [Action] and make sure it always succeeds while wrapping the following decision. It will execute the following actions (Sequence detecting cat and reacting, Sequence detecting dog and reacting, Sequence detecting cow and reacting) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, it will start all over again until an action succeeds. It will execute the following actions (OpenVINOEvent object=cat, reaction sequence for cat) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule by detecting the object cat. It will execute the following actions (MoveRobot Foward, MoveRobot Stop, MoveRobot Backward, MoveRobot Stop) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute the following actions (OpenVINOEvent object=dog, reaction sequence for dog) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule by detecting the object dog. It will execute the following actions (MoveRobot Backward, MoveRobot Stop, MoveRobot Foward, MoveRobot Stop) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute the following actions (OpenVINOEvent object=cow, reaction sequence for cow) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule by detecting the object cow. It will execute the following actions (MoveRobot Left, MoveRobot Stop, MoveRobot Right, MoveRobot Stop) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails.

Next, the mission describes the MoveRobot subtree that converts a target key into a velocity command. It will execute the following actions (SendCommandVel) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. <root main_tree_to_execute="MainTree">
  <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
    <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="2" name="NavigateRecovery">
      <PipelineSequence name="NavigateWithReplanning">
        <RateController hz="1.0">
          <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="4" name="ComputePathToPose">
            <ComputePathToPose goal="{goal}" path="{path}" planner_id="GridBased"/>
            <ReactiveFallback name="PathToPoseFallback">
              <GoalUpdated/>
              <RoundRobin name="GlobalPlannerRecoveryActions">
                <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearGlobalCostmap-Context" service_name="global_costmap/clear_entirely_global_costmap"/>
                <Wait wait_duration="2"/>
              </RoundRobin>
            </ReactiveFallback>
          </RecoveryNode>
        </RateController>
        <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="4" name="FollowPath">
          <FollowPath path="{path}" controller_id="FollowPath"/>
          <ReactiveFallback name="FollowPathFallback">
            <GoalUpdated/>
            <RoundRobin name="PlannerRecoveryActions">
              <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearLocalCostmap-Context" service_name="local_costmap/clear_entirely_local_costmap"/>
              <Spin spin_dist="1.57"/>
            </RoundRobin>
          </ReactiveFallback>
        </RecoveryNode>
      </PipelineSequence>
      <ReactiveFallback name="RecoveryFallback">
        <GoalUpdated/>
        <Wait wait_duration="5"/>
      </ReactiveFallback>
    </RecoveryNode>
  </BehaviorTree>
</root>
mrq: The mission describes a navigation task that periodically replans a global path at 1 Hz and performs recovery actions when path computation or path following fails. First, the robot will execute the following actions (RateController, ComputePathToPose, FollowPath) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails, and while an action is running or has succeeded, it will go to the next action. It will execute delayed RateController and, if execution succeeds, it will start a 1 Hz delay timer and, after each delay period is exhausted, it will resume execution to trigger periodic replanning. It will execute main the ROS action ComputePathToPose and, if it fails, it will execute recovery action (ReactiveFallback) and, after recovery, it will retry main action (the ROS action ComputePathToPose) up to four times and, if recovery action (ReactiveFallback) fails and retries are exhausted, it will stop execution of this subtask. It will execute the following actions (GoalUpdated, RoundRobin) in order and, if GoalUpdated succeeds, it will stop execution, else it will keep executing and re-evaluating previous actions whenever an action is running to provide reactive recovery for path computation. It will execute the custom action RoundRobin for a set number of times or infinitely to iterate over recovery behaviors for global planning. It will run the ROS action ClearEntireCostmap with the global costmap service to attempt to clear obstacles from the global costmap. It will run the ROS action Wait with a duration of 2 seconds to allow the global planner to stabilize after clearing. Next, it will execute main the ROS action FollowPath and, if it fails, it will execute recovery action (ReactiveFallback) and, after recovery, it will retry main action (the ROS action FollowPath) up to four times and, if recovery action (ReactiveFallback) fails and retries are exhausted, it will stop execution of this subtask. It will execute the following actions (GoalUpdated, RoundRobin) in order and, if GoalUpdated succeeds, it will stop execution, else it will keep executing and re-evaluating previous actions whenever an action is running to provide reactive recovery for path following. It will execute the custom action RoundRobin for a set number of times or infinitely to iterate over recovery behaviors for local control. It will run the ROS action ClearEntireCostmap with the local costmap service to attempt to clear obstacles from the local costmap. It will run the custom action Spin with a rotation distance of 1.57 to help the local planner escape from oscillations. Finally, it will execute main the PipelineSequence and, if it fails, it will execute recovery action (ReactiveFallback) and, after recovery, it will retry main action (the PipelineSequence) up to two times and, if recovery action (ReactiveFallback) fails and retries are exhausted, it will stop execution of the overall navigation task. It will execute the following actions (GoalUpdated, Wait) in order and, if GoalUpdated succeeds, it will stop execution, else it will keep executing and re-evaluating previous actions whenever an action is running to provide reactive recovery for the overall navigation. It will run the ROS action Wait with a duration of 5 seconds to allow environmental or goal conditions to change before retrying navigation.

            <input_port default="execute_all_poses" name="service_name"/>
            <input_port default="1" name="stamps">Time stamps for each group reaching the goal, separate with space</input_port>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="ExecuteGroupAngularJointStates">
            <input_port default="0 0 0 0 0 0 0" name="goal"/>
            <input_port default="panda_arm" name="group_name"/>
            <input_port default="execute_group_joint_states" name="service_name"/>
            <input_port default="0.01" name="tolerance"/>

        <Action ID="ExecuteGroupLinearJointStates">

            <input_port default="hand" name="group_name"/>
            <input_port default="execute_group_joint_states" name="service_name"/>

        <Action ID="ExecuteGroupPlan">

            <input_port default="panda_arm" name="group_name"/>
            <input_port default="0" name="is_absolute"/>

            <input_port default="execute_group_plan" name="service_name"/>

        <Action ID="ExecuteGroupPose">

            <input_port default="0" name="goal_type"/>
            <input_port default="panda_arm" name="group_name"/>

mrq: The mission describes a coordinated arm manipulation sequence that prepares both arms of the walker and then executes synchronized target poses. First, the robot will execute the following actions (ExecuteGroupAngularJointStates, ExecuteGroupAngularJointStates, ExecuteAllPoses) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action ExecuteGroupAngularJointStates to drive the walker_left_arm joints to the specified angular targets with the given tolerance using the execute_group_joint_states service. It will run the custom action ExecuteGroupAngularJointStates to drive the walker_right_arm joints to the specified angular targets with the given tolerance using the execute_group_joint_states service after the left arm reaches its targets. It will run the custom action ExecuteAllPoses to command the walker_left_arm and walker_right_arm to reach their respective 6D poses with collision disabled according to the provided time stamps using the execute_all_poses service after both arms are prepared.

<root main_tree_to_execute="BehaviorTree">
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="BehaviorTree">
        <Sequence>
            <Action ID="Print" message="Hello"/>
            <Fallback>
                <Timeout msec="2000">
                    <Action ID="AlwaysRunning"/>
                </Timeout>
                <Sequence>
                    <Condition ID="ConditionCheck" topic="/condition"/>
                    <Action ID="Print" message="Baby"/>
                </Sequence>
                <Action ID="Print" message="World"/>
            </Fallback>
            <Fallback>
                <Sequence>
                    <Condition ID="MessageCheck" topic="/trigger"/>
                    <Action timeout="100" ID="Trigger" service_name="safety"/>
                </Sequence>
                <Action ID="Wait" duration="3000"/>
            </Fallback>
            <Action ID="Print" message="Done"/>
        </Sequence>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <TreeNodesModel>
        <Action ID="AlwaysRunning"/>
        <Condition ID="ConditionCheck">
            <input_port name="topic"/>
        </Condition>
        <Condition ID="MessageCheck">
            <input_port name="topic"/>
        </Condition>
        <Action ID="Print">
            <input_port type="std::string" name="message"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="Trigger">
            <input_port type="std::string" name="service_name">name of the ROS service</input_port>
            <input_port type="unsigned int" name="timeout">timeout to connect to server (milliseconds)</input_port>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="Wait">
            <input_port name="duration">Waiting for duration (ms)</input_port>
        </Action>
    </TreeNodesModel>
    <!-- ////////// -->
</root>
mrq:
The mission describes a linear task that prints a greeting, selects one of several message branches, performs a trigger or waits based on a message, and then prints completion in strict order.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (Print, Fallback, Fallback, Print) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action Print with the message Hello before attempting any branch. It will then evaluate the first Fallback to decide which message branch to execute. It will then evaluate the second Fallback to decide whether to trigger a safety service or wait. It will finally run the custom action Print with the message Done after the previous branches complete.

Next, it will execute the following actions (Timeout, Sequence, Print) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will execute child node, but only for a maximum duration. If the custom action AlwaysRunning fails to execute within specified time limit, it will interrupt the execution and stop it. It will execute the following actions (ConditionCheck, Print) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. While checking a condition for each action is not used in this node. If condition ID is true for the custom condition ConditionCheck on topic /condition, it will run the custom action Print with the message Baby. Else if condition ID is false, the sequence will fail and the Fallback will continue. If both previous actions fail, it will run the custom action Print with the message World.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (Sequence, Wait) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will execute the following actions (MessageCheck, Trigger) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. If condition ID is true for the custom condition MessageCheck on topic /trigger, it will run the custom action Trigger using the service safety with a connection timeout of 100 milliseconds. Else if condition ID is false, the sequence will fail and the Fallback will continue. If the sequence fails, it will run the custom action Wait for a duration of 3000 milliseconds.

mrq:
The mission describes a sequential task that executes ordered actions with nested sequences and a fallback to manage success and failure conditions.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (go1, Sequence2, go2, go3, Sequence4) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action go1 before attempting the next action. It will run the nested subtree Sequence2 only after go1 succeeds. It will run the custom action go2 only after Sequence2 succeeds. It will run the custom action go3 only after go2 succeeds. It will run the nested subtree Sequence4 only after go3 succeeds.

Next, it will execute the following actions (stay1, stay2, Sequence3) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action stay1 before attempting the next action. It will run the custom action stay2 only after stay1 succeeds. It will run the nested subtree Sequence3 only after stay2 succeeds.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (go1, go2, Fallback) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action go1 before attempting the next action. It will run the custom action go2 only after go1 succeeds. It will execute the following actions (Inverter(go4), ForceSuccess(go2), cIName) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails.

Next, it will execute the following actions (go1, go2, isFull, go3) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action go1 before attempting the next action. It will run the custom action go2 only after go1 succeeds. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for isFull, and it will proceed only if the check succeeds. It will run the custom action go3 only after the isFull check succeeds.

Next, it will execute inverted go4. If go4 succeeds, it will reverse it to fail. Else if go4 fails, it will reverse it to succeed.

Next, it will execute go2 and make sure it always succeeds.

Next, it will run the custom action cIName after the fallback selects it as the succeeding option only if both the inverted go4 and the forced-success go2 fail to succeed.

<root main_tree_to_execute = "MainTree">

    <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
        <Fallback>
            <Sequence>
                <IsDoorOpen/>
                <PassThroughDoor/>
            </Sequence>
            <SubTree ID="DoorClosed"/>
            <PassThroughWindow/>
        </Fallback>
    </BehaviorTree>

    <BehaviorTree ID="DoorClosed">
        <Sequence>
            <IsDoorClosed/>
            <RetryUntilSuccesful num_attempts="4">
                <OpenDoor/>
            </RetryUntilSuccesful>
            <PassThroughDoor/>
        </Sequence>
    </BehaviorTree>

    <BehaviorTree ID="IsDoorClosed">
        <Inverter>
            <IsDoorOpen/>
        </Inverter>
    </BehaviorTree>

</root>
mrq:
The mission describes an access task that attempts to pass through an opening using a prioritized set of options with recovery when the door is closed.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (IsDoorOpen, PassThroughDoor) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for the custom action IsDoorOpen. It will run the custom action PassThroughDoor only after the door open check succeeds. Next, it will execute the following actions (DoorClosed, PassThroughWindow) consecutively. If any action (DoorClosed) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will run the subtree DoorClosed as the first fallback option. It will run the custom action PassThroughWindow as the final fallback option if the subtree DoorClosed fails.

Next, it will execute the following actions (IsDoorClosed, RetryUntilSuccesful, PassThroughDoor) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute inverted custom action IsDoorOpen. If IsDoorOpen succeeds, it will reverse it to fail. Else if IsDoorOpen fails, it will reverse it to succeed. It will keep trying to execute the custom action OpenDoor until it succeeds. If try succeeds, it will stop retrying and continue. If try fails, it will keep retrying until it succeeds. It will run the custom action PassThroughDoor only after the door has been opened successfully.

Finally, it will execute inverted custom action IsDoorOpen. If IsDoorOpen succeeds, it will reverse it to fail. Else if IsDoorOpen fails, it will reverse it to succeed.

mrq:
The mission describes a puck-handling task that collects multiple pucks, weighs them, navigates to an accelerator location, and drops pucks into the accelerator in strict order.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (MoveAhead, Turn, GetPuck, GetPuck, GetPuck, GetPuck, GetPuck, GetPuck) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action MoveAhead with forward motion for a distance of 778 units. It will run the custom action Turn with a right 90-degree rotation. It will execute the subtree GetPuck with the provided distance of 485 units. It will execute the subtree GetPuck with the provided distance of 100 units. It will execute the subtree GetPuck with the provided distance of 100 units. It will execute the subtree GetPuck with the provided distance of 100 units. It will execute the subtree GetPuck with the provided distance of 100 units. It will execute the subtree GetPuck with the provided distance of 100 units.

Next, it will execute the following actions (MoveAhead, GrabPuck, ActiverBarillet) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails as the subtree GetPuck. It will run the custom action MoveAhead with forward motion for the parameterized distance dist. It will execute the subtree GrabPuck to pick up a puck and stage it for storage. It will run the custom action ActiverBarillet to index the barrel after the grab.

Next, it will run the custom action MoveAX_5 with a left 90-degree joint motion. It will run the custom action MoveAX_3 to approach the puck with a joint motion. It will run the custom action ActivatePump to create suction for gripping. It will run the custom action MoveAX_3 to retract the end effector with a joint motion. It will run the custom action MoveAX_5 with a right 90-degree joint motion to reorient. It will run the custom action MoveAX_4 to approach the barrel with a joint motion. It will run the custom action ActivatePump to maintain grip or confirm suction. It will run the custom action MoveAX_4 to retract from the barrel with a joint motion.

Next, it will execute the following actions (ActiverBarillet, MoveAhead, Repeat) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails as the subtree WeightPucks. It will run the custom action ActiverBarillet to position the next puck for weighing. It will run the custom action MoveAhead with forward motion for a distance of 828 units. It will execute Repeat for a set number of times by running its child actions five times.

Next, it will run the custom action MoveAX_1 to push a puck with a joint motion once per repetition. It will run the custom action MoveAX_1 to return the pusher with a joint motion once per repetition. It will run the custom action ActiverBarillet to index the barrel once per repetition.

Next, it will execute the following actions (Turn, MoveAhead, Turn, MoveAhead, Turn, MoveAhead) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails as the subtree ReachAccelerator. It will run the custom action Turn with a right 90-degree rotation. It will run the custom action MoveAhead with forward motion for an unspecified distance. It will run the custom action Turn with a left 90-degree rotation. It will run the custom action MoveAhead with forward motion for an unspecified distance. It will run the custom action Turn with a right 90-degree rotation. It will run the custom action MoveAhead with forward motion for an unspecified distance.

Finally, it will run the custom action MoveAX_1 to push a puck with a joint motion as the subtree DropInAccelerator. It will run the custom action MoveAX_1 to retract the pusher with a joint motion after the drop.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (CollectPucks, WeightPucks, ReachAccelerator, DropInAccelerator) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails as the MainTree. It will run the subtree CollectPucks to acquire multiple pucks. It will run the subtree WeightPucks to process and weigh pucks. It will run the subtree ReachAccelerator to navigate to the accelerator. It will run the subtree DropInAccelerator to release pucks into the accelerator.

The mission describes a navigation task that continuously replans a global path at 1 Hz, follows the path, and performs targeted and global recovery behaviors when needed.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (RateController, RecoveryNode(name=ComputePathToPose), RecoveryNode(name=FollowPath)) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. While an action is running or has succeeded, it will go to the next action. It will execute the ROS action RateController at 1.0 Hz to pace the enclosed action. It will execute main the ROS action ComputePathToPose. If it fails, it will execute recovery action the ROS action ClearEntireCostmap(name=ClearGlobalCostmap-Context). After recovery, it will retry main action the ROS action ComputePathToPose. If recovery action the ROS action ClearEntireCostmap(name=ClearGlobalCostmap-Context) fails and retries are exhausted, it will stop execution of this recovery scope. It will execute main the ROS action FollowPath. If it fails, it will execute recovery action the ROS action ClearEntireCostmap(name=ClearLocalCostmap-Context). After recovery, it will retry main action the ROS action FollowPath. If recovery action the ROS action ClearEntireCostmap(name=ClearLocalCostmap-Context) fails and retries are exhausted, it will stop execution of this recovery scope.

Next, it will execute main the PipelineSequence(name=NavigateWithReplanning). If it fails, it will execute recovery action the ReactiveFallback(name=RecoveryFallback). After recovery, it will retry main action the PipelineSequence(name=NavigateWithReplanning). If recovery action the ReactiveFallback(name=RecoveryFallback) fails and retries are exhausted, it will stop execution. It will execute the following actions (GoalUpdated, SequenceStar(name=RecoveryActions)) in order. If an action succeeds, it will stop execution. Else it will keep executing and re-evaluating previous actions whenever an action is running. It will execute the ROS action GoalUpdated and will stop if the goal update succeeds. It will execute the following actions (ClearLocalCostmap-Subtree, ClearGlobalCostmap-Subtree, Spin, Wait) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will execute the ROS action ClearEntireCostmap(name=ClearLocalCostmap-Subtree) to clear the local costmap. It will execute the ROS action ClearEntireCostmap(name=ClearGlobalCostmap-Subtree) to clear the global costmap. It will execute the custom action Spin with a spin distance of 1.57. It will execute the custom action Wait with a wait duration of 5 seconds.

mrq:
The mission describes a navigation task that periodically replans a global path through multiple goals and follows that path while applying layered recoveries for planning, control, and general system issues.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (NavigateWithReplanning, RecoveryFallback) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. While an action is running or has succeeded, it will go to the next action. It will execute main NavigateWithReplanning. If it fails, it will execute recovery action RecoveryFallback. After recovery, it will retry main action NavigateWithReplanning. If recovery action RecoveryFallback fails and retries are exhausted, it will stop execution.

Next, it will execute the following actions (ComputePathThroughPoses, FollowPath) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. While an action is running or has succeeded, it will go to the next action. It will throttle downstream planning to approximately once every three seconds. It will execute main ComputePathThroughPoses. If it fails, it will execute recovery action ComputePathThroughPosesRecoveryFallback. After recovery, it will retry main action ComputePathThroughPoses. If recovery action ComputePathThroughPosesRecoveryFallback fails and retries are exhausted, it will stop execution for this recovery scope.

Next, it will execute the following actions (RemovePassedGoals, ComputePathThroughPoses) in sequence. If RemovePassedGoals fails, it will stop execution. While ComputePathThroughPoses is still running, it will re-evaluate RemovePassedGoals. It will run the custom action RemovePassedGoals to filter out achieved goals within a 0.5 meter radius. It will run the ROS action ComputePathThroughPoses with the provided ordered goals and will store the resulting path after goal filtering succeeds.

Next, it will execute the following actions (GoalUpdated, ClearGlobalCostmap-Context) in order. If GoalUpdated succeeds, it will stop execution. Else it will keep executing and re-evaluating previous actions whenever an action is running. It will check for a new goal and will finish this fallback if a goal update is detected. It will run the ROS action ClearEntireCostmap named ClearGlobalCostmap-Context on the global costmap if no goal update is detected.

Next, it will execute main FollowPath. If it fails, it will execute recovery action FollowPathRecoveryFallback. After recovery, it will retry main action FollowPath. If recovery action FollowPathRecoveryFallback fails and retries are exhausted, it will stop execution for this recovery scope. It will run the ROS action FollowPath using the stored path with the specified controller when a valid path is available.

Next, it will execute the following actions (GoalUpdated, ClearLocalCostmap-Context) in order. If GoalUpdated succeeds, it will stop execution. Else it will keep executing and re-evaluating previous actions whenever an action is running. It will check for a new goal and will finish this fallback if a goal update is detected. It will run the ROS action ClearEntireCostmap named ClearLocalCostmap-Context on the local costmap if no goal update is detected.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (GoalUpdated, RecoveryActions) in order. If GoalUpdated succeeds, it will stop execution. Else it will keep executing and re-evaluating previous actions whenever an action is running. It will check for a new goal and will finish this fallback if a goal update is detected. It will iterate through RecoveryActions in a round-robin pattern to attempt progressive system recovery when no new goal is detected.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (ClearingActions, Spin, Wait, BackUp) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, it will start all over again until an action succeeds. It will execute the following actions (ClearLocalCostmap-Subtree, ClearGlobalCostmap-Subtree) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the ROS action ClearEntireCostmap named ClearLocalCostmap-Subtree on the local costmap. It will run the ROS action ClearEntireCostmap named ClearGlobalCostmap-Subtree on the global costmap after the local clear succeeds. It will run the ROS action Spin to rotate by 1.57 radians if clearing does not succeed. It will run the ROS action Wait for five seconds if spinning does not succeed. It will run the ROS action BackUp to move backward 0.15 meters at 0.025 meters per second if waiting does not succeed.
