            </ReactiveFallback>
          </RecoveryNode>
        </RateController>
        <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="4" name="FollowPath">
          <FollowPath path="{path}" controller_id="FollowPath"/>
          <ReactiveFallback name="FollowPathFallback">
            <GoalUpdated/>
            <RoundRobin name="PlannerRecoveryActions">
              <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearLocalCostmap-Context" service_name="local_costmap/clear_entirely_local_costmap"/>
              <Spin spin_dist="1.57"/>
            </RoundRobin>
          </ReactiveFallback>
        </RecoveryNode>
      </PipelineSequence>
      <ReactiveFallback name="RecoveryFallback">
        <GoalUpdated/>
        <Wait wait_duration="5"/>
      </ReactiveFallback>
    </RecoveryNode>
  </BehaviorTree>
</root>
mrq:
The mission describes a navigation task that continuously replans a global path at 1 Hz and executes recovery behaviors when planning or control actions fail.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (RateController, RecoveryNode) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. While an action is running or has succeeded, it will go to the next action. It will execute delayed RateController. If execution succeeds, it will start a delay timer for a 1.0 Hz tick and will resume execution after each period. It will then execute main the ROS action ComputePathToPose. If it fails, it will execute recovery action (ReactiveFallback). After recovery, it will retry main action (the ROS action ComputePathToPose). If recovery action (ReactiveFallback) fails and retries are exhausted, it will stop execution.

Next, it will execute the following actions (ComputePathToPose, ReactiveFallback) consecutively. If any action (ComputePathToPose) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, it will start all over again until an action succeeds. It will execute the ROS action ComputePathToPose with the goal provided and will store the resulting path. It will execute the following actions [GoalUpdated, RoundRobin] in order. If GoalUpdated succeeds, it will stop execution. Else it will keep executing and re-evaluating previous actions whenever an action is running. It will run the custom action GoalUpdated and will return success if the navigation goal changed. It will execute the custom action RoundRobin and will cycle through the recovery actions ClearEntireCostmap-Context and Wait. It will run the ROS action ClearEntireCostmap-Context using the global costmap clearing service. It will run the custom action Wait for a duration of 2 seconds.

Next, it will execute the following actions (RecoveryNode) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. While an action is running or has succeeded, it will go to the next action. It will execute main the ROS action FollowPath. If it fails, it will execute recovery action (ReactiveFallback). After recovery, it will retry main action (the ROS action FollowPath). If recovery action (ReactiveFallback) fails and retries are exhausted, it will stop execution.

Next, it will execute the following actions (FollowPath, ReactiveFallback) consecutively. If any action (FollowPath) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, it will start all over again until an action succeeds. It will run the ROS action FollowPath using the stored path with the specified controller. It will execute the following actions [GoalUpdated, RoundRobin] in order. If GoalUpdated succeeds, it will stop execution. Else it will keep executing and re-evaluating previous actions whenever an action is running. It will run the custom action GoalUpdated and will return success if the navigation goal changed. It will execute the custom action RoundRobin and will cycle through the recovery actions ClearLocalCostmap-Context and Spin. It will run the ROS action ClearLocalCostmap-Context using the local costmap clearing service. It will run the custom action Spin to rotate in place by 1.57 radians.

Finally, it will execute main PipelineSequence. If it fails, it will execute recovery action (ReactiveFallback). After recovery, it will retry main action (PipelineSequence). If recovery action (ReactiveFallback) fails and retries are exhausted, it will stop execution.

Finally, it will execute the following actions [GoalUpdated, Wait] in order. If GoalUpdated succeeds, it will stop execution. Else it will keep executing and re-evaluating previous actions whenever an action is running. It will run the custom action GoalUpdated and will return success if the navigation goal changed. It will run the custom action Wait for a duration of 5 seconds.

      <RateController hz="1.0">
        <ComputePathToPose goal="{goal}" path="{path}" planner_id="GridBased"/>
      </RateController>
      <FollowPath path="{path}"  controller_id="FollowPath"/>

mrq: The mission describes a navigation task that computes a global path to a goal at 1 Hz and follows that path while advancing on running or success. First, the robot will execute the following actions (RateController, FollowPath) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will, while an action is running or has succeeded, go to the next action. It will run the custom action RateController at 1.0 Hz to constrain the frequency of the subsequent action. It will run the ROS action ComputePathToPose within the RateController using the provided goal and will store the resulting path each time the rate triggers. It will run the ROS action FollowPath using the most recently stored path with the specified controller after each path computation cycle advances.

mrq:
The mission describes an onboard vehicle-manipulator routine that opens the gripper, navigates, searches for an object, grasps it, transports it to an empty carry area, places it, and returns the manipulator to the drive position in strict order.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (MoveGripper, NavigateVehicle, main_manipulator_sequence) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action MoveGripper with the action parameter set to open before any navigation. It will run the custom action NavigateVehicle to reach the operational area. It will then run the nested main_manipulator_sequence only after navigation completes successfully.

Next, it will execute the following actions (find_object, handle_object) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the nested find_object to locate the target before attempting any grasp. It will then run the nested handle_object to pick, place, and park only after the object is found.

Next, it will execute the following actions (force_failure11, search_frame3, force_failure1) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will execute MoveManipulator-based searching that is forced to fail for the first branch to ensure testing of subsequent branches. It will execute the second branch that performs a planned move to the search2 frame and then runs ObjectSearch to determine the object pose. It will execute a final branch that is forced to fail after returning the manipulator to drive position only if the earlier branches do not succeed.

Next, it will execute the following actions (handle_not_able_to_close, leave_object) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the nested handle_not_able_to_close to approach and grip the object with recovery behavior embedded. It will then run the nested leave_object to place the object and return to drive only after a successful grip.

Next, it will execute the following actions (pick_object, force_failure1) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will execute the following actions (main_move_to_object, MoveGripper) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails to complete the grasp attempt. It will execute the following actions (PlanManipulatorPath, MoveManipulator) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails to move the manipulator to the computed object pose. It will run the custom action PlanManipulatorPath with plan_to_frame set to object and object_pose provided to compute a manipulator path. It will run the custom action MoveManipulator using the computed manipulator path to reach the object pose. It will run the custom action MoveGripper with the action parameter set to close to attempt grasping. It will execute MoveManipulator-based return to drive position that is forced to fail only if the grasp sequence fails.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (find_empty_frame, emptyframe, MoveGripper, drive) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute the following actions (check_empty_frame, force_failure2) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will execute the following actions (EmptyFrame carryarea1, EmptyFrame carryarea2, EmptyFrame carryarea3) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will run the custom action EmptyFrame with check_frame set to carryarea1 to test availability and to write the selected empty_frame output if available. It will run the custom action EmptyFrame with check_frame set to carryarea2 only if carryarea1 is not available. It will run the custom action EmptyFrame with check_frame set to carryarea3 only if carryarea2 is not available. It will run a forced-failure path that opens the gripper only if no empty frame is found, which causes the fallback to continue searching for an empty frame. It will execute the following actions (PlanManipulatorPath, MoveManipulator) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails to move to the selected empty_frame. It will run the custom action PlanManipulatorPath with plan_to_frame set to the selected empty_frame to compute a manipulator path. It will run the custom action MoveManipulator using the computed path to reach the empty placement frame. It will run the custom action MoveGripper with the action parameter set to open to release the object. It will execute the following actions (PlanManipulatorPath, MoveManipulator) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails to return the manipulator to the drive position. It will run the custom action PlanManipulatorPath with plan_to_frame set to driveposition to compute a return path. It will run the custom action MoveManipulator using the computed path to park the manipulator for driving.

<root main_tree_to_execute="GPDGraspGeneration">
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="GPDGraspGeneration">
        <Sequence>
            <RetryUntilSuccesful num_attempts="3">
                <TableTopSegmentation original_cloud="{original_cloud}" image_scene="{image_scene}" table_height="{table_height}" original_cloud_transformed="{original_cloud_transformed}" tabletop_cloud="{tabletop_cloud}" plane_cloud="{plane_cloud}" nonplane_cloud="{nonplane_cloud}"/>
            </RetryUntilSuccesful>
            <ConditionalSequence condition="False">
                <Decorator entry="{desired_object}" ID="BlackboardEntryCheck" name="DesiredObjectEntryCheck">
                    <ObjectCloudExtraction original_cloud="{original_cloud}" image_scene="{image_scene}" object_cloud="{object_cloud}" tabletop_cloud="{tabletop_cloud}" desired_object="{desired_object}"/>
                </Decorator>
                <TableTopClustering object_cloud="{object_cloud}" tabletop_cloud="{tabletop_cloud}"/>
            </ConditionalSequence>
            <GenerateGraspingCandidates object_cloud="{object_cloud}" table_height="{table_height}" grasp_candidates="{grasp_candidates}" original_cloud_transformed="{original_cloud_transformed}"/>
        </Sequence>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <TreeNodesModel>
        <Decorator ID="BlackboardEntryCheck">
            <input_port name="entry"/>
        </Decorator>
        <Condition ID="foo"/>
    </TreeNodesModel>
    <!-- ////////// -->
</root>
mrq: The mission describes a grasp generation task that segments a tabletop scene, optionally isolates a desired object, and produces grasp candidates from the resulting object cloud. First, the robot will execute the following actions (RetryUntilSuccessful, ConditionalSequence, GenerateGraspingCandidates) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will keep trying to execute the ROS action TableTopSegmentation until it succeeds or the attempt limit is reached. It will execute the following actions (Decorator, TableTopClustering) in order while checking a condition for each action. If condition ID is true, it will move to the next action; else if condition ID is false, it will end the sequence. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule before running the ROS action ObjectCloudExtraction. It will run the ROS action TableTopClustering on the extracted or preexisting object cloud when the condition allows progression. It will run the ROS action GenerateGraspingCandidates using the available object cloud, the measured table height, and the transformed original cloud to output grasp candidates.

mrq: The mission describes a single-stage sequence that waits, repeatedly commands a named navigation goal with guaranteed success, and then waits again in strict order. First, The robot will execute the following actions (WaitSeconds, ForceSuccess, WaitSeconds) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the ROS action WaitSeconds with a duration of 2 seconds and will complete the wait before proceeding. It will execute the following actions (Repeat) and make sure it always succeeds. It will execute Repeat for a set number of times or infinitely. It will execute the custom action UPOGoToGoalByName with the goal_name stage_1_pos_final_5 using the action server /Make_Plan and will perform this action for 10 cycles. It will run the ROS action WaitSeconds with a duration of 3 seconds and will complete the wait after the forced-success block finishes.

mrq:
The mission describes an animal-like roaming and sheltering task that selects a roaming strategy by default and manages shed-related behavior under specific environmental conditions.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (RandomMoveBig) consecutively. If any action (RandomMoveBig) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, it will start all over again until an action succeeds. It will run the custom action RandomMoveBig to wander broadly across its environment.

Next, it will execute the following actions (IsOnPasture, IsDay, IsInShed, LeaveShed, FindRandomPastureField, Move) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for IsOnPasture. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for IsDay. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for IsInShed. It will run the custom action LeaveShed to exit the shed. It will run the custom action FindRandomPastureField to pick a new pasture location. It will run the custom action Move to travel to the selected location. It will execute the following actions (IsOnPasture, IsNight, Inverter(IsInShed), FindShed, Move, EnterShed) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for IsOnPasture. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for IsNight. It will execute inverted Find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for IsInShed. If the check succeeds, it will reverse it to fail. Else if the check fails, it will reverse it to succeed. It will run the custom action FindShed to locate the shed. It will run the custom action Move to travel toward the shed. It will run the custom action EnterShed to go inside. It will execute the following actions (IsDay, Inverter(IsInShed), RandomMove) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for IsDay. It will execute inverted Find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for IsInShed. If the check succeeds, it will reverse it to fail. Else if the check fails, it will reverse it to succeed. It will run the custom action RandomMove to wander locally. It will execute the following actions (SequenceStar, SequenceStar, SequenceStar) consecutively. If any action (SequenceStar) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, it will start all over again until an action succeeds.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (IsOnPasture, IsDay, IsInShed, LeaveShed, FindRandomPastureField, Move) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for IsOnPasture. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for IsDay. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for IsInShed. It will run the custom action LeaveShed to exit the shed. It will run the custom action FindRandomPastureField to select a target field. It will run the custom action Move to travel to the selected field. Next, it will execute the following actions (IsOnPasture, IsNight, Inverter(IsInShed), FindShed, Move, EnterShed) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for IsOnPasture. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for IsNight. It will execute inverted IsInShed. If IsInShed succeeds, it will reverse it to fail. Else if IsInShed fails, it will reverse it to succeed. It will run the custom action FindShed to locate the shed. It will run the custom action Move to travel to the shed. It will run the custom action EnterShed to enter the shed. Finally, it will execute the following actions (IsDay, Inverter(IsInShed), RandomMove) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for IsDay. It will execute inverted IsInShed. If IsInShed succeeds, it will reverse it to fail. Else if IsInShed fails, it will reverse it to succeed. It will run the custom action RandomMove to wander around the current area.

mrq:
The mission describes a search-and-greet task that clears global cost data, seeks initialization, patrols a set of search poses, navigates to each pose, and interacts upon detecting a person using ordered control with reactive scanning and repeated attempts.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (Nav2ClearGlobalCostMap, RobotSeekInitAction, KeepRunningUntilFailure) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action Nav2ClearGlobalCostMap to clear the global cost map before navigation begins. It will run the custom action RobotSeekInitAction using the configured list of search poses to initialize its search plan. It will execute the following actions (Sequence) and will keep executing them as long as they do not fail.

Next, it will execute the following actions (RobotSeekNextSearchPose, SubTree: MoveRobot, TextToSpeechActionClient, Sequence) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action RobotSeekNextSearchPose to select the next search goal and to prepare a corresponding speech message. It will run the subtree MoveRobot to navigate to the selected goal. It will run the ROS action TextToSpeechActionClient to announce the prepared message after reaching the goal. It will execute the following actions (Fallback) consecutively and will move to the next action until one succeeds or the execution fails if none succeed.

Next, it will execute the following actions (Sequence, Sequence) consecutively and will move to the next action until one succeeds or the execution fails if none succeed. It will execute the following actions (ReactiveSequence, Sequence) in sequence and will stop execution if any action fails while re-evaluating previous actions when an action is still running. It will execute inverted HumanPoseDetect and will reverse success to failure and failure to success to check the absence of a person while scanning. It will run the custom action RobotSpin with a 340 degree rotation at 0.5 velocity to scan the surroundings while continuously rechecking for absence. It will execute the following actions (TextToSpeechActionClient) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails to announce that it did not see anyone if the scan completes without detection.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (HumanPoseDetect, TextToSpeechActionClient) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action HumanPoseDetect with presence checking to confirm a detected person and to provide pose-specific speech strings. It will run the ROS action TextToSpeechActionClient to greet the detected person upon successful detection.

Finally, the mission will execute the following actions (Nav2Client) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails as part of the MoveRobot subtree. It will run the ROS action Nav2Client to navigate to the provided target pose selected by the search logic.

mrq: The mission describes a voice-interaction loop that reacts to a wake word, performs a head gesture, and speaks a greeting repeatedly. First, the robot will execute the following actions (WakeWordDetected, HeadTiltAction, TextToSpeechActionClient) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for the ROS action WakeWordDetected with a detection window of 3.0 seconds. It will run the custom action HeadTiltAction to tilt the head by 10 degrees and dwell for 1500 milliseconds after the wake word check succeeds. It will run the ROS action TextToSpeechActionClient to say “Hey there.” after the head tilt completes. It will execute this action and make sure it always succeeds for the sequence that detects, tilts, and speaks. It will execute the sequence and, if the action succeeds, restart it; else if the action fails, it will stop looping and continue execution.

mrq:
The mission describes a reactive energy management and navigation task that monitors battery status, ensures charging when needed, and proceeds to a destination when appropriate.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (ReactiveFallback, ReactiveFallback) in sequence. If any action fails, it will stop execution. While any action is still running, it will re-evaluate previous actions. It will execute the following actions (ReactiveSequence, ReactiveSequence) in order. If any action succeeds, it will stop execution. Else it will keep executing and re-evaluating previous actions whenever an action is running. It will execute the following actions (YARPCondition BatteryLevel, YARPCondition BatteryNotCharging) in sequence. If any action fails, it will stop execution. While any action is still running, it will re-evaluate previous actions. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for the custom action YARPCondition BatteryLevel. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for the custom action YARPCondition BatteryNotCharging.

Next, it will execute the following actions (ReactiveFallback, AlwaysRunning Wait) in sequence. If any action fails, it will stop execution. While any action is still running, it will re-evaluate previous actions. It will execute the following actions (YARPCondition IsAtChargingStation, YARPAction GoToChargingStation) in order. If any action succeeds, it will stop execution. Else it will keep executing and re-evaluating previous actions whenever an action is running. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for the custom action YARPCondition IsAtChargingStation. It will run the custom action YARPAction GoToChargingStation if the charging station check does not succeed. It will execute the custom action AlwaysRunning Wait and keep it running while the preceding fallback actions are being re-evaluated.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (YARPCondition IsAtDestination, YARPAction GoToDestination) in order. If any action fails, it will stop execution. While any action is still running, it will re-evaluate previous actions. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for the custom action YARPCondition IsAtDestination. It will run the custom action YARPAction GoToDestination if the destination check does not succeed.

  <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
    <ReactiveFallback name="FollowTargets">

      <KeepRunningUntilFailure>

          <NavigateToPose goal="{goal}"/>

      </KeepRunningUntilFailure>
    </ReactiveFallback>
  </BehaviorTree>
</root>

The mission describes a pallet transport task that processes a list of waypoints by navigating to each goal and loading a pallet at each location until all goals are achieved.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (AllGoalsAchieved, KeepRunningUntilFailure) in order. It will execute the following actions [AllGoalsAchieved, KeepRunningUntilFailure] in order. If AllGoalsAchieved succeeds, it will stop execution. Else it will keep executing and re-evaluating previous actions whenever an action is running.

Next, it will execute Load-Process while it does not fail. It will execute [Action]. If [Action] succeeds, restart it. Else if [Action] fails, stop looping and continue execution.

Finally, it will execute the following actions [GetNextGoal, NavigateToPose, LoadPallet, SetBlackboard] in sequence. If any action fails, it will stop execution. While any action is still running, it will re-evaluate previous actions. It will run the custom action GetNextGoal to read the list of goals and to output the next goal while updating the achievement flag. It will run the ROS action NavigateToPose using the current goal to move to the target location. It will run the custom action LoadPallet at the current goal to load the pallet. It will run the custom action SetBlackboard to set the key goal_achieved to the boolean value true.

The mission describes a patrol and interruption handling task that sets goals, cycles between two waypoints with waits, reacts to an interrupt, and diverts to a home waypoint when required.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (SetBlackboard, SetBlackboard, SetBlackboard, SetBlackboard, Fallback) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute the custom action SetBlackboard to store a waypoint labeled Goal_a with coordinates 1.17;-1.65;0.0;1.0. It will execute the custom action SetBlackboard to store a waypoint labeled Goal_b with coordinates 1.1;1.51;0.0;1.0. It will execute the custom action SetBlackboard to store a waypoint labeled Goal_c with coordinates -1.25;0.324;0.0;1.0. It will execute the custom action SetBlackboard to store a waiting time labeled Waiting_time with a value of 2. It will execute the following actions (ReactiveSequence, Sequence) consecutively. If any action (ReactiveSequence) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails.

Next, it will execute the following actions [InterruptEvent, Repeat] in sequence. If [InterruptEvent] fails, it will stop execution. While [Repeat] is still running, it will re-evaluate previous actions. It will execute the custom action InterruptEvent configured with the event name gohome. It will execute [Sequence] for a set number of times or infinitely. It will execute [Sequence] infinitely because the number of cycles is -1.

Next, it will execute the following actions (SubTree, WaitingEvent, SubTree, WaitingEvent) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. While an action is running or has succeeded, it will go to the next action. It will execute the custom action SubTree with ID MoveRobot using the target Goal_a. It will execute the custom action WaitingEvent using the parameter waiting_event and the waiting time from Waiting_time. It will execute the custom action SubTree with ID MoveRobot using the target Goal_b. It will execute the custom action WaitingEvent using the parameter waiting_event and the waiting time from Waiting_time.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (SubTree) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute the custom action SubTree with ID MoveRobot using the target Goal_c.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (MoveBase) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the ROS action MoveBase with the goal provided by the parameter target.

BT:
<root main_tree_to_execute = "MainTree" >
  <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
    <Sequence>
      <!-- Set goal -->
      <SetBlackboard output_key="Goal_a" value="-6.432;3.35;0.000" />
      <SetBlackboard output_key="Goal_b" value="-0.95;0.6;0.0" />
      <SetBlackboard output_key="Goal_c" value="-1.488;3.7;0.00" />
      <SetBlackboard output_key="Goal_backup" value="-1.0;1.0;0.00" />

      <!-- Set joined robot name -->
      <SetBlackboard output_key="robot1" value="" />
      <SetBlackboard output_key="robot2" value="nb2_1" />
      <SetBlackboard output_key="robot3" value="nb2_2" />

      <RetryUntilSuccesful num_attempts="3">  <!-- Retry once fail -->
        <Parallel threshold="3">    <!-- threshold = number of robot -->
          
          <!-- robot1 -->
          <Repeat num_cycles="-1">    <!-- repeat infinitely -->
            <RetryUntilSuccesful num_attempts="3">
              <Fallback>    <!-- set goal to Goal_backup once the Sequence below fails -->
                <Sequence>
                  <SubTree ID="MoveRobot" robot_num="robot1" target="Goal_a" />
                  <SubTree ID="MoveRobot" robot_num="robot1" target="Goal_b" />
                  <SubTree ID="MoveRobot" robot_num="robot1" target="Goal_c" />
                </Sequence>
                <SubTree ID="MoveRobot" robot_num="robot1" target="Goal_backup" />
              </Fallback>
            </RetryUntilSuccesful>
          </Repeat>
          
          <!-- robot2 -->
          <Repeat num_cycles="-1">
            <RetryUntilSuccesful num_attempts="3">
              <Fallback>
                <Sequence>
                  <SubTree ID="MoveRobot" robot_num="robot2" target="Goal_b" />
                  <SubTree ID="MoveRobot" robot_num="robot2" target="Goal_c" />
                  <SubTree ID="MoveRobot" robot_num="robot2" target="Goal_a" />
                </Sequence>
                <SubTree ID="MoveRobot" robot_num="robot2" target="Goal_backup" />
              </Fallback>
            </RetryUntilSuccesful>
          </Repeat>
          
          <!-- robot3 -->
          <Repeat num_cycles="-1">
            <RetryUntilSuccesful num_attempts="3">
              <Fallback>
                <Sequence>
                  <SubTree ID="MoveRobot" robot_num="robot3" target="Goal_c" />
                  <SubTree ID="MoveRobot" robot_num="robot3" target="Goal_a" />
                  <SubTree ID="MoveRobot" robot_num="robot3" target="Goal_b" />
                </Sequence>
                <SubTree ID="MoveRobot" robot_num="robot3" target="Goal_backup" />
              </Fallback>
            </RetryUntilSuccesful>
          </Repeat>

        </Parallel>
      </RetryUntilSuccesful>
    </Sequence>
  </BehaviorTree>


  <BehaviorTree ID="MoveRobot">
    <Sequence name="SetGoal">
      <MoveBase robot="{robot_num}" goal="{target}" />
    </Sequence>
  </BehaviorTree>

</root>
mrq:
The mission describes a multi-robot patrol that sets target waypoints and robot identifiers, then commands three robots to move among waypoints concurrently with retries and fallbacks.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (SetBlackboard, SetBlackboard, SetBlackboard, SetBlackboard, SetBlackboard, SetBlackboard, SetBlackboard, RetryUntilSuccesful) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule by setting Goal_a, Goal_b, Goal_c, and Goal_backup as waypoint strings. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule by setting robot1, robot2, and robot3 identifiers. It will keep trying to execute Execute the following actions [Actions] simultaneously. If either a success threshold is reached or all actions succeed, the execution was successful. Else if any action fails, the execution fails. until it succeeds, with up to three attempts.

Next, it will execute the following actions [Actions] simultaneously. If either a success threshold is reached or all actions succeed, the execution was successful. Else if any action fails, the execution fails. It will include three repeating navigation routines and will require all three to succeed because the success threshold equals three.

Next, it will execute Execute [Action] for a set number of times or infinitely for the robot1 routine. It will execute Execute [Action]. If it fails, re-execute it either for a specified number of times or until it succeeds. for up to three attempts per cycle. It will execute the following actions (Sequence, SubTree) consecutively. If any action (Sequence) fails, move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will execute the following actions (SubTree, SubTree, SubTree) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action SubTree ID=MoveRobot with robot_num=robot1 and target=Goal_a. It will run the custom action SubTree ID=MoveRobot with robot_num=robot1 and target=Goal_b after the previous action succeeds. It will run the custom action SubTree ID=MoveRobot with robot_num=robot1 and target=Goal_c after the previous action succeeds. It will execute the custom action SubTree ID=MoveRobot with robot_num=robot1 and target=Goal_backup if the preceding sequence fails.

Next, it will execute Execute [Action] for a set number of times or infinitely for the robot2 routine. It will execute Execute [Action]. If it fails, re-execute it either for a specified number of times or until it succeeds. for up to three attempts per cycle. It will execute the following actions (Sequence, SubTree) consecutively. If any action (Sequence) fails, move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will execute the following actions (SubTree, SubTree, SubTree) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action SubTree ID=MoveRobot with robot_num=robot2 and target=Goal_b. It will run the custom action SubTree ID=MoveRobot with robot_num=robot2 and target=Goal_c after the previous action succeeds. It will run the custom action SubTree ID=MoveRobot with robot_num=robot2 and target=Goal_a after the previous action succeeds. It will execute the custom action SubTree ID=MoveRobot with robot_num=robot2 and target=Goal_backup if the preceding sequence fails.

Finally, it will execute Execute [Action] for a set number of times or infinitely for the robot3 routine. It will execute Execute [Action]. If it fails, re-execute it either for a specified number of times or until it succeeds. for up to three attempts per cycle. It will execute the following actions (Sequence, SubTree) consecutively. If any action (Sequence) fails, move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will execute the following actions (SubTree, SubTree, SubTree) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action SubTree ID=MoveRobot with robot_num=robot3 and target=Goal_c. It will run the custom action SubTree ID=MoveRobot with robot_num=robot3 and target=Goal_a after the previous action succeeds. It will run the custom action SubTree ID=MoveRobot with robot_num=robot3 and target=Goal_b after the previous action succeeds. It will execute the custom action SubTree ID=MoveRobot with robot_num=robot3 and target=Goal_backup if the preceding sequence fails.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (MoveBase) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the ROS action MoveBase with the provided robot_num and target parameters to drive the addressed platform to the specified goal.

<root main_tree_to_execute="BehaviorTree">
  <BehaviorTree ID="BehaviorTree">
    <RetryUntilSuccesful name="retry_navigate" num_attempts="7">
      <FallbackStar>
        <SequenceStar name="navigate">
          <ComputePathToPose endpoints="${endpoints}" path="${path}"/>
	  <FollowPath path="${path}"/>

        <ForceFailure>
          <SequenceStar name="recovery">
            <clearEntirelyCostmapServiceRequest service_name="/local_costmap/clear_entirely_local_costmap"/>
            <clearEntirelyCostmapServiceRequest service_name="/global_costmap/clear_entirely_global_costmap"/>
            <Spin/>
          </SequenceStar>
        </ForceFailure>
      </FallbackStar>
    </RetryUntilSuccesful>
  </BehaviorTree>
</root>
mrq: The mission describes a navigation task that will repeatedly try to reach a destination by planning a path, following it, and performing recovery when needed. First, the robot will keep trying to execute the following actions (FallbackStar) until it succeeds. Next, it will execute the following actions (SequenceStar, ForceFailure) consecutively. Next, it will execute the following actions (ComputePathToPose, FollowPath) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will run the ROS action ComputePathToPose with the provided endpoints and will store the resulting path. It will run the ROS action FollowPath using the stored path after the path computation succeeds. Finally, it will execute the following actions (SequenceStar) consecutively. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will run the custom action clearEntirelyCostmapServiceRequest with the service name /local_costmap/clear_entirely_local_costmap. It will run the custom action clearEntirelyCostmapServiceRequest with the service name /global_costmap/clear_entirely_global_costmap. It will run the ROS action Spin to reorient in place. It will execute the preceding recovery actions and make sure it always fails.

mrq:
The mission describes a navigation task that periodically replans a global path at 1 Hz, follows that path, and performs costmap-clearing and spin-based recovery when navigation fails.

First, the robot will execute main action and recovery action with bounded retries. It will execute main action. If it fails, it will execute recovery action. After recovery, it will retry main action. If recovery action fails and retries are exhausted, it will stop execution.

Next, it will execute the following actions (RateController, Fallback, FollowPath) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. While an action is running or has succeeded, it will go to the next action.

Next, it will execute delayed action. If execution succeeds, it will start delay timer. After the delay period is exhausted, it will resume execution. It will execute the following actions (GoalReached, ComputePathToPose) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution will fail.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (clearEntirelyCostmapServiceRequest, clearEntirelyCostmapServiceRequest, Spin) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will execute the custom action clearEntirelyCostmapServiceRequest with service_name set to /local_costmap/clear_entirely_local_costmap. It will execute the custom action clearEntirelyCostmapServiceRequest with service_name set to /global_costmap/clear_entirely_global_costmap. It will execute the custom action Spin.

mrq:
The mission describes a navigation task that computes an initial path to a goal, follows that path, and periodically recomputes the path while updating the active path, with success determined by the path-following outcome.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (ComputePathToPose, ParallelNode) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will run the ROS action ComputePathToPose using the blackboard goal and will store the resulting path on the blackboard. It will then run the ParallelNode and will consider the execution successful when at least one child action succeeds, and it will consider the execution failed if any child action fails before the success threshold is met.

Next, it will execute the ROS action FollowPath using the current blackboard path while the ParallelNode is active. It will cause the ParallelNode to finish immediately with success if FollowPath succeeds because the success threshold is one. It will cause the ParallelNode to finish immediately with failure if FollowPath fails because the success threshold is one.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (RateController, ComputePathToPose, UpdatePath) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails while running in parallel with the FollowPath action. While an action is running or has succeeded, it will go to the next action. It will run the custom action RateController to control execution timing at 1.0 Hz. It will run the ROS action ComputePathToPose using the blackboard goal and will overwrite the blackboard path with the new result at the controlled rate. It will run the custom action UpdatePath to send the new blackboard path to the local planner immediately after each recomputation succeeds.

The mission describes an energy-aware patrol that verifies battery health, performs recovery by recharging if needed, switches operating modes, and then patrols multiple waypoints in order.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (CheckComponent, recharging) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute the following actions (CheckComponent, recharging) consecutively. If any action (CheckComponent) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution will fail. It will run the custom action CheckComponent with the input component set to battery. It will execute the following actions (Reconfigure, NavigateToWp, Recharge) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action Reconfigure with the mode set to f_energy_saving_mode before attempting to recharge. It will run the custom action NavigateToWp with the goal set to recharge_station to reach the charging location. It will run the custom action Recharge to restore energy after reaching the station.

Next, it will execute the following actions (Reconfigure, patrolling) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action Reconfigure with the mode set to f_normal_mode before starting patrol. It will execute the following actions (NavigateToWp, NavigateToWp, NavigateToWp, NavigateToWp, NavigateToWp, NavigateToWp, NavigateToWp) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will run the custom action NavigateToWp with the goal set to wp_1. It will run the custom action NavigateToWp with the goal set to wp_2. It will run the custom action NavigateToWp with the goal set to wp_3. It will run the custom action NavigateToWp with the goal set to wp_4. It will run the custom action NavigateToWp with the goal set to wp_5. It will run the custom action NavigateToWp with the goal set to wp_6. It will run the custom action NavigateToWp with the goal set to wp_7.

<?xml version="1.0"?>
<root main_tree_to_execute="BehaviorTree">
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="BehaviorTree">
        <Repeat num_cycles="{num_cycles}">
            <Sequence>
                <Fallback>
                    <Condition ID="LTLPreCheck" ltl_state_current="{ltl_state_current}" ltl_state_desired_sequence="{ltl_state_desired_sequence}" ltl_state_executed_sequence="{ltl_state_executed_sequence}"/>
                    <ForceFailure>
                        <Action ID="ReplanningRequestLevel2" ltl_state_current="{ltl_state_current}" ltl_state_executed_sequence="{ltl_state_executed_sequence}" replanning_request="{replanning_request}"/>
                    </ForceFailure>
                </Fallback>
                <ReactiveSequence>
                    <Fallback>
                        <Condition ID="ReactiveLTLStateCheck" ltl_state_current="{ltl_state_current}" ltl_state_desired_sequence="{ltl_state_desired_sequence}"/>
                        <ForceFailure>
                            <Action ID="ReplanningRequestLevel2" ltl_state_current="{ltl_state_current}" ltl_state_executed_sequence="{ltl_state_executed_sequence}" replanning_request="{replanning_request}"/>
                        </ForceFailure>
                    </Fallback>
                    <Fallback>
                        <Switch4 case_1="move" case_2="stay" case_3="start_training" case_4="terminate_training" variable="{bt_action_type}">
                            <Action ID="MoveAction" bt_action_type="{bt_action_type}" current_action="{current_action}" goal_sent="{goal_sent}" move_base_finished="{move_base_finished}" move_base_idle="{move_base_idle}"/>
                            <Action ID="StayAction" bt_action_type="{bt_action_type}" current_action="{current_action}" ltl_state_current="{ltl_state_current}"/>
                            <Action ID="GuideAction" bt_action_type="{bt_action_type}" current_action="{current_action}" ltl_state_current="{ltl_state_current}"/>
                            <Action ID="BackNormalAction" bt_action_type="{bt_action_type}" current_action="{current_action}" ltl_state_current="{ltl_state_current}"/>
                            <AlwaysFailure/>
                        </Switch4>
                        <ForceFailure>
                            <Action ID="ReplanningRequestLevel3" ltl_state_current="{ltl_state_current}" ltl_state_executed_sequence="{ltl_state_executed_sequence}" replanning_request="{replanning_request}"/>
                        </ForceFailure>
                    </Fallback>
                </ReactiveSequence>
                <Action ID="UpdateLTL" action_sequence="{action_sequence}" action_sequence_executed="{action_sequence_executed}" bt_action_type="{bt_action_type}" current_action="{current_action}" ltl_state_desired_sequence="{ltl_state_desired_sequence}"/>
            </Sequence>
        </Repeat>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <TreeNodesModel>
        <Action ID="BackNormalAction">
            <input_port name="bt_action_type"/>
            <input_port name="current_action"/>
            <inout_port name="ltl_state_current"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="DropAction">
            <input_port name="bt_action_type"/>
            <input_port name="current_action"/>
            <inout_port name="ltl_state_current"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="GuideAction">
            <input_port name="bt_action_type"/>
            <input_port name="current_action"/>
            <inout_port name="ltl_state_current"/>
        </Action>
        <Condition ID="LTLPreCheck">
            <input_port name="ltl_state_current"/>
            <input_port name="ltl_state_desired_sequence"/>
            <inout_port name="ltl_state_executed_sequence"/>
        </Condition>
        <Action ID="LocomotionStart">
            <input_port name="locomotion_status"/>
        </Action>
        <Condition ID="LocomotionStatusCheck">
            <input_port name="locomotion_status"/>
        </Condition>
        <Action ID="MoveAction">
            <input_port name="bt_action_type"/>
            <input_port name="current_action"/>
            <output_port name="goal_sent"/>
            <input_port name="move_base_finished"/>
            <input_port name="move_base_idle"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="PickAction">
            <input_port name="bt_action_type"/>
            <input_port name="current_action"/>
            <inout_port name="ltl_state_current"/>
        </Action>
        <Condition ID="ReactiveLTLStateCheck">
            <input_port name="ltl_state_current"/>
            <input_port name="ltl_state_desired_sequence"/>
        </Condition>
        <Action ID="RecoveryStand">
            <input_port name="locomotion_status"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="ReplanningRequestLevel1">
            <input_port name="ltl_state_current"/>
            <inout_port name="ltl_state_executed_sequence"/>
            <inout_port name="replanning_request"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="ReplanningRequestLevel2">
            <input_port name="ltl_state_current"/>
            <inout_port name="ltl_state_executed_sequence"/>
            <inout_port name="replanning_request"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="ReplanningRequestLevel3">
            <input_port name="ltl_state_current"/>
            <inout_port name="ltl_state_executed_sequence"/>
            <inout_port name="replanning_request"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="StayAction">
            <input_port name="bt_action_type"/>
            <input_port name="current_action"/>
            <inout_port name="ltl_state_current"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="SynchronizedTransitionAction">
            <input_port name="bt_action_type"/>
            <input_port name="current_action"/>
            <inout_port name="ltl_state_current"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="UpdateLTL">
            <inout_port name="action_sequence"/>
            <inout_port name="action_sequence_executed"/>
            <output_port name="bt_action_type"/>
            <output_port name="current_action"/>
            <inout_port name="ltl_state_desired_sequence"/>
        </Action>
    </TreeNodesModel>
    <!-- ////////// -->
</root>
mrq:
The mission describes a looped task that supervises a temporal-logic-driven plan, reacts to state mismatches, selects and executes the current action, and updates the plan status across repeated cycles.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (Sequence) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute the following actions (Fallback) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for the condition LTLPreCheck. It will execute Action and make sure it always fails for the custom action ReplanningRequestLevel2. It will execute the following actions [Actions] in sequence. If [Action] fails, stop execution. While [Action] is still running, re-evaluate previous actions. It will execute the following actions (Fallback) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for the condition ReactiveLTLStateCheck. It will execute Action and make sure it always fails for the custom action ReplanningRequestLevel2. It will execute the following actions (Fallback) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will read blackboard value and compare it against a set of cases. If a case matches the blackboard value, execute corresponding Action. Else if no case matches, execute default Action. It will run the custom action MoveAction when the case matches move. It will run the custom action StayAction when the case matches stay. It will run the custom action GuideAction when the case matches start_training. It will run the custom action BackNormalAction when the case matches terminate_training. It will run the default custom action AlwaysFailure when no case matches. It will execute Action and make sure it always fails for the custom action ReplanningRequestLevel3. It will run the custom action UpdateLTL after the previous actions in the sequence succeed.

Next, it will execute Action for a set number of times or infinitely. It will execute the inner sequence subtree for the number of cycles provided by num_cycles.

<!--
  This Behavior Tree replans the global path periodically at 1 Hz and it also has
  recovery actions.
-->
<root main_tree_to_execute="MainTree">
  <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
    <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="6" name="NavigateRecovery">
      <PipelineSequence name="NavigateWithReplanning">
        <RateController hz="1.0">
          <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="1" name="ComputePathToPose">
            <ComputePathToPose goal="{goal}" path="{path}" planner_id="GridBased"/>
            <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearGlobalCostmap-Context" service_name="global_costmap/clear_entirely_global_costmap"/>
          </RecoveryNode>
        </RateController>
        <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="1" name="FollowPath">
          <FollowPath path="{path}" controller_id="FollowPath"/>
          <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearLocalCostmap-Context" service_name="local_costmap/clear_entirely_local_costmap"/>
        </RecoveryNode>
      </PipelineSequence>
      <ReactiveFallback name="RecoveryFallback">
        <GoalUpdated/>
        <SequenceStar name="RecoveryActions">
          <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearLocalCostmap-Subtree" service_name="local_costmap/clear_entirely_local_costmap"/>
          <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearGlobalCostmap-Subtree" service_name="global_costmap/clear_entirely_global_costmap"/>
          <Spin spin_dist="1.57"/>
          <Wait wait_duration="5"/>
        </SequenceStar>
      </ReactiveFallback>
    </RecoveryNode>
  </BehaviorTree>
</root>
mrq:
The mission describes a navigation task that computes a global path at 1 Hz, follows the path with continuous advancement, and performs layered recoveries when failures occur.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (NavigateWithReplanning, RecoveryFallback) using the RecoveryNode pattern. It will execute main action NavigateWithReplanning. If it fails, it will execute recovery action RecoveryFallback. After recovery, it will retry the main action NavigateWithReplanning until the retry limit is exhausted.

Next, it will execute the following actions (RateController, FollowPath with local recovery) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. While an action is running or has succeeded, it will go to the next action.

Next, it will execute delayed RateController. If execution succeeds, it will start a 1.0 Hz delay timer. After each delay period is exhausted, it will resume execution to regulate the replanning cadence.

Next, it will execute main action ComputePathToPose with a single retry using the RecoveryNode pattern. If it fails, it will execute recovery action ClearEntireCostmap-Context on the global costmap. After recovery, it will retry the main action ComputePathToPose once.

Next, it will run the ROS action ComputePathToPose with the provided goal and will store the resulting path.

Next, it will execute the custom action ClearEntireCostmap-Context on the global costmap service if ComputePathToPose fails inside its local recovery.

Next, it will execute main action FollowPath with a single retry using the RecoveryNode pattern. If it fails, it will execute recovery action ClearEntireCostmap-Context on the local costmap. After recovery, it will retry the main action FollowPath once.

Next, it will run the ROS action FollowPath using the stored path with the specified controller.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (GoalUpdated, RecoveryActions) in order. If GoalUpdated succeeds, it will stop execution. Else it will keep executing and re-evaluating previous actions whenever an action is running.

Finally, it will execute the ROS action GoalUpdated. If it reports that a new goal is available, it will end the recovery fallback immediately.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (ClearLocalCostmap-Subtree, ClearGlobalCostmap-Subtree, Spin, Wait) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning.

Finally, it will execute the custom action ClearLocalCostmap-Subtree to clear the entire local costmap.

Finally, it will execute the custom action ClearGlobalCostmap-Subtree to clear the entire global costmap.

Finally, it will run the ROS action Spin with a spin distance of 1.57 radians.

Finally, it will run the ROS action Wait for a duration of 5 seconds.

<root main_tree_to_execute = "MainTree" >
    <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
       <Sequence name="pick">
            <SelectPick  name="select_pick"/>
            <GetPickObject  name="get_pick_object" object_id="{object_id}"/>
            <GetPoseFromObject  name="get_pose_from_object" object_id="{object_id}" wp_pose="{pose}"/>
            <Parallel success_threshold="1" failure_threshold="1">
                <Track name="pick" pose="{pose}" />
                <ApproachObject name="approach_object" object_pose="{pose}"/>
            </Parallel>
            <Wait seconds="3"/>
            <GetPoseFromObject  name="get_pose_from_object" object_id="{object_id}" wp_pose="{pose}"/>
            <Parallel success_threshold="1" failure_threshold="1">
                <Track name="pick" pose="{pose}" />
                <Pick name="pick" object_id="{object_id}" object_pose="{pose}"/>
            </Parallel>
       </Sequence>
    </BehaviorTree>
</root>
mrq: The mission describes a pick operation that selects an object, localizes it, approaches it while tracking, waits to stabilize, refreshes the pose, and then executes a synchronized track and pick in strict order. First, the robot will execute the following actions (SelectPick, GetPickObject, GetPoseFromObject, Parallel, Wait, GetPoseFromObject, Parallel) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action SelectPick to choose a pick task. It will run the custom action GetPickObject to obtain an object identifier and will store it as object_id. It will run the custom action GetPoseFromObject to compute a waypoint pose for the stored object_id and will store it as pose. It will execute the following actions (Track, ApproachObject) simultaneously. If either a success threshold is reached or all actions succeed, the execution was successful. Else if any action fails, the execution fails. It will run the custom action Track to lock onto the current pose while it runs the custom action ApproachObject to move toward the object using the same pose. Next, it will run the custom action Wait to pause execution for three seconds. It will run the custom action GetPoseFromObject again to update the pose for the same object_id and will store the refreshed pose. Finally, it will execute the following actions (Track, Pick) simultaneously. If either a success threshold is reached or all actions succeed, the execution was successful. Else if any action fails, the execution fails. It will run the custom action Track to maintain alignment on the refreshed pose while it runs the custom action Pick to grasp the object using object_id and the refreshed pose.

mrq:
The mission describes a household retrieval task that attempts to find and grasp a bottle in the kitchen and asks for help if the task cannot progress.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (S1, Ask_for_help) consecutively. If any action (S1) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will run the custom action S1 as the primary strategy. It will run the custom action Ask_for_help only if S1 fails.

Next, it will execute the following actions (FB2, S2) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action FB2 to ensure it is in the kitchen. It will run the custom action S2 to search for, locate, and grasp the bottle after the kitchen step succeeds.

Next, it will execute the following actions (Robot_in_kitchen, Go_to_kitchen) consecutively. If any action (Robot_in_kitchen) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule. It will run the custom action Go_to_kitchen only if the kitchen condition is not already satisfied.

Next, it will execute the following actions (FB3, FB4) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action FB3 to determine whether the bottle is already found or must be found. It will run the custom action FB4 to determine whether the bottle is already grasped or must be grasped.

Next, it will execute the following actions (Bottle_found, Find_bottle) consecutively. If any action (Bottle_found) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule. It will run the custom action Find_bottle only if the bottle is not already found.

Next, it will execute the following actions (Bottle_grasped, FB5) consecutively. If any action (Bottle_grasped) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule. It will run the custom action FB5 only if the bottle has not yet been grasped.

Next, it will execute the following actions (S3, FB6) consecutively. If any action (S3) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will run the custom action S3 as the primary grasping pipeline. It will run the custom action FB6 as a recovery if S3 fails.

Next, it will execute the following actions (FB7, S4) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action FB7 to ensure an inverse pose is computed and valid or to compute it. It will run the custom action S4 to approach the inverse pose, refine the bottle location, and attempt the grasp after the inverse pose step succeeds.

Next, it will execute the following actions (S6, Compute_inv_pose) consecutively. If any action (S6) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will run the custom action S6 to verify that an inverse pose is computed and valid. It will run the custom action Compute_inv_pose only if the checks do not already pass.

Next, it will execute the following actions (Inv_pose_computed, Inv_pose_valid) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule.

Next, it will execute the following actions (FB8, S5) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action FB8 to ensure it is at the inverse pose or to navigate there. It will run the custom action S5 to localize the bottle with confidence and grasp it after arriving at the inverse pose.

Next, it will execute the following actions (Robot_at_inv_pose, Go_to_inv_pose) consecutively. If any action (Robot_at_inv_pose) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule. It will run the custom action Go_to_inv_pose only if it is not already at the inverse pose.

Next, it will execute the following actions (FB9, Grasp_bottle) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action FB9 to ensure the bottle is located with required confidence or to perform localization. It will run the custom action Grasp_bottle to grasp the bottle after the localization step succeeds.

Next, it will execute the following actions (Bottle_located_with_confidence_x, Locate_bottle) consecutively. If any action (Bottle_located_with_confidence_x) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule. It will run the custom action Locate_bottle only if the confidence requirement is not already met.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (Set_inv_pose_not_valid, Keep_running) consecutively. If any action (Set_inv_pose_not_valid) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will run the custom action Set_inv_pose_not_valid to clear the validity of the inverse pose. It will run the custom action Keep_running if clearing the validity does not resolve the failure path.

The mission describes a multi-stage task that pushes a puck, grasps a goldonium puck, weighs it at a scale, and then clears an area, all executed in strict order.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (MoveAhead, Turn, MoveAhead, MoveAX_4, MoveAX_3, MoveAhead, MoveAX_3) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action MoveAhead to advance forward by a specified distance. It will run the custom action Turn to rotate left by ninety degrees. It will run the custom action MoveAhead to advance forward again by a specified distance. It will run the custom action MoveAX_4 to turn the clamp ninety degrees to the right in joint mode. It will run the custom action MoveAX_3 to move the clamp forward in joint mode. It will run the custom action MoveAhead to push the puck forward by a specified distance. It will run the custom action MoveAX_3 to move the clamp backward in joint mode.

Next, it will execute the following actions (MoveAX_1, MoveAhead, MoveAX_1, MoveAX_3, MoveAX_4) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action MoveAX_1 to open the clamp in joint mode. It will run the custom action MoveAhead to advance forward to the goldonium puck by a specified distance. It will run the custom action MoveAX_1 to close the clamp in joint mode. It will run the custom action MoveAX_3 to retract the clamp backward in joint mode. It will run the custom action MoveAX_4 to turn the clamp ninety degrees to the right in joint mode.

Then, it will execute the following actions (MoveAhead, Turn, MoveAhead, MoveAhead, MoveAX_2, OpenClamp, MoveAX_2) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action MoveAhead to move backward by a specified distance. It will run the custom action Turn to rotate left by ninety degrees. It will run the custom action MoveAhead to reach a waiting point by advancing a specified distance. It will run the custom action MoveAhead to reach the scale by advancing a specified distance. It will run the custom action MoveAX_2 to move the clamp forward in joint mode. It will run the custom action OpenClamp and make sure it always succeeds. It will run the custom action MoveAX_2 to move the clamp backward in joint mode.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (MoveAhead, Turn, MoveAhead) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action MoveAhead to move backward by a specified distance. It will run the custom action Turn to rotate left by a specified angle. It will run the custom action MoveAhead to move forward by a specified distance.

The mission describes a clinical support task that continuously checks safety and patient conditions, selects a skill based on context, and executes the matching skill while staying reactive to changes.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (CheckStop, CheckBattery, CheckDiagnosticStatus, CheckPatientCondition, Sequence) in sequence. If any action fails, it will stop execution. While any action is still running, it will re-evaluate previous actions. It will run the custom action CheckStop to verify that no stop condition is active. It will run the custom action CheckBattery to verify that power level and charging state meet operating thresholds. It will run the custom action CheckDiagnosticStatus to verify that system diagnostics report a healthy state. It will run the custom action CheckPatientCondition to verify that the patient state permits proceeding. It will then execute the nested sequence after all checks succeed.

Next, it will execute the following actions (SkillSelection, Fallback) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action SkillSelection to choose a skill and will store the choice in the blackboard as selected_action. It will then execute the following actions (BlackboardCheckString, DevicePairing, BlackboardCheckString, BaseMovement, BlackboardCheckString, ArmMovement, BlackboardCheckString, BatteryCharging, BlackboardCheckString, ConfirmDialog) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule by comparing selected_action to DevicePairing. If the comparison matches, it will run the custom action DevicePairing. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule by comparing selected_action to BaseMovement. If the comparison matches, it will run the custom action BaseMovement. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule by comparing selected_action to ArmMovement. If the comparison matches, it will run the custom action ArmMovement. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule by comparing selected_action to BatteryCharging. If the comparison matches, it will run the custom action BatteryCharging. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule by comparing selected_action to ConfirmDialog. If the comparison matches, it will run the custom action ConfirmDialog.

<root main_tree_to_execute="MainTree">
  <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
    <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="6" name="NavigateRecovery">
      <PipelineSequence name="NavigateWithReplanning">
        <RateController hz="1.0">
          <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="1" name="ComputePathToPose">
            <ComputePathToPose goal="{goal}" path="{path}" planner_id="GridBased"/>
            <ClearEntireCostmap service_name="global_costmap/clear_entirely_global_costmap"/>
          </RecoveryNode>
        </RateController>
        <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="1" name="FollowPath">
          <FollowPath path="{path}" controller_id="FollowPath"/>
          <ClearEntireCostmap service_name="local_costmap/clear_entirely_local_costmap"/>
        </RecoveryNode>
      </PipelineSequence>
      <SequenceStar name="RecoveryActions">
        <ClearEntireCostmap service_name="local_costmap/clear_entirely_local_costmap"/>
        <ClearEntireCostmap service_name="global_costmap/clear_entirely_global_costmap"/>
        <Spin spin_dist="1.57"/>
        <Wait wait_duration="5"/>
      </SequenceStar>
    </RecoveryNode>
  </BehaviorTree>
</root>
mrq:
The mission describes a navigation task that computes a global path at 1 Hz, follows the path, and performs layered recovery when needed.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (NavigateWithReplanning) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. While an action is running or has succeeded, it will go to the next action. It will run the ROS action RateController at 1.0 Hz to pace subsequent planning. It will execute main ComputePathToPose. If it fails, it will execute recovery action (ClearEntireCostmap for the global costmap). After recovery, it will retry main action (ComputePathToPose) once. It will run the ROS action ComputePathToPose using the provided goal and will store the resulting path in the blackboard. It will then execute main FollowPath. If it fails, it will execute recovery action (ClearEntireCostmap for the local costmap). After recovery, it will retry main action (FollowPath) once. It will run the ROS action FollowPath using the stored path with the specified controller.

Next, it will execute main NavigateWithReplanning. If it fails, it will execute recovery action (RecoveryActions). After recovery, it will retry main action (NavigateWithReplanning). If recovery action (RecoveryActions) fails and retries are exhausted, it will stop execution. It will keep this primary-recovery cycle for up to six retries.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (ClearEntireCostmap for the local costmap, ClearEntireCostmap for the global costmap, Spin, Wait) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will clear the local costmap using the specified service. It will clear the global costmap using the specified service. It will run the custom action Spin to rotate in place by 1.57 radians. It will run the custom action Wait to pause for 5 seconds.

mrq:
The mission describes a repetitive pick-and-place task that will continuously try to place a held item or pick a seen item according to goal availability.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (Pursue Goals) consecutively. If any action (Pursue Goals) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will execute the following actions (Place Item, Remove Item) consecutively. If any action (Place Item) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails.

Next, it will execute the following actions (Holding an Item, Place it) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for the condition HoldingItem is true. It will run the custom action Place and will place the held item only after the condition succeeds.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (See an Item, Pick it up) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for the condition SeeItem is true. It will run the custom action Pick and will pick up the seen item only after the condition succeeds.

<root main_tree_to_execute="BehaviorTree">
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="BehaviorTree">
        <Sequence>
            <SubTree ID="Init"/>
            <RetryUntilSuccesful num_attempts="-1">
                <Fallback name="Pursue Goals">
                    <ForceFailure name="No Item to Place">
                        <Sequence name="Place Item">
                            <Condition ID="HoldingItem" name="Holding an Item"/>
                            <Action ID="Place" name="Place it"/>
                        </Sequence>
                    </ForceFailure>
                    <ForceFailure name="No Item to Pick">
                        <Sequence name="Remove Item">
                            <Condition ID="SeeItem" name="See an Item"/>
                            <Action ID="Pick" name="Pick it up"/>
                        </Sequence>
                    </ForceFailure>
                </Fallback>
            </RetryUntilSuccesful>
        </Sequence>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="Init">
        <Action message="Initialize" ID="Print"/>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <TreeNodesModel>
        <Action ID="AlwaysRunning"/>
        <Condition ID="HoldingItem"/>
        <SubTree ID="Init"/>
        <Action ID="Pick"/>
        <Action ID="Place"/>
        <Action ID="Print">
            <input_port name="message"/>
        </Action>
        <Condition ID="SeeItem"/>
    </TreeNodesModel>
    <!-- ////////// -->
</root>
mrq:
The mission describes an item-handling task that initializes the system and then persistently alternates between placing a held item or picking a seen item based on conditions until one succeeds.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (SubTree Init, RetryUntilSuccesful) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action SubTree Init before attempting any picking or placing. It will keep trying to execute the custom action Fallback Pursue Goals until it succeeds. If a try succeeds, it will stop retrying and continue. If a try fails, it will keep retrying until it succeeds.

Next, it will execute the following actions (ForceFailure No Item to Place, ForceFailure No Item to Pick) consecutively. If any action (ForceFailure No Item to Place) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution will fail. It will execute the custom action Sequence Place Item and make sure it always fails. It will execute the custom action Sequence Remove Item and make sure it always fails.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (Condition HoldingItem, Action Place) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule. It will run the ROS action Place if the holding condition check succeeds.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (Condition SeeItem, Action Pick) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule. It will run the ROS action Pick if the seeing condition check succeeds.

Finally, the mission will execute delayed custom action Action Print and, if execution succeeds, will start a delay timer and after the delay period is exhausted, will resume execution.
