<root main_tree_to_execute="MainTree">
  <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
    <PipelineSequence name="NavigateWithReplanning">
      <RateController hz="1.0">
        <Sequence>
          <GoalUpdater input_goal="{goal}" output_goal="{updated_goal}">
            <ComputePathToPose goal="{updated_goal}" path="{path}" planner_id="GridBased"/>
          </GoalUpdater>
         <TruncatePath distance="1.0" input_path="{path}" output_path="{truncated_path}"/>
        </Sequence>
      </RateController>
      <KeepRunningUntilFailure>
        <FollowPath path="{truncated_path}" controller_id="FollowPath"/>
      </KeepRunningUntilFailure>
    </PipelineSequence>
  </BehaviorTree>
</root>
mrq: The mission describes a navigation task that continuously replans toward a dynamic goal and follows a truncated path to maintain a set standoff distance. First, the robot will execute the following actions (RateController, KeepRunningUntilFailure) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. While an action is running or has succeeded, it will go to the next action. Next, it will execute the ROS action RateController at 1.0 Hz and gate the inner sequence execution at this fixed rate. It will execute the following actions (GoalUpdater, TruncatePath) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action GoalUpdater to transform the input goal into an updated goal and then trigger the ROS action ComputePathToPose that is embedded within GoalUpdater to compute a path to the updated goal and store the path. It will run the custom action TruncatePath to shorten the stored path to maintain a distance of 1.0 from the goal and then store the truncated path. Finally, it will execute the following actions (FollowPath) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. While an action is running or has succeeded, it will go to the next action. It will execute the ROS action FollowPath using the truncated path and will keep executing FollowPath again whenever it succeeds, and it will stop looping and continue execution when FollowPath fails.

Next, it will execute the following actions (SkillSelection, Fallback) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action SkillSelection and will write the chosen skill into the blackboard field selected_action. It will then execute the following actions (Sequence, Sequence, Sequence, Sequence, Sequence, Sequence) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails.

Next, it will execute the following actions (CheckBlackboard, FreeWalking) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule. It will compare selected_action against the case FreeWalking. It will execute the custom action FreeWalking after the comparison succeeds.

Next, it will execute the following actions (CheckBlackboard, DevicePairing) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule. It will compare selected_action against the case DevicePairing. It will execute the custom action DevicePairing after the comparison succeeds.

Next, it will execute the following actions (CheckBlackboard, BaseMovement) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule. It will compare selected_action against the case BaseMovement. It will execute the custom action BaseMovement after the comparison succeeds.

Next, it will execute the following actions (CheckBlackboard, ArmMovement) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule. It will compare selected_action against the case ArmMovement. It will execute the custom action ArmMovement after the comparison succeeds.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (CheckBlackboard, BatteryCharging) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule. It will compare selected_action against the case BatteryCharging. It will execute the custom action BatteryCharging after the comparison succeeds.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (CheckBlackboard, ConfirmDialog) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule. It will compare selected_action against the case ConfirmDialog. It will execute the custom action ConfirmDialog after the comparison succeeds.

<root main_tree_to_execute="BehaviorTree">

    <BehaviorTree ID="BehaviorTree">
        <Parallel failure_threshold="1" success_threshold="1">

                <Action ID="VariantAction" value="{variant}"/>
            </Repeat>
            <Sequence>
                <Action ID="objectplacement" query="WOODENBOX_0.1_0.3_0.3_0.3"/>
                <SubTreePlus ID="GoTo" __shared_blackboard="false" x_input="1.61" y_input="7.76"/>
                <Action ID="objectdrop" obj_index="-1"/>
            </Sequence>
        </Parallel>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="GoTo">
        <Sequence name="GoTo">
            <Action ID="pathPlanner" number_of_waypoints="{number_of_waypoints_}" waypoints="{waypoints_}" x="{x_input}" y="{y_input}"/>
            <Condition ID="CheckPositiveCondition" value="{number_of_waypoints_}"/>
            <Repeat num_cycles="{number_of_waypoints_}">
                <Sequence>
                    <Action ID="ExecutePathAction" w_x="{_w_x}" w_y="{_w_y}" waypoints="{waypoints_}"/>
                    <Action ID="moveRoboterPosition" approachRadius="100" x="{_w_x}" y="{_w_y}"/>

        </Condition>
        <Action ID="ExecutePathAction">
            <output_port default="{_w_x}" name="w_x"/>
            <output_port default="{_w_y}" name="w_y"/>
            <input_port default="{waypoints_}" name="waypoints"/>
        </Action>
        <SubTreePlus ID="GoTo">
            <input_port default="false" name="__shared_blackboard">If false (default), the Subtree has an isolated blackboard and needs port remapping</input_port>
            <input_port default="1.61" name="x_input"/>
            <input_port default="7.76" name="y_input"/>

            <output_port default="{x_}" name="x"/>

        <Action ID="moveRoboterPosition">

            <input_port default="0" name="y"/>
        </Action>

        </Action>

mrq:
The mission describes a coordinated task that continuously updates a variant value while placing a specified object, navigating to a target location, and dropping the object with synchronized success. First, the robot will execute the following actions (VariantAction) for a set number of times or infinitely. It will execute the custom action VariantAction continuously until it fails or succeeds. Else it will keep executing the custom action VariantAction forever. It will execute the following actions (objectplacement, GoTo, objectdrop) simultaneously. If either a success threshold is reached or all actions succeed, the execution will be successful. Else if any action fails, the execution will fail. It will execute the custom action objectplacement with the query WOODENBOX_0.1_0.3_0.3_0.3. It will execute the custom action objectdrop with obj_index -1 after the GoTo subtree and objectplacement both succeed within the parallel success constraint.

Next, the mission describes a navigation task that plans waypoints to a target and then visits each waypoint in strict order. It will execute the following actions (pathPlanner, CheckPositiveCondition, Repeat) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action pathPlanner with inputs x 1.61 and y 7.76 and will store number_of_waypoints_ and waypoints_. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule to confirm that number_of_waypoints_ is positive. It will execute the following actions (Sequence) for a set number of times or infinitely using number_of_waypoints_ as the repeat count. It will execute the following actions (ExecutePathAction, moveRoboterPosition) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action ExecutePathAction using waypoints_ and will store _w_x and _w_y. It will run the custom action moveRoboterPosition to position itself at _w_x and _w_y with an approachRadius of 100 after the ExecutePathAction succeeds.

            </Fallback>
        </BB_Precondition>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!--  ===================================  -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="JobLeadAnimalToPasture">
        <BB_Precondition expected="LeadAnimalToPasture" key="JobType">
            <Fallback>
                <SequenceStar>
                    <Action ID="InitAnimalJob"/>
                    <Action ID="Move"/>
                    <Action ID="GrabAnimal"/>
                    <Action ID="GetItemDropPosition"/>
                    <Action ID="Move"/>
                    <Action ID="FinalMoveAnimal"/>
                    <Action ID="ReleaseAnimal"/>
                    <Action ID="FinishJob"/>
                </SequenceStar>
                <Action ID="AbortJob"/>
            </Fallback>
        </BB_Precondition>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!--  ===================================  -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="JobStandard">
        <Fallback>
            <SequenceStar name="StandardJobs">
                <Action ID="InitJob"/>

                <Action ID="Move"/>
                <Action ID="EquipTool"/>

            </SequenceStar>
            <Action ID="AbortJob"/>
        </Fallback>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!--  ===================================  -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="JobTameAnimal">

                    <Action ID="Move"/>
                    <Action ID="FinalMoveAnimal"/>
                    <Action ID="ReleaseAnimal"/>
                    <Action ID="FinishJob"/>

        </BB_Precondition>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!--  ===================================  -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="Jobs">
        <SequenceStar>
            <Action ID="GetJob"/>
            <FallbackStar>
                <SubTree ID="JobButcherAnimal"/>
                <SubTree ID="JobDyeAnimal"/>
                <SubTree ID="JobLeadAnimalToPasture"/>
                <SubTree ID="JobHarvestAnimal"/>

            </FallbackStar>
        </SequenceStar>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!--  ===================================  -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
        <SubTree ID="Jobs"/>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!--  ===================================  -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="MoveAllItemsToJob">
        <Sequence>
            <Inverter>
                <Sequence>
                    <Inverter>
                        <Condition ID="AllItemsInPlaceForJob"/>
                    </Inverter>
                    <SequenceStar>
                        <Action ID="Move"/>
                        <Action ID="PickUpItem"/>
                        <Action ID="GetItemDropPosition"/>
                        <Action ID="Move"/>
                        <Action ID="DropItem"/>
                    </SequenceStar>
                    <Action ID="ReturnAlwaysRunning"/>
                </Sequence>
            </Inverter>
            <Condition ID="AllItemsInPlaceForJob"/>
        </Sequence>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!--  ===================================  -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="OldMoveAllItemsToJob">
        <FallbackStar>
            <Condition ID="AllItemsInPlaceForJob"/>
            <RetryUntilSuccesful num_attempts="20">
                <Sequence>
                    <SequenceStar>
                        <Action ID="Move"/>
                        <Action ID="PickUpItem"/>
                        <Fallback>
                            <Action ID="GetItemDropPosition"/>
                            <Action ID="DropItem"/>
                        </Fallback>
                        <Fallback>
                            <Action ID="Move"/>
                            <Action ID="DropItem"/>
                        </Fallback>
                        <Action ID="DropItem"/>
                    </SequenceStar>
                    <Condition ID="AllItemsInPlaceForJob"/>
                </Sequence>
            </RetryUntilSuccesful>
            <ForceFailure>
                <Action ID="AbortJob"/>
            </ForceFailure>
        </FallbackStar>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!--  ===================================  -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="Sleepy">
        <SequenceStar name="Sleep">
            <Condition ID="IsSleepy"/>
            <ForceSuccess>
                <SequenceStar>
                    <Action ID="FindBed"/>
                    <Action ID="Move"/>
                </SequenceStar>
            </ForceSuccess>
            <Action ID="Sleep"/>
        </SequenceStar>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!--  ===================================  -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="Thirst">
        <SequenceStar>
            <Condition ID="IsThirsty"/>
            <Sequence>
                <SequenceStar name="Thirst">
                    <Action ID="FindDrink"/>
                    <Action ID="Move"/>
                    <Action ID="PickUpItem"/>
                    <ForceSuccess>
                        <SequenceStar>
                            <Action ID="FindDining"/>
                            <Action ID="Move"/>
                        </SequenceStar>
                    </ForceSuccess>
                    <Action ID="Drink"/>
                </SequenceStar>
                <Condition ID="IsDrinkFull"/>
            </Sequence>
        </SequenceStar>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!--  ===================================  -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="Trainer">
        <SequenceStar name="Trainer">
            <Condition ID="IsTrainer"/>
            <Action ID="FindTrainerPosition"/>
            <Action ID="Move"/>
            <Action ID="SuperviseTraining"/>
        </SequenceStar>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!--  ===================================  -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="Training">
        <SequenceStar name="Training">
            <Condition ID="IsTrainingTime"/>
            <Action ID="FindTrainingGround"/>
            <Action ID="Move"/>
            <Action ID="Train"/>
        </SequenceStar>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!--  ===================================  -->
    <TreeNodesModel>
        <Action ID="AbortJob"/>
        <Condition ID="AllItemsInPlaceForJob"/>
        <Condition ID="AllPickedUp"/>
        <Action ID="AttackTarget"/>
        <Action ID="ButcherAnimal"/>
        <Action ID="CheckUniform"/>
        <Action ID="ClaimItems"/>
        <SubTree ID="Combat"/>

        <Action ID="FinishJob"/>
        <Action ID="GetItemDropPosition"/>
        <Action ID="GetJob"/>
        <Action ID="GetTarget"/>
        <SubTree ID="GetUniform"/>
        <Action ID="GetWorkPosition"/>
        <Action ID="GrabAnimal"/>
        <Action ID="HarvestAnimal"/>
        <SubTree ID="Hunger"/>
        <Action ID="InitAnimalJob"/>
        <Action ID="InitButcherJob"/>
        <Action ID="InitJob"/>
        <Condition ID="IsButcherJob"/>
        <Condition ID="IsDrinkFull"/>

        <Condition ID="IsHungry"/>
        <Condition ID="IsInCombat"/>
        <Condition ID="IsSleepy"/>
        <Condition ID="IsThirsty"/>
        <Condition ID="IsTrainer"/>
        <Condition ID="IsTrainingTime"/>
        <Condition ID="IsVeryHungry"/>
        <Condition ID="IsVeryThirsty"/>
        <SubTree ID="JobButcherAnimal"/>
        <SubTree ID="JobDyeAnimal"/>
        <SubTree ID="JobHarvestAnimal"/>
        <SubTree ID="JobHaulMultiple"/>
        <SubTree ID="JobLeadAnimalToPasture"/>
        <SubTree ID="JobStandard"/>

mrq:
The mission describes a workforce robot that selects a job, gathers required items and tools, moves to work locations, handles animals when required, manages personal needs such as hunger, thirst, sleep, and uniform readiness, engages in combat when necessary, and participates in training activities.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (GetJob, FallbackStar over job handlers) in sequence. It will execute the following actions (JobButcherAnimal, JobDyeAnimal, JobLeadAnimalToPasture, JobHarvestAnimal, JobTameAnimal, JobHaulMultiple, JobStandard) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, it will start all over again until an action succeeds.

Next, it will read blackboard value and compare it against a set of cases for the expected value ButcherAnimal under key JobType. It will execute the following actions (Fallback over primary and abort) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will execute the following actions (InitAnimalJob, Move, GrabAnimal, GetItemDropPosition, Move, FinalMoveAnimal, ButcherAnimal, FinishJob) in sequence. If this sequence fails, it will execute the custom action AbortJob.

Next, it will read blackboard value and compare it against a set of cases for the expected value DyeAnimal under key JobType. It will execute the following actions (Fallback over primary and abort) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will execute the following actions (InitJob, ClaimItems, MoveAllItemsToJob, InitAnimalJob, Move, GrabAnimal, GetItemDropPosition, Move, FinalMoveAnimal, DyeAnimal, FinishJob) in sequence. If this sequence fails, it will execute the custom action AbortJob.

Next, it will read blackboard value and compare it against a set of cases for the expected value LeadAnimalToPasture under key JobType. It will execute the following actions (Fallback over primary and abort) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will execute the following actions (InitAnimalJob, Move, GrabAnimal, GetItemDropPosition, Move, FinalMoveAnimal, ReleaseAnimal, FinishJob) in sequence. If this sequence fails, it will execute the custom action AbortJob.

Next, it will read blackboard value and compare it against a set of cases for the expected value HarvestAnimal under key JobType. It will execute the following actions (Fallback over primary and abort) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will execute the following actions (InitAnimalJob, Move, GrabAnimal, HarvestAnimal, ReleaseAnimal, FinishJob) in sequence. If this sequence fails, it will execute the custom action AbortJob.

Next, it will read blackboard value and compare it against a set of cases for the expected value TameAnimal under key JobType. It will execute the following actions (Fallback over primary and abort) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will execute the following actions (InitAnimalJob, Move, TameAnimal, GrabAnimal, GetItemDropPosition, Move, FinalMoveAnimal, ReleaseAnimal, FinishJob) in sequence. If this sequence fails, it will execute the custom action AbortJob.

Next, it will read blackboard value and compare it against a set of cases for the expected value HauleMultipleItems under key JobType. It will execute the following actions (Fallback over primary and abort) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will execute the following actions (InitJob, FindTool, Move, EquipTool, Inverted pickup loop, AllPickedUp check, GetItemDropPosition, Move, DropAllItems, FinishJob) in sequence and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute inverted Sequence that contains an inverted AllPickedUp check, a Sequence of Move and PickUpItem, and the custom action ReturnAlwaysRunning, and it will make sure this inverted group returns the opposite outcome of its child. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for AllPickedUp after the inverted loop completes. It will continue to drop all items only if the AllPickedUp check succeeds. If this sequence fails, it will execute the custom action AbortJob.

Next, it will execute the following actions (Fallback over StandardJobs and AbortJob) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will execute the following actions (InitJob, ClaimItems, FindTool, Move, EquipTool, MoveAllItemsToJob, GetWorkPosition, Move, Work, FinishJob) in sequence and stop the sequence if any action fails. If this sequence fails, it will execute the custom action AbortJob.

Next, it will execute the following actions (SequenceStar over IsInCombat, AbortJob, ForceSuccess, IsInCombat) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for IsInCombat. It will execute the custom action AbortJob after the combat condition succeeds. It will execute the following actions (GetTarget, Move, Sequence over TargetAdjacent and AttackTarget) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will execute these actions and make sure it always succeeds. It will execute the following actions (TargetAdjacent, AttackTarget) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for TargetAdjacent before attacking. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for IsInCombat at the end of the loop.

Next, it will execute the following actions (SequenceStar over CheckUniform, Fallback over Move and UniformCleanUp, EquipUniform) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will execute the following actions (Move, UniformCleanUp) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will execute the custom action EquipUniform after the preparation succeeds.

Next, it will execute the following actions (SequenceStar over IsHungry and inner Sequence) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for IsHungry. It will execute the following actions (SequenceStar Hunger and IsFull) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute the following actions (FindFood, Move, PickUpItem, ForceSuccess over dining move, Eat) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will execute the following actions (FindDining, Move) in sequence and make sure it always succeeds. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for IsFull after eating.

Next, it will execute the following actions (Sequence over Inverted shuttle loop and AllItemsInPlaceForJob) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute inverted Sequence that contains an inverted AllItemsInPlaceForJob check, a SequenceStar with Move, PickUpItem, GetItemDropPosition, Move, DropItem, and the custom action ReturnAlwaysRunning, and it will make sure this inverted group returns the opposite outcome of its child. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for AllItemsInPlaceForJob after the shuttle loop completes.

Next, it will execute the following actions (FallbackStar over AllItemsInPlaceForJob, RetryUntilSuccessful, ForceFailure over AbortJob) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, it will start all over again until an action succeeds. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for AllItemsInPlaceForJob. It will keep trying to execute a Sequence until it succeeds. If a try succeeds, it will stop retrying and continue. If a try fails, it will keep retrying until it succeeds. It will execute the following actions (SequenceStar with Move, PickUpItem, Fallback over GetItemDropPosition and DropItem, Fallback over Move and DropItem, DropItem, AllItemsInPlaceForJob) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute the following actions (GetItemDropPosition, DropItem) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will execute the following actions (Move, DropItem) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will execute the custom action AbortJob and make sure it always fails if the prior options cannot succeed.

Next, it will execute the following actions (SequenceStar over IsSleepy, ForceSuccess over bed move, Sleep) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for IsSleepy. It will execute the following actions (FindBed, Move) in sequence and make sure it always succeeds. It will execute the custom action Sleep after reaching the bed or after a best-effort approach.

Next, it will execute the following actions (SequenceStar over IsThirsty and inner Sequence) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for IsThirsty. It will execute the following actions (SequenceStar Thirst and IsDrinkFull) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute the following actions (FindDrink, Move, PickUpItem, ForceSuccess over dining move, Drink) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will execute the following actions (FindDining, Move) in sequence and make sure it always succeeds. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for IsDrinkFull after drinking.

Next, it will execute the following actions (SequenceStar over IsTrainer, FindTrainerPosition, Move, SuperviseTraining) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for IsTrainer. It will execute the custom action SuperviseTraining after moving to the trainer position.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (SequenceStar over IsTrainingTime, FindTrainingGround, Move, Train) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for IsTrainingTime. It will execute the custom action Train after moving to the training ground.

<root main_tree_to_execute="MainTree">
  <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
    <Sequence name="root">
      <FallbackStar name="check_motion">
        <Inverter name="is_not_stuck">
          <IsStuck/>
        </Inverter>
        <SequenceStar name="stuck_recovery">
          <Stop/>
          <BackUp/>
          <Spin/>
        </SequenceStar>
      </FallbackStar>
      <SequenceStar name="navigate">
        <ComputePathToPose endpoints="${endpoints}" path="${path}"/>
        <FollowPath path="${path}"/>
      </SequenceStar>
    </Sequence>
  </BehaviorTree>
</root>
mrq:
The mission describes a mobile navigation task that first checks for a stuck condition with recovery and then performs path planning and following in order.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (FallbackStar over is_not_stuck, stuck_recovery) consecutively. It will execute inverted the custom action IsStuck. If IsStuck succeeds, it will reverse it to fail. Else if IsStuck fails, it will reverse it to succeed. It will execute the following actions (Stop, BackUp, Spin) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will execute the following actions (is_not_stuck, stuck_recovery) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, it will start all over again until an action succeeds.

Next, it will execute the following actions (ComputePathToPose, FollowPath) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will run the ROS action ComputePathToPose with the provided endpoints and will store the resulting path. It will run the ROS action FollowPath using the stored path after the path computation succeeds.

<root main_tree_to_execute="MainTree">
  <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
    <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="6" name="NavigateRecovery">
      <PipelineSequence name="NavigateWithReplanning">
        <RateController hz="1.0">
          <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="1" name="ComputePathToPose">
            <ComputePathToPose goal="{goal}" path="{path}" planner_id="GridBased"/>
          <!--  <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearGlobalCostmap-Context" service_name="global_costmap/clear_entirely_global_costmap"/>-->
            <!--<Wait wait_duration="3"/>-->
            <SequenceStar name="ResetGlobalPath">
              <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearGlobalCostmap-Subtree" service_name="global_costmap/clear_entirely_global_costmap"/>
              <Wait wait_duration="1"/>
            </SequenceStar>
          </RecoveryNode>
        </RateController>
        <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="1" name="FollowPath">
          <FollowPath path="{path}" controller_id="FollowPath"/>
          <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearLocalCostmap-Context" service_name="local_costmap/clear_entirely_local_costmap"/>
        </RecoveryNode>
      </PipelineSequence>
      <ReactiveFallback name="RecoveryFallback">
        <GoalUpdated/>
        <SequenceStar name="RecoveryActions">
          <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearLocalCostmap-Subtree" service_name="local_costmap/clear_entirely_local_costmap"/>
          <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearGlobalCostmap-Subtree" service_name="global_costmap/clear_entirely_global_costmap"/>
          <Spin spin_dist="1.57"/>
          <Wait wait_duration="5"/>
        </SequenceStar>
      </ReactiveFallback>
    </RecoveryNode>
  </BehaviorTree>
</root>
mrq:
The mission describes a navigation task that periodically replans a global path at 1 Hz, follows the path, and performs recoveries when needed.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (NavigateWithReplanning, RecoveryFallback) under a recovery strategy with six retries. It will execute main NavigateWithReplanning. If it fails, it will execute recovery action RecoveryFallback. After recovery, it will retry main action NavigateWithReplanning. If recovery action RecoveryFallback fails and retries are exhausted, it will stop execution.

Next, it will execute the following actions (RateController, FollowPath) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. While an action is running or has succeeded, it will go to the next action. It will run RateController to pace the upstream planning at 1.0 Hz. It will then run the FollowPath recovery block.

Next, it will execute main ComputePathToPose under a recovery strategy with one retry. If it fails, it will execute recovery action ResetGlobalPath. After recovery, it will retry main action ComputePathToPose. If recovery action ResetGlobalPath fails and retries are exhausted, it will stop execution for this block. It will run the ROS action ComputePathToPose with the provided goal and will store the resulting path. It will then perform ResetGlobalPath only upon failure of ComputePathToPose.

Next, it will execute the following actions (ClearGlobalCostmap-Subtree, Wait) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will call the ROS action ClearEntireCostmap named ClearGlobalCostmap-Subtree to clear the global costmap. It will run the ROS action Wait for 1 second to allow the clearing to take effect.

Next, it will execute main FollowPath under a recovery strategy with one retry. If it fails, it will execute recovery action ClearLocalCostmap-Context. After recovery, it will retry main action FollowPath. If recovery action ClearLocalCostmap-Context fails and retries are exhausted, it will stop execution for this block. It will run the ROS action FollowPath using the stored path with the specified controller. It will call the ROS action ClearEntireCostmap named ClearLocalCostmap-Context to clear the local costmap only upon failure of FollowPath.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (GoalUpdated, RecoveryActions) in order. If any action succeeds, it will stop execution. Else it will keep executing and will re-evaluate previous actions whenever an action is running. It will check for a new goal using the ROS action GoalUpdated and will succeed immediately if a new goal exists. It will execute RecoveryActions if no new goal is detected.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (ClearLocalCostmap-Subtree, ClearGlobalCostmap-Subtree, Spin, Wait) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will call the ROS action ClearEntireCostmap named ClearLocalCostmap-Subtree to clear the local costmap. It will call the ROS action ClearEntireCostmap named ClearGlobalCostmap-Subtree to clear the global costmap. It will run the ROS action Spin with a spin distance of 1.57 to free the robot from local minima. It will run the ROS action Wait for 5 seconds to allow the environment and sensors to settle.

<root main_tree_to_execute="MainTree">
  <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
    <ReactiveFallback name="FollowWaypoints">
      <AllGoalsAchieved goal_achieved="{goal_achieved}"/>
      <KeepRunningUntilFailure>
        <ReactiveSequence>
          <GetNextGoal goals="{goals}" goal="{goal}" goal_achieved="{goal_achieved}"/>
          <ForceSuccess>
            <NavigateToPose goal="{goal}"/>
          </ForceSuccess>
          <SetBlackboard output_key="goal_achieved" value="true"/>
        </ReactiveSequence>
      </KeepRunningUntilFailure>
    </ReactiveFallback>
  </BehaviorTree>
</root>
mrq:
The mission describes waypoint following that continues until all goals are achieved and skips failed navigations while marking each processed goal as achieved.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (AllGoalsAchieved, KeepRunningUntilFailure) in order. If AllGoalsAchieved succeeds, it will stop execution. Else keep executing and re-evaluating previous actions whenever an action is running. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule to determine if all goals are achieved. It will execute ReactiveSequence while it does not fail and will stop looping and continue execution only if ReactiveSequence fails.

Next, it will execute the following actions (GetNextGoal, ForceSuccess, SetBlackboard) in sequence. If any action fails, it will stop execution. While any action is still running, it will re-evaluate previous actions. It will run the custom action GetNextGoal to select the next goal from the provided goals and to update the current goal and the goal achieved status. It will execute NavigateToPose and make sure it always succeeds. It will run the ROS action NavigateToPose using the current goal. It will run the custom action SetBlackboard to set the key goal_achieved to true after the forced-success navigation completes.

mrq:
The mission describes a patrolling task that sets four waypoint goals and navigates to each goal in a fixed order for three full cycles.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (SetBlackboard, SetBlackboard, SetBlackboard, SetBlackboard, Sequence) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action SetBlackboard to write the position -0.579;-1.341;0.0 into the blackboard under the key Goal_a. It will run the custom action SetBlackboard to write the position 1.214;-1.533;0.0 into the blackboard under the key Goal_b. Finally, it will execute the following actions (move right, move down, move down, move left, move left, move left, drop off passenger) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. The mission describes a repeated locomotion and navigation task that enforces a linear temporal logic sequence, maintains locomotion readiness, executes a move action, and updates the task state on each cycle.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (LTLPreCheck, ReactiveSequence, UpdateLTL) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action LTLPreCheck to validate the current temporal logic state against the desired sequence. It will then execute the following actions (Fallback, Fallback) in sequence and, while any action is still running, it will re-evaluate previous actions. It will finally run the custom action UpdateLTL to update the action sequence and desired temporal logic state while producing a navigation goal.

Next, it will execute the following actions (LocomotionStatusCheck, Sequence) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will run the custom action LocomotionStatusCheck to confirm that locomotion is already ready. It will then execute the following actions (RecoveryStand, Delay) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. While the Delay is running or has succeeded, it will go to the next action. It will run the custom action RecoveryStand to achieve a stable standing posture. It will execute delayed custom action LocomotionStart and, if its execution succeeds, it will start a 2000 millisecond delay and will resume after the delay period is exhausted.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (MoveAction) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will run the custom action MoveAction to perform a movement toward the provided navigation goal and to report movement status.

Finally, it will execute the custom action UpdateLTL to append the executed action to the action sequence and to advance the desired temporal logic state using the current navigation goal.

Finally, it will execute the custom action Repeat for a set number of times or infinitely, which will re-run the described sequence for the configured number of cycles.

<root main_tree_to_execute="BehaviorTree">
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="BehaviorTree">
        <Repeat num_cycles="{num_cycles}">
            <Sequence>
                <Fallback>
                    <Condition ID="LTLPreCheck" ltl_state_current="{ltl_state_current}" ltl_state_desired_sequence="{ltl_state_desired_sequence}" ltl_state_executed_sequence="{ltl_state_executed_sequence}"/>
                    <ForceFailure>
                        <Action ID="ReplanningRequestLevel2" ltl_state_current="{ltl_state_current}" ltl_state_executed_sequence="{ltl_state_executed_sequence}" replanning_request="{replanning_request}"/>
                    </ForceFailure>
                </Fallback>
                <ReactiveSequence>
                    <Fallback>
                        <Action ID="FakeDetectionLevel1" replanning_fake_input="{replanning_fake_input}"/>
                        <ForceFailure>
                            <Action ID="ReplanningRequestLevel1" ltl_state_current="{ltl_state_current}" ltl_state_executed_sequence="{ltl_state_executed_sequence}" replanning_request="{replanning_request}"/>
                        </ForceFailure>
                    </Fallback>
                    <Fallback>
                        <Condition ID="ReactiveLTLStateCheck" ltl_state_current="{ltl_state_current}" ltl_state_desired_sequence="{ltl_state_desired_sequence}"/>
                        <ForceFailure>
                            <Action ID="ReplanningRequestLevel2" ltl_state_current="{ltl_state_current}" ltl_state_executed_sequence="{ltl_state_executed_sequence}" replanning_request="{replanning_request}"/>
                        </ForceFailure>
                    </Fallback>
                    <Fallback>
                        <Action ID="FakeDetectionLevel3" replanning_fake_input="{replanning_fake_input}"/>
                        <ForceFailure>
                            <Action ID="ReplanningRequestLevel3" ltl_state_current="{ltl_state_current}" ltl_state_executed_sequence="{ltl_state_executed_sequence}" replanning_request="{replanning_request}"/>
                        </ForceFailure>
                    </Fallback>
                    <Fallback>
                        <Switch4 case_1="move" case_2="stay" case_3="start_training" case_4="terminate_training" variable="{bt_action_type}">
                            <Action ID="MoveAction" bt_action_type="{bt_action_type}" current_action="{current_action}" goal_sent="{goal_sent}" move_base_finished="{move_base_finished}" move_base_idle="{move_base_idle}"/>
                            <Action ID="StayAction" bt_action_type="{bt_action_type}" current_action="{current_action}" ltl_state_current="{ltl_state_current}"/>
                            <Action ID="GuideAction" bt_action_type="{bt_action_type}" current_action="{current_action}" ltl_state_current="{ltl_state_current}"/>
                            <Action ID="BackNormalAction" bt_action_type="{bt_action_type}" current_action="{current_action}" ltl_state_current="{ltl_state_current}"/>
                            <AlwaysFailure/>
                        </Switch4>
                        <ForceFailure>
                            <Action ID="ReplanningRequestLevel3" ltl_state_current="{ltl_state_current}" ltl_state_executed_sequence="{ltl_state_executed_sequence}" replanning_request="{replanning_request}"/>
                        </ForceFailure>
                    </Fallback>
                </ReactiveSequence>
                <Action ID="UpdateLTL" action_sequence="{action_sequence}" action_sequence_executed="{action_sequence_executed}" bt_action_type="{bt_action_type}" current_action="{current_action}" ltl_state_desired_sequence="{ltl_state_desired_sequence}"/>
            </Sequence>
        </Repeat>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <TreeNodesModel>
        <Action ID="BackNormalAction">
            <input_port name="bt_action_type"/>
            <input_port name="current_action"/>
            <inout_port name="ltl_state_current"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="DropAction">
            <input_port name="bt_action_type"/>
            <input_port name="current_action"/>
            <inout_port name="ltl_state_current"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="FakeDetectionLevel1">
            <input_port name="replanning_fake_input"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="FakeDetectionLevel2">
            <input_port name="replanning_fake_input"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="FakeDetectionLevel3">
            <input_port name="replanning_fake_input"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="GuideAction">
            <input_port name="bt_action_type"/>
            <input_port name="current_action"/>
            <inout_port name="ltl_state_current"/>
        </Action>
        <Condition ID="LTLPreCheck">
            <input_port name="ltl_state_current"/>
            <input_port name="ltl_state_desired_sequence"/>
            <inout_port name="ltl_state_executed_sequence"/>
        </Condition>
        <Action ID="LocomotionStart">
            <input_port name="locomotion_status"/>
        </Action>
        <Condition ID="LocomotionStatusCheck">
            <input_port name="locomotion_status"/>
        </Condition>
        <Action ID="MoveAction">
            <input_port name="bt_action_type"/>
            <input_port name="current_action"/>
            <output_port name="goal_sent"/>
            <input_port name="move_base_finished"/>
            <input_port name="move_base_idle"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="PickAction">
            <input_port name="bt_action_type"/>
            <input_port name="current_action"/>
            <inout_port name="ltl_state_current"/>
        </Action>
        <Condition ID="ReactiveLTLStateCheck">
            <input_port name="ltl_state_current"/>
            <input_port name="ltl_state_desired_sequence"/>
        </Condition>
        <Action ID="RecoveryStand">
            <input_port name="locomotion_status"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="ReplanningRequestLevel1">
            <input_port name="ltl_state_current"/>
            <inout_port name="ltl_state_executed_sequence"/>
            <inout_port name="replanning_request"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="ReplanningRequestLevel2">
            <input_port name="ltl_state_current"/>
            <inout_port name="ltl_state_executed_sequence"/>
            <inout_port name="replanning_request"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="ReplanningRequestLevel3">
            <input_port name="ltl_state_current"/>
            <inout_port name="ltl_state_executed_sequence"/>
            <inout_port name="replanning_request"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="StayAction">
            <input_port name="bt_action_type"/>
            <input_port name="current_action"/>
            <inout_port name="ltl_state_current"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="SynchronizedTransitionAction">
            <input_port name="bt_action_type"/>
            <input_port name="current_action"/>
            <inout_port name="ltl_state_current"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="UpdateLTL">
            <inout_port name="action_sequence"/>
            <inout_port name="action_sequence_executed"/>
            <output_port name="bt_action_type"/>
            <output_port name="current_action"/>
            <inout_port name="ltl_state_desired_sequence"/>
        </Action>
    </TreeNodesModel>
    <!-- ////////// -->
</root>
mrq:
The mission describes a repeated supervisory control cycle that validates a temporal task state, reacts to environment and plan status, executes context-appropriate actions, and updates the task progression.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (Fallback, ReactiveSequence, UpdateLTL) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute the following actions (Condition LTLPreCheck, ForceFailure(ReplanningRequestLevel2)) consecutively. If any action (Condition LTLPreCheck) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will run the custom condition LTLPreCheck to verify that the current temporal logic state aligns with the desired sequence and the executed sequence. It will execute the custom action ReplanningRequestLevel2 and make sure it always fails to force the Fallback to proceed when the pre-check does not pass.

Next, it will execute the following actions (Fallback, Fallback, Fallback, Fallback) in sequence. If any action fails, it will stop execution. While any action is still running, it will re-evaluate previous actions. It will execute the following actions (Action FakeDetectionLevel1, ForceFailure(ReplanningRequestLevel1)) consecutively. If any action (Action FakeDetectionLevel1) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will run the custom action FakeDetectionLevel1 to probe replanning triggers using the provided fake input. It will execute the custom action ReplanningRequestLevel1 and make sure it always fails to enable fallback to the detection when the probe does not yield success. It will execute the following actions (Condition ReactiveLTLStateCheck, ForceFailure(ReplanningRequestLevel2)) consecutively. If any action (Condition ReactiveLTLStateCheck) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will run the custom condition ReactiveLTLStateCheck to confirm that the current temporal logic state matches the desired sequence. It will execute the custom action ReplanningRequestLevel2 and make sure it always fails to allow fallback to the condition when the check is not satisfied. It will execute the following actions (Action FakeDetectionLevel3, ForceFailure(ReplanningRequestLevel3)) consecutively. If any action (Action FakeDetectionLevel3) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will run the custom action FakeDetectionLevel3 to test higher-severity replanning cues. It will execute the custom action ReplanningRequestLevel3 and make sure it always fails to bias the fallback toward successful detection. It will execute the following actions (Switch, ForceFailure(ReplanningRequestLevel3)) consecutively. If any action (Switch) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will read blackboard value and compare it against a set of cases. If a case matches the blackboard value, it will execute corresponding Action. Else if no case matches, it will execute default Action. It will run the custom action MoveAction when the blackboard action type equals move. It will run the custom action StayAction when the blackboard action type equals stay. It will run the custom action GuideAction when the blackboard action type equals start_training. It will run the custom action BackNormalAction when the blackboard action type equals terminate_training. It will execute the custom action ReplanningRequestLevel3 and make sure it always fails to compel the Fallback to prefer the switch-selected action.

Finally, it will run the custom action UpdateLTL to publish the next action type and current action while updating the desired temporal sequence after the reactive control completes. It will execute the Repeat for a set number of times or infinitely.

First, the robot will execute main Action using a recovery scheme. It will execute main [Action]. If it fails, execute recovery action ([Action]). After recovery, retry main action ([Action]). If recovery action ([Action]) fails and retries are exhausted, stop execution. It will apply this recovery cycle up to six retries before stopping.

Next, it will execute the following actions (RateController → ComputePathToPose, FollowPath) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. While an action is running or has succeeded, go to the next action. It will run the ROS action ComputePathToPose at a regulated rate of 1.0 Hz and will store the resulting path. It will run the ROS action FollowPath using the stored path while path computation is running or after it succeeds.

Finally, it will execute the following actions [GoalUpdated, Wait] in order. If [Action] succeeds, stop execution. Else keep executing and re-evaluating previous actions whenever an action is running. It will check for a new goal and will stop recovery immediately when a goal update is detected. It will run the ROS action Wait for 5 seconds when no new goal is detected and will continue to re-check for a goal update during the wait.

mrq:
The mission describes a single top-level sequence that organizes movement and fallback strategies using ordered execution, conditional success, forced outcomes, and logical inversion to complete a staged task.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (go1, Fallback, go2, go3, Sequence4) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action go1 as the initial step before any alternative strategy is attempted.

Next, it will execute the following actions (stay1, Sequence, Sequence, stay2) consecutively. If any action (stay1, Sequence, Sequence, stay2) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will run the custom action stay1 as the first attempt to satisfy the fallback. It will then execute the following actions (go1, go5, Sequence3) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action go1 to start this sequence. It will run the custom action go5 immediately after go1 succeeds. It will then execute the following actions (go1, go2, isFull, go3) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action go1 to begin the nested sequence. It will run the custom action go2 after go1 succeeds. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for the condition isFull, and only if isFull is true will it proceed. It will run the custom action go3 only after isFull returns success. It will next execute the following actions (go6, go1, go2, go3) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action go6 to initiate this alternate route. It will run the custom action go1 after go6 succeeds. It will run the custom action go2 after go1 succeeds. It will run the custom action go3 after go2 succeeds. It will finally run the custom action stay2 as the last fallback option.

Next, it will run the custom action go2 as the third step in the top-level sequence after the fallback either succeeds or fails.

Next, it will run the custom action go3 as the fourth step in the top-level sequence after go2 succeeds.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (go1, go2, Fallback) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action go1 to start this concluding segment. It will run the custom action go2 after go1 succeeds. It will then execute the following actions (Inverter, ForceSuccess, cIName) consecutively. If any action (Inverter, ForceSuccess, cIName) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will execute inverted go4 and if go4 succeeds, it will reverse it to fail, else if go4 fails, it will reverse it to succeed. It will execute go2 and make sure it always succeeds. It will run the custom action cIName as the final fallback option for this segment.

      <Sequence name="NavigateWithReplanning">
        <RateController hz="1.0">
          <Fallback>
            <GoalReached/>
            <ComputePathToPose goal="${goal}" path="${path}"/>
          </Fallback>
        </RateController>

mrq:
The mission describes a navigation task that periodically replans a global path at 1 Hz and applies a recovery behavior when navigation fails.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (RateController, Fallback, FollowPath) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute delayed Fallback inside the ROS action RateController at 1.0 Hz and will then resume execution after each delay period. It will execute the following actions (GoalReached, ComputePathToPose) consecutively. If any action (GoalReached) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will run the ROS action GoalReached to test if the goal condition is already satisfied. It will run the ROS action ComputePathToPose with the provided goal and will store the resulting path if the goal is not already reached. It will run the ROS action FollowPath using the stored path after the fallback produces a path or confirms the goal is already reached.

Next, it will execute main Sequence. If it fails, it will execute recovery action (SequenceStar). After recovery, it will retry main action (Sequence). If recovery action (SequenceStar) fails and retries are exhausted, it will stop execution. It will execute the following actions (Spin) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will run the ROS action Spin to attempt to recover localization or obstacle clearance before retrying navigation.

It will run the ROS action ComputePathToPose inside the RateController at 1.0 Hz to update the path to the provided goal and store the result in the path variable. It will run the ROS action FollowPath using the most recently stored path with the specified controller while path computation continues at the defined rate.

It will execute the following actions (GoalReached, ComputePathToPose) consecutively. If any action (GoalReached) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution will fail. It will run the ROS action GoalReached to check whether the goal has been achieved before triggering planning. It will run the ROS action ComputePathToPose with the goal provided and will publish the resulting path to the blackboard when the goal is not yet achieved. mrq:
The mission describes a service task that will autonomously reach a known room, search for a bottle, grasp the bottle, and request help if autonomy fails.

First, it will execute the following actions (Autonomus) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails.

Next, it will execute the following actions (ReachRoom) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails.

Next, it will execute the following actions (ShouldFollow) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule. It will execute the following actions (GoToRoom_Fal) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails.

Next, it will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule. It will execute the custom action GoToRoom if the robot is not already in the room.

Next, it will execute the custom action FollowHuman after the room reaching attempt completes.

Next, it will execute the following actions (BottleFound) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails.

Next, it will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule. It will execute the custom action FindBottle if the bottle is not already found.

Next, it will execute the following actions (Fetch) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails.

Next, it will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule. It will execute the following actions (Grasp) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute the following actions (InvPoseCompute) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will execute the following actions (SeqInvPose) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule. It will execute the custom action ComputeInvPose if either the inverse pose was not computed or the computed pose is not valid.

Next, it will execute the following actions (GoInvPose) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule. It will execute the following actions (prepare) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute the custom action KeepArmsForGrasp. It will execute the custom action GoToInvPose.

Next, it will execute the following actions (Locate) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule. It will execute the custom action LocateBottle if the bottle is not already located.

Next, it will execute the custom action GraspBottle after the bottle is located and the inverse pose navigation completes.

Next, it will execute the custom action SetInvPoseInvaild after the grasp attempt completes.

Next, it will execute the custom action KeepRunning after invalidating the inverse pose.

Finally, it will execute the custom action AskForHelp if all autonomous actions in the main fallback fail.

The mission describes a control routine that manages suction valves, sets motion and scoring behaviors, manipulates an arm, maintains a timer-driven scoring policy, displays the score, and then checks sensors under a time condition.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (ReactiveFallback, ForceSuccess) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute the following actions [Inverter over Condition IsVentouseGauche, Action Attendre] in sequence. If the inverted check fails, it will stop execution. While the action Attendre is still running, it will re-evaluate previous actions. It will execute the following actions [ReactiveSequence, Action Pince] in order. If any earlier action succeeds, it will stop execution. Else it will keep executing and re-evaluating previous actions whenever an action is running. It will execute inverted [Condition IsVentouseGauche]. If the condition succeeds, it will reverse it to fail. Else if the condition fails, it will reverse it to succeed. It will run the custom action Attendre with a 3000 millisecond wait. It will run the custom action Pince with all valves set to false as the default action when the previous attempt does not succeed.

Next, it will execute the following actions (ForceSuccess) and make sure it always succeeds. It will execute the following actions (ForceSuccess, ForceSuccess, Action Bras, Action Bras, ForceSuccess, Action AfficherLeScore, SubTree TestSensors) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. While any action inside ForceSuccess is executed, it will be forced to succeed. It will execute the following actions (Condition IsVentouseGauche, Action Pince with left_valve true, Condition IsVentouseCentre, Action Pince with middle_valve true, Condition IsVentouseDroite, Action Pince with right_valve true) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute [Action] and make sure it always succeeds for this suction sequence. It will execute the following actions (Condition ScoreInferieurA with score 2, Action DefinirLeScore to 5, Action VitesseConstante with linear_x 0.1 angular_z 0 linear_y 0, Action Attendre 1000 ms, Action VitesseConstante with linear_x 0 angular_z 1 linear_y 0, Action Attendre 1000 ms, Action VitesseConstante with all zeros, Action LancerLaMinuterie for 5 seconds, Action Aller to x 0 y 0 theta 0) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute [Action] and make sure it always succeeds for this motion and timer sequence. It will run the custom action Bras with angle_base 100 and angle_pince 50. It will run the custom action Bras with angle_base 100 and angle_pince 150. It will execute the following actions [Condition MinuterieNonEcoulee, Fallback over two scoring sequences] in sequence. If any action fails, it will stop execution. While any action is still running, it will re-evaluate previous actions. It will execute [Action] and make sure it always succeeds for this reactive scoring policy. It will execute the following actions (Sequence with Condition ScoreInferieurA 100 and Action AjouterAuScore 10, Sequence with Condition ScoreSuperieurA 100 and Action Attendre 1000 ms and Action AjouterAuScore 1) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will run the custom action AfficherLeScore. It will execute the subtree TestSensors.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (Inverter, ForceSuccess x9) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails within the TestSensors subtree. It will execute inverted [Condition MinuterieNonEcoulee]. If the condition succeeds, it will reverse it to fail. Else if the condition fails, it will reverse it to succeed. It will execute [Condition IsCampViolet] and make sure it always succeeds. It will execute [Condition IsArretUrgence] and make sure it always succeeds. It will execute [Condition IsTirettePresente] and make sure it always succeeds. It will execute [Condition IsPaletCentre] and make sure it always succeeds. It will execute [Condition IsPaletDroit] and make sure it always succeeds. It will execute [Condition IsPaletGauche] and make sure it always succeeds. It will execute [Condition IsVentouseCentre] and make sure it always succeeds. It will execute [Condition IsVentouseDroite] and make sure it always succeeds. It will execute [Condition IsVentouseGauche] and make sure it always succeeds.

mrq:
The mission describes a pursuit-and-hunt task that first engages a specified target and otherwise hunts for prey or wanders. First, the robot will execute the following actions (GetTarget, Move, Sequence(TargetAdjacent, AttackTarget)) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will run the custom action GetTarget to select a target to pursue. It will run the custom action Move to advance toward the selected target after the target selection succeeds. It will execute the following actions (TargetAdjacent, AttackTarget) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for the condition TargetAdjacent. It will run the custom action AttackTarget only after the adjacency check succeeds.

Next, it will execute the following actions (SequenceStar(Hunt), RandomMove) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, it will start all over again until an action succeeds. It will execute the following actions (IsHungry, FindPrey, Move, KillPrey, EatPrey) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for the condition IsHungry. It will run the custom action FindPrey to locate a prey animal after the hunger check succeeds. It will run the custom action Move to close distance to the prey after the prey is found. It will run the custom action KillPrey to neutralize the prey after it has reached the prey. It will run the custom action EatPrey to consume the prey after the prey is killed. It will run the custom action RandomMove as a fallback whenever the hunt sequence does not yet succeed.

<root main_tree_to_execute="MainTree">
  <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
    <PipelineSequence name="NavigateWithReplanning">
      <RateController hz="1.0">
        <Sequence>
          <GoalUpdater input_goal="{goal}" output_goal="{updated_goal}">
            <ComputePathToPose goal="{updated_goal}" path="{path}" planner_id="GridBased"/>
          </GoalUpdater>
         <TruncatePath distance="1.0" input_path="{path}" output_path="{truncated_path}"/>
        </Sequence>
      </RateController>
      <KeepRunningUntilFailure>
        <FollowPath path="{truncated_path}" controller_id="FollowPath"/>
      </KeepRunningUntilFailure>
    </PipelineSequence>
  </BehaviorTree>
</root>
mrq:
The mission describes a navigation task that continually updates a moving goal, recomputes a path, truncates that path to maintain a following distance, and then follows the truncated path with continuous advancement.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (RateController, KeepRunningUntilFailure) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. While an action is running or has succeeded, it will go to the next action. It will rate-limit the upstream planning sequence to 1 Hz before advancing. It will keep executing the ROS action FollowPath as long as it does not fail.

Next, it will execute the following actions (Sequence) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run these actions at 1 Hz due to the rate controller. It will update the goal by running the custom action GoalUpdater, which will write an updated_goal. It will run the ROS action ComputePathToPose using the updated_goal and will store the resulting path. It will run the custom action TruncatePath to produce a truncated_path that ends 1.0 meters before the target along the computed path.

Finally, it will execute the ROS action FollowPath using the truncated_path with the specified controller after the truncated path becomes available. It will execute FollowPath and, if FollowPath succeeds, it will restart it. It will stop repeating FollowPath if FollowPath fails.

<!--
  This Behavior Tree replans the global path periodically at 1 Hz and it also has
  recovery actions.
-->
<root main_tree_to_execute="MainTree">
  <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
    <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="6" name="NavigateRecovery">
      <PipelineSequence name="NavigateWithReplanning">
        <RateController hz="1.0">
          <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="1" name="ComputePathToPose">
            <ComputePathToPose goal="{goal}" path="{path}" planner_id="GridBased"/>
            <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearGlobalCostmap-Context" service_name="global_costmap/clear_entirely_global_costmap"/>
          </RecoveryNode>
        </RateController>
        <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="1" name="FollowPath">
          <FollowPath path="{path}" controller_id="FollowPath"/>
          <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearLocalCostmap-Context" service_name="local_costmap/clear_entirely_local_costmap"/>
        </RecoveryNode>
      </PipelineSequence>
      <ReactiveFallback name="RecoveryFallback">
        <GoalUpdated/>
        <SequenceStar name="RecoveryActions">
          <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearLocalCostmap-Subtree" service_name="local_costmap/clear_entirely_local_costmap"/>
          <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearGlobalCostmap-Subtree" service_name="global_costmap/clear_entirely_global_costmap"/>
          <Spin spin_dist="1.57"/>
          <Wait wait_duration="5"/>
        </SequenceStar>
      </ReactiveFallback>
    </RecoveryNode>
  </BehaviorTree>
</root>
mrq:
The mission describes a navigation task that periodically replans a global path at 1 Hz, follows the path with local recovery, and escalates to broader recovery behaviors when needed.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (RateController, ComputePathToPose, ClearEntireCostmap-Context, FollowPath, ClearEntireCostmap-Context) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute the following actions (RateController, RecoveryNode, RecoveryNode) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. While an action is running or has succeeded, it will go to the next action. It will run the custom action RateController at 1.0 Hz to pace downstream actions. It will execute main the ROS action ComputePathToPose and make sure it always succeeds by using one retry. If it fails, it will run the ROS action ClearEntireCostmap-Context to clear the global costmap and then retry the ROS action ComputePathToPose once. It will execute main the ROS action FollowPath and make sure it always succeeds by using one retry. If it fails, it will run the ROS action ClearEntireCostmap-Context to clear the local costmap and then retry the ROS action FollowPath once.

Next, it will execute main the PipelineSequence subtree described earlier and, if it fails, it will execute recovery action (RecoveryFallback). After recovery, it will retry main action (the PipelineSequence subtree). If recovery action (RecoveryFallback) fails and retries are exhausted after six attempts, it will stop execution.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (GoalUpdated, RecoveryActions) in order. If any action succeeds, it will stop execution. Else it will keep executing and re-evaluating previous actions whenever an action is running. It will execute the following actions (ClearLocalCostmap-Subtree, ClearGlobalCostmap-Subtree, Spin, Wait) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will run the ROS action GoalUpdated and will stop the fallback if a new goal is detected. It will run the ROS action ClearLocalCostmap-Subtree to clear the local costmap as the first recovery step. It will run the ROS action ClearGlobalCostmap-Subtree to clear the global costmap as the second recovery step. It will run the ROS action Spin with a rotation distance of 1.57 radians to help free the platform. It will run the ROS action Wait for 5 seconds to allow sensors and maps to stabilize.
