mrq:
The mission describes a social interaction task that first tries to gaze at a selected face while adjusting posture, and otherwise lowers its arms, with a separate posture-only subtree and an error speech subtree for a different game-state context.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (GetPoseArray, SelectPose, Parallel) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action GetPoseArray to read a list of face poses from the faces topic and will store the result as a pose array. It will run the custom action SelectPose to pick the first pose from the stored pose array and will store it as a single stamped pose. It will execute the following actions (GazeAtTarget, Fallback) simultaneously. If either a success threshold is reached or all actions succeed, the execution was successful. Else if any action fails, the execution fails. It will run the custom action GazeAtTarget to direct gaze to the stored stamped pose in the base_footprint frame. It will execute the following actions (Sequence, SubTree LowArms) consecutively. If any action (Sequence) fails, move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will execute the following actions (SelectFieldFromPoseStamped, CompareDouble, ReactiveSequence) in order while checking a condition for each action. If condition ID is true move to the next action. Else if condition ID is false, end the sequence. It will run the custom action SelectFieldFromPoseStamped to read the x field from the stored stamped pose and will output it as x_thresh. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule by comparing x_thresh with 1.0 using the less-than condition. It will execute the following actions (SelectFieldFromPoseStamped, MoveTorso) in sequence. If an action fails, stop execution. While an action is still running, re-evaluate previous actions. It will run the custom action SelectFieldFromPoseStamped to read the y field from the stored stamped pose and will output it as y_thresh. It will run the custom action MoveTorso to tilt the torso by −20 degrees on the specified topic. It will run the subtree LowArms if the preceding sequence fails within the fallback.

Next, it will execute the subtree LowArms if the preceding parallel action fails within the outer sequence or if the outer sequence fails within the outer fallback.

Finally, the mission describes an error notification task that repeatedly informs by speech and waits during an unknown game-state condition. It will execute the following actions (Speak, Speak, WaitForXSeconds) in sequence. If any action succeeds, remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, start the sequence again from the beginning. It will run the custom action Speak to say “Não sei o estado do jogo” in European Portuguese with the specified voice. It will run the custom action Speak to say “Chamem o assistente” in European Portuguese with the specified voice. It will run the custom action WaitForXSeconds to wait 60 seconds and then return success.

<?xml version="1.0"?>
<root main_tree_to_execute="BehaviorTree">
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="BehaviorTree">
        <Repeat num_cycles="{num_cycles}">
            <Sequence>
                <Action ID="LTLPreCheck" ltl_state_current="{ltl_state_current}" ltl_state_desired_sequence="{ltl_state_desired_sequence}"/>
                <ReactiveSequence>
                    <Fallback>
                        <Action ID="LocomotionStatusCheck" locomotion_status="{locomotion_status}"/>
                        <Sequence>
                            <Action ID="RecoveryStand" locomotion_status="{locomotion_status}"/>
                            <Delay delay_msec="2000">
                                <Action ID="LocomotionStart" locomotion_status="{locomotion_status}"/>
                            </Delay>
                        </Sequence>
                    </Fallback>
                    <Fallback>
                        <Action ID="MoveAction" action="{action}" move_base_finished="{move_base_finished}" move_base_idle="{move_base_idle}" nav_goal="{nav_goal}"/>
                    </Fallback>
                </ReactiveSequence>
                <Action ID="UpdateLTL" action_sequence="{action_sequence}" ltl_state_desired_sequence="{ltl_state_desired_sequence}" nav_goal="{nav_goal}"/>
            </Sequence>
        </Repeat>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <TreeNodesModel>
        <Action ID="LTLPreCheck">
            <input_port name="ltl_state_current"/>
            <input_port name="ltl_state_desired_sequence"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="LocomotionStart">
            <input_port name="locomotion_status"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="LocomotionStatusCheck">
            <input_port name="locomotion_status"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="MoveAction">
            <output_port name="action"/>
            <input_port name="move_base_finished"/>
            <input_port name="move_base_idle"/>
            <input_port name="nav_goal"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="RecoveryStand">
            <input_port name="locomotion_status"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="Replanning2"/>
        <Action ID="Replanning3"/>
        <Action ID="UpdateLTL">
            <inout_port name="action_sequence"/>
            <inout_port name="ltl_state_desired_sequence"/>
            <output_port name="nav_goal"/>
        </Action>
    </TreeNodesModel>
    <!-- ////////// -->
</root>
mrq:
The mission describes a repeated navigation-and-task execution loop driven by temporal logic goals. First, the robot will execute the following actions (LTLPreCheck, ReactiveSequence, UpdateLTL) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action LTLPreCheck using the current temporal logic state and the desired state sequence. It will run the ReactiveSequence after the pre-check succeeds. It will run the custom action UpdateLTL to update the action sequence and to output the next navigation goal after the ReactiveSequence succeeds.

Next, it will execute the following actions [Fallback, Fallback] in sequence. If any action fails, stop execution. While any action is still running, re-evaluate previous actions. It will execute the following actions (LocomotionStatusCheck, Sequence) consecutively. If any action fails, move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will run the custom action LocomotionStatusCheck to verify that locomotion is ready. It will execute the following actions (RecoveryStand, Delay) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails after the locomotion status check fails. It will run the custom action RecoveryStand to return to a stable stance before starting locomotion. It will execute delayed LocomotionStart and, if execution succeeds, start delay timer and, after the delay period is exhausted, resume execution. It will execute the following actions (MoveAction) consecutively. If any action fails, move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will run the custom action MoveAction to command motion toward the provided navigation goal while monitoring move base status.

Finally, it will execute UpdateLTL for a set number of times or infinitely. It will execute UpdateLTL for the configured number of cycles as specified by num_cycles. It will keep executing the enclosing sequence for the configured number of cycles until the sequence fails or finishes all iterations.

<root main_tree_to_execute="root">

    <BehaviorTree ID="root">
        <Sequence>
            <SubTree ID="FullCompetitionMode" />
        </Sequence>
    </BehaviorTree>

    <BehaviorTree ID="FullCompetitionMode">
        <Repeat num_cycles="-1">
            <Sequence>
                <SubTree ID="GetInRestPose" />
                <SubTree ID="DoYourTrick" />
            </Sequence>
        </Repeat>
    </BehaviorTree>

    <BehaviorTree ID="GetInRestPose">
        <Sequence>
            <GetArmJointsForPose pose="REST" joints_pose="{joints_pose}" />
            <Parallel success_threshold="3" failure_threshold="3">
                <Sequence>
                    <HeadControl pitch="0" yaw="90" />
                    <TorsoControl relative_height="0.05" />
                </Sequence>
                <SetArmJointsLeft joints_pose="{joints_pose}" />
                <SetArmJointsRight joints_pose="{joints_pose}" />
            </Parallel>
        </Sequence>
    </BehaviorTree>

    <BehaviorTree ID="GetInUpTrayPose">
        <Sequence>
            <GetArmJointsForPose pose="UPTRAY" joints_pose="{joints_pose}" />
            <Parallel success_threshold="2" failure_threshold="2">
                <SetArmJointsLeft joints_pose="{joints_pose}" />
                <SetArmJointsRight joints_pose="{joints_pose}" />
            </Parallel>
        </Sequence>
    </BehaviorTree>

    <BehaviorTree ID="ClearAllOctomaps">
        <ForceSuccess>
            <Sequence>
                <ClearOctomap side="left" />
                <ClearOctomap side="right" />
            </Sequence>
        </ForceSuccess>
    </BehaviorTree>

    <BehaviorTree ID="UpdateOctomap">
        <ForceSuccess>
            <Sequence>
                <SubTree ID="ClearAllOctomaps" />
                <!-- <HeadControl pitch="-60.0" yaw="60" />
                <HeadControl pitch="60.0" yaw="60" /> -->
                <!-- <HeadControl pitch="-60.0" yaw="90" /> -->
                <HeadControl pitch="60.0" yaw="90" />
                <!-- <HeadControl pitch="-60.0" yaw="135" /> -->
                <HeadControl pitch="60.0" yaw="135" />
            </Sequence>
        </ForceSuccess>
    </BehaviorTree>

    <BehaviorTree ID="UpdateOctomapLowerHalf">
        <ForceSuccess>
            <Sequence>
                <SubTree ID="ClearAllOctomaps" />
                <HeadControl pitch="-60.0" yaw="90" />
                <HeadControl pitch="60.0" yaw="90" />
                <HeadControl pitch="-60.0" yaw="135" />
                <HeadControl pitch="60.0" yaw="135" />
            </Sequence>
        </ForceSuccess>
    </BehaviorTree>

    <BehaviorTree ID="DoYourTrick">
        <Repeat num_cycles="-1">
            <Sequence>
                <ForceSuccess>
                    <SubTree ID="FillYourTray" />
                </ForceSuccess>
                <ForceSuccess>
                    <SubTree ID="FillTheShelf" />
                </ForceSuccess>
            </Sequence>
        </Repeat>
    </BehaviorTree>

    <BehaviorTree ID="ScanTable">
        <!-- TODO if you query the ScanTable node too soon it crashes. No time to fix now -->
        <RetryUntilSuccesful num_attempts="-1">
            <Sequence>
                <TorsoControl relative_height="0.95" />
                <HeadControl pitch="0.0" yaw="140.0" />
                <Pause time_limit_sec="3" />
                <ScanTableStartScanning />
                <Pause time_limit_sec="3" />
                <SubTree ID="ClearAllOctomaps" />
                <ScanTableGetResults detected_tomato_cans="{detected_tomato_cans}" />
                <TraySetLoci tray_name="table" locus_poses="{detected_tomato_cans}" occupied="true" />
                <TrayUpdatePlanningScene />
            </Sequence>
        </RetryUntilSuccesful>
    </BehaviorTree>

    <BehaviorTree ID="ScanShelves">
        <Sequence>
            <TorsoControl relative_height="0.1" />
            <HeadControl pitch="90.0" yaw="90.0" />
            <Pause time_limit_sec="1" />
            <ScanShelves tomato_can_stocking_target_poses="{tomato_can_stocking_target_poses}" />
            <TraySetLoci tray_name="shelf" locus_poses="{tomato_can_stocking_target_poses}" occupied="false" />
            <TrayUpdatePlanningScene />
        </Sequence>
    </BehaviorTree>

    <BehaviorTree ID="FillYourTray">
        <Sequence>
            <SubTree ID="GoToTable" />
            <SubTree ID="ScanTable" />
            <TorsoControl relative_height="0.95" />
            <HeadControl pitch="0.0" yaw="135.0" />
            <Repeat num_cycles="-1">
                <Sequence>
                    <Sequence>
                        <SetBlackboard output_key="tray" value="table" />
                        <SetBlackboard output_key="side" value="RIGHT" />
                        <SubTree ID="TrayIsEmpty" tray="tray" side="side" />
                    </Sequence>
                    <Inverter>
                        <Sequence>
                            <SetBlackboard output_key="tray" value="backtray" />
                            <SubTree ID="TrayIsFull" tray="tray" />
                        </Sequence>
                    </Inverter>
                    <ForceSuccess>
                        <Sequence>
                            <SetBlackboard output_key="src_tray" value="table" />
                            <SetBlackboard output_key="dst_tray" value="backtray" />
                            <SubTree ID="RightArmFillYourTray" src_tray="src_tray" dst_tray="dst_tray" />
                        </Sequence>
                    </ForceSuccess>
                </Sequence>
            </Repeat>
        </Sequence>
    </BehaviorTree>


    <BehaviorTree ID="FillTheShelf">
        <Sequence>
            <SubTree ID="GetInRestPose" />
            <SubTree ID="GoToShelves" />
            <SubTree ID="ScanShelves" />
            <SubTree ID="UpdateOctomap" />
            <TorsoControl relative_height="0.95" />
            <HeadControl pitch="60.0" yaw="110.0" />
            <Repeat num_cycles="-1">
                <Sequence>
                    <Sequence>
                        <SetBlackboard output_key="tray" value="backtray" />
                        <SetBlackboard output_key="side" value="LEFT" />
                        <SubTree ID="TrayIsEmpty" tray="tray" side="side" />
                    </Sequence>
                    <Inverter>
                        <Sequence>
                            <SetBlackboard output_key="tray" value="shelf" />
                            <SubTree ID="TrayIsFull" tray="tray" />
                        </Sequence>
                    </Inverter>
                    <ForceSuccess>
                        <Sequence>
                            <!-- <SubTree ID="ClearAllOctomaps" /> -->
                            <SetBlackboard output_key="src_tray" value="backtray" />
                            <SetBlackboard output_key="dst_tray" value="shelf" />
                            <SubTree ID="LeftArmFillYourTray" src_tray="src_tray" dst_tray="dst_tray" />
                        </Sequence>
                    </ForceSuccess>
                </Sequence>
            </Repeat>
        </Sequence>
    </BehaviorTree>

    <BehaviorTree ID="GoToTable">
        <Sequence>
            <MoveBase goal="1.5;-0.1;0.0" />
            <MoveBase goal="2.2;-0.1;0.0" />
        </Sequence>
    </BehaviorTree>

    <BehaviorTree ID="GoToShelves">
        <Sequence>
            <MoveBase goal="1.0;-0.3.0;0.0" />
            <MoveBase goal="2.0;-0.3;0.4" />
        </Sequence>
    </BehaviorTree>


    <BehaviorTree ID="MoveTomatoCans">
        <Repeat num_cycles="4">
            <ForceSuccess>
                <KeepRunningUntilFailure>
                    <Fallback>
                        <Sequence>
                            <SubTree ID="LeftArmFillYourTray" src_tray="src_tray" dst_tray="dst_tray" />
                            <SubTree ID="RightArmFillYourTray" src_tray="src_tray" dst_tray="dst_tray" />
                        </Sequence>
                        <SubTree ID="RightArmFillYourTray" src_tray="src_tray" dst_tray="dst_tray" />
                    </Fallback>
                </KeepRunningUntilFailure>
            </ForceSuccess>
        </Repeat>
    </BehaviorTree>

    <BehaviorTree ID="LeftArmFillYourTray">
        <Sequence>
            <TrayPickOccupiedLocus tray_name="{src_tray}" side="LEFT" locus_id="{src_locus_id}" />
            <Fallback>
                <Sequence>
                    <TrayPickEmptyLocus tray_name="{dst_tray}" side="LEFT" locus_id="{dst_locus_id}" locus_pose="{dst_locus_pose}" />
                    <Fallback>
                        <Sequence>
                            <TrayUpdatePlanningScene />
                            <PickAndPlaceLeft object_id="{src_locus_id}" target_pose="{dst_locus_pose}" flat_hand_mode="true" />
                            <TraySetLocusState tray_name="{src_tray}" locus_id="{src_locus_id}" occupied="false" />
                            <TraySetLocusState tray_name="{dst_tray}" locus_id="{dst_locus_id}" occupied="true" />
                        </Sequence>
                        <ForceFailure>
                            <TrayReleaseLocus tray_name="{dst_tray}" locus_id="{dst_locus_id}" />
                        </ForceFailure>
                    </Fallback>
                    <TrayReleaseLocus tray_name="{dst_tray}" locus_id="{dst_locus_id}" />
                </Sequence>
                <ForceFailure>
                    <TrayReleaseLocus tray_name="{src_tray}" locus_id="{src_locus_id}" />
                </ForceFailure>
            </Fallback>
            <TrayReleaseLocus tray_name="{src_tray}" locus_id="{src_locus_id}" />
        </Sequence>
    </BehaviorTree>

    <BehaviorTree ID="RightArmFillYourTray">
        <Sequence>
            <TrayPickOccupiedLocus tray_name="{src_tray}" side="RIGHT" locus_id="{src_locus_id}" />
            <Fallback>
                <Sequence>
                    <TrayPickEmptyLocus tray_name="{dst_tray}" side="RIGHT" locus_id="{dst_locus_id}" locus_pose="{dst_locus_pose}" />
                    <Fallback>
                        <Sequence>
                            <TrayUpdatePlanningScene />
                            <PickAndPlaceRight object_id="{src_locus_id}" target_pose="{dst_locus_pose}" flat_hand_mode="false" />
                            <TraySetLocusState tray_name="{src_tray}" locus_id="{src_locus_id}" occupied="false" />
                            <TraySetLocusState tray_name="{dst_tray}" locus_id="{dst_locus_id}" occupied="true" />
                        </Sequence>
                        <ForceFailure>
                            <TrayReleaseLocus tray_name="{dst_tray}" locus_id="{dst_locus_id}" />
                        </ForceFailure>
                    </Fallback>
                    <TrayReleaseLocus tray_name="{dst_tray}" locus_id="{dst_locus_id}" />
                </Sequence>
                <ForceFailure>
                    <TrayReleaseLocus tray_name="{src_tray}" locus_id="{src_locus_id}" />
                </ForceFailure>
            </Fallback>
            <TrayReleaseLocus tray_name="{src_tray}" locus_id="{src_locus_id}" />
        </Sequence>
    </BehaviorTree>

    <BehaviorTree ID="TrayIsFull">
        <Inverter>
            <Sequence>
                <Fallback>
                    <TrayPickEmptyLocus tray_name="{tray}" side="LEFT" locus_id="{locus_id}" locus_pose="{locus_pose}" />
                    <TrayPickEmptyLocus tray_name="{tray}" side="RIGHT" locus_id="{locus_id}" locus_pose="{locus_pose}" />
                </Fallback>
                <ForceSuccess>
                    <TrayReleaseLocus tray_name="{tray}" locus_id="{locus_id}" />
                </ForceSuccess>
            </Sequence>
        </Inverter>
    </BehaviorTree>

    <BehaviorTree ID="TrayIsEmpty">
        <Fallback>
            <Sequence>
                <TrayPickOccupiedLocus tray_name="{tray}" side="{side}" locus_id="{locus_id}" />
                <ForceSuccess>
                    <TrayReleaseLocus tray_name="{tray}" locus_id="{locus_id}" />
                </ForceSuccess>
                <AlwaysSuccess />
            </Sequence>
            <AlwaysFailure />
        </Fallback>
    </BehaviorTree>

</root>

<root main_tree_to_execute="BehaviorTree">
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="BehaviorTree">
        <Sequence>
            <Action ID="MoveGoal" TargetPoint="{target_point}"/>
            <ForceSuccess>
                <Action ID="Wait" WaitTime="{wait_time}"/>
            </ForceSuccess>
            <Action ID="MoveGoal" TargetPoint="{target_point}"/>
            <ForceSuccess>
                <Action ID="Wait" WaitTime="{wait_time}"/>
            </ForceSuccess>
        </Sequence>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <TreeNodesModel>
        <Action ID="MoveGoal">
            <inout_port default="{target_point}" name="TargetPoint"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="Wait">
            <input_port default="{wait_time}" name="WaitTime"/>
        </Action>

</root>
mrq: The mission describes a point-to-point motion task that moves to a target point, tolerates waiting phases, and repeats the motion in strict order. First, the robot will execute the following actions (MoveGoal, Wait, MoveGoal, Wait) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action MoveGoal using the provided target point. It will execute the custom action Wait and make sure it always succeeds. It will run the custom action MoveGoal again using the same target point after the forced-success wait completes. It will execute the custom action Wait again and make sure it always succeeds.

<root main_tree_to_execute="MainTree">
    <BehaviorTree ID="BlinkLight">
        <Sequence>
            <SetLightState state="true" />
            <Delay delay_msec="500">
                <SetLightState state="false" />
            </Delay>
        </Sequence>
    </BehaviorTree>

    <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
        <Sequence>
            <ForEachMineralSample samples_list="{mineral_samples}" current_sample="{current_sample}">
                <Sequence>
                    <Log message="Moving to next sample..." />
                    <ResetMineralDepositTracker sample="{current_sample}" service_name="/mineral_deposit_tracker/reset" />
                    <GetSamplePose sample="{current_sample}" pose="{sample_pose}" />
                    <PipelineSequence>
                        <RateController hz="1.0">
                            <Sequence>
                                <GoalUpdater input_goal="{sample_pose}" output_goal="{updated_goal}">
                                    <ComputePathToPose goal="{updated_goal}" path="{path}" planner_id="GridBased" />
                                </GoalUpdater>
                            </Sequence>
                        </RateController>
                        <WhileDoElse>
                            <GoalReached goal="{updated_goal}" />
                            <AlwaysSuccess />
                            <KeepRunningUntilFailure>
                                <FollowPath path="{path}" controller_id="FollowPath" />
                            </KeepRunningUntilFailure>
                        </WhileDoElse>
                    </PipelineSequence>
                    <Log message="At sample!" />
                    <SubTree ID="BlinkLight" __shared_blackboard="true" />
                </Sequence>
            </ForEachMineralSample>
            <Log message="All samples complete" />
            <Log message="Calling /park_at_peak action..." />
            <ParkAtPeak />
            <Log message="Action done!" />
            <SubTree ID="BlinkLight" __shared_blackboard="true" />
        </Sequence>
    </BehaviorTree>
</root>
mrq:
The mission describes a mineral sampling campaign that iterates through a list of mineral samples, navigates to each sample, signals arrival with a light blink, and finally parks at a peak while signaling completion.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (ForEachMineralSample, Log, Log, ParkAtPeak, Log, SubTree) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action ForEachMineralSample to iterate over mineral_samples while setting current_sample for each iteration. It will execute the following actions (Sequence) in order for each current_sample and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action Log to record that it is moving to the next sample. It will run the custom action ResetMineralDepositTracker to reset tracking for the current_sample using the service /mineral_deposit_tracker/reset. It will run the custom action GetSamplePose to retrieve the pose of the current_sample and store it as sample_pose. It will execute the following actions (PipelineSequence) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails and, while an action is running or has succeeded, go to the next action. It will execute the following actions (RateController, WhileDoElse) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails and, while an action is running or has succeeded, go to the next action. It will run the custom action RateController at 1.0 Hz around its child sequence to rate-limit goal updates. It will execute the following actions (GoalUpdater) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action GoalUpdater to adjust input_goal sample_pose into output_goal updated_goal while embedding the following planner step. It will run the ROS action ComputePathToPose with the goal updated_goal and will store the resulting path. It will execute the following actions (WhileDoElse) while checking a condition for each action. If GoalReached is true move to the next action. Else if GoalReached is false, end the sequence. It will while GoalReached for updated_goal is true do the custom action AlwaysSuccess. It will if GoalReached for updated_goal is false execute alternative the following actions (KeepRunningUntilFailure). It will execute the following actions (FollowPath) and if the action succeeds, restart it and else if the action fails, stop looping and continue execution. It will run the ROS action FollowPath using the stored path with the specified controller until the controller reports failure. It will run the custom action Log to record arrival at the sample. It will execute the subtree BlinkLight with a shared blackboard to signal arrival. It will run the custom action Log to record that all samples are complete. It will run the custom action Log to record that it is calling the /park_at_peak action. It will run the custom action ParkAtPeak to park at the peak. It will run the custom action Log to record that the action is done. It will execute the subtree BlinkLight with a shared blackboard to signal mission completion.

Next, the mission describes a light signaling routine that toggles a light on and then off with a delay. It will execute the following actions (SetLightState, Delay) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action SetLightState to set the light state to true. It will execute delayed the following actions (SetLightState). If execution succeeds, it will start a delay timer of 500 milliseconds and, after the delay period is exhausted, it will resume execution. It will run the custom action SetLightState to set the light state to false after the delay completes.

<root main_tree_to_execute="MainTree">
  <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
    <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="6" name="NavigateRecovery">
      <PipelineSequence name="NavigateWithReplanning">
        <RateController hz="1.0">
          <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="1" name="ComputePathToPose">
            <ComputePathToPose goal="{goal}" path="{path}" planner_id="GridBased"/>
            <ClearEntireCostmap service_name="global_costmap/clear_entirely_global_costmap"/>
          </RecoveryNode>
        </RateController>
        <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="1" name="FollowPath">
          <FollowPath path="{path}" controller_id="FollowPath"/>
          <ClearEntireCostmap service_name="local_costmap/clear_entirely_local_costmap"/>
        </RecoveryNode>
      </PipelineSequence>
      <SequenceStar name="RecoveryActions">
        <ClearEntireCostmap service_name="local_costmap/clear_entirely_local_costmap"/>
        <ClearEntireCostmap service_name="global_costmap/clear_entirely_global_costmap"/>
        <Spin spin_dist="0.3"/>
        <Wait wait_duration="2"/>
      </SequenceStar>
    </RecoveryNode>
  </BehaviorTree>
</root>
mrq:
The mission describes a navigation task that continuously replans a global path at 1 Hz, follows the path, and performs structured recoveries when needed. First, the robot will execute the following actions (RateController, RecoveryNode ComputePathToPose, RecoveryNode FollowPath) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. While an action is running or has succeeded, it will go to the next action. It will execute delayed RateController and, after execution succeeds, it will start a 1 Hz delay timer and resume execution to enforce periodic replanning. It will execute main the ROS action ComputePathToPose and make sure it always retries once by executing the recovery action ClearEntireCostmap for the global costmap before retrying if the path computation fails. It will execute main the ROS action FollowPath and make sure it always retries once by executing the recovery action ClearEntireCostmap for the local costmap before retrying if following the path fails.

Next, it will execute main NavigateWithReplanning. If it fails, it will execute recovery action (RecoveryActions). After recovery, it will retry main action (NavigateWithReplanning). If recovery action (RecoveryActions) fails and retries are exhausted, it will stop execution.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (ClearEntireCostmap local, ClearEntireCostmap global, Spin, Wait) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will run the ROS action ClearEntireCostmap using the local_costmap clear service. It will run the ROS action ClearEntireCostmap using the global_costmap clear service. It will run the ROS action Spin with a spin distance of 0.3 meters. It will run the ROS action Wait for 2 seconds.

<root main_tree_to_execute = "DevicePairingWithRequest" >
    <BehaviorTree ID="DevicePairingWithRequest">
        <Sequence>
            <!--pairing_mode und device_type müssen den Konstanten im .action file entsprechen-->
            <IntParameterRequest title="Bitte geben Sie den Kopplungsmodus und das Gerät an"
                                   options="pair,unpair"

            <IntParameterRequest options="wheelchair,rollator,hospitalbed,chargingstation"
                                   value="{device_type}"/>
            <DevicePairing pairing_mode="{pairing_mode}" device_type="{device_type}"/>
        </Sequence>
    </BehaviorTree>
 </root>
mrq: The mission describes an interactive device pairing task that requests pairing parameters and then performs the pairing in strict order. First, the robot will execute the following actions (IntParameterRequest, IntParameterRequest, DevicePairing) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action IntParameterRequest with the title “Bitte geben Sie den Kopplungsmodus und das Gerät an” to request a pairing_mode value from the set {pair, unpair}. It will run the custom action IntParameterRequest to request a device_type value from the set {wheelchair, rollator, hospitalbed, chargingstation} after the pairing_mode request succeeds. It will run the custom action DevicePairing using the stored pairing_mode and device_type after both parameter requests succeed.

<root main_tree_to_execute="BehaviorTree">

    <BehaviorTree ID="BehaviorTree">
        <Sequence>
            <Pause       time_limit_sec="3"/>
            <MoveBase    goal="5;-3;-2"/>
            <Pause       time_limit_sec="3"/>
            <MoveBase    goal="-2;-3;-4"/>
            <Pause       time_limit_sec="3"/>
            <MoveBase    goal="2;3;-6"/>
        </Sequence>
    </BehaviorTree>

</root>
mrq: The mission describes a point-to-point navigation task that alternates timed pauses with waypoint transits in strict order. First, the robot will execute the following actions (Pause, MoveBase, Pause, MoveBase, Pause, MoveBase) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action Pause with a time limit of 3 seconds before any movement starts. It will run the custom action MoveBase to reach the waypoint at coordinates (5, -3, -2) after the initial pause succeeds. It will run the custom action Pause with a time limit of 3 seconds after arriving at the first waypoint. It will run the custom action MoveBase to reach the waypoint at coordinates (-2, -3, -4) after the second pause succeeds. It will run the custom action Pause with a time limit of 3 seconds after arriving at the second waypoint. It will run the custom action MoveBase to reach the waypoint at coordinates (2, 3, -6) after the third pause succeeds.

BT:
<!--
  This Behavior Tree replans the global path periodically at 1 Hz and it also has
  recovery actions.
-->
<root main_tree_to_execute="MainTree">
  <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
    <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="6" name="NavigateRecovery">
      <PipelineSequence name="NavigateWithReplanning">
        <RateController hz="1.0">
          <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="1" name="ComputePathToPose">
            <ComputePathToPose goal="{goal}" path="{path}" planner_id="GridBased"/>
            <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearGlobalCostmap-Context" service_name="global_costmap/clear_entirely_global_costmap"/>
          </RecoveryNode>
        </RateController>
        <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="1" name="FollowPath">
          <FollowPath path="{path}" controller_id="FollowPath"/>
          <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearLocalCostmap-Context" service_name="local_costmap/clear_entirely_local_costmap"/>
        </RecoveryNode>
      </PipelineSequence>
      <ReactiveFallback name="RecoveryFallback">
        <GoalUpdated/>
        <SequenceStar name="RecoveryActions">
          <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearLocalCostmap-Subtree" service_name="local_costmap/clear_entirely_local_costmap"/>
          <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearGlobalCostmap-Subtree" service_name="global_costmap/clear_entirely_global_costmap"/>
          <Spin spin_dist="1.57"/>
          <Wait wait_duration="5"/>
        </SequenceStar>
      </ReactiveFallback>
    </RecoveryNode>
  </BehaviorTree>
</root>
mrq:
The mission describes a navigation task that periodically replans a global path at 1 Hz, follows the path, and invokes targeted recovery behaviors when failures occur.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (RateController, RecoveryNode) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. While an action is running or has succeeded, it will go to the next action. It will execute delayed RateController. If execution succeeds, it will start a 1.0 Hz delay timer. After the delay period is exhausted, it will resume execution to enforce periodic replanning. It will execute main the ROS action ComputePathToPose. If it fails, it will execute recovery action the ROS service ClearEntireCostmap for the global costmap. After recovery, it will retry main action the ROS action ComputePathToPose once. If the recovery action fails and retries are exhausted, it will stop execution of this compute-path step.

Next, it will execute the following actions (RecoveryNode) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. While an action is running or has succeeded, it will go to the next action. It will execute main the ROS action FollowPath. If it fails, it will execute recovery action the ROS service ClearEntireCostmap for the local costmap. After recovery, it will retry main action the ROS action FollowPath once. If the recovery action fails and retries are exhausted, it will stop execution of this follow-path step.

Finally, it will execute main the PipelineSequence subtree. If it fails, it will execute recovery action the ReactiveFallback subtree. After recovery, it will retry main action the PipelineSequence subtree up to six times. If the recovery action fails and retries are exhausted, it will stop execution of the overall navigation.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (GoalUpdated, SequenceStar) in order. If GoalUpdated succeeds, it will stop execution. Else it will keep executing and re-evaluating previous actions whenever an action is running. It will execute the following actions (ClearEntireCostmap local, ClearEntireCostmap global, Spin, Wait) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will run the ROS service ClearEntireCostmap for the local costmap. It will run the ROS service ClearEntireCostmap for the global costmap after the local costmap is cleared. It will run the custom action Spin with a spin distance of 1.57 radians after both costmaps are cleared. It will run the custom action Wait for 5 seconds after the spin completes.

<root main_tree_to_execute = "MainTree">
    <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
        <Sequence>
            <SetBlackboard output_key="Goal_a" value="-0.579;-1.341;0.0;1.0" />
            <SetBlackboard output_key="Goal_b" value="5.214;-1.533;0.0;1.0" />
            <SetBlackboard output_key="Goal_c" value="-1.588;1.253;0.0;1.0" />
            <Fallback>
                <ReactiveSequence>
                    <InterruptEvent event="gohome"/>
                    <Repeat num_cycles="10">
                        <Sequence>
                            <SubTree ID="MoveRobot" target="Goal_a" />
                            <SubTree ID="MoveRobot" target="Goal_b" />
                        </Sequence>
                    </Repeat>
                </ReactiveSequence>
                <Sequence>
                    <SubTree ID="MoveRobot" target="Goal_c" />
                </Sequence>
            </Fallback>
        </Sequence>
    </BehaviorTree>

    <BehaviorTree ID="MoveRobot">
        <Sequence name="SetGoal">
            <Nav2Client goal="{target}" />
        </Sequence>
    </BehaviorTree>
</root>
mrq:
The mission describes a patrol and contingency navigation task that sets three goals and cycles between two of them with an interruptible routine before falling back to a contingency goal.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (SetBlackboard, SetBlackboard, SetBlackboard, Fallback) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule when setting Goal_a, and it will store the value "-0.579;-1.341;0.0;1.0". It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule when setting Goal_b, and it will store the value "5.214;-1.533;0.0;1.0". It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule when setting Goal_c, and it will store the value "-1.588;1.253;0.0;1.0". It will execute the following actions (ReactiveSequence, Sequence) consecutively. If any action (ReactiveSequence) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution will fail.

Next, it will execute the following actions [InterruptEvent, Repeat] in sequence. If InterruptEvent fails, it will stop execution. While Repeat is still running, it will re-evaluate previous actions. It will execute [Sequence] for a set number of times or infinitely. It will run the Sequence ten times because a fixed cycle count is set. It will execute the following actions (SubTree, SubTree) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. While the first SubTree is running or has succeeded, it will go to the next action in the outer reactive control. It will execute the custom action SubTree with ID MoveRobot and will pass the target value stored at Goal_a. It will execute the custom action SubTree with ID MoveRobot and will pass the target value stored at Goal_b.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (SubTree) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute the custom action SubTree with ID MoveRobot and will pass the target value stored at Goal_c.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (Nav2Client) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the ROS action Nav2Client with the goal provided by the input port named target and will command the navigation to that single pose.

mrq: The mission describes a short takeoff-relative repositioning task that waits for a fixed time and then commands a goal-directed move using an action server. First, the robot will execute the following actions (WaitSeconds, UPOGoToGoal) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action WaitSeconds with a wait duration of 2 seconds. It will run the custom action UPOGoToGoal after the wait succeeds and will send a goal to the action server /Make_Plan. It will provide the goal pose in the uav_base_link frame with header sequence 1. It will set the goal position to x = 1.0 meters, y = -0.2 meters, and z = 2.4 meters. It will set the goal orientation to x = 0.0, y = 0.0, z = 0.0, and w = 1.0.

mrq:
The mission describes a workforce robot that selects jobs, performs specialized animal-handling and standard tasks, manages personal needs, and engages in combat when required.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (GetJob, FallbackStar of job-specific subtrees) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will run the custom action GetJob to acquire a new assignment. It will execute the following actions (JobButcherAnimal, JobDyeAnimal, JobLeadAnimalToPasture, JobHarvestAnimal, JobTameAnimal, JobHaulMultiple, JobStandard) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, it will start all over again until an action succeeds.

Next, it will execute the following actions (IsInCombat, AbortJob, ForceSuccess of combat pursuit, IsInCombat) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule. It will execute the custom action AbortJob to cancel the current non-combat task. It will execute GetTarget and Move and a nested sequence that checks adjacency then attacks, and make sure it always succeeds. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule.

Next, it will execute the following actions (CheckUniform, Fallback of Move then UniformCleanUp, EquipUniform) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will execute the custom action CheckUniform to verify the uniform state. It will execute the following actions (Move, UniformCleanUp) consecutively. If Move fails, it will move to UniformCleanUp until one succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will execute the custom action EquipUniform to don the verified or cleaned uniform.

Next, it will execute the following actions (IsHungry, Sequence of eating workflow and IsFull) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule. It will execute the following actions (FindFood, Move, PickUpItem, ForceSuccess of finding dining and moving, Eat) in sequence. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will execute FindDining and Move and make sure it always succeeds. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule.

Next, it will read blackboard value and compare it against a set of cases. If a case matches the blackboard value, it will execute corresponding action. Else if no case matches, it will execute default action. It will execute the following actions (InitAnimalJob, Move, GrabAnimal, GetItemDropPosition, Move, FinalMoveAnimal, ButcherAnimal, FinishJob) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will execute the following actions (SequenceStar for butcher flow, AbortJob) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails.

Next, it will read blackboard value and compare it against a set of cases. If a case matches the blackboard value, it will execute corresponding action. Else if no case matches, it will execute default action. It will execute the following actions (InitJob, ClaimItems, SubTree MoveAllItemsToJob, InitAnimalJob, Move, GrabAnimal, GetItemDropPosition, Move, FinalMoveAnimal, DyeAnimal, FinishJob) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will execute the following actions (SequenceStar for dye flow, AbortJob) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails.

Next, it will read blackboard value and compare it against a set of cases. If a case matches the blackboard value, it will execute corresponding action. Else if no case matches, it will execute default action. It will execute the following actions (InitAnimalJob, Move, GrabAnimal, HarvestAnimal, ReleaseAnimal, FinishJob) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will execute the following actions (SequenceStar for harvest flow, AbortJob) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails.

Next, it will read blackboard value and compare it against a set of cases. If a case matches the blackboard value, it will execute corresponding action. Else if no case matches, it will execute default action. It will execute the following actions (InitJob, FindTool, Move, EquipTool, Inverter over pickup loop, AllPickedUp, GetItemDropPosition, Move, DropAllItems, FinishJob) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will execute inverted a sequence that inverts AllPickedUp, moves and picks up, then returns always running. If the inner action succeeds, it will reverse it to fail. Else if the inner action fails, it will reverse it to succeed. It will execute the following actions (SequenceStar for multi-haul flow, AbortJob) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails.

Next, it will read blackboard value and compare it against a set of cases. If a case matches the blackboard value, it will execute corresponding action. Else if no case matches, it will execute default action. It will execute the following actions (InitAnimalJob, Move, GrabAnimal, GetItemDropPosition, Move, FinalMoveAnimal, ReleaseAnimal, FinishJob) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will execute the following actions (SequenceStar for lead-to-pasture flow, AbortJob) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails.

Next, it will execute the following actions (InitJob, ClaimItems, FindTool, Move, EquipTool, SubTree MoveAllItemsToJob, GetWorkPosition, Move, Work, FinishJob) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will execute the following actions (SequenceStar for standard job flow, AbortJob) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails.

Next, it will read blackboard value and compare it against a set of cases. If a case matches the blackboard value, it will execute corresponding action. Else if no case matches, it will execute default action. It will execute the following actions (InitAnimalJob, Move, TameAnimal, GrabAnimal, GetItemDropPosition, Move, FinalMoveAnimal, ReleaseAnimal, FinishJob) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will execute the following actions (SequenceStar for tame flow, AbortJob) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails.

Next, it will execute the following actions (Inverter over relocation loop, AllItemsInPlaceForJob) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. While an action is running or has succeeded, it will go to the next action. It will execute inverted a sequence that inverts AllItemsInPlaceForJob, then executes a SequenceStar of Move, PickUpItem, GetItemDropPosition, Move, DropItem, then executes ReturnAlwaysRunning. If the inner action succeeds, it will reverse it to fail. Else if the inner action fails, it will reverse it to succeed. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule.

Next, it will execute the following actions (FallbackStar of readiness, RetryUntilSuccessful of item placement, ForceFailure of AbortJob) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, it will start all over again until an action succeeds. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule. It will keep trying to execute a sequence of a SequenceStar of Move, PickUpItem, two Fallbacks for getting drop positions and moving, and repeated DropItem, followed by AllItemsInPlaceForJob, until it succeeds. It will execute AbortJob and make sure it always fails.

Next, it will execute the following actions (IsSleepy, ForceSuccess of finding bed and moving, Sleep) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. <!--
  This Behavior Tree replans the global path periodically at 1 Hz and it also has
  recovery actions.
-->
<root main_tree_to_execute="MainTree">
  <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
    <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="6" name="NavigateRecovery">
      <PipelineSequence name="NavigateWithReplanning">
        <RateController hz="2.0">        
          <ComputePathToPose goal="{goal}" path="{path}" planner_id="GridBased"/>          
        </RateController>
        <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="1" name="FollowPath">
          <FollowPath path="{path}" controller_id="FollowPath"/>
          <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearLocalCostmap-Context" service_name="local_costmap/clear_entirely_local_costmap"/>
        </RecoveryNode>
      </PipelineSequence>
      <ReactiveFallback name="RecoveryFallback">
        <GoalUpdated/>
        <SequenceStar name="RecoveryActions">
          <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearLocalCostmap-Subtree" service_name="local_costmap/clear_entirely_local_costmap"/>
          <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearGlobalCostmap-Subtree" service_name="global_costmap/clear_entirely_global_costmap"/>
          <Spin spin_dist="1.0"/>
          <Wait wait_duration="1"/>
        </SequenceStar>
      </ReactiveFallback>
    </RecoveryNode>
  </BehaviorTree>
</root>
mrq:
The mission describes a navigation task that plans a global path at 2 Hz, follows the path with local recovery, and applies broader recovery behaviors when navigation fails.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (ComputePathToPose, FollowPath) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute the following actions (ComputePathToPose, FollowPath) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute the following actions (ComputePathToPose, FollowPath) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute the following actions (ComputePathToPose, FollowPath) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. While an action is running or has succeeded, it will go to the next action. It will execute the following actions (ComputePathToPose) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. While an action is running or has succeeded, it will go to the next action. It will run the ROS action ComputePathToPose at a fixed rate of 2.0 Hz and will update the path to the current goal each cycle. It will then execute main FollowPath and, if it fails, it will execute recovery action (ClearEntireCostmap-Context). After recovery, it will retry main action (FollowPath). If recovery action (ClearEntireCostmap-Context) fails and retries are exhausted, it will stop execution of this local recovery.

Next, it will execute main NavigateWithReplanning. If it fails, it will execute recovery action (RecoveryFallback). After recovery, it will retry main action (NavigateWithReplanning). If recovery action (RecoveryFallback) fails and retries are exhausted, it will stop execution of the overall navigation.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (GoalUpdated, RecoveryActions) in order. If an action succeeds, it will stop execution. Else it will keep executing and re-evaluating previous actions whenever an action is running. It will immediately succeed and stop recovery if the custom action GoalUpdated reports that the goal changed. It will execute the following actions (ClearLocalCostmap-Subtree, ClearGlobalCostmap-Subtree, Spin, Wait) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will run the custom action ClearEntireCostmap-Subtree to clear the local costmap. It will run the custom action ClearEntireCostmap-Subtree to clear the global costmap. It will run the custom action Spin with a spin distance of 1.0 to free the robot from local minima. It will run the custom action Wait for a duration of 1 second to allow map updates to settle.

<root main_tree_to_execute="MainTree">
  <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
    <PipelineSequence name="NavigateWithReplanning">
      <RateController hz="1.0">
        <Sequence>
          <GoalUpdater input_goal="{goal}" output_goal="{updated_goal}">
            <ComputePathToPose goal="{updated_goal}" path="{path}" planner_id="GridBased"/>
          </GoalUpdater>
         <TruncatePath distance="1.0" input_path="{path}" output_path="{truncated_path}"/>
        </Sequence>
      </RateController>
      <KeepRunningUntilFailure>
        <FollowPath path="{truncated_path}" controller_id="FollowPath"/>
      </KeepRunningUntilFailure>
    </PipelineSequence>
  </BehaviorTree>
</root>
mrq:
The mission describes a navigation task that updates a goal, computes and truncates a path at a fixed rate, and continuously follows the truncated path while allowing advancement during running actions.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (RateController, KeepRunningUntilFailure) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will advance to the next action while an action is running or has succeeded.

Next, it will execute the following actions (Sequence) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run this sequence at a rate of 1.0 hertz due to the rate controller. It will execute delayed actions only after the rate controller allows the tick.

Next, it will execute the custom action GoalUpdater and make sure its nested child always runs inside it. It will run the ROS action ComputePathToPose with the updated goal and will store the resulting path. It will execute the ROS action TruncatePath to shorten the stored path to a distance of 1.0 and will output a truncated path.

Finally, it will execute the ROS action FollowPath. If the action succeeds, it will restart it. Else if the action fails, it will stop looping and continue execution.

    <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="3" name="NavigateRecovery">
      <PipelineSequence name="NavigateWithReplanning">
        <RateController hz="2.0">
          <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="3" name="ComputePathToPose">
            <ComputePathToPose goal="{goal}" path="{path}" planner_id="GridBased"/>
            <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearGlobalCostmap-Context" service_name="global_costmap/clear_entirely_global_costmap"/>
          </RecoveryNode>
        </RateController>
        <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="3" name="FollowPath">
          <FollowPath path="{path}" controller_id="FollowPath"/>
          <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearLocalCostmap-Context" service_name="local_costmap/clear_entirely_local_costmap"/>
        </RecoveryNode>
      </PipelineSequence>
      <ReactiveFallback name="RecoveryFallback">
        <GoalUpdated/>
        <SequenceStar name="RecoveryActions">
          <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearLocalCostmap-Subtree" service_name="local_costmap/clear_entirely_local_costmap"/>
          <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearGlobalCostmap-Subtree" service_name="global_costmap/clear_entirely_global_costmap"/>
          <Spin spin_dist="1.0"/>
          <Wait wait_duration="5"/>
        </SequenceStar>
      </ReactiveFallback>
    </RecoveryNode>
  </BehaviorTree>

mrq:
The mission describes a navigation task that plans a path to a provided goal, follows the path with continuous replanning, and attempts structured recovery behaviors when navigation actions fail.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (NavigateWithReplanning, RecoveryFallback) as a main action with a recovery option. It will execute main NavigateWithReplanning. If it fails, it will execute recovery action RecoveryFallback. After the recovery action completes, it will retry the main action NavigateWithReplanning. If the recovery action fails and retries are exhausted, it will stop execution.

Next, it will execute the following actions (RateController, FollowPath) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. While an action is running or has succeeded, it will go to the next action. It will run the custom action RateController at 2.0 Hz to trigger downstream actions at a steady rate. It will execute the following actions (ComputePathToPose, ClearGlobalCostmap-Context) as a main action with a recovery option. It will run the ROS action ComputePathToPose with the goal provided and will store the resulting path. If ComputePathToPose fails, it will run the custom action ClearGlobalCostmap-Context to clear the global costmap. After clearing, it will retry ComputePathToPose up to three times. It will execute the following actions (FollowPath, ClearLocalCostmap-Context) as a main action with a recovery option. It will run the ROS action FollowPath using the stored path with the specified controller. If FollowPath fails, it will run the custom action ClearLocalCostmap-Context to clear the local costmap. After clearing, it will retry FollowPath up to three times.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (GoalUpdated, RecoveryActions) in order. If any action succeeds, it will stop execution. Else it will keep executing and re-evaluating previous actions whenever an action is running. It will execute the ROS action GoalUpdated and will stop the fallback if the goal was updated. It will execute the following actions (ClearLocalCostmap-Subtree, ClearGlobalCostmap-Subtree, Spin, Wait) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will run the custom action ClearLocalCostmap-Subtree to clear the local costmap. It will run the custom action ClearGlobalCostmap-Subtree to clear the global costmap. It will run the ROS action Spin with a spin distance of 1.0 to rotate in place. It will run the ROS action Wait for 5 seconds to pause before the next attempt.

mrq:
The mission describes a deeply nested decision and action flow that will attempt a single action through multiple control layers. First, the robot will execute the following actions (Fallback) consecutively. If any action (Fallback) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution will fail.

Next, it will execute the following actions (Sequence) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will proceed through its only child and will terminate if that child fails.

Next, it will execute the following actions (Sequence) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will pass control to the next inner sequence only if the previous level succeeds.

Next, it will execute the following actions (Sequence) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will continue inward only when the outer sequence reports success.

Next, it will execute the following actions (Fallback) consecutively. If any action (Sequence) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution will fail.

Next, it will execute the following actions (Sequence) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will advance to its child only upon success checks at this level.

Next, it will execute the following actions (Sequence) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will allow progression deeper into the tree only if this sequence succeeds.

Next, it will execute the following actions (Sequence) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will propagate success inward to enable the next control block.

Next, it will execute the following actions (Sequence) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will continue only if this immediate sequence reports success.

Next, it will execute the following actions (Fallback) consecutively. If any action (Fallback) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution will fail.

Next, it will execute the following actions (Fallback) consecutively. If any action (the custom action go4) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution will fail.

Finally, it will execute the custom action go4 and make it the deciding action at the deepest level. It will succeed overall only if go4 succeeds, because all enclosing sequences require success to continue and all enclosing fallbacks provide no alternative beyond go4.

It will then execute the following actions [Parallel, Move] simultaneously. If either a success threshold is reached or all actions succeed, the execution was successful. Else if any action fails, the execution fails.

Next, it will execute the following actions [isObjectPerceived, Scan] simultaneously. If either a success threshold is reached or all actions succeed, the execution was successful. Else if any action fails, the execution fails. It will run the custom action isObjectPerceived with the target set to any to detect the presence of an object. It will run the custom action Scan with the mode set to floor_near to examine the nearby floor area.

Finally, it will run the custom action Move with the goal set from the variable pose while the higher-level parallel execution runs.

            <output_port name="output" type="behavior_path_planner::BehaviorModuleOutput">desc</output_port>
        </Action>
        <Condition ID="SideShift_Ready"/>
        <Condition ID="SideShift_Request"/>
        <SubTree ID="SubTree"/>
        <SubTree ID="a"/>
        <SubTree ID="b"/>
    </TreeNodesModel>
    <!-- ////////// -->
</root>
mrq:
The mission describes a driving behavior selector that prioritizes pull-over, pull-out, lane change, and lane following planning based on requests, readiness, and approvals.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (PullOver_Request, PullOver_Ready, PullOver_Plan) in sequence and will stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute the following actions (PullOut_Request, PullOut_Ready, PullOut_Plan) in sequence and will stop the sequence if any action fails if the first sequence fails. It will execute the following actions (LaneChange_Request, LaneChange_Ready or ForceLaneChange_ForceApproval, LaneChange_Plan) in sequence and will stop the sequence if any action fails if the first two sequences fail. It will execute the ROS action LaneFollowing_Plan if all prior sequences fail. It will execute the following actions (PullOver_Request, PullOver_Ready, PullOver_Plan) consecutively and will move to the next action until an action succeeds and will fail the execution if no action succeeds.

Next, it will execute the following actions (LaneChange_CheckApproval, LaneChange_PlanCandidate) in order. If LaneChange_CheckApproval succeeds, it will stop execution. Else it will keep executing and re-evaluating previous actions whenever an action is running. It will execute the ROS action LaneChange_PlanCandidate and will restart it after each success until it fails.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (Avoidance_CheckApproval, Avoidance_PlanCandidate) in order. If Avoidance_CheckApproval succeeds, it will stop execution. Else it will keep executing and re-evaluating previous actions whenever an action is running. It will execute the ROS action Avoidance_PlanCandidate and will restart it after each success until it fails.

<root main_tree_to_execute="MainTree">
  <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
    <PipelineSequence name="NavigateWithReplanning">
      <RateController hz="1.0">
        <Sequence>
          <GoalUpdater input_goal="{goal}" output_goal="{updated_goal}">
            <ComputePathToPose goal="{updated_goal}" path="{path}" planner_id="GridBased"/>
          </GoalUpdater>
         <TruncatePath distance="1.0" input_path="{path}" output_path="{truncated_path}"/>
        </Sequence>
      </RateController>
      <KeepRunningUntilFailure>
        <FollowPath path="{truncated_path}" controller_id="FollowPath"/>
      </KeepRunningUntilFailure>
    </PipelineSequence>
  </BehaviorTree>
</root>
mrq:
The mission describes a dynamic navigation task that continuously updates a goal pose, replans a path at 1 Hz, truncates the path to maintain a 1.0 meter standoff, and follows the truncated path while allowing progress during planning.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (RateController, KeepRunningUntilFailure) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. While an action is running or has succeeded, it will go to the next action. It will run the custom action RateController at 1.0 Hz to pace upstream planning. It will execute the custom action KeepRunningUntilFailure to repeatedly drive the path follower as long as following does not fail.

Next, it will execute the following actions (Sequence) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action GoalUpdater to refresh the input goal and to output an updated goal for planning. It will run the ROS action ComputePathToPose with the updated goal and will store the resulting path. It will run the ROS action TruncatePath to shorten the stored path to end 1.0 meter from the goal and will output the truncated path.

Finally, it will execute the custom action FollowPath. If FollowPath succeeds, it will restart it. Else if FollowPath fails, it will stop looping and continue execution.

mrq:
The mission describes a single run that starts with an action, then attempts an alternative set of actions where one branch contains nested fallbacks that may choose among multiple actions.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (go1, Fallback) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute the custom action go1 before attempting the fallback branch.

Next, it will execute the following actions (Sequence, Sequence) consecutively. If any action (Sequence) fails, move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will first run a sequence containing the custom action go2, and it will stop this sequence if any action fails. It will then run a second sequence that contains a nested sequence which leads to a fallback that selects between two sequences, and it will stop this second sequence if any action fails.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (Fallback, Sequence) in order. If an action succeeds, stop execution. Else keep executing and re-evaluating previous actions whenever an action is running. It will evaluate a nested fallback chain that contains the custom action go4, and it will succeed if go4 succeeds or if a success threshold is met within the nested fallback. It will evaluate an alternative sequence that contains the custom action go3 if the nested fallback does not succeed.

mrq:
The mission describes a coffee-making task that powers on the machine, completes auto-cleaning, places a cup, selects a coffee type, and finalizes serving in strict order.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (OpenCoffeMachine, AutoClean, PutCoffeCup, CoffeType) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action OpenCoffeMachine. It will run the custom action AutoClean after the machine is confirmed open or switched on. It will run the custom action PutCoffeCup after auto-cleaning completes. It will run the custom action CoffeType after the cup is placed.

Next, it will execute the following actions (IsMachineOpen, IsCleanCupReady, IsMachineOpen, SwitchOnCoffeMachine) consecutively. If any action (IsMachineOpen) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for IsMachineOpen. It will keep trying to execute IsCleanCupReady until it succeeds. It will execute delayed IsMachineOpen. If execution succeeds, it will start a delay timer of 5000 milliseconds and, after the delay period is exhausted, it will resume execution. It will run the custom action SwitchOnCoffeMachine if the machine is not open after the delay.

Next, it will execute the following actions (IsCleanProcessFinished, IsThereEnoughWater, IsWaterTankRemoved, IsWaterTankFull, FillWaterTank, IsWaterTankPlacedInCoffeMachine, Inverter(IsMarroTankFull), IsMarroTankRemoved, IsMarroTankEmpty, EmptyMarroTank, IsMarroTankPlacedInCoffeMachine) consecutively. If any action (Action) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for IsCleanProcessFinished. It will execute the following actions (IsThereEnoughWater, Sequence) consecutively. If any action (IsThereEnoughWater) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for IsThereEnoughWater. It will execute the following actions (IsWaterTankRemoved, IsWaterTankFull, FillWaterTank, IsWaterTankPlacedInCoffeMachine) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute delayed IsWaterTankRemoved. If execution succeeds, it will start a delay timer of 5000 milliseconds and, after the delay period is exhausted, it will resume execution. It will execute the following actions (IsWaterTankFull, FillWaterTank) consecutively. If any action (IsWaterTankFull) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will execute delayed IsWaterTankFull. If execution succeeds, it will start a delay timer of 5000 milliseconds and, after the delay period is exhausted, it will resume execution. It will run the custom action FillWaterTank if the tank is not full after the delay. It will keep trying to execute IsWaterTankPlacedInCoffeMachine until it succeeds. It will execute the following actions (Inverter(IsMarroTankFull), Sequence) consecutively. If any action (Inverter(IsMarroTankFull)) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will execute inverted IsMarroTankFull. If IsMarroTankFull succeeds, it will reverse it to fail. Else if IsMarroTankFull fails, it will reverse it to succeed. It will execute the following actions (IsMarroTankRemoved, IsMarroTankEmpty, EmptyMarroTank, IsMarroTankPlacedInCoffeMachine) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute delayed IsMarroTankRemoved. If execution succeeds, it will start a delay timer of 5000 milliseconds and, after the delay period is exhausted, it will resume execution. It will execute the following actions (IsMarroTankEmpty, EmptyMarroTank) consecutively. If any action (IsMarroTankEmpty) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will execute delayed IsMarroTankEmpty. If execution succeeds, it will start a delay timer of 5000 milliseconds and, after the delay period is exhausted, it will resume execution. It will run the custom action EmptyMarroTank if the tank is not empty after the delay. It will keep trying to execute IsMarroTankPlacedInCoffeMachine until it succeeds.

Next, it will execute the following actions (IsCoffeCupReady, PlaceCoffeCup) consecutively. If any action (IsCoffeCupReady) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will execute delayed IsCoffeCupReady. If execution succeeds, it will start a delay timer of 5000 milliseconds and, after the delay period is exhausted, it will resume execution. It will run the custom action PlaceCoffeCup if the cup is not ready after the delay.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (IsDesiredCoffeSelected, PressDesiredCoffe) consecutively. If any action (IsDesiredCoffeSelected) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will execute delayed IsDesiredCoffeSelected. If execution succeeds, it will start a delay timer of 5000 milliseconds and, after the delay period is exhausted, it will resume execution. It will run the custom action PressDesiredCoffe if the desired coffee is not selected after the delay. It will execute delayed IsCoffeFinished. If execution succeeds, it will start a delay timer of 5000 milliseconds and, after the delay period is exhausted, it will resume execution. It will execute delayed HasCupOffCoffeBeenRemoved. If execution succeeds, it will start a delay timer of 5000 milliseconds and, after the delay period is exhausted, it will resume execution. It will execute delayed the following actions (HasHumanAddedCleaningCup, Sequence) while checking a condition for each action. If condition ID is true move to the next action. Else if condition ID is false, it will end the sequence. It will execute delayed HasHumanAddedCleaningCup. If execution succeeds, it will start a delay timer of 5000 milliseconds and, after the delay period is exhausted, it will resume execution. It will execute the following actions (HasHumanAddedCleaningCup, PlaceCleanCup, SwitchOffCoffeMachine) consecutively. If any action (HasHumanAddedCleaningCup) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will run the custom action PlaceCleanCup after the cleaning cup condition succeeds. It will run the custom action SwitchOffCoffeMachine after the clean cup is placed. It will execute delayed the following actions (HasHumanAddedMilk, Sequence) while checking a condition for each action. If condition ID is true move to the next action. Else if condition ID is false, it will end the sequence. It will execute delayed HasHumanAddedMilk. If execution succeeds, it will start a delay timer of 5000 milliseconds and, after the delay period is exhausted, it will resume execution. It will execute the following actions (Fallback, Fallback) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute the following actions (Inverter(IsMilkDesired), AddMilktoCoffe) consecutively. If any action (Inverter(IsMilkDesired)) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will execute inverted IsMilkDesired. If IsMilkDesired succeeds, it will reverse it to fail. Else if IsMilkDesired fails, it will reverse it to succeed. It will run the custom action AddMilktoCoffe if milk is not desired. It will execute the following actions (Inverter(IsSugarDesired), AddSugarToCoffe) consecutively. If any action (Inverter(IsSugarDesired)) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will execute inverted IsSugarDesired. If IsSugarDesired succeeds, it will reverse it to fail. Else if IsSugarDesired fails, it will reverse it to succeed. It will run the custom action AddSugarToCoffe if sugar is not desired.

mrq:
The mission describes pasture management behavior that governs leaving or entering a shed and moving within a pasture based on time of day and location.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (IsOnPasture, IsDay, IsInShed, LeaveShed, FindRandomPastureField, Move) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for IsOnPasture. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for IsDay. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for IsInShed. It will run the custom action LeaveShed to exit the shed when all preceding conditions have succeeded. It will run the custom action FindRandomPastureField to select a target field within the pasture. It will run the custom action Move to travel to the selected field.

Next, it will execute the following actions (IsOnPasture, IsNight, Inverter(IsInShed), FindShed, Move, EnterShed) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for IsOnPasture. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for IsNight. It will execute inverted IsInShed and, if IsInShed succeeds, it will reverse it to fail, else if IsInShed fails, it will reverse it to succeed. It will run the custom action FindShed to locate the shed. It will run the custom action Move to travel to the shed. It will run the custom action EnterShed to enter the shed after arrival.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (IsDay, Inverter(IsInShed), RandomMove) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for IsDay. It will execute inverted IsInShed and, if IsInShed succeeds, it will reverse it to fail, else if IsInShed fails, it will reverse it to succeed. It will run the custom action RandomMove to wander within the area when it is outside the shed.
