<root main_tree_to_execute="BehaviorTree">
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="BehaviorTree">
        <Sequence>
            <Condition ID="RobotPret" />
            <RealParallel> 
                <SubTree ID="TiretteAndStop" y_mul="y_mul" theta_mul="theta_mul" camp="camp" tirette_presente_last_tick="tirette_presente_last_tick" palets_depart="palets_depart" palet_pente="palet_pente" palet_chaos="palet_chaos" />
                <SubTree ID="MinuterieAndstartAndStopWhenTimeout" palets_depart="palets_depart" palet_pente="palet_pente" palet_chaos="palet_chaos" tirette_presente_last_tick="tirette_presente_last_tick" />
                <SubTree ID="Trier les palets" camp="camp" palets_depart="palets_depart" palet_pente="palet_pente" y_mul="y_mul" theta_mul="theta_mul" palet_chaos="palet_chaos" />
                <SubTree ID="Palets zone chaos" camp="camp" palet_chaos="palet_chaos" y_mul="y_mul" theta_mul="theta_mul" />
                <SubTree ID="Yeux" camp="camp"/>
            </RealParallel>
        </Sequence>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="TiretteAndStop">
        <ForceSuccess>
            <Fallback>
                <Sequence>
                    <Condition ID="TiretteLaserPresente"/>
                    <SetBlackboard value="true" output_key="tirette_presente_last_tick"/>
                    <Action ID="Arreter le robot" />
                    <Action ID="VitesseConstante" linear_x="0.0" linear_y="0.0" angular_z="0.0" />
                    <Action ID="DefinirLeScore" score="40"/>
                    <Action ID="ArreterLaMinuterie"/> 
                    <Action ID="RestartRosbags" />
                    <SetBlackboard value="0" output_key="palets_depart"/>
                    <SetBlackboard value="0" output_key="palet_pente"/>
                    <Fallback>
                        <Sequence>
                            <Condition ID="LaVoieEstLibre-Gauche"/>
                            <Action ID="DefinirPosition" theta="0" x="-0.18" y="-1.18"/>
                            <SetBlackboard value="jaune" output_key="camp"/>
                            <SetBlackboard value="1.0" output_key="y_mul"/>
                            <SetBlackboard value="1.0" output_key="sym_theta"/>
                            <Action ms="500" ID="Attendre"/>
                        </Sequence>
                        <Sequence>
                            <Action ID="DefinirPosition" theta="0" x="-0.18" y="1.18"/>
                            <SetBlackboard value="violet" output_key="camp"/>
                            <SetBlackboard value="-1.0" output_key="y_mul"/>
                            <SetBlackboard value="-1.0" output_key="theta_mul"/>
                            <Action ms="500" ID="Attendre"/>
                        </Sequence>
                    </Fallback>
                </Sequence>
                <Sequence>
                    <SetBlackboard value="false" output_key="tirette_presente_last_tick"/>
                </Sequence>
            </Fallback>
        </ForceSuccess>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="MinuterieAndstartAndStopWhenTimeout">
        <ForceSuccess>
            <Fallback>
                <BlackboardCheckString return_on_mismatch="FAILURE" value_A="{tirette_presente_last_tick}" value_B="true">
                    <Sequence>
                        <Inverter>
                            <Condition ID="TiretteLaserPresente"/>
                        </Inverter>
                        <Action seconds="88" ID="LancerLaMinuterie"/>
                        <SetBlackboard value="1" output_key="palets_depart"/>
                    </Sequence>
                </BlackboardCheckString>
                <Sequence>
                    <Inverter>
                        <Condition ID="MinuterieNonEcoulee"/>
                    </Inverter>
                    <SetBlackboard value="0" output_key="palets_depart"/>
                    <SetBlackboard value="0" output_key="palet_pente"/>
                    <SetBlackboard value="0" output_key="palet_chaos"/>
                    <Action ID="Arreter le robot"/>
                    <!--Action ID="VitesseConstante" linear_x="0.0" linear_y="0.0" angular_z="0.0" /-->
                </Sequence>
            </Fallback>
        </ForceSuccess>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="Trier les palets">
        <ForceSuccess>
            <Fallback>
                <BlackboardCheckString return_on_mismatch="FAILURE" value_A="{palets_depart}" value_B="1">
                    <Fallback>
                        <BlackboardCheckString return_on_mismatch="FAILURE" value_A="{camp}" value_B="jaune">
                            <Sequence>
                                <ReactiveSequence>
                                    <Condition ID="LaVoieEstLibre-Devant"/>
                                    <JouerUnRosbag nom="r2_jaune_13pts.bag" topics="/r2/mobile_base_controller/cmd_vel" />
                                </ReactiveSequence>
                                <Action ID="AjouterAuScore" score="13"/>
                                <SetBlackboard value="0" output_key="palets_depart"/>
                                <SetBlackboard value="0" output_key="palet_chaos"/>
                            </Sequence>
                        </BlackboardCheckString>
                        <BlackboardCheckString return_on_mismatch="FAILURE" value_A="{camp}" value_B="violet">
                            <Sequence>
                            <ReactiveSequence>
                                <Condition ID="LaVoieEstLibre-Devant"/>
                                <JouerUnRosbag nom="r2_violet_13pts.bag" topics="/r2/mobile_base_controller/cmd_vel" />
                            </ReactiveSequence>
                            <Action ID="AjouterAuScore" score="13"/>
                            <SetBlackboard value="0" output_key="palets_depart"/>
                            <SetBlackboard value="0" output_key="palet_chaos"/>
                            </Sequence>
                        </BlackboardCheckString>
                        <Action ID="VitesseConstante" linear_x="0.0" linear_y="0.0" angular_z="0.0" />
                    </Fallback>
                </BlackboardCheckString>
                <BlackboardCheckString return_on_mismatch="FAILURE" value_A="{palet_pente}" value_B="1">
                    <Sequence>
                        <Action ID="Aller" theta="90" x="0.8" y="1.2" y_mul="{y_mul}" theta_mul="{theta_mul}" comment="aller au pied de la balance"/>
                        <Action ID="AjouterAuScore" score="12"/>
                        <SetBlackboard value="0" output_key="palet_pente"/>
                    </Sequence>
                </BlackboardCheckString>
            </Fallback>
        </ForceSuccess>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="Yeux">
        <RealParallel>
            <Sequence>
                <Action message="content.gif" repetition="1" defaut="1" ID="AfficheYeux" fps="2"/>
                <Action ms="10000" ID="Attendre"/>
                <Action message="carre.gif" repetition="1" defaut="1" ID="AfficheYeux" fps="10"/>
                <Action ms="10000" ID="Attendre"/>
                <Action message="folie.gif" repetition="1" defaut="1" ID="AfficheYeux" fps="2"/>
                <Action ms="10000" ID="Attendre"/>
                <Action message="perdu.gif" repetition="1" defaut="1" ID="AfficheYeux" fps="4"/>
                <Action ms="10000" ID="Attendre"/>
                <Action message="stardust.gif" repetition="1" defaut="1" ID="AfficheYeux" fps="1"/>
                <Action ms="10000" ID="Attendre"/>
            </Sequence>
            <ForceSuccess>
                <ReactiveFallback>
                    <Sequence>
                        <Inverter>
                            <Condition ID="RobotEnBonneSante"/>
                        </Inverter>  
                        <Inverter>
                            <Condition ID="MinuterieNonEcoulee"/>
                        </Inverter>  
                        <Action message="CPU !!!" repetition="1" defaut="0" ID="AfficheYeux" fps="40"/>
                    </Sequence>
                    <Sequence>
                        <Condition ID="TiretteLaserPresente"/>
                        <Action message="{camp}" repetition="1" defaut="0" ID="AfficheYeux" fps="30"/>
                    </Sequence>
                    <Sequence>
                        <Condition ID="MinuterieDemarree"/>
                        <Condition ID="MinuterieNonEcoulee"/>
                        <Action ms="10000" ID="Attendre"/>
                        <Action ID="RecupererLeScore" score="{score}"/>
                        <Action message="{score}" repetition="1" defaut="0" ID="AfficheYeux" fps="30"/>
                    </Sequence>
                    <Sequence>
                        <Inverter>
                            <Condition ID="MinuterieNonEcoulee"/>
                        </Inverter>    
                        <Action ms="5000" ID="Attendre"/>
                        <Action ID="RecupererLeScore" score="{score}"/>
                        <Action message="{score}" repetition="1" defaut="0" ID="AfficheYeux" fps="15"/>
                    </Sequence>
                </ReactiveFallback>
            </ForceSuccess>
        </RealParallel>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="Palets zone chaos">
        <ForceSuccess>
            <BlackboardCheckString return_on_mismatch="FAILURE" value_A="{palet_chaos}" value_B="1">
                <Sequence>

                    </RetryUntilSuccesful>

                    <ReactiveSequence>

                        <VitesseConstante linear_x="-0.1" linear_y="0.0" angular_z="0.0" />

    </BehaviorTree>

First, the robot will execute the following actions (RobotPret, RealParallel) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for RobotPret. It will execute the following actions (TiretteAndStop, MinuterieAndstartAndStopWhenTimeout, Trier les palets, Palets zone chaos, Yeux) simultaneously. If either a success threshold is reached or all actions succeed, the execution was successful. Else if any action fails, the execution fails.

Next, the TiretteAndStop subtree will execute an action and make sure it always succeeds. It will execute the following actions (Fallback) consecutively. If any action (Fallback child) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will execute the following actions (Sequence) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for TiretteLaserPresente. It will execute the custom action SetBlackboard to set tirette_presente_last_tick to true. It will execute the custom action Arreter le robot. It will execute the custom action VitesseConstante with zero velocities. It will execute the custom action DefinirLeScore to set the score to 40. It will execute the custom action ArreterLaMinuterie. It will execute the custom action RestartRosbags. It will execute the custom action SetBlackboard to set palets_depart to 0. It will execute the custom action SetBlackboard to set palet_pente to 0. It will execute the following actions (Fallback) consecutively. If any action (Fallback child) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will execute the following actions (Sequence) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for LaVoieEstLibre-Gauche. It will execute the custom action DefinirPosition with theta 0 and coordinates x -0.18 and y -1.18. It will execute the custom action SetBlackboard to set camp to jaune. It will execute the custom action SetBlackboard to set y_mul to 1.0. It will execute the custom action SetBlackboard to set sym_theta to 1.0. It will execute the custom action Attendre with 500 milliseconds. It will execute the following actions (Sequence) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute the custom action DefinirPosition with theta 0 and coordinates x -0.18 and y 1.18. It will execute the custom action SetBlackboard to set camp to violet. It will execute the custom action SetBlackboard to set y_mul to -1.0. It will execute the custom action SetBlackboard to set theta_mul to -1.0. It will execute the custom action Attendre with 500 milliseconds. It will execute the following actions (Sequence) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute the custom action SetBlackboard to set tirette_presente_last_tick to false.

Next, the MinuterieAndstartAndStopWhenTimeout subtree will execute an action and make sure it always succeeds. It will execute the following actions (Fallback) consecutively. If any action (Fallback child) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for a string comparison between tirette_presente_last_tick and true. It will execute the following actions (Sequence) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute inverted TiretteLaserPresente. If TiretteLaserPresente succeeds, it will reverse it to fail. Else if TiretteLaserPresente fails, it will reverse it to succeed. It will execute the custom action LancerLaMinuterie with 88 seconds. It will execute the custom action SetBlackboard to set palets_depart to 1. It will execute the following actions (Sequence) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute inverted MinuterieNonEcoulee. If MinuterieNonEcoulee succeeds, it will reverse it to fail. Else if MinuterieNonEcoulee fails, it will reverse it to succeed. It will execute the custom action SetBlackboard to set palets_depart to 0. It will execute the custom action SetBlackboard to set palet_pente to 0. It will execute the custom action SetBlackboard to set palet_chaos to 0. It will execute the custom action Arreter le robot.

Next, the Trier les palets subtree will execute an action and make sure it always succeeds. It will execute the following actions (Fallback) consecutively. If any action (Fallback child) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for a string comparison between palets_depart and 1. It will execute the following actions (Fallback) consecutively. If any action (Fallback child) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for a string comparison between camp and jaune. It will execute the following actions (Sequence) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute the following actions [Condition, JouerUnRosbag] in sequence. If Condition fails, it will stop execution. While JouerUnRosbag is still running, it will re-evaluate previous actions. It will execute the custom action JouerUnRosbag with nom r2_jaune_13pts.bag and topics /r2/mobile_base_controller/cmd_vel. It will execute the custom action AjouterAuScore with score 13. It will execute the custom action SetBlackboard to set palets_depart to 0. It will execute the custom action SetBlackboard to set palet_chaos to 0. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for a string comparison between camp and violet. It will execute the following actions (Sequence) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute the following actions [Condition, JouerUnRosbag] in sequence. If Condition fails, it will stop execution. While JouerUnRosbag is still running, it will re-evaluate previous actions. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for LaVoieEstLibre-Devant. It will execute the custom action JouerUnRosbag with nom r2_violet_13pts.bag and topics /r2/mobile_base_controller/cmd_vel. It will execute the custom action AjouterAuScore with score 13. It will execute the custom action SetBlackboard to set palets_depart to 0. It will execute the custom action SetBlackboard to set palet_chaos to 0. It will execute the custom action VitesseConstante with zero velocities. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for a string comparison between palet_pente and 1. It will execute the custom action Aller with parameters theta 90, x 0.8, y 1.2, y_mul from blackboard, and theta_mul from blackboard. It will execute the custom action AjouterAuScore with score 12. It will execute the custom action SetBlackboard to set palet_pente to 0.

Next, the Yeux subtree will execute the following actions [Sequence, ForceSuccess] simultaneously. If either a success threshold is reached or all actions succeed, the execution was successful. Else if any action fails, the execution fails. It will execute the following actions (Sequence) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute the custom action AfficheYeux to display content.gif at 2 fps with default on for one repetition. It will execute the custom action Attendre with 10000 milliseconds. It will execute the custom action AfficheYeux to display folie.gif at 2 fps with default on for one repetition. It will execute the custom action Attendre with 10000 milliseconds. It will execute the custom action AfficheYeux to display perdu.gif at 4 fps with default on for one repetition. It will execute the custom action Attendre with 10000 milliseconds. It will execute the custom action AfficheYeux to display stardust.gif at 1 fps with default on for one repetition. It will execute the custom action Attendre with 10000 milliseconds. Else it will keep executing and re-evaluating previous actions whenever an action is running. It will execute the following actions (Sequence) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute inverted RobotEnBonneSante. If RobotEnBonneSante succeeds, it will reverse it to fail. Else if RobotEnBonneSante fails, it will reverse it to succeed. It will execute inverted MinuterieNonEcoulee. If MinuterieNonEcoulee succeeds, it will reverse it to fail. Else if MinuterieNonEcoulee fails, it will reverse it to succeed. It will execute the custom action AfficheYeux to display CPU !!! at 40 fps with default off for one repetition. It will execute the following actions (Sequence) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for MinuterieNonEcoulee. It will execute the custom action Attendre with 10000 milliseconds. It will execute the custom action RecupererLeScore to output score into score. It will execute the custom action AfficheYeux to display the value of score at 30 fps with default off for one repetition. If MinuterieNonEcoulee succeeds, it will reverse it to fail. Else if MinuterieNonEcoulee fails, it will reverse it to succeed. It will execute the custom action Attendre with 5000 milliseconds. It will execute the custom action RecupererLeScore to output score into score. It will execute the custom action AfficheYeux to display the value of score at 15 fps with default off for one repetition.

It will execute the custom action SetBlackboard to set palet_chaos to 0.

<root main_tree_to_execute="MainTree">
  <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
    <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="6">
      <Sequence name="NavigateWithReplanning">
        <RateController hz="1.0">
          <Fallback>
            <GoalReached/>
            <ComputePathToPose goal="${goal}" path="${path}"/>
          </Fallback>
        </RateController>
        <FollowPath path="${path}"/>
      </Sequence>
      <SequenceStar name="RecoveryActions">
        <Spin/>
      </SequenceStar>
    </RecoveryNode>
  </BehaviorTree>
</root>
mrq:
The mission describes a guarded navigation task that periodically replans a global path at 1 Hz and attempts structured recovery if navigation fails. First, the robot will execute the following actions (NavigateWithReplanning, RecoveryActions) using a recovery scheme. It will execute main NavigateWithReplanning. If it fails, it will execute recovery action (RecoveryActions). After recovery, it will retry main action (NavigateWithReplanning). If recovery action (RecoveryActions) fails and retries are exhausted, it will stop execution.

Next, it will execute the following actions (RateController, FollowPath) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. While an action is running or has succeeded, it will go to the next action. It will execute delayed RateController. If execution succeeds, it will start a 1.0 Hz delay timer. After the delay period is exhausted, it will resume execution. It will execute the following actions (GoalReached, ComputePathToPose) consecutively. If any action (GoalReached, ComputePathToPose) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution will fail. It will run the ROS action ComputePathToPose with the provided goal and will store the resulting path when the goal is not yet reached. It will run the ROS action FollowPath using the stored path after the fallback produces a valid path.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (Spin) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will run the ROS action Spin to perform a spinning recovery until the recovery sequence reports success.

mrq: The mission describes a persistent control loop that repeatedly sets a blackboard value while continuing until a failure occurs. First, the robot will execute the following actions (SetBlackboard) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute the custom action SetBlackboard and will write the value 1 to the blackboard entry named test. Finally, it will execute SetBlackboard. If SetBlackboard succeeds, it will restart it. Else if SetBlackboard fails, it will stop looping and continue execution.

mrq:
The mission describes a social navigation task that tracks a target, expresses engagement, sets a waypoint, and navigates toward it in a controlled order.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (TrackAction, Repeat) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action TrackAction configured to track in mode Track at a rate of 3 without detecting voice and without turning the base. It will then proceed to the Repeat block only after tracking starts successfully.

Next, it will execute the custom action Repeat for a set number of times or infinitely. It will execute its child sequence for exactly three cycles.

Next, it will execute the following actions (SmileAction, SetBlackboard, Sequence) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action SmileAction at level 3 for 2000 milliseconds. It will run the custom action SetBlackboard to store the waypoint string -5.40,-5.37,90.0 under the key Goal_a. It will then enter the inner sequence that contains a forced-success navigation step.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (ForceSuccess) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute the custom action ForceSuccess and make sure it always succeeds. It will within that forced-success step run the ROS action Nav2Client with the goal read from the blackboard key Goal_a and with the behavior tree file /home/ubuntu/robot_ws/follow_point_elsabot.xml.

</root>
mrq:
The mission describes a repeated human-pose prompting task that detects a person’s left or right pose and then announces a corresponding phrase.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (Repeat) for a set number of times or infinitely. It will execute the child subtree continuously until it fails or succeeds. Else it will keep executing the child subtree forever.

Next, it will execute the following actions (Sequence) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute the following actions (Sequence) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute the following actions (Fallback, Fallback) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution will fail.

Next, it will execute the following actions (Fallback) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution will fail. It will execute the following actions (Sequence, TextToSpeechActionClient) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution will fail. It will execute the following actions (Sequence) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action HumanPoseDetect and will output a phrase for the left pose into pose_left_speech and a phrase for the right pose into pose_right_speech. It will run the ROS action TextToSpeechActionClient to say the words “Left, ” followed by the value stored in pose_left_speech if the pose detection sequence fails.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (Fallback) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution will fail. It will execute the following actions (Sequence, TextToSpeechActionClient) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution will fail. It will execute the following actions (Sequence) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action HumanPoseDetect and will output a phrase for the left pose into pose_left_speech and a phrase for the right pose into pose_right_speech. It will run the ROS action TextToSpeechActionClient to say the words “Right, ” followed by the value stored in pose_right_speech if the pose detection sequence fails.

<root main_tree_to_execute="BehaviorTree">
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="BehaviorTree">
        <Fallback>
            <Sequence>
                <Action pose_array="face_array" topic="faces" ID="GetPoseArray" name="GetFaceList"/>
                <Action pose_array="{face_array}" ID="SelectPose" pose_stamped="face" position_in_list="0"/>
                <Parallel threshold="2">
                    <Action ID="GazeAtTarget" fixation_pose="{face}" frame_id="base_footprint" action_name="gaze"/>
                    <Fallback>
                        <Sequence>
                            <Action output_val="x_thresh" ID="SelectFieldFromPoseStamped" field="x" pose_stamped="{face}"/>
                            <Condition ID="CompareDouble" A="{x_thresh}" Condition="<" B="1.0"/>
                            <ReactiveSequence>
                                <Action output_val="y_thresh" ID="SelectFieldFromPoseStamped" field="y" pose_stamped="{face}"/>
                                <Action topic="/vizzyArmRoutines/command" ID="ArmRoutines" gesture="STRETCH"/>
                            </ReactiveSequence>
                        </Sequence>
                        <SubTree ID="LowArms"/>
                    </Fallback>
                </Parallel>
            </Sequence>
            <SubTree ID="LowArms"/>
        </Fallback>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="ErrorGameState">
        <SequenceStar>
            <Action ID="Speak" language="pt_PT" action_name="nuance_speech_tts" voice="Joaquim" utterance="Não sei o estado do jogo"/>
            <Action ID="Speak" language="pt_PT" action_name="nuance_speech_tts" voice="Joaquim" utterance="Chamem o assistente"/>
            <Action ID="WaitForXSeconds" result="SUCCESS" seconds="60"/>
        </SequenceStar>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="LowArms">
        <Action topic="/vizzyArmRoutines/command" ID="ArmRoutines" gesture="HOME"/>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <TreeNodesModel>
        <Action ID="ArmCartesian">
            <input_port name="action_name"/>
            <input_port name="frame_id"/>
            <input_port name="pose"/>
            <input_port name="type"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="ArmRoutines">
            <input_port name="gesture"/>
            <input_port name="topic"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="Charge">
            <input_port name="action"/>
            <input_port name="action_name"/>
        </Action>
        <SubTree ID="ChargeST"/>
        <Action ID="CheckBattery">
            <output_port name="battery_state" type="int"/>
            <output_port name="percentage" type="double"/>
            <input_port name="service_name" type="std::string"/>
        </Action>
        <Condition ID="CheckBool">
            <input_port name="variable"/>
        </Condition>
        <Action ID="CheckCharging">
            <output_port name="charging_state" type="int"/>
            <input_port name="service_name" type="std::string"/>
        </Action>
        <Condition ID="CompareDouble">
            <input_port name="A"/>
            <input_port name="B"/>
            <input_port name="Condition"/>
        </Condition>
        <Condition ID="CompareInt">
            <input_port name="A"/>
            <input_port name="B"/>
            <input_port name="Condition"/>
        </Condition>
        <Action ID="DetectBlocks">
            <output_port name="block_list" type="social_cues::BlockList_<std::allocator<void> >"/>
            <input_port name="block_topic" type="std::string"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="DetectFaces">
            <output_port name="faceListOut"/>
            <input_port name="face_topic"/>
        </Action>
        <SubTree ID="ErrorGameState"/>
        <Action ID="FaceCounter">
            <input_port name="faceListIn"/>
            <input_port name="number_faces"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="GazeAtTarget">
            <input_port name="action_name"/>
            <input_port name="fixation_pose"/>
            <input_port name="frame_id"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="GetInt16">
            <output_port name="number"/>
            <input_port name="topic"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="GetPoseArray">
            <output_port name="pose_array"/>
            <input_port name="topic"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="MoveBase">
            <input_port name="action_name"/>
            <input_port name="frame_id"/>
            <input_port name="goal_pose"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="SelectBlock">
            <input_port name="block_list" type="social_cues::BlockList_<std::allocator<void> >"/>
            <output_port name="block_pose" type="geometry_msgs::PoseStamped_<std::allocator<void> >"/>
            <input_port name="position_in_list" type="std::string"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="SelectFieldFromPoseStamped">
            <input_port name="field"/>
            <output_port name="output_val"/>
            <input_port name="pose_stamped"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="SelectPose">
            <input_port name="pose_array"/>
            <output_port name="pose_stamped"/>
            <input_port name="position_in_list"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="Speak">
            <input_port name="action_name" type="std::string"/>
            <input_port name="language" type="std::string"/>
            <input_port name="utterance" type="std::string"/>
            <input_port name="voice" type="std::string"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="WaitForXSeconds">
            <input_port default="SUCCESS" name="result">Result after specified time: SUCCESS or FAILURE</input_port>
            <input_port default="5" name="seconds">Time in seconds</input_port>
        </Action>
    </TreeNodesModel>
    <!-- ////////// -->
</root>
mrq:
The mission describes a social interaction routine that detects a face, orients attention to it, and coordinates an arm gesture, with a safe fallback that lowers the arms.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (GetPoseArray, SelectPose, Parallel) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action GetPoseArray to read a list of face poses from the faces topic and will store the list as face_array. It will run the custom action SelectPose to select the first face from face_array and will store the selected pose as face. It will execute the following actions (GazeAtTarget, Fallback) simultaneously. If either a success threshold is reached or all actions succeed, the execution was successful. Else if any action fails, the execution fails. It will run the custom action GazeAtTarget to fixate its gaze at the stored face pose in the base_footprint frame. It will execute the following actions (Sequence, LowArms) consecutively. If any action fails, move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will execute the following actions (SelectFieldFromPoseStamped, CompareDouble, ReactiveSequence) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action SelectFieldFromPoseStamped to extract the x field from the face pose and will store it as x_thresh. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule where x_thresh is less than 1.0. It will execute the following actions (SelectFieldFromPoseStamped, ArmRoutines) in sequence. If ArmRoutines fails, stop execution. While ArmRoutines is still running, re-evaluate previous actions. It will run the custom action SelectFieldFromPoseStamped to extract the y field from the face pose and will store it as y_thresh. It will run the custom action ArmRoutines on the /vizzyArmRoutines/command topic to perform the STRETCH gesture. It will run the subtree LowArms if the preceding sequence fails to produce a success.

Next, the robot will run the subtree LowArms if the preceding sequence in the Fallback fails to succeed.

Finally, the mission describes the LowArms subtree that positions both arms in a safe posture. First, it will run the custom action ArmRoutines on the /vizzyArmRoutines/command topic to perform the HOME gesture.

<?xml version="1.0"?>
<root main_tree_to_execute="BehaviorTree">
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="BehaviorTree">
        <Repeat num_cycles="{num_cycles}">
            <Sequence>
                <Fallback>
                    <Condition ID="LTLPreCheck" ltl_state_current="{ltl_state_current}" ltl_state_desired_sequence="{ltl_state_desired_sequence}" ltl_state_executed_sequence="{ltl_state_executed_sequence}"/>
                    <ForceFailure>
                        <Action ID="ReplanningRequestLevel2" ltl_state_current="{ltl_state_current}" ltl_state_executed_sequence="{ltl_state_executed_sequence}" replanning_request="{replanning_request}"/>
                    </ForceFailure>
                </Fallback>
                <ReactiveSequence>
                    <Fallback>
                        <Action ID="FakeDetectionLevel1" replanning_fake_input="{replanning_fake_input}"/>
                        <ForceFailure>
                            <Action ID="ReplanningRequestLevel1" ltl_state_current="{ltl_state_current}" ltl_state_executed_sequence="{ltl_state_executed_sequence}" replanning_request="{replanning_request}"/>
                        </ForceFailure>
                    </Fallback>
                    <Fallback>
                        <Condition ID="ReactiveLTLStateCheck" ltl_state_current="{ltl_state_current}" ltl_state_desired_sequence="{ltl_state_desired_sequence}"/>
                        <ForceFailure>
                            <Action ID="ReplanningRequestLevel2" ltl_state_current="{ltl_state_current}" ltl_state_executed_sequence="{ltl_state_executed_sequence}" replanning_request="{replanning_request}"/>
                        </ForceFailure>
                    </Fallback>
                    <Fallback>
                        <Action ID="FakeDetectionLevel3" replanning_fake_input="{replanning_fake_input}"/>
                        <ForceFailure>
                            <Action ID="ReplanningRequestLevel3" ltl_state_current="{ltl_state_current}" ltl_state_executed_sequence="{ltl_state_executed_sequence}" replanning_request="{replanning_request}"/>
                        </ForceFailure>
                    </Fallback>
                    <Fallback>
                        <Switch4 case_1="move" case_2="stay" case_3="pick" case_4="drop" variable="{bt_action_type}">
                            <Action ID="MoveAction" bt_action_type="{bt_action_type}" current_action="{current_action}" goal_sent="{goal_sent}" move_base_finished="{move_base_finished}" move_base_idle="{move_base_idle}"/>
                            <Action ID="StayAction" bt_action_type="{bt_action_type}" current_action="{current_action}" ltl_state_current="{ltl_state_current}"/>
                            <Action ID="PickAction" bt_action_type="{bt_action_type}" current_action="{current_action}" ltl_state_current="{ltl_state_current}"/>
                            <Action ID="DropAction" bt_action_type="{bt_action_type}" current_action="{current_action}" ltl_state_current="{ltl_state_current}"/>
                            <AlwaysFailure/>
                        </Switch4>
                        <ForceFailure>
                            <Action ID="ReplanningRequestLevel3" ltl_state_current="{ltl_state_current}" ltl_state_executed_sequence="{ltl_state_executed_sequence}" replanning_request="{replanning_request}"/>
                        </ForceFailure>
                    </Fallback>
                </ReactiveSequence>
                <Action ID="UpdateLTL" action_sequence="{action_sequence}" action_sequence_executed="{action_sequence_executed}" bt_action_type="{bt_action_type}" current_action="{current_action}" ltl_state_desired_sequence="{ltl_state_desired_sequence}"/>
            </Sequence>
        </Repeat>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <TreeNodesModel>
        <Action ID="BackNormalAction">
            <input_port name="bt_action_type"/>
            <input_port name="current_action"/>
            <inout_port name="ltl_state_current"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="DropAction">
            <input_port name="bt_action_type"/>
            <input_port name="current_action"/>
            <inout_port name="ltl_state_current"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="FakeDetectionLevel1">
            <input_port name="replanning_fake_input"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="FakeDetectionLevel2">
            <input_port name="replanning_fake_input"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="FakeDetectionLevel3">
            <input_port name="replanning_fake_input"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="GuideAction">
            <input_port name="bt_action_type"/>
            <input_port name="current_action"/>
            <inout_port name="ltl_state_current"/>
        </Action>
        <Condition ID="LTLPreCheck">
            <input_port name="ltl_state_current"/>
            <input_port name="ltl_state_desired_sequence"/>
            <inout_port name="ltl_state_executed_sequence"/>
        </Condition>
        <Action ID="LocomotionStart">
            <input_port name="locomotion_status"/>
        </Action>
        <Condition ID="LocomotionStatusCheck">
            <input_port name="locomotion_status"/>
        </Condition>
        <Action ID="MoveAction">
            <input_port name="bt_action_type"/>
            <input_port name="current_action"/>
            <output_port name="goal_sent"/>
            <input_port name="move_base_finished"/>
            <input_port name="move_base_idle"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="PickAction">
            <input_port name="bt_action_type"/>
            <input_port name="current_action"/>
            <inout_port name="ltl_state_current"/>
        </Action>
        <Condition ID="ReactiveLTLStateCheck">
            <input_port name="ltl_state_current"/>
            <input_port name="ltl_state_desired_sequence"/>
        </Condition>
        <Action ID="RecoveryStand">
            <input_port name="locomotion_status"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="ReplanningRequestLevel1">
            <input_port name="ltl_state_current"/>
            <inout_port name="ltl_state_executed_sequence"/>
            <inout_port name="replanning_request"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="ReplanningRequestLevel2">
            <input_port name="ltl_state_current"/>
            <inout_port name="ltl_state_executed_sequence"/>
            <inout_port name="replanning_request"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="ReplanningRequestLevel3">
            <input_port name="ltl_state_current"/>
            <inout_port name="ltl_state_executed_sequence"/>
            <inout_port name="replanning_request"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="StayAction">
            <input_port name="bt_action_type"/>
            <input_port name="current_action"/>
            <inout_port name="ltl_state_current"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="SynchronizedTransitionAction">
            <input_port name="bt_action_type"/>
            <input_port name="current_action"/>
            <inout_port name="ltl_state_current"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="UpdateLTL">
            <inout_port name="action_sequence"/>
            <inout_port name="action_sequence_executed"/>
            <output_port name="bt_action_type"/>
            <output_port name="current_action"/>
            <inout_port name="ltl_state_desired_sequence"/>
        </Action>
    </TreeNodesModel>
    <!-- ////////// -->
</root>
mrq:
The mission describes a repeated task that verifies a temporal-logic plan, reacts to events with replanning when needed, executes a selected action, and updates the plan state.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (Fallback, ReactiveSequence, UpdateLTL) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute the following actions (LTLPreCheck, ForceFailure(ReplanningRequestLevel2)) consecutively. If any action (LTLPreCheck) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for the condition LTLPreCheck. It will execute ReplanningRequestLevel2 and make sure it always fails.

Next, it will execute the following actions (Fallback, Fallback, Fallback, Fallback) in sequence. If any action fails, it will stop execution. While an action is still running, it will re-evaluate previous actions. It will execute the following actions (FakeDetectionLevel1, ForceFailure(ReplanningRequestLevel1)) consecutively. If any action (FakeDetectionLevel1) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will execute ReplanningRequestLevel1 and make sure it always fails. It will execute the following actions (ReactiveLTLStateCheck, ForceFailure(ReplanningRequestLevel2)) consecutively. If any action (ReactiveLTLStateCheck) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for the condition ReactiveLTLStateCheck. It will execute ReplanningRequestLevel2 and make sure it always fails. It will execute the following actions (FakeDetectionLevel3, ForceFailure(ReplanningRequestLevel3)) consecutively. If any action (FakeDetectionLevel3) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will execute ReplanningRequestLevel3 and make sure it always fails. It will execute the following actions (Switch4, ForceFailure(ReplanningRequestLevel3)) consecutively. If any action (Switch4) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will read blackboard value and compare it against a set of cases. If a case matches the blackboard value, it will execute corresponding action. Else if no case matches, it will execute default action. It will run the custom action MoveAction if the case move matches. It will run the custom action StayAction if the case stay matches. It will run the custom action PickAction if the case pick matches. It will run the custom action DropAction if the case drop matches. It will execute ReplanningRequestLevel3 and make sure it always fails.

Finally, it will execute the custom action UpdateLTL. It will update the action sequence, the executed action sequence, the next action type, the current action, and the desired temporal-logic sequence.

mrq:
The mission describes a decision-driven task that tries multiple goal-directed actions in order until one succeeds.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (go1, Sequence2, go7, go8) consecutively. If any action (go1, Sequence2, go7, go8) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will execute the custom action go1 as the first attempt.

Next, it will execute the following actions (go2, Fallback, go6) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. While an action is running or has succeeded, it will go to the next action. It will execute the custom action go2 before evaluating the nested decision.

Next, it will execute the following actions (go3, go4, go5) consecutively. If any action (go3, go4, go5) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will execute the custom actions go3, go4, and go5 as alternative attempts within this decision.

Finally, it will execute the custom action go6 after the nested decision succeeds within the sequence.

<root main_tree_to_execute="MainTree">
  <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
    <PipelineSequence name="NavigateWithReplanning">
      <SpeedController min_rate="0.1" max_rate="1.0" min_speed="0.0" max_speed="0.26" filter_duration="0.3">
        <ComputePathToPose goal="{goal}" path="{path}" planner_id="GridBased"/>
      </SpeedController>
      <FollowPath path="{path}"  controller_id="FollowPath"/>
    </PipelineSequence>
  </BehaviorTree>
</root>
mrq: The mission describes a navigation task that computes a path to a goal at a controlled rate and advances to following that path while planning continues. First, the robot will execute the following actions (SpeedController, FollowPath) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute the following actions (ComputePathToPose) in order within the ROS action SpeedController and will advance while the inner action is running or has succeeded. It will run the ROS action ComputePathToPose with the provided goal and will store the resulting path while the SpeedController enforces a planning rate between 0.1 Hz and 1.0 Hz with a filter duration of 0.3 seconds. It will run the ROS action FollowPath using the stored path with the specified controller while the planning action may still be running.

BT:
<!--
  This Behavior Tree replans the global path periodically at 1 Hz and it also has
  recovery actions.
-->
<root main_tree_to_execute="MainTree">
  <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
    <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="2" name="NavigateRecovery">
      <PipelineSequence name="NavigateWithReplanning">
        <RateController hz="1.0">
          <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="4" name="ComputePathToPose">
            <ComputePathToPose goal="{goal}" path="{path}" planner_id="GridBased"/>
            <ReactiveFallback name="PathToPoseFallback">
              <GoalUpdated/>
              <RoundRobin name="GlobalPlannerRecoveryActions">
                <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearGlobalCostmap-Context" service_name="global_costmap/clear_entirely_global_costmap"/>
                <Wait wait_duration="2"/>
              </RoundRobin>
            </ReactiveFallback>
          </RecoveryNode>
        </RateController>
        <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="4" name="FollowPath">
          <FollowPath path="{path}" controller_id="FollowPath"/>
          <ReactiveFallback name="FollowPathFallback">
            <GoalUpdated/>
            <RoundRobin name="PlannerRecoveryActions">
              <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearLocalCostmap-Context" service_name="local_costmap/clear_entirely_local_costmap"/>
              <Spin spin_dist="1.57"/>
            </RoundRobin>
          </ReactiveFallback>
        </RecoveryNode>
      </PipelineSequence>
      <ReactiveFallback name="RecoveryFallback">
        <GoalUpdated/>
        <Wait wait_duration="5"/>
      </ReactiveFallback>
    </RecoveryNode>
  </BehaviorTree>
</root>
mrq: The mission describes a navigation task that periodically replans a global path at 1 Hz and performs layered recovery while computing and following the path. First, the robot will execute the following actions (RateController, ComputePathToPose, FollowPath) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails, and while an action is running or has succeeded, it will go to the next action. It will run the custom action RateController at 1.0 Hz to pace subsequent actions. It will execute main action the ROS action ComputePathToPose and, if it fails, it will execute recovery action the ReactiveFallback PathToPoseFallback and then retry the main action the ROS action ComputePathToPose up to four times before stopping. It will execute the following actions GoalUpdated, RoundRobin GlobalPlannerRecoveryActions in order, and if any action succeeds, it will stop execution, else it will keep executing and re-evaluating previous actions whenever an action is running. It will execute the following actions (ClearEntireCostmap-Context, Wait) consecutively and, if any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds, and if no action succeeds, it will start all over again until an action succeeds. It will run the ROS action ClearEntireCostmap-Context to clear the global costmap when invoked. It will run the custom action Wait for 2 seconds when invoked. Next, it will execute main action the ROS action FollowPath and, if it fails, it will execute recovery action the ReactiveFallback FollowPathFallback and then retry the main action the ROS action FollowPath up to four times before stopping. It will execute the following actions GoalUpdated, RoundRobin PlannerRecoveryActions in order, and if any action succeeds, it will stop execution, else it will keep executing and re-evaluating previous actions whenever an action is running. It will execute the following actions (ClearEntireCostmap-Context, Spin) consecutively and, if any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds, and if no action succeeds, it will start all over again until an action succeeds. It will run the ROS action ClearEntireCostmap-Context to clear the local costmap when invoked. It will run the ROS action Spin for a rotation of 1.57 radians when invoked. Finally, it will execute main action the PipelineSequence NavigateWithReplanning and, if it fails, it will execute recovery action the ReactiveFallback RecoveryFallback and then retry the main action the PipelineSequence NavigateWithReplanning up to two times before stopping. It will execute the following actions GoalUpdated, Wait in order, and if any action succeeds, it will stop execution, else it will keep executing and re-evaluating previous actions whenever an action is running. It will run the custom action GoalUpdated to signal that the goal has changed when evaluated. It will run the custom action Wait for 5 seconds when evaluated.

mrq:
The mission describes a road-driving planner that will choose among pull-over, pull-out, lane-change, or lane-following behaviors and will produce a single planning output for execution.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (Condition PullOver_Request, Condition PullOver_Ready, Action PullOver_Plan) in sequence. If Condition PullOver_Request fails, it will stop execution of this sequence. While an action is still running, it will re-evaluate previous actions in this sequence. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for PullOver_Request being true. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for PullOver_Ready being true. It will run the custom action PullOver_Plan and will write the resulting plan to the output port when both conditions are satisfied.

Next, it will execute the following actions (Condition PullOut_Request, Condition PullOut_Ready, Action PullOut_Plan) in sequence. If Condition PullOut_Request fails, it will stop execution of this sequence. While an action is still running, it will re-evaluate previous actions in this sequence. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for PullOut_Request being true. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for PullOut_Ready being true. It will run the custom action PullOut_Plan and will write the resulting plan to the output port when both conditions are satisfied.

Next, it will execute the following actions (Condition LaneChange_Request, Fallback of conditions, Action LaneChange_Plan) in sequence. If Condition LaneChange_Request fails, it will stop execution of this sequence. While an action is still running, it will re-evaluate previous actions in this sequence. It will execute the following actions (Condition LaneChange_Ready, Condition ForceLaneChange_ForceApproval) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will run the custom action LaneChange_Plan and will write the resulting plan to the output port when either readiness is true or forced approval is true.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (Action LaneFollowing_Plan) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will run the custom action LaneFollowing_Plan and will write the resulting plan to the output port when all earlier alternatives are not selected.

First, it will execute the following actions (Condition LaneChange_CheckApproval, KeepRunningUntilFailure of Action LaneChange_PlanCandidate) in order. If Condition LaneChange_CheckApproval succeeds, it will stop execution. Else it will keep executing and re-evaluating previous actions whenever an action is running. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for LaneChange_CheckApproval being true. It will run the custom action LaneChange_PlanCandidate. If the custom action LaneChange_PlanCandidate succeeds, it will restart it. Else if the custom action LaneChange_PlanCandidate fails, it will stop looping and continue execution.

Next, it will execute the following actions (Condition Avoidance_CheckApproval, KeepRunningUntilFailure of Action Avoidance_PlanCandidate) in order. If Condition Avoidance_CheckApproval succeeds, it will stop execution. Else it will keep executing and re-evaluating previous actions whenever an action is running. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for Avoidance_CheckApproval being true. It will run the custom action Avoidance_PlanCandidate. If the custom action Avoidance_PlanCandidate succeeds, it will restart it. Else if the custom action Avoidance_PlanCandidate fails, it will stop looping and continue execution.

The mission describes a sequential task that executes a series of actions in strict order to complete a compound routine.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (go1, Sequence2, go2, go3, Sequence4) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action go1 before attempting the nested sequence. It will run the custom action go2 only after the nested sequence succeeds. It will run the custom action go3 only after go2 succeeds. It will run the nested sequence named Sequence4 only after go3 succeeds.

Next, it will execute the following actions (stay1, stay2, Sequence3) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action stay1 before attempting stay2. It will run the custom action stay2 only after stay1 succeeds. It will run the nested sequence named Sequence3 only after stay2 succeeds.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (go1, go2, go3) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action go1 before attempting go2. It will run the custom action go2 only after go1 succeeds. It will run the custom action go3 only after go2 succeeds.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (go1, go2) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action go1 before attempting go2. It will run the custom action go2 only after go1 succeeds.

mrq:
The mission describes a composite task that first performs path planning and following, then conditionally rearranges items, and then iterates a charge-driven decision and indexed navigation routine.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (ComputePathToPose, FollowPath) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the ROS action ComputePathToPose with the provided goal and will store the resulting path. It will run the ROS action FollowPath using the stored path after the path computation succeeds.

Next, it will execute the following actions (IsFlagHigh, Rearrange) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for IsFlagHigh. It will run the custom action Rearrange only after the condition IsFlagHigh succeeds.

Finally, it will execute a set of child nodes multiple times. It will execute the following actions (RetryUntilSuccesful, Switch2) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will keep trying to execute a switch that reads a blackboard value named Charge until it succeeds. It will read blackboard value and compare it against a set of cases. If a case matches the blackboard value, it will execute corresponding action. Else if no case matches, it will execute default action. It will execute the following actions (AlwaysSuccess, AlwaysFailure) simultaneously. If either a success threshold is reached or all actions succeed, the execution was successful. Else if any action fails, the execution fails. It will execute the switch that reads a blackboard value named i after the first switch succeeds. It will read blackboard value and compare it against a set of cases. If a case matches the blackboard value, it will execute corresponding action. Else if no case matches, it will execute default action. It will run the custom action GoToPose with input I equal to n when i equals 0. It will run the custom action GoToPose with input I equal to i-1 when i is greater than 0.

mrq: The mission describes a pursuit-and-engage task that will try to acquire a target, advance toward it, and attack when adjacent, with a fallback to random movement if the main pursuit cannot progress. First, the robot will execute the following actions (GetTarget, Move, Sequence(TargetAdjacent, AttackTarget)) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next, and if any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will run the custom action GetTarget to acquire a valid target. It will run the custom action Move to advance toward the acquired target after the target acquisition succeeds. It will execute the following actions (TargetAdjacent, AttackTarget) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for TargetAdjacent. It will run the custom action AttackTarget after the adjacency check succeeds. Next, it will execute the following actions (SequenceStar(Main), RandomMove) consecutively. If any action (SequenceStar(Main)) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will run the custom action RandomMove if the main sequence cannot make progress.

mrq:
The mission describes a door and window traversal task that will first try a door and then try a window to reach the other side.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (EnsureOpened, PassThroughDoor) consecutively. If any action (EnsureOpened) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will run the custom action EnsureOpened and will proceed to the custom action PassThroughDoor only if EnsureOpened fails.

Next, it will run the custom action PassThroughWindow as the alternative path. It will attempt PassThroughWindow only if both EnsureOpened and PassThroughDoor fail in the previous fallback.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (IsDoorOpen, RetryUntilSuccesful) consecutively. If any action (IsDoorOpen) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will run the custom action IsDoorOpen and will succeed immediately if the door is already open. It will keep trying to execute the custom action OpenDoor until it succeeds, and it will stop retrying after a limited number of attempts if success occurs.

mrq: The mission describes a navigation task that computes a global path to a goal, updates that path after every one meter, and follows the most recent path. First, the robot will execute the following actions (DistanceController, FollowPath) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will, while an action is running or has succeeded, go to the next action. It will run the custom action DistanceController that wraps the ROS action ComputePathToPose and triggers a new path computation each time it has moved one meter. It will run the ROS action ComputePathToPose with the provided goal and will store the resulting path in the blackboard variable path whenever the one-meter threshold is reached. It will then run the ROS action FollowPath using the stored path with the specified controller while new path computations may continue as distance thresholds are met.

        </Action>
        <Action ID="ExecuteAttachBox">
            <input_port name="box_name"/>
            <input_port name="box_pose"/>
            <input_port name="box_size"/>
            <input_port default="gripper" name="eef_group_name"/>
            <input_port default="arm" name="group_name"/>
            <input_port default="execute_attach_box" name="service_name"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="ExecuteDetachObject">
            <input_port default="arm" name="group_name"/>
            <input_port name="obj_name"/>
            <input_port default="execute_detach_object" name="service_name"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="ExecuteGroupAngularJointStates">
            <input_port default="0 0 0 0 0 0 0" name="goal"/>
            <input_port default="panda_arm" name="group_name"/>
            <input_port default="execute_group_joint_states" name="service_name"/>
            <input_port default="0.01" name="tolerance"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="ExecuteGroupLinearJointStates">
            <input_port default="0.04 0.04" name="goal"/>
            <input_port default="hand" name="group_name"/>
            <input_port default="execute_group_joint_states" name="service_name"/>
            <input_port default="0.01" name="tolerance"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="ExecuteGroupManyPoses">
            <input_port name="constraint">If 'r', 'p', or 'y' is given, the eef_frame should not rotate about itself along x-, y-, or z-axis. You can combine them like 'rp', 'ry', 'py', and 'rpy'. Default ''</input_port>
            <input_port default="0.1" name="eef_step"/>
            <input_port default="0" name="goal_type">0: Pose wrt the global base frame (robot base); 1: Pose wrt the base frame transferred to current eef base position; 2: pose wrt the eef frame</input_port>
            <input_port default="0 0 0 0 0 0 1" name="goals">Goals for each waypoint to reach, seperate with ; </input_port>
            <input_port default="arm" name="group_name">The group providing the reference frame</input_port>
            <input_port default="execute_group_many_poses" name="service_name"/>
            <input_port default="0.01" name="tolerance"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="ExecuteGroupPose">
            <input_port name="constraint">If 'r', 'p', or 'y' is given, the eef_frame should not rotate about itself along x-, y-, or z-axis. You can combine them like 'rp', 'ry', 'py', and 'rpy'. Default ''</input_port>
            <input_port default="0 0 0 0 0 0 1" name="goal"/>
            <input_port default="0" name="goal_type">0: Pose wrt the global base frame (robot base); 1: Pose wrt the base frame transferred to current eef base position; 2: pose wrt the eef frame</input_port>
            <input_port default="arm" name="group_name">The group providing the reference frame</input_port>
            <input_port default="execute_group_pose" name="service_name"/>
            <input_port default="0.01" name="tolerance"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="ExecuteGroupShift">
            <input_port default="z" name="axis">Could be x y z roll pitch yaw</input_port>
            <input_port default="0" name="goal"/>
            <input_port default="arm" name="group_name"/>
            <input_port default="0" name="is_absolute"/>
            <input_port default="execute_group_shift" name="service_name"/>
            <input_port default="0.01" name="tolerance"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="ExecutePlanning">
            <input_port name="category">0 for large box, 1 for small box</input_port>
            <output_port default="{pick_pose}" name="pick_pose">Pose</output_port>
            <output_port default="{place_pose}" name="place_pose">Double Array</output_port>
            <input_port default="{box_surface_pose}" name="pose">Pose</input_port>
            <output_port default="{post_middle_pose}" name="post_middle_pose">Double Array</output_port>
            <output_port default="{post_pick_pose}" name="post_pick_pose">Pose</output_port>
            <output_port default="{post_pick_pose_edge}" name="post_pick_pose_edge">Pose</output_port>
            <output_port default="{pre_middle_pose}" name="pre_middle_pose">Double Array</output_port>
            <output_port default="{pre_pick_pose}" name="pre_pick_pose">Pose</output_port>
            <input_port default="execute_planning" name="service_name"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="ExecuteRemoveObject">
            <input_port default="1" name="is_exact"/>
            <input_port name="obj_name"/>
            <input_port default="execute_remove_object" name="service_name"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="ExecuteSuction">
            <input_port default="0" name="enable"/>
            <input_port default="execute_suction" name="service_name"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="FetchDetectedInfo">
            <output_port name="category">String, 0 for large box, 1 for small box</output_port>
            <output_port name="pose">Pose</output_port>
            <input_port default="fetch_detected_info" name="service_name"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="SenseObjectPose">
            <output_port name="category">String, 0 for large box, 1 for small box</output_port>
            <output_port default="{box_surface_pose}" name="pose">Pose</output_port>
            <input_port default="sense_object_pose" name="service_name"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="StoreDetectedInfo">
            <input_port name="category">String, 0 for large box, 1 for small box</input_port>
            <input_port name="pose">Pose</input_port>
            <input_port default="store_detected_info" name="service_name"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="TypeInPose">
            <output_port name="pose_on_blackboard">Pose on blackboard</output_port>
            <input_port default="type_in_pose" name="service_name"/>
            <input_port default="0 0 0 0 0 0 1" name="type_in_pose">Pose to be typed in</input_port>
        </Action>
    </TreeNodesModel>
    <!-- ////////// -->
</root>
mrq:
The mission describes a pick-and-place operation that configures a collision scene, sets a virtual surface, and repeats a left-side pick-and-place routine a fixed number of times in strict order.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (ExecuteAddPlane, ExecuteAddBox, ExecuteAddBox, ExecuteAddBox, ExecuteAddBox) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action ExecuteAddPlane to add a ground plane to the planning scene for the specified arm group. It will run the custom action ExecuteAddBox to add a left wall with the given pose and size as a collision object. It will run the custom action ExecuteAddBox to add a right wall with the given pose and size as a collision object. It will run the custom action ExecuteAddBox to add a top wall with the given pose and size as a collision object. It will run the custom action ExecuteAddBox to add a conveyor with the given pose and size as a collision object.

Next, it will execute the following actions (ExecuteAddBox) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action ExecuteAddBox to add a thin box-surface collision object at the specified pose and size.

Finally, it will execute Execute [Action] for a set number of times or infinitely. It will execute the following actions (SenseObjectPose, ExecutePlanning, ExecuteGroupLinearJointStates, ExecuteRemoveObject, ConnectWaypoints, ExecuteGroupManyPoses, ExecuteSuction, ConnectWaypoints, ExecuteGroupManyPoses, ExecuteGroupLinearJointStates, ExecuteGroupLinearJointStates, ExecuteSuction, ExecuteAddBox) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails for 36 cycles. It will run the custom action SenseObjectPose to detect and store the box surface pose on the blackboard. It will run the custom action ExecutePlanning to compute pre-pick, pick, post-pick, edge, middle, and place poses and to output them onto the blackboard. It will run the custom action ExecuteGroupLinearJointStates to move the arm to the pre-middle joint configuration. It will run the custom action ExecuteRemoveObject to temporarily remove the box-surface collision object from the scene by exact name. It will run the custom action ConnectWaypoints to generate a connected pick-motion from the pre-pick pose to the pick pose. It will run the custom action ExecuteGroupManyPoses to follow the connected pre-pick and pick waypoints with the specified step and tolerance. It will run the custom action ExecuteSuction to enable vacuum and grasp the object. It will run the custom action ConnectWaypoints to generate a connected pull-out motion from the post-pick pose to the post-pick-edge pose. It will run the custom action ExecuteGroupManyPoses to follow the pull-out waypoints with the specified step and tolerance. It will run the custom action ExecuteGroupLinearJointStates to move the arm to the post-middle joint configuration. It will run the custom action ExecuteGroupLinearJointStates to move the arm to the place joint configuration. It will run the custom action ExecuteSuction to disable vacuum and release the object. It will run the custom action ExecuteAddBox to restore the box-surface collision object at the specified pose and size.

mrq:
The mission describes an animal-behavior control that prioritizes mating, birthing, egg laying, production, and shed-related movement with retries until one path succeeds.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (Sex, Birth, EggLayer, Producer, ShedTree) consecutively. If any action (Sex, Birth, EggLayer, Producer, ShedTree) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, it will start all over again until an action succeeds. It will execute the following actions (IsMale, IsAdult, TryHaveSex) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for IsMale. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for IsAdult. It will execute the custom action TryHaveSex. It will execute the following actions (IsFemale, IsReadyToGiveBirth, GiveBirth) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for IsFemale. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for IsReadyToGiveBirth. It will execute the custom action GiveBirth. It will execute the following actions (IsEggLayer, LayEgg) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for IsEggLayer. It will execute the custom action LayEgg. It will execute the following actions (IsProducer, Produce) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for IsProducer. It will execute the custom action Produce. It will execute the subtree ShedTree after the previous actions fail during the current pass.

Next, it will execute the following actions (ShedTree fallback set) consecutively. If any action (first shed sequence, second shed sequence, third shed sequence) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, it will start all over again until an action succeeds. It will execute the following actions (IsOnPasture, IsDay, IsInShed, LeaveShed, FindRandomPastureField, Move) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for IsOnPasture. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for IsDay. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for IsInShed. It will execute the custom action LeaveShed. It will execute the custom action FindRandomPastureField. It will execute the custom action Move. It will execute the following actions (IsOnPasture, IsNight, Inverter(IsInShed), FindShed, Move, EnterShed) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for IsOnPasture. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for IsNight. It will execute inverted Find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for IsInShed. If Find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for IsInShed succeeds, it will reverse it to fail. Else if Find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for IsInShed fails, it will reverse it to succeed. It will execute the custom action FindShed. It will execute the custom action Move. It will execute the custom action EnterShed. It will execute the following actions (IsDay, Inverter(IsInShed), RandomMove, Graze) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for IsDay. It will execute inverted Find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for IsInShed. If Find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for IsInShed succeeds, it will reverse it to fail. Else if Find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for IsInShed fails, it will reverse it to succeed. It will execute the custom action RandomMove. It will execute the custom action Graze.
