<!--
  This Behavior Tree replans the global path periodically at 1 Hz and it also has
  recovery actions.
-->
<root main_tree_to_execute="MainTree">
  <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
    <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="2" name="NavigateRecovery">
      <PipelineSequence name="NavigateWithReplanning">
        <RateController hz="1.0">
          <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="4" name="ComputePathToPose">
            <ComputePathToPose goal="{goal}" path="{path}" planner_id="GridBased"/>
            <ReactiveFallback name="PathToPoseFallback">
              <GoalUpdated/>
              <RoundRobin name="GlobalPlannerRecoveryActions">
                <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearGlobalCostmap-Context" service_name="global_costmap/clear_entirely_global_costmap"/>
                <Wait wait_duration="2"/>
              </RoundRobin>
            </ReactiveFallback>
          </RecoveryNode>
        </RateController>
        <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="4" name="FollowPath">
          <FollowPath path="{path}" controller_id="FollowPath"/>
          <ReactiveFallback name="FollowPathFallback">
            <GoalUpdated/>
            <RoundRobin name="PlannerRecoveryActions">
              <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearLocalCostmap-Context" service_name="local_costmap/clear_entirely_local_costmap"/>
              <Spin spin_dist="1.57"/>
            </RoundRobin>
          </ReactiveFallback>
        </RecoveryNode>
      </PipelineSequence>
      <ReactiveFallback name="RecoveryFallback">
        <GoalUpdated/>
        <Wait wait_duration="5"/>
      </ReactiveFallback>
    </RecoveryNode>
  </BehaviorTree>
</root>

The mission describes a navigation task that continuously replans a global path at 1 Hz, follows the path, and applies layered recovery behaviors when failures occur. First, the robot will execute the following actions (RateController, RecoveryNode) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. While an action is running or has succeeded, it will go to the next action. It will execute delayed RateController and, if execution succeeds, start a 1 Hz delay timer and after the delay period is exhausted, resume execution. It will execute main the ROS action ComputePathToPose and make up to four retries. If it fails, it will execute recovery action (ReactiveFallback). After recovery, it will retry main action (the ROS action ComputePathToPose). If the recovery action fails and retries are exhausted, it will stop execution. It will execute the following actions (GoalUpdated, RoundRobin) in order. If GoalUpdated succeeds, it will stop execution. Else it will keep executing and re-evaluating previous actions whenever an action is running. It will run the custom action GoalUpdated as a check for updated goals. It will execute the custom action RoundRobin and keep trying the enclosed actions one by one in turn. It will run the ROS action ClearEntireCostmap with the global costmap service. It will run the ROS action Wait with a duration of 2 seconds.

Next, it will execute the following actions (RecoveryNode) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. While an action is running or has succeeded, it will go to the next action. It will execute main the ROS action FollowPath and make up to four retries. If it fails, it will execute recovery action (ReactiveFallback). After recovery, it will retry main action (the ROS action FollowPath). If the recovery action fails and retries are exhausted, it will stop execution. It will execute the following actions (GoalUpdated, RoundRobin) in order. If GoalUpdated succeeds, it will stop execution. Else it will keep executing and re-evaluating previous actions whenever an action is running. It will run the custom action GoalUpdated as a check for updated goals. It will execute the custom action RoundRobin and keep trying the enclosed actions one by one in turn. It will run the ROS action ClearEntireCostmap with the local costmap service. It will run the ROS action Spin with a spin distance of 1.57 radians.

Finally, it will execute main PipelineSequence and make up to two retries. If it fails, it will execute recovery action (ReactiveFallback). After recovery, it will retry main action (PipelineSequence). If the recovery action fails and retries are exhausted, it will stop execution. It will execute the following actions (GoalUpdated, Wait) in order. If GoalUpdated succeeds, it will stop execution. Else it will keep executing and re-evaluating previous actions whenever an action is running. It will run the custom action GoalUpdated as a check for updated goals. It will run the ROS action Wait with a duration of 5 seconds.

<root main_tree_to_execute="MainTree">
  <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
    <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="6" name="NavigateRecovery">
      <PipelineSequence name="NavigateWithReplanning">
        <RateController hz="1.0">
          <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="1" name="ComputePathToPose">
            <ComputePathToPose goal="{goal}" path="{path}" planner_id="GridBased"/>
            <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearGlobalCostmap-Context" service_name="global_costmap/clear_entirely_global_costmap"/>
          </RecoveryNode>
        </RateController>
        <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="1" name="FollowPath">
          <FollowPath path="{path}" controller_id="FollowPath"/>
          <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearLocalCostmap-Context" service_name="local_costmap/clear_entirely_local_costmap"/>
        </RecoveryNode>
      </PipelineSequence>
      <ReactiveFallback name="RecoveryFallback">
        <GoalUpdated/>
        <SequenceStar name="RecoveryActions">
          <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearLocalCostmap-Subtree" service_name="local_costmap/clear_entirely_local_costmap"/>
          <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearGlobalCostmap-Subtree" service_name="global_costmap/clear_entirely_global_costmap"/>
          <Spin spin_dist="1.57"/>
          <Wait wait_duration="5"/>
        </SequenceStar>
      </ReactiveFallback>
    </RecoveryNode>
  </BehaviorTree>
</root>
mrq:
The mission describes a navigation task that computes a global path to a goal and follows the path with continuous replanning and structured recovery.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (RateController, RecoveryNode, RecoveryNode) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. While an action is running or has succeeded, it will go to the next action. It will run the custom action RateController at 1.0 Hz to pace downstream actions. It will execute main action the ROS action ComputePathToPose and, if it fails, it will execute recovery action the ROS action ClearEntireCostmap named ClearGlobalCostmap-Context and then retry the main action once. It will execute main action the ROS action FollowPath and, if it fails, it will execute recovery action the ROS action ClearEntireCostmap named ClearLocalCostmap-Context and then retry the main action once.

Next, it will execute the following actions (GoalUpdated, SequenceStar) in order. If any action succeeds, it will stop execution. Else it will keep executing and re-evaluating previous actions whenever an action is running. It will run the custom action GoalUpdated to check for a new goal and will stop this subtree if the goal update succeeds. It will execute the following actions (ClearEntireCostmap, ClearEntireCostmap, Spin, Wait) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will run the ROS action ClearEntireCostmap named ClearLocalCostmap-Subtree on the local costmap. It will run the ROS action ClearEntireCostmap named ClearGlobalCostmap-Subtree on the global costmap. It will run the ROS action Spin with a spin distance of 1.57. It will run the ROS action Wait for 5 seconds.

Finally, it will execute main action the custom action NavigateWithReplanning. If it fails, it will execute recovery action the custom action RecoveryFallback. After recovery, it will retry main action the custom action NavigateWithReplanning. If recovery action the custom action RecoveryFallback fails and retries are exhausted after 6 attempts, it will stop execution.

			<SubTree ID='PICK1'/>
			<SubTree ID='MOVE2'/>
			<SubTree ID='PLACE3'/>
			<SubTree ID='MOVE4'/>
			<SubTree ID='PICK5'/>
			<SubTree ID='MOVE6'/>
			<SubTree ID='MOVE7'/>
			<SubTree ID='PLACE8'/>
			<SubTree ID='MOVE9'/>
			<SubTree ID='PICK10'/>
			<SubTree ID='MOVE11'/>
			<SubTree ID='PLACE12'/>
		</Sequence>
	</BehaviourTree>
</root>
mrq:
The mission describes a pick-and-place task that executes a series of twelve subtrees in strict order to move items between locations.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (PICK1) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action PICK1 to grasp the designated item at its source location.

Next, it will execute the following actions (MOVE2) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action MOVE2 to travel from the first pick location to the first place location.

Next, it will execute the following actions (PLACE3) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action PLACE3 to release the grasped item at the first destination.

Next, it will execute the following actions (MOVE4) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action MOVE4 to travel from the first place location to the second pick location.

Next, it will execute the following actions (PICK5) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action PICK5 to grasp the second designated item.

Next, it will execute the following actions (MOVE6) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action MOVE6 to travel toward an intermediate waypoint.

Next, it will execute the following actions (MOVE7) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action MOVE7 to continue travel from the waypoint to the second place location.

Next, it will execute the following actions (PLACE8) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action PLACE8 to release the second item at its destination.

Next, it will execute the following actions (MOVE9) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action MOVE9 to travel to the third pick location.

Next, it will execute the following actions (PICK10) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action PICK10 to grasp the third designated item.

Next, it will execute the following actions (MOVE11) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action MOVE11 to travel from the third pick location to the third place location.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (PLACE12) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action PLACE12 to release the third item at its destination.

<root main_tree_to_execute="MainTree">
  <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
    <FallbackStar name="root_AutoLocalization">
      <initialPoseReceived/>
      <SequenceStar name="doSelfLocalization">
        <RetryUntilSuccesful num_attempts="5" name="retry_client_request">
          <globalLocalizationServiceRequest/>
        </RetryUntilSuccesful>
        <RetryUntilSuccesful num_attempts="10" name="retry_localization">
          <Sequence>
            <Fallback>
              <IsLocalized/>
              <SequenceStar>
               <Spin/>
               <BackUp/>
               <Spin/>
             </SequenceStar>
            </Fallback>
            <IsLocalized/>
          </Sequence> 
        </RetryUntilSuccesful>
      </SequenceStar>
    </FallbackStar>
  </BehaviorTree>
</root>
mrq:
The mission describes an auto-localization task that first accepts an initial pose if available and otherwise performs global localization with retries until the system becomes localized.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (initialPoseReceived, doSelfLocalization) consecutively. If any action (initialPoseReceived) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, it will start all over again until an action succeeds. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for the action initialPoseReceived. It will execute the following actions (retry_client_request, retry_localization) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning.

Next, it will execute main custom action globalLocalizationServiceRequest. If it fails, it will keep trying to execute custom action globalLocalizationServiceRequest until it succeeds. If try succeeds, it will stop retrying and continue. If try fails, it will keep retrying until it succeeds.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (Fallback, IsLocalized) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. While an action is running or has succeeded, it will go to the next action. It will execute the following actions (IsLocalized, SequenceStar) consecutively. If any action (IsLocalized) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for the action IsLocalized. It will execute the following actions (Spin, BackUp, Spin) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for the action IsLocalized.

mrq:
The mission describes a sheltering and grazing routine that reacts to day and night conditions on a pasture and manages movement between a shed and the pasture.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (IsOnPasture, IsDay, IsInShed, LeaveShed, FindRandomPastureField, Move) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for IsOnPasture. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for IsDay. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for IsInShed. It will run the custom action LeaveShed to exit the shed when all prior checks succeed. It will run the custom action FindRandomPastureField to select a random field on the pasture. It will run the custom action Move to go to the selected field.

Next, it will execute the following actions (IsOnPasture, IsNight, Inverter(IsInShed), FindShed, Move, EnterShed) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for IsOnPasture. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for IsNight. It will execute inverted IsInShed and, if IsInShed succeeds, it will reverse it to fail, else if IsInShed fails, it will reverse it to succeed. It will run the custom action FindShed to locate the shed. It will run the custom action Move to approach the shed. It will run the custom action EnterShed to enter the shed.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (IsDay, Inverter(IsInShed), RandomMove, Graze) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for IsDay. It will execute inverted IsInShed and, if IsInShed succeeds, it will reverse it to fail, else if IsInShed fails, it will reverse it to succeed. It will run the custom action RandomMove to wander on the pasture during the day. It will run the custom action Graze to feed while on the pasture.

                                                                            <Condition ID="checkObjectStaticRightLane"/>
                                                                            <Condition ID="checkCollisionBehindRight"/>
                                                                        </Fallback>
                                                                        <Action ID="setStateLaneKeeping"/>
                                                                    </Sequence>
                                                                    <Action ID="setStateLaneChangeRight"/>
                                                                </Fallback>
                                                            </Sequence>
                                                            <Action ID="setStateLaneKeeping"/>
                                                        </Fallback>
                                                    </Sequence>
                                                </Fallback>
                                            </Fallback>
                                        </Sequence>
                                        <Action ID="setStateLaneKeeping"/>
                                    </Fallback>
                                    <Fallback name="">
                                        <Condition ID="checkMustLaneChange"/>
                                        <AlwaysSuccess/>
                                    </Fallback>
                                </Sequence>
                            </Sequence>
                            <Sequence name="LaneChangeLeft">
                                <Condition ID="checkStateLaneChangeLeft"/>
                                <Fallback name="">
                                    <Sequence name="">
                                        <Condition ID="checkEndLaneChange"/>
                                        <Action ID="setStateLaneKeeping"/>
                                        <Action ID="callPathGlobal"/>
                                    </Sequence>
                                    <Action ID="speedProfileLaneChange"/>
                                </Fallback>
                            </Sequence>
                            <Sequence name="LaneChangeRigth">
                                <Condition ID="checkStateLaneChangeRight"/>
                                <Fallback name="">
                                    <Sequence name="">
                                        <Condition ID="checkEndLaneChange"/>
                                        <Action ID="setStateLaneKeeping"/>
                                        <Action ID="callPathGlobal"/>
                                    </Sequence>
                                    <Action ID="speedProfileLaneChange"/>
                                </Fallback>
                            </Sequence>
                            <Sequence name="Recovery">
                                <Action ID="setStateLaneKeeping"/>
                            </Sequence>
                        </Fallback>
                        <Fallback name="traffic check">
                            <Condition ID="checkPassAbleTrafficLight"/>
                            <Action ID="stopTrafficLight"/>
                        </Fallback>
                        <Fallback name="unprotectedTurn">
                            <Sequence>
                                <Condition ID="checkUnprotected"/>
                                <Condition ID="checkImpassalbeUnprotected"/>
                                <Action ID="stop"/>
                            </Sequence>
                            <AlwaysSuccess/>
                        </Fallback>
                    </Sequence>
                </Sequence>
                <Action ID="setPath"/>
                <Action ID="publishData"/>
                <Fallback name="mission state">
                    <Sequence>
                        <Condition ID="checkArriveGoal"/>
                        <Action ID="setStateMissionArrive"/>
                        <Action ID="publishStateMission"/>
                    </Sequence>
                    <Sequence>
                        <Action ID="setStateMissionGoing"/>
                    </Sequence>
                </Fallback>
            </Sequence>
        </Fallback>
    </BehaviorTree>

        <Action ID="callPathGlobalRight"/>

mrq:
The mission describes on-road autonomous driving that manages lane keeping, lane changes, traffic controls, and mission state from start to goal.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (checkMissionWait, stop) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for checkMissionWait. It will run the custom action stop if the wait condition holds.

Next, it will execute the following actions (checkExistPathGlobal, core initialization, init, gen path, traffic check, unprotected turn handling) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails while executing the following actions (checkExistPathGlobal, state and failure gate, callPathGlobal or always succeed, init, StateSelect, traffic check, unprotectedTurn) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for checkExistPathGlobal. It will execute the following actions (checkStateLaneKeeping, checkFailLaneChange, callPathGlobal) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails, or execute [Action] and make sure it always succeeds as a fallback. It will run the custom action init after the initialization gate succeeds. It will execute the following actions (StateSelect, traffic check, unprotectedTurn) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails while choosing among lane keeping, lane change left, lane change right, or recovery based on state. It will execute the following actions (checkStateLaneKeeping, genPathLaneKeeping, lane-keeping decision, must-change gate) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails for the LaneKeeping branch. It will run the custom action genPathLaneKeeping when lane keeping is active. It will execute the following actions (global lane-change evaluation or collision handling, setStateLaneKeeping) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails, selecting among left change, right change, or maintaining lane. It will execute the following actions (checkGlobalLaneChange, left or right evaluation, setStateLaneKeeping) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails for global triggers. It will execute the following actions (checkLeftChange, left feasibility and safety, setStateLaneKeeping or setStateLaneChangeLeft) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails for left change. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for checkLaneChangeableLeft and then run the custom action genPathLaneChangeLeft if changeable. It will execute the following actions (left-lane static object and rear collision checks, setStateLaneKeeping or setStateLaneChangeLeft) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails to decide state after path generation. It will execute the following actions (right feasibility and safety, setStateLaneKeeping or setStateLaneChangeRight) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails for right change. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for checkLaneChangeableRight and then run the custom action genPathLaneChangeRight if changeable. It will execute the following actions (right-lane static object and rear collision checks, setStateLaneKeeping or setStateLaneChangeRight) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails to decide state after right path generation. It will execute the following actions (checkFrontObject, choose lane_keeping or lane_change) in order while checking a condition for each action. It will execute the following actions (inverted irregular front, near-corner or near-goal checks, setStateLaneKeeping) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails for lane-keeping collision handling. It will execute inverted [Action]. If checkIrregularFront succeeds, reverse it to fail. Else if checkIrregularFront fails, reverse it to succeed. It will execute the following actions (checkNearCorner, checkObjectNearGoal, checkMeNearGoal) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails as a fallback for benign proximity. It will execute the following actions (checkProfitLaneChangeLeft, left safety and feasibility, setStateLaneKeeping or setStateLaneChangeLeft) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails for profitable left change. It will execute the following actions (left-lane static object or irregular left, setStateLaneKeeping) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails as a conservative fallback. It will execute the following actions (checkLaneChangeableLeft, genPathLaneChangeLeft, post-check safety, setStateLaneKeeping or setStateLaneChangeLeft) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails for actionable left change. It will execute the following actions (checkProfitLaneChangeRight, right safety and feasibility, setStateLaneKeeping or setStateLaneChangeRight) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails for profitable right change. It will execute the following actions (right-lane static object or irregular right, setStateLaneKeeping) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails as a conservative fallback. It will execute the following actions (checkLaneChangeableRight, genPathLaneChangeRight, post-check safety, setStateLaneKeeping or setStateLaneChangeRight) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails for actionable right change. It will execute the following actions (setStateLaneKeeping) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails as a final stabilization of lane-keeping state. It will execute the following actions (checkMustLaneChange or always succeed) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails to gate mandatory changes without blocking. It will execute the following actions (checkStateLaneChangeLeft, end-or-execute) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails for the LaneChangeLeft branch. It will execute the following actions (checkEndLaneChange, setStateLaneKeeping, callPathGlobal) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails, or run the custom action speedProfileLaneChange. It will execute the following actions (checkStateLaneChangeRight, end-or-execute) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails for the LaneChangeRight branch. It will execute the following actions (checkEndLaneChange, setStateLaneKeeping, callPathGlobal) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails, or run the custom action speedProfileLaneChange. It will execute the following actions (setStateLaneKeeping) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails for the Recovery branch. It will execute the following actions (checkPassAbleTrafficLight, stopTrafficLight) consecutively. If any action fails, move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for traffic light passability before deciding to run the custom action stopTrafficLight. It will execute the following actions (checkUnprotected, checkImpassalbeUnprotected, stop) consecutively. If any action fails, move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, start all over again until an action succeeds. It will run the custom action stop for an unprotected and impassable turn while allowing success otherwise.

Next, it will execute the following actions (setPath, publishData, mission state) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action setPath to apply the generated path. It will run the custom action publishData to output the driving data. It will execute the following actions (arrive sequence, going sequence) consecutively. If any action fails, move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will execute the following actions (checkArriveGoal, setStateMissionArrive, publishStateMission) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails to report arrival. It will execute the following actions (setStateMissionGoing) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails to report ongoing mission when arrival is not met.

mrq:
The mission describes a pursuit-and-engagement task that selects a target, moves toward it, and attacks if adjacent, with a fallback to random movement.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (GetTarget, Move, Sequence(TargetAdjacent, AttackTarget)) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will run the custom action GetTarget to select a target. It will run the custom action Move to advance toward the selected target after the target selection succeeds. It will execute the following actions (TargetAdjacent, AttackTarget) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for TargetAdjacent. It will run the custom action AttackTarget only after the adjacency check succeeds.

Next, it will execute the following actions (SequenceStar(Main), RandomMove) consecutively. If any action (SequenceStar(Main)) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will run the custom action RandomMove if the main sequence does not succeed.

<root main_tree_to_execute="BehaviorTree">

    <BehaviorTree ID="BehaviorTree">
        <Sequence>
            <Sequence>
                <RetryUntilSuccesful num_attempts="">
                    <Action Color="{color}" ID="RecColor" Point="{color_point}"/>
                </RetryUntilSuccesful>
                <Action ID="MoveGoal" TargetPoint="{target_point}"/>
                <ForceSuccess>

                </ForceSuccess>
            </Sequence>
            <Sequence>

                    <Action Hole="{hole_point}" ID="RecHole"/>
                </RetryUntilSuccesful>

                <Action ID="Launch" LauncherAng="{lnchr_ang}"/>
            </Sequence>
        </Sequence>
    </BehaviorTree>

    <TreeNodesModel>
        <Action ID="CalcLuncherAngle">

        </Action>

        </Action>

            <output_port default="{target_point}" name="TargetPoint"/>
        </Action>

mrq: The mission describes a two-stage task that will first detect a target color and move to a target point with a guaranteed wait, and then detect a hole and launch using a computed launcher angle in strict order. First, the robot will execute the following actions (RetryUntilSuccess, MoveGoal, ForceSuccess) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will keep trying to execute the custom action RecColor until it succeeds. It will execute the custom action MoveGoal with the provided target point after color recognition succeeds. It will execute the custom action Wait and make sure it always succeeds regardless of conditions. Next, it will execute the following actions (RetryUntilSuccess, CalcLuncherAngle, Launch) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will keep trying to execute the custom action RecHole until it succeeds. It will execute the custom action CalcLuncherAngle and will output a launcher angle. It will execute the custom action Launch using the computed launcher angle after the angle calculation succeeds.

mrq:
The mission describes a household fetch-and-carry task that will try autonomous behaviors in order and will ask for help only if all autonomous behaviors fail.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (ReachRoom, BottleFound, Fetch) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute the following actions (ShouldFollow, FollowHuman) consecutively. If any action (ShouldFollow) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will execute the following actions (RoomKnown, GoToRoom_Fal) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule. It will execute the following actions (RobotInRoom, GoToRoom) consecutively. If any action (RobotInRoom) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will run the custom action FollowHuman if the prior fallback fails to reach the room.

Next, it will execute the following actions (BottleFound, FindBottle) consecutively. If any action (BottleFound) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule. It will run the custom action FindBottle when the bottle is not yet confirmed.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (BottleGrasped, Grasp, SetInvPoseInvaild) consecutively. If any action (BottleGrasped) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule. It will execute the following actions (InvPoseCompute, GoInvPose, Locate, GraspBottle) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute the following actions (SeqInvPose, ComputeInvPose) consecutively. If any action (SeqInvPose) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will execute the following actions (InvPoseComputed, InvPoseValid) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule. It will run the custom action ComputeInvPose if inverse pose checks do not pass. It will execute the following actions (RobotAtInvPose, prepare) consecutively. If any action (RobotAtInvPose) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule. It will execute the following actions (KeepArmsForGrasp, GoToInvPose) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action KeepArmsForGrasp to set arm posture. It will run the custom action GoToInvPose to move to the computed inverse pose. It will execute the following actions (BottleLocated, LocateBottle) consecutively. If any action (BottleLocated) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule. It will run the custom action LocateBottle to confirm the bottle pose. It will run the custom action GraspBottle to pick up the bottle after successful localization. It will run the custom action SetInvPoseInvaild to clear the stored inverse pose after grasping succeeds.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (Autonomus, AskForHelp) consecutively. If any action (Autonomus) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will run the custom action AskForHelp if all autonomous attempts do not succeed.

<root main_tree_to_execute="MainTree">
  <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
    <RestoreConfig name="Restore_footprint_publisher" node_name="/footprint_publisher">
    <RestoreConfig name="Restore_global_costmap" node_name="/global_costmap/global_costmap">
      <Sequence>
	<!-- initialize blackboard -->
	<SetBlackboard output_key="current_side" value="center"/>
	<SetBlackboard output_key="config_changed" value="false"/>

	<!-- use center for normal, 0.5 makes transition sudden, but small number cause issue
             passing close to obstacles -->
	<ChangeParam node_name="/global_costmap/global_costmap"
		     param_name="navcog_path_layer.walk_weight"
		     param_value="[0.1, 0.0, 0.1]"  name="ChangeParamToCenter"/>

	<ChangeParam node_name="/global_costmap/global_costmap"
		     param_name="navcog_path_layer.path_adjusted_center"
		     param_value="-0.5"  name="ChangeParamPathAdjustedCenter1"/>
	
	<ChangeParam node_name="/planner_server"
		     param_name="NavCog.path_adjusted_center"
		     param_value="-0.5"  name="ChangeParamPathAdjustedCenter2"/>
	
	<!-- default footprint is smallest, so change the footprint to normal -->
	<ChangeParam node_name="/footprint_publisher"
		     param_name="footprint_mode"
		     param_value="0"  name="ChangeParamToNormal"/>

	<!-- compute a path -->
	<RecoveryNode number_of_retries="999999999" name="InitialComputePathRepeat">
	  <Sequence name="ComputePathSequence">
	    <!-- clear costmap -->
	    <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearGlobalCostmap-context" server_timeout="5000"
				service_name="/global_costmap/clear_entirely_global_costmap"/>
	    
	    <WaitFloat wait_duration_float="0.5"/>
	    <CurrentPose global_frame="map" robot_base_frame="base_footprint" pose="{current_pose}" />
	    <ComputePathToPose start="{current_pose}" goal="{goal}" path="{navcog_path}" planner_id="NavCog"/>
	    <PathToPoses path="{navcog_path}" goals="{navcog_goals}" />
	    <PublishTopicPath topic="path_out" value="{navcog_path}"/> <!-- for debug -->
	    <!-- wait long enough to include neighbor obstacles -->
	    <WaitFloat wait_duration_float="0.5"/>
	    <ComputePathThroughPoses goals="{navcog_goals}" path="{path}" planner_id="GridBased"/>
	    <!-- do not remove this plan topic, this is used by ROS1 node -->
	    <PublishTopicPath topic="plan" value="{path}"/>
	    <!-- front vibration -->
	    <PublishTopic topic="/cabot/event" value="navigation;event;navigation_start" />
	  </Sequence>
	  <ForceSuccess>
	    <WaitFloat wait_duration_float="0.5"/>
	  </ForceSuccess>
	</RecoveryNode>

	<!-- main loop -->
	<RecoveryNode number_of_retries="999999999" name="NavigateRecovery">
	  <!-- navigation -->
	  <PipelineSequence name="NavigateWithReplanning">

	    <!-- Check people status frequently -->
	    <RateController hz="2" name="AvoidPeople">
              <Fallback name="AvoidPersonFallback">
		<Sequence>
		  <!-- check if there is someone detecgted -->
		  <PeopleExist people_topic="people" people_out="{people}" />
		  <PeopleStops people="{people}" threshold="0.2" duration="2.0"/>
		  <!-- find people needs to be avoided based on there walking speed and walking direction -->
		  <NeedToAvoid people="{people}" robot_frame="base_footprint" path="{path}" avoid="{avoid}" avoid_count="{avoid_count}"/>

		  <!-- replaced with NeedToAvoid
		       <SomeoneOnPath path="{path}" people="{people}" person_out="{person}" range="0.5"/>
		      <FarEnough odom_topic="odom" max_acc="0.3" delay="0.2" min_threshold="0" max_threshold="7.0"
		      global_frame="map_global" robot_frame="base_footprint" person="{person}"/>
		  -->

		  <!-- let the costmap know who should be removed, who should not be removed -->
		  <IgnorePeople people="{avoid}" ignore_topic="ignore" wait_duration="0.5"/>

		  <CurrentPose global_frame="map" robot_base_frame="base_footprint" pose="{current_pose}" />
		  <ComputePathToPose start="{current_pose}" goal="{goal}" path="{navcog_path}" planner_id="NavCog"/>
		  <PathToPoses path="{navcog_path}" goals="{navcog_goals}" />
		  <PublishTopicPath topic="path_out" value="{navcog_path}"/> <!-- debug -->
		  <ComputePathThroughPoses goals="{navcog_goals}" path="{path}" planner_id="GridBased"/>
		  <!-- do not remove this plan topic, this is used by ROS1 node -->
		  <PublishTopicPath topic="plan" value="{path}"/>

		  <IfThenElse>
		    <BlackboardCheckInt value_A="{avoid_count}" value_B="1" return_on_mismatch="FAILURE">
		      <AlwaysSuccess/>
		    </BlackboardCheckInt>
		    <PublishTopic topic="/cabot/event" value="navigation;event;bt_navigator_avoid_person" />
		    <PublishTopic topic="/cabot/event" value="navigation;event;bt_navigator_avoid_people" />
		  </IfThenElse>
		  
		  <Wait wait_duration="5.0"/>
		  <!-- replaced with CanPassPerson, which publish path for navigation
		      <ComputePathToPose goal="{goal}" path="{path}" planner_id="GridBased"/>
		  -->
		</Sequence>
		<AlwaysSuccess/>
	      </Fallback>
	    </RateController>>

	    <!-- follow path -->
	    <ReactiveFallback>
	      <!-- if the goal is updated, abort following first, this will be recorvered by RecoveryComputePathRepeat -->
	      <GoalUpdated/>
	      <FollowPath path="{path}" controller_id="FollowPath" goal_checker_id="cabot_goal_checker"/>
	    </ReactiveFallback>
	  </PipelineSequence>

	  <!-- if follow path is aborted, compute path and try again -->
	  <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="999999999" name="RecoveryComputePathRepeat">
	    <Sequence name="ComputePathSequence">
	      <!-- clear costmap -->
	      <ClearCostmapAroundRobot name="ClearGlobalCostmapAroundRobot2" server_timeout="5000"
				       service_name="global_costmap/clear_around_global_costmap"
				       reset_distance="20" />
	      <ClearCostmapAroundRobot name="ClearLocalCostmapAroundRobot" server_timeout="5000"
				       service_name="local_costmap/clear_around_local_costmap"
				       reset_distance="20" />
	      
	      <!-- wait long enough to include neighbor obstacles -->
	      <Wait wait_duration="1.0"/>
	      <!-- do not reroute 
	      <ComputePathToPose goal="{goal}" path="{path}" planner_id="GridBased"/>
	      <CheckPath  path="{path_temp}" path_topic="/path" plan_topic="/plan" path_out="{path}" />
	      -->
	    </Sequence>
	    <AlwaysSuccess/>
	  </RecoveryNode>
	</RecoveryNode>
      </Sequence>
    </RestoreConfig>
    </RestoreConfig>
  </BehaviorTree>
</root>
mrq:
The mission describes a navigation task that initializes configuration, computes an initial path with recovery, continuously replans while avoiding people, follows the path reactively, and performs localized recovery when following fails.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (SetBlackboard, SetBlackboard, ChangeParam, ChangeParam, ChangeParam, ChangeParam, RecoveryNode) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will set the blackboard key current_side to center. It will set the blackboard key config_changed to false. It will run the custom action ChangeParamToCenter to set the parameter navcog_path_layer.walk_weight on the node /global_costmap/global_costmap to [0.1, 0.0, 0.1]. It will run the custom action ChangeParamPathAdjustedCenter1 to set the parameter navcog_path_layer.path_adjusted_center on the node /global_costmap/global_costmap to -0.5. It will run the custom action ChangeParamPathAdjustedCenter2 to set the parameter NavCog.path_adjusted_center on the node /planner_server to -0.5. It will run the custom action ChangeParamToNormal to set the parameter footprint_mode on the node /footprint_publisher to 0. It will execute main ComputePathSequence and, if it fails, execute recovery action WaitFloat and then retry main action ComputePathSequence with retries effectively unbounded.

Next, it will execute the following actions (ClearEntireCostmap, WaitFloat, CurrentPose, ComputePathToPose, PathToPoses, PublishTopicPath, WaitFloat, ComputePathThroughPoses, PublishTopicPath, PublishTopic) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the ROS action ClearEntireCostmap to clear the global costmap. It will run the custom action WaitFloat to wait for 0.5 seconds. It will run the ROS action CurrentPose to read the pose in map using base_footprint and store it in current_pose. It will run the ROS action ComputePathToPose using start current_pose and goal to produce navcog_path with planner NavCog. It will run the ROS action PathToPoses to convert navcog_path into navcog_goals. It will run the ROS action PublishTopicPath to publish navcog_path on the topic path_out. It will run the custom action WaitFloat to wait for 0.5 seconds. It will run the ROS action ComputePathThroughPoses using navcog_goals to produce path with planner GridBased. It will run the ROS action PublishTopicPath to publish path on the topic plan. It will run the ROS action PublishTopic to publish navigation;event;navigation_start on /cabot/event.

Next, it will execute the following actions (WaitFloat) and make sure it always succeeds. It will run the custom action WaitFloat to wait for 0.5 seconds and the result will be forced to succeed.

Next, it will execute main NavigateWithReplanning. If it fails, it will execute recovery action RecoveryComputePathRepeat. After recovery, it will retry main action NavigateWithReplanning with retries effectively unbounded.

Next, it will execute the following actions (RateController, ReactiveFallback) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. While an action is running or has succeeded, it will go to the next action.

Next, it will execute the following actions (Fallback) at 2 Hz. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails.

Next, it will execute the following actions (Sequence, AlwaysSuccess) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, it will start all over again until an action succeeds.

Next, it will execute the following actions (PeopleExist, PeopleStops, NeedToAvoid, IgnorePeople, CurrentPose, ComputePathToPose, PathToPoses, PublishTopicPath, ComputePathThroughPoses, PublishTopicPath, IfThenElse, Wait) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the ROS action PeopleExist to detect people on the topic people and store them in people. It will run the ROS action PeopleStops to check if detected people are nearly stationary with threshold 0.2 for 2.0 seconds. It will run the ROS action NeedToAvoid to compute avoid and avoid_count using people, base_footprint, and the current path. It will run the ROS action IgnorePeople to publish the list avoid on the topic ignore with a wait of 0.5 seconds. It will run the ROS action CurrentPose to update current_pose in map using base_footprint. It will run the ROS action ComputePathToPose using start current_pose and goal to produce navcog_path with planner NavCog. It will run the ROS action PathToPoses to convert navcog_path into navcog_goals. It will run the ROS action PublishTopicPath to publish navcog_path on the topic path_out. It will run the ROS action ComputePathThroughPoses using navcog_goals to produce path with planner GridBased. It will run the ROS action PublishTopicPath to publish path on the topic plan. It will read blackboard value and compare it against a set of cases. If a case matches the blackboard value, it will execute corresponding Action. Else if no case matches, it will execute default Action. It will run the ROS action Wait to pause for 5.0 seconds.

Next, it will execute the following actions (BlackboardCheckInt, PublishTopic, PublishTopic) in order while checking a condition for each action. If condition ID is true, it will move to the next action. Else if condition ID is false, it will end the sequence. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule between avoid_count and 1 while returning FAILURE on mismatch to select the else branch. It will run the ROS action PublishTopic to publish navigation;event;bt_navigator_avoid_person on /cabot/event for the then branch. It will run the ROS action PublishTopic to publish navigation;event;bt_navigator_avoid_people on /cabot/event for the else branch.

Next, it will execute the following actions (GoalUpdated, FollowPath) in order. If any action fails, it will stop execution. While any action is still running, it will re-evaluate previous actions. It will run the ROS action GoalUpdated to check for an updated goal and stop the fallback if updated. It will run the ROS action FollowPath using path with controller FollowPath and goal checker cabot_goal_checker.

Finally, it will execute main ComputePathSequence. If it fails, it will execute recovery action AlwaysSuccess. After recovery, it will retry main action ComputePathSequence with retries effectively unbounded.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (ClearCostmapAroundRobot, ClearCostmapAroundRobot, Wait) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the ROS action ClearCostmapAroundRobot to clear the global costmap around the robot within 20 meters using the service global_costmap/clear_around_global_costmap. It will run the ROS action ClearCostmapAroundRobot to clear the local costmap around the robot within 20 meters using the service local_costmap/clear_around_local_costmap. It will run the ROS action Wait to pause for 1.0 seconds.

<root main_tree_to_execute = "MainTree">
    <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
        <Sequence>
            <SetBlackboard output_key="Goal_a" value="1.17;-1.65;30" />
            <SetBlackboard output_key="Goal_b" value="1.1;1.51;90" />
            <SetBlackboard output_key="Goal_c" value="-1.25;0.324;0" />
            <SetBlackboard output_key="Waiting_time" value="2"/>
            <Fallback>
                <ReactiveSequence>
                    <InterruptEvent event="gohome"/>
                    <Repeat num_cycles="-1">
                        <Sequence>
                            <SubTree ID="MoveRobot" target="Goal_a" />
                            <WaitingEvent name="waiting_event" waiting_time="{Waiting_time}"/>
                            <SubTree ID="MoveRobot" target="Goal_b" />
                            <WaitingEvent name="waiting_event" waiting_time="{Waiting_time}"/>
                        </Sequence>
                    </Repeat>
                </ReactiveSequence>
                <Sequence>
                    <SubTree ID="MoveRobot" target="Goal_c" />
                </Sequence>
            </Fallback>
        </Sequence>
    </BehaviorTree>

    <BehaviorTree ID="MoveRobot">
        <Sequence name="SetGoal">
            <MoveBase goal="{target}" />
        </Sequence>
    </BehaviorTree>
</root>
mrq:
The mission describes a patrol-and-interrupt task that sets patrol goals, waits at each goal, reacts to an interrupt, and returns to a home goal when interrupted.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (SetBlackboard, SetBlackboard, SetBlackboard, SetBlackboard, Fallback) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will write the value 1.17;-1.65;30 to the blackboard under the key Goal_a. It will write the value 1.1;1.51;90 to the blackboard under the key Goal_b. It will write the value -1.25;0.324;0 to the blackboard under the key Goal_c. It will write the value 2 to the blackboard under the key Waiting_time. It will execute the following actions (ReactiveSequence, Sequence) consecutively. If any action (ReactiveSequence) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution will fail.

Next, it will execute the following actions [InterruptEvent, Repeat] in sequence. If Repeat fails, it will stop execution. While Repeat is still running, it will re-evaluate previous actions. It will run the custom action InterruptEvent with the event gohome. It will execute Sequence for a set number of times or infinitely. It will execute Sequence infinitely because the repeat count is negative.

Next, it will execute the following actions (SubTree, WaitingEvent, SubTree, WaitingEvent) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. While an action is running or has succeeded, it will go to the next action. It will run the custom action SubTree with ID MoveRobot using the input target Goal_a. It will run the custom action WaitingEvent with the name waiting_event and the waiting_time read from the blackboard key Waiting_time. It will run the custom action SubTree with ID MoveRobot using the input target Goal_b. It will run the custom action WaitingEvent with the name waiting_event and the waiting_time read from the blackboard key Waiting_time.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (SubTree) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action SubTree with ID MoveRobot using the input target Goal_c.

First, it will execute the following actions (MoveBase) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the ROS action MoveBase with the goal provided by the input port target.

<root main_tree_to_execute="MainTree">
  <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
    <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="6" name="NavigateRecovery">
      <PipelineSequence name="NavigateWithReplanning">
        <RateController hz="1.0">
          <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="1" name="ComputePathToPose">
            <ComputePathToPose goal="{goal}" path="{path}" planner_id="GridBased"/>
            <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearGlobalCostmap-Context" service_name="global_costmap/clear_entirely_global_costmap"/>
          </RecoveryNode>
        </RateController>
        <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="1" name="FollowPath">
          <FollowPath path="{path}" controller_id="FollowPath"/>
          <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearLocalCostmap-Context" service_name="local_costmap/clear_entirely_local_costmap"/>
        </RecoveryNode>
      </PipelineSequence>
      <ReactiveFallback name="RecoveryFallback">
        <GoalUpdated/>
        <SequenceStar name="RecoveryActions">
          <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearLocalCostmap-Subtree" service_name="local_costmap/clear_entirely_local_costmap"/>
          <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearGlobalCostmap-Subtree" service_name="global_costmap/clear_entirely_global_costmap"/>
          <Spin spin_dist="1.57"/>
          <Wait wait_duration="5"/>
        </SequenceStar>
      </ReactiveFallback>
    </RecoveryNode>
  </BehaviorTree>
</root>
mrq:
The mission describes a navigation task that periodically replans a global path at 1 Hz, follows the path, and performs layered recoveries when actions fail.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (RateController, RecoveryNode) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute the following actions (RecoveryNode) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails while an action is running or has succeeded, it will go to the next action. It will execute main the custom action ComputePathToPose. If it fails, it will execute recovery action the custom action ClearEntireCostmap-Context. After recovery, it will retry main action the custom action ComputePathToPose. If recovery action the custom action ClearEntireCostmap-Context fails and retries are exhausted, it will stop execution. It will read that the custom action RateController limits execution to 1.0 Hz and thereby triggers replanning once per second.

Next, it will execute the following actions (RecoveryNode) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails while an action is running or has succeeded, it will go to the next action. It will execute main the ROS action FollowPath. If it fails, it will execute recovery action the custom action ClearEntireCostmap-Context. After recovery, it will retry main action the ROS action FollowPath. If recovery action the custom action ClearEntireCostmap-Context fails and retries are exhausted, it will stop execution.

Finally, it will execute main the custom action NavigateWithReplanning. If it fails, it will execute recovery action the custom action RecoveryFallback. After recovery, it will retry main action the custom action NavigateWithReplanning. If recovery action the custom action RecoveryFallback fails and retries are exhausted, it will stop execution after at most six retries.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (GoalUpdated, SequenceStar) in order. If GoalUpdated succeeds, it will stop execution. Else it will keep executing and re-evaluating previous actions whenever an action is running. It will execute the following actions (ClearLocalCostmap-Subtree, ClearGlobalCostmap-Subtree, Spin, Wait) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will execute the custom action ClearLocalCostmap-Subtree to clear the local costmap. It will execute the custom action ClearGlobalCostmap-Subtree to clear the global costmap. It will execute the custom action Spin to rotate in place by 1.57 radians. It will execute the custom action Wait to pause for 5 seconds.

<root main_tree_to_execute="QueueTree">
  <BehaviorTree ID="QueueTree">
    <RestoreConfig node_name="/footprint_publisher">
      <RestoreConfig node_name="/controller_server">
	<RestoreConfig node_name="/global_costmap/global_costmap">
	  <Sequence>
	    <!-- set the footprint as small -->
	    <ChangeParam node_name="/footprint_publisher"
			 param_name="footprint_mode"
			 param_value="3"  name="ChangeParamFootprintToSmall"/>
	    
	    <ChangeParam node_name="/controller_server"
			 param_name="FollowPath.max_vel_x"
			 param_value="0.5"  name="ChangeParamSpeedDown"/>
	    
	    <ChangeParam node_name="/controller_server"
			 param_name="cabot_goal_checker.xy_goal_tolerance"
			 param_value="0.20"  name="ChangeParamFollowPathXyGoalTolerance"/>
	    <ChangeParam node_name="/controller_server"
			 param_name="cabot_goal_checker.yaw_goal_tolerance"
			 param_value="0.20"  name="ChangeParamFollowPathYawGoalTolerance"/>
	    
	    <ChangeParam node_name="/global_costmap/global_costmap"
			 param_name="navcog_path_layer.walk_weight"
			 param_value="[0.3, 0.0, 0.3] "  name="ChangeParamWalkWeightToCenter"/>
	    <!-- 1: use adjust mode to use actual center of the corridor based on costmap
		 0: use navcog path as the ceter of the corridor -->
	    <ChangeParam node_name="/global_costmap/global_costmap"
			 param_name="navcog_path_layer.path_mode"
			 param_value="0"  name="ChangeParamToAdjust"/>
	    <ChangeParam node_name="/global_costmap/global_costmap"
			 param_name="navcog_path_layer.path_adjusted_center"
			 param_value="0.0"  name="ChangeParamPathAdjustedCenter"/>
	    
	    <ChangeParam node_name="/global_costmap/global_costmap"
			 param_name="inflation_layer.inflation_radius"
			 param_value="0.2"  name="ChangeParamInflationRadius"/>
	    
	    <!-- compute a path -->
	    <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="999999999" name="InitialComputePathRepeat">
	      <Sequence name="ComputePathSequence">
		<!-- clear costmap -->
		<!--
		<ClearCostmapAroundRobot name="ClearCostmapAroundRobot" server_timeout="5000"
					 service_name="global_costmap/clear_around_global_costmap"
					 window_size_x="20" window_size_y="20"/>
		-->
		<ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearGlobalCostmap-context" server_timeout="5000"
				    service_name="global_costmap/clear_entirely_global_costmap"/>
		
		<!-- wait long enough to include neighbor obstacles -->
		<WaitFloat wait_duration_float="0.5"/>
		<ComputePathToPose goal="{goal}" path="{path_temp}" planner_id="GridBased"/>
		<CheckPath  path="{path_temp}" path_topic="/path" plan_topic="/plan" path_out="{path}" />
		<!-- front vibration -->
		<!--
		<PublishTopicInt8 topic="/cabot/notification" value="5" />
		-->
	      </Sequence>
	      <ForceSuccess>
		<WaitFloat wait_duration_float="0.5"/>
	      </ForceSuccess>
	    </RecoveryNode>
	    
	    <!-- main loop -->
	    <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="999999999" name="NavigateRecovery">
	      <!-- navigation -->
	      <PipelineSequence name="NavigateWithReplanning">
		<!-- Check people status frequently -->
		<!-- find people needs to be avoided not in queue -->
		<!--
		<RateController hz="2" name="AvoidPeople">
		  <Fallback name="AvoidPersonFallback">
		    <Sequence name="AvoidPerson">
		      <PeopleExist people_topic="people" people_out="{people}"/>
		      <NeedToAvoid people="{people}" robot_frame="base_footprint" path="{path}" avoid="{people_avoid}"/>
		      <SomeoneNotInQueue queue_topic="queue_people_py/queue" people="{people_avoid}" people_out_not_queue="{people_avoid_not_queue}"/>
		      <IgnorePeople people="{people_avoid_not_queue}" ignore_topic="ignore" wait_duration="0.5"/>
		      <ComputePathToPose goal="{goal}" path="{path_temp}" planner_id="GridBased"/>
		      <CheckPath current="{path}" path="{path_temp}" path_topic="/path" plan_topic="/plan" path_out="{path}" />
		      <Wait wait_duration="5"/>
		    </Sequence>
		    <AlwaysSuccess/>
		  </Fallback>
		</RateController>
		-->
		<RateController hz="2.0" name="UpdatePath">
		  <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="1" name="UpdatePathRecovery">
		    <Sequence>
		      <ClearEntireCostmap name="UpdatePathClearGlobalCostmap-context" server_timeout="5000"
					  service_name="global_costmap/clear_entirely_global_costmap"/>
		      <WaitFloat wait_duration_float="0.5"/>
		      <ComputePathToPose goal="{goal}" path="{path_temp}" planner_id="GridBased"/>
		      <CheckPath  path="{path_temp}" path_topic="/path" plan_topic="/plan" path_out="{path}" />
		    </Sequence>
		    <ForceSuccess>
		      <WaitFloat wait_duration_float="0.5"/>
		    </ForceSuccess>
		  </RecoveryNode>
		</RateController>
		
		<!-- follow path -->
		<RecoveryNode number_of_retries="1" name="FollowPathRecovery">
		  <FollowPath path="{path}" controller_id="FollowPath" />
		  <AlwaysSuccess/>
		</RecoveryNode>
	      </PipelineSequence>
	      
	      <!-- recovery -->
	      <SequenceStar name="RecoveryActions">
		<ClearEntireCostmap name="Recovery-ClearGlobalCostmap-context" server_timeout="5000"
                                    service_name="/global_costmap/clear_entirely_global_costmap"/>
		<ClearEntireCostmap name="Recovery-ClearLocalCostmap-Context" server_timeout="5000"
				    service_name="/local_costmap/clear_entirely_local_costmap"/>
		<Wait wait_duration="1.0"/>
	      </SequenceStar>
	    </RecoveryNode>
	  </Sequence>
	</RestoreConfig>
      </RestoreConfig>
    </RestoreConfig>
  </BehaviorTree>
</root>
mrq:
The mission describes a queued navigation task that reconfigures parameters, computes a valid global path, continuously replans at 2 Hz, follows the path, and applies layered recoveries when navigation degrades.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (ChangeParamFootprintToSmall, ChangeParamSpeedDown, ChangeParamFollowPathXyGoalTolerance, ChangeParamFollowPathYawGoalTolerance, ChangeParamWalkWeightToCenter, ChangeParamToAdjust, ChangeParamPathAdjustedCenter, ChangeParamInflationRadius, InitialComputePathRepeat, NavigateRecovery) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action ChangeParamFootprintToSmall to make the footprint publisher use a small footprint mode. It will run the custom action ChangeParamSpeedDown to set the maximum forward speed on the controller to 0.5 meters per second. It will run the custom action ChangeParamFollowPathXyGoalTolerance to set the XY goal tolerance to 0.20. It will run the custom action ChangeParamFollowPathYawGoalTolerance to set the yaw goal tolerance to 0.20. It will run the custom action ChangeParamWalkWeightToCenter to bias the path layer walk weights toward center alignment. It will run the custom action ChangeParamToAdjust to select the path mode that uses the NavCog path as the center reference. It will run the custom action ChangeParamPathAdjustedCenter to set the adjusted center offset to 0.0. It will run the custom action ChangeParamInflationRadius to set the global inflation radius to 0.2 meters. It will run the custom action InitialComputePathRepeat to obtain an initial valid path with recovery and enforced waits. It will run the custom action NavigateRecovery to perform navigation with periodic replanning and recovery sequences.

Next, it will execute main Execute the following actions (ComputePathSequence, ForceSuccess) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails while using a recovery strategy. It will execute main ComputePathSequence and, if it fails, execute recovery action ForceSuccess and then retry main action ComputePathSequence until retries are exhausted. It will execute the following actions (ClearGlobalCostmap-context, WaitFloat, ComputePathToPose, CheckPath) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action ClearGlobalCostmap-context to clear the entire global costmap before planning. It will run the custom action WaitFloat to wait 0.5 seconds to allow new obstacle data to stabilize. It will run the ROS action ComputePathToPose using the given goal and will store the resulting path in a temporary buffer. It will run the custom action CheckPath to validate and publish the computed path and will store the validated path.

Next, it will execute main Execute the following actions (NavigateWithReplanning, RecoveryActions) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails while using a recovery strategy. It will execute main NavigateWithReplanning and, if it fails, execute recovery action RecoveryActions and then retry main action NavigateWithReplanning until retries are exhausted. It will execute the following actions (UpdatePath, FollowPathRecovery) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails and it will advance on running or success. It will run the custom action UpdatePath at a rate of 2.0 Hz. It will execute main Execute the following actions (UpdatePathClearGlobalCostmap-context, WaitFloat, ComputePathToPose, CheckPath) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails while using a recovery strategy with one retry. It will execute main UpdatePathClearGlobalCostmap-context and, if it fails, execute recovery action WaitFloat and then retry main action UpdatePathClearGlobalCostmap-context until retries are exhausted. It will run the custom action UpdatePathClearGlobalCostmap-context to clear the entire global costmap for replanning. It will run the custom action WaitFloat to wait 0.5 seconds to collect recent obstacle data. It will run the ROS action ComputePathToPose to compute a refreshed path to the goal and will store the result in a temporary buffer. It will run the custom action CheckPath to validate and publish the refreshed path and will store the validated path. It will run the ROS action FollowPath to track the most recently validated path using the configured controller while guarded by a recovery wrapper with one retry. It will run the custom action AlwaysSuccess to ensure the follow-path recovery branch always returns success.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (Recovery-ClearGlobalCostmap-context, Recovery-ClearLocalCostmap-Context, Wait) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will run the custom action Recovery-ClearGlobalCostmap-context to clear the entire global costmap. It will run the custom action Recovery-ClearLocalCostmap-Context to clear the entire local costmap. It will run the custom action Wait to pause for 1.0 seconds to allow maps to repopulate.

BT:
<root main_tree_to_execute="MainTree">
	<BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
		<Repeat num_cycles="10">
			<Sequence>
				<Delay delay_msec="1000">
					<PoseDetectionControlAction enable="true" />
				</Delay>
				<TrackAction mode="Track" rate="3" detect_voice="0" turn_base="0" />

				<!-- Spin a bit to get localized in the case the initial pose was just set -->
				<RobotSpin angle="-180" velocity="0.4" />
				<RobotSpin angle="180" velocity="0.4" />

				<RobotFindInitAction game_file="robot_find.json" object_type="shapes" random_order="0" needs_init="{needs_init}" robot_pose="{robot_pose}" />

				<!--
				<ForceSuccess>
					<Sequence>
						<BlackboardCheckInt value_A="{needs_init}" value_B="0" return_on_mismatch="FAILURE">
							<Sequence>
								<TextToSpeechActionClient msg="Do you want to setup the game?" />
								<SpeechToTextActionClient text="{said}" />
								<TextCompareAction text="{said}" ck_for=".*(yes|yeah|okay|ok|sure|yep).*" />
								<SetBlackboard output_key="needs_init" value="1" />
							</Sequence>
  					</BlackboardCheckInt>
					</Sequence>
				</ForceSuccess>
        -->

				<Fallback>
					<BlackboardCheckInt value_A="{needs_init}" value_B="1" return_on_mismatch="FAILURE">
						<Sequence>
							<TrackAction mode="Track" rate="3" detect_voice="0" turn_base="1" />
							<TextToSpeechActionClient msg="Game setup. I will ask you to stand at each of the locations.  Put both hands on your hips when you arrive at each location to continue." />
							<ForceSuccess>
								<Sequence>
									<TextToSpeechActionClient msg="Please send in the middle of the game. After the robot aligns to you, put both hands on your hips." />
									<RetryUntilSuccesful num_attempts="20">
										<Delay delay_msec="2000">
											<HumanPoseDetect expected_pose_left="OnHip" expected_pose_right="OnHip" pose_lr_check="both" detected_person="{detected_person}" pose_left_speech="{pose_left_speech}" pose_right_speech="{pose_right_speech}" />
										</Delay>
									</RetryUntilSuccesful>
									<TrackAction mode="Track" rate="3" detect_voice="0" turn_base="0" />
								</Sequence>
							</ForceSuccess>

							<ForceSuccess>
								<KeepRunningUntilFailure>
									<Sequence>
										<RobotFindNextStepAction item_name="{item_name}" />
										<TextToSpeechActionClient msg="Please stand on " msg2="{item_name}" />
										<RetryUntilSuccesful num_attempts="20">
											<Delay delay_msec="2000">
												<Sequence>
													<HumanPoseDetect expected_pose_left="OnHip" expected_pose_right="OnHip" pose_lr_check="both" detected_person="{detected_person}" pose_left_speech="{pose_left_speech}" pose_right_speech="{pose_right_speech}" />
													<ObjectLocationStatusAction x="{x}" y="{y}" />
													<RobotFindSetPositionAction x="{x}" y="{y}" />
												</Sequence>
											</Delay>
										</RetryUntilSuccesful>
									</Sequence>
								</KeepRunningUntilFailure>
							</ForceSuccess>
							<TextToSpeechActionClient msg="Finished setting up" />
							<TrackAction mode="Track" rate="3" detect_voice="0" turn_base="0" />
						</Sequence>
					</BlackboardCheckInt>

					<Sequence>
						<Nav2Client goal="{robot_pose}" />
						<TextToSpeechActionClient msg="Let's play the game!" />

						<TrackAction mode="Track" rate="3" detect_voice="0" turn_base="0" />
						<ForceSuccess>
							<KeepRunningUntilFailure>
								<Sequence>
									<RobotFindNextStepAction item_name="{item_name}" />
									<TextToSpeechActionClient msg="Please stand on " msg2="{item_name}" />
									<RetryUntilSuccesful num_attempts="200">
										<Delay delay_msec="1000">
											<Sequence>
												<ObjectLocationStatusAction x="{x}" y="{y}" />
												<RobotFindCheckStepAction x="{x}" y="{y}" />
												<TextToSpeechActionClient msg="Good job" />
											</Sequence>
										</Delay>
									</RetryUntilSuccesful>
								</Sequence>
							</KeepRunningUntilFailure>
						</ForceSuccess>
						<TextToSpeechActionClient msg="Finished playing the game" />
						<TrackAction mode="Track" rate="3" detect_voice="0" turn_base="0" />
					</Sequence>
				</Fallback>
			</Sequence>
		</Repeat>
	</BehaviorTree>
</root>
mrq:
The mission describes a repeated interactive game setup and play routine that aligns to a person, initializes a game board, and then runs game steps with tracking and feedback.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (PoseDetectionControlAction, TrackAction, RobotSpin, RobotSpin, RobotFindInitAction, Fallback) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute delayed PoseDetectionControlAction and, if execution succeeds, start a 1000 millisecond delay timer and resume execution after the delay period is exhausted. It will run the custom action TrackAction in Track mode at rate 3 with voice detection disabled and base turning disabled. It will run the custom action RobotSpin to rotate negative 180 degrees at velocity 0.4. It will run the custom action RobotSpin to rotate positive 180 degrees at velocity 0.4. It will run the custom action RobotFindInitAction using the provided game file and object type, producing needs_init and robot_pose on the blackboard. It will execute the following actions (BlackboardCheckInt, Sequence) consecutively and, if any action fails, move to the next action until an action succeeds and, if no action succeeds, the execution fails.

Next, it will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for needs_init equals 1 and, if the check fails, the node will return FAILURE and the Fallback will move to the next action. It will execute the following actions (TrackAction, TextToSpeechActionClient, ForceSuccess, ForceSuccess, TextToSpeechActionClient, TrackAction) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action TrackAction in Track mode at rate 3 with voice detection disabled and base turning enabled. It will run the ROS action TextToSpeechActionClient to announce the setup instructions. It will execute a child action and make sure it always succeeds. It will execute the following actions (TextToSpeechActionClient, RetryUntilSuccesful, TrackAction) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the ROS action TextToSpeechActionClient to request the user to stand in the middle and place both hands on hips after alignment. It will keep trying to execute delayed HumanPoseDetect until it succeeds and, if try succeeds, stop retrying and continue and, if try fails, keep retrying until it succeeds. It will execute delayed HumanPoseDetect and, if execution succeeds, start a 2000 millisecond delay timer and resume execution after the delay period is exhausted. It will run the custom action HumanPoseDetect to detect both hands on hips and write detected_person, pose_left_speech, and pose_right_speech. It will run the custom action TrackAction in Track mode at rate 3 with voice detection disabled and base turning disabled. It will execute a child action and make sure it always succeeds. It will execute the custom action KeepRunningUntilFailure. It will execute the following actions (RobotFindNextStepAction, TextToSpeechActionClient, RetryUntilSuccesful) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails and, if any action succeeds, restart it, and if any action fails, stop looping and continue execution. It will run the custom action RobotFindNextStepAction to select the next item_name. It will run the ROS action TextToSpeechActionClient to instruct the user to stand on the named item. It will keep trying to execute delayed Sequence until it succeeds and, if try succeeds, stop retrying and continue and, if try fails, keep retrying until it succeeds. It will execute delayed Sequence and, if execution succeeds, start a 2000 millisecond delay timer and resume execution after the delay period is exhausted. It will execute the following actions (HumanPoseDetect, ObjectLocationStatusAction, RobotFindSetPositionAction) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action HumanPoseDetect to verify both hands on hips and write pose and person outputs. It will run the custom action ObjectLocationStatusAction to get the location of the object and write x and y. It will run the custom action RobotFindSetPositionAction to record the confirmed position x and y. It will run the ROS action TextToSpeechActionClient to announce that setup is finished. It will run the custom action TrackAction in Track mode at rate 3 with voice detection disabled and base turning disabled.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (Nav2Client, TextToSpeechActionClient, TrackAction, ForceSuccess, TextToSpeechActionClient, TrackAction) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the ROS action Nav2Client to navigate to robot_pose. It will run the ROS action TextToSpeechActionClient to announce the start of the game. It will run the custom action TrackAction in Track mode at rate 3 with voice detection disabled and base turning disabled. It will execute a child action and make sure it always succeeds. It will execute the custom action KeepRunningUntilFailure. It will execute the following actions (RobotFindNextStepAction, TextToSpeechActionClient, RetryUntilSuccesful) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails and, if any action succeeds, restart it, and if any action fails, stop looping and continue execution. It will run the custom action RobotFindNextStepAction to select the next item_name. It will run the ROS action TextToSpeechActionClient to instruct the user to stand on the named item. It will keep trying to execute delayed Sequence until it succeeds and, if try succeeds, stop retrying and continue and, if try fails, keep retrying until it succeeds. It will execute delayed Sequence and, if execution succeeds, start a 1000 millisecond delay timer and resume execution after the delay period is exhausted. It will execute the following actions (ObjectLocationStatusAction, RobotFindCheckStepAction, TextToSpeechActionClient) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action ObjectLocationStatusAction to read the current x and y of the object. It will run the custom action RobotFindCheckStepAction to verify that the correct location has been reached. It will run the ROS action TextToSpeechActionClient to provide positive feedback. It will run the ROS action TextToSpeechActionClient to announce the end of the game. It will run the custom action TrackAction in Track mode at rate 3 with voice detection disabled and base turning disabled. It will execute the preceding Repeat for a set number of times or infinitely by executing the entire child behavior ten times.

The mission describes a patrol-and-recharge task that cycles through waypoints and falls back to recharging when patrolling cannot proceed.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (Repeat) for a set number of times or infinitely. It will execute the action Repeat for 100 cycles.

Next, it will execute the following actions (Fallback) consecutively. If any action (Sequence patrolling) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails.

Next, it will execute the following actions (Sequence patrolling) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action NavigateToWp with the goal wp_1. It will run the custom action NavigateToWp with the goal wp_2 after the previous navigation succeeds. It will run the custom action NavigateToWp with the goal wp_3 after the previous navigation succeeds. It will run the custom action NavigateToWp with the goal wp_4 after the previous navigation succeeds. It will run the custom action NavigateToWp with the goal wp_5 after the previous navigation succeeds. It will run the custom action NavigateToWp with the goal wp_6 after the previous navigation succeeds. It will run the custom action NavigateToWp with the goal wp_7 after the previous navigation succeeds.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (Sequence recharging) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action NavigateToWp with the goal recharge_station. It will run the custom action Recharge after the navigation to the recharge station succeeds.
