mrq:
The mission describes a charging routine that starts with a vocal notification, waits briefly, navigates to a charging point, and then charges while monitoring completion.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (Speak, WaitForXSeconds, Fallback) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will execute the custom action Speak using the Portuguese voice and will speak the utterance “Tenho fome”. It will execute the custom action WaitForXSeconds and will wait for 2 seconds. It will execute the following actions (SequenceStar) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution will fail.

Next, it will execute the following actions (RetryUntilSuccesful, Parallel) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will execute the custom action MoveBase to navigate in the map frame to the specified pose. It will keep trying to execute MoveBase until it succeeds. If a try succeeds, it will stop retrying and continue. If a try fails, it will keep retrying until it succeeds or attempts are exhausted. It will execute the following actions (SequenceStar, SequenceStar) simultaneously. If either a success threshold is reached or all actions succeed, the execution will be successful. Else if any action fails, the execution will fail.

Next, it will execute the following actions (SetBlackboard, Charge, SetBlackboard) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will find a value on the blackboard and will set charged to 0. It will execute the custom action Charge using the charging_action interface. It will find a value on the blackboard and will set charged to 1.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (WaitForXSeconds, ReactiveFallback) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will execute the custom action WaitForXSeconds and will wait for 45 seconds. It will execute the following actions (CompareInt, RetryUntilSuccesful) in order. If any action succeeds, it will stop execution. Else it will keep executing and re-evaluating previous actions whenever an action is running.

Finally, it will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule. It will compare charged against 1 and will succeed only when charged equals 1. It will keep trying to execute the following action until it succeeds. If a try succeeds, it will stop retrying and continue. If a try fails, it will keep retrying until it succeeds. It will execute the following action and make sure it always fails. It will execute the following actions (Speak, WaitForXSeconds) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute the custom action Speak using the Portuguese voice and will speak the utterance “bip bip”. It will execute the custom action WaitForXSeconds and will wait for 2 seconds.

mrq: The mission describes a gaze task that identifies a face from a provided list and directs attention to it in strict order. First, the robot will execute the following actions (GetPoseArray, SelectPose, GazeAtTarget) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action GetPoseArray to read poses from the topic faces and will store the resulting pose array as face_array. It will run the custom action SelectPose to select the pose at index 0 from the stored face_array and will store the selected pose as face. It will run the custom action GazeAtTarget to command the gaze action_name gaze to fixate on the stored pose face in the frame base_footprint after the pose selection succeeds.

<?xml version="1.0"?>
<root main_tree_to_execute="BehaviorTree">
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="BehaviorTree">
        <Repeat num_cycles="{num_cycles}">
            <Sequence>
                <Fallback>
                    <Condition ID="LTLPreCheck" ltl_state_current="{ltl_state_current}" ltl_state_desired_sequence="{ltl_state_desired_sequence}" ltl_state_executed_sequence="{ltl_state_executed_sequence}"/>
                    <ForceFailure>
                        <Action ID="ReplanningRequestLevel2" ltl_state_current="{ltl_state_current}" ltl_state_executed_sequence="{ltl_state_executed_sequence}" replanning_request="{replanning_request}"/>
                    </ForceFailure>
                </Fallback>
                <ReactiveSequence>
                    <Fallback>
                        <Condition ID="ReactiveLTLStateCheck" ltl_state_current="{ltl_state_current}" ltl_state_desired_sequence="{ltl_state_desired_sequence}"/>
                        <ForceFailure>
                            <Action ID="ReplanningRequestLevel2" ltl_state_current="{ltl_state_current}" ltl_state_executed_sequence="{ltl_state_executed_sequence}" replanning_request="{replanning_request}"/>
                        </ForceFailure>
                    </Fallback>
                    <Fallback>
                        <Switch4 case_1="move" case_2="stay" case_3="pick" case_4="drop" variable="{bt_action_type}">
                            <Action ID="MoveAction" bt_action_type="{bt_action_type}" current_action="{current_action}" goal_sent="{goal_sent}" move_base_finished="{move_base_finished}" move_base_idle="{move_base_idle}"/>
                            <Action ID="StayAction" bt_action_type="{bt_action_type}" current_action="{current_action}" ltl_state_current="{ltl_state_current}"/>
                            <Action ID="PickAction" bt_action_type="{bt_action_type}" current_action="{current_action}" ltl_state_current="{ltl_state_current}"/>
                            <Action ID="DropAction" bt_action_type="{bt_action_type}" current_action="{current_action}" ltl_state_current="{ltl_state_current}"/>
                            <AlwaysFailure/>
                        </Switch4>
                        <ForceFailure>
                            <Action ID="ReplanningRequestLevel3" ltl_state_current="{ltl_state_current}" ltl_state_executed_sequence="{ltl_state_executed_sequence}" replanning_request="{replanning_request}"/>
                        </ForceFailure>
                    </Fallback>
                </ReactiveSequence>
                <Action ID="UpdateLTL" action_sequence="{action_sequence}" action_sequence_executed="{action_sequence_executed}" bt_action_type="{bt_action_type}" current_action="{current_action}" ltl_state_desired_sequence="{ltl_state_desired_sequence}"/>
            </Sequence>
        </Repeat>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <TreeNodesModel>
        <Action ID="DropAction">
            <input_port name="bt_action_type"/>
            <input_port name="current_action"/>
            <inout_port name="ltl_state_current"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="GuideAction">
            <input_port name="bt_action_type"/>
            <input_port name="current_action"/>
            <inout_port name="ltl_state_current"/>
        </Action>
        <Condition ID="LTLPreCheck">
            <input_port name="ltl_state_current"/>
            <input_port name="ltl_state_desired_sequence"/>
            <inout_port name="ltl_state_executed_sequence"/>
        </Condition>
        <Action ID="LocomotionStart">
            <input_port name="locomotion_status"/>
        </Action>
        <Condition ID="LocomotionStatusCheck">
            <input_port name="locomotion_status"/>
        </Condition>
        <Action ID="MoveAction">
            <input_port name="bt_action_type"/>
            <input_port name="current_action"/>
            <output_port name="goal_sent"/>
            <input_port name="move_base_finished"/>
            <input_port name="move_base_idle"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="PickAction">
            <input_port name="bt_action_type"/>
            <input_port name="current_action"/>
            <inout_port name="ltl_state_current"/>
        </Action>
        <Condition ID="ReactiveLTLStateCheck">
            <input_port name="ltl_state_current"/>
            <input_port name="ltl_state_desired_sequence"/>
        </Condition>
        <Action ID="RecoveryStand">
            <input_port name="locomotion_status"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="ReplanningRequestLevel1">
            <input_port name="ltl_state_current"/>
            <inout_port name="ltl_state_executed_sequence"/>
            <inout_port name="replanning_request"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="ReplanningRequestLevel2">
            <input_port name="ltl_state_current"/>
            <inout_port name="ltl_state_executed_sequence"/>
            <inout_port name="replanning_request"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="ReplanningRequestLevel3">
            <input_port name="ltl_state_current"/>
            <inout_port name="ltl_state_executed_sequence"/>
            <inout_port name="replanning_request"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="StayAction">
            <input_port name="bt_action_type"/>
            <input_port name="current_action"/>
            <inout_port name="ltl_state_current"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="SynchronizedTransitionAction">
            <input_port name="bt_action_type"/>
            <input_port name="current_action"/>
            <inout_port name="ltl_state_current"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="UpdateLTL">
            <inout_port name="action_sequence"/>
            <inout_port name="action_sequence_executed"/>
            <output_port name="bt_action_type"/>
            <output_port name="current_action"/>
            <inout_port name="ltl_state_desired_sequence"/>
        </Action>
    </TreeNodesModel>
    <!-- ////////// -->
</root>
mrq:
The mission describes a repeated task that verifies temporal logic preconditions, reacts to state changes while executing domain actions, and updates the temporal logic state each cycle.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (Fallback, ReactiveSequence, UpdateLTL) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute the following actions (LTLPreCheck, ForceFailure(ReplanningRequestLevel2)) consecutively. If any action (LTLPreCheck) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will execute ReplanningRequestLevel2 and make sure it always fails.

Next, it will execute the following actions (Fallback, Fallback) in sequence. If any action fails, it will stop execution. While an action is still running, it will re-evaluate previous actions. It will execute the following actions (ReactiveLTLStateCheck, ForceFailure(ReplanningRequestLevel2)) consecutively. If any action (ReactiveLTLStateCheck) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will execute ReplanningRequestLevel2 and make sure it always fails. It will execute the following actions (Switch4, ForceFailure(ReplanningRequestLevel3)) consecutively. If any action (Switch4) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will read blackboard value and compare it against a set of cases. If a case matches the blackboard value, it will execute corresponding Action. Else if no case matches, it will execute default Action. It will run the custom action MoveAction when the case move matches. It will run the custom action StayAction when the case stay matches. It will run the custom action PickAction when the case pick matches. It will run the custom action DropAction when the case drop matches. It will execute ReplanningRequestLevel3 and make sure it always fails.

Finally, it will run the custom action UpdateLTL to write the next bt_action_type and current_action and to synchronize the desired sequence with the executed sequence.

The mission describes that the entire sequence will execute for a set number of times. It will execute Sequence for a set number of times or infinitely.

BT:
<?xml version="1.0"?>
<root main_tree_to_execute="BehaviorTree">
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="BehaviorTree">
        <Repeat num_cycles="{num_cycles}">
            <Sequence>
                <Fallback>
                    <Condition ID="LTLPreCheck" ltl_state_current="{ltl_state_current}" ltl_state_desired_sequence="{ltl_state_desired_sequence}" ltl_state_executed_sequence="{ltl_state_executed_sequence}"/>
                    <ForceFailure>
                        <Action ID="ReplanningRequestLevel2" ltl_state_current="{ltl_state_current}" ltl_state_executed_sequence="{ltl_state_executed_sequence}" replanning_request="{replanning_request}"/>
                    </ForceFailure>
                </Fallback>
                <ReactiveSequence>
                    <Fallback>
                        <Action ID="FakeDetectionLevel1" replanning_fake_input="{replanning_fake_input}"/>
                        <ForceFailure>
                            <Action ID="ReplanningRequestLevel1" ltl_state_current="{ltl_state_current}" ltl_state_executed_sequence="{ltl_state_executed_sequence}" replanning_request="{replanning_request}"/>
                        </ForceFailure>
                    </Fallback>
                    <Fallback>
                        <Condition ID="ReactiveLTLStateCheck" ltl_state_current="{ltl_state_current}" ltl_state_desired_sequence="{ltl_state_desired_sequence}"/>
                        <ForceFailure>
                            <Action ID="ReplanningRequestLevel2" ltl_state_current="{ltl_state_current}" ltl_state_executed_sequence="{ltl_state_executed_sequence}" replanning_request="{replanning_request}"/>
                        </ForceFailure>
                    </Fallback>
                    <Fallback>
                        <Action ID="FakeDetectionLevel3" replanning_fake_input="{replanning_fake_input}"/>
                        <ForceFailure>
                            <Action ID="ReplanningRequestLevel3" ltl_state_current="{ltl_state_current}" ltl_state_executed_sequence="{ltl_state_executed_sequence}" replanning_request="{replanning_request}"/>
                        </ForceFailure>
                    </Fallback>
                    <Fallback>
                        <Switch4 case_1="move" case_2="stay" case_3="pick" case_4="drop" variable="{bt_action_type}">
                            <Action ID="MoveAction" bt_action_type="{bt_action_type}" current_action="{current_action}" goal_sent="{goal_sent}" move_base_finished="{move_base_finished}" move_base_idle="{move_base_idle}"/>
                            <Action ID="StayAction" bt_action_type="{bt_action_type}" current_action="{current_action}" ltl_state_current="{ltl_state_current}"/>
                            <Action ID="PickAction" bt_action_type="{bt_action_type}" current_action="{current_action}" ltl_state_current="{ltl_state_current}"/>
                            <Action ID="DropAction" bt_action_type="{bt_action_type}" current_action="{current_action}" ltl_state_current="{ltl_state_current}"/>
                            <AlwaysFailure/>
                        </Switch4>
                        <ForceFailure>
                            <Action ID="ReplanningRequestLevel3" ltl_state_current="{ltl_state_current}" ltl_state_executed_sequence="{ltl_state_executed_sequence}" replanning_request="{replanning_request}"/>
                        </ForceFailure>
                    </Fallback>
                </ReactiveSequence>
                <Action ID="UpdateLTL" action_sequence="{action_sequence}" action_sequence_executed="{action_sequence_executed}" bt_action_type="{bt_action_type}" current_action="{current_action}" ltl_state_desired_sequence="{ltl_state_desired_sequence}"/>
            </Sequence>
        </Repeat>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <TreeNodesModel>
        <Action ID="BackNormalAction">
            <input_port name="bt_action_type"/>
            <input_port name="current_action"/>
            <inout_port name="ltl_state_current"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="DropAction">
            <input_port name="bt_action_type"/>
            <input_port name="current_action"/>
            <inout_port name="ltl_state_current"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="FakeDetectionLevel1">
            <input_port name="replanning_fake_input"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="FakeDetectionLevel2">
            <input_port name="replanning_fake_input"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="FakeDetectionLevel3">
            <input_port name="replanning_fake_input"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="GuideAction">
            <input_port name="bt_action_type"/>
            <input_port name="current_action"/>
            <inout_port name="ltl_state_current"/>
        </Action>
        <Condition ID="LTLPreCheck">
            <input_port name="ltl_state_current"/>
            <input_port name="ltl_state_desired_sequence"/>
            <inout_port name="ltl_state_executed_sequence"/>
        </Condition>
        <Action ID="LocomotionStart">
            <input_port name="locomotion_status"/>
        </Action>
        <Condition ID="LocomotionStatusCheck">
            <input_port name="locomotion_status"/>
        </Condition>
        <Action ID="MoveAction">
            <input_port name="bt_action_type"/>
            <input_port name="current_action"/>
            <output_port name="goal_sent"/>
            <input_port name="move_base_finished"/>
            <input_port name="move_base_idle"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="PickAction">
            <input_port name="bt_action_type"/>
            <input_port name="current_action"/>
            <inout_port name="ltl_state_current"/>
        </Action>
        <Condition ID="ReactiveLTLStateCheck">
            <input_port name="ltl_state_current"/>
            <input_port name="ltl_state_desired_sequence"/>
        </Condition>
        <Action ID="RecoveryStand">
            <input_port name="locomotion_status"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="ReplanningRequestLevel1">
            <input_port name="ltl_state_current"/>
            <inout_port name="ltl_state_executed_sequence"/>
            <inout_port name="replanning_request"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="ReplanningRequestLevel2">
            <input_port name="ltl_state_current"/>
            <inout_port name="ltl_state_executed_sequence"/>
            <inout_port name="replanning_request"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="ReplanningRequestLevel3">
            <input_port name="ltl_state_current"/>
            <inout_port name="ltl_state_executed_sequence"/>
            <inout_port name="replanning_request"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="StayAction">
            <input_port name="bt_action_type"/>
            <input_port name="current_action"/>
            <inout_port name="ltl_state_current"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="SynchronizedTransitionAction">
            <input_port name="bt_action_type"/>
            <input_port name="current_action"/>
            <inout_port name="ltl_state_current"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="UpdateLTL">
            <inout_port name="action_sequence"/>
            <inout_port name="action_sequence_executed"/>
            <output_port name="bt_action_type"/>
            <output_port name="current_action"/>
            <inout_port name="ltl_state_desired_sequence"/>
        </Action>
    </TreeNodesModel>
    <!-- ////////// -->
</root>
mrq:
The mission describes a cyclic task that repeats a control loop to validate temporal logic progress, react to detections and state, dispatch domain actions, and then update the temporal logic sequence.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (Fallback, ReactiveSequence, UpdateLTL) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute the Fallback that will execute the following actions (LTLPreCheck, ForceFailure) consecutively. If any action (LTLPreCheck, ForceFailure) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will run the Condition LTLPreCheck to find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for aligning the current LTL state with the desired and executed sequences. It will then execute ForceFailure to execute the custom action ReplanningRequestLevel2 and make sure it always fails, which will cause the Fallback to accept the prior success only when LTLPreCheck succeeds.

Next, it will execute the following actions [Fallback, Fallback, Fallback, Fallback] in sequence. If any action fails, it will stop execution. While any action is still running, it will re-evaluate previous actions. It will execute the first Fallback that will execute the following actions (FakeDetectionLevel1, ForceFailure) consecutively. If any action (FakeDetectionLevel1, ForceFailure) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will run the custom action FakeDetectionLevel1 to process a fake replanning input for level 1. It will then execute ForceFailure to execute the custom action ReplanningRequestLevel1 and make sure it always fails, which will cause the Fallback to accept FakeDetectionLevel1 when it succeeds. It will execute the second Fallback that will execute the following actions (ReactiveLTLStateCheck, ForceFailure) consecutively. If any action (ReactiveLTLStateCheck, ForceFailure) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will run the Condition ReactiveLTLStateCheck to find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for the current LTL state meeting the desired sequence. It will then execute ForceFailure to execute the custom action ReplanningRequestLevel2 and make sure it always fails, which will cause the Fallback to accept ReactiveLTLStateCheck when it succeeds. It will execute the third Fallback that will execute the following actions (FakeDetectionLevel3, ForceFailure) consecutively. If any action (FakeDetectionLevel3, ForceFailure) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will run the custom action FakeDetectionLevel3 to process a fake replanning input for level 3. It will then execute ForceFailure to execute the custom action ReplanningRequestLevel3 and make sure it always fails, which will cause the Fallback to accept FakeDetectionLevel3 when it succeeds. It will execute the fourth Fallback that will execute the following actions (Switch4, ForceFailure) consecutively. If any action (Switch4, ForceFailure) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will read blackboard value and compare it against a set of cases. If a case matches the blackboard value, it will execute corresponding action among the custom actions MoveAction, StayAction, PickAction, or DropAction. Else if no case matches, it will execute default action that always fails. It will then execute ForceFailure to execute the custom action ReplanningRequestLevel3 and make sure it always fails, which will cause the Fallback to accept the action selected by the switch only when a case-specific action succeeds.

Finally, it will execute the custom action UpdateLTL to update and output the next domain action and to synchronize the desired sequence with the executed sequence. It will execute UpdateLTL continuously until it fails or succeeds. Else it will keep executing UpdateLTL forever. It will execute the following actions (Fallback, ReactiveSequence, UpdateLTL) for a set number of times or infinitely.

<!--
  This Behavior Tree replans the global path periodically at 1 Hz and it also has
  recovery actions.
-->
<root main_tree_to_execute="MainTree">
  <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
    <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="2" name="NavigateRecovery">
      <PipelineSequence name="NavigateWithReplanning">
        <RateController hz="1.0">
          <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="4" name="ComputePathToPose">
            <ComputePathToPose goal="{goal}" path="{path}" planner_id="GridBased"/>
            <ReactiveFallback name="PathToPoseFallback">
              <GoalUpdated/>
              <RoundRobin name="GlobalPlannerRecoveryActions">
                <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearGlobalCostmap-Context" service_name="global_costmap/clear_entirely_global_costmap"/>
                <Wait wait_duration="2"/>
              </RoundRobin>
            </ReactiveFallback>
          </RecoveryNode>
        </RateController>
        <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="4" name="FollowPath">
          <FollowPath path="{path}" controller_id="FollowPath"/>
          <ReactiveFallback name="FollowPathFallback">
            <GoalUpdated/>
            <RoundRobin name="PlannerRecoveryActions">
              <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearLocalCostmap-Context" service_name="local_costmap/clear_entirely_local_costmap"/>
              <Spin spin_dist="1.57"/>
            </RoundRobin>
          </ReactiveFallback>
        </RecoveryNode>
      </PipelineSequence>
      <ReactiveFallback name="RecoveryFallback">
        <GoalUpdated/>
        <Wait wait_duration="5"/>
      </ReactiveFallback>
    </RecoveryNode>
  </BehaviorTree>
</root>
mrq:
The mission describes a navigation task that periodically replans a global path, follows the path, and performs layered recoveries when actions fail.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (RateController, RecoveryNode) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. While an action is running or has succeeded, it will go to the next action. It will execute the custom action RateController to constrain downstream execution to 1.0 Hz. It will execute main the ROS action ComputePathToPose and make up to 4 retries when failures occur. If the main action fails, it will execute recovery action (ReactiveFallback) and then retry the main action until retries are exhausted.

Next, it will execute the following actions (FollowPath) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. While an action is running or has succeeded, it will go to the next action. It will execute main the ROS action FollowPath and make up to 4 retries when failures occur. If the main action fails, it will execute recovery action (ReactiveFallback) and then retry the main action until retries are exhausted.

Finally, it will execute main the previous PipelineSequence and make up to 2 retries when failures occur. If the main action fails, it will execute recovery action (ReactiveFallback) and then retry the main action until retries are exhausted. It will execute the following actions [GoalUpdated, RoundRobin] in order. If GoalUpdated succeeds, it will stop execution. Else it will keep executing and re-evaluating previous actions whenever an action is running. It will execute the custom action RoundRobin to alternate between clearing the specified costmap and waiting for 5 seconds.

<root main_tree_to_execute="MainTree">
  <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
    <PipelineSequence name="NavigateWithReplanning">
      <DistanceController distance="1.0">
        <ComputePathToPose goal="{goal}" path="{path}" planner_id="GridBased"/>
      </DistanceController>
      <FollowPath path="{path}"  controller_id="FollowPath"/>
    </PipelineSequence>
  </BehaviorTree>
</root>
mrq: The mission describes a navigation task that plans a global path and follows it while advancing to execution even when planning is running or has just succeeded. First, the robot will execute the following actions (DistanceController, FollowPath) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will, while an action is running or has succeeded, go to the next action. It will execute the custom action DistanceController, which will wrap the ROS action ComputePathToPose and trigger it whenever the traveled distance reaches 1.0 meters. It will run the ROS action ComputePathToPose with the provided goal and will store the resulting path each time the 1.0-meter threshold is met. It will run the ROS action FollowPath using the most recently stored path with the specified controller as the planning updates advance the sequence.

BT:
<?xml version="1.0"?>
<root main_tree_to_execute="BehaviorTree">
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="BehaviorTree">
        <Parallel failure_threshold="1" success_threshold="1">
            <Repeat num_cycles="-1">
                <Action ID="VariantAction" value="{variant}"/>
            </Repeat>
            <Switch3 case_1="pick" case_2="deliver" case_3="dock" variable="{variant}">
                <SubTreePlus ID="PickDeliverCharge"/>
                <SubTreePlus ID="DeliverCharge"/>
                <SubTreePlus ID="Charge"/>
                <SubTreePlus ID="PickDeliverCharge"/>
            </Switch3>
        </Parallel>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="Charge">
        <Sequence name="Charge">
            <SubTreePlus ID="GoTo" __shared_blackboard="false" x_input="-9.33" y_input="2.17"/>
        </Sequence>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="Deliver">
        <Sequence name="deliver">
            <SubTreePlus ID="GoTo" __shared_blackboard="false" x_input="-8.08" y_input="4.11"/>
            <Action ID="objectdrop" obj_index="-1"/>
        </Sequence>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="DeliverCharge">
        <Sequence name="Deliver/Charge">
            <SubTreePlus ID="Deliver"/>
            <SubTreePlus ID="GoTo" __shared_blackboard="false" x_input="-9.33" y_input="8"/>
        </Sequence>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="GoTo">
        <Sequence name="GoTo">
            <Action ID="pathPlanner" number_of_waypoints="{number_of_waypoints_}" waypoints="{waypoints_}" x="{x_input}" y="{y_input}"/>
            <Condition ID="CheckPositiveCondition" value="{number_of_waypoints_}"/>
            <Repeat num_cycles="{number_of_waypoints_}">
                <Sequence>
                    <Action ID="ExecutePathAction" w_x="{_w_x}" w_y="{_w_y}" waypoints="{waypoints_}"/>
                    <Action ID="moveRoboterPosition" approachRadius="150" x="{_w_x}" y="{_w_y}"/>
                </Sequence>
            </Repeat>
        </Sequence>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="PickDeliverCharge">
        <Sequence name="Pick/Deliver/Charge">
            <SubTreePlus ID="pick_subtree"/>
            <SubTreePlus ID="Deliver"/>
            <SubTreePlus ID="GoTo" __shared_blackboard="false" x_input="-9.33" y_input="2.17"/>
        </Sequence>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="pick_subtree">
        <Sequence>
            <Action ID="objectplacement" query="WOODENBOX_0.1_0.3_0.3_0.3"/>
            <Repeat num_cycles="4">
                <ForceSuccess>
                    <Sequence name="pick">
                        <Action ID="TxtToWaypointsAction" goal_path="boxes" x="{x}" y="{y}"/>
                        <SubTreePlus ID="GoTo" __shared_blackboard="false" x_input="{x}" y_input="{y}"/>
                        <Action ID="objectplacement" query="MOCK_0.5_0.3_0.3_0.3"/>
                    </Sequence>
                </ForceSuccess>
            </Repeat>
        </Sequence>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <TreeNodesModel>
        <SubTreePlus ID="Charge"/>
        <Condition ID="CheckPositiveCondition">
            <input_port default="{number_of_waypoints}" name="value"/>
        </Condition>
        <SubTreePlus ID="Deliver"/>
        <SubTreePlus ID="DeliverCharge"/>
        <Action ID="ExecutePathAction">
            <output_port default="{_w_x}" name="w_x"/>
            <output_port default="{_w_y}" name="w_y"/>
            <input_port default="{waypoints_}" name="waypoints"/>
        </Action>
        <SubTreePlus ID="GoTo">
            <input_port default="false" name="__shared_blackboard">If false (default), the Subtree has an isolated blackboard and needs port remapping</input_port>
            <input_port default="1.61" name="x_input"/>
            <input_port default="7.76" name="y_input"/>
        </SubTreePlus>
        <SubTreePlus ID="PickDeliverCharge"/>
        <Action ID="TxtToWaypointsAction">
            <input_port default="boxes" name="goal_path"/>
            <output_port default="{x}" name="x"/>
            <output_port default="{y}" name="y"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="VariantAction">
            <output_port default="{variant}" name="value"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="activateLocalization"/>
        <Action ID="approachLocation">
            <input_port default="1" name="locationId"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="approachWall"/>
        <Action ID="deactivateLocalization"/>
        <Action ID="getBaseCharging">
            <output_port default="{charging}" name="charging"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="getPersonPose">
            <input_port default="MARIA" name="personname"/>
            <output_port default="{x_}" name="x"/>
            <output_port default="{y_}" name="y"/>
            <output_port default="{z_}" name="z"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="initNavigation"/>
        <Action ID="loadMap"/>
        <Action ID="loadNavigationMap">
            <input_port default="navigation-map" name="mapname"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="moveRoboterBackwardsLocation"/>
        <Action ID="moveRoboterBackwardsRelative"/>
        <Action ID="moveRoboterOrientateRobotAbs"/>
        <Action ID="moveRoboterOrientateRobotRel"/>
        <Action ID="moveRoboterPosition">
            <input_port default="50" name="approachRadius"/>
            <input_port default="0" name="x"/>
            <input_port default="0" name="y"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="objectdrop">
            <input_port name="obj_index"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="objectplacement">
            <input_port name="query"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="pathPlanner">
            <output_port default="{number_of_waypoints_}" name="number_of_waypoints"/>
            <output_port default="{waypoints_}" name="waypoints"/>
            <input_port default="0" name="x"/>
            <input_port default="0" name="y"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="peoplerecognition"/>
        <SubTreePlus ID="pick_subtree"/>
        <Action ID="setRobotPose">
            <input_port default="0" name="x"/>
            <input_port default="0" name="y"/>
            <input_port default="0" name="yaw"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="startJoystickNavigation"/>
        <Action ID="startReactiveNavigation"/>
        <Action ID="stopJoystickNavigation"/>
        <Action ID="stopReactiveNavigation"/>
        <Action ID="triggerGlobalLocalization"/>
    </TreeNodesModel>
    <!-- ////////// -->
</root>
mrq:
The mission describes a multi-mode task that continuously selects between pick, deliver, and dock workflows while streaming variant updates and executing the chosen workflow in parallel.

First, it will execute the following actions (Repeat, Switch) simultaneously. If either a success threshold is reached or all actions succeed, the execution was successful. Else if any action fails, the execution fails. It will execute the following action continuously until it fails or succeeds. Else keep executing the custom action VariantAction forever. It will read blackboard value and compare it against a set of cases. If a case matches the blackboard value, execute corresponding action. Else if no case matches, execute default action. It will route to the subtree PickDeliverCharge when the blackboard value matches the case pick. It will route to the subtree DeliverCharge when the blackboard value matches the case deliver. It will route to the subtree Charge when the blackboard value matches the case dock. It will route to the subtree PickDeliverCharge when no case matches.

Next, it will execute the following actions (GoTo) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute the subtree GoTo with an isolated blackboard using the inputs x = -9.33 and y = 2.17.

Next, it will execute the following actions (Deliver, GoTo) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the subtree Deliver. It will execute the subtree GoTo with an isolated blackboard using the inputs x = -9.33 and y = 8.

Next, it will execute the following actions (GoTo) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the ROS action pathPlanner with inputs x and y taken from x_input and y_input and will store number_of_waypoints and waypoints. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule. It will execute the following action continuously until it fails or succeeds. Else keep executing the action forever. It will execute the following actions (ExecutePathAction, moveRoboterPosition) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action ExecutePathAction using the stored waypoints and will output a single waypoint to w_x and w_y. It will run the custom action moveRoboterPosition to approach the output waypoint within the specified radius.

Next, it will execute the following actions (pick_subtree, Deliver, GoTo) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the subtree pick_subtree. It will run the subtree Deliver. It will execute the subtree GoTo with an isolated blackboard using the inputs x = -9.33 and y = 2.17.

Finally, the robot will execute the following actions (objectplacement, Repeat) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action objectplacement with the specified query to prepare a target object. It will execute the following action for a set number of times or infinitely. It will execute the following action and make sure it always succeeds. It will execute the following actions (TxtToWaypointsAction, GoTo, objectplacement) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action TxtToWaypointsAction to fetch a waypoint from the path boxes and will store it in x and y. It will execute the subtree GoTo with an isolated blackboard using the stored x and y. It will run the custom action objectplacement with a mock query to finalize the pick attempt.

<root main_tree_to_execute="BehaviorTree">
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="BehaviorTree">
        <Sequence>
            <SetBlackboard value="hello" output_key="myMsg"/>
            <SubTree ID="MySubtree" message="myMsg"/>
            <SetBlackboard value="world" output_key="myMsg"/>
            <SubTree ID="MySubtree" message="myMsg"/>
        </Sequence>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="MySubtree">
        <Action msg="{message}" ID="SaySomething"/>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <TreeNodesModel>
        <SubTree ID="MySubtree">
            <input_port name="message"/>
        </SubTree>
        <Action ID="SaySomething">
            <input_port name="msg"/>
        </Action>
    </TreeNodesModel>
    <!-- ////////// -->
</root>
mrq: The mission describes a messaging task that writes a value to memory and then speaks that value twice in strict order. First, the robot will execute the following actions (SetBlackboard, MySubtree, SetBlackboard, MySubtree) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action SetBlackboard to write the value hello into the blackboard under the key myMsg. It will run the custom action MySubtree after the blackboard write succeeds. It will read the blackboard value myMsg inside MySubtree and will pass it as the input port message. It will run the custom action SaySomething inside MySubtree with the input port msg bound to the passed message. It will run the custom action SetBlackboard to overwrite the blackboard key myMsg with the value world after the first MySubtree succeeds. It will run the custom action MySubtree again after the second blackboard write succeeds. It will read the updated blackboard value myMsg inside MySubtree and will pass it as the input port message. It will run the custom action SaySomething inside MySubtree with the input port msg bound to the updated message.

<root main_tree_to_execute="BehaviorTree">

            <Repeat num_cycles="-1">
                <Action ID="VariantAction" value="{variant}"/>
            </Repeat>
            <Sequence>
                <Action ID="peoplerecognition"/>
                <Fallback>

                    <Action ID="moveRoboterPosition" approachRadius="200" x="3000" y="-12000"/>
                </Fallback>
            </Sequence>
        </Parallel>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <TreeNodesModel>
        <SubTree ID="Recharge"/>
        <SubTree ID="SearchThomasRoom2"/>
        <SubTree ID="SearchThomasRoom4"/>
        <Action ID="VariantAction">
            <output_port default="{variant}" name="value"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="activateLocalization"/>
        <Action ID="approachLocation">
            <input_port default="1" name="locationId"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="approachWall"/>
        <Action ID="deactivateLocalization"/>
        <Action ID="getBaseCharging">
            <output_port default="{charging}" name="charging"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="getPersonPose">
            <input_port default="MARIA" name="personname"/>
            <output_port default="{x_}" name="x"/>
            <output_port default="{y_}" name="y"/>
            <output_port default="{z_}" name="z"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="initNavigation"/>
        <Action ID="loadMap"/>
        <Action ID="loadNavigationMap">
            <input_port default="navigation-map" name="mapname"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="moveRoboterBackwardsLocation"/>
        <Action ID="moveRoboterBackwardsRelative"/>
        <Action ID="moveRoboterOrientateRobotAbs"/>
        <Action ID="moveRoboterOrientateRobotRel"/>
        <Action ID="moveRoboterPosition">

            <input_port default="0" name="x"/>
            <input_port default="0" name="y"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="peoplerecognition"/>
        <Action ID="setRobotPose">

            <input_port default="0" name="yaw"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="startJoystickNavigation"/>
        <Action ID="startReactiveNavigation"/>
        <Action ID="stopJoystickNavigation"/>
        <Action ID="stopReactiveNavigation"/>
        <Action ID="triggerGlobalLocalization"/>
    </TreeNodesModel>
    <!-- ////////// -->
</root>

The mission describes a concurrent person-seeking task that continuously updates a variant value while detecting a person and then either acquiring the person’s pose or moving to a fallback location.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (VariantAction) for a set number of times or infinitely. It will execute the custom action VariantAction continuously until it fails or succeeds. It will otherwise keep executing the custom action VariantAction forever.

Next, it will execute the following actions (peoplerecognition, Fallback) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action peoplerecognition before attempting to obtain a target pose. It will execute the following actions (getPersonPose, moveRoboterPosition) consecutively. If any action (getPersonPose) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will run the custom action getPersonPose using the person name THOMAS and will store the resulting coordinates in x_, y_, and z_. It will run the custom action moveRoboterPosition with an approach radius of 200 millimeters to coordinates x = 3000 and y = -12000 if the person pose retrieval fails. It will execute the following actions (Repeat, Sequence) simultaneously. If either a success threshold is reached or all actions succeed, the execution was successful. Else if any action fails, the execution fails.

If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, it will start all over again until an action succeeds. If Sequence succeeds, it will reverse it to fail. Else if Sequence fails, it will reverse it to succeed. If no action succeeds, it will start all over again until an action succeeds. It will execute the following actions (Condition AllItemsInPlaceForJob, RetryUntilSuccesful) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, it will start all over again until an action succeeds. It will keep trying to execute Sequence until it succeeds. If try succeeds, it will stop retrying and continue. If try fails, it will keep retrying until it succeeds. It will execute the following actions (SequenceStar, Condition AllItemsInPlaceForJob) in order and it will stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute the following actions (the custom action Move, the custom action PickUpItem, Fallback, Fallback, the custom action DropItem) in sequence and it will remember which ones have already succeeded. It will execute the following actions (the custom action GetItemDropPosition, the custom action DropItem) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution will fail. It will execute the following actions (the custom action Move, the custom action DropItem) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution will fail. It will execute the custom action AbortJob and make sure it always fails.

mrq:
The mission describes an access and egress task that attempts to pass through an opening and then secures the entry point.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (PassThroughDoor) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action PassThroughDoor to move through the door.

Next, it will execute the following actions (PassThroughWindow, PassThroughWindow) consecutively. If any action (PassThroughWindow) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will run the custom action PassThroughWindow to pass through a window on the first attempt. It will run the custom action PassThroughWindow again to pass through a window on the second attempt if the first attempt fails.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (root_Fallback, CloseDoor) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will read the outcome of the previous fallback to confirm that either the door or window passage has succeeded before proceeding. It will run the custom action CloseDoor to secure the door after a successful passage.

<!--
  This Behavior Tree replans the global path periodically at 1 Hz and it also has
  recovery actions such as moving forward and spinning.
-->
<root main_tree_to_execute="MainTree">
  <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
    <ReactiveFallback name="BatteryFallback">
      <Sequence name="LowBatteryBehavior">
        <IsBatteryLow min_battery="70" battery_topic="/battery_status" is_voltage="true"/>
        <PipelineSequence name="GoBase">
            <RateController hz="1.0">
              <ComputePathToPose goal="0;0;0" path="{path}" planner_id="GridBased"/>
            </RateController>
            <FollowPath path="{path}" controller_id="FollowPath"/>
          </PipelineSequence>
      </Sequence>
      <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="6" name="NavigateRecovery">
        <PipelineSequence name="NavigateWithReplanning">
          <RateController hz="1.0">
            <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="1" name="ComputePathToPose">
              <ComputePathToPose goal="{goal}" path="{path}" planner_id="GridBased"/>
              <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearGlobalCostmap-Context" service_name="global_costmap/clear_entirely_global_costmap"/>
            </RecoveryNode>
          </RateController>
          <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="1" name="FollowPath">
            <FollowPath path="{path}" controller_id="FollowPath"/>
            <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearLocalCostmap-Context" service_name="local_costmap/clear_entirely_local_costmap"/>
          </RecoveryNode>
        </PipelineSequence>
        <ReactiveFallback name="RecoveryFallback">
          <GoalUpdated/>
          <SequenceStar name="RecoveryActions">
            <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearLocalCostmap-Subtree" service_name="local_costmap/clear_entirely_local_costmap"/>
            <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearGlobalCostmap-Subtree" service_name="global_costmap/clear_entirely_global_costmap"/>
            <Spin spin_dist="1.57"/>
            <Wait wait_duration="5"/>
          </SequenceStar>
        </ReactiveFallback>
      </RecoveryNode>
    </ReactiveFallback>
    
  </BehaviorTree>
</root>
mrq:
The mission describes a navigation task that prioritizes returning to base on low battery and otherwise navigates to a goal with periodic replanning and layered recovery.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (LowBatteryBehavior, NavigateRecovery) in order. If LowBatteryBehavior succeeds, it will stop execution. Else it will keep executing and re-evaluating previous actions whenever an action is running.

Next, it will execute the following actions (IsBatteryLow, GoBase) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action IsBatteryLow with a 70 percent voltage threshold on the specified battery topic. It will execute the following actions (RateController→ComputePathToPose, FollowPath) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. While an action is running or has succeeded, it will go to the next action. It will execute delayed RateController wrapping the ROS action ComputePathToPose at 1.0 Hz and will store the resulting path to reach the origin goal. After a plan is running or has succeeded, it will run the ROS action FollowPath using the stored path with the specified controller.

Next, it will execute main PipelineSequence. If it fails, it will execute recovery action (RecoveryFallback). After recovery, it will retry main action (PipelineSequence). If recovery action (RecoveryFallback) fails and retries are exhausted, it will stop execution. It will execute the following actions (RateController→ComputePathToPose-Recovery, FollowPath-Recovery) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. While an action is running or has succeeded, it will go to the next action. It will execute delayed RateController wrapping ComputePathToPose-Recovery at 1.0 Hz and will store the resulting path to reach the provided goal. It will execute main ComputePathToPose. If it fails, it will execute recovery action (ClearEntireCostmap-Context). After recovery, it will retry main action (ComputePathToPose). If recovery action (ClearEntireCostmap-Context) fails and retries are exhausted, it will stop execution. After a plan is running or has succeeded, it will execute FollowPath-Recovery. It will execute main FollowPath. If it fails, it will execute recovery action (ClearEntireCostmap-Context). After recovery, it will retry main action (FollowPath). If recovery action (ClearEntireCostmap-Context) fails and retries are exhausted, it will stop execution.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (GoalUpdated, RecoveryActions) in order. If GoalUpdated succeeds, it will stop execution. Else it will keep executing and re-evaluating previous actions whenever an action is running. It will execute the following actions (ClearLocalCostmap-Subtree, ClearGlobalCostmap-Subtree, Spin, Wait) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will run the custom action ClearLocalCostmap-Subtree to clear the local costmap completely. It will run the custom action ClearGlobalCostmap-Subtree to clear the global costmap completely. It will run the custom action Spin to rotate by 1.57 radians. It will run the custom action Wait to pause for 5 seconds.

mrq: The mission describes a navigation task that computes a global path at a fixed rate and advances to path following while allowing replanning to continue. First, the robot will execute the following actions (RateController, FollowPath) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute the following actions (ComputePathToPose) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails while controlled by the ROS action RateController at 1.0 Hz. It will run the ROS action ComputePathToPose with the provided goal and will store the resulting path each second. While an action is running or has succeeded, it will go to the next action in the sequence. Next, it will run the ROS action FollowPath using the most recently stored path with the specified controller while upstream replanning continues at 1.0 Hz.

The mission describes a collision-avoidance and forward-motion task that first ensures a clear path using a fallback strategy and then advances.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (Fallback) consecutively. If any action (CheckLaser) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will run the custom action CheckLaser to determine if the laser reports a clear path. It will execute the following actions (Backward, Turn) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails only if the laser check fails. It will run the custom action Backward for 3 seconds to create clearance. It will run the custom action Turn for 4 seconds to reorient after backing up.

Next, it will execute the following actions (Forward) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action Forward to advance once either the laser check succeeds or the backup-and-turn completes successfully.

mrq:
The mission describes a reactive pick-and-place cycle that manages placing an item if held, picking an item if seen, and maintaining continuous operation.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (Fallback, Fallback, AlwaysRunning) in sequence. If any action fails, it will stop execution. While an action is still running, it will re-evaluate previous actions. It will execute the following actions (Inverter, Place) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will execute inverted the custom action Holding an Item. If Holding an Item succeeds, it will reverse it to fail. Else if Holding an Item fails, it will reverse it to succeed. It will run the custom action Place it and will make the placing attempt when the inverted check succeeds. It will then execute the following actions (Inverter, Pick) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will execute inverted the custom action See an Item. If See an Item succeeds, it will reverse it to fail. Else if See an Item fails, it will reverse it to succeed. It will run the custom action Pick it up and will make the picking attempt when the inverted check succeeds. It will run the custom action Keep Running and will keep the sequence active by remaining in a running state.

<root main_tree_to_execute="MainTree">
  <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
    <PipelineSequence name="NavigateWithReplanning">
      <RateController hz="1.0">
        <Sequence>
          <GoalUpdater input_goal="{goal}" output_goal="{updated_goal}">
            <ComputePathToPose goal="{updated_goal}" path="{path}" planner_id="GridBased"/>
          </GoalUpdater>
         <TruncatePath distance="1.0" input_path="{path}" output_path="{truncated_path}"/>
        </Sequence>
      </RateController>
      <KeepRunningUntilFailure>
        <FollowPath path="{truncated_path}" controller_id="FollowPath"/>
      </KeepRunningUntilFailure>
    </PipelineSequence>
  </BehaviorTree>
</root>
mrq:
The mission describes a dynamic navigation task that will update a moving goal, recompute a path at 1 Hz, truncate the path to maintain a 1.0 meter standoff, and continuously follow the truncated path until following fails.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (RateController, KeepRunningUntilFailure) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. While an action is running or has succeeded, it will go to the next action. It will run the custom action RateController at 1.0 Hz to pace the upstream planning sequence. It will execute the following actions (Sequence) in order at each control tick inside the rate-controlled loop.

Next, it will execute the following actions (GoalUpdater, TruncatePath) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action GoalUpdater to update the input goal and to provide an updated_goal output. It will run the ROS action ComputePathToPose with the updated goal and will store the resulting path. It will run the custom action TruncatePath to shorten the stored path so that the endpoint remains 1.0 meters from the updated goal and to output the truncated_path.

Finally, it will execute FollowPath and make sure it always restarts after success while continuing until a failure occurs. It will run the ROS action FollowPath using the truncated_path with the specified controller.

<root main_tree_to_execute="BehaviorTree">

    <BehaviorTree ID="BehaviorTree">

            <Sequence>

                <Action ID="moveRoboterPosition" approachRadius="200" x="5630" y="-12000"/>
                <Switch2 case_1="one" case_2="two" variable="{variant}">
                    <SubTree ID="Recharge"/>
                    <SubTree ID="Recharge"/>
                    <Fallback name="SearchThomas">
                        <SubTree ID="SearchThomasRoom1"/>
                        <SubTree ID="SearchThomasRoom2"/>
                        <SubTree ID="Recharge"/>
                    </Fallback>
                </Switch2>
            </Sequence>
        </Parallel>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="Recharge">
        <Sequence name="Recharge">
            <Action ID="moveRoboterPosition" approachRadius="200" name="Door8" x="23200" y="-12000"/>
            <Action ID="moveRoboterPosition" approachRadius="10" name="GotoCharger" x="23200" y="-14800"/>
        </Sequence>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!-- ////////// -->

            <Sequence name="SearchRoom1">

                <Action ID="moveRoboterPosition" approachRadius="200" name="Door2" x="7520" y="-12000"/>
            </Sequence>
            <ForceFailure>
                <Action ID="moveRoboterPosition" approachRadius="200" name="Door2" x="7520" y="-12000"/>
            </ForceFailure>
        </Fallback>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="SearchThomasRoom2">
        <Fallback name="Room4">
            <Sequence name="SearchRoom4">
                <Action ID="moveRoboterPosition" approachRadius="200" name="Door4" x="14500" y="-12000"/>
                <Action ID="moveRoboterPosition" approachRadius="200" x="14400" y="-9640"/>
                <Action ID="getPersonPose" personname="THOMAS" x="{x_}" y="{y_}" z="{z_}"/>
                <Action ID="moveRoboterPosition" approachRadius="500" name="MoveToPerson" x="{x_}" y="{y_}"/>
                <Action ID="moveRoboterPosition" approachRadius="200" name="Door4" x="14500" y="-12000"/>
            </Sequence>
            <ForceFailure>
                <Action ID="moveRoboterPosition" approachRadius="200" name="Door4" x="14500" y="-12000"/>
            </ForceFailure>
        </Fallback>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <TreeNodesModel>
        <SubTree ID="Recharge"/>
        <SubTree ID="SearchThomasRoom1"/>
        <SubTree ID="SearchThomasRoom2"/>
        <Action ID="VariantAction">
            <output_port default="{variant}" name="value"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="activateLocalization"/>
        <Action ID="approachLocation">
            <input_port default="1" name="locationId"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="approachWall"/>
        <Action ID="deactivateLocalization"/>
        <Action ID="getBaseCharging">
            <output_port default="{charging}" name="charging"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="getPersonPose">
            <input_port default="MARIA" name="personname"/>
            <output_port default="{x_}" name="x"/>
            <output_port default="{y_}" name="y"/>
            <output_port default="{z_}" name="z"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="initNavigation"/>
        <Action ID="loadMap"/>
        <Action ID="loadNavigationMap">
            <input_port default="navigation-map" name="mapname"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="moveRoboterBackwardsLocation"/>
        <Action ID="moveRoboterBackwardsRelative"/>
        <Action ID="moveRoboterOrientateRobotAbs"/>
        <Action ID="moveRoboterOrientateRobotRel"/>
        <Action ID="moveRoboterPosition">
            <input_port default="50" name="approachRadius"/>
            <input_port default="0" name="x"/>
            <input_port default="0" name="y"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="peoplerecognition"/>
        <Action ID="setRobotPose">
            <input_port default="0" name="x"/>
            <input_port default="0" name="y"/>
            <input_port default="0" name="yaw"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="startJoystickNavigation"/>
        <Action ID="startReactiveNavigation"/>
        <Action ID="stopJoystickNavigation"/>
        <Action ID="stopReactiveNavigation"/>
        <Action ID="triggerGlobalLocalization"/>
        <Action ID="objectplacement">
            <input_port default="0" name="query"/>
        </Action>
    </TreeNodesModel>
    <!-- ////////// -->
</root>

<root main_tree_to_execute="MainTree">
	<BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
		<Repeat num_cycles="3">
			<Sequence>
				<SetBlackboard output_key="Goal_a" value="-4.3,-4,180.0" />
				<SetBlackboard output_key="Goal_b" value="-4.7,-6.6,270.0" />
				<SetBlackboard output_key="Goal_c" value="-6.0,-4.2,206.0" />
				<Sequence>
					<ReactiveSequence>
						<Inverter>
							<HumanPoseDetect pose_lr_check="presence" expected_pose_left="" expected_pose_right=""
								detected_person="{detected_person}" pose_left_speech="{pose_left_speech}"
								pose_right_speech="{pose_right_speech}" />
						</Inverter>
						<SubTree ID="MoveRobot" target="Goal_a" />
					</ReactiveSequence>
					<ReactiveSequence>
						<Inverter>
							<HumanPoseDetect pose_lr_check="presence" expected_pose_left="" expected_pose_right=""
								detected_person="{detected_person}" pose_left_speech="{pose_left_speech}"
								pose_right_speech="{pose_right_speech}" />
						</Inverter>
						<SubTree ID="MoveRobot" target="Goal_b" />
					</ReactiveSequence>
					<ReactiveSequence>
						<Inverter>
							<HumanPoseDetect pose_lr_check="presence" expected_pose_left="" expected_pose_right=""
								detected_person="{detected_person}" pose_left_speech="{pose_left_speech}"
								pose_right_speech="{pose_right_speech}" />
						</Inverter>
						<SubTree ID="MoveRobot" target="Goal_c" />
					</ReactiveSequence>
				</Sequence>
			</Sequence>
		</Repeat>
	</BehaviorTree>

	<BehaviorTree ID="MoveRobot">

		</Sequence>
	</BehaviorTree>
</root>

The mission describes a patrolling task that cycles through three waypoints and conditionally moves to each one based on human presence.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (SetBlackboard, SetBlackboard, SetBlackboard, Sequence) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will store the position -4.3,-4,180.0 under the key Goal_a on the blackboard. It will store the position -4.7,-6.6,270.0 under the key Goal_b on the blackboard. It will store the position -6.0,-4.2,206.0 under the key Goal_c on the blackboard. It will then execute the following actions (ReactiveSequence, ReactiveSequence, ReactiveSequence) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails.

Next, it will execute the following actions [Inverter, SubTree] in sequence. If SubTree fails, stop execution. While SubTree is still running, re-evaluate previous actions. It will execute inverted the custom action HumanPoseDetect with presence checking enabled and no specific pose expected. If HumanPoseDetect succeeds, reverse it to fail. Else if HumanPoseDetect fails, reverse it to succeed. It will run the custom action MoveRobot with the target set to Goal_a after the inverted presence check succeeds.

Next, it will execute the following actions [Inverter, SubTree] in sequence. If SubTree fails, stop execution. While SubTree is still running, re-evaluate previous actions. It will execute inverted the custom action HumanPoseDetect with presence checking enabled and no specific pose expected. If HumanPoseDetect succeeds, reverse it to fail. Else if HumanPoseDetect fails, reverse it to succeed. It will run the custom action MoveRobot with the target set to Goal_b after the inverted presence check succeeds.

Finally, it will execute the following actions [Inverter, SubTree] in sequence. If SubTree fails, stop execution. While SubTree is still running, re-evaluate previous actions. It will execute inverted the custom action HumanPoseDetect with presence checking enabled and no specific pose expected. If HumanPoseDetect succeeds, reverse it to fail. Else if HumanPoseDetect fails, reverse it to succeed. It will run the custom action MoveRobot with the target set to Goal_c after the inverted presence check succeeds.

Finally, it will execute MoveRobot for a set number of times or infinitely. It will execute the following actions (Sequence) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action Nav2Client with the goal provided by the target input to drive to the specified waypoint. It will execute Sequence for a set number of times or infinitely to respect the Repeat configuration of the calling tree.

The mission describes a navigation task that will repeatedly update a moving goal, recompute a path, shorten that path to maintain a standoff distance, and advance along the truncated path with continuous replanning.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (RateController, KeepRunningUntilFailure) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. While an action is running or has succeeded, it will go to the next action. It will run the custom action RateController to tick its inner sequence at 1.0 Hz. It will run the custom action KeepRunningUntilFailure to keep executing the following FollowPath action as long as it does not fail.

Next, it will execute the following actions (GoalUpdater, TruncatePath) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action GoalUpdater to accept the current goal and produce an updated_goal that incorporates dynamic changes. It will run the ROS action ComputePathToPose within GoalUpdater to compute a path to the updated_goal and will store the path. It will run the custom action TruncatePath to produce a truncated_path that stops 1.0 meters before the goal.

Finally, it will execute FollowPath. If FollowPath succeeds, it will restart it. Else if FollowPath fails, it will stop looping and continue execution. It will run the ROS action FollowPath using the truncated_path with the specified controller.

<root main_tree_to_execute="MainTree">
  <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
    <PipelineSequence name="NavigateWithReplanning">
      <DistanceController distance="1.0">
        <ComputePathToPose goal="{goal}" path="{path}" planner_id="GridBased"/>
      </DistanceController>
      <FollowPath path="{path}"  controller_id="FollowPath"/>
    </PipelineSequence>
  </BehaviorTree>
</root>
mrq: The mission describes a navigation task that computes a path to a goal and then follows that path while advancing to subsequent actions when earlier actions are running or have succeeded. First, the robot will execute the following actions (DistanceController, FollowPath) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute the following actions (ComputePathToPose) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails within the custom action DistanceController. It will run the custom action DistanceController to gate path computation based on a distance threshold of 1.0 meter. It will run the ROS action ComputePathToPose with the provided goal and will store the resulting path when the distance condition within DistanceController permits execution. It will advance from DistanceController to the next action while DistanceController is running or has succeeded as defined by the PipelineSequence. Next, it will run the ROS action FollowPath using the stored path with the specified controller after the path computation has started running or has succeeded as allowed by the PipelineSequence.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (Repeat, Sequence, ExecuteGroupLinearJointStates) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute ExecuteGroupLinearJointStates for one cycle by executing the action for a set number of times or infinitely. It will execute the following actions (ExecuteGroupLinearJointStates) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action ExecuteGroupLinearJointStates for the arm group with the joint goals 0.9737, 1.4487, 2.0451, -0.3455, 0.0, 0.0 using the service execute_group_joint_states and the tolerance 0.01.

<root main_tree_to_execute="MainTree">
  <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
    <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="6" name="NavigateRecovery">
      <ResetterNode>
        <GoalUpdated/>
        <ProgressCheckerPipeline name="NavigateWithReplanning">
          <RateController hz="0.02">
            <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="1" name="ComputePathToPose">
              <ComputePathToPose goal="{goal}" path="{path_coarse}" planner_id="GridBased"/>
              <ReactiveFallback name="ComputePathToPoseRecoveryFallback">
                <GoalUpdated/>
                <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearGlobalCostmap-Context" service_name="global_costmap/clear_entirely_global_costmap"/>
              </ReactiveFallback>
            </RecoveryNode>
          </RateController>
          <DistanceController distance="2.0">
            <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="1" name="SmoothPath">
              <SmoothPath path_in="{path_coarse}" path_out="{path}" smoother_id="SmoothPath"/>
              <ReactiveFallback name="SmoothPathRecoveryFallback">
                <GoalUpdated/>
                <Sequence>
                  <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearGlobalCostmapSmoother-Context" service_name="global_costmap/clear_entirely_global_costmap"/>
                  <ComputePathToPose goal="{goal}" path="{path_coarse}" planner_id="GridBased"/>
                </Sequence>
              </ReactiveFallback>
            </RecoveryNode>
          </DistanceController>
          <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="1" name="FollowPath">
            <FollowPath path="{path}" controller_id="FollowPath"/>
            <ReactiveFallback name="FollowPathRecoveryFallback">
              <GoalUpdated/>
              <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearLocalCostmap-Context" service_name="local_costmap/clear_entirely_local_costmap"/>
            </ReactiveFallback>
          </RecoveryNode>
          <HadFeedback name="HadFeedback_FollowPath"/>
        </ProgressCheckerPipeline>
      </ResetterNode>
      <ReactiveFallback name="RecoveryFallback">
        <GoalUpdated/>
        <RoundRobin name="RecoveryActions">
          <Sequence name="ClearingActions">
            <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearLocalCostmap-Subtree" service_name="local_costmap/clear_entirely_local_costmap"/>
            <ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearGlobalCostmap-Subtree" service_name="global_costmap/clear_entirely_global_costmap"/>
          </Sequence>
          <!-- <Spin spin_dist="1.57"/> -->
          <Wait wait_duration="5"/>
          <BackUp backup_dist="0.15" backup_speed="0.025"/>
        </RoundRobin>
      </ReactiveFallback>
    </RecoveryNode>
  </BehaviorTree>
</root>
mrq:
The mission describes goal-driven navigation with planning, smoothing, following, continuous progress checking, and structured recovery.

First, the robot will execute main NavigateRecovery as: Execute main [Action]. If it fails, execute recovery action ([Action]). After recovery, retry main action ([Action]). If recovery action ([Action]) fails and retries are exhausted, stop execution. It will run the custom action ResetterNode as the main action and will allow up to six retries when failures occur. It will run the ReactiveFallback named RecoveryFallback as the recovery action when the main action fails.

Next, the robot will run the custom action ResetterNode and will treat it as the main behavior body that may fail or succeed. It will execute the following actions (GoalUpdated, ProgressCheckerPipeline) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action GoalUpdated to confirm that a new goal condition is present. It will run the custom action ProgressCheckerPipeline only after the goal update check succeeds.

Next, the robot will execute the following actions [Actions] in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. While an action is running or has succeeded, go to the next action. It will run the custom action RateController at 0.02 Hz to throttle planning updates and will advance when the planner is running or has succeeded. It will run the RecoveryNode named ComputePathToPose after the rate controller advances. It will run the custom action DistanceController with a 2.0 meter threshold to gate smoothing based on proximity and will advance when smoothing is running or has succeeded. It will run the RecoveryNode named SmoothPath after the distance controller advances. It will run the RecoveryNode named FollowPath after path smoothing advances. It will run the custom action HadFeedback_FollowPath to confirm that controller feedback has been received.

Next, the robot will execute main [Action]. If it fails, execute recovery action ([Action]). After recovery, retry main action ([Action]). If recovery action ([Action]) fails and retries are exhausted, stop execution. It will run the ROS action ComputePathToPose with the provided goal to produce a coarse path and will allow one retry upon failure. It will run the ReactiveFallback named ComputePathToPoseRecoveryFallback as the recovery mechanism when the path computation fails.

Next, the robot will execute the following actions [Actions] in order. If [Action] succeeds, stop execution. Else keep executing and re-evaluating previous actions whenever an action is running. It will run the custom action GoalUpdated to check for a new goal and will preempt recovery if the goal update succeeds. It will run the ROS action ClearEntireCostmap named ClearGlobalCostmap-Context to clear the global costmap if the goal update does not succeed and will allow repeated re-evaluation while actions are running.

Next, the robot will execute main [Action]. If it fails, execute recovery action ([Action]). After recovery, retry main action ([Action]). If recovery action ([Action]) fails and retries are exhausted, stop execution. It will run the ROS action SmoothPath to convert the coarse path into a smoothed path and will allow one retry upon failure. It will run the ReactiveFallback named SmoothPathRecoveryFallback as the recovery mechanism when the smoothing fails.

Next, the robot will execute the following actions [Actions] in order. If [Action] succeeds, stop execution. Else keep executing and re-evaluating previous actions whenever an action is running. It will run the custom action GoalUpdated to check for a new goal and will preempt recovery if the goal update succeeds. It will execute the following actions (ClearGlobalCostmapSmoother-Context, ComputePathToPose) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails when the goal update does not succeed. It will run the ROS action ClearEntireCostmap named ClearGlobalCostmapSmoother-Context to clear the global costmap before replanning. It will run the ROS action ComputePathToPose to regenerate the coarse path after the costmap clearing succeeds.

Next, the robot will execute main [Action]. If it fails, execute recovery action ([Action]). After recovery, retry main action ([Action]). If recovery action ([Action]) fails and retries are exhausted, stop execution. It will run the ROS action FollowPath using the smoothed path with the specified controller and will allow one retry upon failure. It will run the ReactiveFallback named FollowPathRecoveryFallback as the recovery mechanism when following fails.

Next, the robot will execute the following actions [Actions] in order. If [Action] succeeds, stop execution. Else keep executing and re-evaluating previous actions whenever an action is running. It will run the custom action GoalUpdated to check for a new goal and will preempt recovery if the goal update succeeds. It will run the ROS action ClearEntireCostmap named ClearLocalCostmap-Context to clear the local costmap if the goal update does not succeed and will allow repeated re-evaluation while actions are running.

Finally, the robot will execute the following actions [Actions] in order. If [Action] succeeds, stop execution. Else keep executing and re-evaluating previous actions whenever an action is running. It will run the custom action GoalUpdated to check for a new goal and will preempt recovery if the goal update succeeds. It will run the custom action RoundRobin named RecoveryActions to cycle through recovery behaviors if the goal update does not succeed.

Finally, the robot will execute the following actions (ClearingActions) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the ROS action ClearEntireCostmap named ClearLocalCostmap-Subtree to clear the local costmap as the first clearing step. It will run the ROS action ClearEntireCostmap named ClearGlobalCostmap-Subtree to clear the global costmap as the second clearing step.

Finally, the robot will run the ROS action Wait with a wait duration of five seconds as one of the round-robin recovery actions.

Finally, the robot will run the ROS action BackUp with a distance of 0.15 meters and a speed of 0.025 meters per second as one of the round-robin recovery actions.
