mrq:
The mission describes a navigation task that plans a path toward a goal at 1 Hz, follows the computed path, and performs layered recovery if planning or control fail.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (RateController, FollowPath) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute delayed actions by running the RateController at 1.0 Hz and then resuming execution each tick. It will execute the following actions (GoalReached, ComputePathToPose, ClearEntireCostmap) consecutively. If any action (GoalReached) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will run the custom action GoalReached to check whether the goal is already satisfied. It will run the ROS action ComputePathToPose with the provided goal and will store the resulting path if the goal is not yet reached. It will run the ROS action ClearEntireCostmap using the service /global_costmap/clear_entirely_global_costmap only if path computation fails. It will run the ROS action FollowPath using the stored path after a valid path becomes available.

Next, it will execute the following actions (ClearEntireCostmap, ClearEntireCostmap) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will run the ROS action ClearEntireCostmap using the service /local_costmap/clear_entirely_local_costmap to clear the local costmap. It will run the ROS action ClearEntireCostmap using the service /global_costmap/clear_entirely_global_costmap to clear the global costmap after the local clearing succeeds.

Finally, it will execute main action and, if it fails, execute recovery action and then retry the main action up to six times. It will execute the main action that performs the ordered navigation with planning and following as described in the first subtree. If the main action fails, it will execute the recovery actions that clear the local and global costmaps as described in the second subtree. After recovery, it will retry the main action until retries are exhausted.

<?xml version="1.0"?>
<root main_tree_to_execute="BehaviorTree">
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="BehaviorTree">
        <Repeat num_cycles="{num_cycles}">
            <Sequence>
                <Fallback>
                    <Action ID="LTLPreCheck" ltl_state_current="{ltl_state_current}" ltl_state_desired_sequence="{ltl_state_desired_sequence}"/>
                    <ForceFailure>
                        <Action ID="ReplanningRequestLevel2" replanning_request="{replanning_request}"/>
                    </ForceFailure>
                </Fallback>
                <ReactiveSequence>
                    <Fallback>
                        <RetryUntilSuccesful num_attempts="3">
                            <Fallback>
                                <Action ID="LocomotionStatusCheck" locomotion_status="{locomotion_status}"/>
                                <Sequence>
                                    <Action ID="RecoveryStand" locomotion_status="{locomotion_status}"/>
                                    <Delay delay_msec="5000">
                                        <Action ID="LocomotionStart" locomotion_status="{locomotion_status}"/>
                                    </Delay>
                                </Sequence>
                            </Fallback>
                        </RetryUntilSuccesful>
                        <ForceFailure>
                            <Action ID="ReplanningRequestLevel3" replanning_request="{replanning_request}"/>
                        </ForceFailure>
                    </Fallback>
                    <Fallback>
                        <Action ID="MoveAction" action="{action}" move_base_finished="{move_base_finished}" move_base_idle="{move_base_idle}" nav_goal="{nav_goal}"/>
                        <ForceFailure>
                            <Action ID="ReplanningRequestLevel3" replanning_request="{replanning_request}"/>
                        </ForceFailure>
                    </Fallback>
                </ReactiveSequence>
                <Action ID="UpdateLTL" action_sequence="{action_sequence}" ltl_state_desired_sequence="{ltl_state_desired_sequence}" nav_goal="{nav_goal}"/>
            </Sequence>
        </Repeat>
    </BehaviorTree>
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <TreeNodesModel>
        <Action ID="LTLPreCheck">
            <input_port name="ltl_state_current"/>
            <input_port name="ltl_state_desired_sequence"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="LocomotionStart">
            <input_port name="locomotion_status"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="LocomotionStatusCheck">
            <input_port name="locomotion_status"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="MoveAction">
            <output_port name="action"/>
            <input_port name="move_base_finished"/>
            <input_port name="move_base_idle"/>
            <input_port name="nav_goal"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="RecoveryStand">
            <input_port name="locomotion_status"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="ReplanningRequestLevel2">
            <inout_port name="replanning_request"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="ReplanningRequestLevel3">
            <inout_port name="replanning_request"/>
        </Action>
        <Action ID="UpdateLTL">
            <inout_port name="action_sequence"/>
            <inout_port name="ltl_state_desired_sequence"/>
            <output_port name="nav_goal"/>
        </Action>
    </TreeNodesModel>
    <!-- ////////// -->
</root>
mrq:
The mission describes a repeated task that validates a temporal logic plan, ensures locomotion is ready, moves to a navigation goal, and updates the plan in a strict cycle.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (LTLPreCheck, ReplanningRequestLevel2) consecutively. It will execute the custom action LTLPreCheck with the current temporal state and the desired sequence. If LTLPreCheck fails, it will execute the custom action ReplanningRequestLevel2 and make sure it always fails.

Next, it will execute the following actions [LocomotionStatusCheck or RecoveryStand and LocomotionStart, ReplanningRequestLevel3] consecutively. It will keep trying to execute the following actions (LocomotionStatusCheck, RecoveryStand and LocomotionStart) until it succeeds. It will execute the following actions (LocomotionStatusCheck, RecoveryStand and LocomotionStart) consecutively. If LocomotionStatusCheck fails, it will execute the following actions (RecoveryStand, LocomotionStart) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute delayed custom action LocomotionStart. If execution succeeds, it will start a 5000 ms delay timer and, after the delay period is exhausted, it will resume execution. If the locomotion readiness group fails, it will execute the custom action ReplanningRequestLevel3 and make sure it always fails. It will execute the following actions [MoveAction, ReplanningRequestLevel3] consecutively. It will execute the custom action MoveAction with the provided navigation goal and status ports. If MoveAction fails, it will execute the custom action ReplanningRequestLevel3 and make sure it always fails. It will execute the following actions [Locomotion and Move groups] in sequence. If any action fails, it will stop execution. While an action is still running, it will re-evaluate previous actions.

Finally, it will execute the ROS action UpdateLTL with the action sequence and desired temporal sequence and will produce a navigation goal for the next cycle. It will execute UpdateLTL after the prior sequence succeeds. It will execute UpdateLTL once per cycle. It will execute UpdateLTL only after locomotion and navigation have been processed. It will execute UpdateLTL to maintain the link between the temporal logic plan and the produced navigation goal. It will execute UpdateLTL to finalize the cycle state before the next repetition. It will execute UpdateLTL for each cycle because the behavior will execute UpdateLTL in a repeat loop. It will execute UpdateLTL for the number of cycles specified or indefinitely. It will execute UpdateLTL and then the repeat control will execute the sequence again according to the configured count.

<?xml version="1.0"?>
<root main_tree_to_execute="BehaviorTree">
    <!-- ////////// -->
    <BehaviorTree ID="BehaviorTree">
        <Repeat num_cycles="{num_cycles}">
            <Sequence>
                <Fallback>
                    <Condition ID="LTLPreCheck" ltl_state_current="{ltl_state_current}" ltl_state_desired_sequence="{ltl_state_desired_sequence}" ltl_state_executed_sequence="{ltl_state_executed_sequence}"/>
                    <ForceFailure>
                        <Action ID="ReplanningRequestLevel2" ltl_state_current="{ltl_state_current}" ltl_state_executed_sequence="{ltl_state_executed_sequence}" replanning_request="{replanning_request}"/>
                    </ForceFailure>
                </Fallback>
                <ReactiveSequence>
                    <Fallback>
                        <Action ID="FakeDetectionLevel1" replanning_fake_input="{replanning_fake_input}"/>
                        <ForceFailure>
                            <Action ID="ReplanningRequestLevel1" ltl_state_current="{ltl_state_current}" ltl_state_executed_sequence="{ltl_state_executed_sequence}" replanning_request="{replanning_request}"/>
                        </ForceFailure>
                    </Fallback>
                    <Fallback>
                        <Condition ID="ReactiveLTLStateCheck" ltl_state_current="{ltl_state_current}" ltl_state_desired_sequence="{ltl_state_desired_sequence}"/>
                        <ForceFailure>
                            <Action ID="ReplanningRequestLevel2" ltl_state_current="{ltl_state_current}" ltl_state_executed_sequence="{ltl_state_executed_sequence}" replanning_request="{replanning_request}"/>
                        </ForceFailure>
                    </Fallback>
                    <Fallback>
                        <Action ID="FakeDetectionLevel3" replanning_fake_input="{replanning_fake_input}"/>
                        <ForceFailure>

mrq:
The mission describes a repeated supervisory control loop that validates task progress, reacts to state and cues, executes the selected action, and updates the task specification.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (Fallback, ReactiveSequence, UpdateLTL) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute the following actions (LTLPreCheck, ReplanningRequestLevel2) consecutively. If any action (LTLPreCheck) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will execute ReplanningRequestLevel2 and make sure it always fails. It will then execute the following actions (Fallback, Fallback, Fallback, Fallback) in sequence. If any action fails, it will stop execution. While an action is still running, it will re-evaluate previous actions. It will finally run the custom action UpdateLTL with the provided action and state interfaces.

Next, it will execute the following actions (FakeDetectionLevel1, ReplanningRequestLevel1) consecutively. If any action (FakeDetectionLevel1) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will execute ReplanningRequestLevel1 and make sure it always fails.

Next, it will execute the following actions (ReactiveLTLStateCheck, ReplanningRequestLevel2) consecutively. If any action (ReactiveLTLStateCheck) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will execute ReplanningRequestLevel2 and make sure it always fails.

Next, it will execute the following actions (FakeDetectionLevel3, ReplanningRequestLevel3) consecutively. If any action (FakeDetectionLevel3) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will execute ReplanningRequestLevel3 and make sure it always fails.

Finally, it will read blackboard value and compare it against a set of cases. If a case matches the blackboard value, it will execute the corresponding action. Else if no case matches, it will execute the default action. It will execute the following actions (Switch4, ReplanningRequestLevel3) consecutively. If any action (Switch4) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will run the custom action MoveAction when the value matches case move. It will run the custom action StayAction when the value matches case stay. It will run the custom action GuideAction when the value matches case start_training. It will run the custom action BackNormalAction when the value matches case terminate_training. It will execute ReplanningRequestLevel3 and make sure it always fails. It will execute the custom action UpdateLTL after the ReactiveSequence succeeds. It will execute UpdateLTL for a set number of times or infinitely.

The mission describes a repeated supervisory control loop that verifies temporal logic progress, ensures locomotion readiness, executes the selected action, and updates the temporal logic state.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (LTLPreCheck, ReplanningRequestLevel2) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute the following actions (LTLPreCheck, ReplanningRequestLevel2) consecutively. If any action (LTLPreCheck) fails, move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will execute ReplanningRequestLevel2 and make sure it always fails.

Next, it will execute the following actions [Fallback, Fallback] in sequence. If [Fallback] fails, stop execution. While [Fallback] is still running, re-evaluate previous actions. It will execute the following actions (RetryUntilSuccesful, ReplanningRequestLevel1) consecutively. If any action (RetryUntilSuccesful) fails, move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will keep trying to execute Fallback until it succeeds. If try succeeds, stop retrying and continue. If try fails, keep retrying until it succeeds. It will execute the following actions (LocomotionStatusCheck, Sequence) consecutively. If any action (LocomotionStatusCheck) fails, move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, start all over again until an action succeeds. It will execute the following actions (RecoveryStand, Delay) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. While an action is running or has succeeded, go to the next action. It will execute delayed LocomotionStart. If execution succeeds, start delay timer. After delay period is exhausted, resume execution. It will execute ReplanningRequestLevel1 and make sure it always fails.

Next, it will execute the following actions (Switch3, ReplanningRequestLevel3) consecutively. If any action (Switch3) fails, move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will read blackboard value and compare it against a set of cases. If a case matches the blackboard value, execute corresponding Action. Else if no case matches, execute default Action. It will execute ReplanningRequestLevel3 and make sure it always fails.

Finally, it will execute UpdateLTL continuously until it fails or succeeds. Else keep executing UpdateLTL forever.

mrq:
The mission describes a repeated LTL-driven task execution that checks prerequisites, reacts to state and detections, dispatches actions by type, and then updates the LTL progression.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (Fallback, ReactiveSequence, UpdateLTL) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute the following actions (LTLPreCheck, ForceFailure(ReplanningRequestLevel2)) consecutively. It will move to the next action if LTLPreCheck fails and will then execute ReplanningRequestLevel2 while making sure it always fails. It will succeed immediately if LTLPreCheck succeeds.

Next, it will execute the following actions (Fallback, Fallback, Fallback, Fallback) in sequence. If any action fails, it will stop execution. While any action is still running, it will re-evaluate the previous actions. It will execute the following actions (FakeDetectionLevel1, ForceFailure(ReplanningRequestLevel1)) consecutively. It will move to the next action if FakeDetectionLevel1 fails and will then execute ReplanningRequestLevel1 while making sure it always fails. It will succeed immediately if FakeDetectionLevel1 succeeds. It will execute the following actions (ReactiveLTLStateCheck, ForceFailure(ReplanningRequestLevel2)) consecutively. It will move to the next action if ReactiveLTLStateCheck fails and will then execute ReplanningRequestLevel2 while making sure it always fails. It will succeed immediately if ReactiveLTLStateCheck succeeds. It will execute the following actions (FakeDetectionLevel3, ForceFailure(ReplanningRequestLevel3)) consecutively. It will move to the next action if FakeDetectionLevel3 fails and will then execute ReplanningRequestLevel3 while making sure it always fails. It will succeed immediately if FakeDetectionLevel3 succeeds. It will execute child node based on blackboard value. It will read blackboard value and compare it against a set of cases. If a case matches the blackboard value, it will execute corresponding action among the custom actions MoveAction, StayAction, PickAction, DropActionNew, and ResetAction. Else if no case matches, it will execute the default action that always fails. It will then execute ReplanningRequestLevel3 while making sure it always fails if the action selection fails.

Finally, it will run the custom action UpdateLTL to publish the next bt_action_type and current_action and to synchronize the desired LTL sequence with the executed sequence. It will execute UpdateLTL only after the preceding sequence has completed successfully. It will use UpdateLTL to record the executed action into the action_sequence_executed log. It will use UpdateLTL to refresh the action_sequence for the next cycle. It will execute UpdateLTL for each iteration of the outer repetition until the configured number of cycles is completed.

<root main_tree_to_execute="MainTree">
  <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
    <PipelineSequence name="NavigateWithReplanning">
      <SpeedController min_rate="0.1" max_rate="1.0" min_speed="0.0" max_speed="0.26" filter_duration="0.3">
        <ComputePathToPose goal="{goal}" path="{path}" planner_id="GridBased"/>
      </SpeedController>
      <FollowPath path="{path}"  controller_id="FollowPath"/>
    </PipelineSequence>
  </BehaviorTree>
</root>
mrq: The mission describes a navigation task that computes a path to a goal at a controlled rate and then follows that path while advancing as soon as actions are running or succeed. First, the robot will execute the following actions (SpeedController, FollowPath) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails, and while an action is running or has succeeded, it will go to the next action. It will run the custom action SpeedController and will regulate the inner action execution rate between 0.1 Hz and 1.0 Hz with a speed bound between 0.0 m/s and 0.26 m/s and with a filter duration of 0.3 seconds. It will run the ROS action ComputePathToPose inside the SpeedController with the provided goal and will store the resulting path. It will run the ROS action FollowPath using the stored path with the specified controller once the inner computation is running or has succeeded.

<root main_tree_to_execute="MainTree">
  <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
    <PipelineSequence name="NavigateWithReplanning">
      <RateController hz="1.0">
        <Sequence>
          <GoalUpdater input_goal="{goal}" output_goal="{updated_goal}">
            <ComputePathToPose goal="{updated_goal}" path="{path}" planner_id="GridBased"/>
          </GoalUpdater>
         <TruncatePath distance="1.0" input_path="{path}" output_path="{truncated_path}"/>
        </Sequence>
      </RateController>
      <KeepRunningUntilFailure>
        <FollowPath path="{truncated_path}" controller_id="FollowPath"/>
      </KeepRunningUntilFailure>
    </PipelineSequence>
  </BehaviorTree>
</root>
mrq:
The mission describes a dynamic navigation task that continuously updates a moving goal, recomputes a path, truncates it to maintain a standoff distance, and follows the truncated path with continuous replanning.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (RateController, KeepRunningUntilFailure) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. While an action is running or has succeeded, it will go to the next action. It will run the custom action RateController at 1.0 Hz to rate-limit the inner sequence. It will execute the following actions (FollowPath) and will keep executing FollowPath as long as it does not fail.

Next, it will execute the following actions (GoalUpdater, TruncatePath) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action GoalUpdater to transform the input goal into an updated goal while nesting the ROS action ComputePathToPose that uses the updated goal to produce a path. It will run the custom action TruncatePath to shorten the produced path to a distance of 1.0 meter and will store the truncated path.

Finally, it will execute FollowPath. If FollowPath succeeds, it will restart it. Else if FollowPath fails, it will stop looping and continue execution.

<root main_tree_to_execute = "MainTree" >
    <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
       <Sequence name="root_sequence">
           <SaySomething     message="start thinking..." />
           <ThinkWhatToSay   text="{the_answer}"/>
           <SaySomething     message="{the_answer}" />
           <SaySomething2    message="SaySomething2 works too..." />
           <SaySomething2    message="{the_answer}" />
       </Sequence>
    </BehaviorTree>
</root>
mrq: The mission describes a communication task that generates a message and then speaks it in strict order. First, the robot will execute the following actions (SaySomething, ThinkWhatToSay, SaySomething, SaySomething2, SaySomething2) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action SaySomething with the fixed message "start thinking..." before any message generation. It will run the custom action ThinkWhatToSay to compute a message and will store the computed text in the variable the_answer. It will run the custom action SaySomething using the stored variable the_answer after the message computation succeeds. It will run the custom action SaySomething2 with the fixed message "SaySomething2 works too..." after the previous speech action succeeds. It will run the custom action SaySomething2 using the stored variable the_answer only after all preceding actions in the sequence have succeeded.

<root main_tree_to_execute="MainTree">
  <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
    <Sequence name="root">
      <FallbackStar name="AutoLocalization">
        <initialPoseReceived/>
        <SequenceStar name="doSelfLocalization">
          <RetryUntilSuccesful num_attempts="5" name="retry_client_request">
            <globalLocalizationServiceRequest/>
         </RetryUntilSuccesful>
         <RetryUntilSuccesful num_attempts="10" name="retry_localization">
            <Sequence>
              <Fallback>
                <IsLocalized/>
                <SequenceStar>
                 <Spin/>
                <BackUp/>
                 <Spin/>
              </SequenceStar>
             </Fallback>
             <IsLocalized/>
            </Sequence> 
          </RetryUntilSuccesful>
        </SequenceStar>
      </FallbackStar>
      <FallbackStar name="check_motion">
        <Inverter name="is_not_stuck">
          <IsStuck/>
        </Inverter>
        <SequenceStar name="stuck_recovery">
          <Stop/>
          <BackUp/>
          <Spin/>
        </SequenceStar>
      </FallbackStar>
      <SequenceStar name="navigate">
        <ComputePathToPose endpoints="${endpoints}" path="${path}"/>
        <ParallelNode threshold="1">
          <FollowPath path="${path}"/>
          <Sequence>
            <RateController hz="2">
              <ComputePathToPose endpoints="${endpoints}" path="${path}"/>
            </RateController>
            <UpdatePath/>
          </Sequence>
        </ParallelNode>
      </SequenceStar>
    </Sequence>
  </BehaviorTree>
</root>
mrq:
The mission describes autonomous startup localization, motion safety recovery, and dynamic goal-directed navigation for a mobile robot operating from an unknown initial pose.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (initialPoseReceived, doSelfLocalization) consecutively. If any action (initialPoseReceived) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, it will start all over again until an action succeeds. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for the initial pose reception. It will execute the following actions (retry_client_request, retry_localization) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will keep trying to execute the custom action globalLocalizationServiceRequest until it succeeds. It will keep trying to execute the following actions (localization_check_and_motion, IsLocalized) until they succeed. It will execute the following actions (localization_check_and_motion) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. While an action is running or has succeeded, it will go to the next action. It will execute the following actions (IsLocalized, motion_pattern) consecutively. If any action (IsLocalized) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution will fail. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for being localized. It will execute the following actions (Spin, BackUp, Spin) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule for being localized.

Next, it will execute the following actions (is_not_stuck, stuck_recovery) consecutively. If any action (is_not_stuck) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, it will start all over again until an action succeeds. It will execute inverted the custom action IsStuck. If IsStuck succeeds, it will reverse it to fail. Else if IsStuck fails, it will reverse it to succeed. It will execute the following actions (Stop, BackUp, Spin) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (ComputePathToPose, dynamic_follow) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will run the ROS action ComputePathToPose using the provided endpoints and will store the resulting path. It will execute the following actions [FollowPath, replan_and_update] simultaneously. If either a success threshold is reached or all actions succeed, the execution will be successful. Else if any action fails, the execution will fail. It will run the ROS action FollowPath using the stored path. It will execute the following actions (replanner_rate, UpdatePath) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. While an action is running or has succeeded, it will go to the next action. It will run the custom action RateController at 2 Hz to invoke the ROS action ComputePathToPose using the provided endpoints and will update the stored path. It will run the custom action UpdatePath to apply the newly computed path.

<root main_tree_to_execute="MainTree">
  <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
    <ReactiveFallback name="FollowWaypoints">
      <AllGoalsAchieved goal_achieved="{goal_achieved}"/>
      <KeepRunningUntilFailure>
        <ReactiveSequence>
          <GetNextGoal goals="{goals}" goal="{goal}" goal_achieved="{goal_achieved}"/>
          <RecoveryNode number_of_retries="1">
            <NavigateToPose goal="{goal}"/>
            <Wait wait_duration="5"/>
          </RecoveryNode>
          <SetBlackboard output_key="goal_achieved" value="true"/>
        </ReactiveSequence>
      </KeepRunningUntilFailure>
    </ReactiveFallback>
  </BehaviorTree>
</root>
mrq:
The mission describes a waypoint-following task that navigates through provided goals and marks each goal as achieved, with reactive monitoring and simple recovery.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (AllGoalsAchieved, KeepRunningUntilFailure) in order. It will execute the following actions AllGoalsAchieved and KeepRunningUntilFailure in order. If AllGoalsAchieved succeeds, it will stop execution. Else it will keep executing and re-evaluating previous actions whenever an action is running.

Next, it will execute the custom action KeepRunningUntilFailure. It will execute KeepRunningUntilFailure. If KeepRunningUntilFailure succeeds, it will restart it. Else if KeepRunningUntilFailure fails, it will stop looping and continue execution.

Finally, it will execute the following actions [GetNextGoal, RecoveryNode, SetBlackboard] in sequence. If any action fails, it will stop execution. While an action is still running, it will re-evaluate previous actions. It will run the custom action GetNextGoal using the provided goals and will output a single goal while updating the goal achieved flag. It will execute main the ROS action NavigateToPose. If it fails, it will execute recovery action the ROS action Wait. After recovery, it will retry main action the ROS action NavigateToPose. If recovery action the ROS action Wait fails and retries are exhausted, it will stop execution. It will run the custom action SetBlackboard and will set the key goal_achieved to true after navigation handling completes.

mrq: The mission describes a spinning maneuver that rotates the platform through a defined set of angles in a fixed order for one full cycle. First, the robot will execute the following actions (RobotSpin, RobotSpin, RobotSpin, RobotSpin, RobotSpin) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action RobotSpin to rotate 180 degrees at a velocity of 0.5. It will run the custom action RobotSpin to rotate -180 degrees at a velocity of 0.5 after the previous rotation succeeds. It will run the custom action RobotSpin to rotate -90 degrees at a velocity of 0.5 after the previous rotation succeeds. It will run the custom action RobotSpin to rotate 90 degrees at a velocity of 0.5 after the previous rotation succeeds. It will run the custom action RobotSpin to rotate 360 degrees at a velocity of 0.5 after the previous rotation succeeds. Next, it will execute this action sequence for a set number of times or infinitely. It will run the sequence exactly once because the repeat count is one.

mrq:
The mission describes a continuous locomotion pattern that commands a turtle-like robot to move in a rectangular loop forever with guaranteed cycle completion.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (Turtle, Turtle, Turtle, Turtle) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute the custom action Turtle with enable set to 1, direction set to 1, distance set to 2, vel_x set to 2, and vel_y set to 0. It will execute the custom action Turtle with enable set to 1, direction set to 1, distance set to 2, vel_x set to 0, and vel_y set to 2 after the first Turtle action succeeds. It will execute the custom action Turtle with enable set to 1, direction set to 0, distance set to 2, vel_x set to 2, and vel_y set to 0 after the second Turtle action succeeds. It will execute the custom action Turtle with enable set to 1, direction set to 0, distance set to 2, vel_x set to 0, and vel_y set to 2 after the third Turtle action succeeds.

Next, it will execute the custom action described above and make sure it always succeeds.

Finally, it will execute the custom action described above for a set number of times or infinitely. It will execute the custom action described above continuously until it fails or succeeds. It will keep executing the custom action described above forever.

mrq:
The mission describes a patrol task that alternates between two predefined goals and records snapshots at each goal for three full cycles. First, the robot will execute the following actions (SetBlackboard, SetBlackboard, Sequence) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will set the blackboard key Goal_a to the pose -0.579;-1.341;0.0;1.0. It will set the blackboard key Goal_b to the pose 5.214;-1.533;0.0;1.0. It will then execute the following actions (SubTree: MoveRobot with target=Goal_a, SnapshotClient, SubTree: MoveRobot with target=Goal_b, SnapshotClient) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails.

Next, it will execute the following actions (Nav2Client) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the ROS action Nav2Client with the goal read from the blackboard key target.

Finally, it will execute SubTree: MoveRobot with target=Goal_b followed by SnapshotClient in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the ROS action Nav2Client with the goal read from the blackboard key target. It will execute SnapshotClient after arriving at the goal.

Next, it will execute [Action] for a set number of times or infinitely. It will execute the inner behavior three times in total and will restart it after each completion until the count reaches three.

mrq: The mission describes a navigation task that computes a path to a goal and then follows that path while advancing to the next step as soon as actions start running or succeed. First, the robot will execute the following actions (DistanceController, FollowPath) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will, while an action is running or has succeeded, go to the next action. It will run the custom action DistanceController, which will execute delayed ComputePathToPose. It will run the ROS action ComputePathToPose with the provided goal and will store the resulting path when the distance threshold condition is met. Next, it will run the ROS action FollowPath using the stored path with the specified controller after the path computation starts or succeeds.

mrq:
The mission describes a pan-tilt unit pointing routine that sets three target orientations and then aims the unit at each target in a fixed order for two cycles.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (SetBlackboard, SetBlackboard, SetBlackboard, Repeat) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule when writing the key Goal_a to 0.5;-0.2. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule when writing the key Goal_b to -0.5;0.5. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule when writing the key Goal_c to 0.0;0.0. It will execute the following action (Sequence) for a set number of times or infinitely by running it exactly two times.

Next, it will execute the following actions (SubTree: Move_PTU[target=Goal_a], SubTree: Move_PTU[target=Goal_b], SubTree: Move_PTU[target=Goal_c]) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute the BehaviorTree Move_PTU with the target set to Goal_a. It will execute the BehaviorTree Move_PTU with the target set to Goal_b after the first target completes. It will execute the BehaviorTree Move_PTU with the target set to Goal_c after the second target completes.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (MovePTU) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action MovePTU using the ptu_goal provided through the target blackboard key.

mrq: The mission describes a manipulation task that retrieves a list of poses, selects the first pose, and moves the right arm to that pose in strict order. First, the robot will execute the following actions (GetPoseArray, SelectPose, ArmCartesian) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action GetPoseArray to read a pose array from the topic named poses and will store the array in memory. It will run the custom action SelectPose to extract the pose at index 0 from the stored pose array and will save the selected pose as a stamped pose. It will run the custom action ArmCartesian to command a CARTESIAN motion of the right arm using the saved pose in the base_footprint frame via the action server at /vizzy/right_arm_cartesian_controller/cartesian_action after the pose selection succeeds.

mrq:
The mission describes a patrol task that sets three waypoints and then alternates between two waypoints with an interrupt, with a fallback to a third waypoint.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (SetBlackboard, SetBlackboard, SetBlackboard, Fallback) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule after setting each key, by virtue of the sequence semantics that require each action to succeed before continuing. It will set the blackboard key Goal_a to the coordinate string "-0.579;-1.341;0.0" as the first setup action. It will set the blackboard key Goal_b to the coordinate string "5.214;-1.533;0.0" as the second setup action. It will set the blackboard key Goal_c to the coordinate string "-1.588;1.253;0.0" as the third setup action. It will execute the following actions (ReactiveSequence, Sequence) consecutively. If any action (ReactiveSequence or Sequence) fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will execute the following actions (InterruptEvent, Repeat) in sequence. If InterruptEvent fails, it will stop execution. While Repeat is still running, it will re-evaluate previous actions. It will execute InterruptEvent with event name "gohome" as the guard that can preempt the patrol. It will execute Repeat for a set number of times or infinitely. It will execute Repeat for 10 cycles as configured. It will execute the following actions (Sequence) for each cycle. It will execute the following actions (SubTree MoveRobot with target Goal_a, SubTree MoveRobot with target Goal_b) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. While a subtree is running or has succeeded, it will advance to the next action only when the current one completes as required by sequence semantics. It will execute SubTree MoveRobot with the target bound to Goal_a to drive to the first patrol waypoint. It will execute SubTree MoveRobot with the target bound to Goal_b to drive to the second patrol waypoint. If the ReactiveSequence does not succeed, it will execute the following actions (SubTree MoveRobot with target Goal_c) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute SubTree MoveRobot with the target bound to Goal_c to drive to the fallback waypoint.

Next, it will execute the following actions (MoveBase) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the ROS action MoveBase with the goal provided by the subtree input named target. It will succeed for MoveRobot when MoveBase reaches the provided target.

mrq:
The mission describes a navigation task that continuously updates a moving goal, computes a path to the updated goal, and advances path following while replanning at a fixed rate.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (RateController, KeepRunningUntilFailure) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. While an action is running or has succeeded, it will go to the next action.

Next, it will execute the ROS action RateController at 2.0 Hz to pace downstream planning and control. It will execute the following actions (Sequence) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails each time the controller ticks.

Next, it will execute the following actions (GoalUpdater) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action GoalUpdater to transform the input goal into an updated_goal for planning. It will run the ROS action ComputePathToPose with the updated_goal and will store the resulting path using the GridBased planner after the goal update succeeds.

Finally, it will execute FollowPath and, if this action succeeds, restart it. Else if this action fails, it will stop looping and continue execution. It will run the ROS action FollowPath using the most recently computed path with the FollowPath controller while the loop does not report a failure.

mrq: The mission describes a navigation task that computes a path toward a goal and follows it while periodically replanning based on speed. First, the robot will execute the following actions (SpeedController, FollowPath) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will, while an action is running or has succeeded, go to the next action. It will run the custom action SpeedController that adjusts the replan rate between 0.1 and 1.0 Hz using the speed range 0.0 to 0.26 m/s and a filter duration of 0.3 seconds. It will, within the SpeedController, run the ROS action ComputePathToPose using the provided goal and will store the resulting path. Next, it will run the ROS action FollowPath using the stored path with the specified controller after the path computation starts or succeeds, and it will continue even if the ComputePathToPose action is still running.

<root main_tree_to_execute="MainTree">
	<BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
		<Sequence>
      <PoseDetectionControlAction enable="false" />
			<Nav2ClearGlobalCostMap />
			<RobotSeekInitAction search_poses="-4.3,-4,180.0;-4.7,-6.6,270.0;-6.0,-4.2,206.0" />
			<!-- RobotSeekInitAction search_poses="-4.3,-4,180.0;0.75,-3.7,206.0;0.71,-6.0,206.0;-4.7,-6.6,270.0;-6.0,-4.2,206.0;-6.4,-1.9,0.0;-8.0,1.5,90.0;-4.4,0.95,218.0"
				/ -->
			<!-- RobotSeekInitAction
				search_poses="-4.3,-4,180.0;-4.7,-6.6,270.0;-6.0,-4.2,206.0;-6.4,-1.9,0.0;-8.0,1.5,90.0;-4.4,0.95,218.0" />
			<SetBlackboard output_key="found" value="0" / -->
			<Fallback>
				<KeepRunningUntilFailure>
					<ReactiveSequence>
  					<Fallback>
							<Inverter>
							  <ObjectDetectionAction class="person" pose="detected_Pose" min_confidence="0.95" />
								<!-- HumanPoseDetect pose_lr_check="presence" expected_pose_left="" expected_pose_right=""
									detected_person="{detected_person}" pose_left_speech="{pose_left_speech}"
									pose_right_speech="{pose_right_speech}" / -->
							</Inverter>
							<ForceFailure>
								<SetBlackboard output_key="found" value="1" />
							</ForceFailure>
						</Fallback>
						<Sequence>
							<RobotSeekNextSearchPose next_goal="{next_goal}" goal_speech="{goal_speech}" />
							<SubTree ID="MoveRobot" target="next_goal" />
							<TextToSpeechActionClient msg="{goal_speech}" />
							<RobotSpin angle="359" velocity="0.5" />
							<TextToSpeechActionClient msg="I didn't see anyone." />
						</Sequence>
					</ReactiveSequence>
				</KeepRunningUntilFailure>
				<Sequence>
					<BlackboardCheckInt value_A="{found}" value_B="1" return_on_mismatch="FAILURE">
						<TextToSpeechActionClient msg="I found you!" />
					</BlackboardCheckInt>
				</Sequence>
			</Fallback>
		</Sequence>
	</BehaviorTree>

	<BehaviorTree ID="MoveRobot">
		<Sequence name="SetGoal">
			<Nav2Client goal="{target}" />
		</Sequence>
	</BehaviorTree>
</root>
mrq:
The mission describes a human-seeking task that disables pose-based detection control, clears navigation costmaps, initializes search behavior, and then repeatedly searches for a person across predefined poses until a detection is registered and confirmed.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (PoseDetectionControlAction, Nav2ClearGlobalCostMap, RobotSeekInitAction, Fallback) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action PoseDetectionControlAction with detection control disabled before any navigation begins. It will run the ROS action Nav2ClearGlobalCostMap to reset the global cost map and remove stale obstacles. It will run the custom action RobotSeekInitAction to load the specified sequence of search poses. It will execute the following actions (KeepRunningUntilFailure, Sequence) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails.

Next, it will execute the following actions (ReactiveSequence) and make sure it always restarts while the child does not fail. It will execute the following actions (Fallback, Sequence) in sequence. If any action fails, it will stop execution. While any action is still running, it will re-evaluate previous actions. It will execute the following actions (Inverter, ForceFailure) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, it will start all over again until an action succeeds. It will execute inverted the custom action ObjectDetectionAction that looks for a person with minimum confidence 0.95 and will flip success to failure and failure to success. It will execute SetBlackboard and make sure it always fails to write the value 1 to the key found. It will execute the following actions (RobotSeekNextSearchPose, SubTree: MoveRobot, TextToSpeechActionClient, RobotSpin, TextToSpeechActionClient) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action RobotSeekNextSearchPose to compute the next_goal and a goal_speech message. It will run the subtree MoveRobot using the target set to next_goal. It will run the custom action TextToSpeechActionClient to speak the computed goal_speech. It will run the custom action RobotSpin to rotate by 359 degrees at velocity 0.5. It will run the custom action TextToSpeechActionClient to announce that it did not see anyone.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (BlackboardCheckInt) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will find a value on the blackboard and check if it matches a given rule that compares found to 1 and returns failure on mismatch while speaking “I found you!”. It will cause the enclosing fallback to succeed when the check matches and the message is spoken.

The mission describes motion to a provided target within the MoveRobot subtree.

First, it will execute the following actions (Nav2Client) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the ROS action Nav2Client to navigate to the target provided through the subtree input.

<?xml version="1.0"?>
<root main_tree_to_execute="BehaviorTree">
	<!-- ////////// -->
	<BehaviorTree ID="BehaviorTree">
		<Repeat num_cycles="9999999">
			<Fallback>
				<Sequence>
					<SetBlackboard output_key="server_timeout" value="10000" />
					<IsFirstRun name="isFirstRun" current_id="{current_id}" found="{found}" moved_flag="{moved_flag}" got_initial_goal_output="{got_initial_goal_output}" got_initial_goal_input="{got_initial_goal_output}" />
					<FindHuman name="findHuman1" server_timeout="10000" current_id="{current_id}" target_id="{current_id}" got_initial_goal_output="{got_initial_goal_output}" found="{found}" goal="{goal}" />
				</Sequence>
				<Sequence>
					<IsHumanFound name="isHumanFound1" found="{found}" />
					<PipelineSequence>
						<RateController hz="1.0">
							<Fallback>
								<FindHuman name="findHuman2" server_timeout="10000" current_id="{current_id}" target_id="{current_id}" got_initial_goal_output="{got_initial_goal_output}" found="{found}" goal="{goal}" />
								<Delay delay_msec="200">
									<FindHuman name="findHuman3" server_timeout="10000" current_id="{current_id}" target_id="{current_id}" got_initial_goal_output="{got_initial_goal_output}" found="{found}" goal="{goal}" />
								</Delay>
							</Fallback>
						</RateController>
						<RecoveryNode number_of_retries="6" name="NavigateRecovery">
							<PipelineSequence name="NavigateWithReplanning">
								<RateController hz="1.0">
									<RecoveryNode number_of_retries="1" name="ComputePathToPose">
										<Sequence>
											<GoalUpdater input_goal="{goal}" output_goal="{updated_goal}">
												<ComputePathToPose goal="{updated_goal}" path="{path}" planner_id="GridBased" />
											</GoalUpdater>
											<TruncatePath distance="1.6" input_path="{path}" output_path="{truncated_path}" />
										</Sequence>
										<ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearGlobalCostmap-Context" service_name="global_costmap/clear_entirely_global_costmap" />
									</RecoveryNode>
								</RateController>
								<RecoveryNode number_of_retries="1" name="FollowPath">
									<FollowPath path="{truncated_path}" controller_id="FollowPath" />
									<ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearLocalCostmap-Context" service_name="local_costmap/clear_entirely_local_costmap" />
								</RecoveryNode>
							</PipelineSequence>
							<ReactiveFallback name="RecoveryFallback">
								<GoalUpdated />
								<SequenceStar name="RecoveryActions">
									<ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearLocalCostmap-Subtree" service_name="local_costmap/clear_entirely_local_costmap" />
									<ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearGlobalCostmap-Subtree" service_name="global_costmap/clear_entirely_global_costmap" />
									<Spin spin_dist="3.14" />
									<Wait wait_duration="5" />
								</SequenceStar>
							</ReactiveFallback>
						</RecoveryNode>
					</PipelineSequence>
				</Sequence>
				<Sequence>
					<Fallback>
						<HasMoved name="hasMovedToLastPosition" moved_flag="{moved_flag}" />
						<Sequence>
							<RecoveryNode number_of_retries="6" name="NavigateRecovery">
								<PipelineSequence name="NavigateWithReplanning">
									<RateController hz="1.0">
										<RecoveryNode number_of_retries="1" name="ComputePathToPose">
											<GoalUpdater input_goal="{goal}" output_goal="{updated_goal}">
												<ComputePathToPose goal="{updated_goal}" path="{path}" planner_id="GridBased" />
											</GoalUpdater>
											<ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearGlobalCostmap-Context" service_name="global_costmap/clear_entirely_global_costmap" />
										</RecoveryNode>
									</RateController>
									<RecoveryNode number_of_retries="1" name="FollowPath">
										<FollowPath path="{path}" controller_id="FollowPath" />
										<ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearLocalCostmap-Context" service_name="local_costmap/clear_entirely_local_costmap" />
									</RecoveryNode>
								</PipelineSequence>
								<ReactiveFallback name="RecoveryFallback">
									<GoalUpdated />
									<SequenceStar name="RecoveryActions">
										<ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearLocalCostmap-Subtree" service_name="local_costmap/clear_entirely_local_costmap" />
										<ClearEntireCostmap name="ClearGlobalCostmap-Subtree" service_name="global_costmap/clear_entirely_global_costmap" />
										<Spin spin_dist="3.14" />
										<Wait wait_duration="5" />
									</SequenceStar>
								</ReactiveFallback>
							</RecoveryNode>
							<SetBlackboard output_key="moved_flag" value="true" />
							<ResetKalman name="resetKalman" server_timeout="10000" />
						</Sequence>
					</Fallback>
					<Fallback>
						<RetryUntilSuccesful num_attempts="20">
							<Sequence>
								<Delay delay_msec="300">
									<Spin spin_dist="0.5" />
								</Delay>
								<FindHuman name="findHuman4" server_timeout="10000" current_id="{current_id}" target_id="{current_id}" got_initial_goal_output="{got_initial_goal_output}" found="{found}" goal="{goal}" />
								<SetBlackboard output_key="moved_flag" value="false" />
							</Sequence>
						</RetryUntilSuccesful>
						<Sequence>
							<SetBlackboard output_key="got_initial_goal_output" value="false" />
							<SetBlackboard output_key="current_id" value="-1" />
						</Sequence>
					</Fallback>
				</Sequence>
			</Fallback>
		</Repeat>
	</BehaviorTree>
	<!-- ////////// -->
</root>
mrq:
The mission describes a human-following navigation task that repeatedly searches for a target human, plans a path toward the target, follows the path with recovery behaviors, and manages internal state on a blackboard.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (SetBlackboard, IsFirstRun, FindHuman) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will set the blackboard key server_timeout to 10000. It will run the custom action IsFirstRun to initialize the current_id, found, moved_flag, and got_initial_goal_output fields. It will run the custom action FindHuman to search for a human and to write the target’s goal and found status.

Next, it will execute the following actions (IsHumanFound, PipelineSequence) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action IsHumanFound to confirm that a human has been found. It will execute the following actions (RateController with Fallback, RecoveryNode) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails, and while an action is running or has succeeded, it will go to the next action. It will run the RateController at 1.0 Hz to limit inner retries to 1 Hz. It will execute the following actions (FindHuman, Delay with FindHuman) consecutively, and if any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds, and if no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will run the custom action FindHuman to refresh the goal and found status. It will execute delayed FindHuman, and if execution succeeds, it will start a 200 ms delay timer, and after the delay period is exhausted, it will resume execution. It will execute the main action PipelineSequence, and if it fails, it will execute the recovery action ReactiveFallback, and after recovery it will retry the main action, and if recovery fails and retries are exhausted, it will stop execution. It will execute the following actions (RateController with RecoveryNode, RecoveryNode) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails, and while an action is running or has succeeded, it will go to the next action. It will run the RateController at 1.0 Hz to limit planning retries to 1 Hz. It will execute the main action Sequence, and if it fails, it will execute the recovery action ClearEntireCostmap, and after recovery it will retry the main action, and if recovery fails and retries are exhausted, it will stop execution. It will execute the following actions (GoalUpdater with ComputePathToPose, TruncatePath) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action GoalUpdater to adjust the input goal and to invoke the ROS action ComputePathToPose within it to generate a path using the GridBased planner. It will run the custom action TruncatePath to shorten the path to 1.6 meters and to output the truncated_path. It will execute the main action FollowPath, and if it fails, it will execute the recovery action ClearEntireCostmap, and after recovery it will retry the main action, and if recovery fails and retries are exhausted, it will stop execution. It will run the ROS action FollowPath using the truncated_path with the FollowPath controller. It will execute the following actions (GoalUpdated, SequenceStar) in order, and if the first action succeeds it will stop execution, else it will keep executing and re-evaluating previous actions whenever an action is running. It will execute the following actions (ClearEntireCostmap, ClearEntireCostmap, Spin, Wait) in sequence, and if any action succeeds it will remember it and move to the next, and if any action fails it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will clear the local costmap. It will clear the global costmap. It will rotate in place to 3.14 radians. It will wait for 5 seconds.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (Fallback, Fallback) consecutively, and if any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds, and if no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will execute the following actions (HasMoved, Sequence) consecutively, and if any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds, and if no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will run the custom action HasMoved to check whether it has moved to the last position. It will execute the main action PipelineSequence, and if it fails, it will execute the recovery action ReactiveFallback, and after recovery it will retry the main action, and if recovery fails and retries are exhausted, it will stop execution. It will execute the following actions (RateController with RecoveryNode, RecoveryNode) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails, and while an action is running or has succeeded, it will go to the next action. It will run the RateController at 1.0 Hz to limit planning retries to 1 Hz. It will execute the main action GoalUpdater with ComputePathToPose, and if it fails, it will execute the recovery action ClearEntireCostmap, and after recovery it will retry the main action, and if recovery fails and retries are exhausted, it will stop execution. It will run the custom action GoalUpdater to adjust the input goal and to invoke the ROS action ComputePathToPose within it to generate a path using the GridBased planner. It will execute the main action FollowPath, and if it fails, it will execute the recovery action ClearEntireCostmap, and after recovery it will retry the main action, and if recovery fails and retries are exhausted, it will stop execution. It will run the ROS action FollowPath using the path with the FollowPath controller. It will execute the following actions (GoalUpdated, SequenceStar) in order, and if the first action succeeds it will stop execution, else it will keep executing and re-evaluating previous actions whenever an action is running. It will execute the following actions (ClearEntireCostmap, ClearEntireCostmap, Spin, Wait) in sequence, and if any action succeeds it will remember it and move to the next, and if any action fails it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will clear the local costmap. It will clear the global costmap. It will rotate in place to 3.14 radians. It will wait for 5 seconds. It will set the blackboard key moved_flag to true. It will run the custom action ResetKalman to reset state estimation. It will execute the following actions (RetryUntilSuccessful, Sequence) consecutively, and if any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds, and if no action succeeds, it will start all over again until an action succeeds. It will keep trying to execute the following actions (Sequence) until it succeeds, and if a try succeeds it will stop retrying and continue, and if a try fails it will keep retrying until it succeeds. It will execute the following actions (Delay with Spin, FindHuman, SetBlackboard) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will execute delayed Spin, and if execution succeeds it will start a 300 ms delay timer, and after the delay period is exhausted it will resume execution. It will run the custom action Spin to rotate in place by 0.5 radians. It will run the custom action FindHuman to refresh the goal and found status. It will set the blackboard key moved_flag to false. It will execute the following actions (SetBlackboard, SetBlackboard) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will set the blackboard key got_initial_goal_output to false. It will set the blackboard key current_id to -1.

mrq: The mission describes a single navigation routine that advances through remembered steps and moves to a specified position. First, the robot will execute the following actions (moveRoboterPosition) in sequence. If any action succeeds, it will remember it and move to the next. If any action fails, it will start the sequence again from the beginning. It will run the custom action moveRoboterPosition with the target coordinates x = -2100, y = 0 and with an approach radius of 50. It will proceed to the next action only after the moveRoboterPosition action has succeeded or is retried until success within the sequence-with-memory policy.

mrq:
The mission describes a combat engagement behavior that reacts to being under attack, manages health and ammunition constraints, and executes tracking and attacking actions based on visibility and status checks.

First, the robot will execute the following actions (NoAmmo, Sequence(low_hp → dodge)) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will run the custom action NoAmmo and will succeed early if the robot is out of ammunition. It will then execute the following actions (LowHP, Dodge) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action LowHP and will proceed only if the low health condition is detected. It will run the custom action Dodge and will complete this branch if the dodge action succeeds.

Next, it will execute the following actions (UnderAttack, Dodge) consecutively. If any action fails, it will move to the next action until an action succeeds. If no action succeeds, the execution fails. It will run the custom action UnderAttack and will succeed early if an attack is detected. It will run the custom action Dodge and will succeed if the dodge action completes successfully.

Next, it will execute the following actions (LowHP, Dodge, Track, Attack) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action LowHP and will proceed only if the low health condition is detected. It will run the custom action Dodge and will proceed only if the dodge action succeeds. It will run the custom action Track and will proceed only if target tracking succeeds. It will run the custom action Attack and will complete this branch if the attack succeeds.

Next, it will run the custom action LowHP and will succeed if the low health condition is detected. It will run the custom action Track and will succeed if target tracking succeeds. It will run the custom action Attack and will succeed if the attack action completes successfully.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (LowHP, EnamyVisable, Track, Attack) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action LowHP and will proceed only if the low health condition is detected. It will run the custom action EnamyVisable and will proceed only if the enemy is visible. It will run the custom action Track and will proceed only if target tracking succeeds. It will run the custom action Attack and will complete this branch if the attack succeeds.

Finally, it will execute the following actions (EnamyVisable, Track, Attack) in order and stop the sequence if any action fails. It will run the custom action EnamyVisable and will proceed only if the enemy is visible. It will run the custom action Track and will proceed only if target tracking succeeds. It will run the custom action Attack and will complete this branch if the attack succeeds.
